aa1.text: U+4E2B ngaa1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.74 丫) 木之枝頭也 (Page 1|n44 丫) A fork, a crotch (Kangxi: 物之岐頭); the parting of two branches, fingers, or tines; _a t'au_ (aa1 tau4 丫頭) or _a wan_ (aa1 waan4 丫鬟), a slave girl; _a kok kai_ (aa1 gok3 gai3 丫角髻) a little boy; _a ch'a_ (aa1 caa1 丫杈), a fork, a rest for clothes' sticks; _shu a_ (syu6 aa1 樹丫) fork of a tree; _sam a lo_ (saam1 aa1 lou6 三丫路) meeting of three ways, a trivium. Williams' 丫頭, 丫鬟: Yes, people kept slaves in the 19th century. Williams' 丫頭, 丫鬟, 丫角髻: From the double-bun hairstyle. Williams' 三丫路: Now rarely heard; 三叉路 is more common.

U+5416 呀 啊 a1 (shorter syllable) (Page 1|n44 吖) A colloquial word. An interrogative particle; also one indicating that the affirmation is indisputable; a mere final answering tone, having no meaning; _ho a_ (hou2 aa1 好吖) well, very well; _a wai_ (aa1 wai3 吖？) poor, necessitous; _k'u kom wa a_ (keoi5 gam2 waa6 aa1 佢噉話吖), he said so; _mat ye a_ (mat1 je5 aa1 乜嘢吖), what? _kom tso a_ (gam2 zou6 aa1 噉做吖), no, this is the way to do it. (Page 1|n44 呀; here normalised to 吖) A colloquial word. An interjection used in answers, denoting surprise or alarm; alas! dreadful. 【吖】 (a1, aa1) An interrogative-ish final particle suggesting the preceding. 今晚吖 (gam1 maan5-1 a1): "How about tonight." 你行先吖 (nei5 haang4 sin1 a1): "Why don't you go first." An imperative-ish final particle (when the preceding is spoken with force). 飲吖 (jam2 a1): "Drink it." An emphatic final particle. 好吖 (hou2 a1): "OK" (as an active, enthusiastic endorsement). 【吖嘛】 (a1 maa3) An indicative-ish final particle pointing out the preceding. Similar to ending on a falling intonation in English. 天氣凍吖嘛 (tin1 hei3 dung3 a1 maa3): "Because the weather's cold." An interrogative-ish final particle seeking affirmation of the preceding. 今晚吖嘛 (gam1 maan5-1 a1 maa3): "That's tonight, right?"

aa4.text: U+5440 a4 (shorter syllable) 【呀】 (a4) An interrogative-ish final particle of surprise, suspicion, disappointment, etc. 今晚呀 (gam1 maan5-1 a4): "Tonight?" 唔飲呀 (m4 jam2 a4): "Oh, you're not drinking it?" A vocative particle, especially used before asking a question. 爹爹呀 (de1 de1 a4): "Daddy," (followed by a question). 女呀 (neoi5-2 a4): "Daughter," (followed by a question).

aa6.text: U+5440 a6 (shorter syllable for elided "ten" sense) (Page 1|n44 呀) A colloquial word. Also spoken _a_ (aa6); ten, used after a higher number; an answer, a word of reply like Aye! _a ho_ (aa6 hou2 呀好), yes, well; _sz' a ts'in_ (sei3 aa6 cin4 四呀錢), forty cash. Williams' 四呀錢: The "ten" sense would never actually appear in writing as 呀; it would still be spelt 十, with the pronunciation being a matter of preference.

ngaa4.text: U+5440 (Page 1|n44 呀) Read _nga_ (ngaa4); the wrangling of children; to open the mouth wide; _nga lan mai_ (ngaa4 laan1 mai5 呀囒米), cochineal. It is tempting to declare that the "wrangling" and "wide-mouth" senses ought to be read _ha_ (haa1) per 《廣韻》許加切, but it is more complicated than that. The character 呀 also appears under 《廣韻》五加切 _nga_ (ngaa4), explained 吧呀. In turn, the character 吧 has two readings: 普巴切 _p'a_ (paa1), explained 吧呀大口皃 (whence big-mouth); and 伯加切 _pa_ (baa1), explained 吧呀小兒忿爭 (whence children wrangling).

aa3.text: U+554A 呀 a3 (shorter syllable) (Page 1|n44 呀; here normalised to 啊) A colloquial word. A final particle, adding intensity to the meaning; _'m ho a_ (m4 hou2 aa3 唔好啊) not at all good. Williams' 唔好啊: Also an emphatic form of the imperative "don't". 【啊-final】 (a3, aa3) A final particle (with a hint of emphasis). 到未啊 (dou3 mei6 a3): "Are we there yet?" 未到啊 (mei6 dou3 a3): "Not there yet." 有排啊 (jau5 paai4 a3): "It'll be ages." A vocative particle, especially used before airing a grievance. 阿爹啊 (aa3 de1 a3): "Dad!" 【啊-list】 (aa3) A particle after each item in a list of examples. Similar to the non-falling intonation for list items in English. 例如蘋果啊、橙啊、香蕉啊 (lai6 jyu4 ping4 gwo2 aa3, caang4-2 aa3, hoeng1 ziu1 aa3): "Such as apples, oranges, bananas, ...."

aa1.text: U+555E ngaa1 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 1|n44 啞 _A_ (aa2); here corrected to _A_ (aa1)) The confused noise of children ~~studying~~ ``learning to speak`` (Kangxi: 啞嘔小兒學言);

aa2.text: U+555E 瘂 ngaa2 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.75 啞) 口不能言曰啞 (F2.75 瘂) 同上 (F2.75 𤺘) 同上 (Page 1|n44 啞) dumb (Kangxi: 瘖也); to keep silent (Kangxi: 不言也), for which the next (瘂) is used; _a mai_ (aa2 mai4 啞謎) an enigma; _ts'ing nan a yan_ (cing4 naan4 aa2 jan2 情難啞忍), hard to bear it patiently. (Page 1|n44 瘂; here normalised to 啞) Dumb, unable to speak (Kangxi 瘂: 瘖瘂也, 同啞, 瘖也); dull, faded; a cracked sound, as a bell; wheezing; _a tsai_ (aa2 zai2 啞仔), a dumb boy; _a hau mo in_ (aa2 hau2 mou4 jin4 啞口無言), will not speak, sulky; _a tuk_ (aa2 duk6 啞毒) dumb people are revengeful; _sheng tai a_ (seng1 daai3 aa2 聲帶啞), the sound is indistinct. Williams' 啞謎: Changed _mai_ (mai6) to common reading _mai_ (mai4). 【啞】 ('aeX) (_adjective-like_) dumb

ak1.text: U+555E aak1 (longer syllable) ngak1 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 1|n44 啞) Also read _ak_ (ak1); _ak ak_ (ak1 ak1 啞啞) the sound of giggling (Kangxi: 笑語之聲也).

aa1.text: U+9D09 ngaa1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.74 鴉) 鳥名 (F2.74 鵶) 同上 (Page 1|n44 鴉) A raven with a white streak on its breast (Kangxi: 小而腹下白 etc.); _lo a_ (lou5 aa1 老鴉), a raven or crow; _t'o a_ (tou4 aa1 塗鴉), written roughly; _a p'in nai_ (aa1 pin3 nai4 鴉片坭), opium. Williams' 鴉片坭: Dated; opium is now simply called 鴉片. 【鴉】 ('ae) (_noun-like_) crow, raven

aa2.text: U+7602 (F2.75 啞) 口不能言曰啞 (F2.75 瘂) 同上 (F2.75 𤺘) 同上 (Page 1|n44 瘂) nape of the neck (Kangxi: 人身項後入髮際五分爲瘂門);

aa3.text: U+4E9E ngaa3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.76 亞) 次也孟子曰亞聖 (Page 2 亞~~阿~~) Hunchbacked (Kangxi 亞: 象人局背之形); to esteem lightly, to regard as inferior; second, next to, junior, inferior (Kangxi 亞: 亞次也); _pat a u yan_ (bat1 aa3 jyu1 jan4 不亞於人), not inferior to others. Williams does not provide separate entries for 亞 and 阿, but notes that 阿 is "often used" for the name prefix sense. I have split things up accordingly. 【亞】 ('aeH) (_adjective-like_) second

U+5A6D 亞 ngaa3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.76 亞) 姻婭兩壻相謂曰婭 (Page 2 婭) Brothers-in-law (Kangxi: 兩壻相謂曰婭 etc.); _yan a_ (jan1 aa3 姻婭) relatives by marriage, especially brothers or parents; _mo tik yan a_ (mou4 dik1 jan1 aa3 無滴姻婭) I am not related to him at all.

U+963F 亞 (Page 2 亞阿) a syllable prefixed to proper names, for which the second (阿) is often used; it also impersonates epithets; _a mui_ (aa3 mui6-2 阿妹) a younger sister, a lass; _a mat_ (aa3 mat1 阿乜) Mr. So and So; _a pi_ (aa3 bei6 阿？) a silly fellow; _a ch'o_ (aa3 co1 阿初), a raw hand; _a pa_ (aa3 baa1 阿爸), papa; _a t'ai_ (aa3 tai5 阿娣), boy, you lad; Williams does not provide separate entries for 亞 and 阿. I have split things up accordingly.

aai1.text: U+6328 ngaai1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.25 挨) 屋齋切倚也推也擊也又背負也 (Page 2 挨) To lean upon, to trust to; to push away (Kangxi: 推也), to carry on the back; to place alongside (Kangxi: 旁排也); to strike (Kangxi: 擊也); to force, to crowd (Kangxi: 强進曰挨), as with the elbows; to graft; next, near (Kangxi: 今俗凡物相近謂之挨); passable; to lie down, to recline; _ai mai ni pin_ (aai1 maai4 ni1 bin6 挨埋呢便) lean it against here; help here a little; _yau tak ai pang_ (jau5 dak1 aai1 bang6 有得挨憑) has some dependance or resource; _yau tai shan ai_ (jau5 daai6 saan1 aai1 有大山挨), he has powerful friends; _ai ha tik_ (aai1 haa5 dik1 挨吓滴) lie down a little; _ai tak hu_ (aai1 dak1 heoi3 挨得去) it will answer; _ai mun ai u_ (aai1 mun4 aai1 wu6 挨門挨戶) to gad and stare, as a virago; to go from door to door to examine; _ai i_ (aai1 ji2 挨倚), to mutually rely upon; _ai lai_ (aai1 laai6 挨賴) to trust for help; _ai man_ (aai1 maan5-1 挨晚), towards evening.

aai3.text: U+55CC ngaai3 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 3 嗌) A colloquial word; to call after, to bawl; to quarrel; crowded, thronged; _ai kau_ (aai3 gaau1 嗌交), to wrangle, to dispute; _ai p'o hau lung_ (aai3 po3 hau4 lung4 嗌破喉嚨), hallooed till he split his throat; _ai nau_ (aai3 naau6 嗌鬧) thronged; brawling; _ai mat ye_ (aai3 mat1 je5 嗌乜嘢), what are you scolding about? _shap ai kau chang ngan_ (sap6 aai3 gau2 zaang1 ngan4 十嗌九爭銀), ten to one it is a quarrel about money. Williams' 十嗌九爭銀: "Ten to one" is perhaps better rendered "nine in ten". 【嗌】 (aai3) To shout. 大聲嗌 (daai6 seng1 aai3): "Shout out loud." To quarrel. 你哋嗌飽佢啦 (nei5 dei6 aai3 baau2 keoi5 la1): "Why don't youse quarrel over it to your heart's content." 又嗌交喇 (jau6 aai3 gaau1 laa3): "They're quarrelling again." To order food. 嗌餸未 (aai3 sung3 mei6): "Ordered dishes yet?"

ak1.text: U+9628 隘 aak1 (longer syllable) ngak1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.32 阨) 困窮也 (Page 3 隘 _Ai_ (aai3); here corrected to _Ak_ (ak1) and normalised to 阨) impeded, stopped up; (Page 3 阨 _Ak_ (ak1)) An obstruction (Kangxi: 限也), a stop (Kangxi: 塞也); embarrassed, distressed, poor; _kw'an ak_ (kwan3 ak1 困阨) hampered, restrained. Most modern speakers prefer the longer syllable _ak_ (aak1). Williams' "embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed". 【阨】 ('eak) (_noun-like_) pass

aai3.text: U+9698 阨 ngaai3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.28 隘) 窄狹也陋也塞也 (Page 3 隘) A pass, a defile (Kangxi: 古之爲軍也不以阻隘也 etc.); narrow (Kangxi: 陋也, 陝也), confined, straitened; distressed, urgent (Kangxi: 急也); narrowminded, mean; _yan kom ai_ (jan4 gam3 aai3 人咁隘) way is all crowded, a jam; _hap ai_ (haap6 aai3 狹隘) narrow as a pass; a contracted mind. (Page 3 阨 _Ak_ (ak1); here corrected to _Ai_ (aai3) and normalised to 隘) a defile, a pass (Kangxi: 阸陋也); a dangerous path; 【隘】 ('eaH) (_noun-like_) defile (_adjective-like_) narrow

ak1.text: U+5384 戹 阨 (for "adversity" sense) aak1 (longer syllable) ngak1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.33 厄) 困窮也阻難也 (F2.33 戹) 同上 (Page 3 厄~~𢨪~~) small door in a house (Kangxi 厄: 同戹); impeded, cramped; to be ill-used, to be straitened; _ak k'ung_ (ak1 kung4 厄窮), poor; _ngai ak_ (ngai4 ak1 危厄) in danger. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 卮 to 戹. Most modern speakers prefer the longer syllable _ak_ (aak1). Williams erroneously has 𢨪 for an alternative form instead of 戹. Adversity, hardship. 【厄】 ('eak) (_noun-like_) adversity, hardship

aak1.text: U+5443 阨 ak1 (shorter syllable) ngaak1 (alternative) (Page 3 阨; here normalised to 呃) A colloquial word; also pronounced _ak_ (ak1) and _ngak_ (ngak1) To deceive, to delude, to take in; _kom yeung ak yan ke_ (gam2 joeng6-2 aak1 jan4 ge3-2 噉樣呃人嘅) are you going to cheat him so! 【呃】 (aak1, ngaak1) To deceive, to trick, to scam. 你呃人 (nei5 ngaak1 jan4): "You're deceiving others." 呃錢 (aak1 cin4-2): "That's a scam for my money."

ak1.text: U+63E1 aak1 (longer syllable) ngak1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.32 握) 持也 (Page 3 握) To grasp, to hold within the hand (Kangxi: 搤持也 etc.); a little, as much as the palm can hold; _ak teng_ (ak1 deng6 握定) to grasp firmly; _ak shau_ (ak1 sau2 握手), to shake hands; _ak ch'i_ (ak1 ci4 握持), to hold in the hand; _ak iu_ (ak1 jiu3 握要) necessary. Most modern speakers prefer the longer syllable _ak_ (aak1). 【握】 ('aewk) (_verb-like_) to grasp

aak1.text: U+8EDB 厄 ak1 (shorter syllable) ngaak1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.33 軛 _Ak_ (ak1)) 轅端橫木駕馬頸者 (F2.90 軛 _Ak_ (aak3)) 牛軛 (Page 3 軛) A yoke (Kangxi: 轅端橫木駕馬領者); the yoke of a carriage; a yoke, a restraint, a principle of conscience. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 本 to 木. 【軛】 ('eak) (_noun-like_) yoke

aak3.text: U+922A aak3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) ngaak3 (alternative) (F2.90 鈪) 哀百切鈪鐲也 (Page 3 鈪) An unauthorized character. A bracelet; a bangle; _keuk ak_ (goek3 aak3-2 腳鈪) a``n`` anklet; _kam ak_ (gam1 aak3-2 金鈪) a gold wristlet; _seung ak_ (soeng1 aak3-2 鑲鈪) a plated or gilded ring; _ak t'au_ (aak3-2 tau4 鈪頭), clasps of bracelets. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 表 to 哀, and 燭 to 鐲. 【鈪】 (aak3-2) A bracelet or bangle. 手鈪 (sau2 aak3-2): "A hand bracelet."

aam2.text: U+83E1 haam5 (per 《廣韻》胡感切 lengthened) ham5 (per 《廣韻》胡感切) (F2.64 菡) 菡萏荷花也 (Page 4 菡) An unopened flower (Kangxi: 菡猶含也未吐之意); _am tam_ (aam2 daam6 菡萏), the lotus flower; a poetical name. 【菡】 (homX) Used in 菡萏. 【菡萏】 (homX domX) (_noun-like_) lotus flower

aan3.text: U+664F aan3-2 (rising second in vernacular 晏晏地) ngaan3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.70 晏) 日旰也晚也又安也 (Page 4 晏) Evening, sunset; a serene, clear, sky (Kangxi: 天淸也); tardy, behindhand (Kangxi: 晚也); peaceful, gentle (Kangxi: 和柔也); rich, full, said of furs (Kangxi: 鮮盛也 etc.); a tiffin; _hi shan an_ (hei2 san1 aan3 起身晏) to rise late; _sheung an_ (soeng6 aan3 上晏) forenoon; _fan an kau_ (fan3 aan3 gaau3 瞓晏覺) to take a siesta; _fong an hok_ (fong3 aan3 hok6 放晏學) recess in school; _shik an_ (sik6 aan3 食晏) to eat lunch; _an chau_ (aan3 zau3 晏晝) noon; _tso an_ (zou6 aan3 做晏) to get tiffin; _kom an_ (gam3 aan3 咁晏) so late! Fan Wan entry: Corrected 旴 (dawn) to 旰 (late). Williams' "tiffin": British for "lunch". Williams' 上晏: Now rarely heard; 上晝 is more common. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【晏】 ('aenH) (_adjective-like_) clear, cloudless (_adjective-like_) serene (_adjective-like_) late 【晏】 (aan3) Late (in the day). 三點咁晏 (saam1 dim2 gam3 aan3): "3 o'clock? that late?" 晏得嚌 (aan3 dak1 zai6): "That's too late in the day." 晏啲 (aan3 di1): "Later in the day." Lunch. 食晏未 (sik6 aan3 mei5): "Eaten lunch yet?" 【晏晝】 (aan3 zau3) Afternoon. 晏晝有戲睇 (aan3 zau3 jau5 hei3 tai2): "There's a movie to watch in the afternoon." Olden attestation for 晏 _an_ (aan3) meaning "late": 《廣韻》烏澗切 晏 柔也天淸也又晚也又姓左傳齊有晏氏代爲大夫 《康熙字典》晏 《唐韻》烏㵎切《集韻》《韻會》《正韻》於諫切𠀤音曣... 又《玉篇》晚也 《儀禮士相見禮》問日之早晏 《淮南子天文訓》日至于桑野是謂晏食 《屈原離騷》及年歲之未晏兮...

U+9DC3 鴳 ngaan3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.70 鷃) 鳸鷃鳥名 (Page 4 鷃) A kind of small (Kangxi: 小雀也) partridge, which breeds on the ground (Kangxi: 鷃不木處), and is said to crow in the morning (Kangxi: 常晨鳴如雞). Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰斤鳥 to 鳸. 【鷃】 ('aenH) (_noun-like_) quail

ang1.text: U+7F43 (F2.30 罃) 魏侯名 (Page 4 罃)

aang1.text: U+7F4C 罃 甖 ang1 (shorter syllable) ngaang1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.30 罃 _Ang_ (ang1)) 魏侯名 (F2.30 罌 _Ang_ (ang1)) 瓦屬 (F2.88 𦉍) 屋坑切瓶屬 (F2.88 ？) 同上 (Page 4 罃 _Ang_ (ang1); here normalised to 罌) An earthen jar, with a narrow neck, to hold oil, &c. (Kangxi 罃: 備火長頸甁也); _ang ong tsai_ (ang1 ong3 zai2 罌盎仔), a tadpole. (Page 4 甖罌) Used with the last but four (罃 _Ang_ (ang1)). a vase, a jar, a gallipot, an earthen pitcher (Kangxi 罌: 缶也, 甁也, 瓦器也); _t'ong kwo ang_ (tong4 gwo2 aang1 糖果罌), a jar of sweetmeats; _yau ang_ (jau4 aang1 油罌), an oil can; _yat ang ye_ (jat1 aang1 je5 一罌嘢), a thing; _shui ang_ (seoi2 aang1 水罌), a water pitcher. 【罌】 ('eang) (_noun-like_) jar 【罌】 (aang1) A jar or cylindrical-ish container. 罌鹽 (aang1 jim4): "The jar of salt." 買多罌 (maai5 do1 aang1): "Buy one more jar."

aap3.text: U+62BC (F2.65 押) 管押簽押 (Page 5 押)

aat3.text: U+62BC aap3 (per 《廣韻》烏甲切) ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.65 押 _Ap_ (aap3)) 管押簽押 (Page 5 押 _Ap_ (aap3); here normalised to _At_ (aat3)) To sign, to affix a seal, to stamp (Kangxi: 署也 etc.); to control, to guard (Kangxi: 按也一曰管拘也); to arrest, to seize, to detain; to compel, to force; to escort; to suppress; a lockup or detention room in a yamun (衙門); _wak fa ~~ap~~ ``at``_ (waak6 faa1 aat3 畫花押) to make one's mark, to write a device; _~~ap~~ ``at`` fo_ (aat3 fo3 押貨) to escort goods; _~~ap~~ ``at`` k'u tso_ (aat3 keoi5 zou6 押佢做) make him do it; _~~ap~~ ``at`` sung yan_ (aat3 sung3 jan4 押送人), an escort; _'m kam ~~ap~~ ``at`` ni_ (m4 gaam1 aat3 nei5 唔監押你), I'll not keep you; not detain you; _~~ap~~ ``at`` wan_ (aat3 wan5 押韻) to rhyme; _~~ap~~ ``at`` shun_ (aat3 syun4 押船), custom-house boats, guard-boats; _~~ap~~ ``at`` chan_ (aat3 zan6 押陣) to go into battle; _ts'im ~~ap~~ ``at`` fong_ (cim1 aat3 fong4 簽押房), a lockup; _ts'im ~~ap~~ ``at``_ (cim1 aat3 簽押) to endorse an edict, in red ink; _~~ap~~ ``at`` pong_ (aat3 bong1 押幫) to look after, to escort; _~~ap~~ ``at`` tung_ (aat3 dung1 押冬), kept over winter; left over the season, as teas. (Page 5 押 _At_ (aat3)) To pawn for a time; to reserve, to keep back; _at kwai_ (aat3 gwai6 押櫃) deposit money; _heung at_ (hoeng2 aat3 餉押) recognized pawn-shops; _kai at_ (gai3 aat3 髻押) a hair band-pin; _at p'iu_ (aat3 piu3 押票) to pledge or shave a pawn ticket; _kau 'ng at_ (gau2 ng5 aat3 九五押) a five per cent. discount pawnbroker; _siu at_ (siu2 aat3 小押) a little pawn-shop; _tong at_ (dong3 aat3 當押) to pawn. Williams has the non-pawn senses under _Ap_ (aap3) and the pawn senses under _At_ (aat3). Whilst _ap_ (aap3) is expected from Middle Chinese 烏甲切, the common reading of 押 is _at_ (aat3). Note that 押 is (probably) cognate with 壓, and while 壓 also has a Middle Chinese reading of 烏甲切, its modern reading as aat3 is uncontroversial. Williams' 押韻: Changed _wan_ (wan6) to common reading _wan_ (wan5). 【押】 ('aep) (_verb-like_) to stamp, to seal

aap3.text: U+9D28 aap3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) ngaap3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.65 鴨) 家鶩又名田鳧 (Page 5 鴨) A duck, a mallard; _hin ap_ (hin2 aap3-2 蜆鴨) a wild duck; _lap ap_ (laap6 aap3-2 臘鴨) dried ducks; _ch'au ap p'in_ (caau2 aap3 pin3-2 炒鴨片) fried duck cutlet; _ap miu_ (aap3 miu4 鴨苗), ducklings; _pui ap tsai_ (bui6 aap3 zai2 焙鴨仔), to hatch ducks artificially. 【鴨】 ('aep) (_noun-like_) duck 【鴨】 (aap3-2) (_slang_) A male prostitute. Compare 雞 gai1 "chicken" for female prostitute. (TODO) 做鴨 (zou6 aap3-2): "Be a male-prostitute."

aat3.text: U+58D3 aap3 (per 《廣韻》烏甲切) ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.72 壓) 鎮也覆也 (Page 6 壓) To overthrow, as a wall (Kangxi: 壞也); to supply a want (Kangxi: 塞補也); to press down, to steady, to settle (Kangxi: 笮也); to bend; to subject, to suppress, to conquer (Kangxi: 一曰鎭壓); to injure, to oppress; to repress, as alarm; to intimidate; _at wai_ (aat3 waai6 壓壞) to crush, to hurt by laying on; _at pak sing_ (aat3 baak3 sing3 壓百姓) to vex the people; _t'an at_ (taan4 aat3 彈壓) to subdue, to put a stop to, as a row; _at king_ (aat3 ging1 壓驚), to quiet one's fears; _at chu_ (aat3 zyu6 壓住) to steady; _chan at_ (zan3 aat3 鎮壓) to repress (a revolt); to remove evils; _at shat_ (aat3 sat6 壓實) to press solid. 【壓】 ('aep) (_verb-like_) to press down, to suppress

U+605D gaat3 (per 《正韻牋》訖黠切) ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.72 恝) 恝然無愁之貌 (Page 5 恝) To dismiss care; contented, lighthearted; ~~happy looking~~ ``without sorrow`` (Kangxi: 無愁貌); _at in_ (aat3 jin4 恝然), ~~pleased~~ ``indifferent``. 【恝】 (keat) (_adjective-like_) indifferent, sorrowless
U+621E (F2.72 戞) 擊也 (Page 5 戞)

U+63E0 ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.72 揠) 拔也助禾苗長也 (Page 5 揠) To pull up (Kangxi: 拔也); to eradicate; _at miu_ (aat3 miu4 揠苗), to pull up a shoot in order to hasten its growth. 【揠】 ('eat) (_verb-like_) to pull up
U+79F8 (F2.72 秸) 禾稾去其皮曰秸 (Page 5 秸)

U+904F ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.72 遏) 止也絕也 (Page 5 遏) To stop, to bring to a standstill (Kangxi: 遏止也); unforeseen obstacle; to reach, to arrive (Kangxi: 逮也); to cut off, to put a stop to (Kangxi: 絕也); _cho at_ (zo2 aat3 阻遏) to stop one doing, to restrain; _at tek_ (aat3 dek6 遏糴) to hold on to rice; _at yuk nan_ (aat3 juk6 naan4 遏慾難), hard to curb one's lusts. 【遏】 ('at) (_verb-like_) to curb, to arrest

U+95BC ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.72 閼) 閉也塞也又音謁又音煙 (Page 6 閼) To shut, to stop up (Kangxi: 遮壅也); to obstruct (Kangxi: 止也塞也); to suffocate; to prevent superiors knowing; _at sak_ (aat3 sak1 閼塞) to stop up, as water. 【閼】 ('at) (_verb-like_) to block

U+981E ngaat3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.72 頞) 額也 (Page 6 頞) The root of the nose (Kangxi: 鼻莖也); the frontal sinus; a saddle (Kangxi: 頞鞍也); _ts'uk at_ (cuk1 aat3 蹙頞) to knit the brows; _yau at_ (jau1 aat3 幽頞) a lemur. 【頞】 ('at) (_noun-like_) nosebridge

aau1.text: U+5773 ngaau1 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 6 坳) A colloquial word. Poor, destitute; _au ai_ (aau1 ai3 坳屭) in want.

aau3.text: U+5773 㘭 aau1 (per 《廣韻》於交切 for literary "uneven-ground" senses) ngaau3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.42 㘭) 地㘭下也不平也 (Page 6 坳 _Au_ (aau1); here normalised to _Au_ (aau3)) A furrow, a hollow (Kangxi: 窊下也); an unevenness in the ground; undulations; rolling, as land. (Page 7 坳) A colloquial word; a turn, a corner; _chun au_ (zyun2 aau3 轉坳) turn the corner. The reading _au_ (aau3) is not unattested; Kangxi has 《集韻》於敎切音拗義同. 【坳】 ('aew, 'aewH) (_noun-like_) depression, uneven-ground

aau2.text: U+62D7 ngaau2 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.42 上聲 拗) 屋狡切以手拉折物 (Page 7 拗) To snap a thing in two (Kangxi: 手拉也, 折也); to twist, to break; ~~to drag along;~~ _au chit_ (aau2 zit3 拗折) to break in two; _au sui_ (aau2 seoi3 拗碎) to snap in shivers; _au shau keng_ (aau2 sau2 geng2 拗手頸), to try the strength of the hands; to fall out about a matter; _au chun kong_ (aau2 zyun2 gong2 拗轉講), to speak in irony, badinage; _au u_ (aau2 jyu4-2 拗魚), to catch fish in a lifting net. A colloquial word. Warped, bent; curved; _au au tik_ (aau2 aau2 dik1 拗拗滴) a little crooked; _au lun_ (aau2 lyun1 拗攣), to curve. Williams' "to drag along": This is a misreading of Kangxi 手拉也. 【拗】 ('aewX) (_verb-like_) to snap 【拗】 (aau2) To bend or curve. 拗手瓜 (aau2 sau2 gwaa1): "Arm wrestle." 拗柴 (aau2 caai4): "Sprained ankle."

aau3.text: U+62D7 ngaau3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.42 去聲 拗) 偏急不相合也 (Page 7 拗) Obstinate, self-willed, perverse (Kangxi: 拗戾); to rush against madly (Kangxi: 固相違也); _au keng_ (aau3 geng2 拗頸), stiffnecked; _au hi_ (aau3 hei3 拗氣) disputatious, opinionated; _au to tim wai chi_ (aau3 dou3 dim6 wai4 zi2 拗到掂為止), I must have it so or not at all; _chap au_ (zap1 aau3 執拗) set in his way; _fan au_ (faan2 aau3 反拗) whimsical, contrariwise. There is a subtle difference between 拗 aau3 "stubborn" and 詏 aau3 "argue". Kangxi has: For 拗: 拗戾固相違也 "stubborn-perverse: obstinately going against". For 詏: 言逆也 "speech defying". Nevertheless, being stubborn is not too far from being argumentative; together with 拗 being a more commonly seen character, its use as an alternative form of 詏 is understandable.

U+51F9 aau1 (per 《集韻》於交切) ngaau3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.42 凹) 灣也曲也 (Page 7 凹) Indented (Kangxi: 窊也); meandering; the dip of the horizon, the undulations in hills; a hole, a depression, a pit; _shan au_ (saan1 aau3 山凹) a valley; _au tsz'_ (aau3 zi6 凹字) characters cut in bas relief.

U+8A4F 拗 ngaau3 (hypercorrected initial) There is a subtle difference between 拗 aau3 "stubborn" and 詏 aau3 "argue". Defiant speech (Kangxi: 言逆也). 【詏】 (aau3) To argue. 詏撬 (aau3 giu6): "Argument." 詏贏 (aau3 jeng4): "Win the argument." Olden attestation for 詏 _au_ (aau3) meaning "argue": 《康熙字典》詏 《集韻》《正韻》𠀤於敎切妖去聲言逆也

ai1.text: U+54CE 𫫃 唉 ngai1 (common for "beg" sense) (Page 2 𫫃; here normalised to 哎) A particle of surprise or pain; to beg, to ask; _ai ya_, (ai1 jaa1 哎吔) whew! halloo! oh, dear! _ai, mat ni kom tso_ (ai1 mat1 nei5 gam2 zou6 哎乜你噉做) oh! why did you do so? _ai k'au ni_ (ai1 kau4 nei5 哎求你), I intreat you. Williams' 哎求你: Corrected _k'au_ (kau1) to _k'au_ (kau4). 【哎吔-pain】 (ai1 ja4) An interjection of pain, mistake, etc. 哎吔 (ai1 ja4): "Ouch", "Whoops", etc. 【哎吔-indignation】 (ai1 jaa1) An interjection of surprise, shock, indignation, etc. 哎吔 (ai1 jaa1): "Oh dear!", "How dare you!", etc. So-called, or nominal. 哎吔經理 (ai1 jaa1 ging1 lei5): "So-called manager."

e1.text: U+2BAC3 nge1 (alternative for onomatopoeic whine sense) (Page 42 𫫃) A final particle, denoting are you willing, if you please, is it not so? _Ngo kau ni tuk e_ (ngo5 gaau3 nei5 duk6 e1 我教你讀𫫃), shall I teach you to read? _shi yat shi e_ (si3 jat1 si3 e1 試一試𫫃), try, will you? (Page 713 𫫃) A tone, a whine; _e e sheng_ (e1 e1 seng1 𫫃𫫃聲), the whining moan of a sick child. Also pronounced _nge_ (nge1).

nge4.text: U+2BAC3 (Page 724 _Nge_ (nge4); here assigned to 𫫃) A colloquial word. A low tone; _nge nge sheng_ (nge4 nge4 seng1 𫫃𫫃聲), a grumble, indistinct muttering.

ai2.text: U+77EE ngai2 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.31 矮) 不高也 (Page 2 矮) Low; diminutive, short (Kangxi: 短人也, 矬也); _ai lo tsai_ (ai2 lou2 zai2 矮佬仔), a pigmy, a little old man; _pi cheung ai tang tsai ni ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (bei2 zoeng1 ai2 dang3 zai2 nei5 co5 畀張矮凳仔你坐) get a low stool for you to sit on---to delude one; _ai tak tsai_ (ai2 dak1 zai6 矮得嚌) too low. Williams' 矮佬仔: Corrected _lo_ (lou5) to _lo_ (lou2). Williams' 畀張矮凳仔你坐: Corrected _tso_ (zo6 去聲) to _ts'o_ (co5 上聲). 【矮】 ('eaX) (_adjective-like_) short

ai3.text: U+5C6D ngai3 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 2 屭) A colloquial word; grief, sorrow; _pai ai_ (bai3 ai3 贔屭) trouble, resulting from affliction or poverty. 【贔屭】 (bai3 ai3) To be exceedingly afflicted or troubled by something.

hei3.text: U+5C6D (Page 2 屭 _Ai_ (ai3); here corrected to _Hi_ (hei3)) Strong, herculean, athletic (Kangxi: 壯大貌).

ai3.text: U+66C0 ngai3 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.32 曀) 陰而風也 (Page 2 曀) Cloudy and windy (Kangxi: 隂而風也); the sun obscured and nearly hid by the clouds (Kangxi: 雲風曀日光 etc.). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 𦠉 to 曀. 【曀】 ('ejH) (_adjective-like_) (windily) overcast (_adjective-like_) obscured

U+7E0A ngai3 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.32 縊) 懸樑自縊也 (Page 2 縊) To hang, to strangle one's self (Kangxi: 經也, 懸繩曰縊); ~~the warp of cloth;~~ _ai sz' kwai_ (ai3 sei2 gwai2 縊死鬼), ghost of a suicide; _ai keng_ (ai3 geng2 縊頸), hung by the neck. Williams' "the warp of cloth": This is a misreading of Kangxi 經也. 【縊】 ('ejH, 'jieH) (_verb-like_) to hang, to strangle

U+7FF3 ngai3 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.32 翳) 障翳 (Page 2 翳) A feather fan or screen, a flabellum (Kangxi: 華蓋也); to screen, to intercept (Kangxi: 翳猶屛也); to seclude from observation (Kangxi: 翳障也); to keep close, to repress (Kangxi: 隱也蔽也, 䕃也); to destroy (Kangxi: 一曰滅也); anything that intercepts; an obscurity in the eyes; trees ~~dying~~ ``dead and not standing`` (Kangxi: 木立死曰葘自死爲翳); a fabulous sort of pheasant (Kangxi: 鳥名也似鳳). A colloquial word. Feverish; sultry, hot; hurried breathing, a stricture in the breast; _ai hi_ (ai3 hei3 翳氣) difficulty of breathing; _ai it_ (ai3 jit6 翳熱) hot weather; _shan tsz' ai it_ (san1 zi2 ai3 jit6 身子翳熱) feverish; _ai fai_ (ai3 fai3 翳肺) to feel grieved; _ai chai_ (ai3 zai6 翳滯) indigestible. 【翳】 ('ejH, 'ej) (_noun-like_) screen (_verb-like_) to screen, to cover (up) (_noun-like_) fallen-tree
U+26809 ngai3 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 2 𦠉) Lean, cadaverous, meager, poor (Kangxi: 瘦也). Fan Wan entry: The headword 𦠉 is a misprint of 曀.

ak1.text: U+5E44 aak1 (longer syllable) ngak1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.32 幄) 覆帳也 (Page 3 幄) A curtain, a screen (Kangxi: 帳也, 幬也); a large tent, a markee (Kangxi: 大帷); to protect, to shelter. 【幄】 ('aewk) (_noun-like_) tent, canopy

U+6E25 aak1 (longer syllable) ngak1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.32 渥) 厚也沾濡也洽也潤也 (Page 3 渥) To moisten, to soak (Kangxi: 霑也); to enrich with favors; to fertilize; to cover or daub thick (Kangxi: 厚漬也); shining, rich; _ak mung_ (ak1 mung4 渥蒙), deeply grateful for; _yan ak_ (jan1 ak1 恩渥) imperial favor; _ak tan_ (ak1 daan1 渥丹), to make very red. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 沽 to 沾. 【渥】 ('aewk) (_verb-like_) to moisten, to imbue

am1.text: U+557D ngam1 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 724 啽) _om mak_ (am1 mak6 啽默) to be silent; to give no answer~~, as one whs is very sleepy~~. The reading _om_ (am1) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 烏含切, explained 啽嘿不言.

U+5EB5 菴 ngam1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.87 庵) 園屋小草舍也又僧寺也 (F2.87 菴) 同上 (Page 341 庵菴) A round hut (Kangxi 庵: 圜屋爲庵), a thatched cottage, a straw hovel (Kangxi 庵: 小草舍也) for soldiers or guard; a nunnery; _sz' ku om_ (si1 gu1 am1 師姑庵), a Budhist nunnery; _yap om_ (jap6 am1 入庵), to become a nun; _om t'ong_ (am1 tong4 庵堂), a convent.

ap3.text: U+5EB5 ngap3 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 341 庵菴 _Om_ (am1); here corrected to _Op_ (ap3)) a pig-sty (Kangxi: 豕屋);

am1.text: U+83F4 (F2.87 庵) 園屋小草舍也又僧寺也 (F2.87 菴) 同上 (Page 341 庵菴)

U+8AF3 闇 am2 (per Fan Wan) ngam1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.87 諳 _Om_ (am2)) 諳練曉也 (Page 342 諳 _Om_ (am2); here normalised to _Om_ (am1)) Accustomed, skilled in, versed (Kangxi: 練也歷也, 熟聞也); to know, fully acquainted with (Kangxi: 悉也, 知也, 曉也); knowing thoroughly, to commit to memory (Kangxi: 記也, 憶也), to recite, to chant or repeat (Kangxi: 誦也大聲也); _om lin_ (am1 lin6 諳練) well practiced in, mature; _'m om shai ts'ing_ (m4 am1 sai3 cing4 唔諳世情), unacquainted with the world; _om tsung_ (am1 zung6 諳誦) to repeat exactly.

am2.text: U+8AF3 (F2.87 諳) 諳練曉也 (Page 342 諳)
U+95C7 (F2.87 闇) 暗也 (Page 342 闇)

am3.text: U+95C7 am2 (per Fan Wan) ngam3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.87 闇 _Om_ (am2)) 暗也 (Page 342 闇 _Om_ (am2); here normalised to _Om_ (am3)) To shut the door (Kangxi: 閉門也), to withdraw from society, to retire from; retired, dark, an obscure recess (Kangxi: 冥也, 幽也); ignorant, dark minded; badly lighted``, unenlightened`` (Kangxi: 不明也); evening (Kangxi: 闇夜也); an eclipse (Kangxi: 闇日月食也); _om mun tse hak_ (am3 mun4 ze6 haak3 闇門謝客) to decline to see friends; _om yeuk_ (am3 joek6 闇弱) unenlightened. 【闇】 ('omH) (_verb-like_) to shut away, to conceal (_adjective-like_) gloomy, unenlightened

am1.text: U+9D6A 䳺 ngam1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.87 䳺) 䳺鶉鳥名 (Page 341 䳺; here normalised to 鵪) A quail (Kangxi 䳺: 䳺鶉); _om ~~shun~~ ``ch'un``_ (am1 ceon1 鵪鶉), a quail, under which two or three species of Coturnix are included; _lung om_, (lung4 am1 籠鵪) quails beaten in fight, which are then put aside for the table. Williams' 鵪鶉: Changed _shun_ (seon4) to common reading _ch'un_ (ceon1).
U+4CFA (F2.87 䳺) 䳺鶉鳥名 (Page 341 䳺)

am2.text: U+5535 ngam2 (hypercorrected initial) (Page 3 唵 _Am_; here corrected to _Om_) To feed with the hands (Kangxi: 手進食也); to hold in the mouth (Kangxi: 含也); a word used by Budhists (Kangxi: 釋呪多用唵字). Corrected the Williams pronunciation from _am_ to _om_ per 《廣韻》烏感切 (notwithstanding both map to am2 in modern Cantonese). Williams' "a word used by Budhists": A transliteration of the Sanskrit ॐ (_Om_).
U+63A9 (Page 3 掩) Williams has am2 "cover" twice: once as 掩 _Am_ (am2) on Page 3, and once as 掩 _Om_ (am1) as a variant of 揞 _Om_ (am2) on Page 341.

U+63DE 掩 ngam2 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.87 揞) 藏也覆之也 (Page 3 掩 _Am_ (am2); here normalised to 揞 _Om_ (am2)) A colloquial word. To cover with the hand (Kangxi 揞: 手覆也); to conceal from (Kangxi 揞: 藏也); _am mai sheung ngan_, (am2 maai4 soeng1 ngaan5 揞埋雙眼) to cover both eyes; _am chu_ (am2 zyu6 揞住) hide it; _am tik pui shau_, (am2 dik1 bui6 sau2 揞滴背手) give him a little sop, cover his palm. (Page 341 掩揞) To cover, to hide, to screen, by putting something on (Kangxi 揞: 掩也); ~~to extinguish, to suppress;~~ _om chu hau_ (am2 zyu6 hau2 揞住口), to put the hand over the mouth; _che om pat chu_ (ze1 am2 bat1 zyu6 遮揞不住) can not be hid; _om chu t'au hok_ (am2 zyu6 tau4 hok3 揞住頭殼) to lean the head on the hand; _pi shau om chu_ (bei2 sau2 am2 zyu6 畀手揞住) cover it with your hand; _om mat lo ku_ (am2 mat6 lo4 gu2 揞密鑼鼓), "muffle the gong," _i. e._ keep it quiet; _om fung k'am_ (am2 fung1 kam4 揞風琴), to play a piano; ~~_om mit_ (am2 mit6 揞滅) to put out, as fire; to quash;~~ _om pak mai mung_ (am2 baak6 mai4 mung4 揞白迷濛), blindman's buff; _om chu k'u sheung ngan_ (am2 zyu6 keoi5 soeng1 ngaan5 揞住佢雙眼), blindfold his eyes; _om mak_ (am2 mak6 揞脈) to feel the pulse; _om pui shau_ (am2 bui6 sau2 揞背手) to give hush-money, to bribe. The first character (掩) is read _im_ (jim2), but also used with this meaning in the _p'ing shing_ (平聲). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𡈼 to 也. Williams' "to extinguish, to suppress", 揞滅: These are dubious. Kangxi 揞 has 《揚子方言》滅也荆楚曰揞 under the reading 於咸切黯平聲 _am_ (am1). But 《抱經堂叢書》本 annotates a reading of 烏感反 _am_ (am2), and says that 滅 is a typo for 藏. Williams' 揞白迷濛: Corrected _pak_ (baak3) to _pak_ (baak6). Williams' 揞背手: Now rarely heard; 揞口費 is more common for "hush money". 【揞】 (am2) To cover with the hand. 揞口費 (am2 hau2 fai3): "Hush money." Olden attestation for 揞 _om_ (am2) meaning "cover": 《廣韻》烏感切 揞 手覆 《康熙字典》揞 《唐韻》《集韻》《韻會》《正韻》𠀤烏感切庵上聲藏也手覆也...
U+6B3F (F2.87 欿) 欿然不足之貌 (Page 342 欿)

U+9EEF ngam2 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.87 黯) 黯黯不明貌又漢臣汲黯 (Page 342 黯) Dark, pitch dark (Kangxi: 深黑也), dark as a midnight; astonied and silent, as if lost in amazement; _K'ap Om_ (kap1 am2 汲黯) a distinguished statesman of the Han (漢) dynasty. Saddened at parting (Kangxi: 黯然傷別貌). 【黯】 ('eamX, 'eam) (_noun-like_) pitch-black (_adjective-like_) dejected

am3.text: U+6697 闇 am3-2 (rising second in vernacular 暗暗地) ngam3 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.88 暗) 不明也又目不明貌 (Page 342 暗) Sun obscured by clouds or otherwise (Kangxi: 日無光也); dark, dimly lighted (Kangxi: 不明也), sombre; gloomy, not shining bright; clandestine, unobserved, secret, underhand; dull, not perspicacious; private, mental; _hak om_ (hak1 am3 黑暗) dark; _t'in shik om_ (tin1 sik1 am3 天色暗) a dark day; _tang om_ (dang1 am3 燈暗) a dim lamp; _om seung_ (am3 soeng2 暗想), to think over; _ta om ho_ (daa2 am3 hou6 打暗號) to give a hint; _om chung tso sz'_ (am3 zung1 zou6 si6 暗中做事) to do things in the dark; _om mui_ (am3 mui6 暗昧) dull of perception; _ho pau om nam_ (ho4 baau1 am3 nam5 荷包暗稔), a full purse, well supplied with cash; _om kan_ (am3 gaan1 暗間), in the dark; _om liu_ (am3 liu5 暗了), it is now night; _om hoi_ (am3 hoi6 暗害) to privily injure. 【暗】 ('omH) (_adjective-like_) dark, dim (_adjective-like_) covert 【暗算】 (am3 syun3) To wound (usually mortally) with a hidden weapon.

ang1.text: U+56B6 (F2.30 嚶) 鳥鳴聲之和 (Page 4 嚶)

U+9DAF ngang1 (hypercorrected initial) (F2.30 鶯) 黃鶯鳥名 (Page 4 鶯) The nightingale or a gay species of thrush (Kangxi: 鶯鳥也卽黃鸝), that nestles on the willow and sings well; variegated plumes (Kangxi: 鳥羽文); _wong ang_ (wong4 ang1 黃鶯), the mango bird; _ang ko_ (ang1 go1 鶯哥), a parrot; _ang ko pi_ (ang1 go1 bei6 鶯哥鼻) a Roman nose. A colloquial word; a knot in a stick of wood; _p'ek kau ch'ai ang_ (pek3 gau6 caai4 ang1 劈嚿柴鶯), split this knot for me. 【鶯】 ('eang) (_noun-like_) oriole (_adjective-like_) variegated

ang2.text: U+54FD (F2.31 哽) 鶯上聲哽咽 (Page 4 哽)

U+3F98 哽 ngang2 (alternative) (Page 4 㾘) A colloquial word; to stick up, to press into or on; to inchase or emboss; _ang tak keuk t'ung_ (ang2 dak1 goek3 tung3 㾘得腳痛) they hurt my foot; _ang fa_ (ang2 faa1 㾘花), to emboss; _shang shek ang_ (saang1 sek6 ang2 生石㾘), to have a stone bruise; _ang sam_ (ang2 sam1 㾘心), to disturb one, as bad news; painful. Williams' 生石㾘: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). 【㾘】 (ang2) To exert (or experience) a pressing force over a smallish or lump-sized area. 㾘牙 (ang2 ngaa4): "The food is hard and presses against my teeth." 㾘腳 (ang2 goek3): "It presses against my foot."

ang3.text: U+66F4 (Page 4 更) A colloquial word, a corruption of _kang_ (gang3). More, still more; try again; _ang yau t'im_ (ang3 jau5 tim1 更有添), there are more; _ang noi ti_ (ang3 noi6 di1 更耐啲) rather longer time. Williams' 更耐啲: Corrected _ti_ (dei6) to _ti_ (di1).

ap1.text: U+6D3D ngap1 (alternative) (Page 4 洽) A colloquial word. To cover, over, to keep from the air; to cover, as with a poultice; poor, inferior; slovenly, neglected; _ap wai_ (ap1 waai6 洽壞) spoiled by mold; _ap ap ts'ui_ (ap1 ap1 ceoi3 洽洽脆) crinkling, as light pastry; _ap chap p'o_ (ap1 zap1 pou3 洽汁舖) a miserable, dirty shop; _ap ch'ong_ (ap1 cong1 洽瘡), to poultice; _ui shu ap u_ (wui1 syu4 ap1 wu6 煨薯洽芋) to roast a potato or taro in ashes; _met._ to enjoy one's self secretly. 【洽】 (ap1) To cover and keep wet (when undesirable to do so).

au1.text: U+5340 ngau1 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.49 區) 人姓又音拘 (Page 6 區) A surname (Kangxi: 姓); a measure of four pecks (Kangxi: 四豆爲區); _puk au_ (buk6 au1 蹼區), to conceal. Williams' "four pecks": It is disputed whether one 豆 is 0.4 pecks (豆實四升) or 1 peck (豆當爲斗毛居正曰豆古斗字). To conceal (Kangxi: 匿也). 【區】 ('uw) (_noun-like_) urna (_verb-like_) to conceal
U+5614 (Page 6 嘔 _Au_ (au2); here corrected to _Au_ (au1)) a child's prattle (Kangxi: 唲嘔小兒語也); _au a_ (au1 aa1 嘔啞), children humming;

au2.text: U+5614 歐 ngau2 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.54 嘔) 吐也 (Page 6 歐 _Au_ (au1); _Au_ (au2) implied) Used for the next (嘔), to vomit; (Page 6 嘔) Interchanged with the last (歐) (Kangxi: 與歐同吐也). To vomit (Kangxi: 吐也); the noise of retching; to spit out; _au hut_ (au2 hyut3 嘔血) to spit blood; _au fan ch'ut lai_ (au2 faan1 ceot1 lai4 嘔返出嚟), to disgorge; to pay back; _tsok au_ (zok3 au2 作嘔), a disposition to vomit; _au hiu ki tam_ (au2 hiu1 gei2 daam6 嘔嘵幾啖) threw up several times; _au t'o_ (au2 tou3 嘔吐) to puke; _au long_ (au2 long6 嘔浪) seasick. 【嘔】 ('uwX) (_verb-like_) to vomit

au1.text: U+6B50 ngau1 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.49 歐) 歐陽覆姓也 (Page 6 歐) _au to_ (au1 dou1 歐刀), a headsman's sword (Kangxi: 𠛬人之刀也). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 歐侯 and 歐陽 (compound surnames) (Kangxi: 歐侯歐陽俱複姓).
U+6F1A ngau1 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.49 漚) 水上泡 (Page 6 漚) Read _au_ (au1); bubbles on the water (Kangxi: 浮漚也); frothy spume.

au3.text: U+6F1A ngau3 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.55 漚) 以水久浸物曰漚 (Page 6 漚) To soak, to macerate, to steep (Kangxi: 久漬也); to soften by soaking (Kangxi: 漚柔也); _au lan_ (au3 laan6 漚爛) soaked and spoiled; to macerate to rags; _au mui_ (au3 mui4 漚霉), soaked till moldy; _'m k'am au_ (m4 kam1 au3 唔𠸐漚) it will spoil if soaked long; _au to ch'au_ (au3 dou3 cau3 漚到臭) soaked till it stinks; _au nau_ (au3 nau6 漚腬) glutted, loathing food; _t'ang shin au chu yuk_ (tang4 sin5 au3 zyu1 juk6 藤鱔漚豬肉) to fry eels in pork fat, _met._ to add insult to injury. Williams' 藤鱔漚豬肉: Corrected _shin_ (sin6) to _shin_ (sin5). 【漚】 ('uwH) (_verb-like_) to steep 【漚泥】 (au3 nai4) To rot in mud. Used to express discontent, contempt, etc.

au1.text: U+750C ngau1 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.49 甌) 茶甌 (Page 6 甌) A bowl (Kangxi: 小盆也, 瓦器), a deep cup (Kangxi: 今俗謂盌深者爲甌); _nga au_ (ngaa5 au1 瓦甌), a basin to hold water; _ch'a au_ (caa4 au1 茶甌), a tea-cup; _in au_ (jin1 au1 煙甌), a tobacco-cup; _pun au_ (bun3 au1 半甌), half a cup, as of wine; _muk au_ (muk6 au1 木甌), a wooden bowl.

U+8B33 嘔 歐 ngau1 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.49 謳) 謳歌長吟也 (Page 6 謳) To sing songs in recitative (Kangxi: 歌也); a chant (Kangxi: 吟也); a ballad, a ditty; _ut au_ (jyut6 au1 粵謳), Canton Songs; _au yam_ (au1 jam4 謳吟), to sing songs. (Page 6 歐) To sing; to crow merrily; in which it is like the last (謳) (Kangxi 歐: 與謳同氣出而歌也). (Page 6 嘔 _Au_ (au2); here corrected to _Au_ (au1)) to sing, to make a glad noise (Kangxi 嘔: 與謳通); 【謳】 ('uw) (_verb-like_) to sing

U+9DD7 ngau1 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.49 鷗) 水鳥 (Page 6 鷗) A gull (Kangxi: 鷗水鴞也); _pak au_ (baak6 au1 白鷗) or _han au_ (haan4 au1 閒鷗), a kind of gull found about Canton; _sha au_ (saa1 au1 沙鷗), a white gull.

au2.text: U+6BC6 ngau2 (hypercorrected initial) (F1.54 毆) 毆辱捶擊也 (Page 6 毆) To fight, to hit with a stick (Kangxi: 捶毄物也, 以杖擊也); to strike, to knock; _tau au_ (dau3 au2 鬥毆), to wrangle, to fisticuff; _au yuk_ (au2 juk6 毆辱) to disgrace a man by knocking him; _au ta_ (au2 daa2 毆打), to beat, to take a stick to. 【毆】 ('uwX) (_verb-like_) to beat

caa1.text: U+53C9 (Page 8 叉) A fork in a road; a prong, a crotch; to cross or interlace the fingers (Kangxi: 手指相錯, 指相交也); _wo ch'a_ (wo4 caa1 禾叉), a pitch-fork; _ngan ch'a_ (ngan4 caa1 銀叉), a silver fork; _u ch'a_ (jyu4 caa1 魚叉), a fish grains; _ch'a shiu_ (caa1 siu1 叉燒), to roast on a fork; _sha ch'a_ (saa2 caa1 耍叉), to fence with tridents. 【叉】 (caa1) Short for 叉燒. 叉鴨米 (caa1 aap3 mai5): "Char shiu, roast duck vermicelli." Euphemistic substitute for any profane intensifier. 冇叉用 (mou5 caa1 jung6): "Bleeping useless." 【叉燒】 (caa1 siu1) Char shiu, a type of barbecued pork. 叉燒包 (caa1 siu1 baau1): "Barbecued-pork bun." 叉燒腸 (caa1 siu1 coeng4-2): "Barbecued-pork rice-noodle rolls."

U+5C94 caa3 (per 《字彙補》丑亞切) (Page 8 ~~汊~~岔) (...) roads diverging (Kangxi 岔: 三分路也); _sam ch'a lo hau_ (saam1 caa1 lou6 hau2 三岔路口), a trivium; The reading _ch'a_ (caa1) is not unattested; the character 岔 appears under 《正韻牋》初加切逸字. Williams does not provide separate entries for 岔 and 汊, probably following Kangxi, which has 與汊同 under 岔. I have split things up accordingly. Williams' 三岔路口: Also spelt 三叉路口.

U+5DEE (F2.74 差) 差錯舛謬也 (Page 8 差) Mistaken (Kangxi: 舛也); to err, to differ (Kangxi: 貳也不相値也); to miss the mark, to exceed; error, fault, discrepancy, excess, differing, unlike, unassorted; _tai ch'a tak ch'a_ (daai6 caa1 dak6 caa1 大差特差), very wrong; _ch'a tak un_ (caa1 dak1 jyun5 差得遠), very different; _ch'a ts'o keuk_ (caa1 co3 goek3 差錯腳) a blunder, a faux pas; _ch'a tit 'm t'ung_ (caa1 dit1 m4 tung4 差啲唔同), differ very slightly; _ch'a pat to_ (caa1 bat1 do1 差不多), nearly the same; _ch'a ts'o_ (caa1 co3 差錯) a mistake; _ch'ing ch'a sai_ (cing3 caa1 saai3 秤差嗮) all weighed wrong. (Page 615 差 _Ts'z'_ (ci1); here corrected to _Ch'a_ (caa1)) _~~ts'z'~~ ``ch'a`` ho yan i_ (caa1 ho2 jan4 ji3 差可人意) like what most men think. The etymology of 阿差, a slur for "South Asian" (see below), is unclear, although it is almost certainly not derived from the "mistake" or "differ" senses. I have heard it said that it is from the presence of Indian policemen in colonial Hong Kong, either: from 摩囉差 (mo1 lo1 caa1), an historical term for an Indian policeman, or from 差人 (caai1 jan4), meaning "officer". 【差-discrepancy】 (tsrhae) (_noun-like_) discrepancy, error 【阿差】 (aa3 caa1) (_derogatory_) A South Asian person.

caai1.text: U+5DEE (F2.25 差) 使也 (Page 11|n54 差) To commission, to send on a message (Kangxi: 差使也); to manage, to act; a messenger, an envoy; a legate, a commissioner; _ch'ai sz'_ (caai1 si3 差使) a bailiff; _ch'ai yik_ (caai1 jik6 差役) a policeman, a lictor; _ch'ai sz'_ (caai1 si6 差事) an errand; _kai ch'ai_ (gaai3 caai1 解差), a criminal's guard; _fu ch'ai_ (fu2 caai1 苦差), an unpleasant duty; _ch'ut ch'ai_ (ceot1 caai1 出差), sent as legate; _p'au king ch'ai_ (paau2 ging1 caai1 跑京差), a courier with dispatches. To select, to choose (Kangxi: 擇也, 簡也). 【差-send】 (tsrheaj) (_verb-like_) to send (_noun-like_) messenger (_verb-like_) to select 【差】 (caai1) An officer. 差人 (caai1 jan4): "Police officer." 差佬 (caai1 lou2): "Copper." 差婆 (caai1 po4): "Copperess." 當差 (dong1 caai1): "Serve as a copper." 郵差 (jau4 caai1): "Post officer."

ci1.text: U+5DEE (F2.34 差) 參差不齊 (Page 615 差) Uneven, projecting irregularly (Kangxi: 不齊等也); _ts'am ts'z'_ (caam1 ci1 參差) irregular, not uniform; Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'i_ per 《廣韻》楚宜切. 【差-uneven】 (tsrhje) Used in 參差.

caa1.text: U+6260 (Page 8 扠) To fork out, to nip (Kangxi: 挾取也); to drive out, to pitch out, to strike; _ch'a ch'ut hu_ (caa1 ceot1 heoi3 扠出去) turn him out; _ch'a chu k'u_ (caa1 zyu6 keoi5 扠住佢), harpoon it.

U+6C4A caa3 (per 《集韻》楚嫁切) (Page 8 汊~~岔~~) Streams (...) diverging (Kangxi 汊: 水岐流也); _sam ch'a ho_ (saam1 caa1 ho4 三汊河), a river in Liautung (遼東). Williams does not provide separate entries for 岔 and 汊, probably following Kangxi, which has 與汊同 under 岔. I have split things up accordingly.
U+8256 co1 (per Fan Wan) (F2.58 艖 _Ts'o_ (co1)) 舟名 (Page 583 艖 _Ts'o_ (co1)) A skiff (Kangxi: 小舟也); _im ts'o_ (jim4 co1 鹽艖), a salt boat, vessels used in salt transportation. Also read _ch'a_ (caa1).

caa2.text: U+9454 caa4 (for onomatopoeic senses; low first in reduplication 鑔鑔) A (usually small) cymbal. 打鑔鑔 (daa2 caa4 caa2): "Play the cymbal." 京鑔 (ging1 caa2): "The capital-style cymbal." (_onomatopoeia_) The sound of cymbals in Chinese opera. 鑔鑔拼 (caa4 caa4 peng3): "Char char pang!"

zaa1.text: U+5412 Used in mythological names. 哪吒 (naa4 zaa1): "Nezha."

zaa3.text: U+5412 咤 zaa1 (alternative per 《集韻》陟加切) (F2.76 咤 _Ch'a_ (caa3)) 叱咤怒也噴也 (Page 9|n52 吒咤 _Ch'a_ (caa3); here corrected to _Cha_ (zaa3)) To foam and sputter in anger (Kangxi 吒: 噴也叱怒也); to talk angrily; to grumble; _ch'ik ~~ch'a~~ ``cha``_ (cik1 zaa3 叱吒) to rail at. 【吒】 (traeH) (_verb-like_) to sputter

caa3.text: U+54A4 (F2.76 咤) 叱咤怒也噴也 (Page 9|n52 吒咤)

zaa3.text: U+54A4 (Page 9|n52 吒咤 _Ch'a_ (caa3); here corrected to _Cha_ (zaa3))

caa3.text: U+597C 姹 zaa3 (per 《廣韻》陟駕切) (F2.76 奼) 美女也 (Page 9|n52 奼) A handsome girl (Kangxi: 美女也), a beautiful unmarried young lady (Kangxi: 少女也).
U+8721 (F2.28 蜡 _Ch'ai_ (caai3)) 釵去聲祭名八蜡所以報土也 (F2.76 蜡) 八蜡祭名 (Page 9|n52 蜡) Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰釒⿱丿乂 to 釵, corrected 入 to 八, and corrected 士 to 土.

ceoi3.text: U+8721 (Page 596 蜡) A flyblow, eggs of the common fly (Kangxi: 蠅膽也). 【蜡-unvoiced】 (tshjoH) (_noun-like_) maggot

zaa3.text: U+8721 caa3 (per Fan Wan) caai3 (per Fan Wan) zaa6 (per 《廣韻》鋤駕切) (F2.28 蜡 _Ch'ai_ (caai3)) 釵去聲祭名八蜡所以報土也 (F2.76 蜡 _Ch'a_ (caa3)) 八蜡祭名 (Page 9|n52 蜡 _Ch'a_ (caa3); here normalised to _Cha_ (zaa3)) To bind up, sealed up, as in winter (Kangxi: 蜡也者索也 etc.); _pat ~~ch'a~~ ``cha``_ (baat3 zaa3 八蜡) a sacrifice of thanksgiving to the earth in winter for ripening crops. (Page 596 蜡) Read _cha_ (zaa3); the sacrifice made at the winter solstice (Kangxi: 年終祭名), when the cold binds everything close (Kangxi: 蜡也者索也 etc.). The reading _cha_ (zaa3) is per 《廣韻》又音乍. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰釒⿱丿乂 to 釵, corrected 入 to 八, and corrected 士 to 土. 【蜡-voiced】 (dzraeH) (_noun-like_) winter-sacrifice

caa3.text: U+8A6B 吒 咤 (F2.76 詫) 誇也 (Page 9|n52 詫) To talk extravagantly, to vaunt (Kangxi: 誇也); to deceive (Kangxi: 誑也); _ch'a i_ (caa3 ji6 詫異) ~~strange, foolish~~ ``wonder, astonishment``. (Page 9|n52 吒咤) Used for the last (詫). Astonished.

caa4.text: U+55CF caa1 (per 《字彙》初加切) (F2.75 嗏) 唱曲長聲也 (Page 9|n52 嗏) A euphonic final sound used in chanting, to prolong the line (Fan Wan: 唱曲長聲也).

U+643D (Page 9|n52 搽) An unauthorized character. To smear, to rub in; to anoint; to rub in; _ch'a pak pi ko_ (caa4 baak6 bei6 go1 搽白鼻哥), to whiten his nose, _i.e._ to palm off, to dupe; _ch'a fa hau min_ (caa4 faa1 hau2 min6 搽花口面) to disguise one's face; _ch'a yeuk_ (caa4 joek6 搽藥) to spread a plaster; _ch'a fan_ (caa4 fan2 搽粉), to rub on cosmetics. 【搽-spread】 (caa4) To apply, rub, or spread an ointment, cream, etc. 搽牛油 (caa4 ngau4 jau4): "Spread butter." 搽藥膏 (caa4 joek6 gou1): "Apply ointment." Olden attestation for _ch'a_ (caa4) meaning "apply", "rub", etc. exists in an alternative reading of 塗 (per Zhengzhang): 《廣韻》宅加切 塗 塗飾又音徒

caa5.text: U+643D 扠 I have chosen 搽 over 扠 as the canonical form of caa5 because: It seems more plausible to me that the word is derived from smudging (with a calligraphic ink brush or finger) than from crossing out (with a pencil or biro); In terms of pitch distance, it is nicer to have two readings for 搽 in 陽平 (caa4) and 陽上 (caa5), than to have two readings for 扠 in 陰平 (caa1) and 陽上 (caa5); and The form 搽 for _ch'a_ (caa5) is attested in Eitel (1877). Wiktionary as of 2026-01-01 suggests 䵦 as the original character, but I am not convinced by this. While the classical meaning (to dirty) is reasonable, the sound is less so. Starting with 《廣韻》徐野切, if we insist on initial 邪 and final 麻 in rising tone, we get _ts'a_ and not _ch'a_. 【搽-smudge】 (caa5) To smudge or scribble out. 搽咗佢 (caa5 zo2 keoi5): "Scribble it out." 【搽𢰸】 (caa5 wo5) To ruin or spoil. 搽𢰸咗個計畫 (caa5 wo5 zo2 go3 gai3 waak6): "It's spoilt the plan."

caa4.text: U+67E5 (Page 9|n52 查) A raft, a float (Kangxi: 水中浮木); to examine, to inquire into (Kangxi: 考察也); it appears that, having learned, I have ascertained; _ch'a ch'at_ (caa4 caat3 查察) to examine, to scrutinize; _fong ch'a_ (fong2 caa4 訪查), to ask about; _ch'a kai_ (caa4 gaai1 查街), to patrol the streets; _ch'a kwo sai_ (caa4 gwo3 saai3 查過嗮) inspected them all; _ch'a shau_ (caa4 sau1 查收), I find it has been received; _ch'a ch'a la la_ (caa4 caa4 laa2 laa2 查查拉拉), idle prying into matters, tedious questioning.
U+69CE (F2.75 槎) 水中浮木 (Page 9|n52 槎) Drift-wood (Kangxi: 同查); a small raft (Kangxi: 桴也); to fell trees, to hew wood (Kangxi: 衺斫也); _shing ch'a_ (sing4 caa4 乘槎), to raft timber.
U+75C4 (Page 8 痄 _Cha_ (zaa2); here corrected to _Ch'a_ (caa4)) a severe disease (Kangxi: 痄疨病甚);

zaa3.text: U+75C4 zaa2 (per 《廣韻》側下切) (Page 8 痄 _Cha_ (zaa2); here normalised to _Cha_ (zaa3)) A running ulcer (Kangxi: 瘡不合也); _shang cha soi_ (saang1 zaa3 soi1 生痄腮), a swelling on the cheek. Williams' 生痄腮: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1).

caa4.text: U+82F4 Plants floating in water (Kangxi: 水中浮草也), or withered plants (Kangxi: 苴是草木之枯槁者 etc.). 【苴-plant】 (dzrae) (_noun-like_) floating withered-plant

zeoi1.text: U+82F4 ceoi1 (per 《廣韻》七余切 for "grass-insole" sense and per 《集韻》千余切 for "hempseed" sense) zeoi2 (per 《廣韻》子與切 for "grass-insole" sense) (F1.44 苴) 蔴無子曰苴又受賄賂曰包苴 (Page 594 苴) ~~The female~~ hemp ~~plant~~ (Kangxi: 麻也) ``or hempseed`` (Kangxi: 苴麻子也); sackcloth; a rush, a sedge used for sandals (Kangxi: 履中草); coarse, unpolished; _tsu po_ (zeoi1 bou3 苴布) mourning sackcloth; _po tsu_ (bou2 zeoi1 補苴), to correct bad composition; _tsu cheung_ (zeoi1 zoeng6 苴杖) the mourner's staff. Grass padding (Kangxi: 苴藉也). 【苴-hemp】 (tshjo) (_noun-like_) hemp, hempseed 【苴-insole】 (tshjo, tsjoX) (_noun-like_) grass-insole 【苴-padding】 (tsjo) (_noun-like_) grass-padding

caa4.text: U+8336 (F2.75 茶) 茗也華烹之可止渴 (Page 8 茶) Tea (Kangxi: 春藏葉可以爲飮, 茗也); the infusion of tea, tea; _po ch'a_ (bou1 caa4 煲茶) to make tea; _hin ch'a_ (hin3 caa4 獻茶), to hand tea; _ch'a fa_ (caa4 faa1 茶花), the Camellia; _hak ch'a_ (hak1 caa4 黑茶), black tea; _luk ch'a_ (luk6 caa4 綠茶), green tea; _kuk ch'a_ (guk6 caa4 焗茶), to draw tea; _ch'a pui_ (caa4 bui1 茶杯), a tea-cup; _ch'a u_ (caa4 wu4-2 茶壺), a tea-pot; _ch'a ~~shi~~ ``ch'i``_ (caa4 ci4 茶匙), a tea-spoon; _ch'a shun_ (caa4 syun4 茶船), a long tea saucer; _ch'au ch'a_ (caau2 caa4 炒茶), to fire tea; _tso ch'a_ (zou6 caa4 做茶), to cure tea; to set out tea; _ch'a lam_ (caa4 lam4 茶林), a tea store; _ch'a ku_ (caa4 geoi1 茶居), a tea-saloon; _to ch'a lai_ (dou2 caa4 lai4 倒茶嚟), bring tea to the visitor; _ch'a seung_ (caa4 soeng1 茶箱), a tea-caddy; _yat seung ch'a ip_ (jat1 soeng1 caa4 jip6 一箱茶葉) a box of tea; _ch'a ki_ (caa4 gei1 茶几), a teapoy; _ch'a sz'_ (caa4 si1 茶師) a tea-taster; _ch'a mut_ (caa4 mut6 茶末) tea dust. Williams' 茶匙: Changed _shi_ (si4) to common reading _ch'i_ (ci4). 【茶走】 (caa4 zau2) Milk tea but with condensed milk in place of normal milk and sugar.

caai1.text: U+640B 搓 To knead, to rub. 【搋波】 (caai1 bo1) (_ball games_) To pass the ball back and forth.

co1.text: U+6413 (F2.58 搓) 挪然也 (Page 583 搓) To twist by rubbing on the knee; to rub or roll between the palms (Kangxi: 搓挪也); _ts'o mai yat t'un_ (co1 maai4 jat1 tyun4 搓埋一團), roll it into a ball; _ts'o sin_ (co1 sin3 搓線) to twist thread; _ts'o heung_ (co1 hoeng1 搓香), to cover incense sticks by rolling; _ts'o un kam pin_ (co1 jyun4 gam6 bin2 搓圓撳扁), can be rolled or flattened; _met._ an easy obliging man; _ts'o no_ (co1 no4 搓挪), to roll and mix between the hands; rustling, flexible, like willow twigs. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 梛 to 挪.

caai1.text: U+731C May appear as either ⿰犭靑 or ⿰犭青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.25 猜) 疑也度也 (Page 11|n54 猜) To guess; to doubt, to conjecture (Kangxi: 疑也); to dislike; to abhor (Kangxi: 恨也); to fear, to apprehend, lest (Kangxi: 懼也); _ch'ai yat kuk chung_ (caai1 jat1 guk6 zung1 猜一焗盅), to bet a glass of wine; _ch'ai kwat p'ai_ (caai1 gwat1 paai4-2 猜骨牌), to guess on dominoes; _ch'ai tok_ (caai1 dok6 猜度) an opinion or idea, to guess; _ch'ai mui_ (caai1 mui4-2 猜枚), or _ch'ai ma_ (caai1 maa5 猜馬), morra, to guess at the fingers---a game; _ch'ai i_ (caai1 ji4 猜疑), to suspect, to doubt of; _ch'ai im_ (caai1 jim4 猜嫌), to suspect and dislike; _ch'ai ngam_ (caai1 ngaam1 猜啱) guessed right; _ch'ai yat ch'ai_ (caai1 jat1 caai1 猜一猜) make a guess; _ni shi ch'ai~~, ha~~ ``ha``_ (nei5 si3 caai1 haa5 你試猜吓) just guess. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ts'ai_ per 《廣韻》倉才切. Williams' 你試猜一吓: Corrected _ha_ (haa6) to _ha_ (haa5).

U+91F5 (F2.25 釵) 婦人之簪 (Page 11|n54 釵) A broad hair-pin (Kangxi: 筓屬); _met._ females; _puk ch'ai_ (buk1 caai1 卜釵), to divine by a hair-pin when a husband will return; _kw'an ch'ai_ (kwan4 caai1 群釵), a bevy of women; _king ch'ai_, (ging1 caai1 荊釵) a thorn hair-pin, _met._ poverty; _fung ch'ai_ (fung6 caai1 鳳釵), an ornamental hair-pin with a phoenix.

caai2.text: U+8E29 The character 踩 is not attested in the Middle Chinese rime books. Perhaps the pre-merger reading should be _ts'ai_ instead of _ch'ai_ based on the initial of the phonetic 采, but the syllable _ts'ai_ does not exist under any tone in Fan Wan. For the purposes of Cantonese, 踩 is indistiguishable from 踹. Note that the Mandarin readings are distinct (cai and chuai).

U+8E39 踩 cyun5 (per 《廣韻》市兖切) dyun3 (per 《廣韻》丁貫切) (F2.27 踹) 足踐踏也 (Page 11|n54 踹) Also read _tun_ (dyun3). To stamp, to trample, to tread on with the heel (Kangxi: 踹足蹀足也); to rase, to destroy; the heel (Kangxi: 足踹也, 足跟也); _ch'ai un sok_ (caai2 jyun5 sok3 踹軟索) a treader on rope ladders---a thief; _pi sai kwa p'i ni ch'ai_ (bei2 sai1 gwaa1 pei4 nei5 caai2 畀西瓜皮你踹), let you step on a melon skin---trip you up; _ch'ai p'o ying p'un_ (caai2 po3 jing4 pun4 踹破營盤), to remain victorious; _ch'ai chu k'u_ (caai2 zyu6 keoi5 踹住佢), step on it; _ki shi ngau ch'ai keuk_ (gei2 si4 ngau4 caai2 goek3 幾時牛踹腳) when is your birthday? _ch'ai shat_ (caai2 sat6 踹實) to tread on firmly; _ch'ai tang fa_ (caai2 dang1 faa1 踹燈花), to tread on lampwicks (a thief); _ch'ai shing yuk tseung_ (caai2 sing4 juk6 zoeng3 踹成肉醬) trampled to death. Williams' "Also read ...": Corrected _tun_ (dyun2) to _tun_ (dyun3). See 踩 caai2 for commentary on 踩 versus 踹.

caai3.text: U+562C saai3 (alternative per Williams) (F2.28 嘬) 齧也一舉盡臠又捐其食曰嘬 (Page 11|n54 嘬) To gnaw, to bite (Kangxi: 齧也); to eat rapidly; to swallow, to bolt; _ch'ai hut_ (caai3 hyut3 嘬血) to suck blood; _ch'ai ngit_ (caai3 ngit6 嘬齧) to eat with a noise. Also read _shai_ (saai3). 【嘬】 (tsrhwaejH) (_verb-like_) to gnaw
U+7625 (Page 583 瘥 _Ts'o_ (co1); here corrected to _Ch'ai_ (caai3)) To get convalescent, to recover (Kangxi: 疾愈也, 瘥癒也);

co4.text: U+7625 co1 (per Williams) ze1 (alternative per 《廣韻》子邪切) (Page 583 瘥 _Ts'o_ (co1); here normalised to _Ts'o_ (co4)) a slight ailment (Kangxi: 病也), slight epidemic, like influenza. 【瘥】 (dza, tsjae) (_noun-like_) ailment, (_met._) plague

zaai3.text: U+7635 caai3 (per Fan Wan) (F2.28 瘵 _Ch'ai_ (caai3)) 勞病 (Page 11|n54 瘵 _Ch'ai_ (caai3); here normalised to _Chai_ (zaai3)) Weary, sick (Kangxi: 勞病也, 病也); _lo ~~ch'ai~~ ``chai``_ (lou4 zaai3 癆瘵) consumption, a wasting disease; _tsz' ~~ch'ai~~ ``chai``_ (zi6 zaai3 自瘵) to bring trouble on one's self. 【瘵】 (tsreajH) (_noun-like_) distress (_noun-like_) disease, wasting-disease

caai3.text: U+8806 (F2.28 蠆) 蜂毒也 (Page 11|n54 蠆) The sting of a bee, or other insect; a sort of scorpion with a long tail (Kangxi: 螫蟲). 【蠆】 (trhaejH) (_noun-like_) scorpion, sting

caai4.text: U+5115 (F2.26 儕) 等也輩也 (Page 11|n54 儕) A class, a company (Kangxi: 等輩也); persons of the same sort; it makes the plural of the first person; _'ng ch'ai_ (ng4 caai4 吾儕), we. 【儕】 (dzreaj) (_noun-like_) class, compeer

U+67F4 (F2.26 柴) 薪也 (Page 11|n54 柴) Firewood, faggots; brushwood (Kangxi: 小木散材); fuel; to stop up (Kangxi: 塞也); _yat pa ch'ai_ (jat1 baa2 caai4 一把柴), a faggot of firewood; _ch'ai t'au_ (caai4 tau4 柴頭), half burned sticks; _kon ch'ai pang fo_ (gon1 caai4 bang6 fo2 乾柴憑火), if dry wood is near the fire, will it not catch? _ch'ai fa_ (caai4 faa1 柴花), kindlings; _ch'ai fo_ (caai4 fo2 柴火), firewood; _p'o ch'ai_ (po3 caai4 破柴), to cleave wood; _ch'ai mun_ (caai4 mun4 柴門), a cottage door; _p'au ch'ai_ (paau4 caai4 刨柴), shavings; _ch'ai san_ (caai4 san1 柴薪), faggots of wood. To burn firewood (for sacrifice) (Kangxi: 燔柴曰柴). To protect (Kangxi: 護也). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【柴】 (dzrea) (_noun-like_) brushwood, firewood

U+8C7A (F2.26 豺) 狼屬 (Page 11|n54 豺) A ravenous beast (Kangxi: 豺貪殘之獸); a lynx or wolf (Kangxi: 狼屬); _ch'ai long tong to_ (caai4 long4 dong1 dou6 豺狼當道) the wolf is in the way---said of oppressive rulers; _ch'ai long_ (caai4 long4 豺狼), a wolf; _ch'ai long sing ts'ing_ (caai4 long4 sing3 cing4 豺狼性情), a wolfish disposition. 【豺】 (dzreaj) (_noun-like_) dhole Used in 豺狼. 【豺狼】 (dzreaj lang) (_noun-like_) wolf

cak1.text: U+60FB caak1 (longer syllable) (F2.32 惻) 隱傷也痛也 (Page 12|n55 惻) Secret grief, distress (Kangxi: 痛也); to pity, to commisserate, to sympathize; _ch'ak yan chi sam_ (cak1 jan2 zi1 sam1 惻隱之心), kind-hearted, humane. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𭙡 to 痛. Williams' 惻隱之心: Corrected _yan_ (jan4) to _yan_ (jan2). 【惻】 (tsrhik) (_verb-like_) to compassion

U+6E2C caak1 (longer syllable) (F2.32 測) 度也 (Page 12|n55 測) To sound, to fathom (Kangxi: 度深曰測); to estimate, to measure (Kangxi: 凡測度之稱); clear, as varnish (Kangxi: 淸也 etc.); _ch'ak tok_ (cak1 dok6 測度) to comprehend, to conjecture; _ch'ak tsz'_ (cak1 zi6 測字) to dissect characters, as when divining; _pat ch'ak_ (bat1 cak1 不測) unexpected; _t'ui ch'ak_ (teoi1 cak1 推測) to explain, to reason; _pat ho ch'ak_ (bat1 ho2 cak1 不可測) incomprehensible. Williams' 推測: Corrected _t'ui_ to _t'ui_. Extreme depth (Kangxi: 深所至也). Deep. 【測】 (tsrhik) (_verb-like_) to fathom

caak3.text: U+518A 册 策 (for "bamboo-slip" sense) (F2.89 冊) 排門冊黃冊又皇后初立曰冊立 (Page 14|n57 册; here normalised to 冊) A slip; an inventory, a register, a list; a patent or commission of nobility; a census book; to choose; to plan, to make a plan or scheme; _t'in ch'ak_ (tin4 caak3 填冊) to enrol, to write a list; _u hau ch'ak_ (wu6 hau2 caak3 戶口冊) a register, a census of people; _p'ai mun ch'ak_ (paai4 mun4 caak3 排門冊) the register of families; _wong ch'ak_ (wong4 caak3 黃冊) imperial census. 【冊】 (tsrheak) (_noun-like_) slip, register, book

U+577C 拆 (Page 14|n57 坼) To rive, to crack (Kangxi: 裂也); to open, to burst open, as buds (Kangxi: 植物房初開也); split, riven, cracked; _kap ch'ak_ (gaap3 caak3 甲坼) bursting of buds; _hom ch'ak_ (ham2 caak3 砍坼) cracked, as a bowl; _ch'ak ch'ak ti sheng_ (caak3 caak3 dei6-2 seng1 坼坼地聲), a wheezing, cracked voice. (Page 14|n57 拆; here normalised to 坼) (...) _ch'ak hau yat_ (caak3 hau2 jat6-2 坼口日) unlucky days for consulting; _ch'ak hau ko yeuk_ (caak3 hau2 gou1 joek6 坼口膏藥) plasters for chapped feet. 【坼】 (trhaek) (_verb-like_) to rive, to crack 【爆坼】 (baau3 caak3) Chapped. 皮膚爆嗮坼 (pei4 fu1 baau3 saai3 caak3): "The skin's all chapped." Olden attestation for 坼 _ch'ak_ (caak3) meaning "cracked": 《康熙字典》坼 《唐韻》丑格切《集韻》《韻會》《正韻》恥格切𠀤同𤖴裂也 《禮月令》仲冬地始坼 《後漢安帝紀》日南地坼長百餘里 《晉史天文志》惠帝中張華爲司空三台星坼...

U+62C6 (F2.90 拆) 分開也又拆卸 (Page 14|n57 拆) To break up or open (Kangxi: 裂也開也); to split, to unrip; to destroy (Kangxi: 毀也), to pull down, to take away; to dissect, to take to pieces; _ch'ak she_ (caak3 se3 拆舍) to pull a house down; _ch'ak san_ (caak3 saan3 拆散) to scatter, by breaking or taking to pieces; _ch'ak fung_ (caak3 fung1 拆封), to open a letter; _ch'ak i sheung_ (caak3 ji1 soeng4 拆衣裳), to unrip clothes; _ch'ak sai_ (caak3 saai3 拆嗮) pulled to pieces; _yau mat ch'ak fat_ (jau5 mat1 caak3 faat3 有乜拆法) what way shall we arrange the matter? Williams' 拆散: Note the subtle difference between the two readings _ch'ak san_ (caak3 saan3) "scatter" and _ch'ak san_ (caak3 saan2) "break up". 【拆】 (trhaek) (_verb-like_) to dismantle, to open (up)

U+67F5 栅 (F2.90 柵) 營寨編木爲之 (Page 14|n57 栅; here normalised to 柵) Posts of a stockade (Kangxi 柵: 編樹木也, 豎木以立柵也); a railing; a palisade; _hon ch'ak_ (hon1 caak3 看柵) a guard at gates; _ch'ak lan_ (caak3 laan4 柵欄), gateway of streets; _mun ch'ak_ (mun4 caak3 門柵) door posts.

U+7B56 (F2.89 策) 簡策謀策籌策 (Page 13|n56 策) A bamboo slip (Kangxi: 策簡也), such as books were once engraved on; a book, writings; a stratagem, a plan, a means, an expedient, a scheme (Kangxi: 籌也謀也); a switch, a whip (Kangxi: 馬箠); a divining slip; to switch; _kan ch'ak_ (gaan2 caak3 簡策) books, chapters, &c.; _mo ch'ak_ (mou4 caak3 無策) without plan, schemeless; _ch'au ch'ak_ (cau4 caak3 籌策) to contrive; _ch'ak ma_ (caak3 maa5 策馬), to whip a horse. ~~Used for the next (茦).~~ Yarrow for divination (Kangxi: 蓍也, 策神蓍也). 【策】 (tsrheak) (_noun-like_) slip (_noun-like_) strategy, scheme (_noun-like_) whip (_noun-like_) yarrow-stalk
U+8326 (Page 14|n57 茦) A thorn, a spike, a prickle (Kangxi: 莿也, 茦刺); to prick, to pierce with a thorn.

caak6.text: U+8808 賊 (F2.33 蠈) 蟊蠈食禾之虫也 (Page 551 賊; here normalised to 蠈) a fly in grain (Kangxi 賊: 害苗之蟲); (Page 551 蠈) A fly which eats the grain at its joints (Kangxi: 食禾節蟲); a sort of Hessian fly, commonly called _wong ch'ung_ (wong4 cung4 黃蟲) or the yellow bug.

U+8CCA caak3 (vernacular) caak6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.33 賊) 盗賊又賊害 (Page 551 賊) A thief, a robber, a highwayman (Kangxi: 盜也); a bandit; seditious, those who rise against the government; the enemy; a term of contempt; to rob (Kangxi: 刧人也), to murder (Kangxi: 殺人曰賊); to maltreat, to oppress (Kangxi: 賊虐); _lo ts'ak_ (lou5 caak6-2 老賊) an old robber; _ts'ak kom ngan_ (caak6 gam2 ngaan5 賊噉眼), eyes sharp as a thief's; _ts'ak ping_ (caak6 bing1 賊兵), the enemy's force; _hoi ts'ak_ (hoi2 caak6 海賊) a pirate, corsair, freebooter; _ts'ak t'au_ (caak6 tau4-2 賊頭), a ringleader of robbers; _ts'ak tsai_ (caak6-2 zai2 賊仔), a rascal, a pilferer; _tso ts'ak_ (zou6 caak6-2 做賊) to turn robber; _ts'ak chong_ (caak6 zong1 賊贓), booty; _ts'ak ying_ (caak6-2 jing4 賊型), a suspicious looking fellow. To injure (Kangxi: 賊傷害也). 【賊】 (dzok) (_verb-like_) to injure (_noun-like_) bandit, renegade
U+9C02 Used in 鰞鰂 (cuttlefish) (Kangxi: 鰞鰂魚名, 一名墨魚). Note that in Cantonese, the synonym 墨魚 "inkfish" is preferred.

zak1.text: U+9C02 鯽 (Page 551 鯽; here normalised to 鰂) A species of bream _(Cyprinus gibelioides)_ with a long dorsal, called _tsak na u_ (zak1 naa2 jyu4-2 鰂乸魚); _shuk kwat tsak_ (suk1 gwat1 zak1 縮骨鰂) the blunt headed bream _(Cyprinus abbreviatus)_; _kam tsak_ (gam1 zak1 金鰂) the red tailed bream _(Cyprinus auratus)_. Used in 鰂魚涌 Quarry Bay, in Hong Kong. Williams' 鰂乸魚: Corrected _na_ (naa5) to _na_ (naa2).

caam1.text: U+53C3 叅 (F2.62 參) 參差不齊又干預也謀度也又音深 (F2.62 叅) 叅考叅拜上官論下官亦曰叅 (Page 553 參~~？~~``叅``) To be concerned with (Kangxi 參: 干與也), to join with for consultation (Kangxi 參: 相謁也), to advise, to give counsel to; to blend, to mix, to form one of three (Kangxi 參: 三相參爲參); to have an audience, to salute, to see one (Kangxi 參: 覲也); to impeach, to report to the throne on other officers; confused, mixed, as colors; a deposition, an impeachment; _ts'am tsau_ (caam1 zau3 參奏) to memorialize against; _ta ts'am_ (daa2 caam1 打參), to make an obeisance; _ts'am pai_ (caam1 baai3 參拜) to visit one; to worship; _ts'am ts'am ts'z' ts'z'_ (caam1 caam1 ci1 ci1 參參差差) uneven, not well ranked, unassorted; _ts'am kak_ (caam1 gaak3 參革) to degrade an officer and report it; _ts'am tsap_ (caam1 zaap6 參雜) to throw into confusion; _ts'am tseung_ (caam1 zoeng3 參將) a colonel; _ts'am ts'eung_ (caam1 coeng4 參詳), to consult upon; _ch'iu ts'am_ (ciu4 caam1 朝參), admitted to a levee; to worship the Supreme; _ts'am ting mo ngo_ (caam1 ding3 mou4 ngo4 參訂無訛), I've examined it and found no mistakes. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰阝頁 to 預. 【參】 (tshom) (_verb-like_) to form three, to partake (in) (_verb-like_) to pay visit (unto) (_verb-like_) to consult, to examine (_verb-like_) to impeach Used in 參差. 【參差】 (tsrhim tsrhje) (_adjective-like_) uneven
U+53C5 (F2.62 參) 參差不齊又干預也謀度也又音深 (F2.62 叅) 叅考叅拜上官論下官亦曰叅 (Page 553 參~~？~~``叅``)

U+6519 caam4 (per 《廣韻》士咸切 for "stab" sense) (Page 16|n59 攙 _Ch'am_ (caam4); here normalised to _Ch'am_ (caam1)) To stab (Kangxi: 刺也); to sustain, to support (Kangxi: 一曰扶也), to lead; to divide with another; _ch'am fan lai t'im_ (caam1 fan6 lai4 tim1 攙分嚟添), make another share for him; _ch'am fu_ (caam1 fu4 攙扶), to uphold. Whilst _ch'am_ (caam4) is attested in Middle Chinese, it is only explained "stab" (刺也). Kangxi records the canonical reading 楚銜切 _ch'am_ (caam1), explained both "stab" (刺也) and "arm-support" (一曰扶也). It is possible that Williams has mistakenly parsed 楚銜切 as 陽平 rather than 陰平.

zaam6.text: U+6519 (Page 16|n59 攙 _Ch'am_ (caam4); here corrected to _Cham_ (zaam6)) to supply a want, to make up (Kangxi: 完補也); to pull out (Kangxi: 一曰旁掣也);

caam1.text: U+7BF8 (Page 554 篸 _Ts'am_ (caam2); here corrected to _Ts'am_ (caam1)) Bamboos varying in length (Kangxi: 篸𥰭竹貌); a pandean pipe (Kangxi: 洞簫也); Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_; 《集韻》 has the reading 初簪切, explained 篸差竹貌. Kangxi spells this as 篸𥰭, making it the bamboo version of 參差.

caam2.text: U+7BF8 (F2.64 ？) 竹器 (F2.64 篸) 同上 (Page 554 篸) an open basket like a fan; _fat ts'am_ (fat1 caam2 㧾篸), a basket scuttle; _fui ts'am_ (fui1 caam2 灰篸), a mortar-hod; _kuk tau ts'am_ (guk1 dau1 caam2 榖兜篸), a grain fan; _lap sap ts'am_ (laap6 saap3 caam2 垃圾篸), a dirt basket.

caam1.text: U+9A42 (F2.62 驂) 駕車外兩馬曰驂馬 (Page 553 驂) Three horses abreast in a chariot (Kangxi: 驂馬, 駕三馬也); the horses outside of the thills (Kangxi: 車中兩馬曰服兩馬驂其外小退曰驂); _ts'am shing_ (caam1 sing6 驂乘) three sitters in a carriage; _t'ut ts'am i tsang_ (tyut3 caam1 ji4 zang6 脫驂而贈) he unhitched the wheelhorse and gave it to him. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿰馬叅 to 驂. 【驂】 (tshom) (_noun-like_) outside-horse

caam2.text: U+6158 𢡖 (F2.64 𢡖) 酷毒也愁戚也甚也 (F2.64 慘) 同上 (Page 554 慘𢡖) Cruel, hard-hearted, inhuman (Kangxi 慘: 毒也); to be afflicted, injured, wounded in the mind (Kangxi 慘: 痛也); a superlative, applied to sufferings; _pi ts'am_ (bei1 caam2 悲慘), extreme grief; _sheung tak ts'am_ (soeng1 dak1 caam2 傷得慘), horribly wounded; _ts'am huk_ (caam2 huk6 慘酷) unfeeling, callous; _ho ts'am_ (hou2 caam2 好慘), very shocking; _ts'am hak_ (caam2 hak1 慘刻) oppressive, fleecing the people; _ts'am tso wang wo_ (caam2 zou1 waang4 wo6 慘遭橫禍) to meet with cruelty and misfortunes.

U+61AF (F2.64 憯) 痛也 (Page 554 憯) Pained, feeling acutely, grieved; distressed; sickened from former sufferings; already. The Kangxi entry is simply 《正字通》俗？字, where the illegible character is one of ⿰忄⿱⿰兂兂曰 or 㦧, neither of which I can locate.
U+22856 (F2.64 𢡖) 酷毒也愁戚也甚也 (F2.64 慘) 同上 (Page 554 慘𢡖)
U+647B saam2 (per 《廣韻》所斬切) (F2.64 摻) 羅而取之 (Page 554 摻) to take up with the hand (Kangxi: 取也一曰執也). 【摻】 (sreamX) (_verb-like_) to take (hold)

cim1.text: U+647B saam1 (per 《廣韻》所咸切) (F2.47 摻) 與纖同又音覽 (Page 554 摻 _Ts'am_ (caam2); here corrected to _Ts'im_ (cim1)) A delicate hand (Kangxi: 女手貌), a hand with tapering fingers; fine, tapering, small; The reading _ts'im_ (cim1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 思廉切, explained 摻摻女好手貌.

caam3.text: U+5133 caam4 (per 《廣韻》士咸切 for "ugly" sense and Kangxi 士咸切 for "quick" sense) zaam6 (per 《廣韻》仕陷切 for "imprudent-speech" sense and 《集韻》仕懺切 for "flippant" sense) (F2.64 儳) 儳越叅雜也 (Page 16|n59 儳) Irregular, disorderly (Kangxi: 儳互不齊也), as undrilled troops (Kangxi: 師次不整); ~~unequal,~~ uneven (Kangxi: 亦不齊也); _lam ch'am_ (laam4 caam3 儖儳) ugly (Kangxi: 惡貌); confused, disorderly~~, sulky~~. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 叅 and 雜. Williams' "Irregular, disorderly, as undrilled troops": Kangxi has this sense under 士咸切 _ch'am_ (caam4). Still, the reading _ch'am_ (caam3) for "disorderly" is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 叉鑑切, explained 儳仾不齊也. Kangxi paraphrases this as 又《集韻》初鑒切音懺亦不齊也又雜言也. Quick (Kangxi: 疾也). Imprudent speech, words spoken without due consideration (Kangxi: 輕言也). Flippant, unserious (Kangxi: 謂苟且不整肅也, 可輕賤之貌). Confused speech (Kangxi: 雜言也). 【儳-flippant】 (dzreamH) (_adjective-like_) flippant 【儳-jumbled】 (tsrhaemH) (_adjective-like_) jumbled
U+61FA (F2.64 杉) 懺悔自陳其悔也 (Page 16|n59 懺) To repent (Kangxi: 悔也); to confess one's errors; _ch'am fui_ (caam3 fui3 懺悔) to repent.

U+6749 saam1 (per 《廣韻》所咸切) (F2.64 杉) 木名 (Page 16|n59 杉) Fir, pine (Kangxi: 煔似松); _ch'am muk_ (caam3 muk6 杉木) deal; _yat luk ch'am_ (jat1 luk1 caam3 一碌杉) a block of pine, a blockhead; _ch'am muk ling p'ai_ (caam3 muk6 ling4 paai4 杉木靈牌), a pine tablet---is worthless. Williams 一碌杉: Corrected _luk_ (luk6) to _luk_ (luk1).

caam4.text: U+5296 (F2.63 劖) 錐也刺也 (Page 553 劖) To cut apart, to chop (Kangxi: 斷也); to chissel off (Kangxi: 劖鑿也); to cut with a burin; _ts'am to_ (caam4 dou1 劖刀), a beveled knife or chissel. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_ per 《廣韻》鋤銜切.

U+5DC9 嶃 漸 磛 𥕌 zaam6 (alternative per 《廣韻》仕檻切) (F2.63 巉) 高危也 (F2.63 磛嶃) 俱同上 (Page 554 巉𥕌) A high, inaccessible peak or cliff (Kangxi 巉: 巉巖高也); a cloud-capt summit; _ts'am ngam_ (caam4 ngaam4 巉巖), a lofty, craggy summit; also high piled up rocks. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_ per 《廣韻》鋤銜切. 【巉】 (dzraem, dzraemX) (_adjective-like_) precipitous
U+6159 (F2.63 慚) 愧也作也 (F2.63 慙) 同上 (Page 554 慚慙)

U+615A 慙 (F2.63 慚) 愧也作也 (F2.63 慙) 同上 (Page 554 慚慙) Ashamed, mortified, chopfallen (Kangxi 慙: 媿也); to feel ashamed of, to blush for; to be sensible of one's defects or failure; _sau ts'am_ (sau1 caam4 羞慚), to redden from mortification; _tai in pat ts'am_ (daai6 jin4 bat1 caam4 大言不慚), he does not blush for a lie, he has no modesty; _ts'am tak_ (caam4 dak1 慚德) conscious of one's defects. 【慚】 (dzam) (_adjective-like_) ashamed

U+6BDA (F2.63 毚) 狡兔也 (Page 554 毚) Crafty, wily, artful, as a hare (Kangxi: 狡兔也), which has many ways of escape; _ts'am t'o_ (caam4 tou3 毚兔) a crafty rabbit. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_ per 《廣韻》士咸切 and 鋤銜切. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 免 to 兔. 【毚】 (dzream, dzraem) (_adjective-like_) wily
U+6F38 Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_ per 《集韻》鋤銜切.

zim1.text: U+6F38 (F2.50 漸 _Tsim_ (zim6)) 次也徐也稍也漬也 (Page 571 漸 _Tsim_ (zim6); here corrected to _Tsim_ (zim1)) to find its way in, as water does (Kangxi: 流入也); to flow; to imbibe, to tinge, to soak, to penetrate (Kangxi: 漬也濕也, 浸也染也); 【漸-level】 (tsjem) (_verb-like_) to inflow (_verb-like_) to soak

zim6.text: U+6F38 zim6-2 (rising second in reduplication 漸漸) (F2.50 漸) 次也徐也稍也漬也 (Page 571 漸) Name of an affluent of the Yangtsz' kiang (揚子江) near Nganking fu (安慶府) (Kangxi: 水名, 水出丹陽黟南蠻中東入海); to advance by degrees, slowly, by little and little (Kangxi: 漸次也進也稍也 etc.); stealthily, as decay; _tsim tsim loi_ (zim6 zim6 loi4 漸漸來), came on gradually; _tsim yap kai king_ (zim6 jap6 gaai1 ging2 漸入佳境), opens up a fine prospect; gradually improving, as one's prospects; _tsim ts'z'_ (zim6 ci3 漸次) many times, gradually; _ts'un tsu tsim tsun_ (ceon4 zeoi6 zim6 zeon3 循序漸進) to follow on regularly, to advance in order. 【漸-rising】 (dzjemX) (_adverb-like_) gradually (_verb-like_) to proceed gradually, to graduate, (_met._) to increase

caam4.text: U+2554C (F2.63 巉) 高危也 (F2.63 磛嶃) 俱同上 (Page 554 巉𥕌)
U+8695 (F2.63 蠶) 吐絲之虫 (F2.63 蚕) 同上 (Page 553 蠶蚕)

U+8836 蚕 (F2.63 蠶) 吐絲之虫 (F2.63 蚕) 同上 (Page 553 蠶蚕) The silkworm (Kangxi 蠶: 絲蟲也); caterpillars which weave coccoons (Kangxi 蠶: 任絲也); _ts'am ch'ung_ (caam4 cung4-2 蠶蟲), the silk-worm; _ts'am ch'ung kon_ (caam4 cung4-2 gon1 蠶蟲乾), dried silkworms used for food; _ts'am ku_ (caam4 gu1 蠶姑), goddess of silkgrowers; _ts'am hau_ (caam4 hau2 蠶口), silken, silk fabrics; _ts'am shik_ (caam4 sik6 蠶食) to gnaw like a silkworm, to incroach on other's domains; _ts'am kan_ (caam4 gaan2 蠶繭), a coccoon. 【蠶】 (dzom) (_noun-like_) silkworm

U+8B92 (F2.63 讒) 讒佞也毀譖良善曰讒 (Page 554 讒) ~~To adulate, to humor, to flatter;~~ to misrepresent, to gloss over, to calumniate, to asperse, to traduce, to detract (Kangxi: 譖也, 崇飾惡言毀善害能也, 傷良曰讒); ~~servility;~~ detraction, malignity; _ts'am ning_ (caam4 ning6 讒佞) to cajole and asperse; _ts'am ~~chim~~ ``ch'im``_ (caam4 cim2 讒諂), to intrigue against, to slander; _ts'am yan_ (caam4 jan4 讒人), a backbiter; _ts'am in ye wo_ (caam4 jin4 je5 wo6 讒言惹禍) calumnies induce troubles; _ts'am shan_ (caam4 san4 讒臣), an intriguing officer; _ts'am hau kau ka_ (caam4 hau2 gaau1 gaa1 讒口交加), he is everywhere spoken against. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_ per 《廣韻》士咸切. Williams' "To adulate, to humor, to flatter": Kangxi 佞也 is unhelpful. Williams' 讒諂: Corrected _chim_ (zim2) to _ch'im_ (cim2). 【讒】 (dzream) (_noun-like_) calumny

U+995E (F2.63 饞) 貪饕也 (Page 554 饞) To gormandise, to be fond of eating; greedy, gluttonous, voracious (Kangxi: 不廉, 饕也); _hau ts'am_ (hau2 caam4 口饞), omniverous. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'am_ per 《廣韻》士咸切.

caam5.text: U+68D6 caan5 (per Williams) (Page 20|n63 棖 _Ch'an_ (caan5); here normalised to _Ch'am_ (caam5)) A colloquial word. A door sill; a threshold; _k'i mun ~~ch'an~~ ``ch'am``_ (kei5 mun4 caam5 企門棖), standing in the door-way. 【門棖】 (mun4 caam5) A doorsill.

caang4.text: U+68D6 (F2.88 棖) 兩邊門枋曰棖又人名申棖 A door-post (Kangxi: 棖謂之楔, 棖門兩旁長木). 【棖】 (draeng) (_noun-like_) door-post

caam5.text: U+93E8 劖 (for vernacular "pierce" sense) zaam6 (alternative per 《廣韻》藏濫切) (F2.64 鏨) 鉄鑿 (Page 553 鏨 _Tsam_ (zaam5); here corrected to _Ts'am_ (caam5)) A stone-cutter's chissel; a small cold chissel (Kangxi: 小鑿也); to pierce, as a thorn; to cut in, to cut or chissel out; to carve, as flowers; _~~tsam~~ ``ts'am`` fa_ (caam5 faa1 鏨花), to carve flowers; _~~tsam~~ ``ts'am`` to_ (caam5 dou1 鏨刀), a stone chissel; _~~tsam~~ ``ts'am`` hoi pin_ (caam5 hoi1 bin1 鏨開邊), to split open an edge; _pi lak ~~tsam~~ ``ts'am``_ (bei2 lak6-1 caam5 畀竻鏨), the thorn pierced me. Whilst Fan Wan does not explicitly record the reading of 鏨 (as is the case for most entries), the tone at the very least can be inferred based on the ordering with respect to other entries. Because 鏨 appears in the rising tone section, and strictly after 攬覽擥, which are 陽上 (tone 5), we conclude that 鏨 also is 陽上 (tone 5). Williams' placement of 鏨 under _Tsam_ (zaam5) is problematic as an unaspirated initial cannot occur with 陽上 (tone 5). I have corrected this to the aspirated _Ts'am_ (caam5), because: It is consistent with the vernacular _ts'am_ (caam5) for "pierced"; and It is consistent with 《廣韻》才敢切 if aspirated rising _ts'am_ (caam5) is not recast to unaspirated departing _tsam_ (zaam6). 【鏨】 (caam5) To pierce, especially in a wedging or chisel-like fashion. 鏨刺 (caam5 ci3): "Pierced by a thorn."

caan1.text: U+98E1 (F2.67 餐) 熟食也 (F2.67 飡) 同上 (Page 555 餐飡)

U+9910 飡 (F2.67 餐) 熟食也 (F2.67 飡) 同上 (Page 555 餐飡) To swallow (Kangxi 餐: 吞也), to eat; a meal (Kangxi 餐: 飮饌曰餐); a classifier of meals; to gather and to select (Kangxi 餐: 採也); _yat ts'an fan_ (jat1 caan1 faan6 一餐飯) a meal; _tso ts'an_ (zou2 caan1 早餐) breakfast; _tai ts'an_ (daai6 caan1 大餐), dinner; _man ts'an_ (maan5 caan1 晚餐) supper; _yat shik sam ts'an_ (jat6 sik6 saam1 caan1 日食三餐), three meals a day; _ts'an t'au fan_ (caan1 tau4 faan6 餐頭飯) a common meal; _chan ts'an shik_ (zaan6 caan1 sik6 賺餐食) works for his food; _sau shik hom ts'an_ (sau3 sik1 ham1 caan1 秀色堪餐), handsome enongh to be eaten; _pau ts'an_ (baau2 caan1 飽餐), a good meal; _ts'an wan min ut_ (caan1 wan4 min4 jyut6 餐雲眠月) dines in the clouds and sleeps on the moon --- an enthusiast; _ch'i fan ts'an_ (ci1 faan6 caan1 黐飯餐), a diner out; _ts'an chuk ts'an fan_ (caan1 zuk1 caan1 faan6 餐粥餐飯) a meal of congee and of rice --- poor. Williams' 大餐: Dated; 大餐 now simply means a big meal, or feast. Williams' 黐飯餐: A diner out in the sense of freeloading food. 【餐】 (tshan) (_verb-like_) to swallow, to consume (_noun-like_) meal

caan2.text: U+5257 (F2.69 剷) 削平也 (F2.69 剗) 同上 (Page 20|n63 剷剗)

zin2.text: U+5257 (Page 20|n63 ~~剷~~剗 _Ch'an_ (caan2); here corrected to _Chin_ (zin2)) to reap (Kangxi 剗: 刈也);

caan2.text: U+5277 剗 鏟 (F2.69 剷) 削平也 (F2.69 剗) 同上 (Page 20|n63 剷剗) Interchanged with the next (鏟). To level off (Kangxi 剗: 削也, 平也), to spade up; to trim iron; _ch'an t'ai to_ (caan2 tai3 dou1 剷剃刀), to plane a razor; _hom ka ch'an_ (ham6 gaa1 caan2 冚家剷), may your race all be cut off; _ch'an heung_ (caan2 hoeng1 剷香), sandalwood shavings burned for the dead. 【剷】 (caan2) To delete, especially permanently. 剷咗佢 (caan2 zo2 keoi5): "Delete it." 【冚家剷】 (ham6 gaa1 caan2) A curse that someone's entire family be annihilated.
U+5D7C (F2.69 嵼) 𡾰嵼山屈曲貌 (Page 20|n63 嵼) Tortuous paths among the mountains (Kangxi: 𡾰嵼山屈曲貌); _kin ch'an_ (gin2 caan2 𡾰嵼), winding, crooked, as paths.

U+7522 saan2 (per 《廣韻》所簡切) (F2.69 產) 生業也又生產 (Page 20|n63 產) To produce, to grow (Kangxi: 生也); to bear, to increase; an estate, a patrimony; an occupation (Kangxi: 民業曰產); the productions of a country (Kangxi: 本其所生長之地曰產); natives; a sort of flageolet (Kangxi: 大籥似笛三孔而短); a birth, act of parturition (Kangxi: 婦生子曰產); _po ch'an_ (bou2 caan2 保產), a strengthening pill`` for pregnancy``; _siu ch'an_ (siu2 caan2 小產), ~~an~~ ``a spontaneous`` abortion; _fan ch'an_ (fan1 caan2 分產) to divide the patrimony; _shang ch'an_ (sang1 caan2 生產), an increase, to have a child; _ch'an ip_ (caan2 jip6 產業) one's property, real estate; _ka ch'an_ (gaa1 caan2 家產), family possessions; _t'o ch'an_ (tou2 caan2 土產), ``native ``productions; _k'ing ka pai ch'an_ (king1 gaa1 baai6 caan2 傾家敗產) the family lost everything. Williams' 小產: Now called a miscarriage. 【產】 (sreanX) (_verb-like_) to produce, to bear (_noun-like_) property

U+93DF 剷 (F2.69 鏟) 平木之器 (Page 20|n63 鏟) A thin iron plate (Kangxi: 鏶也); a shovel, a plane; a spoke-shave, an iron shave (Kangxi: 一曰平䥫, 平木鐵器); to cut and pare (Kangxi: 損削也); to level; _wok ch'an_ (wok6 caan2 鑊鏟), a rice shovel; _yat fu ch'an k'im_ (jat1 fu3 caan2 kim4 一副鏟鉗), a set of shovel and tongs; _ch'an ts'o ch'u kan_ (caan2 cou2 ceoi4 gan1 鏟草除根), to root out, to extirpate; _ch'an shan_ (caan2 saan1 鏟山), to scarp hills, to dig in a level. 【煙鏟】 (jin1 caan2) A heavy smoker. 正煙鏟 (zing3 jin1 caan2): "He's a right smokaholic."

caan3.text: U+71E6 (F2.70 燦) 光明也 (Page 556 燦) Resplendent, clear (Kangxi: 燦爛明瀞貌); attractive, bright, gay; _ts'an lan_ (caan3 laan6 燦爛) lustrous, brilliant; _ts'an t'au_ (caan3 tau4 燦頭), you're a bright one! The (now) derogatory sense of "immigrant from the Mainland" is ultimately traced to the 1979 TVB Series 網中人 (''The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly''). For an in-depth discussion on the term 阿燦, see the Chinese Wikipedia article. 【阿燦】 (aa3 caan3) (_derogatory_) An immigrant into Hong Kong from Mainland China.
U+74A8 (F2.70 璨) 玉名 (Page 556 璨) The lustre of gem, a bright gem (Kangxi: 玉光也); _ts'ui ts'an_ (ceoi1 caan3 璀璨) pendent gems, said of hanging flowers like the Vanda. Beautiful jade (Kangxi: 美玉).

U+7CB2 (F2.70 粲) 美也 (Page 556 粲) ~~Half a _tau_ (dau2 斗) or peck;~~ a meal (Kangxi: 餐也); many, much, a multitude (Kangxi: 衆意); clear, bright (Kangxi: 察也明也, 光明也); to agree upon; fine, excellent (Kangxi: 優也); beautiful, as a woman; to laugh, smiling (Kangxi: 笑貌); fine rice (Kangxi: 精鑿食也); _pak ts'an_ (baak6 caan3 白粲) white rice, anciently picked over by criminals (Kangxi: 漢𠛬有白粲 etc.); _ts'an ts'an_ (caan3 caan3 粲粲) elegant, as dress; _ts'an in i siu_ (caan3 jin4 ji4 siu3 粲然而笑) he laughed outright; _ts'an t'au_ (caan3 tau4-2 粲頭), a worthless fellow --- a colloquial phrase. Williams' "a meal": According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, this sense is an alternative form of 餐, rather than just a synonym (鄭風傳曰粲餐也此謂粲爲餐之叚借也). A corollary of this view is that the reading ought to be level, rather than departing. Williams' "Half a _tau_ or peck": This is a misreading of Kangxi 爲米六斗大半斗曰粲, which puts the measure at 6⅔ pecks, not half a peck. 【粲】 (tshanH) (_noun-like_) polished-grain (_adjective-like_) splendid, beaming

zin1.text: U+6214 caan4 (per 《廣韻》昨干切) (F1.20 戔) 戔戔淺小之貌 (Page 573 戔) Small, narrow, cramped (Kangxi: 戔戔淺小之意); contracted, as one's mind. The reading _tsin_ (zin1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 將先切, explained 戔戔猥積皃 (cramped?). The reading _ts'an_ (caan4) is the sole reading that appears in 《廣韻》, explained 傷也 (the sense interchangeable with 殘) and 戔戔束帛皃易曰束帛戔戔 (bundley? in the Classic of Changes). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 少 to 小. 【戔】 (dzan, tsen) (_adjective-like_) slender, cramped

caan4.text: U+6B98 戔 caan4-2 (rising second in vernacular 殘殘地) (F2.68 殘) 傷殘餘也落也賤也害也 (Page 556 殘) To ruin, to destroy (Kangxi: 賊也); to injure, to spoil; to mangle, to tread on (Kangxi: 殘踐也); to kill, to slaughter (Kangxi: 殺也); mischievous, wicked, cruel (Kangxi: 惡也); spoiled, pillaged; withered; broken food, leavings (Kangxi: 食餘也); deficient (Kangxi: 殘缺也), nearly used up, ready to vanish away; _ts'an hoi_ (caan4 hoi6 殘害) truculent, savage; _shau tsuk seung ts'an_ (sau2 zuk1 soeng1 caan4 手足相殘), fraternal strifes; _ts'an fa_ (caan4 faa1 殘花), a withered flower---a prostitute; _ts'an nin_ (caan4 nin4 殘年), year going ont; an old man; _shik ts'an_ (sik6 caan4 食殘), leavings, orts; _ts'an yan_ (caan4 jan4 殘人), to injure people; a villain; _ts'an pui_ (caan4 bui1 殘杯), heeltaps; _ts'an k'ut_ (caan4 kyut3 殘缺) deficient, injured; _ts'an yan_ (caan4 jan2 殘忍), cruel, unfeeling. (Page 573 戔 used for _Ts'an_ (caan4)) Used for _ts'an_ (caan4) 殘 cruel (Kangxi 戔: 通作殘). Remainder, leftover. 【殘】 (dzan) (_verb-like_) to hurt, to damage, to deteriorate (_adjective-like_) cruel (_adjective-like_) fragmentary

caang1.text: U+6399 (Page 21|n64 掙) Read _ch'ang_ (caang1);. to pierce, to stab (Kangxi: 刺也).

zaang6.text: U+6399 (Page 21|n64 掙) A colloquial word. To caulk; to wedge in, to squeeze in, to force in; _ta chang_ (daa2 zaang6 打掙) to caulk seams; _chang nga_ (zaang6 ngaa4 掙牙), to get between the teeth; _chang sha kai_ (zaang6 saa1 gai1 掙沙雞), to stuff a cock with sand; _ngang chang_ (ngaang6 zaang6 硬掙) determined, energetic, as in bearing pain.
