Williams' 硬掙: Corrected _chang_ (zaang3) to _chang_ (zaang6). 【掙】 (zaang6) To wedge in or stuff in, especially for the purposes of sealing off or widening. 掙住 (zaang6 zyu6): "It's wedged in and stuck." 掙闊啲 (zaang6 fut3 di1): "Wedge the gap and make it wider." 掙爆咗 (zaang6 baau3 zo2): "Burst from stuffing."

zang1.text: U+6399 To struggle, to break free. 掙扎 (zang1 zaat3): "Struggle."

zang3.text: U+6399 (Page 21|n64 掙) Read _chang_ (zang3); ~~to file~~ ``to break, to snap`` (Kangxi: 掙剉也)~~;~~``.``

coeng1.text: U+6436 槍 (F1.89 搶) 拒也 (Page 567 搶) To defend, to withstand (Kangxi: 拒也); to thrust at (Kangxi: 搶猶刺也); to rush against (Kangxi: 突也); _i t'au ts'eung ti_ (ji5 tau4 coeng1 dei6 以頭搶地) to knock the head against the ground. (Page 567 槍; here normalised to 搶) to ~~bow~~ ``knock`` the head low (Kangxi 槍: 抵也); 【搶】 (tshjang) (_verb-like_) to knock (against)

coeng2.text: U+6436 (F1.92 搶) 搶奪 (Page 568 搶) To take, to extort, to force away, to ravish, to snatch (Kangxi: 爭取也); to dispute for, to struggle for; ~~sudden, abrupt, rude;~~ to rob, as bands do; _ts'eung pak ki ku_ (coeng2 baak6 gei2 geoi3 搶白幾句) he denounced him in many phrases; _ts'eung kip_ (coeng2 gip3 搶劫) to take by force; _ts'eung sin hang_ (coeng2 sin1 hang4 搶先行), to thrust one's self forward; _ts'eung p'au t'au_ (coeng2 paau3 tau4 搶炮頭), to struggle for the rocket stick on the 2d of 2d month; _ts'eung tut_ (coeng2 dyut6 搶奪) to snatch; _tun ts'eung_ (dyun3 coeng2 斷搶), to grab at all. ~~Read _ts'ong_ (cong1); to saw (Kangxi: 搶搪鋸也).~~ Williams' "sudden, abrupt, rude": This is a misreading of Kangxi 突也. Williams' 搶炮頭: Corrected _p'au_ (pau3) to _p'au_ (paau3). Williams' "to saw": This sense should actually be written 槍, as seen under 《集韻》千剛切. Kangxi 搶搪 is a typo for 槍搪.

caang1.text: U+6490 撑 (F2.30 撐 _Ch'ang_ (cang1)) 窓爭切駕船也 (F2.30 撐) 策平聲以物撐物也 (Page 21|n64 撐撑) To prop, to shore up; to fasten open, as with a stretcher; to pole; to push off (Kangxi 撐: 撥也); to make a show; an inclined post (Kangxi: 與樘同, 樘衺柱也), a fulcrum, a prop, a stay; to border on, to adjoin, to stretch or run up into; _ch'ang shun_ (caang1 syun4 撐船), to pole a boat; _ch'ang to_ (caang1 dou6 撐渡) to pole across the ferry; to intrigue for a friend with the officer; _ch'ang 'm lai_ (caang1 m4 lai4 撐唔嚟), he could not show off long; _ch'ung ch'ang_ (cung1 caang1 充撐), to curry favor among friends; _ch'ang sheung hu shai_ (caang1 soeng5 heoi3 saai3 撐上去曬) stretch them up to dry. Williams' "inclined post": According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, the qualifier 衺 (inclined) should not be present before 柱 (pillar).

caang3.text: U+6490 橕 撑 (Page 21|n64 橕; here normalised to 撐) Read _ch'ang_ (caang3); a colloquial word``.`` To stretch out, to open; _ch'ang iu k'i chu_ (caang3 jiu1 kei5 zyu6 撐腰企住) to stand akimbo; _ch'ang hoi cheung mun_ (caang3 hoi1 coeng1 mun4-2 住開窗門), fasten open the window; _ch'ang chu_ (caang3 zyu6 撐住) to open by a stretcher; _ch'ang nga kau_ (caang3 ngaa4 gaau3 撐牙鉸) to praise one's self. Williams does not consider leg-thrusting 𨅝 to be a different word to the generic 撐. I have split things up accordingly.

caang1.text: U+69CD 搶 (Page 567 搶 _Ts'eung_ (coeng1); here corrected to _Ch'ang_ (caang1) and normalised to 槍) a comet (Kangxi 搶: 欃槍或作攙搶彗星也);

coeng1.text: U+69CD 鎗 (F1.89 鎗) 刀鎗 (Page 567 槍) Interchanged with the last (鎗); a spear, sharp at both ends (Kangxi: 木兩頭銳也); a lance, a boarding-pike; _ts'eung fat_ (coeng1 faat3 槍法) the hand manual; _ts'eung t'au_ (coeng1 tau4 槍頭), a spear head; _ha ma ts'eung_ (haa6 maa5 coeng1 下馬槍), to give a back thrust when unhorsed, to leave in the lurch, to outwit.

caang1.text: U+6A55 (Page 21|n64 橕) A branch stretching out; a fulcrum; a prop (Kangxi: 橕之言䟫也).
U+9397 (Page 567 鎗 _Ts'eung_ (coeng1); here corrected to _Ch'ang_ (caang1)) the tinkle of bells, a jingling sound (Kangxi: 鐘聲也);

coeng1.text: U+9397 槍 (F1.89 鎗) 刀鎗 (Page 567 鎗) a gun; a spear; _niu ts'eung_ (niu5 coeng1 鳥鎗), a fowling-piece, a matchlock; _shik ts'eung leung_ (sik6 coeng1 loeng4 食鎗糧), to be a soldier; _shau ts'eung_ (sau2 coeng1 手鎗), a pistol; _fung ts'eung_ (fung1 coeng1 風鎗) an air-gun. Williams' 鳥鎗: Corrected _niu_ (niu2) to _niu_ (niu5).

caang1.text: U+943A 罉 (for vernacular "pot" sense) 鎗 cang1 (shorter syllable) (Page 567 鎗 _Ts'eung_ (coeng1); here corrected to _Ch'ang_ (caang1) and normalised to 鐺) a sort of pot (Kangxi 鐺: 釜屬, 鬴有足曰鐺) to hold spirits (Kangxi 鐺: 三足溫酒器也); 【鐺】 (caang1) A cooking pot with a flat bottom. 飯鐺 (faan6 caang1): "Rice cooking pot." 鐺湯凍咗 (caang1 tong1 dung3 zo2): "The pot of broth has gone cold." Olden attestation for 鐺 _ch'ang_ (cang1) or _ch'ang_ (caang1) meaning "pot": 《廣韻》楚庚切 鎗 鼎類 《廣韻》楚庚切 鐺 俗本音當 《康熙字典》鎗 《唐韻》楚庚切《集韻》《韻會》楚耕切𠀤音璫... 又《廣韻》鼎類《六書故》三足鬴也俗作鐺... 《康熙字典》鐺 ...又《廣韻》楚庚切《集韻》《韻會》楚耕切𠀤音鎗 釜屬《通俗文》鬴有足曰鐺... The full story is that: The original character is 鎗, read _ch'ang_ (cang1), meaning a pot with legs. Later, 鐺 is used vulgarly for 鎗. Later, the character 罉 is created for the Cantonese vernacular sense of flat-bottomed pot.

caang4.text: U+6A59 棖 caang4-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.88 橙) 棑橙果名 (Page 21|n64 橙) The coolie orange (Kangxi: 橘屬 etc.); _ch'ang tsau_ (caang4-2 zau2 橙酒), spirits in which orange peel has been soaked; _t'ong ch'ang_ (tong4 caang4-2 糖橙), orange sweetmeats; _ch'ang kom man_ (caang4-2 gam3 maan4 橙咁蠻) sharp as an orange; _t'im ch'ang_ (tim4 caang4-2 甜橙), sweet oranges; _shan ch'ang_ (saan1 caang4-2 山橙), a melodinus. The original level reading caang4 is almost never heard, having been supplanted by vernacular rising caang4-2 in all senses. The colour orange. 【橙】 (dreang) (_noun-like_) orange-tree, orange

caang3.text: U+2815D 橕 jaang3 (alternative) (Page 21|n64 橕; here normalised to 𨅝) Read _ch'ang_ (caang3); a colloquial word``.`` kick at; _ch'ang k'u ch'ut hu_ (caang3 keoi5 ceot1 heoi3 𨅝佢出去) kick him out; expel him; Williams does not consider leg-thrusting 𨅝 to be a different word to the generic 撐. I have split things up accordingly. 【𨅝】 (caang3, jaang3) To thrust outward or down with the leg or the foot. 一腳𨅝開 (jat1 goek3 jaang3 hoi1): "Thrust away in one action of the foot." 𨅝樓梯 (jaang3 lau4 tai1): "Leg-thrust upon stairs" (i.e. climb stairs). To brace or support by extension of the leg. 【𨅝檯腳】 (caang3 toi4-2 goek3, jaang3 toi4-2 goek3) To eat or discourse at the same table. (Literally, "kick/brace with legs the table's legs".)

caang4.text: U+5000 (Page 21|n64 倀) The reading _ch'ang_ for 倀 is present in Williams, but not with the correct senses. Standing alone (Kangxi: 獨立貌).

coeng1.text: U+5000 (Page 21|n64 倀 _Ch'ang_ (caang4); here corrected to _Ch'eung_ (coeng1)) Groping, going blindly (Kangxi: 狂行不知所如也 etc.); madly (Kangxi: 狂也), carelessly; to fall down (Kangxi: 一曰仆也); _~~ch'ang~~ ``ch'eung`` kwai_ (coeng1 gwai2 倀鬼), a lares eaten by a tiger, _met._ one who entices to evil courses. 【倀】 (trhjang) (_adjective-like_) frenetic

caang4.text: U+252C7 caang4-2 (rising second in vernacular 𥋇𥋇地) 【𥋇】 (caang4) To dazzle or glare. 畀太陽𥋇住 (bei2 taai3 joeng4 caang4 zyu6): "Glared at by the sun." 【𥋇眼】 (caang4 ngaan5) Dazzling or glaring to the eyes. 太𥋇眼 (taai3 caang4 ngaan5): "It's too dazzling."

caap3.text: U+6271 (F2.66 挿) 以手刺人 (F2.66 扱) 同上 (Page 23|n66 扱) Wrongly used for the last (揷). To take, to receive (Kangxi: 收也, 取也獲也); to raise (Kangxi: 引也舉也); to curtsey with the hands bowed to the ground (Kangxi: 拜手至地也); to tuck up the skirts (Kangxi: 扱衽曰襭 etc.). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 剌 to 刺. 【扱】 (tsrheap) (_verb-like_) to collect (_verb-like_) to tuck
U+633F (F2.66 挿) 以手刺人 (F2.66 扱) 同上 Fan Wan entry: Corrected 剌 to 刺.

U+63D2 挿 揷 (F2.66 挿) 以手刺人 (F2.66 扱) 同上 (Page 23|n66 揷; here normalised to 插) To insert, to pierce, to set in, to stick into (Kangxi 插: 刺肉也, 刺入也); to tuck in, to thrust into or through; to transplant; to set in a socket; to meddle, to interfere in; an iron pointed pole used to carry sheaves (Kangxi 插: 插者擔也 etc.), or pry up clods, for which 鍤 is also used (Kangxi 插: 與鍤同刺土器也); _ch'ap yeung_ (caap3 joeng1 插秧), to set out rice shoots; _ch'ap tsui_ (caap3 zeoi2 插嘴), to interrupt (in talking); _ch'ap yik nan fi_ (caap3 jik6 naan4 fei1 插翼難飛) he can not fly away; _ch'ap tsong_ (caap3 zong1 插贓), to implicate one by leaving something in his house; _ch'ap piu_ (caap3 biu1 插標), to stick on labels; _ch'ap shiu_ (caap3 siu1 插燒), a stuffing put into roast pigs; a concubine's child; _shi ch'ap_ (si4 caap3 匙插) a narrow purse; _ch'ap shau lok hu_ (caap3 sau2 lok6 heoi3 插手落去) to meddle in officiously; _pat t'oi pat ch'ap_ (baat3 toi4 baat3 caap3 八擡八插) eight bearers and eight outriders; _ch'ap i yau ying_ (caap3 ji5 jau4 jing4 插耳遊營), to stick flags in the ears when whipping a thief. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 剌 to 刺. 【插】 (tsrheap) (_verb-like_) to insert 【插】 (caap3) To criticise. 【插贓嫁禍】 (caap3 zong1 gaa3 wo6) To plant evidence and shift blame (onto another). (Literally, "insert loot and transfer disaster".)

caat3.text: U+5237 caat3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) saat3 (per 《廣韻》數刮切) (F2.72 _Shat_ (saat3)) 成也歸也又振作也 (Page 427 刷 _Shat_ (saat3); here normalised to _Ch'at_ (caat3)) A brush, a scraper; a curry-comb; to brush; to cleanse, to scour (Kangxi: 刮也), to scrub (Kangxi: 刷淸也); a card for cotton; to print, as the Chinese do; _hon ~~shat~~ ``ch'at``_ (hon2 caat3 刊刷) to cut and print books; _~~shat~~ ``ch'at`` ch'i koi hang_ (caat3 ci2 goi2 hang6 刷恥改行) to wipe off disgrace by reforming, 【刷】 (srjwet, srwaet) (_verb-like_) to scrub
U+5239 (F2.72 刹) 稱僧寺曰梵刹 (Page 24|n67 刹)

U+5BDF 詧 (F2.72 察) 審也考也省也又察察潔淨貌 (F2.72 詧) 同上 (Page 24|n67 察) To examine, to judge, to search out the truth (Kangxi: 覆審也, 諦也知也); to observe closely, to inquire into all particulars (Kangxi: 考也廉視也); _ch'a ch'at_ (caa4 caat3 查察) to scrutinize; _kam ch'at_ (gaam3 caat3 鑒察) beg you to look at, said by suppliants; _king ch'at_ (ging1 caat3 京察) in the censorate; _ch'at tut_ (caat3 dyut6 察奪) examine and decide respecting the petition. 【察】 (tsrheat) (_verb-like_) to inspect

U+64E6 caat3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.72 擦) 粲入聲揩必也磨也 (Page 560 擦) To rub hard, to brush; to feel for; a brush; _nga ts'at_ (ngaa4 caat3-2 牙擦) a toothbrush; _ts'at ni tui hai_ (caat3 ni1 deoi3 haai4 擦呢對鞋) brush this pair of shoes; _mo ts'at_ (mo4 caat3 磨擦) to make smooth; _ts'at kon tseng_ (caat3 gon1 zeng6 擦乾淨) brush it clean; _ts'at lap_ (caat3 laap6 擦蠟) to wax and brighten. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 摖 to 擦. The character 擦 is not attested in the Middle Chinese rime books. Kangxi has 《篇海》《字彙》𠀤初戛切音察, which is _ch'at_ rather than _ts'at_. In modern usage, the noun sense of "brush" is spelt 刷; see 刷 caat3. 【擦膠】 (caat3 gaau1) A rubber or eraser. 【擦鞋】 (caat3 haai4) To bootlick, to brownnose. (Literally, "polish shoes".) 【擦紙膠】 (caat3 zi2 gaau1) Synonym of 擦膠.

U+737A taat3 (per 《廣韻》他達切) (F2.72 獺) 水狗食魚之物 (Page 24|n67 獺) An otter (Kangxi: 如小狗 etc.); _shan ch'at_ (saan1 caat3 山獺) a beaver, or fresh-water otter; _hoi ch'at_ (hoi2 caat3 海獺) a seal; _ch'at ~~ling~~ ``leng``_ (caat3 leng5 獺領), seal skin collars or tippets; _ch'at tsai_ (caat3 zai2 獺仔), a tanka woman. Williams' 獺領: Changed _ling_ (ling5) to vernacular reading _leng_ (leng5). 【獺】 (that, trhaet) (_noun-like_) otter

caau1.text: U+527F 𠞰 勦 To plagiarise, to copy.

ziu2.text: U+527F 𠞰 勦 (F2.6 勦) 征勦截殺也又勦集 (F2.6 剿) 同上 (Page 581 剿𠞰) To destroy utterly, to exterminate, to devastate; _tsiu mit_ (ziu2 mit6 剿滅) to extirpate, to rase, as is done to rebels or their towns; _tsiu tsut tong u_ (ziu2 zyut6 dong2 jyu5 剿絕黨與), to extirpate all the adherents of a cabal. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 王 to 上. To cut off (Kangxi 𠞰: 絕也).
U+52E6 (F2.6 勦) 征勦截殺也又勦集 (F2.6 剿) 同上 Fan Wan entry: Corrected 王 to 上. To toil (Kangxi: 勞也).

caau1.text: U+6284 剿 𠞰 勦 鈔 (F2.41 抄) 抄寫 (Page 29|n72 抄) To take, to seize (Kangxi: 略取也); to search; to engross, to transcribe (Kangxi: 謄寫也); to lade out, to spoon out (Kangxi: 叉取也); to confiscate, to escheat, to sequestrate, to resume; _shau ch'au_ (sau2 caau1 手抄), a MS. copy; _ch'au ts'ing ka_ (caau1 cing1 gaa1 抄清家), all his property is taken; _chiu pun ch'au t'ang_ (ziu3 bun2 caau1 tang4 照本抄謄), copied exactly; _ch'au se_ (caau1 se2 抄寫), to copy; _ch'au pak_ (caau1 baak6 抄白) it is copied clearly; _ch'au sai_ (caau1 saai3 抄嗮) it is all copied. (Page 30|n73 鈔 _Ch'au_ (caau3); _Ch'au_ (caau1) implied) Interchanged with 抄 to copy (Kangxi: 今俗別作抄, 謄寫也); 【抄】 (tsrhaew, tsrhaewH) (_verb-like_) to seize (_verb-like_) to copy

U+9214 caau3 (per 《廣韻》初敎切) (F2.42 鈔 _Ch'au_ (caau3)) 鈔錢又畧取也 (Page 30|n73 鈔 _Ch'au_ (caau3); here normalised to _Ch'au_ (caau1)) a document, a government paper, a receipt (Kangxi: 官收物而給印信文憑也); paper money (Kangxi: 楮貨名); _shun ch'au_ (syun4 caau1 船鈔) tonnage dues, charges on ships; _fong ch'au_ (fong3 caau1 放鈔) to burn paper money to the gods; _ts'in ch'au_ (cin4 caau1 錢鈔) bills and money; _ch'au kwan_ (caau1 gwaan1 鈔關), a custom house; _shui ch'au_ (seoi3 caau1 稅鈔) duties on goods, transit dues. Both the level reading 楚交切 _ch'au_ (caau1) and the departing reading 初敎切 _ch'au_ (caau3) are attested in 《廣韻》, explained 略也 "seize".

caau3.text: U+9214 (F2.42 鈔) 鈔錢又畧取也 (Page 30|n73 鈔)

caau2.text: U+5435 (Page 30|n73 吵) To clamor, to wrangle; _ch'au nau_ (caau2 naau6 吵鬧) to scold; _ts'o ch'au_ (cou4 caau2 嘈吵), to raise a row; _tai ch'au yat ch'eung_ (daai6 caau2 jat1 coeng4 大吵一場), a great hubbub; _ch'au yan i_ (caau2 jan4 ji5 吵人耳), a din in the ears. Williams' 大吵一場: Corrected _ch'eung_ (coeng2) to _ch'eung_ (coeng4).

U+7092 (F2.42 炒) 乾熬食物曰炒 (F2.42 煼) 同上 (Page 30|n73 炒) To roast in a boiler; to fry in fat or batter; _kon ch'au_ (gon1 caau2 乾炒), to fry brown; to roast to dryness; _ch'au ch'a_ (caau2 caa4 炒茶), to fire tea; _ch'au ~~ka fi~~ ``ka fe``_ (caau2 gaa3 fe1 炒咖啡), to roast coffee; _ch'au mai_ (caau2 maai6 炒賣) to cook and sell; _ch'au fung lut_ (caau2 fung1 leot6-2 炒風栗) to roast chestnuts; _tsin ch'au_ (zin1 caau2 煎炒), to fry; _siu ch'au yuk_ (siu2 caau2 juk6 小炒肉) fried hash and vegetables; _ch'au shuk_ (caau2 suk6 炒熟) to fry thoroughly. Williams' 炒咖啡: Changed now dated _ka fi_ (gaa1 fei1) to modern _ka fe_ (gaa3 fe1). This change is perhaps inappropriate as it is anachronistic for 1856; see the page-level commentary at fe. Williams' 炒賣: Now to buy speculatively and sell. 【炒】 (caau2) To stir-fry. Short for 炒魷魚. 炒咗佢 (caau2 zo2 keoi5): "Fired him." To speculate, or to flip. 炒賣 (caau2 maai6): "Speculate and sell." 炒樓 (caau2 lau4-2): "Flip property." To crash. 炒單車 (caau2 daan1 ce1): "Stacked my bike." 【炒魷魚】 (caau2 jau4 jyu4-2) To sack, to fire. 【炒魷】 (caau2 jau4-2) Short for 炒魷魚.

caau3.text: U+6477 【摷】 (caau3) To rummage or rifle through. 摷鎖匙 (caau3 so2 si4): "Rummage for the keys." 摷垃圾 (caau3 laap6 saap3): "Rummage through rubbish."

caau4.text: U+5DE2 caau4-2 (rising second in vernacular 巢巢地) (F2.41 巢) 鳥在木上曰巢 (Page 29|n72 巢) A nest on a tree (Kangxi: 鳥在木上曰巢 etc.); a den, a retreat; a lurking place; to nestle, to make a nest; _ch'au ut_ (caau4 jyut6 巢穴) to skulk in, as a brigand; _ch'au wo_ (caau4 wo1 巢窩), or _tseuk ch'au_ (zoek3 caau4 雀巢), a nest; _ts'ak ch'au_ (caak6 caau4 賊巢), a den or resort of robbers; _pak niu kwai ch'au_ (baak3 niu5 gwai1 caau4 百鳥歸巢), the birds have gone to roost. (Page 30|n73 窠; here corrected to 巢) A colloquial word. Crumpled, wrinkled, corrugated, rough; shriveled, as dried fruit; _ho ts'z' a p'o p'i kom ch'au_ (hou2 ci5 aa3 po4 pei4 gam3 caau4 好似阿婆皮咁巢), wrinkled as a granny's face; _ch'au mang mang_ (caau4 mang1 mang1 巢𠵼𠵼), creased, rumpled. Williams' 好似阿婆皮咁巢: Corrected _ts'z'_ (ci2) to _ts'z'_ (ci5). A large free-reed instrument (Kangxi: 樂器, 大笙謂之巢). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【巢】 (dzraew) (_noun-like_) nest 【巢】 (caau4) Wrinkled. 巢𠵼𠵼 (caau4 mang1 mang1): "Wrinkly." 巢皮 (caau4 pei4): "Wrinkled skin."

U+6F05 巢 ziu2 (per 《廣韻》子小切) (F2.41 漅) 湖名在合肥亦作巢 (Page 30|n73 漅) Name of a large lake in the south of the province of Nganhwui (安徽) (Kangxi: 湖名). Also read _tsiu_ (ziu2). The last (巢) is usually used for this (Kangxi: 亦作巢). The reading _ch'au_ (caau4) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 鋤交切, explained 湖名. Williams' "Also read ...": Corrected _tsiu_ (ziu1) to _tsiu_ (ziu2).
U+8F48 (F2.41 轈) 兵車加轈以望敵也 (Page 30|n73 轈) A turret on a chariot, from the top of which to observe the troops (Kangxi: 兵高車加巢以望敵也).

cai1.text: U+59BB (F1.28 妻) 夫妻 (Page 549 妻) A wife, a partner (Kangxi: 妻與己齊者也), a spouse, a consort who is taken with legal ceremonies; _ts'ai tsz'_ (cai1 zi2 妻子) a wife; _ling ts'ai_ (ling6 cai1 令妻), your wife; _hak ts'ai_ (haak3 cai1 客妻), a courtesan; _lo t'au ts'ai_ (lou6 tau4 cai1 路頭妻), a wayside wife, one taken while sojourning elsewhere; _p'ing ts'ai_ (ping4 cai1 平妻), equal to a wife, _i. e._ a concubine; _ts'u ts'ai_ (ceoi2 cai1 娶妻), to take a wife; _in ts'ai_ (jin4 cai1 賢妻) my good wife, a virtuous wife; _kiu ts'ai mi ts'ip_ (giu1 cai1 mei5 cip3 嬌妻美妾) an accomplished wife and handsome concubine. Williams' 娶妻: Changed _ts'u_ (ceoi3) to common reading _ts'u_ (ceoi2). 【妻-noun】 (tshej) (_noun-like_) wife

cai3.text: U+59BB (F1.32 妻) 以女嫁人曰妻 (Page 549 妻) Also read _ts'ai_ (cai3); to give a daughter as wife to one (Kangxi: 以女嫁人曰妻之). 【妻-verb】 (tshejH) (_verb-like_) to wive

cai1.text: U+60BD (F1.28 悽) 悲也 (Page 549 悽) grieved, afflicted, sorrowing (Kangxi: 痛也, 悲也); suffering from hunger (Kangxi: 悽悽饑病貌); _pi ts'ai_ (bei1 cai1 悲悽), pitiable, suffering; _ts'ai ts'ai_ (cai1 cai1 悽悽), gaunt, famishing.

U+68F2 栖 sai1 (per 《廣韻》先稽切) (F1.28 棲) 棲息又牀也 (Page 550 棲~~梄~~``栖``) To roost, to perch, to nestle (Kangxi 栖: 日在西方而鳥棲 etc.) (Kangxi 棲: 同栖鳥棲也); to sojourn, to stay at, to reside; to settle down, to rest, to repose (Kangxi 栖: 凡物止息皆曰栖); at peace; a perch, a roost; a sleeping-place (Kangxi 棲: 棲謂之牀); a lodging-place; _kai ts'ai_ (gai1 cai1 雞棲), a hen-roost; _tsam shi ts'ai chu_ (zaam6 si4 cai1 zyu6 暫時棲住) to live here for a while; _mo teng ts'ai shan_ (mou4 deng6 cai1 san1 無埞棲身), no place to stay at; _ts'ai sit_ (cai1 sit3 棲屑) to and fro, like a stranger. 【棲】 (sej) (_verb-like_) to roost

U+6DD2 凄 悽 (for "grieved/afflicted" senses) (F1.28 淒) 寒也又淒凉 (Page 549 凄淒) Intense cold (Kangxi 淒: 寒涼也); bleak, cloudy, wintry (Kangxi 淒: 雲雨起貌); a cold wind; shivering, freezing; calamitous, afflicted, grievous; _fung u ts'ai ts'ai_ (fung1 jyu5 cai1 cai1 風雨淒淒), sleety rain and wind; _ts'ai ts'it_ (cai1 cit3 淒切) bitter suffering; _ts'ai leung_ (cai1 loeng4 淒涼), grievous; _kom ts'ai leung_ (gam3 cai1 loeng4 咁淒涼), so sad; so serious, as consequences; _ts'ai ts'am_ (cai1 caam2 淒慘), oppressed, borne down; _ts'ai ch'o sham_ (cai1 co2 sam6 淒楚甚) in urgent case, needy. The second (淒) also means gathering clouds. (Page 549 悽) Similar to the last (淒); 【淒】 (tshej) (_adjective-like_) cold (_adjective-like_) sad
U+7DC0 cai2 (alternative per 《廣韻》千禮切) (F1.28 緀) 緀斐文章相錯貌 (Page 549 緀) The stripes, colored or plain, in silken fabrics (Kangxi: 帛文貌); ornamented, blended colors and stripes (Kangxi: 緀斐文章相錯貌).

U+840B (F1.28 萋) 草盛 (Page 549 萋) Luxuriant foliage (Kangxi: 草盛貌); _ts'ai ts'ai_ (cai1 cai1 萋萋), moving clouds (Kangxi: 雲行貌); a man exerting himself (Kangxi: 萋萋臣盡力也). 【萋】 (tshej) (_adjective-like_) luxuriant

cai3.text: U+5207 (F1.32 切) 齊也衆也 (Page 550 切) All, every, the whole; _yat ts'ai_ (jat1 cai3 一切) the entire lot (Kangxi: 一切大凡也); _chiu ku yat ts'ai_ (ziu3 gu3 jat1 cai3 照顧一切) I thank you for all your kindness. 【一切】 ('jit tshejH) (_noun-like_) everything 【切-departing】 (tshejH) Used in 一切.

cit3.text: U+5207 (F1.26 切) 近也要也 (Page 580 切) To cut, to carve, to mince, to slice (Kangxi: 割也刻也); to urge, to press (Kangxi: 迫也急也); a superlative, very, vehement, earnest, eager, pressing, important, serious, momentous; the important parts, a resume (Kangxi: 切要也); sincere (Kangxi: 愨實也); to feel, as the pulse (Kangxi: 切按也); to spell, to express a sound by joining initials and finals (Kangxi: 反切); _ts'it iu_ (cit3 jiu3 切要) important; _t'au ts'it_ (tau3 cit3 透切) plain, lucid; _ts'it yuk_ (cit3 juk6 切肉) minced meat; _ts'it mat hu_ (cit3 mat6 heoi3 切勿去) stay, don't go; _ts'it mak_ (cit3 mak6 切脈) to feel the pulse; _ts'it oi_ (cit3 oi3 切愛) warm love; _ts'it to sui_ (cit3 dou3 seoi3 切到碎) cut to pieces, cut fine; _ts'it ts'o_ (cit3 co1 切磋), to turn in a lathe; to work and carve; ~~to dehort, as a friend;~~ _ts'it yam_ (cit3 jam1 切音), to spell, to join an initial and a final; _k'ok ts'it pat i_ (kok3 cit3 bat1 ji4 確切不移), unchangeably fixed. Williams' 切磋: Not to dehort, but to learn from one another, especially through an activity (e.g. sparring, a game, etc.). Used in 反切 (back-dissection) or alone, referring to the method of indicating (Middle) Chinese pronunciation by two characters, marking the initial (consonant) and the final (rime) respectively. To bite or gnash (the teeth). Close (in the sense of near). 【切-entering】 (tshet) (_verb-like_) to cut, to dissect, (_met._) to gnash (_verb-like_) to take (pulse) (_adjective-like_) (_met._) pressing Used in 反切. 【切】 (cit3) In time. 嚟唔切 (lai4 m4 cit3): "Can't make it in time." 趕得切 (gon2 dak1 cit3): "Rushed there in time." Short for 白切雞 (plain cut chicken). 叉切飯 (caa1 cit3 faan6): "Char shiu, plain cut chicken rice." 【切雞】 (cit3 gai1) Short for 白切雞 (plain cut chicken). 切雞飯 (cit3 gai1 faan6): "Plain cut chicken rice."

cai3.text: U+780C (F1.32 砌) 堦基也 (Page 550 砌) A stone step (Kangxi: 階甃也); ornamented tiles laid for steps; to lay, as tiles; to place regularly, to fit in; _ts'ai ho_ (cai3 hou2 砌好), lay them even, as bricks; _ts'ai mai_ (cai3 maai4 砌埋), place them straight; _ts'ai shek lo_ (cai3 sek6 lou6 砌石路) to pave with stones; _ts'ai ts'eung_ (cai3 coeng4 砌牆), to lay a wall; _ts'ai nga_ (cai3 ngaa5 砌瓦), to tile roofs. 【砌】 (tshejH) (_noun-like_) step (_verb-like_) to assemble, to lay

cai4.text: U+568C (Page 549 嚌 read _Tsai_ (zai1); here corrected to _Ts'ai_ (cai4)) ~~Read _tsai_ (zai1).~~ a plaintive sound (Kangxi: 鳥哀聲).

zai6.text: U+568C (Page 549 嚌) ~~Read _tsai_ (zai1).~~ To taste (Kangxi: 嘗也), to sip, to wet the lips; A colloquial word. A superlative, denoting that the extreme has been reached; _~~tam~~ ``t'am`` tak tsai_ (taam5 dak1 zai6 淡得嚌) excessively weak, as tea; _mo mat tsai_ (mou5 mat1 zai6 冇乜嚌) not much there, nothing to speak of; _tai tak tsai_ (daai6 dak1 zai6 大得嚌) far too large; _hai tak tsai_ (hai6 dak1 zai6 係得嚌) certainly. Williams' "Read _tsai_": This is probably an incorrect parsing of Kangxi 音劑, which is intended in the departing tone. Williams' 淡得嚌: Corrected _tam_ (daam6 去聲) to _t'am_ (taam5 上聲). Williams' 係得嚌: Dated. 【嚌】 (dzejH) (_verb-like_) to taste 【得嚌】 (dak1 zai6) A final particle of excessive degree. 多得嚌 (do1 dak1 zai6): "Too much." 熱得嚌 (jit6 dak1 zai6): "It's too hot." 【咁嚌】 (gam3 zai6) A final particle of almostness. 熱到死咁嚌 (jit6 dou3 sei2 gam3 zai6): "It's so hot we've died, almost." 食完咁嚌 (sik6 jyun4 gam3 zai6): "We've finished eating, almost." 【乜嚌】 (mat1 zai6) A final particle of almost-nothingness. 冇乜嚌 (mou5 mat1 zai6): "Almost nothing." 冇食乜嚌 (mou5 sik6 mat1 zai6): "Barely ate any of it."

cai4.text: U+61E0 zai6 (alternative per 《廣韻》在詣切) (F1.32 懠 _Tsai_ (zai3)) 怒也 (Page 549 懠 _Tsai_ (zai3); here corrected to _Ts'ai_ (cai4)) Angry, enraged (Kangxi: 怒也); _t'in chi fong ~~tsai~~ ``ts'ai``_ (tin1 zi1 fong1 cai4 天之方懠) when heaven is angry. 【懠】 (dzej, dzejH) (_adjective-like_) wroth

zai1.text: U+61E0 (Page 549 懠 _Tsai_ (zai3); here corrected to _Tsai_ (zai1)) suspicious, envious of (Kangxi: 懠疑猶猜疑也); to doubt;

cai4.text: U+8810 (F1.30 蠐) 螬蠐蟲名 (Page 550 蠐) ~~A maggot~~ ``The scarab beetle grub`` (Kangxi: 蠐螬); _ts'ai ts'o_ (cai4 cou4 蠐螬), a grub, larvae in mulberry and other trees. 【蠐】 (dzej) Used in 蠐螬. 【蠐螬】 (dzej dzaw) (_noun-like_) scarab grub 【蝤蠐】 (dzjuw dzej) (_noun-like_) tree grub

U+9F4A (F1.30 齊) 整齊不參差也又國名 (Page 550 齊) Even (Kangxi: 整也), equal (Kangxi: 等也), uniform; to equalize, to tranquillize; to adjust, to classify, to arrange methodically or according to rank; at once, all, together, alike; quick, smart; ~~good;~~ to discriminate (Kangxi: 辨也); an ancient feudal state comprising the part of Shantung (山東) east of T'ai shan (泰山); the 210th radical ("even"); _ching ts'ai_ (zing2 cai4 整齊), well arranged, properly placed; _ts'ai chau_ (cai4 zau1 齊州), an old name for China; _yat ts'ai hu_ (jat1 cai4 heoi3 一齊去) all go at once; _shau ts'ai cheung muk_ (sau1 cai4 zoeng3 muk6 收齊賬目) to collect all the accounts; _ts'ai mi_ (cai4 mei4 齊眉), an old couple; _'m pin tak yan ts'ai_ (m4 bin3 dak1 jan4 cai4 唔變得人齊), he was not turned out a full man --- said of a young scapegrace; _to ts'it kom ts'ai_ (dou1 cit3 gam3 cai4 刀切咁齊), trimmed even, all of a size; _ts'ai sam_ (cai4 sam1 齊心), of one mind; _tang ts'ai_ (dang2 cai4 等齊), wait till all come; _ts'ai pi_ (cai4 bei6 齊備) all are ready, fully prepared; _pat ts'ai_ (bat1 cai4 不齊), incomplete, unequal; _ts'ai ka_ (cai4 gaa1 齊家), to govern a family. Williams' "good": Kangxi has 又《廣韻》好也, but it is unclear whether 好 is rising-tone "good" or departing-tone "fond". A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【齊】 (dzej) (_adjective-like_) equal, uniform (_verb-like_) to equal, to uniform, (_met._) to put in order (_adjective-like_) together, complete

zaai1.text: U+9F4A (F2.25 齊) 竹佳切莊也恭也潔也洗心曰齊 (F2.25 齋) 齋成又燕居之室曰齋 (F2.25 斋) 同上 (Page 10|n53 齊)

cai5.text: U+6CDA (F1.31 泚) 汗出貌 (Page 550 泚)

ci2.text: U+6CDA cai2 (alternative per 《廣韻》千禮切) cai5 (per Fan Wan) (F1.31 泚 _Ts'ai_ (cai5)) 汗出貌 (Page 550 泚 _Ts'ai_ (cai5); here normalised to _Ts'z'_ (ci2)) Perspiring (Kangxi: 汗出貌), sweating on the forehead; clear water (Kangxi: 水淸也); fresh, clean; 【泚】 (tshjeX, tshejX) (_adjective-like_) limpid (_adjective-like_) perspiring

zi2.text: U+6CDA (Page 550 泚 _Ts'ai_ (cai5); here corrected to _Tsz'_ (zi2)) name of a stream (Kangxi: 水名).

cai5.text: U+85BA (F1.31 薺) 甘菜 (Page 550 薺) Cress, shepherd's purse (Kangxi: 甘菜); a term for small esculent plants like pepper-grass, cress, mint. 【薺】 (dzejX) (_noun-like_) shepherd's-purse

U+9C6D 鮆 (Page 550 鱭鮆) A thin fish, with a silvery belly; a mullet; _ma ts'ai_ (maa5 cai5 馬鱭), a kind of anchovy _(Coilia playfairii)_; _wong mi ts'ai_ (wong4 mei5 cai5 黃尾鱭), yellow tail mullet _(Mullus xanthurus)_; _pak ts'ai_ (baak6 cai5 白鱭), green ish mullet _Mugil ventricosus_.

cam1.text: U+4FB5 (F2.36 侵) 侵占侵凌侵害 (Page 552 侵) To usurp, to incroach upon, to enter gradually (Kangxi: 漸進也); to appropriate, to plunder, to invade (Kangxi: 朘削也); possessed, by as a spirit; bad, calamitous, as a year (Kangxi: 歲凶); _ts'am hoi_ (cam1 hoi6 侵害) to injure insensibly; _ts'am chim_ (cam1 zim3 侵佔) to unjustly incroach on; _ts'am pun_ (cam1 bun2 侵本), to draw on the principal; _ts'am tik yap hu_ (cam1 dik1 jap6 heoi3 侵滴入去) stick in a few bad ones; _ts'am pin p'ai_ (cam1 bin1 paai4-2 侵辮牌), to braid in false hair; _tai ts'am chi nin_ (daai6 cam1 zi1 nin4 大侵之年), a bad year; _ts'am ling_ (cam1 ling4 侵凌), to intimidate, to oppress. 【侵】 (tshim) (_verb-like_) to invade, to encroach 【侵】 (cam1) To add in. 侵水 (cam1 seoi2): "Add in some water." To join in. 侵埋我玩吖 (cam1 maai4 ngo5 waan2 a1): "Let me join in too."

cam2.text: U+4FB5 (F2.37 侵) 漸進也 (Page 552 侵) Read _ts'am_ (cam2); diminutive, small (Kangxi: 貌不揚也, 短小曰侵). Fan Wan entry: The explanation 漸進也 is erroneous (it should apply to the level-tone reading _ts'am_ (cam1)).

cam1.text: U+6D78 zam3 (per Williams, common) (Page 551 浸 _Tsam_ (zam3); here normalised to _Ts'am_ (cam1)) _k'i shut ~~tsam~~ ``ts'am`` yam_ (kei4 syut3 cam1 jam4 其說浸淫), his words are insidious and persuasive;

zam3.text: U+6D78 湛 zam6 (for vernacular "drown" sense) (F2.38 浸) 漬也潤也 (Page 15|n58 浸 _Cham_) A colloquial word. A coat, as of paint; a thickness, an envelop, a skin; _ts'at sam cham_ (cat1 saam1 zam3 漆三浸) varnished it three times; _mok yat cham p'i_ (mok1 jat1 zam3 pei4 剝一浸皮), pull off one covering. (Page 16|n59 湛 _Cham_ (zaam3); here corrected to _Tsam_ (zam3) and normalised to 浸) to soak, to immerse, to imbibe, as a sponge (Kangxi: 與浸同漬也); to plunge in (Kangxi: 投物水中也); (Page 551 浸 _Tsam_) To soak through, to penetrate (Kangxi: 漬也); to saturate, to steep in, to macerate, to wet (Kangxi: 潤也); to submerge, to immerse (Kangxi: 沒也); to flood, to lay under water, as fields (Kangxi: 涵也沈也澤之總名也); drenched, wet, imbued with, watered; gradually, gently (Kangxi: 漸也); _tsam sz'_ (zam3 sei2 浸死) drowned; _tsam t'ong_ (zam3 tong4 浸糖) soaked in syrup; _tsam yun_ (zam3 jeon6 浸潤) to imbue with, to prejudice against, to bias; _tsam cha_ (zam3 zaa6 浸？) niggardly, closefisted; _shui tsam_ (seoi2 zam3 水浸) overflowed``, flooded``; _hon tsam hung ngan_ (hon6 zam3 hung4 ngaan4 汗浸紅顏), the perspiration rolled down his face; _tsam shap_ (zam3 sap1 浸濕) wet it, soak it; _pi shui tsam chu_ (bei2 seoi2 zam3 zyu6 畀水浸住) cover it with water; _tsam tsau_ (zam3 zau2 浸酒), spirits in which fruits have been soaked; _tsam yap sam_ (zam3 jap6 sam1 浸入心), it is wet through. Some hold that the reading variation zam6 for the vernacular "drown" sense is from Middle Chinese 直禁切 _cham_ (zam6) for 沈 (now spelt 沉). For this departing-tone reading of 沈, note that: 《廣韻》 provides no explanation (it simply cross-references the level-tone reading _ch'am_ (cam4) as 又直壬切). 《集韻》 has the explanation 沒也. Kangxi has 亦沒也 followed by the alternative sense 一曰投物水中也. Williams' 汗浸紅顏: Although 紅顏 does not preclude men, it is generally used to describe a lady's face. 【浸】 (tsimH) (_verb-like_) to soak, to immerse, (_met._) lake (_adverb-like_) gradually 【浸】 (zam3) Classifier for layers. 剷甩浸油 (caan2 lat1 zam3 jau4-2): "Strip away the layer of paint." 有浸肥膏 (jau5 zam3 fei4 gou1): "There's a layer of fat."

cam1.text: U+7972 (F2.36 祲) 精氣感祥也 (Page 552 祲) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 䘲 to 祲.

zam1.text: U+7972 cam1 (per Fan Wan) zam3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子鴆切) (F2.36 祲 _Ts'am_ (cam1)) 精氣感祥也 (Page 552 祲 _Ts'am_ (cam1); here normalised to _Tsam_ (zam1)) To influence, to act upon; a malign halo (Kangxi: 妖氛也); abundant, great, full (Kangxi: 盛也). The reading _ts'am_ (cam1) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 七林切, explained 精氣感祥又日旁氣. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 䘲 to 祲.

cam1.text: U+92DF (F2.36 鋟) 刻也 (Page 552 鋟)

cim1.text: U+92DF cam1 (alternative per 《集韻》千尋切) cam2 (per 《廣韻》七稔切) zim1 (per 《廣韻》子廉切) (F2.36 鋟 _Ts'am_ (cam1)) 刻也 (Page 552 鋟 _Ts'am_ (cam1); here normalised to _Ts'im_ (cim1)) To cut, to engrave (Kangxi: 刻也); sharp-pointed (Kangxi: 錐也); a point; an awl, a graver. The reading _ts'im_ (cim1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 千廉切, explained 刻也.

cam1.text: U+99F8 (F2.36 駸) 馬疾行也 (Page 552 駸) ~~Occurs used for the preceding (侵);~~ properly a ~~swift~~ horse ``moving swiftly`` (Kangxi: 駸駸馬行疾貌); _ts'am ts'am_ (cam1 cam1 駸駸), a fast running courser. 【駸】 (tshim, tsrhim) (_adjective-like_) galloping

cam2.text: U+5BE2 寑 (F2.37 寢) 臥也息也又內室曰寢室 (Page 552 寢) To sleep (Kangxi: 臥也); rest, sleep; to rest, to desist, to repose (Kangxi: 息也); a back chamber, a bed-chamber (Kangxi: 堂室也); an adytum in a temple (Kangxi: 寢廟凡廟前曰廟後曰寢); a retiring-room in a palace; a dwelling-house (Kangxi: 凡居室皆曰寢); a mausoleum or house near a grave (Kangxi: 陵寢); _on ts'am_ (on1 cam2 安寢), to sleep quietly; _k'i sz' sui ts'am_ (kei4 si6 seoi6 cam2 其事遂寢), the affair then terminated; _ts'am shat_ (cam2 sat1 寢室) a dormitory; the ancestral hall in a mansion; _nan ts'am_ (naan4 cam2 難寢), I cannot get asleep; _ts'am ping_ (cam2 bing1 寢兵), to give rest to troops; _ling ts'am_ (ling4 cam2 陵寢), the imperial mausolea near Mukten (瀋陽); _ching ts'am_ (zing3 cam2 正寢), room behind the hall. Ugly in appearance. 【寢】 (tshimX) (_verb-like_) to sleep, to repose (_noun-like_) chamber

cam3.text: U+8B56 (F2.37 譖) 讒言毀人之行也 (Page 15|n58 譖) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 殷 to 毀.

zam3.text: U+8B56 僭 cam3 (per Fan Wan) (F2.37 譖 _Ch'am_ (cam3)) 讒言毀人之行也 (Page 15|n58 譖 _Ch'am_ (cam3); here normalised to _Cham_ (zam3)) To slander, to vilify (Kangxi: 愬也, 䛼也, 讒也); to discredit; _~~ch'am~~ ``cham`` in_ (zam3 jin4 譖言), slanderous aspersions; _~~ch'am~~ ``cham`` yan_ (zam3 jan4 譖人), to asperse one. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 殷 to 毀. 【譖】 (tsrimH) (_verb-like_) to slander

cam3.text: U+8B96 唚 (for vernacular "long-winded" sense) 譖 (for vernacular "long-winded" sense) (F2.37 讖) 符讖騐也凡讖緯皆以術數言將來之騐也 (Page 15|n58 讖) To verify, to prove (Kangxi: 驗也); a prognostic (Kangxi: 凡讖緯皆言將來之驗也); to fulfill; _ch'am u_ (cam3 jyu5 讖語), a prophecy, a hidden meaning; _ch'am hi_ (cam3 hei3 讖氣) tiresome, talkative; _ch'am wai_ (cam3 wai5 讖緯), to agree with the prophecy. Williams' 讖緯: Corrected _wai_ (wai2) to _wai_ (wai5). 【讖】 (cam3) Long-winded or verbose. 又長又讖 (jau6 coeng4 jau6 cam3): "Both long and verbose." 咪咁讖啦 (mai5 gam3 cam3 la1): "Don't be so long-winded!"

cam4.text: U+5C0B (F2.37 尋) 搜求也覓也 (Page 552 尋) To hunt after, to search, on the track of; to seek, to investigate (Kangxi: 繹理也, 求也); to use, to employ (Kangxi: 用也); to continue, to connect with (Kangxi: 仍也繼也); usually, ordinarily; temporary, unexpectedly (Kangxi: 俄也); ~~for a long time;~~ common (Kangxi: 俗謂庸常爲尋常); a measure of eight cubits (度名, 八尺曰尋), or like the outstretched arms; _ts'am 'm cheuk_ (cam4 m4 zoek6 尋唔着) I can not find it; _ts'am mi_ (cam4 mei6 尋味) to wish more of, as of a tidbit; _ts'am t'au lo_ (cam4 tau4 lou6 尋頭路) seeking for work; _ts'am sheung_ (cam4 soeng4 尋常), usual, common; _kan ts'am_ (gan1 cam4 跟尋), to hunt after one; _ts'am mut_ (cam4 mut6 尋歿) died unexpectedly; _ts'am t'iu kai_ (cam4 tiu4 gai3-2 尋條計) to think out a plan; _chau ts'am_ (zaau2 cam4 找尋), to seek everywhere for; _ts'am ch'an to k'u_ (cam4 can3 dou3-2 keoi5 尋趁到佢), I've got track of him; _ts'am kan man tai_ (cam4 gan1 man6 dai2 尋根問底), to investigate thoroughly; _ts'am sz'_ (cam4 si6 尋事) to meddle with; _ts'am mat_ (cam4 mat6 尋物) a colloquial phrase for yesterday. Fan Wan entry: The headword 尋 is printed like ⿳彑⿰工几寸. Williams' "for a long time": Kangxi's explanation 長也 is accompanied by an example ending in 凡物長謂之尋, so it appears to indicate spatial length rather than temporal. Williams' 尋物: Probably assimilation of 尋日 (cam4 jat6) into 尋物 (cam4 mat6). 【尋】 (zim) (_noun-like_) fathom 【尋日】 (cam4 jat6) Yesterday. 尋日病咗 (cam4 jat6 beng6 zo2): "Yesterday I got sick."

U+6C89 沈 湛 cam4-2 (rising second in vernacular 沉沉地) (F2.37 沉) 汲也溺也 (Page 15|n58 沉沈) To sink (Kangxi 沈: 沒也); to immerse; to put under water, to be lost; to quash, to suppress; muddy, deep, dull, as colors; a lake (Kangxi 沈: 齊人謂湖曰沈); _shat shau ch'am chui_ (sat6 sau2 cam4 zeoi6 實首沉墜) garrulous; _ch'am lun_ (cam4 leon4 沉淪), lost, ruined; _ch'am mut_ (cam4 mut6 沉沒) to lose in the water; _ch'am un_ (cam4 jyun1 沉淵), lost in the depths; _ch'am on_ (cam4 on3 沉案) to quash a case; _ch'am nik_ (cam4 nik1 沉溺) drowned, in doting on; _ch'am ~~chung~~ ``ch'ung``_ (cam4 cung5 沉重) very sick; _shap ch'am kau p'o_ (sap6 cam4 gau2 pou4 十沉九浮), the odds are against him; _ch'am heung_ (cam4 hoeng1 沉香), aloes. (Page 16|n59 湛 _Cham_ (zaam3); here corrected to _Ch'am_ (cam4) and normalised to 沉) to sink in (Kangxi 湛: 與沈同); Williams' 沉溺: Changed _nik_ (nik6) to common reading _nik_ (nik1). Williams' 沉重: Corrected _chung_ (zung6 去聲) to _ch'ung_ (cung5 上聲). For commentary on departing-tone 直禁切 _cham_ (zam6) in relation to the vernacular zam6 for "drown", see 浸 zam3. 【沉】 (drim) (_verb-like_) to sink

zaam3.text: U+6E5B zaam6 (per Kangxi 宅減切 for non-surname senses and 丈陷切 for surname sense) (F2.65 湛) 澄湛清也淡也又姓 (Page 16|n59 湛) A northern affluent of the Yellow R. near Shansi (山西) (Kangxi: 一曰湛水豫章浸 etc.); deep (Kangxi: 深貌), limpid, still (Kangxi: 澄也澹也), as water; clear, calm (Kangxi: 湛安也), as a quiet moonlight. Heavy, thick (Kangxi: 重厚貌), abundantly dewy (Kangxi: 露盛貌). A surname (Kangxi: 姓也). 【湛】 (dreamX) (_adjective-like_) heavy, thick (_adjective-like_) deep

cam4.text: U+71D6 (F2.37 燖) 火熟物也 (Page 552 燖) To scald, as a fowl, in order to pluck it; to steep in hot broth, to make a broth of. Fan Wan entry: The component 尋 of the headword is printed like ⿳彑⿰工几寸.
U+9414 jam4 (alternative per 《廣韻》餘針切) (F2.37 鐔) 劍鼻 (Page 552 鐔) The guard on the hilt of a sword (Kangxi: 劒鼻也); a dagger, a dirk (Kangxi: 鐔似劒而小);
U+9703 (F2.37 霃) 天久陰 (Page 15|n58 霃) Dark, cloudy for a long time (Kangxi: 久隂也); _yam yam ch'am ch'am_ (jam1 jam1 cam4 cam4 陰陰霃霃), dull, rainy-looking.

U+9B35 cim4 (alternative per 《廣韻》昨鹽切) (F2.37 鬵) 釜鬵 (Page 552 鬵) A vase with a large belly and small mouth (Kangxi: 一曰鼎大上小下若甑曰鬵); a large kettle or boiler (Kangxi: 大釜也). Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𩰿 to 鬵. 【鬵】 (zim, dzim, dzjem) (_noun-like_) cauldron

U+9B6B sam2 (per 《廣韻》式荏切) zam2 (per Fan Wan) (F2.37 魫 _Cham_ (zam2)) 魚名 (Page 15|n58 ~~𩵫~~``魫`` _Ch'am_ (cam2); here corrected to _Ch'am_ (cam4)) The roe or eggs of fish (Kangxi: 魚子); the name of a fish (Fan Wan: 魚名). The reading _ch'am_ (cam4) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 持林切, explained 魚子也. Williams' placement under _Ch'am_ (cam2) is erroneous because it does not follow any of the following: _cham_ (zam2) per Fan Wan (which places it between 枕 and 怎) _ch'am_ (cam4) per 《集韻》持林切. _sham_ (sam2) per 《廣韻》式任切 rising. It is possible that Williams has simply misprinted __ as __, in which case _ch'am_ (cam4) is intended.
U+9C4F (F2.37 鱏) 魚名 (Page 552 鱏) An enormous fish, with a long nose (Kangxi: 鱏長鼻魚也重千斤), found in the Yangtsz' kiang (揚子江); probably a sturgeon.
U+9C58 (F2.37 鱘) 鱘龍無鱗魚也 (Page 552 鱘) The sturgeon; _ts'am lung u_ (cam4 lung4 jyu4-2 鱘龍魚), the sturgeon. Fan Wan entry: The component 尋 of the headword is printed like ⿳彑⿰工几寸.

can1.text: U+55D4 May appear as either ⿰口眞 or ⿰口真 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.81 嗔) 怒也 (Page 17|n60 嗔) To get angry (Kangxi: 怒也); passionate, angry; to rail; _shang ch'an_ (sang1 can1 生嗔), to get into a rage; _ch'an kwai_ (can1 gwaai3 嗔怪) to scold.

U+56AB 親 (Page 554 親; here normalised to 嚫) _kong ts'an ho hau lan_ (gong2 can1 hou2 hau2 laan6 講嚫好口爛) split my mouth if I ever said so; _p'ung ts'an_ (pung3 can1 碰嚫), to run against; _kwan ts'an_ (gwaan3 can1 躀嚫), to slip down; Williams provides no explanation for the vernacular _ts'an_ (can1), notwithstanding he has supplied examples of its use. 【嚫】 (can1) Suffixed after a verb to mark negative consequence. 冷嚫 (laang5 can1): "I've caught a cold." 撞嚫 (zong6 can1): "I've bumped something." 整嚫 (zing2 can1): "I've hurt myself." 跌嚫 (dit3 can1): "I've had a fall." Suffixed after a verb to indicate "every time". 上嚫都漏 (soeng5 can1 dou1 lau6): "Every time I chow, I leak good tiles." 射嚫都中 (se6 can1 dou1 zung3): "Every time he shoots, he's on target." 飲嚫都係當歸 (jam2 can1 dou1 hai6 dong1 gwai1): "Every time she drinks medicine, it's dong quai."

U+778B May appear as either ⿰目眞 or ⿰目真 (assumed here), depending on the font. 嗔 (F1.81 瞋) 怒而張目也 (Page 18|n61 瞋) To stare at angrily, to glare at (Kangxi: 張目也, 怒也); _ch'an no mo to_ (can1 nou6 mou4 dou6 瞋怒無度) angry beyond measure. 【瞋】 (tsyhin) (_verb-like_) to glare

U+89AA (F1.81 親) 愛也近也姻也眷也 (Page 554 親) To love, to be attached to (Kangxi: 愛也); liking, pleased with; near, intimate (Kangxi: 近也); near to, belonging to one's self, personal, myself (Kangxi: 躬也, 親猶自也); a relative, related to, having affinity, kindred, kin, kith (Kangxi: 襯也, 姻也, 親者屬也, 親九族也); parents (Kangxi: 六親父母...也); a wife (Kangxi: 六親...妻...也); affianced; used for _san_ (san1 新), new (Kangxi: 通作新); _ts'an ts'ik_ (can1 cik1 親戚) one's relatives; _ts'u ts'an_ (ceoi2 can1 娶親), to take a wife; _shing ts'an_ (sing4 can1 成親), to ~~consummate a marriage~~ ``become married``; _im ts'an_ (jim4 can1 嚴親) or _fu ts'an_ (fu6 can1 父親), a father; _ts'z' ts'an_ (ci4 can1 慈親) or _mo ts'an_ (mou5 can1 母親), a mother; _ying ts'an_ (jing4 can1 迎親), to escort the bride; _teng t'au ts'an sz'_ (deng6 tau4 can1 si6 定頭親事) contracted the marriage; _yau ts'an_ (jau5 can1 有親), related to him; _ts'an pat se_ (can1 bat1 se2 親筆寫), wrote it myself; _ts'an it_ (can1 jit6 親熱) very intimate; _ts'an oi_ (can1 oi3 親愛) to love much; _ts'an kan_ (can1 gan6 親近) near to one; nearly related; _ts'an tsui_ (can1 zeoi2 親嘴), to kiss; _ts'an ngan kin_ (can1 ngaan5 gin3 親眼見) I saw it myself; _ts'an sheung ka ts'an_ (can1 soeng6 gaa1 can1 親上加親), to be doubly connected; _p'an k'ung tun luk ts'an_ (pan4 kung4 dyun6 luk6 can1 貧窮斷六親), nobody knows his poor relations. Williams' 娶親: Changed _ts'u_ (ceoi3) to common reading _ts'u_ (ceoi2). Williams' 成親: There is no connotation of sexual consummation in 成親. Although 成 can mean "complete" or "fulfil", I think it is better rendered here as "become". 【親-close】 (tshin) (_noun-like_) close one, parent (_verb-like_) to be close, to touch, (_met._) to love (_adjective-like_) close, (_met._) one's own

can3.text: U+89AA Pertaining to the in-laws. 親家 (can3 gaa1): "The in-laws' family of my son/daughter." Olden attestation for 親 _ts'an_ (can3) meaning "in-laws": 《康熙字典》親 ...又《唐韻》七遴切《集韻》《韻會》七刃切《正韻》寸遴切𠀤七去聲 ... 又《廣韻》親家也《集韻》婚姻相謂爲親... 【親-in-law】 (tshinH) (_adjective-like_) in-law

can2.text: U+54C2 (F1.82 哂) 微笑 (Page 18|n61 哂) To smile, with slight contempt (Kangxi: 笑也, 微笑); to look pleased; _ch'an siu_ (can2 siu3 哂笑) to smile; _ch'an nap_ (can2 naap6 哂納) to smile on receiving. 【哂】 (syinX) (_verb-like_) to smile (at)

U+755B zan1 (per 《廣韻》職鄰切) zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 畛) 田間水道 (Page 18|n61 畛) ~~To~~ ``Roads that`` divide fields (Kangxi: 井田閒陌也); to come before the gods ``and pray unto them`` (Kangxi: 致也); to announce ~~the emperor's coming~~ (Kangxi: 吿也); to ~~die~~ ``exterminate`` (Kangxi: 殄也, 謂殄絕); a motive; _kw'ai ch'an_ (kwai4 can2 畦畛), dikes between fields. 【畛】 (tsyinX, tsyin) (_noun-like_) interfield-path, (_met._) demarcation (_verb-like_) to announce (unto)

U+75A2 疹 can3 (per 《廣韻》丑刃切) (F1.82 疢) 熱病 (Page 18|n61 疹疢) Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𤍠 to 熱. Williams incorrectly considers 疢 as an alternative form of 疹 when it is actually the other way round. An entry for 疢 (not 疹) is here supplemented. Fever (Kangxi 疢: 熱病也). Sickness (Kangxi 疢: 疢猶病也). 【疢】 (trhinH) (_noun-like_) fever, sickness

U+75B9 zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 疹) 豆疹 (Page 18|n61 疹~~疢~~) Pustules of any kind (Kangxi 疹: 疹診也有結氣可得診見也); eruptions, pimples (Kangxi 疹: 癮疹皮外小起也); a cutaneous disease; sore lips (Kangxi 疹: 脣瘍也); _tau ch'an_ (dau6 can2 痘疹) small pox pustules; _pan ch'an_ (baan1 can2 斑疹), eruptions.

U+77E7 矤 (F1.82 矧) 况也 (Page 18|n61 矧) To stretch; still more, how much more (Kangxi: 况也); ~~to grin wide;~~ _ch'an ut_ (can2 jyut6 矧曰) to say further. Williams' "to grin wide": This is a misreading of Kangxi's example 笑不至矧, in which 矧 means "tooth-base", not "grin wide". Moreover, this sense should be read _ngan_ (ngan4) as it is an alternative form of 齗. Williams' 矧曰: Corrected _ut_ (jyut3) to _ut_ (jyut6). 【矧】 (syinX) (_conjunction-like_) moreover, let alone

U+7D3E zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 紾) 縳也繩以縳之也 (Page 18|n61 紾) To bind with cords; to turn``,`` to twist (Kangxi: 轉也); to revolve; a revolution; perverse, crabbed (Kangxi: 戾也). 【紾】 (tsyinX, trjenX) (_verb-like_) to twist

U+7E1D May appear as either ⿰糹眞 or ⿰糹真 (assumed here), depending on the font. zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 縝) 結也縷也 (Page 18|n61 縝) Filaments from a coccoon (Kangxi: 縝緻也); a thread (Kangxi: 縝纑縷也); to tie (Kangxi: 結也單也); black (Kangxi: 縝黑也); _shui ch'an pat pin_ (seoi4 can2 bat1 bin3 誰縝不變) whose black hair will not change?

U+8897 振 zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 袗) 單也衣服也盛服也又畫也 (Page 18|n61 袗) Single thin garments (Kangxi: 單也), black silk worn in summer (Kangxi: 玄服); embroidered garments (Kangxi: 畫衣); _ch'an hi kwik_ (can2 hei1 gwik1 袗絺綌) single grasscloth. 【袗】 (tsyinX, tsyinH) (_noun-like_) unlined-garment

U+8A3A zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 診) 視也騐也又診脉 (Page 18|n61 診) To examine, to look at (Kangxi: 視也); to try, to verify (Kangxi: 驗也); _ch'an mung_ (can2 mung6 診夢) to interpret a dream; _ch'an mak_ (can2 mak6 診脈) to feel the pulse. 【診】 (tsyinX, drinH) (_verb-like_) to examine

U+8EEB zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 軫) 車橫木所以斂載也又宿名 (Page 18|n61 軫) The boards of a carriage (Kangxi: 車後橫木也); the cross board; to move (Kangxi: 轉也動也); a carriage; cramped, disturbed, distressed (Kangxi: 軫懷痛念也); the last of the 28 constellations (Kangxi: 宿名), _β_, _δ_, _η_, _γ_, in Corvus; pegs of a lute (Kangxi: 琴下轉絃者謂之軫); _~~hu~~ ``u`` ch'an_ (jyu1 can2 紆軫), crooked, winding. Williams' "boards of a carriage": Strictly speaking, 橫木 does not specifically refer to boards, only to "horizontal timber". Williams' 紆軫: Corrected _hu_ (heoi1) to _u_ (jyu1).

U+9B12 May appear as either ⿱髟眞 or ⿱髟真 (assumed here), depending on the font. zan2 (per 《廣韻》章忍切) (F1.82 鬒) 髮之黑也 (Page 18|n61 鬒) Bushy, black hair (Kangxi: 稠髮也, 黑髮也); a fine head of black hair. 【鬒】 (tsyinX) (_noun-like_) bushy-hair, black-hair

can3.text: U+6AEC (F1.85 櫬) 棺櫬 (Page 19|n62 櫬) A coffin (Kangxi: 棺也); to collect firewood (Kangxi: 櫬采薪); _kun ch'an_ (gun1 can3 棺櫬) a coffin; _kun ch'an uk_ (gun1 can3 uk1 棺櫬屋) a sort of dead house or shed at a grave. Hibiscus flowers (Kangxi: 櫬木槿, 今王蒸). The Chinese parasol tree (Kangxi: 櫬梧, 今梧桐).

U+896F can3-2 (rising second in vernacular 襯襯地) (F1.85 襯) 貼身衣也又襯貼 (Page 18|n61 襯) Inner garments (Kangxi: 近身衣也); to help, to assist; to patronize; to befriend; to give (Kangxi: 施與亦曰襯); to give effect to, to show off; to match; _ch'an kan_ (can3 gan1 襯巾), a sash; _ch'an sham_ (can3 saam1 襯衫), inner skirts; _pong ch'an_ (bong1 can3 幫襯) to give custom to; _ch'an chu k'u_ (can3 zyu6 keoi5 襯住佢), to fellowship; _ch'an chi_ (can3 zi2 襯紙), a flyleaf in books; _ch'an t'ip_ (can3 tip3-2 襯貼) plaits on a petticoat. 【幫襯】 (bong1 can3) To patronise (be a customer of a business). 【襯】 (can3) To match, to suit. 佢哋幾襯吖 (keoi5 dei6 gei2 can3 a1): "They do quite match each other." 撞色唔襯 (zong6 sik1 m4 can3): "The colours clash and don't suit." 【老襯】 (lou5 can3) A gullible person. 做老襯 (zou6 lou5 can3): "Be a mug." 搵老襯 (wan2 lou5 can3): "Find a sucker of someone" (i.e. trick a gullible person).

U+8D81 趂 (F1.85 趂) 及時也逐也從也 (Page 19|n62 趁趂) To follow, to come up behind, to walk after (Kangxi 趁: 𧾍也, 自後及之也, 逐也); to avail, to take; to embrace an opportunity; at the time of; _ch'an hu ta shi_ (can3 heoi1 daa2 si5 趁墟打市), to go to market; _ch'an shi hau_ (can3 si4 hau6 趁時候) to improve the time; _ch'an ki ui_ (can3 gei1 wui6 趁機會) take advantage of the opportunity; _ch'an fung shai li_ (can3 fung1 sai2 lei5 趁風駛𢃇), hoist sail when there's a wind; _ch'an un_ (can3 jyun6 趁願) as I wished it; _ch'an shai_ (can3 sai3 趁勢) avail of a favorable time. Williams' "improve": In the (now) dated sense of employing to good purpose.
U+8D82 (F1.85 趂) 及時也逐也從也 (Page 19|n62 趁趂)
U+9F54 (Page 19|n62 齔) To shed the milk teeth, as children do (Kangxi: 毀齒也 etc.); shedding or renewing the teeth.

can4.text: U+5875 (F1.82 塵) 塵上塵埃 (Page 18|n61 塵) Dust, small particles (Kangxi: 埃也); effluvia, atoms, molecules; the world; worldly vice and pleasures; confused times; _ch'an t'au tai_ (can4 tau4 daai6 塵頭大) a cloud of dust; _sai ch'an_ (sai2 can4 洗塵), to wash down the dust, to give a feast to friends come back; _po hau ch'an_ (bou6 hau6 can4 步後塵), to follow another's example; _ch'an oi_ (can4 oi1 塵埃), dust; _ch'an kau_ (can4 gau3 塵垢), covered with dust; _fung ch'an_ (fung1 can4 風塵), dusty from travel; _mo yat tim ch'an_ (mou4 jat1 dim2 can4 無一點塵), clean; _ch'an tsuk_ (can4 zuk6 塵俗), _ch'an shai_ (can4 sai3 塵世), or _hung ch'an_ (hung4 can4 紅塵), the dusty world; _fat ch'an_ (fat1 can4 拂塵), to dust. Williams' "confused times": Especially warfare. Williams' 塵垢: Changed _kau_ (gau2) to common reading _kau_ (gau3). 【塵】 (drin) (_noun-like_) dust 【沙塵】 (saa1 can4) Arrogant or cocky. 咁沙塵 (gam3 saa1 can4): "So arrogant of him." There is also the extended version 沙塵白霍, but that leads into a rabbit hole of debate about the origins of the phrase, and whether 塵 should be written 陳, and whether 霍 should be written 攉 or 醭.
U+852F (Page 18|n61 蔯) A medicinal herb (Kangxi: 茵蔯也), good for rheumatism; _yan ch'an_ (jan1 can4 茵蔯), a plant like origanum.

U+9673 can4-2 (vernacular rising-tone for endearment) (F1.82 陳) 列也張也敷也告也又姓 (Page 18|n61 陳) To arrange, to set in order, to spread out (Kangxi: 列也布也); to say, to reply; to state, to express; to memorialize; a long time, an old thing (Kangxi: 故也, 久也); many, all (Kangxi: 衆也); a feudal state (Kangxi: 國名) near the present capital of Honan (河南); _tsoi ch'an ~~ts'ut~~ ``tsut`` leung_ (zoi6 can4 zyut6 loeng4 在陳絕糧), to lack food in Ch'in---to be in straits; _p'o ch'an_ (pou1 can4 鋪陳), to seat or rank in order; _ch'an ch'it_ (can4 cit3 陳設) to arrange in order; _sheung piu ch'an ts'ing_ (soeng5 biu2 can4 cing4 上表陳情), to state one's thoughts to H. I. M.; _ch'an p'i kom kau_ (can4 pei4 gam3 gau6 陳皮咁舊) old as a dried orange skin, faded. Williams' 在陳絕糧: Corrected _ts'ut_ (cyut6) to _tsut_ (zyut6). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【陳】 (drin) (_verb-like_) to arrange, to display (_verb-like_) to describe (_adjective-like_) aged

zan6.text: U+9673 (F1.85 陣) 軍師行伍之列 (F1.85 陳) 同上 (Page 17|n60 陣陳)

cang4.text: U+5C64 (F2.31 層) 重叠也 (Page 557 層) A story of a house; a layer, a strata, a tier, a stage; a lamina, a plate, or whatever is piled or laid on; still more, added to; a step, a degree above (Kangxi: 級也); a classifier of stories; _ko k'u yat ts'ang_ (gou1 keoi5 jat1 cang4 高佢一層), one peg higher than it; _mo ts'ang_ (mou4 cang4 無層), no progress, it can't be done; _ts'in ts'ang chi_ (cin1 cang4 zi2 千層紙), foliated mica; _sheung tai sam ts'ang_ (soeng5 dai6 saam1 cang4 上第三層), go up to the third story; _~~chung~~ ``ch'ung`` yat ts'ang_ (cung5 jat1 cang4 重一層), one degree heavier; _yau ts'ang yau ts'z'_ (jau5 cang4 jau5 ci3 有層有次) there are proper gradations. Williams' 重一層: Corrected _chung_ (zung6 去聲) to _ch'ung_ (cung5 上聲). A multi-storey house (Kangxi: 重屋也). Multi-layered (Kangxi: 凡物之重者通曰層). 【層】 (dzong) (_noun-like_) storey

U+66FE (F2.31 曾) 嘗也又不料之詞又反詞 (Page 557 曾) An adverb of time; past, already, done (Kangxi: 經也, 嘗也); how? a sign of past time; _'m ts'ang_ (m4 cang4 唔曾) or _mi ts'ang_ (mei6 cang4 未曾), not yet; often a polite way of saying no; _ya ts'ang tso kwo_ (jaa5 cang4 zou6 gwo3 也曾做過) it is already done; _ho ts'ang_ (ho4 cang4 何曾), when ~~will it be~~ ``was it``? _ts'ang kin_ (cang4 gin3 曾見) I have seen it; _tso 'm ts'ang_ (zou6 m4 cang4 做唔曾), is it done yet? Williams' "how?": This appears to be an attempt at rendering the very vague and unhelpful 《說文》詞之舒也. Note that Kangxi has this sense under 昨稜切 _ts'ang_ (cang4), and includes such examples as 《論語》曾是以爲孝乎 that other sources would instead put under the reading _tsang_ (zang1). For example, 萌典 puts the "astonishingly" sense (乃、竟然) under zeng with the example ...曾是以為孝乎. Williams' 唔曾: Dated. Astonishingly. 【曾-voiced】 (dzong) (_adverb-like_) once, ever (_adverb-like_) astonishingly, (_met._) and yet, dare

zang1.text: U+66FE (F2.29 曾) 則也乃也又曾祖曾孫 (Page 556 曾) To duplicate (Kangxi: 曾猶重也), (...); _tsang sun_ (zang1 syun1 曾孫), a great-grandson; _tsang tso mo_ (zang1 zou2 mou5 曾祖母), a great-grandmother; _tsang tso_ (zang1 zou2 曾祖), a great-grandfather. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【曾-unvoiced】 (tsong) (_adjective-like_) great-

cap1.text: U+54A0 緝 zap1 (alternative per 《廣韻》子入切) To whisper in the ear (Kangxi: 聶語也, 咠咠口舌聲也, 咠咠譖言也). 【咠】 (tship, tsip) (_verb-like_) to whisper

U+6222 zap1 (per 《廣韻》阻立切) (F2.39 戢) 安集也止也歛也藏也 (Page 557 戢) To collect in an arsenal, to store up weapons (Kangxi: 藏兵也); to gather (Kangxi: 斂也); to desist from (Kangxi: 止也), to lay aside, as arms; to fold, as wings; _ts'ai ts'ap ts'ap_ (cai4 cap1 cap1 齊戢戢) to cut off evenly; _ch'ai ts'ap to shat_ (caai2 cap1 dou3 sat6 踹戢到實) tread it, press it down very solid; _tsoi ts'ap kon kwo_ (zoi3 cap1 gon1 gwo1 載戢干戈), to lay by shields and spears. Williams' 載戢干戈: Corrected _tsoi_ (zoi2 上聲) to _tsoi_ (zoi3 去聲). 【戢】 (tsrip) (_verb-like_) to store (up), to gather (in), (_met._) to restrain, to check 【齊戢戢】 (cai4 cap1 cap1) Neat and even (not jagged).

U+6FC8 zap1 (per 《廣韻》阻立切) (F2.39 濈) 口舌聲 (Page 557 濈) Water rapidly flowing out (Kangxi: 濈水行出也); rapid (Kangxi: 疾貌); cordial, harmonious, agreeing (Kangxi: 和也); to accord with. 【濈】 (tsrip) (_adjective-like_) huddled, accordant

U+7DDD (F2.39 緝) 繼續也續也 (Page 557 緝) To twist a cord (Kangxi: 績也); to join, to continue (Kangxi: 續也); to come after, to follow, to succeed; to pursue, to follow after, to be on the search for; to apprehend; _ts'ap wok_ (cap1 wok6 緝獲) to pursue and seize; _ts'ap sz'_ (cap1 si1 緝私) to watch smugglers; _ts'ap sz' shun_ (cap1 si1 syun4 緝私船), revenue cutters; _ts'ap cheuk_ (cap1 zoek6 緝着) caught him; _ch'a ts'ap_ (caa4 cap1 查緝) on the search; _ts'un ts'ap_ (ceon4 cap1 巡緝) to get on the trail. 【緝】 (tship) (_verb-like_) to spin (thread), to hem (_verb-like_) to continue Used in 緝熙. 【緝熙】 (tship xi) (_adjective-like_) brilliant

U+847A zap1 (per 《廣韻》子入切 for spinous-grass sense and Kangxi 子入切 for "piled" sense) (F2.39 葺) 修補也 (Page 558 葺) To put in order, to repair (Kangxi: 修補也); to cover, to thatch (Kangxi: 覆也, 苫也); a spinous grass (Kangxi: 茨也); _sau ts'ap_ (sau1 cap1 修葺) to refit, as a house; _ts'ap po_ (cap1 bou2 葺補), to repair. Piled, stacked (Kangxi: 累也).

U+8F2F zap6 (per 《廣韻》秦入切) (F2.39 輯) 斂也聚也集也 (Page 558 輯) To collect the materials to form a chariot, a car well arranged (Kangxi: 車和輯也 etc.); union, concord, agreement (Kangxi: 和也, 睦也); to make every thing agreeable, put in its proper place; to compile; to act gently and cordially (Kangxi: 顏色和柔亦曰輯); to collect, to congregate (Kangxi: 斂也); _wo ts'ap_ (wo4 cap1 和輯) in good accord, to pacify; _ning ts'ap_ (ning4 cap1 寧輯) peaceful; _sau ts'ap lun pong_ (sau1 cap1 leon4 bong1 修輯鄰邦), to induce the neighboring states to be at peace. Classifier for parts, volumes, etc. of published works. 【輯】 (dzip) (_adjective-like_) concordant (_verb-like_) to gather (up) (_verb-like_) to compile

cat1.text: U+4E03 柒 (anti-alteration) (F1.86 七) 數名 (F1.86 㭍) 同上 (Page 559 七柒) Seven; _ts'at shap_ (cat1 sap6 七十) seventy; _shap ts'at_ (sap6 cat1 十七) seventeen; _ts'at ling pat lok_ (cat1 ling4 baat3 lok6 七零八落) near destruction, devastated, almost ruined; _ts'at ts'ang kung fu_ (cat1 cang4 gung1 fu1 七層功夫), a work nearly done; _ts'at tsik_ (cat1 zik6 七夕) 7th evening of the 7th month, a festival; _ts'at tsut_ (cat1 zyut6 七絕) a pentameter of four lines; _ts'at lut_ (cat1 leot6 七律) one of eight lines; _yat yat ts'at_ (jat1 jat1 cat1 一一七) I don't think much of that, so so; _tso ts'at_ (zou6 cat1 做七) to attend to the funereal rites on every seventh day; _tsok ts'at p'in_ (zok3 cat1 pin1 作七編), to write seven essays. The second (柒) ``is`` used for the compound form (...). Williams' 七絕: While each seven-character line can be though of as being in pentameter, the Chinese view does not have a notion of stressed vs unstressed syllables; rather, the phrasing of the line is viewed as a four-character run followed by a three-character run. Williams' 七律: Corrected _lut_ (leot1) to _lut_ (leot6). 【七】 (tshit) (_noun-like_) seven (_verb-like_) to septuple
U+67D2 (F1.86 七) 數名 (F1.86 㭍) 同上 (Page 559 七柒) The second (柒) (...) is also a sort of tree (Kangxi 柒: 木名) (...).

U+6F06 柒 桼 (F1.86 漆) 木之滓也可以漆物 (Page 559 七柒) The second (柒) (...) is also (...) a contracted form of the next (漆) (Kangxi 柒: 俗漆字). (Page 559 ~~？~~``漆``) Varnish, lacquer; viscid or resinous juices which can be used in painting (Kangxi: 木汁可髤物); to paint, to varnish; black, as lacquer (Kangxi: 物之黑者曰漆); adhesive; exceedingly friendly, doating on; _yau ts'at_ (jau4 cat1 油漆) to paint and varnish; _yau ts'at lo_ (jau4 cat1 lou2 油漆佬), a house-painter; _ts'at shik_ (cat1 sik6 漆蝕) poisoned with lacquer; _ts'at hi_ (cat1 hei3 漆器) lacquered-ware; _fo ts'at_ (fo2 cat1 火漆) sealing-wax; _fo ts'at p'in_ (fo2 cat1 pin3 火漆片) wafers; _ho ts'at shui_ (hou2 cat1 seoi2 好漆水), lustrous varnish; _ts'at tsing_ (cat1 zing1 漆精), fine varnish; _ts'ing u kau ts'at_ (cing4 jyu4 gaau1 cat1 情如膠漆) his disposition is very affectionate; _kam ts'at_ (gam1 cat1 金漆) gilt lacquer; _yap ts'at_ (jap6 cat1 入漆) been varnished; _ts'at shu_ (cat1 syu6 漆樹) the varnish tree. 【漆】 (tshit) (_noun-like_) varnish-tree, varnish

cat6.text: U+28CCD 𫵱 **One of the five profanities censored on Hong Kong television.** 【𨳍】 (cat6) (_vulgar_) A flaccid penis. A literal translation would be "droop-dick". For non-literal senses, see the Wiktionary entry for 𨳍. Suprisingly, the alternative form 𫵱 is recorded in Eitel (1877) under _ts'at_ (cat6); the entry simply reads "_Vulgar._ the penis".

ciu3.text: U+5062 (Page 562 偢 _Ts'au_ (cau1); here corrected to _Ts'iu_ (ciu3)) Malevolent (Kangxi: 傻偢不仁); ill-natured;

cau1.text: U+59AF cau4 (alternative per 《集韻》陳留切 for "moved" sense) dik6 (alternative per 《集韻》亭歴切 for "moved" and "unsettled" senses) duk6 (alternative per 《集韻》徒沃切 for "moved" sense) luk6 (alternative per 《集韻》盧谷切 for "moved" sense) The heart moved (Kangxi: 心動也). Moved (Kangxi: 動也). Unsettled (Kangxi: 人不靜曰妯 etc.). 【妯】 (trhjuw) (_verb-like_) to move

zuk6.text: U+59AF suk6 (per Fan Wan) (F1.66 妯 _Shuk_ (suk6)) 妯娌嬸姆曰妯娌 (Page 35* 妯) Two sisters-in-law call each other _chuk li_ (zuk6 lei5 妯娌) (Kangxi: 兄弟之妻相呼曰妯娌); ~~also called _chau li_ (zau3 lei5) in Canton.~~ Fan Wan entry: Corrected 口 to 曰. Williams' "also called ...": I suspect _chau_ (zau3) is a typo for _chau_ (zau6), which would be a misreading based on analogy with 袖.

ciu2.text: U+6100 cau1 (alternative per 《集韻》雌由切) cau5 (alternative per 《廣韻》在九切) (F1.50 愀 _Ts'au_ (cau1)) 色變也 (Page 562 ~~愁~~``愀`` _Ts'au_ (cau1); here normalised to _Ts'iu_ (ciu2)) ~~To blush, to redden, to change color~~ ``To change the countenance`` (Kangxi: 容色變也); ~~downhearted, sad.~~ Williams misprints 愀 as 愁. In doing so, Williams has erroneously included the definition "downhearted, sad". That definition is only applicable to 愁. 【愀】 (tshjewX, dzjuwX) (_verb-like_) to change countenance

cau1.text: U+62BD (F1.50 抽) 收也援引也 (Page 26|n69 抽) To take from, to take out (Kangxi: 拔也除也); to take a dividend; to expand, as spring does; to lift; to levy, to assess, as duty; ~~to select,~~ to draw out (Kangxi: 引也); to utter aloud (Kangxi: 讀也); to whip; to receive one's portion (Kangxi: 收也); to raise water; a tenth, a tithe; _ch'au shui_ (cau1 seoi2 抽水), to take the fee at gaming; _ch'au kan_ (cau1 gan1 抽筋), spasms of the tendons; _ch'au hi_ (cau1 hei2 抽起), lift it out; _ch'au ch'ing k'u lai_ (cau1 cing3 keoi5 lai4 抽秤佢嚟), bring him along; _ta ch'au fung_ (daa2 cau1 fung1 打抽風), to beg aid; _pi pin ch'au k'u_ (bei2 bin1 cau1 keoi5 畀鞭抽佢), drive him off with a whip; _ch'au k'u leung ha_ (cau1 keoi5 loeng5 haa5 抽佢兩吓), whipped him twice; _ch'au shau_ (cau1 sau1 抽收), to levy duty on; _ch'au shan pat ha_ (cau1 san1 bat1 haa6 抽身不暇) no leisure at all; _ch'au shat_ (cau1 sat6 抽實) lift it up firmly; _ch'au mok_ (cau1 mok1 抽剝) to extort, to exact a high fee; _ch'au ch'au_ (cau1 cau4 抽籌), or _ch'au t'au_ (cau1 tau4 抽頭), to divide winnings; _ch'au han_ (cau1 haan4 抽閒), to take a leisure hour; _ch'au fan_ (cau1 fan1 抽分), to take a commission, to reserve a part; _ka yat ch'au_ (gaa1 jat1 cau1 加一抽), take a tenth. Williams' 抽筋: Medically, these are skeletal muscle contractions (i.e. cramps). Williams' 抽剝: Corrected _mok_ (mok6) to _mok_ (mok1). 【抽】 (trhjuw) (_verb-like_) to pull out 【抽】 (cau1) Classifier for things in a bunch that will fall together when carried by holding the top (or a handle or similar at the top). 大抽鎖匙 (daai6 cau1 so2 si4): "Big bunch of keys." To fight. (See 隻抽 (_Chinese chess_) To win a piece with check, either by skewering, forking, or discovery. 將軍抽車 (zoeng1 gwan1 cau1 geoi1): "Check, skewering a chariot." 抽炮 (cau1 paau3): "Check, winning a cannon." 【抽氣扇】 (cau1 hei3 sin3) A ventilating fan. 【一抽二褦】 (jat1 cau1 ji6 nang3) Many bags or much baggage to be carried. (Literally, "one carried-bunch and two linked-bunches".) 【老抽】 (lou5 cau1) Old soy sauce (dark coloured but less salty). 【生抽】 (saang1 cau1) Young soy sauce (light coloured but more salty). 【隻抽】 (zek3 cau1) To fight one-on-one.
U+63AB (Page 560 掫 _Tsau_ (zau1); here corrected to _Ch'au_ (cau1)) to seize, to take with the hand (Kangxi: 手取物也);

zau1.text: U+63AB (F1.50 掫) 擊也守夜擊刀斗之類 (Page 560 掫) To strike the rounds at night, to pace the beat at night (Kangxi: 夜戒守有所擊也); _kan tsau tsau_ (gan2 zau1 zau1 緊掫掫), tie it up very tight,---a colloquial phrase.

U+63EA 揫 cau1 (per Fan Wan) (Page 562 揫揪 _Ts'au_ (cau1); here normalised to _Tsau_ (zau1)) To bind, to gather in sheaves (Kangxi 揫: 束也, 斂也); to compress in binding; to collect, to assemble (Kangxi 揫: 聚也); to lift, to grasp, to clutch; to gripe; _~~ts'au~~ ``tsau`` chu_ (zau1 zyu6 揪住) to seize fast; _~~ts'au~~ ``tsau`` i_ (zau1 ji5 揪耳), to nab by the ears; _~~ts'au~~ ``tsau`` ch'uk_ (zau1 cuk1 揪束) to bind up, as a sheaf is. Modern dictionaries distinguish 揫 for the "bind" sense, and 揪 for the "grasp/clutch" sense. Kangxi considers 揪 to be the same as 揫, and their separation to be erroneous: 按揪揫同字分音各訓誤. 【揪】 (zau1) To catch or capture. 畀差佬揪 (bei2 caai1 lou2 zau1): "Caught by a copper."
U+63EB

cau1.text: U+6978 (F1.50 楸) 木名 (Page 562 楸) A strong wood; the tree is like a yew or cypress; _ts'au p'ing_ (cau1 ping4 楸枰), a chess-board (Kangxi: 棊局也). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 本 to 木. Williams' 楸枰: Specifically, a surrounding-chess (go) board.
U+6E6B (F1.50 湫) 水名 (Page 562 湫) A pond, a fish-pond, a pool (Kangxi: 北人呼水池爲湫); Kangxi has the "pool" sense under 卽由切 or 將由切 _tsau_ (zau1), but 《廣韻》 has 七由切 _ts'au_ (cau1), explained 水池名北人呼.

zau1.text: U+6E6B cau1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.50 湫 _Ts'au_ (cau1)) 水名 (Page 562 湫 _Ts'au_ (cau1); here normalised to _Tsau_ (zau1)) a small tributary of the Yellow R. near Lin hien (臨縣) in Shansi (山西); cool (Kangxi: 涼貌); mournful (Kangxi: 憂愁貌), to excite sad feelings.

ziu2.text: U+6E6B Narrow (Kangxi: 湫隘也). Low-lying (Kangxi: 湫下也).

cau1.text: U+714D cau5 (per 《玉篇》慈糾切) ziu2 (per 《玉篇》子了切) (Page 563 煍) To change the color of (Kangxi: 變色也); to colly, to blacken, as with smoke; _fo t'am ts'au_ (fo2 taam4 cau1 火燂煍), the fire blackens it; _ts'au hak_ (cau1 hak1 煍黑) smoked and blackened. Williams' : Changed _hak_ (haak1) to _hak_ (hak1).

U+7633 (F1.50 瘳) 病好曰瘳 (Page 26|n69 瘳) Convalescent (Kangxi: 疾病瘉也); curable; to cure; _k'ut tsat pat ch'au_ (kyut3 zat6 bat1 cau1 厥疾不瘳), his disease was incurable. Williams' 厥疾不瘳: 《書經》 has 厥疾弗瘳. To diminish, to damage (Kangxi: 損也). 【瘳】 (trhjuw) (_verb-like_) to recuperate

U+7785 偢 cau2 (possibly from Mandarin-matching) (Page 562 偢; here normalised to 瞅) _'m ts'au ts'oi_ (m4 cau1 coi2 唔瞅睬), not to speak to, the cut direct. The character 瞅 is not attested in the Middle Chinese rime books, nor is it recorded in Kangxi. Modern Cantonese dictionaries typically record a reading of cau2 (rising tone), which I wager is from analogy with the Mandarin reading chou. And yet: The variant form 偢 appears under the Kangxi entry for 睬, with the explicit reading _ts'au_ (cau1) in level tone: 《康熙字典》睬 《字彙補》此宰切音彩偢睬俗言也詞家多用此字偢音秋 The common Cantonese reading is cau1, consistent with Williams, and as sung by Alan Tam in 誰可改變 (1984) (containing the phrase 不瞅不睬).

U+79CB 秌 (F1.50 秋) 四季之一 (F1.50 龝) 同上 (Page 562 秋) Autumn (Kangxi: 金行之時); harvest, the time of reaping; the return of the year, a year; autumnal; sorrowful, unhappy (Kangxi: 西方者秋秋愁也 etc.); _mak ts'au_ (mak6 cau1 麥秋), wheat harvest; _ts'au fan_ (cau1 fan1 秋分), autumnal equinox; _chang ts'au it_ (zaang1 cau1 jit6 爭秋熱) heat of dog-days; _ts'au t'in_ (cau1 tin1 秋天) autumn; _ts'au ho mo fan_ (cau1 hou4 mou4 faan6 秋毫無犯) not the error of an autumn's down; _ts'in ts'au_ (cin1 cau1 千秋), a thousand autumns --- a birthday; _ts'au kun_ (cau1 gun1 秋官), a judge, because cases are decided in autumn; _ts'au shau_ (cau1 sau1 秋收), harvest; _ts'au hau shin_ (cau1 hau6 sin3 秋後扇) a fan in winter --- a useless thing; _ts'au hi_ (cau1 hei3 秋氣) cool autumnal weather. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 土 to 上. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【秋】 (tshjuw) (_noun-like_) autumn
U+7BD8 (F1.50 篘) 酒篘 (Page 26|n69 篘) A strainer made of wicker or bamboo for straining spirits (Kangxi: 酒籠漉取酒也).

U+7D2C (Page 27|n70 紬 _Ch'au_ (cau4); here corrected to _Ch'au_ (cau1)) to draw out threads for weaving (Kangxi: 紬抽也); to arrange the details of a subject, to search for first causes (Kangxi: 紬繹者引其端緒也);

cau4.text: U+7D2C (F1.52 綢) 綢緞又密也纏也 (F1.52 紬) 同上 (Page 27|n70 紬)

cau1.text: U+97A6 (F1.50 鞦) 馬靷又鞦韆 (Page 563 鞦) The traces of a carriage (Kangxi: 車鞦也); _ts'au ts'in_ (cau1 cin1 鞦韆), a swing, a whirligig, a see-saw; _ta ts'au ts'in_ (daa2 cau1 cin1 打鞦韆), to swing. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 䩑 to 靷. In Cantonese, 韆鞦 (cin1 cau1) "thousand autumns" is preferred over 鞦韆 (cau1 cin1) "autumns thousand". See 韆鞦.
U+9C0C (F1.50 鰌) 與鰍同泥鰌又海鰌魚 (Page 563 鰌鰍)

U+9C0D 鰌 (F1.50 鰍) 魚名 (Page 563 鰌鰍) An eel; the mud or conger eel (Kangxi 鰌: 鰼也); _nai ts'au_ (nai4 cau1 泥鰍), a freshwater eel; _hoi ts'au_ (hoi2 cau1 海鰍), a sea-dragon, myriads of miles long; a long, narrow boat; _ts'au u kwat_ (cau1 jyu4 gwat1 鰍魚骨) whalebone. Williams' "eel": More a loach than an eel.
U+9D96 (F1.50 鶖) 秃鶖食魚之鳥 (Page 563 鶖) A long legged bird like an adjutant, having red eyes and bare neck (Kangxi: 水鳥也 etc.); it eats snakes. 【鶖】 (tshjuw) (_noun-like_) marabou

cau2.text: U+4E11 (F1.54 丑) 辰名 (Page 28|n71 丑) The second of the 12 horary signs; the 4th watch, from 1 to 3 A. M.; it is denoted by an ox, and persons born in this hour are likely to be dull. A clown, a fool character. 【丑】 (trhjuwX) (_noun-like_) ox-branch
U+8DA3 Horse master (Kangxi: 趣馬, 趣七口反掌馬之官). 【趣-rising】 (tshuwX) Used in 趣馬. 【趣馬】 (tshuwX maeX) (_noun-like_) horse master

ceoi3.text: U+8DA3 (F1.48 趣) 趣味趣致 (Page 596 趣) Similar to _ts'u_ (ceoi1) 趨 to run; to go, to advance quickly (Kangxi: 疾也); to see what is proper and agreeable, and hasten to do it; to show alacrity or haste to please (Kangxi: 遽也); to regard pleasurably; sprightly, playful, graceful; amusing, pleasing; _'m shik ts'u_ (m4 sik1 ceoi3 唔識趣) unaccommodating, mulish; _tak kom ts'u_ (dak1 gam3 ceoi3 得咁趣) very agreeable, humorous; _ho ts'u_ (hou2 ceoi3 好趣) very lively, gleesome; _ts'u mi_ (ceoi3 mei6 趣味) entertaining, as a friend; _ts'u ts'u tik_ (ceoi3 ceoi3 dik1 趣趣滴) rather pleasing, novel; _chi ts'u hai kom ts'u tik_ (zi3 ceoi3 hai6 gam3 ceoi3 dik1 至趣係咁趣的) this is truly exhilirating, this is exquisite. 【趣-departing】 (tshjuH) (_verb-like_) to haste

cuk1.text: U+8DA3

cau2.text: U+919C (F1.54 醜) 惡也不美也又醜類 (Page 28|n71 醜) Ugly, deformed, vile, ill looking (Kangxi: 貌惡也); disagreeable; disgraceful, shameful, ashamed (Kangxi: 羞也); to hate, to dislike (Kangxi: 惡之也); shamefaced; ashamed of; to compare (Kangxi: 比也); a group, a sort (Kangxi: 類也); _kin ch'au pat u ts'ong chut_ (gin3 cau2 bat1 jyu4 cong4 zyut3 見醜不如藏拙) to shame one is worse than keeping one's incapacity private; _~~ming~~ ``meng`` ch'au_ (meng6 cau2 命醜) an unhappy lot; _ch'au 'm ch'au_ (cau2 m4 cau2 醜唔醜), are you not ashamed? _ch'au mau_ (cau2 maau6 醜貌) ill-looking, cross, unbeseeming; _pat chi ch'au_ (bat1 zi1 cau2 不知醜), brazenfaced; _kin ch'au_ (gin3 cau2 見醜) ashamed for; _ch'au ok p'i hi_ (cau2 ok3 pei4 hei3 醜惡脾氣) vile disposition, an ingrate; _ch'au lui_ (cau2 leoi6 醜類) a vile set; _ch'au lau_ (cau2 lau6 醜陋) unworthy of him, disgraceful, wicked; _p'a ch'au_ (paa3 cau2 怕醜), bashful; _ch'au yeung_ (cau2 joeng6-2 醜樣) bad looking; _ch'au sz'_ (cau2 si6 醜事) a disgraceful affair. Willliams' 命醜: Changed _ming_ (ming6) to vernacular reading _meng_ (meng6). 【醜】 (tsyhuwX) (_adjective-like_) ugly (_verb-like_) to abhor (_noun-like_) humiliation (_noun-like_) sort, (_met._) peers 【醜樣】 (cau2 joeng6-2) Ugly. 【怕醜】 (paa3 cau2) Shy or bashful.

cau3.text: U+51D1 (F1.55 凑) 會合也 (Page 563 凑湊)

U+55C5 齅 hung3 (per 《集韻》香仲切) (F1.55 嗅) 鳥哀鳴聲 (Page 28|n71 嗅) To smell (Kangxi: 以鼻就臭, 以鼻取氣), to perceive odors as a dog does; the plaintive cry of birds (Fan Wan: 鳥哀鳴聲). 【嗅】 (xjuwH) (_verb-like_) to smell

U+6E4A 凑 輳 (for "gather" senses) (F1.55 凑) 會合也 (Page 563 凑湊) To collect, to gather (Kangxi: 一曰聚也); to run together; to run into each other; a gathering on the water, as at a regatta (Kangxi: 水上人所會也); a reunion; to go with one, to take care of; with, together; a concurrence of circumstances, to estimate the probabilities of; to strive to excel (Kangxi: 競進也); for; _ni hu ts'au ngo ta ~~t'eng~~ ``t'ing``_ (nei5 heoi3 cau3 ngo5 daa2 ting3 你去湊我打聽) will you go and learn about it for me; _ts'au hau_ (cau3 haau2 湊巧), a lucky guess, a fortunate coincidence, just as; _ts'au ngam_ (cau3 ngaam1 湊啱), a lucky hit; just the thing; _pi sam ki ts'au k'u_ (bei2 sam1 gei1 cau3 keoi5 畀心機湊佢), look carefully after him; _t'ai lai ts'au_ (tai2 lai4 cau3 睇嚟湊) I'll see how I can do; _ts'au kau_ (cau3 gau3 湊夠) to make up enough; _'m ts'au ni ho_ (m4 cau3 nei5 hou2 唔湊你好), I'll have nothing to do with you; _ts'au mai yat hi_ (cau3 maai4 jat1 hei3 湊埋一氣) lump it all in one; _ts'au hop_ (cau3 hap6 湊合) to group. (Page 563 輳) Similar to the preceding (湊) (Kangxi 輳: 通作湊); Williams' 你去湊我打聽: Corrected _t'eng_ (teng3) to _t'ing_ (ting3). The use of 湊 as "for" is very dated. 【湊】 (cau3) To look after, to raise, to baby sit. 湊仔 (cau3 zai2): "Look after kids." 湊大佢 (cau3 daai6 keoi5): "Raised him up."

cok3.text: U+7C07 (Page 599 ~~蔟~~簇 _Tsuk_ (zuk6); here corrected to _Ch'ok_ (cok3)) a mold for cakes (Kangxi 簇: 作餠具); head or barb of a dart (Kangxi 簇: 矢金也);

cuk1.text: U+7C07 蔟 (for "collect" sense) zuk6 (per Williams) (F1.66 蔟 _Tsuk_ (zuk6)) 聚也又音秀 (Page 599 蔟簇 _Tsuk_ (zuk6); here normalised to _Ts'uk_ (cuk1)) ~~These two (蔟, 簇) are used interchangeably.~~ Tufts of small bamboo (Kangxi 簇: 小竹); to collect, to crowd together (Kangxi 蔟: 與簇同聚也攢也); a crowd of people; _~~tsuk~~ ``ts'uk`` yung ts'in loi_ (cuk1 jung2 cin4 loi4 簇擁前來), came pressing on in a crowd. Williams' "used interchangeably": Here only for the "collect" sense. Williams does not provide separate entries for 簇 and 蔟. I have split things up accordingly.

cau3.text: U+7CD7 hau2 (per 《廣韻》去久切) jau2 (common) (F1.55 糗) 乾粮 (Page 28|n71 糗) Roasted or parched wheat or rice (Kangxi: 熬米麥也又乾飯屑也); coarsely pounded rice like grits; _ch'au leung_ (cau3 loeng4 糗糧), dried or cured grain. 【糗】 (khjuwX) (_noun-like_) parched-grain

U+81ED cau3-2 (rising second in vernacular 臭臭地) (F1.55 臭) 氣息也又臭穢 (Page 28|n71 臭) To track by the scent (Kangxi: 禽走臭而知其迹者犬也); effluvia, savor, smell, putridity, odor, stench (Kangxi: 凡氣之總名); to stink (Kangxi: 惡氣); scent (Kangxi: 香也); to rot (Kangxi: 敗也); to smell; disreputable; a bad name; _ch'au hi_ (cau3 hei3 臭氣) a bad breath; a stink; _ok man ch'au hi_ (ok3 man4 cau3 hei3 惡聞臭氣) vile language; _ch'au hang hang_ (cau3 hang1 hang1 臭亨亨), a vile stench; _ch'au ming_ (cau3 ming4 臭名), a bad reputation; _ch'au ch'ung_ (cau3 cung4 臭蟲), bedbugs; vagabonds, foulmouthed fellows; _t'ung ch'au_ (tung4 cau3 銅臭) purseproud; _ch'au keuk_ (cau3 goek3 臭腳) foul footed, said of a woman; _seng ch'au_ (seng1 cau3 腥臭) rank, noisome, as bad fish; _ch'au mi_ (cau3 mei6 臭味) a bad ~~taste~~ ``smell``. Williams' "To track by the scent": This verb sense is now written 嗅. Note that 禽走臭而知其迹者犬也 is a quote from 《說文》 explaining the presence of the component 犬 (hound). 【臭】 (tsyhuwH) (_noun-like_) smell

U+851F 簇 (Page 599 蔟簇) These two (蔟, 簇) are used interchangeably. Also read _ts'au_ (cau3). Williams intends the reading _ts'au_ (cau3) as an alternative for the other senses, but strictly it is for the musical-pitch sense, here supplemented. Used in 太蔟 (one of the twelve musical pitches; the first lunar month).

cuk1.text: U+851F zuk6 (per Fan Wan) (F1.66 蔟 _Tsuk_ (zuk6)) 聚也又音秀 (Page 599 蔟~~簇~~ _Tsuk_ (zuk6); here normalised to _Ts'uk_ (cuk1)) a nest (Kangxi 蔟: 巢也); a framework or whisk on which silk-worms spin coccoons (Kangxi 蔟: 行蠶蓐); Williams does not provide separate entries for 簇 and 蔟. I have split things up accordingly.

zuk6.text: U+851F (F1.66 蔟) 聚也又音秀 (Page 599 蔟~~簇~~)

cau3.text: U+27F52 hung3 (per 《廣韻》香仲切) (Page 28|n71 𧽒) Read _ch'au_ (cau3); to walk, to go as if weary (Kangxi: 行也, 䠢𧽒疲行貌).

cau5.text: U+27F52 (Page 28|n71 𧽒) A colloquial word. To sprain the ankle; _ch'au ts'an keuk_ (cau5 can1 goek3 𧽒嚫腳) sprained the foot.

cau3.text: U+8F33 (F1.55 輳) 輻輳猶聚集也 (Page 563 輳) centre of a wheel, place where the rays centre (Kangxi: 輻輳輻共轂也); to collect, to bring together (Kangxi: 輳聚也); concentrated.

cau4.text: U+4EC7 (F1.52 讐) 寃讐 (F1.52 仇) 同上 (Page 26|n69 讐仇) Williams does not provide separate entries for 仇 and 讐. In modern usage: The "enmity" sense is spelt 仇 and read _shau_ (sau4); see 仇 sau4. The "proofread" sense is spelt 讎 and read _ch'au_ (cau4); see 讎 cau4. I have split things up accordingly.

U+5114 (F1.52 儔) 侣也衆也等類也 (Page 27|n70 儔) A company of four (Kangxi: 四人爲儔); a party (Kangxi: 衆也); a comrade; a mate, fellows, friends; a class (Kangxi: 等類也); _ch'au lui_ (cau4 leoi6 儔類) a concourse; to assist, to sympathize; _ch'au tong_ (cau4 dong2 儔黨), to join one's clique or party.

U+56DA (F1.53 囚) 囚犯拘罪人也 (Page 563 before 囚) (The three next (囚, 酋, 遒) are often read _ch'au_.) (Page 563 囚) To imprison, to confine, to cage (Kangxi: 繫也, 拘也); to ~~handcuff~~ ``restrain`` (Kangxi: 囚拘也主拘繫當𠛬殺者); a prisoner, a felon; a prison; _ts'au lung_ (cau4 lung4 囚籠), a cage in which criminals are carried; _yap ts'au_ (jap6 cau4 入囚), to imprison; _ts'au fan_ (cau4 faan6-2 囚犯) a criminal. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰才巳 to 犯. Williams' "to handcuff": Kangxi has 囚拘也主拘繫當𠛬殺者 followed by commentary on various wooden restraints depending on the crime/punishment. In particular, 桎 applies to the feet, so 囚 is better rendered generically as "restrain", rather than "handcuff". 【囚】 (zjuw) (_verb-like_) to imprison (_noun-like_) prisoner
U+5E6C (Page 27|n70 幬) ~~A single~~ ``An unlined`` curtain (Kangxi: 襌帳也) on a carriage (Kangxi: 車帷也); a leather screen or partition (Kangxi: 幔轂之革也); 【幬】 (drjuw) (_noun-like_) drape
U+60C6 (F1.52 惆) 惆悵 (Page 27|n70 惆) Disappointed (Kangxi: 失意也); ~~deceived;~~ frustrated; _ch'au cheung_ (cau4 zoeng3 惆悵) sad, sick at heart.
U+6906 (Page 27|n70 椆) A hard kind of wood resembling rose wood (Kangxi: 木也);

zau3.text: U+6906 (Page 27|n70 椆 _Ch'au_ (cau4); here corrected to _Chau_ (zau3)) a pole for poling boats (Kangxi: 木椆船篙木).

cau4.text: U+72A8 (F1.52 犨) 白色牛又牛息聲又生 (Page 28|n71 犨) A white ox (Fan Wan: 白色牛); the lowing or snorting of a cow (Kangxi: 牛息聲); ~~to issue from, to proceed from~~ ``protruding`` (Kangxi: 出也).

U+7587 儔 (for "who" sense) (F1.52 疇) 耕治之田也又衆也類也 (Page 27|n70 疇) A cultivated field; to till, to cultivate (Kangxi: 耕治之也); a field of hemp (Kangxi: 麻田曰疇); formerly, time past (Kangxi: 曩也); to class; a class, sort (Kangxi: 類也); who? (Kangxi: 疇誰也) _ch'au sik_ (cau4 sik1 疇昔) formerly, in time past. (Page 27|n70 儔) Used for the preceding (疇) (for the "who" sense) (Kangxi 儔: 誰也...與疇同). 【疇】 (drjuw) (_noun-like_) farming-field, hemp-field (_pronoun-like_) who (_noun-like_) category Used in 疇昔. 【疇昔】 (drjuw sjek) (_noun-like_) former day

U+7A20 綢 (F1.52 稠) 密也 (Page 27|n70 綢) close, thick, crowded (Kangxi 綢: 綢者綢緻之言故爲密也); (Page 27|n70 稠) Grain growing close (Kangxi: 穊也); thick set, close together; crowded, dense (Kangxi: 密也); _yan in ch'au mat_ (jan4 jin1 cau4 mat6 人煙稠密) people closely crowded; _hung ch'au yan chung mai shat_ (hung2 cau4 jan4 zung1 mai4 sat1 恐稠人中迷失) lest they should lose each other in the crowd; _ch'au mat_ (cau4 mat6 稠密) close, crowded, intimate. 【稠】 (drjuw) (_adjective-like_) dense

U+7C4C cau4-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.52 籌) 算也又壺矢也 (Page 28|n71 籌) To compute, to calculate (Kangxi: 籌算也); to devise, to plan, to arrange (Kangxi: 籌策); a lot; a reed; a time, a tally, a tillot; _chap ch'au_ (zap1 cau4-2 執籌) or _ts'im ch'au_ (cim4 cau4-2 撍籌), to draw lots; _kang ch'au_ (gaang1 cau4 更籌), a watchman beating the hour; _tuk ki ch'au shu_ (duk6 gei2 cau4 syu1 讀幾籌書), how many times have you studied it? _p'ai ch'au_ (paai3 cau4-2 派籌), to give tickets; _ch'au ch'ak_ (cau4 caak3 籌策) a stratagem; to scheme; _ch'au wak_ (cau4 waak6 籌劃) to settle a plan of action; _ch'au ch'i_ (cau4 ci2 籌矢), wands thrown into a jar, a sort of game. Williams' "To compute, to calculate": Alternatively, a counting-stick. Williams' 更籌: Corrected _kang_ (gang1) to _kang_ (gaang1). 【籌】 (drjuw) (_noun-like_) throwing-arrow (_noun-like_) counting-stick (_noun-like_) plan 【籌】 (cau4-2) A ticket or a token for a queue or allocation. 派籌 (paai3 cau4-2): "Handing out tickets." 攞籌 (lo2 cau4-2): "Getting a ticket."

U+7DA2 紬 (for "silk" sense) (F1.52 綢) 綢緞又密也纏也 (F1.52 紬) 同上 (Page 27|n70 紬; here normalised to 綢) Silk (Kangxi 紬: 大絲繒也); pongee, senshaw, levantine, lustring; _shang ch'au_ (saang1 cau4 生綢), stiff pongee; _shuk ch'au_ (suk6 cau4 熟綢), soft silk; _ning ch'au_ (ning4 cau4 寧綢), finest pongee; _shu leung ch'au_ (syu4 loeng4 cau4 薯莨綢), rust colored senshaw; _ch'au tun_ (cau4 dyun6 綢緞) silks and satins; _fong ch'au_ (fong2 cau4 紡綢), reeled pongee; _min ch'au_ (min4 cau4 綿綢), fabric of silk and cotton; _kan ch'au_ (gaan2 cau4 繭綢), coarse rough serge; _ch'un ch'au_ (cyun1 cau4 川綢), Sz'chuen (四川) pongee. (Page 27|n70 綢) Used for the last (紬), when denoting silk; to bind, to wrap around, to twine about (Kangxi 綢: 繆也, 綢縛束也); _ch'au mau_ (cau4 mau4 綢繆), to tie up; to be intimate with, to consult upon. Williams' 生綢: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). 【綢】 (drjuw) (_verb-like_) to wind Used in 綢繆. 【綢繆】 (drjuw mjuw) (_verb-like_) to wind and bind

U+88EF (F1.52 裯) 單被也 (Page 27|n70 裯) ~~A single~~ ``An unlined`` coverlet (Kangxi: 被也, 襌被也); a bed curtain (Kangxi: 牀帳也); an under-shirt; to cover, as with bed clothes. 【裯】 (drjuw) (_noun-like_) bedsheet

U+8B8E 讐 sau4 (common) (F1.52 讐) 寃讐 (F1.52 仇) 同上 (Page 26|n69 讐~~仇~~; here normalised to 讎) to verify, to collate with (Kangxi 讎: 應驗也); _ch'au tui_ (cau4 deoi3 讎對) to collate; Williams does not record the form 讎, and does not provide separate entries for 仇 and 讐. In modern usage: The "enmity" sense is spelt 仇 and read _shau_ (sau4); see 仇 sau4. The "proofread" sense is spelt 讎 and read _ch'au_ (cau4). I have split things up accordingly.

U+8B90 (F1.52 讐) 寃讐 (F1.52 仇) 同上 (Page 26|n69 讐仇) Williams does not provide separate entries for 仇 and 讐. In modern usage: The "enmity" sense is spelt 仇 and read _shau_ (sau4); see 仇 sau4. The "proofread" sense is spelt 讎 and read _ch'au_ (cau4); see 讎 cau4. I have split things up accordingly.

U+8E8A (Page 27|n70 躊) Embarrassed (Kangxi: 躊躇也); _ch'au ch'u_ (cau4 cyu4 躊躇), undecided, undetermined, unable to progress. Williams' "Embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed".

U+9052 (F1.53 遒) 聚也 (Page 563 before 囚) (The three next (囚, 酋, 遒) are often read _ch'au_.) (Page 563 遒) To urge, to constrain, to press upon (Kangxi: 迫也); a crowd, a throng; to exhaust, to carry to the utmost, to end (Kangxi: 盡也); brave, strong, unyielding (Kangxi: 健也勁也); to bring together (Kangxi: 聚也); _ts'au yan_ (cau4 jan4 遒人), a herald. 【遒】 (tsjuw, dzjuw) (_verb-like_) to rally, to consolidate
U+914B (F1.53 酋) 夷狄有酋長 (Page 563 酋)
U+9167 (F1.52 酬) 答也報也 (F1.52 酧) 同酬 (Page 27|n70 酬酧)

U+916C 讎 讐 酧 醻 (F1.52 酬) 答也報也 (F1.52 酧) 同酬 (Page 26|n69 讐~~仇~~) used for the next (酬) (Kangxi 讎: 通作酬); (Page 27|n70 酬酧) To pledge a guest (Kangxi: 主人實觶酬賔); to recompense; to make a return; to invite a guest to drink; _ch'au tsok_ (cau4 zok6 酬酢) pledging as host and guest; _ch'au tse_ (cau4 ze6 酬謝) to return thanks, to present in return; _ch'au tap_ (cau4 daap3 酬答) to recompense, to respond; _ch'au shan_ (cau4 san4 酬神) to thank the gods; _ch'au un_ (cau4 jyun6 酬願) to make a thank offering; _ch'au lo_ (cau4 lou4 酬勞), present for carefulness, as to workmen; _ch'au wan_ (cau4 waan4 酬還), to pay, as a vow; _ch'au tap t'in yan_ (cau4 daap3 tin1 jan1 酬答天恩), to requite heaven's favor---in being saved from fire. 【酬】 (dzyuw) (_verb-like_) to toast (_verb-like_) to repay

ce1.text: U+5513 ce5 (lower-pitch alternative) The rising tone variation is often re-falling at the tail. 【唓】 (ce1, ce5) An interjection of dismissal or dissatisfaction. 唓、得兩個人 (ce1, dak1 loeng5 go3 jan4): "Pff, only two people."

U+5962 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. se1 (per 《廣韻》式車切) (F2.83 奢) 侈也泰也 (Page 31|n74 奢) Wasteful, extravagant (Kangxi: 侈也); to spread out (Kangxi: 張也); affluent; _ch'e mong_ (ce1 mong6 奢望) extravagant hopes; _ch'e ch'i_ (ce1 ci2 奢侈), prodigal; _ch'e wa_ (ce1 waa4 奢華), gay, wasteful. 【奢】 (syae) (_adjective-like_) extravagant
U+5C3A 伬 (for an octave lower) 鿈 (for an octave higher) ce2 (possibly from Mandarin-matching) (_music_) Rough equivalent of solfege _re_ in 工尺譜 (Gongche notation).

cek3.text: U+5C3A cek3-2 (in 間尺) cik3 (uncommon) (F1.78 尺) 十寸曰尺 (Page 15* before 尺) (The next character (尺) is usually pronounced _ch'ek_ (cek).)
