(Page 15* 尺 _Ch'ik_ (cik6); _Ch'ek_ (cek6) implied by variational note; here corrected to _Ch'ek_ (cek3)) A cubit, or 10 _ts'un_ (Kangxi: 十寸也); the Chinese foot; _ch'ek ts'un_ (cek3 cyun3 尺寸) regular, proportioned; _ki to ch'ek ts'un_ (gei2 do1 cek3 cyun3 幾多尺寸) what are the dimensions? _'m kau ch'ek ts'un_ (m4 gau3 cek3 cyun3 唔夠尺寸) not of full stature; _sam ch'ek fat_ (saam1 cek3 faat3 三尺法) laws of the land, prohibitions; _p'ai ts'in ch'ek_ (paai4 cin4 cek3 排錢尺) the full length cubit; _kan ch'ek_ (gaan3 cek3-2 間尺) a ruler; _leung t'in ch'ek_ (loeng4 tin1 cek3 量天尺) a quadrant; _ch'ek wok_ (cek3 wok6 尺蠖) a geometrical worm; _ch'ek yat chi shu_ (cek3 jat1 zi1 syu1 尺一之書), an epistle, a note; _ch'ek to_ (cek3 dou6 尺度) ~~to~~ measure, measurable; _luk ch'ek chi ku_ (luk6 cek3 zi1 gu1 六尺之孤), a minor reigning; _sam ch'ek pak_ (saam1 cek3 baak6 三尺帛) a halter, a short rope. Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of 尺 (_ch'ik_) in its own series *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 尺 is read 陽入 _ch'ik_ (cik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 勅勑敕斥叱螫𪅙赤 (_ch'ik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 尺 under _ch'ek_ (cek3) here. Williams' 尺蠖: Changed _wok_ (wok3) to common reading _wok_ (wok6). Williams' 尺一之書: From the length of 1.1 _ch'ek_ for the writing-boards used for imperial decrees. Williams' 尺度: Not the verb "to measure", but the noun "measure", as 度 is in departing tone. 【尺】 (tsyhek) (_noun-like_) ruler, rule 【間尺】 (gaan3 cek3-2) A ruler.

ce1.text: U+8ECA (F2.83 車) 輪輿總名車屬 (Page 31|n74 車) A chariot, a carriage, a cart, any vehicle (Kangxi: 輿輪總名); a framed wheel; to turn a wheel, to roll over; _shui ch'e_ (seoi2 ce1 水車), a fire-engine; _ch'e luk_ (ce1 luk1 車轆) a caster; _ch'e fu_ (ce1 fu1 車夫), a charioteer; _ch'e me_ (ce1 me2 車歪), a teetotum; _ch'e me kom chun_ (ce1 me2 gam3 zyun3 車歪咁轉), fidgetty as a teetotum; _ch'e lun_ (ce1 leon4 車輪), a wheel; _fung ch'e_ (fung1 ce1 風車), a windmill; _ch'e ch'ong_ (ce1 cong4 車床), a lathe; _ma ch'e_ (maa5 ce1 馬車), a horse and carriage; _fo ch'e_ (fo2 ce1 火車), a locomotive. (Page 183 車 _Ku_ (geoi1); here corrected to _Ch'e_ (ce1)) the jaw-bones (Kangxi: 牙車牙所載也 etc.); Williams' 車歪: Corrected _me_ (me4) to _me_ (me2). Williams' 車歪咁轉: Corrected _me_ (me4) to _me_ (me2), and corrected _chun_ (zyun2) to _chun_ (zyun3). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 子車 (a compound surname) (Kangxi: 子車複姓). 【車】 (tsyhae) (_noun-like_) car

ce2.text: U+4E14 (F2.84 且) 語詞 (Page 31|n74 且) A conjunctive particle; and, further, moreover, still; also, and also (Kangxi: 又也); thus, so (Kangxi: 此也); still, yet; ~~a word of doubt,~~ if, should (Kangxi: 借曰之辭); _hang ch'e_ (hang4 ce2 行且), on the point of being; _ch'e ~~san~~ ``sun`` ch'e i_ (ce2 seon3 ce2 ji4 且信且疑), half believing, half doubting; _fu ch'e kwai_ (fu3 ce2 gwai3 富且貴) rich and so honored; _kau ch'e liu sz'_ (gau2 ce2 liu5 si6 苟且了事) slovenly way of doing things; _fong ch'e_ (fong3 ce2 況且), still further; _ch'e man_ (ce2 maan6 且慢) ~~by and by, presently~~; _ku ch'e_ (gu1 ce2 姑且) or _tsam ch'e_ (zaam6 ce2 暫且), for a time. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ts'e_ per 《廣韻》七也切. Fan Wan groups it with 扯 (same as 撦), whose reading is _ch'e_ (ce2) per 《廣韻》昌者切. Notwithstanding, this is all academic given the initials _ts'_ and _ch'_ are now considered merged. Williams' "a word of doubt": This is an attempt at rendering Kangxi 未定之辭. A better rendering is "a word indicating uncertainness". Note that this is a separate sense to subjunctive "if", "should". Williams' 且信且疑: Corrected _san_ (san3) to _sun_ (seon3). Williams' 且慢: Not an adverbial "soon" or "shortly", but an imperative "slow down" or "hang on". Temporarily, for now. Shall, about to (Kangxi: 將也). Still. Or. 【且-rising】 (tshjaeX) (_conjunction-like_) and (also), moreover (_determiner-like_) this (_adverb-like_) for now (_auxiliary-like_) shall, about to (_adverb-like_) still (_conjunction-like_) or

zeoi1.text: U+4E14 (F1.44 且) 語詞 (Page 594 且) A phonetic particle, used in the Shi King (詩經) at the end of sentences, as an affirmative (Kangxi: 語餘聲). Numerous, many (Kangxi: 多貌). The sixth lunar month (Kangxi: 六月爲且). 【且-level】 (tsjo) (_speech-assist_) (_adjective-like_) many

ce2.text: U+54C6 (Page 12* 哆) (....); _~~ch'i~~ ``ch'e`` in_ (ce2 jin4 哆然), sneering at, disobedient (Kangxi: 哆然衆有不服之心)``.`` Also read _ch'e_ (ce2); the lower lip hanging down (Kangxi: 脣下垂貌). Williams' 哆然: Corrected _ch'i_ (ci2) to _ch'e_ (ce2).

ci2.text: U+54C6 de2 (per 《集韻》丁寫切 for "fish-gape" sense) (Page 12* 哆) Gaping (Kangxi: 張口也); an open mouth, like a fish (Kangxi: 魚口張貌); large lipped; (...)``.`` 【哆】 (tsyheX) (_adjective-like_) open-mouthed

ce2.text: U+626F 撦 (F2.84 抯) 止也拽也 (F2.84 扯) 同上 (Page 31|n74 撦~~抯~~扯) To tear open, to pull apart (Kangxi 撦: 裂開也); to tear away; to pull up or on; to haul, to drag; _ch'e sheung_ (ce2 soeng5 扯上), haul up; _lai ch'e_ (laai1 ce2 拉扯), to pull and haul, to borrow and lend; _lai lai ch'e ch'e_ (laai1 laai1 ce2 ce2 拉拉扯扯), borrowing here and there; on the average, lumping the whole; _ch'e hoi_ (ce2 hoi1 扯開), to pull apart; _ch'e mai_ (ce2 maai4 扯埋), to pull up, to close, as a skylight; _ch'e chu_ (ce2 zyu6 扯住) to stop from going. A colloquial word. To clear out, to go off, to abscond; to send off; to go away, to take one's self off; _ch'e lo_ (ce2 lo1 扯囉), go! let us go; _ngo ch'e lok_ (ngo5 ce2 lok3 我扯咯) I'm off. Fan Wan (and Williams following it) considers 抯 the same as 扯. Strictly, this is erroneous; the three readings of 抯 in 《廣韻》 are: _cha_ (zaa1) per 側加切, explained 挹也 (per 說文); _tse_ (ze6) per 徐野切, explained 取也 (or _ts'e_ (ce5) if insisting on rising tone); and _tse_ (ze2) per 兹野切, also explained 取也. None of these are 陰上 _ch'e_ (ce2). 【扯】 (ce2) To leave. 你扯啦 (nei5 ce2 laa1): "Leave!"
U+64A6 May appear with 者 dotless (+11 assumed here) or dotted (+12), depending on the font. (Page 31|n74 撦~~抯~~扯)

ce3.text: U+659C ce3-2 (rising second in vernacular 斜斜地) (Page 565 斜) A colloquial word. Not level, rising, as a road; _fong ts'e_ (fong3 ce3 放斜) lay it one end higher; _ts'e ts'e lok_ (ce3 ce3 lok6 斜斜落) go down winding; _ts'e po_ (ce3 bo1 斜坡), an ascending plain; _hau t'au ts'e_ (hau4 tau4 ce3 喉頭斜) greedy. 【斜路】 (ce3 lou6-2) An incline, slope, or ramp. 行斜路 (haang4 ce3 lou6-2): "Walking up/down a ramp" (as opposed to stairs). 寫字上斜路 (se2 zi6 soeng5 ce3 lou6-2): "Your writing is going up an incline" (i.e. not level). 【長命斜】 (coeng4 meng6 ce3) A long and steep incline or slope. Williams views departing-tone 斜 _ts'e_ (ce3) as a word in its own right, but personally I view it merely as a vernacular reading variation of level-tone 斜 _ts'e_ (ce4). (E.g. 斜坡 is read both as _ts'e po_ (ce3 bo1) and as _ts'e po_ (ce4 bo1).) Some point to 笡 as the original character for the departing-tone reading: 《廣韻》遷謝切 笡 斜逆也 But whilst the reading _ts'e_ (ce3) is a match, the explanation 斜逆也 seems more a metaphorical "heretical" than a physical "inclined" or "sloped", so I am yet to be convinced of 笡.

ce4.text: U+659C 衺 邪 (F2.84 斜) 歪斜 (Page 565 斜) Slanting, inclined, oblique, across, crisscross, diagonal, aslant, not with the rule (Kangxi: 不正也); distorted, scattered (Kangxi: 散也); ~~often used for the preceding (邪);~~ _ts'e man po_ (ce4 man4 bou3 斜紋布) drillings, twilled cloth; _it t'au pai ts'e_ (jit6 tau4-2 baai2 ce4 熱頭擺斜), the sun is declining; _ts'e shi_ (ce4 si6 斜視) to look askance; _ts'e ts'e tik_ (ce4 ce4 dik1 斜斜滴) it is rather awry; _sai ts'e it_ (sai1 ce4 jit6 西斜熱) the afternoon sun is hot; _ts'e fung_ (ce4 fung1 斜風), an unsteady wind; _ts'e ngan_ (ce4 ngaan5 斜眼), cross-eyed. 【斜】 (zjae) (_adjective-like_) slanted 【西斜】 (sai1 ce4) Exposure to the western sun.

U+90AA 衺 ce4-2 (in 唔信邪) (F2.84 邪) 邪曲不直也又奸佞也 (Page 564 邪) Deflected, inclined, swerving from the right line (Kangxi: 不正也); whatever leads astray; depraved, bad, crooked, vicious, corrupting, obscene (Kangxi: 姦思也); specious, deluding (Kangxi: 佞也); heretical, illegal, not acknowledged by the state, or in the classics; _'m sun ts'e_ (m4 seon3 ce4-2 唔信邪) don't believe heresies; _ts'e to_ (ce4 dou6 邪道) erroneous doctrines; _ts'e peng_ (ce4 beng6 邪病) epileptic sickness; _ts'e fat_ (ce4 faat3 邪法) sorceries, tricks; _ts'e shan_ (ce4 san4 邪神), illegal, unacknowledged gods, those whose worship corrupts; _kan ts'e_ (gaan1 ce4 奸邪), malicious, fornicating, illicit; _koi ts'e kwai ching_ (goi2 ce4 gwai1 zing3 改邪歸正) ~~forsake errors~~ ``alter from wickedness`` and ~~cleave~~ ``return`` to the right; _ts'e shut_ (ce4 seot6 邪術) charms, philtres, black art; _sam ts'e_ (sam1 ce4 心邪), impure, lewd in mind; _ts'e yam_ (ce4 jam4 邪淫), obscene, licentious. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 㚥 to 奸. 【邪】 (zjae) (_adjective-like_) wicked 【唔信邪】 (m4 seon3 ce4-2) Not believing in negative superstitions.

cek3.text: U+24DEB 赤 cek3-2 (rising second in vernacular 𤷫𤷫地) cik3 (alternative) (Page 571 𤷫 _Ts'ik_ (cik3); _Ts'ek_ (cek3) exemplified) A fullness in the stomach; a pain from laughing; rheumatic twinges, pain in the bones; _siu to t'o ts'ek_ (siu3 dou3 tou5 cek3 笑到肚𤷫) laughed till he ached; _kwat ts'ek_ (gwat1 cek3 骨𤷫) shooting pains; _sam ts'ek_ (sam1 cek3 心𤷫) sorry for, deeply pained for; _t'au ts'ek_ (tau4 cek3 頭𤷫) nervous headache. 【𤷫】 (cek3) Pain or aching. 頭𤷫 (tau4 cek3): "Headache."

cik3.text: U+8D64 cek3 (alternative) (F1.74 赤) 赤色 (Page 14* 赤) Carnation, purplish or light red (Kangxi: 朱色也); flesh color (Kangxi: ...肉色也); naked, destitute, barren, poor (Kangxi: 空盡無物曰赤); to redden; to denude (Kangxi: 裸裎曰赤體見肉色也); the 155th radical ("red") of characters referring to red; _ch'ik tam_ (cik3 daam2 赤膽), pureminded; _ch'ik to_ (cik3 dou6 赤道) the equator; _ch'ik yat_ (cik3 jat6 赤日) full day; heat of the day; _ch'ik mai_ (cik3 mai5 赤米), red rice; _ch'ik tsz'_ (cik3 zi2 赤子) an infant; we, the people; _ch'ik shan_ (cik3 san1 赤身), naked; _ch'ik sam_ (cik3 sam1 赤心), guileless, sincere; _ch'ik pak nan fan_ (cik3 baak6 naan4 fan1 赤白難分), hard to distinguish; _ta ch'ik keuk_ (daa2 cik3 goek3 打赤腳) to bare the feet; _ch'ik ti_ (cik3 dei6 赤地) barren wastes. Ardent, loyal. 【赤】 (tsyhek) (_adjective-like_) red (_adjective-like_) bare Used in 赤子. 【赤子】 (tsyhek tsiX) (_noun-like_) babe

ceng1.text: U+9751 (Page 577 靑青 _Ts'ing_ (cing1); _Ts'eng_ (ceng1) implied by variational note)

cing1.text: U+9751 (F1.68 靑) 東方水色 (Page 577 靑青)

ceng1.text: U+9752 (Page 577 靑青 _Ts'ing_ (cing1); _Ts'eng_ (ceng1) implied by variational note)

cing1.text: U+9752 靑 ceng1 (vernacular for "green" senses) (F1.68 靑) 東方水色 (Page 577 靑青) The color of nature, as the azure of the sky or the green of growing plants (Kangxi 靑: 靑生也); a very light green or blue tint; wan, fading away, pale; the 174th radical ("blue"); _ts'ing kwo_ (cing1 gwo2 青果), the olive; _fat ts'ing_ (fat6 cing1 佛青), ultra-marine; _un ts'ing_ (jyun4 cing1 玄青), black; _ts'ing kam_ (cing1 gam1 青金), lapis-lazuli color; _luk ts'ing_ (luk6 cing1 綠青), copperas; _ts'ing fan_ (cing1 faan4 青礬), blue vitriol; _yeung ts'ing_ (joeng4 cing1 洋青), smalts; _~~ts'ing~~ ``ts'eng`` t'oi_ (ceng1 toi4 青苔), ~~mossy, slippery, as stones~~ ``moss``; _ts'ing t'in pak yat_ (cing1 tin1 baak6 jat6 青天白日) a clear bright day; _ts'ing nin_ (cing1 nin4 青年), young in years; _mit ts'ing_ (mit6 cing1 篾青), bamboo splints; _t'ang ts'ing_ (tang4 cing1 藤青), rattan cords; _kom ngan ts'ing_ (gam3 ngaan5 cing1 咁眼青) in such a fury, outrageous; _piu ts'eng_ (biu1 ceng1 標青), new style, fine; _tap ts'eng_, (daap6 ceng1 踏青) to worship the tombs; _ch'a ts'ing_ (caa4 cing1 茶青), a yellowish green; _k'i ip ts'eng ts'eng_ (kei4 jip6 ceng1 ceng1 其葉青青), tender and green are the leaves. Williams' 青苔: Changed _ts'ing_ (cing1) to vernacular reading _ts'eng_ (ceng1). 【青】 (tsheng) (_adjective-like_) blue, green, black 【標青】 (biu1 ceng1) Outstanding, excellent. 【青】 (ceng1) Green, especially light green. 青吡吡 (ceng1 bi1 bi1): "Limey-green." 採青 (coi2 ceng1): "Plucking the green" (i.e. the lettuce in a Lion Dance). Green garnishes (spring onion, coriander, parsley, etc.). 走青 (zau2 ceng1): "Without any green garnishes." Faded or cut close to the scalp (of hair). Unclear if this should be spelt 清 instead. 兩邊剷青 (loeng5 bin1 caan2 ceng1): "Faded on both sides." 飛青啲 (fei1 ceng1 di1): "Cut it closer." 【青豆】 (ceng1 dau6-2) Green peas. 【青瓜】 (ceng1 gwaa1) Cucumber. 【青檸】 (ceng1 ning4-2) Lime. (Literally, "green lemon".) 【面青】 (min6 ceng1) Pale complexion (as a sign of unhealth).

ceng2.text: U+8ACB May appear as either ⿰訁靑 or ⿰訁青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (Page 578 請 _Ts'ing_ (cing2); _Ts'eng_ (ceng2) implied by variational note)

cing2.text: U+8ACB May appear as either ⿰訁靑 or ⿰訁青 (assumed here), depending on the font. ceng2 (vernacular) (F1.72 請) 敬請迎請又托也 (Page 578 請) To request, to ask courteously, to pray, to beg (Kangxi: 謁也, 求也, 乞也問也, 祈也扣也); to request liberty to do, to ask, as for orders; to invite, to bid, to engage; to confess, to acknowledge; to inform (Kangxi: 吿也); _ts'ing ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (cing2 co5 請坐) please be seated; _ts'eng tsau_ (ceng2 zau2 請酒), to ask to a dinner; _ts'ing tsau t'ip_ (cing2 zau2 tip3-2 請酒帖) an invitation to dinner; _ts'eng kau_ (ceng2 gaau3 請教) please tell me; _ts'ing k'i_ (cing2 kei4 請期), to appoint the time of marriage; _ts'ing pin_ (cing2 bin6 請便) don't let me take your time; _ts'eng liu_ (ceng2 liu5 請了), has been invited; _ts'eng sin shang_ (ceng2 sin1 saang1 請先生), to engage a teacher; _ts'ing ch'a_ (cing2 caa4 請茶), please take your tea; _ts'ing ts'am_ (cing2 caam1 請參), to request dismissal from office, to throw it up; _ts'ing ka_ (cing2 gaa3 請駕) please go, Sir; _ts'ing ming_ (cing2 ming6 請命) what do you wish? _ts'ing a_ (cing2 aa3 請啊) please; your good health; _ts'ing tsui_ (cing2 zeoi6 請罪) to apologize, to confess; _ts'eng yik_ (ceng2 jik1 請益) please let me know; _ts'eng man_ (ceng2 man6 請問) to beg, to ask; _ts'ing ts'ing_ (cing2 cing2 請請), thank you; good bye; (the word _chin-chin_ is derived from this phrase;) _ts'eng hu shik fan_ (ceng2 heoi3 sik6 faan6 請去食飯) invited out to dine. Williams' 請坐: Corrected _tso_ (zo6 去聲) to _ts'o_ (co5 上聲). Williams' 請教: To ask or consult with someone (more knowledgeable than oneself). Williams' 請先生: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). Williams' 請命: More a third-person indicative than a second-person interrogative. Williams' 請益: More a third-person indicative than a second-person request. Williams' 請問: More a second-person interrogative than a third-person indicative. Williams' 請去食飯: Corrected _fan_ (faan3) to _fan_ (faan6). 【請】 (tshjengX) (_verb-like_) to request, to ask

ceoi1.text: U+50AC (F2.80 催) 促也趲也 (Page 598 催) To urge, to press, to egg on (Kangxi: 促也迫也); to importune; to hasten, as the payment of a debt; ~~to reiterate;~~ _mok ko ts'ui ngo_ (mok6 go3 ceoi1 ngo5 莫個催我), don't hurry me; _ts'ui pik_ (ceoi1 bik1 催逼) or _ts'ui kam_ (ceoi1 gam2 催？), to urge on, to stimulate; _ts'ui k'u fai tik_ (ceoi1 keoi5 faai3 dik1 催佢快滴) hurry him on a little; _fat kai tsau ts'ui_ (fat6 gaai3 zau2 ceoi1 乏价走催), I've no servant to send to hasten you --- a phrase on invitation cards; _hi shai kom ts'ui_ (hei2 sai3 gam3 ceoi1 起勢咁催), hurrying and urging one rather causelessly. Williams' 乏价走催: Changed _fat_ (faat6) to modern _fat_ (fat6). 【催】 (tshwoj) (_verb-like_) to press (upon)

U+5439 炊 龡 (F2.55 吹) 風吹又吹噓 (F2.55 龡) 同上 (Page 34* 吹龡) To blow (Kangxi 吹: 噓也, 出氣也), as the wind or by breath; to whistle; to puff (Kangxi 吹: 吹噓相佐助也); a blast, a gust; to play on a wind instrument; _ch'ui hu ha_ (ceoi1 heoi1 haa5 吹噓吓), recommend him; _ch'ui ch'eung_ (ceoi1 coeng3 吹唱) singing and playing; _ch'ui t'ung_ (ceoi1 tung4 吹筒), to blow the sumpitan; _ch'ui tang_ (ceoi1 dang1 吹燈), blow out the lamp; _tang ch'ui_ (dang1 ceoi1 燈吹), a blowpipe; _ch'ui ta lo_ (ceoi1 daa2 lou2 吹打佬), musicians; _ch'ui fo t'ung ch'ut shan_ (ceoi1 fo2 tung4 ceot1 san1 吹火筒出身), began life as a firelighter. (Page 34* 炊) Used for the last (吹) (Kangxi 炊: 與吹同). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword (with 比-like centre-left instead of ⿲口口口) to 龡. 【吹】 (tsyhwe) (_verb-like_) to blow 【吹】 (ceoi1) Short for 吹水. Short for 吹脹 in the rhetorical interrogative sense, where one taunts a victim for retributive action after inflicting an injustice upon them. 吹呀 (ceoi1 a4): "Gonna rile me?" (i.e. what can you do in response?) 吹咩 (ceoi1 me1): "Gonna rile me?" (i.e. what can you do in response?) 【吹水】 (ceoi1 seoi2) To brag, to boast, to exaggerate. 又吹水 (jau6 ceoi1 seoi2): "Talking big again eh?" 正一吹水哥 (zing1 jat1 ceoi1 seoi2 go1): "He's a right big-mouth." To converse casually. 得閒吹吓水 (dak1 haan4 ceoi1 haa5 seoi2): "When we're free let's have a chat." 【吹脹】 (ceoi1 zoeng3) To vex, to irk. 真係吹脹 (zan1 hai6 ceoi1 zoeng3): "It's really riled me up!" 吹佢唔脹 (ceoi1 keoi5 m4 zoeng3): "Can't rile him up."

U+5D14 ceoi4 (per 《廣韻》昨回切 for "lofty" sense) (F2.80 崔) 高貌又姓 (Page 598 崔) Lofty, mountainous (Kangxi: 高大也, 崔嵬也); rugged, as a hilly country; _ts'ui ngai_ (ceoi1 ngai4 崔嵬), lofty, rocky hills and peaks. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【崔】 (dzwoj) (_adjective-like_) lofty, mountainous
U+63A8 (F2.55 推) 讓也奬也薦也又尋繹也 (F2.80 推 _T'ui_ (teoi1)) 透杯切盪也排擠也祛也 (Page 34* 推)

U+6467 ceoi4 (per 《廣韻》昨回切) (F2.81 摧 _Ts'ui_ (ceoi4)) 挫也折也抑也阻摧 (Page 598 摧 _Ts'ui_ (ceoi4); here normalised to _Ts'ui_ (ceoi1)) To stop, to drive back, to repress (Kangxi: 挫也抑也); to overpower, to set down; to push upon, to thrust forward, to crowd against, to impel (Kangxi: 擠也); to break (Kangxi: 一曰折也), as the wind does trees; to reach (Kangxi: 摧詹至也); to put out, to extinguish, as a family (Kangxi: 滅也); _ts'ui yik_ (ceoi1 jik1 摧抑) to abate, to put down; _ts'ui chit_ (ceoi1 zit3 摧折) to break off by pushing against. 【摧】 (dzwoj) (_verb-like_) to push (_verb-like_) to break, to destroy

ceoi4.text: U+6467 (F2.81 摧) 挫也折也抑也阻摧 (Page 598 摧)

ceoi1.text: U+708A (F2.55 炊) 炊爨 (Page 34* 炊) To dress food; to cook (Kangxi: 爨也); _ch'ui fan_ (ceoi1 faan6 炊飯) to cook rice. Fan Wan entry: Corrected bottom component of 爨 from 夾 to ⿳林大火.
U+7480 ceoi2 (per 《廣韻》七罪切 ) (F2.80 璀) 璀璨丰彩貌 (Page 598 璀) The lustre of a gem (Kangxi: 璀璨玉光也); a gem hanging down, as from the girdle (Kangxi: 璀璨珠垂貌). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 琛 to 璨.

U+7E17 衰 (F2.80 衰) 齊衰喪服也 (F2.80 縗) 同上 (Page 598 衰縗) The unhemmed, frayed edges of mourning clothes (Kangxi: 喪服也), which are left so; to ravel out; _cham ts'ui_ (zaam2 ceoi1 斬縗), clipped, unhemmed. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 裘 to 喪, and corrected 土 to 上. 【縗】 (tshwoj) (_noun-like_) mourning-clothes, sackcloth

U+86C6 zeoi1 (common) (F1.44 蛆) 蛆虫 (Page 595 蛆) Maggots in putrid flesh (Kangxi: 肉中蟲也); flyblows, eggs of flies (Kangxi: 蠅乳); _shang ts'u_ (saang1 ceoi1 生蛆), it has bred worms; _sut ts'u_ (syut3 ceoi1 雪蛆), worms found in snow in Sz'chuen (四川), a sort of Protococcus. Williams' 生蛆: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1).

zeoi1.text: U+86C6 (Page 595 蛆) Read _tsu_ (zeoi1), a millipede; _tsik tsu_ (zik1 zeoi1 蝍蛆), a centipede (Kangxi: 蝍蛆食蛇蜈蚣也).

ceoi1.text: U+8870 (F2.80 衰) 齊衰喪服也 (F2.80 縗) 同上 (Page 598 衰縗) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 裘 to 喪, and corrected 土 to 上.

U+8DA8 趋 (F1.44 趨) 趨向 (F1.44 趋) 同上 (Page 595 趨趋) To hasten to one's place (Kangxi: 疾行曰趨趨赴也赴所至也, 趨今之捷步), to stride off (Kangxi: 行而張足曰趨), as in a superior's presence; to run, to walk quick (Kangxi 趨: 走也, 行也); _ts'u shi_ (ceoi1 si4 趨時), to run after, as a fashion; _ts'u po_ (ceoi1 bou6 趨步) to straddle off, as a courier does; _ts'u im fu shai_ (ceoi1 jim4 fu6 sai3 趨炎附勢) to run to the flame and stick to the strong --- a parasite. 【趨】 (tshju) (_verb-like_) to haste toward
U+9FA1 (F2.55 吹) 風吹又吹噓 (F2.55 龡) 同上 (Page 34* 吹龡) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword (with 比-like centre-left instead of ⿲口口口) to 龡.

ceoi2.text: U+53D6 (F1.46 取) 索也獲也收也愛也攬也資也 (Page 595 取) To take, to lay hold on (Kangxi: 捕取也), to use; to exact; to seize on, to take away; to assume, to appropriate (Kangxi: 資也收也); to receive (Kangxi: 受也), to apply to one's use; to get, to induce, to bring upon; taken, applied, selected; _shau ts'u_ (sau1 ceoi2 收取), to receive; _ts'u 'm sheung_ (ceoi2 m4 soeng5 取唔上), not needed, unfit; _ts'u chung_ (ceoi2 zung3 取中) promoted, chosen, selected``;`` _tai yau ho ts'u_ (daai6 jau5 ho2 ceoi2 大有可取), estimable, one who is liked; _ts'u ch'ut_ (ceoi2 ceot1 取出) taken out; _ts'u yap_ (ceoi2 jap6 取入) brought in; _t'am nong ts'u mat_ (taam3 nong4 ceoi2 mat6 探囊取物) as easy as taking cash out of the purse; _sok ts'u_ (sok3 ceoi2 索取), to beg earnestly for, as a loan; _ts'u siu k'u_ (ceoi2 siu3 keoi5 取笑佢), to get the joke on him; _tsz' ts'u wo_ (zi6 ceoi2 wo6 自取禍) to bring down misery on one's self; _tsau ti ts'u ts'oi_ (zau6 dei6 ceoi2 coi4 就地取材), happily I just then turned it up, as something that was wanted; _'m shing kwo ts'u_ (m4 sing4 gwo3 ceoi2 唔成過取), I don't want much; _hip ts'u_ (hip3 ceoi2 脅取), to force from, to extort; _p'ang man ts'u luk_ (pang4 man4 ceoi2 luk6 憑文取錄) to select the compositions according to their style; _mung ts'u fo_ (mung4 ceoi2 fo3 蒙取貨) obliged to you for taking goods, --- as shopmen say. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱宀⿱宀厼 to 索, and normalised 𫉬 to 獲. 【取】 (tshjuX, tshuwX) (_verb-like_) to take

U+5A36 取 ceoi3 (per 《廣韻》七句切) (F1.48 娶 _Ts'u_ (ceoi3)) 取婦曰娶 (Page 595 取; here normalised to 娶) to take, as a wife; (Page 596 娶 _Ts'u_ (ceoi3); here normalised to _Ts'u_ (ceoi2)) To marry a woman with the legal ceremonies (Kangxi: 取婦也); a marriage; _ts'u ts'ai_ (ceoi2 cai1 娶妻), or _ts'u lo p'o_ (ceoi2 lou5 po4 娶老婆), to take a wife; _ts'u san fu_ (ceoi2 san1 fu5 娶新婦) or _ts'u ~~sam~~ ``san`` p'o_ (ceoi2 san1 pou5 娶新婦), ~~somebady~~ ``a son`` marrying; _wing ts'u 'm ts'ang_ (wing4 ceoi2 m4 cang4 榮娶唔曾), have you ~~been married~~ ``taken a wife`` yet? _ts'u ts'ip_ (ceoi2 cip3 娶妾) or _ts'u p'in fong_ (ceoi2 pin1 fong4 娶偏房), to take a concubine home to one. Williams' 娶新婦 (second occurrence): Corrected _ts'o_ (cou2) to _ts'u_ (ceoi2) and corrected _sam_ (sam1) to _san_ (san1). 【娶】 (tshjuH) (_verb-like_) to take a wife

ceoi3.text: U+5A36 (F1.48 娶) 取婦曰娶 (Page 596 娶)

ceoi2.text: U+60F4 (F2.56 惴) 恐懼貌 (Page 34* 惴)

zeoi3.text: U+60F4 ceoi2 (per Fan Wan) cyun2 (common) (F2.56 惴 _Ch'ui_ (ceoi2)) 恐懼貌 (Page 34* 惴 _Ch'ui_ (ceoi2); here normalised to _Chui_ (zeoi3)) ~~Mournful~~ ``Anxious, apprehensive`` (Kangxi: 憂懼也); ~~in straits;~~ _~~ch'ui ch'ui~~ ``chui chui``_ (zeoi3 zeoi3 惴惴), ~~mo``u``rning, grieving~~; _~~ch'ui~~ ``chui`` ku_ (zeoi3 geoi6 惴懼) anxious. Anxious, apprehensive (Kangxi: 憂懼也). 【惴】 (tsyweH) (_adjective-like_) apprehensive

ceoi2.text: U+63E3 (F2.56 揣) 吹上聲揣度揣摩 (Page 34* 揣)

cyun2.text: U+63E3 ceoi2 (per 《廣韻》初委切) (F2.56 揣 _Ch'ui_ (ceoi2)) 吹上聲揣度揣摩 (Page 34* 揣) Also read _ch'un_ (cyun2). To estimate, to calculate, to reckon (Kangxi: 量也, 量度也, 凡稱量忖度皆曰揣); to feel, to try, to rub, in order to ascertain (Kangxi: 一曰捫而察之也); to detect; ~~to~~ _~~ch'ui~~ ``ch'un`` tok_ (cyun2 dok6 揣度) to conjecture; _~~ch'ui~~ ``ch'un`` mo_ (cyun2 mo1 揣摩), to feel, to learn one's wishes, to seek out the sense; _pat ~~ch'ui~~ ``ch'un``_ (bat1 cyun2 不揣), unable to detect. Williams' "Also read ...": This reading _ch'un_ (cyun2) is the canonical reading today. I have normalised _ch'ui_ (ceoi2) in the examples to it. 【揣】 (tsrhjweX) (_verb-like_) to measure, to reckon

ceoi2.text: U+6F3C (F2.81 漼 _Ts'ui_ (ceoi4)) 情回切水深貌 (Page 598 漼 _Ts'ui_ (ceoi4); here corrected to _Ts'ui_ (ceoi2)) Deep (Kangxi: 深也), clear water; tears trickling down (Kangxi: 涕垂貌); fresh, new (Kangxi: 鮮明也), clean; spoiling, ruining (Kangxi: 壞貌); name of a stream (Kangxi: 水名). 【漼】 (tshwojX) (_adjective-like_) deep

ceoi4.text: U+6F3C (F2.81 漼) 情回切水深貌 (Page 598 漼) snow drifted (Kangxi: 漼溰雪霜積聚貌);

ceoi3.text: U+5550 seoi6 (per Fan Wan, common) (F2.24 啐 _Tsut_ (zyut3)) 尊入聲嘗入口也又咄啐呼也 (F2.57 啐 _Sui_ (seoi6)) 咄啐呼也驚也又嘗入口也 (Page 609 啐 _Tsut_ (zyut3); here corrected to _Ts'ui_ (ceoi3)) To taste (Kangxi: 嘗也, 嘗入口)~~, to craunch~~; A berating puff (Kangxi: 咄啐嘑也). 【啐】 (tshwojH) (_verb-like_) to sip, to taste

zyut3.text: U+5550 zeot1 (per 《廣韻》子聿切 for onomatopoeic senses) (F2.24 啐) 尊入聲嘗入口也又咄啐呼也 (Page 609 啐) (...), the noise of sucking (Kangxi: 吮聲)~~, or swallowing with a gusto~~;

ceoi3.text: U+6BF3 (F2.56 毳 _Ch'ui_) 獸毛褥也 (Page 597 毳) The down on birds, the fine undergrowth of hair on animals (Kangxi: 獸細毛也); ~~birds molting;~~ furry, downy, soft, fine, delicate; like felt, velvety; _min ts'ui_ (min4 ceoi3 綿毳) cotton staple; _fo ts'ui_ (fo2 ceoi3 火毳) asbestos cloth; _ts'ui mok_ (ceoi3 mok6 毳幕) a felt tent. 《廣韻》 gives both 此芮切 _ts'ui_ (per Williams) and 楚稅切 _ch'ui_ (per Fan Wan). Williams' "birds molting": Probably a misreading of Kangxi 鳥腹毛曰毳. 【毳】 (tshjwejH, tsrhjwejH) (_noun-like_) fine-hair

U+7FE0 (F2.56 翠) 翡翠鳥羽也赤曰翡靑曰翠 (Page 597 翠) The female of a kingfisher, whose feathers are used in plumagery (Kangxi: 靑羽雀也, 雄曰翡雌曰翠); _ts'ui kiu_ (ceoi3 giu6-2 翠轎) a bridal chair covered with feathers; _ts'ui t'ing_ (ceoi3 ting4 翠亭), a feathered shrine ~~or car~~; _tim ts'ui_ (dim2 ceoi3 點翠) to put on feathers; _ts'ui mo_ (ceoi3 mou4 翠毛), the kingfisher's feathers; _ts'ui shik_ (ceoi3 sik1 翠色) a ~~purplish blue~~ ``jade-like green``. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 日 to 曰.
U+8103 May appear as either ⿰⺼色 (assumed here) or ⿰⺼⿱刀巴, depending on the font. (Page 597 脃脆)

U+8106 脃 ceoi3-2 (rising second in vernacular 脆脆地) (F2.56 脆) 物易斷也又輕薄也 (Page 597 脃脆) Delicate, easily broken (Kangxi 脃: 小耎易斷也); brittle, crackling; shivery, crumbling; crisp, light (Kangxi 脃: 輕也), as crust; trifling, unsteady in character; _shong ts'ui_ (song2 ceoi3 爽脆) delicate, as cakes; _kon ts'ui_ (gon1 ceoi3 乾脆) cracks when dry; _ham ts'ui fa shang_ (haam4 ceoi3 faa1 sang1 鹹脆花生), baked salt ground nuts; _t'iu meng ap ap ts'ui_ (tiu4 meng6 ap1 ap1 ceoi3 條命洽洽脆) life snaps asunder like crust. Williams' 爽脆: More crunchy than delicate. Williams' 乾脆: Figuratively, clear-cut. 【脆】 (tshjwejH) (_adjective-like_) brittle 【扑扑脆】 (bok1 bok1 ceoi3) Crispy. (Also heard as lok1 lok1 ceoi3, or ngok1 ngok1 ceoi3.) 【脆扑扑】 (ceoi3 bok1 bok1) Crispy.
U+89B0 (F1.48 覻) 竊視曰覻 (Page 596 覰) Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword 覻.

U+89B7 覰 (F1.48 覻) 竊視曰覻 (Page 596 覰; here normalised to 覷) To spy, to look at; to peep, to descry, to reconnoitre (Kangxi 覰: 伺視也); _ts'u pin_ (ceoi3 bin1 覷邊), to look after the frontiers closely. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword 覻.

ceoi4.text: U+53A8 (F1.45 厨) 庖厨 (Page 32* 廚厨) Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𢊍 to 厨.

cyu4.text: U+53A8 (F1.45 厨) 庖厨 (Page 32* 廚厨) Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𢊍 to 厨.

ceoi4.text: U+210F4 隨 (Page 597 隨; here normalised to 𡃴) A colloquial word. A smell, a stench; bad breath; _yat pung ts'ui_ (jat1 bung6 ceoi4 一𩗴𡃴), a stench; _yau tik ts'ui sik_ (jau5 dik1 ceoi4 sik1 有滴𡃴息) there is some bad smell. 【𡃴】 (ceoi4) A smell or odour. 有𠹻𡃴 (jau5 zam6 ceoi4): "There's an odour." 男人𡃴 (naam4 jan4 ceoi4): "Man smell." 老人𡃴 (lou5 jan4 ceoi4): "Old person smell."

U+5EDA 厨 ceoi4-2 (uncommon vernacular for "chef" sense) cyu4 (alternative) cyu4-2 (vernacular for "chef" sense) (F1.45 厨) 庖厨 (Page 32* 廚厨) A kitchen, a cook-room (Kangxi: 庖屋也); a slaughter-house; a ~~quiver~~ ``box or cabinet`` (Kangxi 廚: 櫝也); _ch'u yan_ (cyu4 jan4 廚人) or _ch'u kun_ (cyu4 gun1 廚官), a cook; _pong ch'u yan_ (bong1 cyu4 jan4 幫廚人), a cook's mate, a scullion; _kiu tso ch'u_ (giu3 zou6 cyu4-2 叫做廚), call the cook; _ch'u fong_ (cyu4 fong4-2 廚房), a kitchen. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𢊍 to 厨. 【廚】 (drju) (_noun-like_) kitchen

cyu4.text: U+5EDA (F1.45 厨) 庖厨 (Page 32* 廚厨) Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𢊍 to 厨.

ceoi4.text: U+5F90 (F1.45 徐) 緩也又姓 (Page 595 徐) Grave, serious, leisurely; tardy, slow (Kangxi: 緩也, 遲也); composed (Kangxi: 安行也), majestic, dignified (Kangxi: 威儀也); to walk carefully and formally; _ts'u po_ (ceoi4 bou6 徐步) to walk gracefully; _ts'u ts'u_ (ceoi4 ceoi4 徐徐), gravely, tranquilly, as a gait. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰糸⿱刀交 to 緩. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【徐】 (zjo) (_adverb-like_) slowly, gently (_adjective-like_) composed, dignified
U+6376 搥 zeoi2 (per 《廣韻》之累切) (Page 34* 搥; here normalised to 捶) To beat (Kangxi 捶: 以杖擊也); to strike, as on a bell; 【捶】 (tsyweX) (_verb-like_) to beat
U+690E (F2.56 椎) 木椎 (F2.56 槌) 同上 (Page 34* 槌椎)

zeoi1.text: U+690E A vertebra (in 脊椎 etc.). A type of tree, like the chestnut but small (Kangxi: 木名似栗而小).

ceoi4.text: U+69CC 搥 捶 (for verb senses) 椎 ceoi4-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.56 椎) 木椎 (F2.56 槌) 同上 (Page 34* 槌椎) A mallet, a beater, a club, a bludgeon (Kangxi 槌: 棒槌所以擊也); a beetle; to beat, to strike (Kangxi 槌: 擊也); _ch'ui ku_ (ceoi4 gu2 槌鼓), to drum; _ku ch'ui_ (gu2 ceoi4 鼓槌), a drumstick; _ta chong ch'ui_ (daa2 zong1 ceoi4 打樁槌), a pile-hammer; _lui ch'ui_ (leoi4 ceoi4 擂槌), a triturating pestle; _met._ large fingers; _ch'ui pui_ (ceoi4 bui3 槌背) to shampoo``.`` (Page 34* 搥) Like the last (槌). Williams' 擂槌: Corrected _lui_ to _lui_.
U+6EC1 cyu4 (uncommon) (F1.45 滁) 水名 (Page 32* 滁) A branch of the R. Yangtsz' (揚子) flowing in between Nanking (南京) and Iching (？？); a city in the east of Nganhwui (安徽).

cyu4.text: U+6EC1 (F1.45 滁) 水名 (Page 32* 滁)

ceoi4.text: U+7BE8 cyu4 (uncommon) (F1.45 篨) 籧篨醜疾也 (Page 32* 篨) A mat (Kangxi: 籧篨竹席) rolled up; _k'u ch'u_ (keoi4 ceoi4 籧篨), a coarse mat; _met._ a bad ailment, repulsive (Kangxi: 醜疾名)~~, hunchbacked~~. Williams' 籧篨: Not hunchbacked, but swollen. Kangxi has 編籧篨爲囷如人之擁腫而不能俯故以名醜疾也, so 籧篨 is the inability to look down (which is somewhat the opposite of being hunchbacked).

cyu4.text: U+7BE8 (F1.45 篨) 籧篨醜疾也 (Page 32* 篨)

ceoi4.text: U+870D cyu4 (alternative) syu4 (uncommon) (Page 32* 蜍) A toad (Kangxi: 蟾蜍也); _shim ch'u_ (sim4 ceoi4 蟾蜍), vulgo, _k'am k'u_ (kam4 keoi4 蠄蟝), a toad.

U+8E95 cyu4 (alternative) (F1.45 蹰) 踟蹰 (Page 32* ~~𨆼~~``躕``) Embarrassed, hindered (Kangxi: 踟躕行不進貌); _ch'i ch'u_ (ci4 ceoi4 踟躕), entangled. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿰𧾷⿸广⿰豆寸 to 蹰. Williams' "Embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed". 【躕】 (drju) Used in 踟躕.

cyu4.text: U+8E95 (F1.45 蹰) 踟蹰 (Page 32* ~~𨆼~~``躕``) Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿰𧾷⿸广⿰豆寸 to 蹰.

ceoi4.text: U+9318 鎚 (for "hammer" senses) ceoi4-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.56 錘) 鉄錘又秤錘 (Page 34* 錘鎚) The weight on a steelyard (Kangxi 錘: 稱錘也); a hammer; to hammer; to beat, to pound; a blow, a knock; _ch'ui t'au_ (ceoi4 tau4 錘頭), the fist; _wang ch'ui_ (waang4 ceoi4 橫錘), a side blow; _yat```` ch'ui ta to_ (jat1 ceoi4 daa2 dou2 一錘打倒), felled him with a blow; _ch'ui lan_ (ceoi4 laan6 錘爛) hammered to pieces. The first word (錘) also means a weight of 12 taels (Kangxi: 六兩曰鍿倍鍿曰錘〇按此與說文解異). Williams' "12 taels": As noted by Kangxi, this differs from the 《說文》 definition of 八銖, which works out to one third of a tael. Williams' 一錘打倒: Corrected _yat_ (jat) to _yat_ (jat1). 【錘】 (ceoi4) A punch with the fist. 打佢兩錘 (daa2 keoi5 loeng5 ceoi4): "Hit him with two blows." 【錘仔】 (ceoi4 zai2) A hammer.

U+9664 cyu4 (uncommon) (F1.45 除) 消除 (Page 32* 除) Steps to a palace (Kangxi: 殿陛也); a vestibule (Kangxi: 除謂門屛之閒); to exclude, to remove (Kangxi: 去也); to deduct, to subtract; to except from; to cure (Kangxi: 病愈者或謂之除); to exchange (Kangxi: 易也); to vacate (Kangxi: 開也); to divide (Kangxi: 算法有乗除); besides, exclusive of; _ch'u lat_ (ceoi4 lat1 除甩) throw it away as useless; _ch'u fi_ (ceoi4 fei1 除非), if, when, thenceafter, premising, not reckoning; _ch'u ch'ut_ (ceoi4 ceot1 除出) to exclude, to lay by; _ch'u fuk_ (ceoi4 fuk6 除服) to lay aside mourning; _kwai ch'u_ (gwai1 ceoi4 歸除), to multiply or divide; _ch'u p'i ki to kan_ (ceoi4 pei4 gei2 do1 gan1 除皮幾多斤), how many pounds beside the tare? _ch'u ch'ak mai shing ni tik_ (ceoi4 caak3 maai4 sing6 ni1 dik1 除拆埋剩呢滴) so much remains after deducting; _ch'u kun_ (ceoi4 gun1 除官), to exchange office; to promote to a post; _ch'u hiu ni mo yan tso tak_ (ceoi4 hiu1 nei5 mou4 jan4 zou6 dak1 除嘵你無人做得) nobody can do it besides you; _ch'u hu_ (ceoi4 heoi3 除去) to push away, to eject. 【除】 (drjo) (_verb-like_) to remove (_verb-like_) to divide 【除】 (ceoi4) To remove clothing, accessories, footwear, etc. 除衫 (ceoi4 saam1): "Take off clothes." 除褲 (ceoi4 fu3): "Take off pants." 除帽 (ceoi4 mou6-2): "Take off a hat." 除鞋 (ceoi4 haai4): "Take off shoes."
U+968B (F2.56 隋) 國名 (Page 597 隋) A dynasty, which ruled from A. D. ~~590~~~``581``--~~620~~~``619``, preceding the T'ang (唐) dynasty.

U+96A8 (F2.56 隨) 從也順也 (Page 597 隨) To follow, to accord with, to yield, to comply obediently (Kangxi: 從也順也); to permit, to let; the way or usage of; an adverb, according to, as, like, wherever; forthwith, presently; a feudal state now _Ts'ui chau_ (ceoi4 zau1 隨州) in the northeast of Hupeh (湖北); _ts'ui shi tso_ (ceoi4 si4 zou6 隨時做) do it as I have time; _ts'ui pin_ (ceoi4 bin6-2 隨便) as you please; very well; _ts'ui ngo lai_ (ceoi4 ngo5 lai4 隨我嚟), come after me; _ts'ui tsoi ngo_ (ceoi4 zoi6 ngo5 隨在我), the decision is with me; _ts'ui tak k'u_ (ceoi4 dak1 keoi5 隨得佢), let him do as he likes; _ts'ui yat to i_ (ceoi4 jat1 dou3 ji6 隨一到二) do the second like (or after) the first; _ts'ui ka mui_ (ceoi4 gaa3 mui6-2 隨嫁妹) a waiting-maid given at marriage, like Zilpah; _ts'ui ch'u kong_ (ceoi4 cyu3 gong2 隨處講), talkiing about it everywhere; _ts'ui shiu loi_ (ceoi4 siu2 loi4 隨少來), bring a little of it, as you can get it; _ts'ui tsik_ (ceoi4 zik1 隨即) immediately; _ts'ui ch'u kai in_ (ceoi4 cyu3 gaai1 jin4 隨處皆然), it is all about, they are everywhere. 【隨】 (zjwe) (_verb-like_) to follow, (_met._) according unto

ceon1.text: U+6625 (F2.52 春) 四季之首 (Page 37* 春) Spring (Kangxi: 春爲靑陽 etc.), beginning of the year (Kangxi: 春者何歲之始也); joyous, glad; ~~wanton~~ ``amorous``; _met._ times, period; budding, starting; _ch'un shik_ (ceon1 sik1 春色) a parade of people with tables meeting the spring; _ch'un ngau_ (ceon1 ngau4 春牛), a clay ox made in the spring; _ch'un i_ (ceon1 ji3 春意), sexual desires; _san ch'un_ (san1 ceon1 新春), the ~~first month~~ ``new year``. Williams' 春意: Corrected _i_ (ji4) to _i_ (ji3). A year. Vitality, life. A type of alcohol (Kangxi: 酒名). 【春】 (tsyhwin) (_noun-like_) spring, springtime

U+693F (F2.52 椿) 木名父曰椿母曰萱 (Page 37* 椿) A longlived tree (Kangxi: 上古有大椿者以八千歲爲春八千歲爲秋), a kind of banian; _ch'un t'ong_ (ceon1 tong4 椿堂), ~~a~~ father ``and mother``; _ch'un hun_ (ceon1 hyun1 椿萱), father and mother. A colloquial word. A classifier of affairs; an incident, an adventure; _yat ch'un k'i sz'_ (jat1 ceon1 kei4 si6 一椿奇事) a singular affair. Williams' 椿堂: Father alone would instead be 椿庭 _ch'un t'ing_ (ceon1 ting4).

U+81A5 春 (F2.52 膥) 鳥之卵 (Page 37* ~~𱼚~~``膥``) An egg, fish roe, crab's eggs; _lui kung ch'un_ (leoi4 gung1 ceon1 雷公膥), 'thunder eggs,' truffles? Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𱼚 to 膥, and corrected missing left dot in 卵. 【膥】 (ceon1) Egg or roe. 雞膥 (gai1 ceon1): "Chicken egg." 魚膥 (jyu4 ceon1): "Fish roe." 水蛇膥咁長 (seoi2 se4 ceon1 gam3 coeng4): "Long as water-snake eggs." (_vulgar_) The testicles. 燒膥袋 (siu1 ceon1 doi6): "Have your ball sack burnt" (as a punishment in hell; see ''The Conmen in Vegas''). For the (vulgar) intensifier senses, and also for the hypothesised relation to springtime in Old Chinese, see the Wiktionary entry for 春. 【石膥】 (sek6 ceon1) A pebble.

ceon2.text: U+60F7 蠢 (F2.53 惷) 亂也 Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 亂. Disorderly (Kangxi: 亂也). Agitated (Kangxi: 惷惷動擾貌). Thick.

U+8822 惷 (F2.53 蠢) 愚也鈍也又虫動曰蠢 (Page 37* ~~舂~~蠢) To crawl, to wriggle, as worms (Kangxi 蠢: 蟲動也); to move, to stir (Kangxi 蠢: 蠢作也, 蠢動作也); foolish, simple. ~~The first also means embarrassed;~~ _ch'un tun_ (ceon2 deon6 蠢鈍) silly, a simpleton; _ch'un ts'oi_ (ceon2 coi4 蠢材), doltish; _ch'un chik_ (ceon2 zik6 蠢直) unaffected; _chai ch'un_ (zai3 ceon2 ？蠢), appearing foolish, silly looking``.`` Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 鈍, and corrected 虫曰蠢動 to 虫動曰蠢. Williams erroneously has 舂 for an alternative form instead of 惷. Insolent, disrespectful (Kangxi: 蠢不遜也). 【蠢】 (tsyhwinX) (_verb-like_) to wriggle, to stir (_adjective-like_) insolent

U+8E33 cyun2 (per 《集韻》尺兖切) (F2.53 踳) 雜也乖舛也 (Page 37* 踳) Blended, mixed, as colors; _ch'un pok_ (ceon2 bok3 踳駮) variegated, colors dissimilar (Kangxi: 踳駮色雜不同). Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰夕生 to 舛. Opposing, going against (Kangxi: 踳駮相乖舛).

ceon4.text: U+5DE1 廵 循 (F2.52 巡) 巡視行也遍也又逡巡退却也 (Page 603 循) to inspect, to go the rounds, to perambulate, to go on the circuit (Kangxi 循: 巡也); (Page 603 廵巡) To cruize (Kangxi 巡: 巡行也); to go on circuit (Kangxi 巡: 徧也, 謂巡繞徧行之), to examine what ``another`` is doing (Kangxi 巡: 巡視行貌), to go on a tour of inspection (Kangxi 巡: 巡守也); a ~~course at a feast~~ ``round of drinks``; _ts'un fu_ (ceon4 fu2 巡撫), the governor of a province; _ts'un kim_ (ceon4 gim2 巡檢), the supervisor or judge of a _sz'_ (si1 司) or township; _ts'un shun_ (ceon4 syun4 巡船), revenue cruizers; _ts'un po_ (ceon4 bou6 巡捕) special aids of the governor-general and governor; _ts'un kang_ (ceon4 gaang1 巡更), on post, as a watchman; _ts'un chun ch'u_ (ceon4 zyun2 cyu3 巡轉處) to go about and examine; _ch'ut ts'un_ (ceot1 ceon4 出巡), to start on a circuit; to reconnoitre; _ts'un ting_ (ceon4 ding1 巡丁), tidewaiters, cruizing officers. Used in 逡巡 (hesitant, retreative) (Kangxi: 逡巡郤退之貌). 【巡】 (zwin) (_verb-like_) to patrol, to inspect Used in 巡守. 【巡守】 (zwin syuwH) (_verb-like_) to make tour of inspection

U+5FAA 揗 (for "comply", "rub", and "console" senses) (F2.52 循) 順也又遵循撫循又循良 (Page 603 循) To follow a leader; to comply with (Kangxi: 自也率循也); to accord (Kangxi: 行順也), to follow or go round with (Kangxi: 依也); to revolve (Kangxi: 循環謂旋繞往來); good (Kangxi: 善也)~~, easy, docile~~; ~~leisurely;~~ to soothe, to console (Kangxi: 撫循慰安也); to explain (Kangxi: 述也); _ts'un kw'ai to ku_ (ceon4 kwai1 dou6 geoi2 循規蹈矩), to accord with and follow ~~instructions~~ ``the rules``; _ts'un fat_ (ceon4 faat3 循法) to obey the laws; _yan ts'un 'ng sz'_ (jan1 ceon4 ng6 si6 因循誤事) lazily hindering the prosecution of business; _yan ts'un_ (jan1 ceon4 因循), not interfering with, indifferent to (Kangxi: 無所作爲曰因循); _ts'un ts'un shin yau_ (ceon4 ceon4 sin6 jau5 循循善誘), gradually and gently lead him on, as to good habits; _ts'un hang ~~ko~~ ``kau`` sz'_ (ceon4 hang4 gau6 si6 循行舊事) act as has been formerly done. Williams' 循行舊事: Corrected _ko_ (gou3) to _kau_ (gau6). Orderly, gradually (Kangxi: 循次序也). To rub (Kangxi: 循摩也). 【循】 (zwin) (_verb-like_) to go along, to comply (_adjective-like_) orderly (_verb-like_) to rub

U+63D7 seon6 (per 《廣韻》食尹切 and 食閏切) (F2.52 揗) 以手相安慰曰揗 (Page 603 揗) To pat, to stroke; to encourage, to soothe (Kangxi: 手相安慰也); to take a sympathizing interest in (Kangxi: 凡以恩相撫以心相恤皆曰揗). To rub (Kangxi: 摩也).

U+65EC (F2.52 旬) 十日曰一旬 (Page 604 旬) A decade of years or days (Kangxi: 徧也十日爲旬); a complete number finished, completed (Kangxi: 徧也); the whole of (Kangxi: 旬均也); in mourning, seven days make a _ts'un_ (旬); _ts'un sui_ (ceon4 seoi3 旬歲) a complete year (Kangxi: 旬滿也旬歲猶言滿歲也); the _sheung_ (soeng6 上), _chung_ (zung1 中), and _ha ts'un_ (haa6 ceon4 下旬), are the first, second, and third decades of a month; _ts'at ts'un_ (cat1 ceon4 七旬), the 49 days of mourning; also 70 years; _ho shui ts'un_ (hou2 seoi2 ceon4 好水旬), the tide will serve you well. 【旬】 (zwin) (_noun-like_) ten-days

U+79E6 (F2.52 秦) 國名又姓 (Page 603 秦) A kind of rice (Kangxi: 一曰秦禾名); a feudal state (Kangxi: 國名) occupying the region of the rivers Wei (渭) and King (涇); the range of mountains in ``the`` south of Shensi (陝西); _Ts'un ch'iu_ (ceon4 ciu4 秦朝), the Ts'in (秦) dynasty, B.C. ~~186~~~``221``--~~120~~~``207``; _ts'un ~~Chi~~ ``Ch'i`` wong tai_ (ceon4 ci2 wong4 dai3 秦始皇帝) the emperor First of this Ts'in (秦) dynasty, who centralized the Chinese empire. Williams' 秦始皇帝: Corrected _Chi_ (zi2) to _Ch'i_ (ci2). A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

U+7D03 seon4 (alternative per 《廣韻》食倫切) (F2.52 紃) 組紃 (Page 603 紃) Fine, silken cords or bindings (Kangxi: 圜采也, 絛紃也, etc.); tassels, ornaments; a model, a pattern (Kangxi: 法也); _tit ts'un_ (dit3 ceon4), the spindle of a spinning-wheel --- a colloquial term. 【紃】 (zwin, zywin) (_noun-like_) silk-cord
U+8793 (F2.52 螓) 似蟬而小 (Page 604 螓) A small species of cicada (Kangxi: 蟲名似蟬而小 etc.); ~~eggs of the mantis;~~ a breeze or large blue-bottle fly (Kangxi: 大蠅曰胡螓). 【螓】 (dzin) (_noun-like_) small-cicada
U+99B4 (F2.52 馴) 善也漸也 (Page 603 馴)

ceot1.text: U+51FA (F2.55 出) 外也退也生也發也 (Page 42 出) To go out (Kangxi: 出入也), to issue (Kangxi: 吐也), to proceed forth (Kangxi: 進也), to manifest (Kangxi: 見也), according to the tenor of the following or preceding word; to eject, to put out; an auxiliary verb, implying completion or action; _ch'ut yap_ (ceot1 jap6 出入) to go in and out, back and forth; _se ch'ut loi_ (se2 ceot1 loi4 寫出來), ~~will~~ write out; _ch'a ch'ut loi_ (caa4 ceot1 loi4 查出來), ~~about to~~ examine; _ch'ut chung_ (ceot1 zung3 出眾) preeminent, to surpass; _ni pin ch'u ch'ut shai_ (nei5 bin1 cyu3 ceot1 sai3 你邊處出世) where were you born? _ch'ut kung_ (ceot1 gung1 出恭), to retire, to ease nature; _ch'ut ka_ (ceot1 gaa3 出嫁) to marry a husband; _ch'ut shan_ (ceot1 san1 出身), to hold office; _ch'ut yau ~~po~~ ``p'o`` sat_ (ceot1 jau4 pou4 saat3 出遊菩薩) to carry idols in procession; _ch'ut ~~ki~~ ``k'i``_ (ceot1 kei4 出奇), surprising; _ch'ut p'iu_ (ceot1 piu3 出票) to issue a warrant, to advertise; _ch'ut hi_ (ceot1 hei3 出氣) to fume or steam, to avenge another's quarrel; _ch'ut t'au_ (ceot1 tau4 出頭), to take the lead; _ch'ut ch'au_ (ceot1 cau2 出醜), to ~~be despised~~ ``make a fool of oneself``; _ch'ut ts'ai_ (ceot1 cai1 出妻), to divorce; _ch'ut kai_ (ceot1 gai3 出繼) to be adopted by an uncle; _ts'ui ni ch'ut sam_ (ceoi4 nei5 ceot1 sam1 隨你出心), as you like to do it; _hon 'm ch'ut seung_ (hon3 m4 ceot1 soeng3 看唔出相) to belie his looks; _seung 'm ch'ut_ (soeng2 m4 ceot1 想唔出) can not recall to mind; _ch'ut ngan_ (ceot1 ngan4-2 出銀), to pay; _pat ch'ut sho liu_ (bat1 ceot1 so2 liu6 不出所料) to guess aright. There is also the reading variation _ch'ui_ (ceoi3) per 《廣韻》尺類切 (unexplained). Kangxi has 《正韻》凡物自出則入聲非自出而出之則去聲然亦有互用者, which prescribes entering tone _ch'ut_ (ceot1) for the intransitive verb senses and departing tone _ch'ui_ (ceoi3) for the transitive verb senses (though conceding some degree of interchangeability). In modern usage, this distinction is absolutely obsolete, and _ch'ut_ (ceot1) is the only reading in all senses. Williams' 出入: Corrected _yap_ (jap1) to _yap_ (jap6). Williams' 出恭: That is, to go to the toilet. Williams' 出遊菩薩: Corrected _po_ (bou6) to _p'o_ (pou4). Williams' 出奇: Corrected _ki_ (gei1) to _k'i_ (kei4). Williams' 不出所料: Corrected _liu_ (liu3) to _liu_ (liu6). To supply, to put forth. To produce. A sister's child (Kangxi: 生也, 男子謂姊妹之子爲出). 【出-transitive】 (tsyhwijH) (_verb-like_) to bring out, to send forth (_verb-like_) to supply 【出-intransitive】 (tsyhwit) (_verb-like_) to go out 【出】 (ceot1) To go out to a place. 出街 (ceot1 gaai1): "Go out." 出城 (ceot1 seng4-2): "Go out to the city." Out. 出便 (ceot1 bin6): "Outside." 褪出啲 (tan3 ceot1 di1): "Shift it out a bit."

zeot1.text: U+6035 ceot1 (per 《廣韻》丑律切) (F2.55 怵) 怵惕恐懼悽愴 (Page 41 怵) Fear, terror (Kangxi: 恐也); to dread; _chut ~~tik~~ ``t'ik``_ (zeot1 tik1 怵惕) timorous. ~~Also pronounced _tut_ (deot).~~ Williams' 怵惕: Corrected _tik_ (dik1) to _t'ik_ (tik1). Pitiful, sorrowful (Kangxi: 悽愴也). 【怵】 (trhwit) (_verb-like_) to fear (_verb-like_) to pity, to sorrow
U+7D40 (F2.55 黜) 諄入聲貶斥也 (F2.55 絀) 同上 (Page 41 絀) (Page 713 絀)

zyut3.text: U+7D40 fat1 (per 《正韻牋》曲勿切 for "bent/withdrawn" sense) zeot1 (per 《廣韻》竹律切) (F1.86 絀 _Fat_ (fat1)) 嬴絀猶屈伸也 (Page 41 絀 _Chut_ (zeot1); here normalised to _Chut_ (zyut3)) Crimson (Kangxi: 絳也) silk; to sew (Kangxi: 紩也); ~~to baste or sew badly;~~ _ying chut_ (jing4 zyut3 嬴絀) to retire and come forth (Kangxi: 猶言屈伸也); unequal; (Page 713 絀 _Chut_ (zeot1); here normalised to _Chut_ (zyut3)) This word also means ~~obstructed,~~ bent; withdrawn~~, as money from circulation~~; _ts'oi yung chi chut_ (coi4 jung6 zi1 zyut3 財用之絀) ~~obstructions in the way of trade~~ ``insufficiency of funds``. Williams' "to baste or sew badly": This is a misreading of Kangxi ...故作秫絀耳此蓋言其女工箴縷之麤拙也. Here, it is 秫 that supplies the meaning of "poor". The character 絀 simply means "sew" or "stitch". Insufficient.

zeot1.text: U+9EDC 絀 ceot1 (per 《廣韻》丑律切) zyut3 (common) (F2.55 黜) 諄入聲貶斥也 (F2.55 絀) 同上 (Page 41 黜) ~~To blame;~~ to degrade, to dismiss, to put out of office (Kangxi: 貶下也, 退也貶也下也去也放絕也減也); to expel; _chut t'ui_ (zeot1 teoi3 黜退) to cashier; _sam chut_ (saam3 zeot1 三黜) ~~a third reprimand~~ ``thrice dismissed``; _yuk chut_ (juk6 zeot1 辱黜) degraded and ashamed. (Page 41 絀; here normalised to 黜) used for the preceding (黜) (Kangxi 絀: 同黜). Williams' 三黜: Corrected _sam_ (saam1) to _sam_ (saam3) per 《論語集注》三去聲. Here, 黜 refers to dismissal, not a reprimand. To reject. To abolish. To rebuke. 【黜】 (trhwit) (_verb-like_) to expel, to reject, to dismiss, to degrade

ci1.text: U+545E (F1.36 呞) 口張開也 (Page 10* 呞𪗪)

zi2.text: U+5470 (F2.35 呰) 呰短窳弱言短力弱才不能動作也 (Page 613 呰) ~~To molest, to be needlessly strict;~~ to revile``, to denigrate`` (Kangxi: 苛也, 口毀也); weak (Kangxi: 弱也); Williams' "to be needlessly strict": Here, Williams has been misled by the space in Kangxi between 《說文》苛也 and 又《玉篇》口毀也. This space should not be present, as both mean the same sense "denigrate". Fan Wan entry: Corrected 溺 to 弱.

ci1.text: U+55E4 (F1.36 嗤) 笑也 (Page 10* 嗤) Laughing (Kangxi: 笑貌); to laugh at; _hom ch'i_ (ham1 ci1 堪嗤), ridiculous; _siu ch'i ch'i_ (siu3 ci1 ci1 笑嗤嗤), to laugh heartily. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𠷣 to 嗤.

U+5AB8 (F1.36 媸) 妍媸 (Page 11* ~~𡟎~~``媸``) Ugly (Kangxi: 醜也); a homely woman; lewd (Kangxi: 淫也), worthless, foolish (Kangxi: 癡也); _ch'i no_ (ci1 nou4 媸奴), a harridan; _in ch'i tsz' ~~kin~~ ``in``_ (jin4 ci1 zi6 jin6 妍媸自見) he knows whether he is handsome or ugly. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𡟎 to 媸. Williams' "homely": In the sense of "unattractive". Williams' 妍媸自見: Corrected _kin_ (gin6) to _in_ (jin6).

U+75B5 呰 訾 ci4 (per 《廣韻》疾移切) (F2.34 疵 _Ts'z'_ (ci4)) 症也吹毛求疵 (F2.35 疵 _Ts'z'_ (ci3)) 瑕疵 (Page 613 呰 _Tsz'_ (zi2); here corrected to _Ts'z'_ (ci4) and normalised to 疵 _Ts'z'_ (ci1)) a defect, a flaw (Kangxi 呰: 瑕也). (Page 615 疵 _Ts'z'_ (ci4); here normalised to _Ts'z'_ (ci1)) Malady (Kangxi: 病也), disease, infirmity; an imperfection, as a mole; a failing, a fault; an eccentricity; _ha ts'z'_ (haa4 ci1 瑕疵) a flaw, any fault which renders a thing imperfect; _siu ts'z'_ (siu2 ci1 小疵) a little fault, as in a man's character; _hak ts'z'_ (hak1 ci1 黑疵) a black spot. In its entry for 訾, Kangxi gives the sense 病也 "defect" with the example 《禮檀弓》故子之所刺於禮者亦非禮之訾也《註》病也 under the reading 卽移切 _tsz'_ (zi1). The reading should instead be 疾移切 _ts'z'_ (ci4), if we read 訾 as an alternative form of 疵 (as I have done here). 【疵】 (dzje) (_noun-like_) defect

ci4.text: U+75B5 (F2.34 疵) 症也吹毛求疵 (F2.35 疵 _Ts'z'_ (ci3)) 瑕疵 (Page 615 疵)

ci1.text: U+75F4 (F1.36 痴) 痴呆 (F1.36 癡) 同上愚也 (Page 10* 癡痴) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 土 to 上.

U+7661 痴 (F1.36 痴) 痴呆 (F1.36 癡) 同上愚也 (Page 10* 癡痴) Foolish, silly (Kangxi 癡: 不慧也); stupid, inapt (Kangxi 癡: 癡者神思不足); idiotic; doting after, lustful, hankering; out of his head, wandering; _ch'i ngoi_ (ci1 ngoi4 癡呆), foolish stolid; _ch'i chut kom ch'un lun ch'u_ (ci1 zyut3 gam2 cyun1 lyun6 cyu3 癡拙噉穿亂處) running in and out, making such a rumpus; _ch'i ts'ing_ (ci1 cing4 癡情), salacious; _ch'i ch'in_ (ci1 cin4 癡纏) bound up in, loving; _pat ch'i pat lung_ (bat1 ci1 bat1 lung4 不癡不聾), if I am not foolish and deaf I cannot reign; _shu ch'i_ (syu1 ci1 書癡), doting on books; _ch'i mai pat sing_ (ci1 mai4 bat1 sing2 癡迷不醒) mad after. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 土 to 上. Williams' 癡拙噉穿亂處: Corrected _kom_ (gam3) to _kom_ (gam2). Williams' 癡迷不醒: Corrected _mai_ (maai4) to _mai_ (mai4).

U+7B1E (F1.36 笞) 捶擊也 (Page 10* 笞) To punish with a bamboo, to bastinado; to correct, so as to reform (Kangxi: 笞者所以敎之也); to scourge, to flog, to bamboo (Kangxi: 捶擊也); _ch'i cheung_ (ci1 zoeng6 笞杖) the stick used in a yamun (衙門) to punish; _ch'i chak_ (ci1 zaak3 笞責) to chastise culprits, vulgo, _ta pan tsz'_ (daa2 baan2 zi2 打板子).
U+25F1D (Page 10* 𥼝黐)
U+80DD (F1.36 胝) 皮厚也 (Page 10* 胝)

zi1.text: U+80DD ci1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.36 胝 _Ch'i_ (ci1)) 皮厚也 (Page 10* 胝 _Ch'i_ (ci1); here normalised to _Chi_ (zi1)) Thickened skin (Kangxi: 腄也, 腁胝, 皮厚也) on the hand; growing callous; _p'in ~~ch'i~~ ``chi``_ (pin4 zi1 胼胝), horny skin. Whilst Kangxi has the reading _ch'i_ (ci1) per 《集韻》稱脂切, it is explained 鳥胃 "bird's stomach" (same as 胵). Williams' 胼胝: Corrected _p'in_ (pin1) to _p'in_ (pin4).

ci1.text: U+86A9 (F1.36 蚩) 無知貌 (Page 10* 蚩) A worm (Kangxi: 蟲名); ignorant, stupid (Kangxi: 騃也); unpolished, sincere, rustic; to impose on; to treat as if ignorant, to despise; _ch'i man_ (ci1 man4 蚩民), the uneducated people; _ch'i ch'i_ (ci1 ci1 蚩蚩), honest, blunt, rustic. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿱山虫 to 蚩.
U+892B (F1.36 褫) 奪也 (Page 10* 褫)

ci2.text: U+892B ci1 (per Fan Wan, common) (F1.36 褫 _Ch'i_ (ci1)) 奪也 (Page 10* 褫 _Ch'i_ (ci1); here normalised to _Ch'i_ (ci2)) To take away insignia, to degrade; ``to strip,`` to take off clothes, to denude (Kangxi: 奪衣也); a fringe (Kangxi: 衣絮偏也); _ch'i kak_ (ci2 gaak3 褫革) to dismiss from office; _ch'i chin_ (ci2 zin1 褫氈), plush, felt. 【褫】 (drje, drjeX, trhjeX) (_verb-like_) to strip

ci1.text: U+8A3E

ci4.text: U+8A3E

zi1.text: U+8A3E (Page 615 訾 _Ts'z'_ (ci1); here corrected to _Tsz'_ (zi1)) to think, to consult (Kangxi: 思也, 量也); to restrict, to limit (Kangxi: 限也); its meaning varies according to the one joined with it. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【訾-level】 (tsje) (_verb-like_) to think, to measure (_verb-like_) to restrict

zi2.text: U+8A3E 呰 (for "slander" sense) 訿 (for "slander" sense) (F2.34 訾 _Ts'z'_ (ci1)) 詆也誚也 (F2.35 訿 _Ts'z'_ (ci2)) 相詆也詩翕翕訿訿 (Page 615 訾 _Ts'z'_ (ci1); here corrected to _Tsz'_ (zi2)) To speak hastily, to utter things unadvisedly (Kangxi: 不思稱意也); to slander, to detract from (Kangxi: 毀也); ~~faulty, licentious (Kangxi: 恣也);~~ ~~impertinent, evil;~~ its meaning varies according to the one joined with it. Williams' "faulty, licentious": This is a reasonable rendering of Kangxi 恣也. But since the associated commentary is 訾讀爲恣, this would make 訾 an alternative form of 恣, and therefore to be read _tsz'_ (zi3) instead of _tsz'_ (zi2). Williams' "impertinent, evil": This is a misreading of Kangxi 惡也. Here, 惡 is departing tone (loathe), not entering tone (evil). To loathe (Kangxi: 惡也). 【訾-rising】 (tsjeX) (_verb-like_) to traduce (_verb-like_) to loathe

ci1.text: U+96CC (F2.34 雌) 鳥之母者曰雌 (Page 615 雌) The female of birds, a hen bird (Kangxi: 鳥母也); _met._ weak; _kin ko ts'z' hung_ (gin3 go3 ci1 hung4 見個雌雄), see which of them will beat. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 毌 to 母. 【雌】 (tshje) (_noun-like_) hen, female-bird (_noun-like_) female
U+9AB4

ci3.text: U+9AB4 胔 ci1 (per common parsing of 音疵) ci4 (per 《廣韻》疾移切) zi6 (per 《廣韻》疾智切) (F2.35 骴) 殘骨 (Page 617 骴) An old bone of men or beasts (Kangxi: 鳥獸殘骨曰骴, 謂死人骨也); putrid bones, having some flesh on them (Kangxi: 骨之尚有肉者, 肉腐曰胔, 有骨曰胔亦作骴).

ci1.text: U+9D1F (F1.36 鴟) 鴟鴞惡鳥攫鳥子而食者 (Page 10* 鴟) A kind of owl, or a sort of hawk (Kangxi: 䳠也); _kok ch'i_ (gok3 ci1 角鴟) or _ch'i hiu_ (ci1 hiu1 鴟鴞), the horned owl; _ch'i cheung_ (ci1 zoeng1 鴟張), to act unscrupulously, as this bird does. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 欔 to 攫. Short for 鴟夷 (a wineskin) (Kangxi: 鴟夷革囊也, 盛酒者也). 【鴟】 (tsyhij) (_noun-like_) owl Used in 鴟鴞. 【鴟鴞】 (tsyhij hjew) (_noun-like_) owl

U+9ED0 𥼝 (Page 10* 𥼝黐) To attach to, to stick on; glutinous, sticky, pasty (Kangxi 黐: 黏也); _ch'i kau_ (ci1 gaau1 黐膠), birdlime; _ch'i keuk_ (ci1 goek3 黐腳) to sorn, to sponge for meals; _'m ch'i mai_ (m4 ci1 maai4 唔黐埋), it don't stick; _ch'i tak ho shat_ (ci1 dak1 hou2 sat6 黐得好實) sticks very tightly; _ch'i mak hong_ (ci1 mak6 hong1 黐麥糠), a sponger, a sorner. 【黐】 (ci1) To stick, to be sticky, to cling. 黐住咗 (ci1 zyu6 zo2): "It's stuck onto it." 好黐牙 (hou2 ci1 ngaa4): "Really clingy to the teeth." 黐底 (ci1 dai2): "Stuck to the bottom of the pot." 黐鑊 (ci1 wok6): "Stuck to the wok." 黐𣲷𣲷 (ci1 nap6 nap6): "Sticky and wet." 黐手 (ci1 sau2): "Sticky hands" (a training technique in Wing Ch'un). To freeload, to impose on another (for food etc.). 黐餐 (ci1 caan1): "Sponge a meal." 黐車坐 (ci1 ce1 co5): "Freeload for a car ride." Short for 黐線. 做到黐咗 (zou6 dou3 ci1 zo2): "Worked till he's crazy." 【黐肺】 (ci1 fai3) To be exhausted. (Literally, "stuck-together lungs".) 熱到黐肺 (jit6 dou3 ci1 fai3): "So hot I'm knackered." 【黐脷根】 (ci1 lei6 gan1) To have a lisp. (Literally, "sticky tongue-root".) 【黐孖筋】 (ci1 maa1 gan1) Crazy, insane. 【黐線】 (ci1 sin3) Crazy, insane. 黐咗線咩 (ci1 zo2 sin3 me1): "Have you gone crazy?" 黐線㗎 (ci1 sin3 ga3): "It's crazy!" Olden attestation for 黐 _ch'i_ (ci1) meaning "glue", hence "sticky": 《廣韻》吕支切 黐 黏也又丑知切 《廣韻》丑知切 黐 所以粘鳥又吕支切 《康熙字典》黐 《廣韻》丑知切《集韻》《正韻》抽知切𠀤音摛《玉篇》黏也 《廣韻》黐膠所以黏鳥 又《廣韻》呂支切《集韻》鄰知切𠀤音離義同

U+9F5D 呞 𪗪 (F1.36 呞) 口張開也 (Page 10* 呞𪗪; here normalised to 齝) To ruminate, to chew the cud (Kangxi 齝: 吐而噍也), vulgarly called _ngau t'o ts'o_ (ngau4 tou3 cou2 牛吐草). The Fan Wan defines it (呞) to gape (Fan Wan: 口張開也).
U+2A5EA (Page 10* 呞𪗪)

ci2.text: U+4F4C (F2.35 佌) 小也詩佌佌彼有屋 (Page 616 佌) Small, contracted, diminutive (Kangxi: 佌佌小也); _ts'z' ts'z' chi mat_ (ci2 ci2 zi1 mat6 佌佌之物) a very little thing. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 佌佊 to 佌佌彼. 【佌】 (tshjeX) (_adjective-like_) small, petty

U+4F88 (F1.39 侈) 大也又奢也 (Page 12* 侈) Extravagant, profuse (Kangxi: 奢也泰也); large, superfluous; prodigal; _ch'e ch'i_ (ce1 ci2 奢侈), wasteful; _ch'i fu_ (ci2 fu3 侈富) prodigal and showy; _ch'i in_ (ci2 jin4 侈言), exaggerated talk. 【侈】 (tsyheX) (_adjective-like_) prodigal

U+59CB (F1.39 始) 初也原也 (Page 11* 始) The beginning (Kangxi: 初也); an opening, a start; to begin, to commence; at the beginning of a sentence, it often means then, was, at that time; _ch'i ch'o_ (ci2 co1 始初), the commencement, first; _ch'i chung_ (ci2 zung1 始終), beginning and end; the whole period, the circumstances; _ch'i ch'ong_ (ci2 cong3 始創) invention. 【始】 (syiX) (_noun-like_) beginning (_adverb-like_) (_met._) first, just
U+5F1B (F1.39 弛) 放也棄也廢也 (Page 11* 弛)

ci4.text: U+5F1B ci2 (per 《廣韻》施是切) (F1.39 弛 _Ch'i_ (ci2)) 放也棄也廢也 (Page 11* 弛 _Ch'i_ (ci2); here normalised to _Ch'i_ (ci4)) A bow unstrung (Kangxi: 弓解也); to let go the string of a bow (Kangxi: 放也); to relax (Kangxi: 緩也); to cast off (Kangxi: 置也舍也); injured, spoiled (Kangxi: 壞也); easy, dissolute, unconcerned (Kangxi: 易也); _fai ch'i_ (fai3 ci2 廢弛), heedless, careless; to fail, to grow old or useless, obsolete; _kai ch'i_ (gaai2 ci2 解弛), to unloose, to throw off; _ch'i lik_ (ci2 lik6 弛力) remiss. 【弛】 (syeX) (_verb-like_) to slacken

ci2.text: U+6065 耻 (F1.39 恥) 慚愧 (F1.39 耻) 同上 (Page 12* 恥耻) Ashamed (Kangxi 恥: 辱也); to feel shame, disgraced, humbled, chagrined (Kangxi 恥: 慙也); to blush or redden; _sau ch'i_ (sau1 ci2 羞恥), shame; _mung ch'i_ (mung4 ci2 蒙恥), covered with disgrace; _ni 'm kin sau ch'i_ (nei5 m4 gin3 sau1 ci2 你唔見羞恥), are you not mortified? _mo ch'i chi t'o_ (mou4 ci2 zi1 tou4 無恥之徒), a brazenfaced rascal; _ch'i ma_ (ci2 maa6 恥罵) afraid of a scolding. Fan Wan entry: Corrected duplicate headword 耻 to 恥. 【恥】 (trhiX) (_noun-like_) shame

U+675D 柂 ci5 (per 《廣韻》池爾切) to1 (alternative per Kangxi 叶託何切) (F1.39 柂) 析薪而循其理 (Page 12* 杝柂) To split wood with the grain (Kangxi 杝: 析薪也, 杝隨其理也); to fall down (Kangxi 杝: 落也); ~~These are also read _i_ (ji4), _li_ (lei4), and _t'o_ (to4), with the same meanings.~~ The reading _ch'i_ (ci2) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 尺里切, explained 析木觀其理. Williams' "read ... with the same meanings": This is not quite correct. 杝 read _i_ (ji4) is the name of a tree. 杝 read _li_ (lei4) is used for 離. 杝 read _t'o_ (to4) is a mistaken parsing of Kangxi 叶託何切音佗 _t'o_ (to1), which is the only reading having the same meaning of "splitting with the grain". 【杝】 (drjeX) (_verb-like_) to split with the grain
U+67C2 (F1.39 柂) 析薪而循其理 (Page 12* 杝柂)

ci4.text: U+67FF (F2.34 柿) 乾柿 (Page 616 柿)

ci5.text: U+67FF ci4 (per Fan Wan) ci5-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.34 柿 _Ts'z'_ (ci4)) 乾柿 (Page 616 柿 _Ts'z'_ (ci4); here normalised to _Ts'z'_ (ci5)) The persimmon, the Diospyrus or China fig; _kon ts'z'_ (gon1 ci5-2 乾柿) or _ts'z' peng_ (ci5-2 beng2 柿餅), dried persimmons or figs, prepared for exportation; _ngau sam ts'z'_ (ngau4 sam1 ci5-2 牛心柿) the large sort; _kai sam ts'z'_ (gai1 sam1 ci5-2 雞心柿) the small sort; _shui tsam ts'z'_ (seoi2 zam3 ci5-2 水浸柿) the yellow persimmon. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'i_ per 《廣韻》鉏里切 for 𣐈. 【柿】 (dzriX) (_noun-like_) persimmon

ci2.text: U+6B64 (F2.35 此) 彼此斯也 (Page 616 此) A pronoun, this (Kangxi: 此者彼之對); here, now; this, the last spoken of; the thing in hand; _pi ts'z'_ (bei2 ci2 彼此) that, this; you, I; there, here; _u ts'z'_ (jyu4 ci2 如此) like this; _'m tsoi ts'z'_ (m4 zoi6 ci2 唔在此) not here; _i ts'z'_ (ji5 ci2 以此) by, or on this account; _ts'z' yan ts'z' tak_ (ci2 jan1 ci2 dak1 此恩此德) for this very kindness. Thus, then. 【此】 (tshjeX) (_determiner-like_) this

U+77E2 (F1.39 矢) 箭也 (Page 12* 矢) An arrow or dart (Kangxi: 弓弩矢也); swift as an arrow; direct, openly; to set forth, to arrange (Kangxi: 陳也); to swear, to vow (Kangxi: 誓也); to display; to resolve, to form a purpose; the 111th radical ("arrow"); _yat ch'i chi ti_ (jat1 ci2 zi1 dei6 一矢之地) a little distance, a bowshot; _ch'i chik_ (ci2 zik6 矢直) straight as an arrow; _fat yat ch'i_ (faat3 jat1 ci2 發一矢), let fly an arrow; _ch'i shai_ (ci2 sai6 矢誓) to take an oath; _ch'i hau pat i_ (ci2 hau2 bat1 ji4 矢口不移), to affirm constantly. Upright (Kangxi: 正也直也). 【矢】 (syijX) (_noun-like_) arrow (_verb-like_) to arrange (_verb-like_) to swear
U+803B (F1.39 恥) 慚愧 (F1.39 耻) 同上 (Page 12* 恥耻) Fan Wan entry: Corrected duplicate headword 耻 to 恥.

U+8C55 (F1.39 豕) 猪也 (Page 12* 豕) A hog, a pig (Kangxi: 彘也); the ~~153d~~ ``152d`` radical ("pig") of characters relating mostly to swine; _ch'i yuk_ (ci2 juk6 豕肉) pork. Williams radical: Corrected 153d to 152d. 【豕】 (syeX) (_noun-like_) swine

U+9F52 (F1.39 齒) 牙齒又年齒也 (Page 12* 齒) The upper front teeth (Kangxi: 口齗骨也, 上曰齒下曰牙); _met._ the mouth, words; age, years ~~(Kangxi: 齒壽也)~~; sort, class (Kangxi: 類也); associates, equals; to commence (Kangxi: 齒始也); to sort, to class by the age (Kangxi: 年也又列也, 論量...歲數); ~~to record, to write in;~~ the 211th radical ("tooth") of characters relating to teeth; _ch'i cheung_ (ci2 zoeng2 齒長), old; _fu chi ch'i_ (fu6 zi1 ci2 父之齒) a father's associates; _nin ch'i mui ts'ui_ (nin4 ci2 mui5 ceoi1 年齒每催), years are hastening one on; _nin ch'i_ (nin4 ci2 年齒), age, years; _ling nga li ch'i_ (ling4 ngaa4 lei6 ci2 伶牙俐齒), eloquent; _pok ch'i_ (bok6 ci2 薄齒), young, without distinction; _ch'i in_ (ci2 jin4 齒言), specious. Kangxi 齒壽也: Modern editions have 壽也 corrected to 齯齒壽也 per the original text of 《爾雅釋詁》. Williams' "to record, to write in": This is (I think) a misreading of Kangxi 錄也. Kangxi 錄 has 《增韻》齒也總也, i.e. to command. (Note that 《廣韻》 has 齒録也 for this sense, which is not very helpful.) Williams' 年齒每催: A misspelling of 年矢每催 (from Thousand Character Classic). 【齒】 (tsyhiX) (_noun-like_) front-tooth, tooth, (_met._) age

ci3.text: U+4F7D (F2.35 佽) 便利也又比也助也 (Page 616 佽) Light, nimble, sprightly (Kangxi: 佽飛...輕疾若飛也); to help, to assist (Kangxi: 助也); ~~to compare;~~ for, instead (Kangxi: 代也遞也); _ts'z' cho_ (ci3 zo6 佽助) to aid. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 㐸 to 佽. Williams' "to compare": This is a misreading of Kangxi 比也, which explains 《詩小雅》決拾旣佽, with commentary 《註》利也謂相次然後射 and lesser-note 《鄭箋》謂指相次比也. In 次比, we have 比 in departing tone, meaning "put beside" or "pack together" (as in 鱗次櫛比 etc.). 【佽】 (tshijH) (_verb-like_) to help, to convenience

U+523A cik3 (alternative for "stab" senses) (F2.35 刺) 刺史又挿也又譏諷 (Page 617 刺) A thorn (Kangxi: 棘芒也); a sting; to pierce, to prick, to thrust a sharp point into (Kangxi: 針刺也); to embroider (Kangxi: 以針黹物曰刺); to brand, to tattoo; to cut into; a visiting-card (Kangxi: 書姓名於奏白曰刺), because it was once cut in wood; to stab (Kangxi: 刺直傷也) and kill (Kangxi: 刺殺也); to spade up, as plants with a trowel; to reprehend, to blame (Kangxi: 刺責之); to criticise, to lampoon; satire; to examine (Kangxi: 刺訊決也); _ts'z' nga_ (ci3 ngaa4 刺牙), to pick the teeth; _hang ts'z'_ (hang4 ci3 行刺) to stab to death; _tsok shi ts'z' k'u_ (zok3 si1 ci3 keoi5 作詩刺佢), to pasquinade in verse; _~~tsam~~ ``ts'am`` ts'z'_ (caam5 ci3 鏨刺) to engrave, to prick; _t'au ts'z'_ (tau4 ci3 投刺) to send in a card; _shut ts'z'_ (syut3 ci3 說刺) or _ki ts'z'_ (gei1 ci3 譏刺) a sarcasm, an inuendo, a taunt; Williams' 行刺: Metaphorically, to assassinate. Williams' 鏨刺: Corrected _tsam_ (zaam5) to _ts'am_ (caam5). 【刺-noun】 (tshjeH) (_noun-like_) thorn 【刺-verb】 (tshjeH, tshjek) (_verb-like_) to pierce, to stab, (_met._) to slay, to assassinate, to castigate, to lampoon

cik3.text: U+523A (Page 571 刺) To bore through (Kangxi: 穿也傷也); to stab; to embroider (Kangxi: 針黹也); to tattoo, to mark the skin; a prickle, a thorn; _ts'ik ts'ik pat yau_ (cik3 cik3 bat1 jau1 刺刺不休), incessant talking (Kangxi: 刺刺多言貌). (Page 617 刺 _Ts'z'_ (ci3); here corrected to _Ts'ik_ (cik3)) to pole, as a boat (Kangxi: 撐也); to pry into, to overhear (Kangxi: 偵伺也); _~~ts'z' ts'z'~~ ``ts'ik ts'ik``_ (cik3 cik3 刺刺) to talk much (Kangxi: 刺刺多言貌).

ci3.text: U+53A0 (F2.35 厠) 囷厠糞也 (Page 617 廁厠) Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'i_ per 《廣韻》初吏切 for 廁.

U+557B 翅 (F1.42 啻) 但也止也 (Page 12* 啻) Only; to stop at (Kangxi: 啻猶止也猶言何止); an overplus (Kangxi: 餘也, 過多也); _pat ch'i_ (bat1 ci3 不啻) not only, more than; _ho ch'i ts'in man_ (ho4 ci3 cin1 maan6 何啻千萬) can it stop at ten millions? _pat ch'i fu mo_ (bat1 ci3 fu6 mou5 不啻父母), more than father and mother. ~~Often read _tai_ (dai3).~~ (Page 12* 翅翄) Used for the last (啻) (Kangxi 翅: 同啻). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 伹 to 但. Williams' "Often read ...": 《集韻》 has the reading 丁計切, explained 高聲一曰諟也, but this does not correspond to the senses given here. 【啻】 (syeH) (_adverb-like_) only, merely, (_met._) less than

U+5E5F 織 si3 (alternative per 《廣韻》式吏切) (F1.42 幟) 旗幟 (Page 12* 幟) The fringe or border on a flag; a flag, a streamer or pennon, long and fringed (Kangxi: 旌旗之屬, 旗幟, 標也, 旛也); to tie or sew on fringes, as a signal (Kangxi: 記也); to signalize. 【幟】 (tsyhiH, syiH) (_noun-like_) flag

U+5EC1 厠 (F2.35 厠) 囷厠糞也 (Page 617 廁厠) To cleanse, to wash (Kangxi 廁: 廁淸也); a place which must be kept clean (Kangxi 廁: ...至穢之處宜常修治使潔淸也); a privy, a necessary (Kangxi 廁: 或曰溷, 或曰圊); in order, to put in proper places, as guests (Kangxi 廁: 閒也次也); a gorge, where a stream forces its way (Kangxi 廁: 高岸夾水曰廁); _ts'z' hang_ (ci3 haang1 廁坑) or _ts'z' sho_ (ci3 so2 廁所), a privy. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _ch'i_ per 《廣韻》初吏切.
U+6063 (F2.35 恣) 縱恣無忌 (Page 617 恣)

zi3.text: U+6063 ci3 (per Fan Wan, common) (F2.35 恣 _Ts'z'_ (ci3)) 縱恣無忌 (Page 617 恣 _Ts'z'_ (ci3)) Relaxation, dissipation; licentious, gay, loose (Kangxi: 縱也); to follow one's passions, to throw off all restraint (Kangxi: 恣謂狡㹟淫戲不以禮也); _fong ts'z'_ (fong3 ci3 放恣) profligate, dissipated; to give the rein to one's lusts; _tsung ts'z'_ (zung3 ci3 縱恣) unrestrained. 【恣】 (tsijH) (_verb-like_) to wanton, to indulge

ci3.text: U+61E5 (F1.42 懥) 忿恨怒也 (Page 12* 懥)

zi3.text: U+61E5 ci3 (alternative per 《集韻》丑吏切) (F1.42 懥 _Ch'i_ (ci3)) 忿恨怒也 (Page 12* 懥 _Ch'i_ (ci3); here normalised to _Chi_ (zi3)) ~~Vexation, irritated~~ ``Angry`` (Kangxi: 怒也); hatred; _fan ~~ch'i~~ ``chi``_ (fan5 zi3 忿懥) enraged, in a great anger. 【懥】 (tsyijH, trijH) (_noun-like_) enragement

ci3.text: U+673F (Page 617 朿) To be distinguished from 束 _ch'uk_ (cuk1) to bind. A thorn, a prickle, a sliver, a sharp slit (Kangxi: 木芒也).

U+6B21 (F2.35 次) 次第 (Page 616 次) Neither first nor best (Kangxi: 不前不精也); coming after, second in order, inferior, secondary; next, succeeding; to put in order (Kangxi: 位次); a halting-place, an encampment (Kangxi: 師止曰次, 安行旅之處爲旅次)``;`` a rest-house, an inn, a shed or stall (Kangxi: 凡舍皆曰次); a mansion (Kangxi: 次舍); a position, as of the stars (Kangxi: 星之躔舍爲次); a time, a place, a stated post; to halt, to reach (Kangxi: 至也); _ts'z' tai_ (ci3 dai6 次第) in order, regularly; _tsoi k'i ts'z'_ (zoi6 kei4 ci3 在其次) in that place; next in order; _t'au ts'z'_ (tau4 ci3 頭次) the first time; _ts'ang ts'ang ts'z' ts'z'_ (cang4 cang4 ci3 ci3 層層次次) rising gradually like a gallery; _ts'z' yat_ (ci3 jat6 次日) next day; _tso ts'z'_ (zou6 ci3 造次) flighty, flurried; _lui ts'z'_ (leoi5 ci3 屢次) repeatedly; _ts'z' ngan_ (ci3 ngan4 次銀), inferior silver; _wai ts'z'_ (wai6 ci3 位次) a seat, a position; _hak ts'z'_ (haak3 ci3 客次) a stranger's lodging, travelers' houses. 【次】 (tshijH) (_adjective-like_) next, second (_verb-like_) to come next (_noun-like_) sequence (_verb-like_) to camp, to lodge Used in 造次.

U+71BE (F1.42 熾) 火盛 (Page 12* 熾) Flame, blaze; splendor (Kangxi: 熾盛也); the glare of lights; numerous; _i yau kau fo, u p'ok u ch'i_ (ji5 jau4 gau3 fo2 jyu6 pok3 jyu6 ci3 以油救火愈撲愈熾) as if you should try to put out a fire with oil, the more the fiercer it burns. 【熾】 (tsyhiH) (_adjective-like_) blazing
U+7CE6 (F1.42 饎) 酒食 (F1.42 糦) 黍稷 (Page 12* 饎糦)

zik1.text: U+7E54 zi3 (per Kangxi 職利切 for "dyed-weaving" sense) (F1.76 織) 扣結布帛之總名 (Page 13* 織) To weave (Kangxi: 作布帛之總名); woven; _chik u_ (zik1 jyu4 織餘), a thrum left in the web; _ts'uk chik_ (cuk1 zik1 促織) a cricket; _chik ki_ (zik1 gei1 織機), a loom; _chik nu_ (zik1 neoi5-2 織女), the star Wega _α_ in Lyra; _chik po_ (zik1 bou3 織布) to weave cloth. Williams' 織布: Corrected _po_ (bou6) to _po_ (bou3). Dyed woven cloth (Kangxi: 織染絲織之士衣染繒也). 【織-departing】 (tsyiH) (_noun-like_) dyed-weaving 【織-entering】 (tsyik) (_verb-like_) to weave

ci3.text: U+7FC4 (Page 12* 翅翄)

U+7FC5 翄 (F1.42 翅) 鳥翼 (Page 12* 翅翄) Wings (Kangxi 翄: 翼也); fins; _ch'i yik_ (ci3 jik6 翅翼) wings; _u ch'i_ (jyu4 ci3 魚翅) fins; the shark's fins; _chin ch'i_ (zin2 ci3 展翅) to ~~flap~~ ``spread`` the wings; _ch'i p'ai_ (ci3 paai4 翅排), spread out like a comb; _kw'an ch'i_ (kwan4 ci3 裙翅) and _tui ch'i_ (deoi1 ci3 堆翅) are sorts of shark's fins.
U+86D3 螆 A type of hairy caterpillar (Kangxi: 毛蟲也).

zi1.text: U+86D3 (Page 610 蛓螆)

ci3.text: U+8786 May appear as either ⿰虫兹 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰虫茲 (+10), depending on the font.

zi1.text: U+8786 May appear as either ⿰虫兹 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰虫茲 (+10), depending on the font. 蛓 (Page 610 蛓螆) A louse, a nit; insects that ~~irritates~~ ``irritate`` the skin; an itching, pickling; _lai tsz'_ (laai3 zi1 癩螆) the itch; _yat shan tsz'_ (jat1 san1 zi1 一身螆) whole person is frowzy; _shang tsz' kau_ (saang1 zi1 gau2 生螆狗), a mangy dog; _shang tsz' kau kom sheng_ (saang1 zi1 gau2 gam2 seng1 生螆狗噉聲), a peevish child, always whining for something to eat; _shui tsz'_ (seoi2 zi1 水螆) water spiders; _mo tsz'_ (mou4 zi1 毛螆) hairy worms. Williams' 生螆狗, 生螆狗噉聲: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). 【螆】 (zi1) A flea, louse, or small insect. 水螆 (seoi2 zi1): "Water flea." 生螆 (saang1 zi1): "Got lice." 蚊螆 (man1 zi1): "Mosquitoey." An itch.

ci3.text: U+8CDC si3 (per 《廣韻》斯義切) (F2.35 賜) 與也惠也 (Page 617 賜) To confer (Kangxi: 予也); bestowed by a superior, usually refers to the emperor; imperial grants; a favor (Kangxi: 賜猶惠也); a benefit; _sheung ts'z'_ (soeng2 ci3 賞賜) to reward; _yam ts'z'_ (jam1 ci3 欽賜) imperial favors; _shau ts'z'_ (sau6 ci3 受賜) to receive a benefit; _t'in kun ts'z' fuk_ (tin1 gun1 ci3 fuk1 天官賜福) may heaven send down blessings; _ts'z' muk_ (ci3 muk6 賜目) to look at; _pai ts'z'_ (baai3 ci3 拜賜) to thank; _mung ts'z' ku_ (mung4 ci3 gu3 蒙賜顧) obliged for your patronage---a phrase on bills; _u ts'z' fa ling_ (jyu6 ci3 faa1 ling4 御賜花翎), an imperial gift of a peacock's feather. 【賜】 (sjeH) (_verb-like_) to confer

U+994E 糦 (F1.42 饎) 酒食 (F1.42 糦) 黍稷 (Page 12* 饎糦) To cook or dress provisions (Kangxi 饎: 炊黍稷曰饎); wine and food (Kangxi 饎: 饎酒食也), victuals; millet used in sacrifice (Kangxi 糦: 大祭亦稷也); _ch'i chan_ (ci3 zaan6 饎饌) food and drink; _ch'i in_ (ci3 jin4 饎筵), a feast. 【饎】 (tsyhiH) (_noun-like_) rice-offering, feast

ci4.text: U+577B (F1.38 坻) 水中高地 (Page 11* 坻) An islet (Kangxi: 小渚曰沚小沚曰坻); a ledge of rocks in a stream (Kangxi: 水中高地也); a bank; to bank in; 【坻】 (drij) (_noun-like_) islet

U+5880 (F1.38 墀) 丹墀 (Page 11* 墀) A porch, a vestibule or piazza, reached by a few steps (Kangxi: 涂地也, 階上地也); a courtyard; the path or walk somewhat elevated between the gate and palace; _tan ch'i_ (daan1 ci4 丹墀), the vermilion avenue, _i. e._ the emperor's palace.

U+6148 May appear as either ⿱兹心 (+9 assumed here) or ⿱茲心 (+10), depending on the font. (F2.34 慈) 慈相心之柔也愛也 (Page 615 慈) Maternal affection (Kangxi: 父母之愛子也); love (Kangxi: 愛也), kindness, mercy, compassion; gentle, soft, tender to (Kangxi: 柔也); a mother; _ka ts'z'_ (gaa1 ci4 家慈) my mother; _ts'z' sam_ (ci4 sam1 慈心), kindhearted; _ts'z' mo_ (ci4 mou5 慈母), kind mothers---often spoil their children; _shing ts'z' ming_ (sing4 ci4 ming6 承慈命) I received my mother's orders; _ts'z' pi_ (ci4 bei1 慈悲), compassionate; _ts'z' ts'eung_ (ci4 coeng4 慈祥), long-suffering, forbearance; _ts'z' oi_ (ci4 oi3 慈愛) loving affection. Williams' 承慈命: Used in marriage invitations, indicating that the groom/bride's father has arranged the marriage according to the wishes of the groom/bride's father's mother. 【慈】 (dzi) (_noun-like_) affection, kindness, (_met._) mother

U+6301 (F1.38 持) 執持又扶持 (Page 11* 持) To take, to lay hold of, to grasp (Kangxi: 握也, 執也); to observe or maintain; firm, decided; to direct resolutely; _pa ch'i_ (baa2 ci4 把持), to manage an affair; _ts'o shin i ch'i_ (cou1 sin3 ji6 ci4 粗扇二持), two common fans; _fu ch'i_ (fu4 ci4 扶持), to assist; _chap ch'i_ (zap1 ci4 執持), to hold in the hand; _ts'o ch'i_ (cou1 ci4 操持), to do with energy; _ch'i fat_ (ci4 faat3 持法) to maintain the law. 【持】 (dri) (_verb-like_) to hold

U+6C60 (F1.38 池) 池沼 (Page 11* 池) A tank, a pool, a pond (Kangxi: 治也...停水曰池); a ditch, a fosse (Kangxi: 城塹曰溝池); stagnant water; _~~sheng~~ ``shing`` ch'i_ (sing4 ci4 城池), a moat; _chung ch'i_ (zung1 ci4 中池), the heart; _in ch'i_ (jin6 ci4 硯池), a water-cup for writing; _tsau ch'i yuk lam_ (zau2 ci4 juk6 lam4 酒池肉林), a wine tank and meat-grove, _i. e._ plenty; _ch'a ch'i_ (caa1 ci4 差池), a mirage pool, a take-in, a mistake; _ch'i t'ong_ (ci4 tong4 池塘) or _u ch'i_ (jyu4 ci4 魚池), a fish-pool. Williams' 城池: Changed _sheng_ (seng4) to literary reading _shing_ (sing4). Williams' 酒池肉林: Originally used to describe the excesses of 紂王. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【池】 (drje) (_noun-like_) pool

U+6CBB (Page 9* 治 _Chi_ (zi6); here corrected to _Ch'i_ (ci4)) Name of a stream (Kangxi: 水名) in the east of Shantung (山東); In modern usage, the level-tone reading _ch'i_ (ci4) for the verb senses is considered obsolete. See commentary at 治 zi6.

zi6.text: U+6CBB ci4 (for literary verb senses) (F1.43 治) 理也平也 (Page 9* 治) to govern, to rule well, to manage (Kangxi: 亦理也); to heal; to oversee (Kangxi: 監督也), to care for; to form; to try causes (Kangxi: 聽獄之成辭亦曰治); experienced, talented (Kangxi: 才多亦曰治); prosperity, good government; _chi song_ (zi6 song1 治喪), to oversee a funeral; _chi ha che_ (zi6 haa6 ze2 治下者), subjects, the governed; _i chi_ (ji1 zi6 醫治) to cure; _t'in ha tai chi_ (tin1 haa6 daai6 zi6 天下大治) the empire well governed, firm peace; _mo fat ho chi_ (mou4 faat3 ho2 zi6 無法可治) there's no way of managing him; _chi ka_ (zi6 gaa1 治家), to order a family; _chi lun_ (zi6 lyun6 治亂) to suppress rebellion. The level-tone reading _ch'i_ (ci4) is the classical reading for verb senses of govern (e.g. 治國 "govern the nation"), as opposed to departing-tone _chi_ (zi6) for adjective senses of govern (e.g. 國治 "the nation governed"). Notwithstanding, the only conclusion that can be drawn from 《廣韻》 is that the proper-noun sense is in level tone: 《廣韻》直之切 治 水名出東萊亦理也 《廣韻》直利切 治 治理也又直之切 《廣韻》直吏切 治 治理也又丈之切 Nevertheless, the distinction in reading between the verb "govern" and the adjective "governed" is not made up. In 《大學章句》, 治其國 is annotated with 治平聲, whilst 國治 is annotated with 治去聲. In modern usage, level-tone _ch'i_ (ci4) is considered obsolete. 【治-verb】 (dri) (_verb-like_) to govern, to administer, to regulate 【治-adjective】 (driH) (_adjective-like_) governed, administered, regulated 【三文治】 (saam1 man4 zi6) A sandwich. 【治】 (zi6) Short for 三文治. 腿蛋治 (teoi2 daan6-2 zi6): "Ham and egg sandwich."

ci4.text: U+74F7 磁 (F2.34 磁) 磁器 (Page 615 磁; here normalised to 瓷) porcelain, chinaware (Kangxi: 瓦器也); _ts'z' hi_ (ci4 hei3 瓷器) crockeryware; _yeung ts'z'_ (joeng4 ci4 洋瓷) foreign chinaware. Williams does not consider 瓷 (porcelain) to be a different word to 磁 (magnet). I have split things up accordingly.

U+78C1 (F2.34 磁) 磁器 (Page 615 磁) The loadstone (Kangxi: 石名可以引鍼); _ts'z' shek_ (ci4 sek6 磁石) the loadstone; Williams does not consider 瓷 (porcelain) to be a different word to 磁 (magnet). I have split things up accordingly. Williams' "loadstone": An old word for a (natural) magnet. Also spelt "lodestone".

U+7960 (F2.34 祠) 祖祠 (Page 615 祠) To sacrifice to ancestors in the spring (Kangxi: 祭也, 春曰祠); a family shrine, an ancestral temple; a temple, a shrine (Kangxi: 廟也); to get a blessing (Kangxi: 報賽也, 求福曰禱得求曰祠); _shan ts'z'_ (san4 ci4 神祠) shrine to the gods, said of the national ones; an ancestral tablet; _ts'z' t'ong_ (ci4 tong4-2 祠堂), an ancestral temple; _fuk tak ts'z'_ (fuk1 dak1 ci4 福德祠) shrine of street gods; _tsit hau ts'z'_ (zit3 haau3 ci4 節孝祠) a temple to virtuous women. 【祠】 (zi) (_noun-like_) spring-sacrifice, sacrifice (_noun-like_) ancestral-shrine

U+7BEA (F1.38 篪) 塤篪又樂器 (Page 11* 篪) A kind of bamboo flute, with seven holes (Kangxi: 管樂也, 周禮云篪七孔); the sound resembles the wailing of children (Kangxi: 篪啼也...如嬰兒啼聲); _ch'i hun_ (ci4 hyun1 篪壎), two instruments which chord; fraternal love. 【篪】 (drje) (_noun-like_) flute
U+7CA2

zi1.text: U+7CA2 (F2.34 粢) 黍稷曰粢 (Page 612 粢) A common name for grains ~~and pulse~~ (Kangxi: 稷也); rice used in sacrifices, which must be of the best; Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱禾氺 to 黍. 【粢】 (tsij) (_noun-like_) millet

ci4.text: U+7CCD May appear as either ⿰米兹 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰米茲 (+10), depending on the font.

U+81CD (F2.34 臍) 肚臍 (Page 616 臍) The navel (Kangxi: 肶臍也); ~~to cut off even;~~ _ts'z' tai_ (ci4 daai3-2 臍帶) the umbilical cord; _shai ts'z' mok k'ap_ (sai6 ci4 mok6 kap6 噬臍莫及) hard as for one to bite his own navel; _t'o ts'z'_ (tou5 ci4 肚臍) the navel; _nun ts'z' ko_ (nyun5 ci4 gou1 暖臍膏), a plaster to keep the navel warm. Williams' "to cut off even": This is a misreading of either Kangxi 劑也 (which means "portion") or Kangxi 通作齊 (which indicates that 臍 can be written 齊, not the other way round). 【臍】 (dzej) (_noun-like_) navel

U+8328 (F2.34 茨) 茨菰 (Page 616 茨) To thatch (Kangxi: 以茅蓋屋); grass on a wall; a spinous trailing plant with triangular seed (Kangxi: 蒺藜也); to collect (Kangxi: 積也聚也); _ts'z' ku_ (ci4 gu1 茨菰), the _Caladium sagittifolium_, a species of Arum. 【茨】 (dzij) (_verb-like_) to thatch (_noun-like_) puncture-vine
U+8332 Used in 龜茲 (Kucha, the name of a kingdom) (Kangxi: 國名).

zi1.text: U+8332 兹 (F2.34 兹) 此也 (Page 610 茲兹) Herbs and shrubs in great abundance (Kangxi 茲: 草木多益也); a mat (Kangxi 茲: 蓐席也); an adjective, this (Kangxi 茲: 此也); an adverb, here; a conjunction, but, still; _tsz' che_ (zi1 ze2 茲者), the following---a phrase used at the beginning of a notice; _tsoi tsz'_ (zoi6 zi1 在茲) here; _tsz' kam_ (zi1 gam1 茲今), now; _nim tsz' tsoi tsz'_ (nim6 zi1 zoi6 zi1 念茲在茲) thinking of a thing without distraction; _u tsz'_ (jyu1 zi1 於茲) hereupon, now then---an illative phrase. 【茲】 (tsi) (_determiner-like_) this (_adverb-like_) now, at this time

ci4.text: U+86B3 (F1.38 蚳) 姓 (Page 11* 蚳) Eggs or larva of ants (Kangxi: 蟻卵也). ~~One says, a bloodsucker.~~ 【蚳】 (drij) (_noun-like_) ant-egg

U+8A5E 辭 (F2.34 詞) 言詞 (Page 615 詞) An expression, a phrase, a sentence, a word (Kangxi: 說也, 言也); a particle; a writing (Kangxi: 文也), an accusation; to speak, to tell the thoughts, to say (Kangxi: 意內而言外也)~~,~~; to accuse (Kangxi: 吿也); to request, to ask (Kangxi: 請也); _ts'z' pat tat i_ (ci4 bat1 daat6 ji3 詞不達意) the sentence does not fully convey the idea; _ts'z' lam_ (ci4 lam4 詞林), the Hanlin (翰林) Academy; _man ts'z'_ (man4 ci4 文詞) writings; _chong ts'z'_ (zong6 ci4 狀詞) an accusation, a petition. (Page 615 辭辞) Similar to the last (詞); 【詞】 (zi) (_noun-like_) phrase, expression

U+8E1F (F1.38 踟) 踟𨆼 (Page 11* 踟) Irresolute (Kangxi: 踟躕行不進也); _ch'i ch'u_ (ci4 cyu4 踟躕), undecided, embarrassed, going on and then returning. Williams' "embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed". 【踟】 (drje) Used in 踟躕. 【踟躕】 (drje drju) (_verb-like_) to walk about hesitatingly
U+8F9E (F2.34 辭) 言辭又不受也 (F2.34 辞) 同上 (Page 615 辭辞)

U+8FAD 辞 辤 (F2.34 辭) 言辭又不受也 (F2.34 辞) 同上 (Page 615 辭辞) an expression, a word, a particle, a part of speech; evidence (Kangxi: 訟辭也), speech, words (Kangxi: 辭說也); to refuse, to decline with thanks (Kangxi: 卻不受也); to resign, to leave, to go, to retire, to depart (Kangxi: 別去也); _t'ui ts'z'_ (teoi1 ci4 推辭) to decline, as an office; _ts'z' hu_ (ci4 heoi3 辭去) to leave, as one's service; _ko ts'z'_ (gou3 ci4 告辭) to take leave, to bid good bye; _sam ts'z'_ (sam1 ci4 心辭) to dislike, averse to; _kung ts'z' pak sheung_ (gung1 ci4 bak1 soeng5 恭辭北上), a respectful adieu on going to Peking (北京); _kok chap yat ts'z'_ (gok3 zap1 jat1 ci4 各執一辭) each held to his own argument; _ts'z' t'ip_ (ci4 tip3-2 辭帖) to send a card of invitation back; _ts'z' hang t'ip_ (ci4 hang4 tip3-2 辭行帖) a card to take leave; _hu ts'z'_ (heoi1 ci4 虛辭) a falsehood; _t'an u ts'z'_ (taan3 jyu5 ci4 嘆語辭) an interjection; _ho ts'z' ku_ (hou2 ci4 geoi3 好辭句) a good style. Williams' 辭帖, 辭行帖: Corrected _t'ip_ (tip6) to _t'ip_ (tip3). 【辭】 (zi) (_noun-like_) statement (_verb-like_) to decline (_verb-like_) to take leave

U+9072 遟 ci4-2 (rising second in vernacular 遲遲地) (F1.38 遲) 緩也又迎也又陵遲 (Page 11* 遲) Slow, dilatory (Kangxi: 徐行也); late, tardy; not urgent (Kangxi: 緩也, 緩而不迫也); to delay, to walk leisurely; to procrastinate; _loi tak ch'i_ (loi4 dak1 ci4 來得遲), came late; _ch'i sam yat tsau loi_ (ci4 saam1 jat6 zau6 loi4 遲三日就來), come in three days; _ch'i hiu_ (ci4 hiu1 遲嘵), you are late! _t'o ch'i_ (to5 ci4 ？遲), to put off continually; _kom ch'i_ (gam3 ci4 咁遲), so late! _fui han ch'i_ (fui3 han6 ci4 悔恨遲), unavailing repentance; _ch'i loi pat k'ap_ (ci4 loi4 bat1 kap6 遲來不及) you will be too late to reach the boat; _ch'i ha t'im_ (ci4 haa5 tim1 遲吓添) wait a little longer; _ch'i yat chan_ (ci4 jat1 zan6 遲一陣) wait a while; _ch'i 'm ch'i_ (ci4 m4 ci4 遲唔遲), will it be late? am I late? _ch'eung ngoi yat ch'i ch'i_ (coeng1 ngoi6 jat6 ci4 ci4 窗外日遲遲), the sun goes down slowly. 【遲-level】 (drij) (_adjective-like_) slow

zi6.text: U+9072 遟 (Page 11* 遲 _Ch'i_ (ci4); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi6)) to wait for (Kangxi: 待也); 【遲-departing】 (drijH) (_verb-like_) to await

ci4.text: U+9908 粢 糍 (F2.34 餈) 飯餅 (Page 612 粢 _Tsz'_ (zi1); here corrected to _Ts'z'_ (ci4) and normalised to 餈) a cake of glutinous rice (Kangxi 粢: 稻餠與餈同). (Page 616 餈) Unleavened cakes; pancakes, or dumplings made of rice flour (Kangxi: 稻餠也); _yau ts'z'_ (jau4 ci4 油餈) cakes fried in fat; _ye ts'z'_ (je4 ci4 椰餈) cocoanut flour cakes. The alternative form 糍 is commonly used in the term 糯米糍 (see below). 【糯米糍】 (no6 mai5 ci4) Glutinous rice balls.

U+99B3 (F1.38 馳) 馳驅又馳騖 (Page 11* 馳) To gallop, to ride fast (Kangxi: 走奔也, 馳騖也疾驅也), as a courier; ~~a fleet horse;~~ to go fast; fast; far, spread abroad; _ch'i to_ (ci4 dou6 馳道) the middle road for the emperor; _ngoi mo fan ch'i_ (ngoi6 mou6 fan1 ci4 外騖分馳), harrassed with cares; _ch'i ma_ (ci4 maa5 馳馬), to gallop a horse; _ch'i yik_ (ci4 jik6 馳驛) to ride post; _ch'i k'u_ (ci4 keoi1 馳驅), to ride fast. 【馳】 (drje) (_verb-like_) to gallop, to speed

U+9DBF May appear as either ⿱兹鳥 (+9 assumed here) or ⿱茲鳥 (+10), depending on the font. 鷀 (F2.34 鷀) 鸕鷀水鳥 (Page 616 鷀; here normalised to 鶿) The fishing cormorant (Kangxi 鶿: 水鳥), the _Phalacrocorax_; _lo ts'z'_ (lou4 ci4 鸕鶿) the cormorant, poetically called _shui lo a_ (seoi2 lou5 aa1 水老鴉), or the old water crow.
U+9DC0 May appear as either ⿰兹鳥 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰茲鳥 (+10), depending on the font. (F2.34 鷀) 鸕鷀水鳥 (Page 616 鷀)

ci5.text: U+4F3C ci5-2 (rising second in vernacular 似似地) (F2.35 似) 如也肖也 (Page 616 似) Like, similar; as, as if, as it were, appearing, resembling (Kangxi: 肖也); to appear, to have the aspect of; _ho ts'z'_ (hou2 ci5 好似) like; _ts'z' 'm ts'z'_ (ci5 m4 ci5 似唔似) is it like or not? _'m to ts'z'_ (m4 do1 ci5 唔多似) not very similar; _ts'z' tsuk ni_ (ci5 zuk1 nei5 似足你), just like you; _ts'z' shi i fi_ (ci5 si6 ji4 fei1 似是而非), somewhat of right, but still wrong; _yau shing ts'z' ngo tik_ (jau5 sing3 ci5 ngo5 dik1 有勝似我的) there are those superior to me; _yan yau seung ts'z'_ (jan4 jau5 soeng1 ci5 人有相似) they resemble each other.
