To continue (Kangxi: 嗣也). To present, to give (Kangxi: 况也奉也). 【似】 (ziX) (_verb-like_) to resemble (_conjunction-like_) like Cantonese prefers classical 似 over 像. 【似】 (ci5) To resemble, to be like. 似佢阿媽 (ci5 keoi5 aa3 maa1): "She resembles her mum." 好似得咗 (hou2 ci5 dak1 zo2): "Seems like it worked." 【似樣】 (ci5 joeng6-2) To resemble in appearance. 好似樣 (hou2 ci5 joeng6-2): "They really look alike." To appear acceptable, to look the part. 噉似樣啲 (gam2 ci5 joeng6-2 di1): "It looks more acceptable like this."
U+59D2 (F2.35 姒) 兄弟之妻相謂曰姒又文王之妃曰太姒 (Page 616 姒) An elder brother's wife; _ts'z' tai_ (ci5 dai6 姒娣) sisters-in-law; _ts'z' fu_ (ci5 fu5 姒婦), an old lady---a term used by the young.

U+6C5C (Page 616 汜) A stream leaving the main branch, and afterwards flowing into it again (Kangxi: 水別復入水也); _ts'z' shui_ (ci5 seoi2 汜水), a small river in Honan (河南) (Kangxi: 水名 etc.), west of K'aifung fu (開封府), having a town of the same name. Unflowing water (Kangxi: 一曰汜窮瀆也, 謂困窮不通之水瀆名汜也). 【汜】 (ziX) (_noun-like_) rejoining-water

cik1.text: U+52C5 (F1.74 勅) 天子制書曰勅命 (F1.74 勑) 同上 (F1.74 敕) 同上又戒謹 (Page 14* 勅敕)

U+53F1 𠮟 cat1 (per 《廣韻》昌栗切) (F1.74 叱) 呵叱怒罵也 (Page 15* 叱) To scold (Kangxi 𠮟: 訶也), to cry out to; to hoot at; to mention; _ch'ik ma_ (cik1 maa6 叱罵) to rail at; _ch'ik kau_ (cik1 gau2 叱狗), to drive out a dog; _ch'ik ming chi i_ (cik1 ming4 zi3 ji3 叱名致意) mention my name, and make my respects. 【叱】 (tsyhit) (_verb-like_) to berate, to shout (at)
U+617C (F1.75 慼) 憂也 (Page 571 慼) Grief, sorrow (Kangxi: 憂也); to be afflicted, to be sad; pained, mournful.

U+621A 慼 (for "grief" sense) 鏚 (for "battle-axe" sense) (F1.75 戚) 親戚憂戚又戚戚心動貌 (Page 570 戚) A sort of pole-ax (Kangxi: 戉類, 戚斧也); to compassionate, to pity; mournful, sorry (Kangxi: 哀也); angry (Kangxi: 憤也), aroused, vexed (Kangxi: 憂也, 惱也); deformed~~, marked in any way~~ (Kangxi: 醜疾人曰戚施); near, related to, dear to (Kangxi: 戚近也); relatives (Kangxi: 親也); _ts'an ts'ik_ (can1 cik1 親戚) one's kindred; _yau ts'ik seung kwan_ (jau1 cik1 soeng1 gwaan1 休戚相關), to feel for other's woes, mutually effected; _ts'ik ts'ik_ (cik1 cik1 戚戚) melancholy. (Page 571 慼) Used for the preceding (戚) (for the "grief" sense) (Kangxi 慼: 又通作戚). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【戚】 (tshek) (_noun-like_) battle-axe (_adjective-like_) grieved, distressed, mournful (_noun-like_) kin

U+6555 勅 勑 飭 sik1 (common for "order" sense spelt 飭) (F1.74 勅) 天子制書曰勅命 (F1.74 勑) 同上 (F1.74 敕) 同上又戒謹 (Page 14* 勅敕) An order, a prohibition or command from the emperor; an ordinance, a special precept (Kangxi: 誡也); a charter, a commission; _ch'ik shu_ (cik1 syu1 敕書), credentials; letters-patent; _ch'ik fung_ (cik1 fung1 敕封), a special perferment; _ch'ik ming_ (cik1 ming6 敕命) an imperial order; _ch'ik fu_ (cik1 fu4 敕符) to make a charm efficacious by spurting water on it; _ch'ik shau_ (cik1 sau6 敕受) to receive commands; _ch'ik tsang_ (cik1 zang6 敕贈) to bestow honors on one's dead parents. (Page 439 飭 _Shik_ (sik1); here normalised to 敕 _Ch'ik_ (cik1)) To enjoin, to direct, to command~~, as an inferior~~ (Kangxi 飭: 與敕同命令也); _~~shik~~ ``ch'ik`` ch'ai_ (cik1 caai1 敕差), to dispatch on public service; _shan ~~shik~~ ``ch'ik``_ (san1 cik1 申敕) to strictly charge, to order; _~~shik~~ ``ch'ik`` fong_ (cik1 fong4 敕房), to direct the proper office or department; _~~shik~~ ``ch'ik`` ling_ (cik1 ling6 敕令) to command, to direct. 【敕】 (trhik) (_verb-like_) to caution (_verb-like_) to command 【得敕】 (dak1 cik1) Smug.

U+65A5 㡿 (F1.74 斥) 開拒也又斥逐擯斥又斥候又斥鹵鹹地曰斥鹵 (Page 14* 斥) To exclude, to expel, to send off, to drive off (Kangxi: 逐也遠也); to scold; to pry into``, to spy`` (Kangxi: 斥度也𠋫視也望也); reaching far, extending to, extensive (Kangxi: 斥廣也), plenty (Kangxi: ...言其多); _ch'ik chuk_ (cik1 zuk6 斥逐) to order away; _min ch'ik_ (min6 cik1 面斥) to reprimand, to speak rudely to; _ch'ik lo_ (cik1 lou5 斥鹵), to let salt-water into the pans; _chi ch'ik_ (zi2 cik1 指斥) to point out faults; _ch'ik fong_ (cik1 fong3 斥放) to dismiss and banish; _ch'ik ch'ut_ (cik1 ceot1 斥出) to remove, to expel; _ch'ik pak k'u_ (cik1 baak6 keoi5 斥白佢), rate him plainly; _ch'ik fat_ (cik1 faat3 斥法) to juggle. Williams' "to juggle": In the (now) archaic sense of performing magical trickery. Salty earth (Kangxi: 斥謂地鹹). To open up (Kangxi: 斥開也). To allocate or spend. 【斥】 (tsyhek) (_verb-like_) to reject (_noun-like_) saline-earth
U+87AB (F1.74 螫) 蟲行毒也詩自求辛螫 (Page 15* 螫)
U+93DA (F1.75 鏚) 斧也 (Page 571 鏚) A sort of pole-ax, a bill (Kangxi: 干鏚斧鉞也, 鏚斧也); an ornamental kind of ax carried in processions. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 釜 to 斧.

U+98ED 敕 sik1 (per Fan Wan, common) (F1.76 飭 _Shik_ (sik1)) 致堅也又整備也又修飭 (Page 439 飭 _Shik_ (sik1); here normalised to _Ch'ik_ (cik1)) to strengthen (Kangxi: 致堅也), to repair; to adjust, to prepare and arrange (Kangxi: 修治); ready, prepared (Kangxi: 整備也); compact, firm; reverent, careful, respectful (Kangxi: 謹貌); _ching ~~shik~~ ``ch'ik`` ho k'u_ (zing2 cik1 hou2 keoi5 整飭好佢), put it in order, arrange it properly; Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱⿰朿攵正 to 整. 【飭】 (trhik) (_verb-like_) to strengthen (_verb-like_) to prepare, to arrange
U+9D92 鷘 (F1.74 𪅙) 鸂𪅙鳥名 (Page 15* 鷘; here normalised to 鶒) A bird with a crest which frequents brooks; a gay colored water-bird (Kangxi 鶒: 鸂鶒水鳥也); a hoopoe? Fan Wan entry: Corrected 雞 to 鸂.
U+9DD8 (F1.74 𪅙) 鸂𪅙鳥名 (Page 15* 鷘) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 雞 to 鸂.

cim1.text: U+50C9 (F2.47 僉) 咸也皆也共也 (F2.47 ？) 咸也皆也共也 (Page 571 僉) All, the whole (Kangxi: 皆也咸也); an unanimous voice, the general opinion (Kangxi: 衆共言之也); a flail (Kangxi: 連枷亦曰僉打穀具也); _ts'im ~~ap~~ ``at``_ (cim1 aat3 僉押) all signed it. Williams' 僉押: Changed _ap_ (aap3) to common reading _at_ (aat3). 【僉】 (tshjem) (_adjective-like_) all
U+20803 (F2.47 ？) 切也割也 (Page 571 𠠃) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 竊 to 切.

U+3510 𠠃 (F2.47 ？) 切也割也 (Page 571 𠠃; here normalised to 㔐) To cut (Kangxi 𠠃: 切割也); to stick in the throat; _ts'im k'u hau lung_ (cim1 keoi5 hau4 lung4 㔐佢喉嚨), pierce his weasand; _ts'im chu_ (cim1 zyu1 㔐豬), to stick a pig; _ts'im shu_ (cim1 syu6 㔐樹) to ingraft by slips. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 竊 to 切. I have normalised 𠠃 to 㔐 for the better font support. 【㔐】 (cim1) To cut or sever. (_Chinese chess_) To capture a piece on an upper corner of the palace using an advisor. 士㔐 (si6 cim1): "Advisor captures on the upper corner." Olden attestation for 㔐 _ts'im_ (cim1) meaning "cut" or "sever": 《廣韻》七廉切 𠠃 𠠃切割也 《康熙字典》𠠃 《五音集韻》七廉切音籤切割也 《康熙字典》㔐 《玉篇》千廉切音僉割也〇按《五音集韻》作𠠃
U+5B45 (Page 572 孅) crafty, subtle (Kangxi: 孅趨巧佞也);

zim1.text: U+5E68 襜 cim1 (per 《廣韻》處占切) cim3 (per 《廣韻》昌豔切 for "lapel" sense) (F2.47 幨) 帖也 (Page 16* 幨) Similar to the last (襜). A curtain of a carriage (Kangxi: 車襜); a curtain over an opening (Kangxi: 帷也); an overlap ``or lapel`` ~~which hides a seam~~ (Kangxi: 披衣, 襟謂之幨). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𭘖 to 帖. To fray(?), to purse up (Kangxi: 絕也, 幨謂絕起也). 【幨-level】 (tsyhem) (_verb-like_) to fray, to purse up 【幨-departing】 (tsyhemH) (_noun-like_) lapel

cim1.text: U+61B8 (F2.47 憸) 利也 (Page 572 憸) Disputatious, skilled at arguing (Kangxi: 小人而謂之憸者形容其沾沾便捷之狀也); insidious, lying flattery (Kangxi: 憸詖也); _ts'im yan_ (cim1 jan4 憸人), a flatterer. 【憸】 (sjem, tshjem) (_adjective-like_) glib-tongued

U+6BB2 瀸 zim1 (per 《廣韻》子廉切) (F2.47 殲) 滅也盡也 (Page 572 瀸; here normalised to 殲) (...) (Kangxi 瀸: 滅也通殲); _ts'im mit_ (cim1 mit6 殲滅) to extirpate. (Page 572 殲) To destroy utterly, to exterminate (Kangxi: 殲盡也), to pierce, to kill; _yat to ts'im sz' k'u_ (jat1 dou1 cim1 sei2 keoi5 一刀殲死佢), stabbed him dead at a blow; _ts'im k'ut k'u fui_ (cim1 kyut3 keoi4 fui1 殲厥渠魁), killed the chief himself. 【殲】 (tsjem) (_verb-like_) to annihilate
U+7038 zim1 (per 《廣韻》子廉切) (F2.47 瀸) 汲也 (Page 572 瀸) To soak, to moisten, to dampen (Kangxi: 漬也洽也); ~~to overflow;~~ a spring appearing and reappearing (Kangxi: 泉一見一否爲瀸 etc.);

U+7C3D (F2.47 簽) 竹簾又簽書 (Page 571 簽) to subscribe, to write one's name (Kangxi: 簽書文字也); a sort of basket (Kangxi: 簽籯籠也); _ts'im tsz'_ (cim1 zi6 簽字) a circular, a subscription paper; _ts'im meng_ (cim1 meng4-2 簽名), to write one's name; _ts'im t'ai_ (cim1 tai4 簽題), to subscribe to; _ts'im kwan_ (cim1 gwaan1 簽關), to join in engaging a teacher;

U+7C64 簽 (F2.47 籖) 神籤騐也又竹籌 (Page 571 簽; here normalised to 籤) Slips of bamboo written on for lots (...); _ts'im u_ (cim1 jyu5 籤語), to draw lots for a response. (Page 571 ~~韱~~``籤``) Similar to the above (簽); a slip, a lot (Kangxi: 驗也, 竹籤用以卜者); a warrant, a permit, a ticket having the officer's name on it; to probe, to bore through (Kangxi: 一曰...貫也); sharp-pointed (Kangxi: 一曰銳也); _chai ts'im_ (zai3 cim1 掣籤) or _cha ts'im_ (zaa1 cim1 揸籤), to lot out officers to their stations; _ngo ts'im_ (ngou4 cim1 摮籤), to ~~draw~~ ``cast`` lots before idols; _ts'im t'ung_ (cim1 tung4 籤筒), a cup to hold the slips; _ch'ut ts'im_ (ceot1 cim1 出籤), to issue a warrant or permit; _k'au ts'im_ (kau4 cim1 求籤), to divine.

U+7E96 摻 (for "delicate" senses) 孅 (for "delicate" senses) (F2.47 纖) 細微也 (F2.47 摻) 與纖同又音覽 (Page 572 纖摻) Fine silk (Kangxi: 繒帛之細者謂之纖); small, delicate (Kangxi: 細也, 纖小也, 纖細也), like silken fibres; silk woven with a white woof and black warp (Kangxi: 黑經白緯曰纖); niggardly, stingy (Kangxi: 纖儉嗇也); _ts'im ho_ (cim1 hou4 纖毫), like a fibre, minute; _ts'im ts'im yuk shau_ (cim1 cim1 juk6 sau2 纖纖玉手), a delicate hand; _ts'im iu_ (cim1 jiu1 纖腰), a slender waist, (Page 572 孅; here normalised to 纖) slender, sharp-pointed, acute (Kangxi 孅: 銳細也與纖通); _ts'im yeuk_ (cim1 joek6 纖弱) weak and delicate. 【纖】 (sjem) (_adjective-like_) delicate (_noun-like_) chequered-silk

zim1.text: U+895C cim1 (per 《廣韻》處占切) (F2.47 襜) 整也 (Page 16* 襜) An apron (Kangxi: 衣蔽前謂之襜); a flap, a skirt; a curtain of a car (Kangxi: 帷也, 車上施帷 etc.); to adjust, to put the dress in order (Kangxi: 整貌); _ts'in chim_ (cin4 zim1 前襜), the front skirt; _chim chim_ (zim1 zim1 襜襜), nice and trim; flapping, as a curtain. To flap (Kangxi: 搖動貌). 【襜】 (tsyhem) (_noun-like_) apron (_verb-like_) to flap, to adjust

cim2.text: U+8AC2 讇 (F2.49 諂) 諛媚也 (F2.49 讇) 同上 (Page 16* 諂~~𨶒~~``讇``) To flatter (Kangxi: 同讇省諛也), to lie to; to cajole; to give a god or man worship or praise not due to him; to fawn on, to court; gratifying to one's feelings, adulation, sycophancy; _ch'im mi chi yan_ (cim2 mei6 zi1 jan4 諂媚之人), a flatterer; _ch'im u_ (cim2 jyu6 諂譽) to praise extravagantly; _ch'im siu_ (cim2 siu3 諂笑) to cajole and laugh with; _p'an i mo ch'im_ (pan4 ji4 mou4 cim2 貧而無諂), poor without being sycophantic. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 謟 to 諂, headword ⿵門謟 to 讇, and headword self-reference to 上. 【諂】 (trhjemX) (_verb-like_) to flatter
U+8B87 (F2.49 諂) 諛媚也 (F2.49 讇) 同上 (Page 16* 諂~~𨶒~~``讇``) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 謟 to 諂, headword ⿵門謟 to 讇, and headword self-reference to 上.

cim3.text: U+50ED cim5 (common) zim3 (per 《廣韻》子念切) (F2.49 僭) 越分也爭也 (Page 572 僭) To arrogate to one's self, to usurp, to assume (Kangxi: 假也, 儗也); to overstep one's limits or rank; usurped, despotic, assuming; _ts'im fan_ (cim3 fan6 僭分) to assume to one's self; _ts'im wai_ (cim3 wai6 僭位) to aspire to the throne; to take another's rank; _ts'im sin kong_ (cim3 sin1 gong2 僭先講), to speak before his turn. 【僭】 (tsemH) (_verb-like_) to usurp (_adjective-like_) erroneous 【僭建】 (cim3 gin3, cim5 gin3) Unauthorised addition of a structure to a building.

zam3.text: U+50ED (Page 572 僭 _Ts'im_ (cim3); here corrected to _Cham_ (zam3)) dubious, false (Kangxi: 僭始不信之端也 etc.);

cim3.text: U+5879 壍 (F2.49 塹) 坑塹 (F2.49 壍) 同上 (Page 572 塹壍) The moat around a wall (Kangxi 塹: 遶城水也), a fosse, a ditch (Kangxi 塹: 坑也); a sluice to lead water upon trees; to deepen, to dig out; _kwat ts'im_ (gwat6 cim3 掘塹) to dig a sluice; _hang ts'im_ (haang1 cim3 坑塹) a gutter. Williams' 掘塹: Corrected _kwat_ (gwat1) to _kwat_ (gwat6). 【塹】 (tshjemH) (_noun-like_) trench, moat
U+58CD (F2.49 塹) 坑塹 (F2.49 壍) 同上 (Page 572 塹壍)

U+66B9 cim1 (per 《廣韻》息廉切) (F2.49 暹) 日光也升也進也 (Page 572 暹) The increasing light and splendor of the sun (Kangxi: 日光升也); advancing, rising higher and higher (Fan Wan: 進也); _Ts'im lo kwok_ (cim3 lo4 gwok3 暹羅國) Siam, a word probably corrupted from _Sien_ through the Portuguese language.

U+69E7 cim5 (alternative per 《廣韻》慈染切) (F2.49 槧) 斬木爲槧 (Page 572 槧) Boards for cutting books on (Kangxi: 牘樸也); tablets for memoranda, tablets to be written on with a pencil. 《廣韻》 has four readings, among which 慈染切 _ts'im_ (cim5) and 七豔切 _ts'im_ (cim3) have explanations consistent with the "board" sense. Williams' "pencil": In the (now) dated sense of a brush.

zim1.text: U+4828 cim1 (per 《集韻》處占切) cim3 (per 《廣韻》昌豔切) (F2.47 䠨) 仰視 (Page 16* 䠨) A horse traveling at full speed (Kangxi: 馬急行). Fan Wan entry: The explanation 仰視 is erroneous (it explains 瞻 rather than 䠨).

cim4.text: U+648D zaam2 (per 《廣韻》子感切 for "hand-movement" sense) zaam3 (per Kangxi 作紺切 for "take-up" sense) zam1 (per 《廣韻》側吟切 for "speed" sense) (Page 572 撍) To move with the hand (Kangxi: 手動也), to take up (Kangxi: 掇也); to select (Kangxi: 摘也), to pull out, as from a pile; to draw, as lots; quick, rapid, urgent (Kangxi: 疾也, 速也); _ts'im 'm to_ (cim4 m4 dou3-2 撍唔到), I drew a blank lot; _ts'im ch'ut_ (cim4 ceot1 撍出) pull it out; _ts'im ch'au_ (cim4 cau4-2 撍籌), to draw a slip. The reading _ts'im_ (cim4) is not unattested for the "select" sense; 《集韻》 has the reading 慈鹽切, explained 摘也. 【撍】 (cim4) To select or draw out, especially from a pile. 撍鎖匙 (cim4 so2 si4): "Fishing for the keys." Olden attestation for 撍 _ts'im_ (cim4) meaning "select": 《康熙字典》撍 ...又《集韻》慈鹽切《韻會》昨鹽切𠀤音潛摘也...

U+6F5B 潜 zim6 (alternative per 《廣韻》慈豔切 for "hide" sense) (F2.48 潛) 伏也藏也 (Page 572 潛潜) To ford, to wade across, to cross water (Kangxi 潛: 涉水也); to dive (Kangxi 潛: 潛涵沉也又游也), to walk on the bottom (Kangxi 潛: 潛行水中亦曰游); to hide away, to abscond, to secrete one's self (Kangxi 潛: 藏也); an ancient name for the River Han (漢) (Kangxi 潛: 漢水之別名); carefully, feeling one's way, heedfully; ~~a cess-pool;~~ _ts'im po_ (cim4 bou6 潛步) careful steps; _ts'im hang shui chung_ (cim4 hang4 seoi2 zung1 潛行水中), to walk in the water; _ch'am ts'im_ (cam4 cim4 沉潛), reserved, diligent. Williams' "cess-pool": This is not correct. Perhaps "fish-pool", per Kangxi 魚之所息謂之潛, 潛糝也. According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, there are (competing) interpretations of 糝 in 潛糝也: Wood dropped into water, around which fish gather; and Water into which grain is thrown, to feed fish. 【潛-dive】 (dzjem) (_verb-like_) to dive 【潛-hide】 (dzjem, dzjemH) (_verb-like_) to hide

cin1.text: U+5343 仟 (anti-alteration) (F1.19 千) 十百曰千 (Page 574 千) A thousand (Kangxi: 十百也); many, an indefinite number; _ts'in man iu tso_ (cin1 maan6 jiu3 zou6 千萬要做) it must by all means be done; _ts'in k'i fan lai_ (cin1 kei4 faan1 lai4 千祈返嚟), I earnestly beg you to return; _ts'in kam_ (cin1 gam1 千金), your daughter; _ts'in li ma_ (cin1 lei5 maa5 千里馬), a racehorse; a courier; _ts'in tsung_ (cin1 zung2 千總), a lieutenant; _lo ts'in_ (lou5 cin1 老千), an old blackleg; name of a card; _ta ts'in_ (daa2 cin1 打千), to bend one knee; _ts'in kan_ (cin1 gan1 千斤), a wrench to extract nails; _ts'in sui_ (cin1 seoi3 千歲) your Highness, said to princes; _ts'in kam kwai t'ai_ (cin1 gam1 gwai3 tai2 千金貴體), your honorable self, said to any one; _ts'in ku k'i sz'_ (cin1 gu2 kei4 si6 千古奇事) exceedingly strange; _ts'in yat pat koi_ (cin1 jat1 bat1 goi2 千一不改), a thousand to one he'll not reform. 【千】 (tshen) (_noun-like_) thousand 【老千】 (lou5 cin1) A swindler, conman, or cheat. 正一老千 (zing3 jat1 lou5 cin1): "He's a right swindler." An act of cheating, especially in gambling games. 咪出老千 (mai5 ceot1 lou5 cin1): "Don't you do any cheats."
U+828A (F1.19 芊) 草盛貌 (Page 574 芊) Exuberant foliage, flourishing vegetation (Kangxi: 芊芊草盛貌); green, like jade (Kangxi: 碧貌); _ts'in ts'in_ (cin1 cin1 芊芊), luxuriant.
U+8E9A May appear as either ⿰𧾷⿺辶⿳覀大㔾 (+15 assumed here) or ⿰𧾷⿺辶⿳覀大巳 (+16), depending on the font. (F1.19 躚) 蹁躚 (Page 574 躚)
U+8FC1 (F1.19 遷) 移徙 (F1.19 迁) 同上 (Page 574 遷迁) Note that 《廣韻》 considers 迁 a different word to 遷, with 迁 explained 伺候也進也又迁葬又標記也.

U+9077 May appear as either ⿺辶⿳覀大㔾 (+11 assumed here) or ⿺辶⿳覀大巳 (+12), depending on the font. 迁 (F1.19 遷) 移徙 (F1.19 迁) 同上 (Page 574 遷迁) To move, to remove, to place elsewhere (Kangxi: 移物曰遷); to ascend (Kangxi 遷: 登也, 去下之高也); to be promoted; to change, to transpose (Kangxi 遷: 變易也), to improve (Kangxi 遷: 遷徙也); to banish, to deport, to drive off (Kangxi 遷: 謫也放逐也); _pun ts'in_ (bun1 cin1 搬遷), to move, as one's residence; _ts'in ku_ (cin1 geoi1 遷居), to change one's lodgings; _~~ap~~ ``at`` ts'in_ (aat3 cin1 押遷), to eject, as a tenant; _tso ts'in_ (zo2 cin1 左遷), to degrade to a lower place or rank. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 徒 to 徙. Williams' 押遷: Changed _ap_ (aap3) to common reading _at_ (aat3). 【遷】 (tshjen) (_verb-like_) to shift
U+9621 (F1.19 阡) 田基也南北曰阡東西曰陌 (Page 574 阡) A road going north and south (Kangxi: 路南北爲阡); a cross-way through a field, a path to a grave (Kangxi: 墓道也).

U+97C6 May appear as either ⿰革⿺辶⿳覀大㔾 (+15 assumed here) or ⿰革⿺辶⿳覀大巳 (+16), depending on the font. (F1.19 韆) 鞦韆 (Page 575 韆) To swing to and fro, to play upon the whirligig; _ts'au ts'in_ (cau1 cin1 鞦韆), a swing (Kangxi: 鞦韆繩戲也). In Cantonese, 韆鞦 (cin1 cau1) "thousand autumns" is preferred over 鞦韆 (cau1 cin1) "autumns thousand". See below. 【韆鞦】 (cin1 cau1) A hanging-seat swing. Whilst 鞦韆 (cau1 cin1) "autumns thousand" is the form attested in Middle Chinese, Kangxi has commentary saying that 千秋 (cin1 cau1) "thousand autumns" is the original (being a well-wish), with 秋千 (cau1 cin1) "autumns thousand" being a verbal corruption that later got spelt 鞦韆: 《康熙字典》韆 ...《廣韻》鞦韆繩戲也《集韻》鞦韆北方戲以習輕趫者 《張有復古編》詞人高無際作鞦韆賦漢武帝後庭繩戲 本云千秋祝壽詞也語譌轉爲秋千後人譌爲鞦韆

cin2.text: U+4FF4 (F1.22 俴) 淺也詩小戎俴收 (Page 575 俴)

cin5.text: U+4FF4 cin2 (per Fan Wan) (F1.22 俴 _Ts'in_ (cin2)) 淺也詩小戎俴收 (Page 575 俴 _Ts'in_ (cin2); here normalised to _Ts'in_ (cin5)) Shallow, thin, beaten out, as a piece of metal (Kangxi: 淺也 etc.); superficial, ignorant. Unarmoured. 【俴】 (dzjenX) (_adjective-like_) shallow, (_met._) thin-armoured

cin2.text: U+6DFA (F1.22 淺) 不深也 (Page 575 淺) Shallow, not deep (Kangxi: 水不深也); superficial, not profound; light, as coloring; easy, having few strokes, simple, as characters; short-haired (Kangxi: 凡獸之淺毛者皆曰淺); ~~dripping;~~ _sham ts'in_ (sam1 cin2 深淺), deep---shallow; hard---easy; _ts'in kan chi t'o_ (cin2 gan6 zi1 tou4 淺近之徒), a superficial scholar; _ts'in wong_ (cin2 wong4 淺黃), a light yellow; _ts'in ts'in ho kong_ (cin2 cin2 ho2 gong2 淺淺可講), he can understand a very little; _ts'in sut_ (cin2 seot1 淺率) vulgar and superficial, as a scholar; _ho ts'in_ (hou2 cin2 好淺), very shallow, very easy; _ngan ts'in_ (ngaan5 cin2 眼淺), impatient, having respect to persons; _hoi yan pat ts'in_ (hoi6 jan4 bat1 cin2 害人不淺), to endamage one seriously. Williams' "dripping": This is a misinterpretation of the senses interchangeable with 濺 zin1, 濺 zin3, or 灒 zaan3. 【淺】 (tshjenX) (_adjective-like_) shallow

zin1.text: U+6DFA

cin5.text: U+8E10 cin2 (common) zin6 (recast to departing) (Page 574 踐 _Tsin_ (zin6); often read _Ts'in_ (cin5)) To tread upon (Kangxi: 履也), to trample on or over (Kangxi: 踐蹋也, 踐殘也使殘壞也); ~~to walk,~~ to step; ~~to arrange out;~~ _~~tsin~~ ``ts'in`` in_ (cin5 jin4 踐言), to fulfill one's promise; _~~tsin~~ ``ts'in`` tap_ (cin5 daap6 踐踏) to tread down or in; _~~tsin~~ ``ts'in`` ku_ (cin5 geoi3 踐？) to tread down the heels; _~~tsin~~ ``ts'in`` wai_ (cin5 wai6 踐位) to ascend the throne; to walk in another's steps. Often read _ts'in_ (cin5). Williams' "to walk", "to arrange out": These are misreadings of Kangxi 行也, 列也 (which mean "column" and "row" rather than "walk" and "arrange"). 【踐】 (dzjenX) (_verb-like_) to trample, to tread, (_met._) to realise, to fulfil

zin2.text: U+95E1 cin2 (per 《廣韻》昌善切) (F1.22 闡) 發也開也 (Page 17* 闡) To open, to spread out, to enlarge from the original condition (Kangxi: 開也); manifest, clear, plain (Kangxi: 顯也, 明也); _chin hoi_ (zin2 hoi1 闡開), to open, to extend; _chin ming_ (zin2 ming4 闡明), to state clearly; _chin tat_ (zin2 daat6 闡達) to comprehend. 【闡】 (tsyhenX) (_verb-like_) to open (up), (_met._) to elucidate

cin4.text: U+524D (F1.21 前) 前後 (Page 575 前) Before, in front of, in presence of (Kangxi: 前後之對); in advance (Kangxi: 進也); previously (Kangxi: 先也, 猶故也); formerly, anciently; the former; to advance, to lead forward (Kangxi: 前猶導也); _ts'in hau_ (cin4 hau6 前後) before, after; _ts'in yat_ (cin4 jat6 前日) day before yesterday; _tai ts'in yat_ (daai6 cin4 jat6 大前日) three days ago; _ts'in t'au_ (cin4 tau4 前頭), in front; _ts'in ut_ (cin4 jyut6 前月) last month; _ts'in yan ta p'a hau yan_ (cin4 jan4 daa2 paa3 hau6 jan4 前人打怕後人), I am afraid, I have been once deceived; _ngan ts'in_ (ngaan5 cin4 眼前), before one's eyes; _ts'in loi_ (cin4 loi4 前來), come here, advance; about coming; _tsoi ts'in_ (zoi6 cin4 在前), before, then; _sheung ts'in hu_ (soeng5 cin4 heoi3 上前去) go ahead, go on; _tsau tung pat ts'in_ (zau2 dung6 bat1 cin4 走動不前), I can't well get on with it; it's a difficult job to manage; _ts'in ch'ing nan liu_ (cin4 cing4 naan4 liu6 前程難料) it's not easy to guess how it will turn out; _ts'in king_ (cin4 ging1 前經), already, before done. 【前】 (dzen) (_adjective-like_) fore, front (_postposition-like_) before (_adjective-like_) former Cantonese (in the vernacular register) makes a distinction between 以前 and 之前. 【以前】 (ji5 cin4) In the relatively distant past. 以前去過 (ji5 cin4 heoi3 gwo3): "I've been there in the past." 【之前】 (zi1 cin4) In the relatively near past. 之前去過 (zi1 cin4 heoi3 gwo3): "I've been there before." Before (when used as a suffix to an event). 瞓覺之前 (fan3 gaau3 zi1 cin4): "Before sleeping." 上機之前 (soeng5 gei1 zi1 cin4): "Before boarding the flight."

U+5EDB (F1.21 廛) 市宅 (Page 17* 廛) Ground allotted a serf (Kangxi: 一畝半一家之居 etc.); a shop, a village market, a bazaar (Kangxi: 市邸也); _ts'un hang ch'in sz'_ (ceon4 hang4 cin4 si3 巡行廛肆) to inspect the markets; _ch'in shi_ (cin4 si5 廛市), a market. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword (with 厂 and 黑-like interior) to 廛. 【廛】 (drjen) (_noun-like_) farming-lot (_noun-like_) shop

U+7E8F zin6 (alternative per 《廣韻》持碾切) (F1.21 纏) 牽纏 (Page 17* 纏) To wind around (Kangxi: 繞也), to bind up (Kangxi: 約也, 束也), to wrap, to bandage; to entwine, to cling to; to molest, to implicate; to bother; _ch'in keuk_ (cin4 goek3 纏腳) to bandage the feet; _ch'in t'au_ (cin4 tau4 纏頭), to bind the head with a turban; _ch'in shau_ (cin4 sau2 纏手), to prevent doing; _ch'in kan_ (cin4 gan2 纏緊), bind it tight; _ch'in toi_ (cin4 doi6-2 纏袋) a waist bag for money; _ch'in min_ (cin4 min4 纏綿), to think of; _ch'in chu_ (cin4 zyu6 纏住) importunate, to bother; _ch'i ch'in_ (ci1 cin4 癡纏), troublesome, to annoy. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword (with 厂 and 黑-like interior) to 纏. Williams' 纏綿: More to be entangled or caught up in. Williams' 癡纏: Corrected _ch'i_ to _ch'i_. Also to be madly in love with. 【纏】 (drjen) (_verb-like_) to twine

U+8E94 (F1.21 躔) 踐踏也又星辰躔度 (Page 18* 躔) To tread (Kangxi: 踐也); to follow in order (Kangxi: 星之躔次星所履行也); to turn in an orbit (Kangxi: 歷行也日運爲躔); a trodden path (Kangxi: 躔徑也), a rut (Kangxi: 行徑轍迹也), a footstep (Kangxi: 脚所踐處); a course, an orbit; _tsik ch'in_ (zik1 cin4 跡躔), a footmark, a vestige; _ch'in to_ (cin4 dou6 躔度) an orbit; _ch'in tap_ (cin4 daap6 躔踏) to tread on. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword (with 厂 and 黑-like interior) to 躔.

U+9322 cin4-2 (vernacular for "money" sense) (F1.21 錢) 十分爲錢 (F1.21 ？) 同上 (Page 575 錢？) Copper coin, cash, coppers (Kangxi: 冶銅爲錢易貨也); coin, a piece of money; cash, wealth, money, property (Kangxi: 財也); a tenth of a tael (両), or mace, which the cash once weighed; _t'ung ts'in_ (tung4 cin4 銅錢), copper cash; _kam ts'in_ (gam1 cin4 金錢), gold coins; _yau ts'in_ (jau5 cin4-2 有錢), rich, wealthy; _ts'in kuk_ (cin4 guk6-2 錢局) a mint; _chau ts'in_ (zaau2 cin4 找錢), to exchange into cash; _ki ts'in ~~chung~~ ``ch'ung``_ (gei2 cin4 cung5 幾錢重) how many mace does it weigh? _fong lo ts'in_ (fong3 lou6 cin4 放路錢), to scatter paper money at burials; _tai kong ts'in_ (daai6 gong1 cin4 大？錢), large coins of Kanghi (康熙); _lan ts'in_ (laan6 cin4 爛錢), bad cash; _ts'in pan kom hau_ (cin4 baan2 gam3 hau5 錢板咁厚) thick as a cash board; _li ts'in_ (lei6 cin4 利錢), interest; _shau ts'in no_ (sau2 cin4 nou4 守錢奴), a miser; _nan ts'in_ (nan2 cin4-2 㤻錢), to gamble; _ts'in leung_ (cin4 loeng4 錢糧), taxes, revenue; _ts'in leung tsz'_ (cin4 loeng2 zi2 錢両子) shot, bullets; _chang ts'in_ (zaang1 cin4-2 爭錢), disputing about money; _ts'in shan pai po_ (cin4 san4 baai2 bou3 錢神擺布) money serves for everything; _ts'in shui tai_ (cin4 seoi2 dai1 錢水低), the rate of exchange is low. Williams' 幾錢重: Corrected _chung_ (zung6 去聲) to _ch'ung_ (cung5 上聲). Williams' 錢板咁厚: Changed _hau_ (hau6) to common reading _hau_ (hau5). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【錢-level】 (dzjen) (_noun-like_) coin 【錢銀】 (cin4-2 ngan4-2) Money. 錢銀嘢 (cin4-2 ngan4-2 je5): "Matters of money."

zin2.text: U+9322 A farming tool (Kangxi: 銚也古田器). 【錢-rising】 (tsjenX) (_noun-like_) adze-hoe

cing1.text: U+6A89 (F1.71 檉 _Ch'ing_ (cing2)) 河邊柳 (Page 21* 檉 _Ch'ing_ (cing2); here corrected to _Ch'ing_ (cing1)) A reddish bark willow, growing by water courses (Kangxi: 河柳也), and thought to indicate rain (Kangxi: 天之將雨檉先起氣以應之故一名雨師). 【檉】 (trhjeng) (_noun-like_) tamarisk
U+6DF8 (F1.68 淸) 淸潔淸正淸廉淸白 (Page 577 淸) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 曰 to 白.

U+6E05 淸 (F1.68 淸) 淸潔淸正淸廉淸白 (Page 577 淸; here normalised to 清) Pure, limpid (Kangxi 淸: 朖也), clear (Kangxi 淸: 澂水之貌), unsullied, sharpsighted (Kangxi 淸: 視淸明也); clean (Kangxi 淸: 潔也), incorruptible, right principled; clear, ringing, as a sound; to settle, to become clear; to purify; to settle an account; style of the present Manchu dynasty; Manchu; _shui ts'ing_ (seoi2 cing1 水清) pure water; _ka shai ts'ing pak_ (gaa1 sai3 cing1 baak6 家世清白) a family of unsullied reputation; _ts'ing kun_ (cing1 gun1 清官), an honest magistrate; _shau ts'ing_ (sau2 cing1 守清), a betrothed girl refusing to marry; _~~ning~~ ``ling`` ts'ing_ (ling1 cing1 拎清), taken all away; _ts'ing kam_ (cing1 gaam2 清減), sallow, poor; _ts'ing ch'o liu li_ (cing1 co2 liu5 lei6 清楚了利) the whole matter is done; _~~ts'eng~~ ``ts'ing`` sho_ (cing1 sou3 清數) to settle an account; _sho mi 'm ts'ing_ (sou3 mei5 m4 cing1 數尾唔清), some unbalanced accounts; _ts'ing to k'i_ (cing1 dou6 kei4 清道旗), a banner to clear the way; _ts'ing sau_ (cing1 sau3 清秀) elegant, well formed; _ts'ing ts'ing tik_ (cing1 cing1 dik1 清清滴) take a little; _tsam chung ts'ing_ (zaam6 zung1 cing1 暫中清), occasionally, when at leisure; _shui sai 'm ts'ing_ (seoi2 sai2 m4 cing1 水洗唔清), it cannot be washed out; _e.g._ a sullied name; _ts'ing t'au lo_ (cing1 tau4 lou2 清頭佬), an old bachelor; _ts'ing sam_ (cing1 sam1 清心), to clear one's character, to purify the heart; _ts'ing ch'iu_ (cing1 ciu4 清朝), the Pure or Manchu dynasty; _ts'ing tsz'_ (cing1 zi6 清字) Manchu words. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 曰 to 白. Williams considers _ts'eng_ (ceng1) a possible reading variation for 清. Williams' 拎清: Changed _ning_ (ning1) to _ling_ (ling1). Williams' 清數: Changed _ts'eng_ (ceng1) to _ts'ing_ (cing1). Quiet. (_phonetics_) Unvoiced. 【清】 (tshjeng) (_adjective-like_) clear, pure (_adjective-like_) unvoiced
U+79E4 (F1.68 秤) 較物輕重 (Page 20* 秤)

cing3.text: U+79E4 稱 (F1.73 秤) 所以稱物之輕重也 (Page 21* 稱; here normalised to 秤) To weigh, to adjust; a steelyard or dotchin (Kangxi 秤: 正斤兩也); _ch'ing fo_ (cing3 fo3 秤貨) to weigh goods; _ch'ing t'o_ (cing3 to4 秤砣), the yard weight; _ch'ing kon_ (cing3 gon1 秤桿), the beam; _ch'ing ~~kau~~ ``ngau``_ (cing3 ngau1 秤鉤), the hook; _sz' ma ch'ing_ (si1 maa5 cing3 司馬秤) to weigh full weight; _cha wan ch'ing_ (zaa1 wan2 cing3 揸穩秤) weight it true; _yat pa ch'ing_ (jat1 baa2 cing3 一把秤) a steelyards; _yat ch'ing kung fan_ (jat1 cing3 gung1 fan1 一秤公分), to divide justly; For the character 秤, Fan Wan records level-tone _ch'ing_ (cing1) for the verb "weigh" and departing-tone _ch'ing_ (cing3) for the noun "scales". This is consistent with the classical tone distinction for 稱 (level-tone verb and departing-tone noun), notwithstanding 《廣韻》 only records departing-tone for the character 秤. In modern Cantonese usage, the verb "weigh" is nevertheless commonly read in departing tone as _ch'ing_ (cing3). Williams has also recorded departing-tone 稱 for the verb "weigh" (here normalised to the character 秤); it is unclear whether this is a mistake on his part or a deliberate attestation of (classical-violating) usage in Cantonese. Williams' 秤鉤: Changed _kau_ (gau1) to common reading _ngau_ (ngau1). 【稱-noun】 (tsyhingH) (_noun-like_) steelyard

cing1.text: U+7A31 秤 (for "weigh" sense) (F1.68 稱) 稱揚稱舉稱道 (F1.68 称) 同上 (F1.68 秤) 較物輕重 (Page 20* 稱) To call, to name, to designate, to style (Kangxi: 名號謂之稱); to say (Kangxi: 言也), to talk about; to simulate; to compliment, to commend (Kangxi: 揚也謂也); to admire; to feign, to plead as an excuse; a designation, an appellation, a name; _tsz' ch'ing k'i fu ut ka fu_ (zi6 cing1 kei4 fu6 jyut6 gaa1 fu6 自稱其父曰家父) one styles his own father _ka-fu_ (gaa fu); _ch'ing fu_ (cing1 fu1 稱呼), termed; _ch'ing tsan_ (cing1 zaan3 稱讚) to praise; _ch'ing yeung_ (cing1 joeng4 稱揚), to speak abroad, to laud; _ch'ing k'i_ (cing1 kei4 稱奇), to regard as excellent; _t'ung ch'ing_ (tung1 cing1 通稱), a general designation; _ch'ing peng_ (cing1 beng6 稱病) to malinger, to feign sickness. (Page 20* 秤; here normalised to 稱) Same as the last (稱), but usually applied only to weighing; to adjust; to weigh (Kangxi 稱: 知輕重也); _ch'ing to chun_ (cing1 dou3 zeon2 稱到準), it is weighed exactly; _ch'ing ch'a_ (cing1 caa4 稱茶), to weigh teas. For the character 秤, Fan Wan records level-tone _ch'ing_ (cing1) for the verb "weigh" and departing-tone _ch'ing_ (cing3) for the noun "scales". This is consistent with the classical tone distinction for 稱 (level-tone verb and departing-tone noun), notwithstanding 《廣韻》 only records departing-tone for the character 秤. In modern Cantonese usage, the verb "weigh" is nevertheless commonly read in departing tone as _ch'ing_ (cing3). Williams has also recorded departing-tone 稱 for the verb "weigh"; it is unclear whether this is a mistake on his part or a deliberate attestation of (classical-violating) usage in Cantonese. Williams' 自稱其父曰家父: Corrected _ut_ (jyut3) to _ut_ (jyut6). To raise (Kangxi: 舉也). 【稱-verb】 (tsyhing) (_verb-like_) to weigh (_verb-like_) to refer to, to proclaim (_verb-like_) to extol (_verb-like_) to raise

cing3.text: U+7A31 (F1.73 稱) 度量也快意曰稱意又均等也 (Page 21* 稱) suitable (Kangxi: 適物之宜也), as one wishes (Kangxi: 愜意); corresponding to (Kangxi: 相等也), satisfied with; _ho i seung ch'ing_ (ho2 ji5 soeng1 cing3 可以相稱) they can pass together; symmetrical; _i pat ch'ing shan_ (ji1 bat1 cing3 san1 衣不稱身), his dress does not fit him; _'m ch'ing_ (m4 cing3 唔稱) unfitting; _ch'ing i_ (cing3 ji3 稱意) as I like. 【稱-adjective】 (tsyhingH) (_adjective-like_) meet, (_met._) competent

cing1.text: U+83C1 May appear as either ⿱艹靑 or ⿱艹青 (assumed here), depending on the font.

zing1.text: U+83C1 May appear as either ⿱艹靑 or ⿱艹青 (assumed here), depending on the font. cing1 (alternative per 《集韻》倉經切 for "luxuriant" sense) (F1.67 菁) 草盛也 (Page 576 菁) Scallion blossoms (Kangxi: 韭華也); a kind of three ribbed rush (Kangxi: 菁茅, 江淮之閒有一茅而三脊 etc.); flowery, ~~elegant,~~ flourishing (Kangxi: 菁菁花盛貌); _mo tsing_ (mou4 zing1 蕪菁) a sort of greens like mustard. Splendid, magnificent (Kangxi: 菁華英也). 【菁】 (tsjeng) (_noun-like_) leek-flower, chive-flower (_noun-like_) spikerush (_adjective-like_) luxuriant

cing1.text: U+873B May appear as either ⿰虫靑 or ⿰虫青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.67 蜻 _Tsing_ (zing1)) 蜻蜓 (Page 576 蜻 _Tsing_ (zing1); here corrected to _Ts'ing_ (cing1)) A dragon-fly, called _~~tsing t'ing~~ ``ts'ing t'ing``_ (cing1 ting4 蜻蜓), which sips the water (Kangxi: 蜻蜓蟲); _~~tsing~~ ``ts'ing`` ling_ (cing1 ling4 蜻蛉)~~, a kind of locust~~. Fan Wan erroneously reads _tsing_ (zing1) (which Williams has followed). Williams' 蜻蜓: Corrected _t'ing_ (ting1) to _t'ing_ (ting4). Williams' "a kind of locust": This explanation is dubious.

zing1.text: U+873B May appear as either ⿰虫靑 or ⿰虫青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.67 蜻) 蜻蜓 (Page 576 蜻) _tsing lit_ (zing1 lit6 蜻蛚) a sort of cricket (Kangxi: 蜻蛚也...詳蟀字註); Fan Wan 蜻蜓 is erroneous for this reading.

cing1.text: U+87F6 sing3 (common) (F1.71 蟶 _Ch'ing_ (cing2)) 蚌屬 (Page 21* 蟶 _Ch'ing_ (cing2); here corrected to _Ch'ing_ (cing1)) A bivalve (Kangxi: 蚌屬), the razor-sheath or Solen, also called _sha lo_ (saa1 lo4 沙螺), or sand screw.

U+8D6C 䞓 zing1 (common) (F1.71 頳 _Ch'ing_ (cing2)) 赤色 (Page 21* 赬 _Ch'ing_ (cing2); here corrected to _Ch'ing_ (cing1)) A carnation or pink color; cloth dyed twice over (Kangxi: 再染謂之赬), to dye red (Kangxi: 赬染赤); _met._ distressing toil which flushes one. 【赬】 (trhjeng) (_adjective-like_) red

cing2.text: U+62EF zing2 (per 《廣韻》蒸上聲) (F1.71 拯) 上舉也援也救也助也 (Page 21* 拯) To rescue (Kangxi: 救也助也, 援也), to pull out, as from a well; to deliver, to save; to raise up (Kangxi: 舉也); _ch'ing kau_ (cing2 gau3 拯救) to save from danger; _ch'ing man u shui fo chi chung_ (cing2 man4 jyu1 seoi2 fo2 zi1 zung1 拯民於水火之中), to save the people from danger like fire and water. 【拯】 (tsyingX) (_verb-like_) to lift (up) (_verb-like_) to rescue

U+88CE 《廣韻》 has 丑郢切 _ch'ing_ (cing2) explained 襌衣. Kangxi erroneously has 《揚子方言》襌衣無袌者謂之裎衣 under the level-tone reading _ch'ing_ (cing4). It correctly has synonymous (but easily misinterpreted) 深衣也 under the rising-tone reading _ch'ing_ (cing2). Note that the text of 《揚子方言》 is 襌衣...無袌者謂之裎衣古謂之深衣. An unlined garment (Kangxi: 深衣也).

cing4.text: U+88CE (F1.69 裎) 裸裎露身也 (Page 21* 裎) ~~The upper part of the body;~~ naked (Kangxi: 倮也); to disrobe one's self; a girdle (Kangxi: 佩紟謂之裎, 裎佩帶也); _lo ch'ing_ (lo2 cing4 裸裎), naked. Williams' 裸裎: Changed _lo_ (lo5) to common reading _lo_ (lo2). 【裎】 (drjeng) (_verb-like_) to nake

cing2.text: U+901E (F1.71 逞) 矜也誇也成也 (Page 21* 逞) Froward, presuming, confident of one's power or pretensions (Kangxi: 矜而自逞也); precipitate, hasty (Kangxi: 疾也); to act on an impulse; pleased with (Kangxi: 快也); ~~to pervade;~~ to exhaust (Kangxi: 極也盡也); _pat ch'ing_ (bat1 cing2 不逞), dissatisfied, careless of; _ch'ing kung nang_ (cing2 gung1 nang4 逞功能), confident of one's power; _pat ch'ing chi ~~to~~ ``t'o``_ (bat1 cing2 zi1 tou4 不逞之徒), a reckless rascal; _ch'ing pun sz'_ (cing2 bun2 si6 逞本事) to boast of one's abilities; _ch'ing ok hang kip_ (cing2 ok3 hang4 gip3 逞惡行劫) to rob without fear; _kw'a ch'ing_ (kwaa1 cing2 誇逞), to brag. Williams' 不逞之徒: Corrected _to_ (dou4) to _t'o_ (tou4). To resolve (Kangxi: 解也). 【逞】 (trhjengX) (_verb-like_) to let loose, to indulge
U+90E2 (F1.71 郢) 地名 (Page 21* 郢) 郢 jing5 (TODO)

cing4.text: U+5448 May appear as ⿱口𡈼 (assumed here) or ⿱口王, depending on the font. The two forms are actually separate characters in Kangxi (p.179#2 and p.181#17 respectively). The definitions here pertain to the first (⿱口𡈼). (F1.69 呈) 申也示也露也 (Page 20* 呈) A statement, a plea of a case; to present a petition or statement; to offer, to present to; to state to; to show, to ~~discover~~ ``reveal`` (Kangxi: 示也見也, 露也); _ch'ing sheung_ (cing4 soeng5 呈上), to inform another; _tsun ch'ing_ (zeon3 cing4 進呈), to memorialize; _tsik ch'ing_ (zik1 cing4 即呈), now send this to you; _ch'ing tai_ (cing4 dai6 呈遞) to present to a superior; _ch'ing pan_ (cing4 ban2 呈稟), to hand up a petition; _ch'ing ~~sz'~~ ``tsz'``_ (cing4 zi2 呈子) a petition; _ch'ing tin_ (cing4 din6 呈？) stated for your inspection; _hung ch'ing_ (hung4 cing4 紅呈), a statement from the gentry. Williams' "to discover": This is a misreading of Kangxi 見也. Here, 見 is used for 現, "reveal". Williams' 呈子: Corrected _sz'_ (si2) to _tsz'_ (zi2). 【猜呈沉】 (caai1 cing4 cam4) To play rock-scissors-paper. 【呈沉磨鉸叉燒包】 (cing4-1 cam4-1 mo4 gaau3 caa1 siu1 baau1) Long-form chant for rock-scissors-paper. Of the three items in rock-scissors-paper: The spelling for "rock" (here 呈沉) is disputed, noting that in 猜呈沉 it is read low (cing4 cam4), and in 呈沉磨鉸叉燒包 it is read high (cing1 cam1). (Allusion to a heavy weight falling suggests 秤 for the first and 沉 for the second.) The spelling for "scissors" is 磨鉸, "sharpened scissors". The spelling for "paper" is 叉燒包, the bun of a barbecued-pork bun being a wrapper that wraps its filling.

U+57D5 (F1.69 埕) 瓦器 (Page 21* 埕) An unauthorized character. An amphora or earthen jar of a pear shape, without handles, for carrying water, oil, or spirits; _shui ch'ing_ (seoi2 cing4 水埕), a water jar; _yat ch'ing yau_ (jat1 cing4 jau4 一埕油), a jar of oil; _kwai lam ch'ing_ (gwai3 lam4 cing4 桂林埕), large jars from Kwangsi (廣西).

U+60C5 May appear as either ⿰忄靑 or ⿰忄青 (assumed here), depending on the font. cing4-2 (in 實情) (F1.71 情) 理也心中也性之所發也 (Page 578 情) The passions (Kangxi: 人欲之謂情), reckoned to be seven (Kangxi: 喜怒哀懼愛惡欲); the feelings, the desires; temper, passion, affection; lust, concupiscence; the circumstances, the facts of a case; an affair, a matter; _sing ts'ing_ (sing3 cing4 性情), temper, disposition; _yung ts'ing_ (jung6 cing4 用情), to assist one heartily; _mo ts'ing_ (mou4 cing4 無情) or _pok ts'ing_ (bok6 cing4 薄情), ungrateful; _to ts'ing_ (do1 cing4 多情), or _ts'ing sham_ (cing4 sam1 情深), very grateful; _kwat ts'ing_ (gwat6 cing4 倔情), thankless; _sz' ts'ing_ (si6 cing4 事情), an affair, a concern; _shat ts'ing_ (sat6 cing4 實情), the real facts; _mo ts'oi ts'ing_ (mou4 coi4 cing4 無才情), inapt, stolid; _~~ts'eng~~ ``ts'ing`` yau_ (cing4 jau4 情由), circumstances or causes of a thing; _sz' ts'ing_ (si1 cing4 私情), illicit, fornicating; _ts'ing un tso_ (cing4 jyun6 zou6 情願做) I'll willingly do it; _ts'ing sheung ok kwo_ (cing4 soeng6 ok3 gwo3 情上惡過) a hard temper; _ts'ing li_ (cing4 lei5 情理), common sense, reasonable; _ts'ing t'au_ (cing4 tau4 情投), a mutual liking. Williams considers _ts'eng_ (ceng4) a possible reading variation for 情. Williams' 多情: Also amorous. Williams' 情由: Changed _ts'eng_ (ceng4) to _ts'ing_ (cing4). 【情】 (dzjeng) (_noun-like_) sentiment, (_met._) sense, nature (_noun-like_) truth, circumstance

U+61F2 惩 (F1.69 懲) 懲治懲戒懲創 (Page 21* 惩懲) To repress, to curb (Kangxi: 㣻也); to warn; to reprove, to reprimand; a warning, a caution, a lesson; _siu ch'ing_ (siu2 cing4 小懲), an admonitory hint; _ch'ing chi_ (cing4 zi6 懲治) to ~~govern strictly~~ ``punish``. Williams' 懲治: To punish, not necessarily in relation to governance. 【懲】 (dring) (_verb-like_) to chastise

U+6674 May appear as either ⿰日靑 or ⿰日青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.71 晴) 雲收雨止而日出也 (Page 578 晴) The sky clearing up after rain (Kangxi: 雨止也晴明也無雲也), the clear blue sky; the stars coming out (Kangxi: 夝雨而夜除星見也, 今俗作晴非); _t'in ts'ing_ (tin1 cing4 天晴), a clear sky; _u ts'ing_ (jyu5 cing4 雨晴), it has stopped raining.

U+6F84 澂 (F1.69 澄) 澄淸 (Page 21* 澄) Still water; limpid, clear (Kangxi: 水靜而淸也); _ch'ing ts'ing_ (cing4 cing1 澄清), pure, still. Williams' 澄清: In modern usage, usually only used in the verb sense "clarify". 【澄】 (dring) (_adjective-like_) clear

U+77AA (F1.69 瞪) 直視也 (Page 21* 瞪) To stare at, to look straight at (Kangxi: 直視也), to fix the eye on; _ch'ing ngan mong_ (cing4 ngaan5 mong6 瞪眼望) gazed at with fixed eyes; _ch'ing shi_ (cing4 si6 瞪視) gazing, staring.

U+7A0B (F1.69 程) 限期也又程式課程路程 (Page 20* 程) ~~The 1⁄100th part of a _ts'un_ (cyun3 寸) (Kangxi: ...十程爲分十分爲寸);~~ a portion or percentage; a rule, a pattern, a regulation (Kangxi: 式也), to rule; a limit, a period (Kangxi: 期也, 限也); a job or measure; an allowance; a part; a road, a journey; to travel; _ch'ing sho_ (cing4 sou3 程數) a percentage on one's accounts; _sung ch'ing i_ (sung3 cing4 ji4 送程儀), to give one his traveling expenses; _hi ch'ing_ (hei2 cing4 起程), to start on a journey; _han ch'ing_ (haan6 cing4 限程), a day's journey; _kim ch'ing_ (gim1 cing4 兼程), a forced march, a rapid journey; _kok ku ts'in ch'ing_ (gok3 gu3 cin4 cing4 各顧前程), each regards his future preferment; _fai ch'ing_ (faai3 cing4 快程), to travel fast; _ch'ing shik_ (cing4 sik1 程式) a form; _mo ch'ing shik_ (mou4 cing4 sik1 無程式) nothing to compare it to; _kau ch'ing kau to yau_ (gau2 cing4 gau2 dou1 jau5 九程九都有), most probably, ~~ten to one~~ ``nine times out of nine`` it will; _yat ch'ing_ (jat1 cing4 一程), a tenth; _tak fan kau ch'ing pun_ (dak1 faan1 gau2 cing4 bun3 得返九程半) got back 95 per cent.; _pat ch'ing k'i lik_ (bat1 cing4 kei4 lik6 不程其力) I wrongly estimated his strength. Williams' "1⁄100th part ...": According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, 十程爲分 should be 一程爲分. This makes 程 a tenth of a 寸. Williams' 得返九程半: Corrected _tak_ (dak6) to _tak_ (dak1). Measurement (Kangxi: 程者物之準也, 程度量之總名). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【程】 (drjeng) (_noun-like_) measurement, measure (_noun-like_) norm

U+9172 (F1.69 酲) 酒病也 (Page 21* 酲) ~~To drink till sick and drunk~~ ``Sick from drink`` (Kangxi: 病酒也); half sobered, and ashamed at being drunk (Kangxi: 一曰醉而覺也); feelings after a bout. 【酲】 (drjeng) (_adjective-like_) hungover, drunk-sick

cip3.text: U+59BE (F2.51 妾) 次妻曰妾 (Page 579 妾) A concubine, a wife taken without the legal ceremonies (Kangxi: 接也得接于君子者也), a secondary wife; term by which ladies call themselves; _ts'ip shi_ (cip3 si6 妾侍) or _shi ts'ip_ (si6 cip3 侍妾) a concubine; _lap ts'ip_ (lap6 cip3 立妾) to bring in a concubine; _ts'ip shan_ (cip3 san1 妾身), I, your handmaid; _siu ts'ip_ (siu2 cip3 小妾) my concubine; _oi ts'ip_ (oi3 cip3 愛妾) your concubine; _shiu ts'ip_ (siu3 cip3 少妾) my young concubine. 【妾】 (tshjep) (_noun-like_) concubine

cit3.text: U+5FB9 May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+11) or 4 strokes (+12 assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.26 徹) 通明也透也 (Page 23* 徹) Pervious, perspicacious, discerning (Kangxi: 通也); to penetrate, to pass through (Kangxi: 徹達也); to remove, to take away (Kangxi: 去也); ~~a road, a thoroughfare;~~ to skin, to peel off (Kangxi: 剝取也), to destroy (Kangxi: 毀也); to ~~cultivate~~ ``regulate`` (Kangxi: 治也); _ch'it chan_ (cit3 zaan6 徹饌) to remove food; _ch'it tai_ (cit3 dai2 徹底), to understand thoroughly; _ch'it t'au_ (cit3 tau3 徹透) fully known. Williams' "a road, a thoroughfare": This is a misreading of Kangxi 道也 (but an understandable one, since no example is supplied). According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, this explanation applies to 天命不徹 (in 《詩經》). There, 徹 is usually interpreted as "follow/comply", although James Legge interprets it as "explain". 【徹】 (trhjet, drjet) (_verb-like_) to pervade (_verb-like_) to remove, to withdraw (_verb-like_) to regulate

U+63A3 (Page 10|n53 掣 _Chai_ (zai3); here corrected to _Ch'it_ (cit3)) to take, to choose out, to raise up (Kangxi: 揭也取也); to select; to pull, to draw (Kangxi: 挽也); _~~chai~~ ``ch'it`` ts'im_ (cit3 cim1 掣籤), to draw lots; (Page 23* 掣) To choose, to draw out (Kangxi: 取也); _ch'it ts'im_ (cit3 cim1 掣籤), to draw lots for official duty.

zai3.text: U+63A3 cai3 (per 《廣韻》尺制切) (F1.32 掣) 掣肘滯隔不進也 (Page 10|n53 掣) To obstruct, to hinder from ~~entering~~ ``advancing`` (Kangxi: 滯隔不進也); _hin chai_ (hin1 zai3 牽掣) to compel, to force; _cha chai cha tsung_ (zaa3 zai3 zaa3 zung3 乍掣乍縱) a twitching, as of children in convulsions; _chai tin_ (zai3 din6 掣電) like lightning, as an electrical machine acts; _hip chai_ (hip6 zai3 挾掣) to restrain; _chai chau_ (zai3 zaau2 掣肘), to impede, to embarrass. Williams' 掣肘: Changed _chau_ (zau2) to common reading _chau_ (zaau2). Williams' "to embarrass": In the (now) archaic sense of "impede", "obstruct". To hinder by pulling or tugging back (Kangxi: 甹夆掣曳也). 【掣】 (tsyhejH) (_verb-like_) to tug, to drag 【掣】 (zai3) A push-button, switch, pedal, etc. 手掣 (sau2 zai3): "Handbrake." 撳掣 (gam6 zai3): "Press the button." 煞掣 (saat3 zai3): "Slam the brakes." 總掣 (zung2 zai3): "The main switch." 跳掣 (tiu3 zai3): "Trip the breaker." 閂掣 (saan1 zai3): "Turn off the switch." 開掣 (hoi1 zai3): "Turn on the switch." 電掣 (din6 zai3): "An electrical switch."

cit3.text: U+64A4 May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+11) or 4 strokes (+12 assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.26 撤) 抽除也握也 (Page 23* 撤) To send away, to remove (Kangxi: 除去也); to reject, to recall; to put aside; to take off, to flay (Kangxi: 抽也剝也); _ch'it nung_ (cit3 nung4 撤膿), to come to a head, as a boil; _pi fung ch'it hu_ (bei2 fung1 cit3 heoi3 畀風撤去) whisked away by the wind; _ch'it hu_ (cit3 heoi3 撤去) to send or take away; _ch'it hoi_ (cit3 hoi1 撤開), to remove; _ch'it ui_ (cit3 wui4 撤回), to put back, to recall an officer; _ch'it tsik san t'oi_ (cit3 zik6 saan3 toi4 撤席散檯), to clear off and leave the table. 【撤】 (trhjet, drjet) (_verb-like_) to remove, to withdraw

U+6F88 May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+11) or 4 strokes (+12 assumed here), depending on the font. zit6 (per 《廣韻》直列切) (Page 23* 澈) Pure, transparent, pellucid (Kangxi: 水澄也); _ch'ing ch'it_ (cing4 cit3 澄澈) pureminded, clear.

U+8A2D (F1.26 設) 置主也陳設也 (Page 23* 設) To institute, to establish (Kangxi: 立也); to set forth (Kangxi: 施陳也); to spread, as a net; to arrange, to place, to set up (Kangxi: 置也); to suppose (Kangxi: 假借之辭); a band of soldiers, or its officer (Kangxi: 突厥別部典兵者曰設); large, said of a sword (Kangxi: 大也); _'m shai ch'it kun_ (m4 sai2 cit3 gun1 唔使設官), not to care anything for the magistrates, or there's no need of them; _ch'it lap_ (cit3 lap6 設立) to establish; _ch'it in_ (cit3 jin4 設筵), to make a feast; _ch'it fat_ (cit3 faat3 設法) to devise means; _ch'it kai_ (cit3 gai3 設計) to plot against; _ch'it wak_ (cit3 waak6 設或) or _ch'it sz'_ (cit3 si2 設使) suppose that; _p'o ch'it_ (pou1 cit3 鋪設) to set in order, to prepare; _ch'it kau_ (cit3 gaau3 設教) or _ch'it cheung_ (cit3 zoeng3 設帳) to open a school. A colloquial word. A very little, a bit, not half enough; _tun ch'it kom tun_ (dyun2 cit3 gam3 dyun2 短設咁短), not half long enough. 【設】 (syet) (_verb-like_) to set (_verb-like_) to establish (_verb-like_) to suppose

U+8F4D May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+11) or 4 strokes (+12 assumed here), depending on the font. zit6 (per 《廣韻》直列切) (F1.26 轍) 車輪 (Page 23* 轍) A rut of a wheel (Kangxi: 車輪所碾迹也); _k'ok ch'it_ (kok3 cit3 涸轍) a dry rut; _met._ one in great straits; _ying to ts'in ch'it_ (jing4 dou6 cin4 cit3 仍蹈前轍) to go in the old rut, to act badly again. 【轍】 (drjet) (_noun-like_) wheel-rut, rut

ciu1.text: U+5F28 (F2.2 弨) 弛也 (Page 25* 弨) To fly back, as a bow (Kangxi: 弓反也); to spring back (Kangxi: 翻起曰弨); a bow unbent (Kangxi: 弓弛貌). 【弨】 (tsyhew) (_adjective-like_) relaxed, undrawn

U+662D 炤 ziu1 (per 《廣韻》止遥切) (F2.2 昭) 明也光也 (Page 25* 昭) The brightness of the sun (Kangxi: 日明也); bright, splendid (Kangxi: 明也, 光也); manifest (Kangxi: 昭見也, 著也); luminous, refulgent; _ch'iu muk_ (ciu1 muk6 昭穆) the order of precedence in the ancestral hall; _i muk ch'iu cheung_ (ji5 muk6 ciu1 zoeng1 耳目昭彰), bright to the eyes and clear to the ears, as a reputation; _ch'iu in_ (ciu1 jin4 昭然), intelligible; _ch'iu ch'euk nin heng_ (ciu1 coek3 nin4 heng1 昭灼年輕), beautiful and young. 【昭】 (tsyew) (_adjective-like_) luminous, illuminate

ziu3.text: U+70A4 (F2.6 照) 明也燭也 (F2.6 炤) 同上 (Page 24* 照炤)

ciu1.text: U+8D85 (F2.2 超) 越也躍而過也 (Page 25* 超) To leap over, to trip (Kangxi: 跳也); to hop across, to vault, to step over, to go before, to precede (Kangxi: 出前也); to excel, to surpass (Kangxi: 超卓也); to promote; to raise; _ch'iu pat_ (ciu1 bat6 超拔) to override an officer; _ch'iu yau_ (ciu1 jau1 超幽) or _ch'iu to_ (ciu1 dou6 超度) to release spirits from suffering; _ch'iu tang_ (ciu1 dang2 超等), to exceed in the bachelor's examination; _ch'iu kw'an_ (ciu1 kwan4 超群), excelling; _ch'iu shang_ (ciu1 sang1 超生), to restore to health, to save from death``, to be reincarnated``; _ch'iu ch'euk_ (ciu1 coek3 超卓) to surpass. Williams' "to trip": In the (now) dated sense of skipping lightly. Williams' 超拔: Changed _pat_ (baat6) to modern _pat_ (bat6). 【超】 (trhjew) (_verb-like_) to leap (over) (_verb-like_) to transcend 【超】 (ciu1) To stare or glare. 眼超超 (ngaan5 ciu1 ciu1): "Eyes staring." Sunglasses. 黑超 (hak1 ciu1): "Sunnies." An interjection of dismissal or dissatisfaction. 超、得兩個人 (ciu1, dak1 loeng5 go3 jan4): "Pff, only two people."

U+91D7 ziu1 (per 《廣韻》止遥切) (F2.2 釗) 遠也見也勉也又弩牙也 (Page 25* 釗) To pare, to lop off (Kangxi: 刓也); to see, to wait on (Kangxi: 見也); distant (Kangxi: 遠也); to encourage (Kangxi: 釗勉也); _min ch'iu_ (min5 ciu1 勉釗), to stimulate one; _yat ch'iu_ (jat1 ciu1 一釗), one dig, one delve.

U+936C 鍫 (Page 582 鍫 _Ts'iu_ (ciu3); here corrected to _Ts'iu_ (ciu1) and normalised to 鍬) A spade or pick (Kangxi 鍫: 臿也); an implement for turning up the ground; to dig deep; _ts'iu t'ong_ (ciu1 tong4 鍬塘), to dig out a fishpond; _tai tung ts'iu_ (daai6 dung6 ciu1 大動鍬) a crowbar.

ciu2.text: U+6084 ciu3 (common) (F2.5 悄) 憂也 (Page 581 悄) Sad, downcast, sorrowing (Kangxi: 憂也); _yau sam ts'iu ts'iu_ (jau1 sam1 ciu2 ciu2 憂心悄悄), sick at heart, quietly sad, sorrowing in secret. The common reading ciu3 is sung by Danny Chan in 深愛著你 (1985) (containing the line 悄悄看我熟睡). Quietly. 【悄】 (tshjewX) (_adjective-like_) distressed

ciu3.text: U+4FCF (F2.5 俏 _Ts'iu_ (ciu2)) 密行也又似也像也 (Page 581 俏 _Ts'iu_ (ciu2); here corrected to _Ts'iu_ (ciu3)) Like, as if, similar (Kangxi: 似也像也); handsome, pretty, beautiful (Kangxi: 好貌); superior; _ts'iu p'i_ (ciu3 pei4 俏皮), pretty; _mai ts'iu_ (maai6 ciu3 賣俏), to show off one's pretty face, as by standing in the door; _shang tak ts'iu_ (saang1 dak1 ciu3 生得俏), bright, as eyes; _ho ts'iu tui ngan_ (hou2 ciu3 deoi3 ngaan5 好俏對眼), sparkling eyes; _ts'iu shang_ (ciu3 saang1 俏生), like life, as puppets or images. (Page 582 肖; here corrected to 俏) _ts'iu p'i_ (ciu3 pei4 俏皮), pretty; Williams' 生得俏, 俏生: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1).

U+5CED (F2.5 峭 _Ts'iu_ (ciu2)) 陖也險也 (Page 581 峭 _Ts'iu_ (ciu2); here corrected to _Ts'iu_ (ciu3)) A steep, abrupt hill (Kangxi: 山峻拔峭絕也); a cliff, which obstructs the way (Kangxi: 嶮也); severe, strict, vehement (Kangxi: 嚴厲也); _ts'iu pik ko_ (ciu3 bik1 gou1 峭壁高), a perpendicular cliff. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 唆 to 陖.

U+8096 (F2.7 肖) 似也像也 (Page 582 肖) To assimilate, to imitate (Kangxi: 肖法也); a likeness or relationship between parent and child (Kangxi: 骨肉相似也), said to be proved by mixing their blood; resembling, like (Kangxi: 似也, 肖類也); small (Kangxi: 小也); ~~a class;~~ alike; _pat ts'iu_ (bat1 ciu3 不肖) not like it, inferior, degenerate; also used by a son to his father; _ts'iu ni ko yeung_ (ciu3 nei5 go3 joeng6-2 肖你個樣) resembles you, as a bust or child; _shap i shang ts'iu_ (sap6 ji6 sang1 ciu3 十二生肖) the twelve animals which are thought to be like the twelve branches. Williams' "a class": This is a misreading of Kangxi 肖類也. 【不肖】 (pjut sjewH) (_adjective-like_) unbecoming 【肖】 (sjewH) (_verb-like_) to resemble, (_met._) to emulate Used in 不肖.

U+8A9A 譙 ziu6 (per 《廣韻》才笑切) (F2.7 誚) 以言相譏詆曰誚 (Page 581 譙) Often used for _ts'iu_ (ciu3) 誚, to scold (Kangxi: 譙誚二字古通). to ridicule (Kangxi 譙: 嬈譊也); to bawl out at (Kangxi 譙: 呵也); to reprehend (Kangxi 譙: 責也); (Page 582 誚) To ridicule; to blame, to reprehend (Kangxi: 讓也, 誚責也); to speak harshly against; _ki ts'iu_ (gei1 ciu3 譏誚) to satirize. 【誚】 (dzjewH) (_verb-like_) to blame

ciu4.text: U+8B59 樵 (F2.5 譙) 地名又殺也 (Page 581 樵) a lookout terrace, for which the next (譙) is used (Kangxi 樵: 與譙通, ...高樓以望敵也); (Page 581 譙) A lookout tower or loft (Kangxi: 樓之別稱也); _~~tsiu~~ ``ts'iu`` ku_ (ciu4 geoi1 譙車), a high turret laid on wheels for archers. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿱誰灬 to 譙. Williams' 譙車: Corrected _tsiu_ (ziu4) to _ts'iu_ (ciu4). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【譙】 (dzjew) (_noun-like_) turret Used in 譙譙. 【譙譙】 (dzjew dzjew) (_adjective-like_) worn out

ciu3.text: U+9798 (F2.5 鞘 _Ts'iu_ (ciu2)) 刀劍殿 (Page 582 鞘 _Ts'iu_ (ciu2); here corrected to _Ts'iu_ (ciu3)) A scabbard, a sheath (Kangxi: 刀室也) (...).

ciu4.text: U+6194 (F2.5 憔) 憔悴？苦貌 (Page 581 憔) Lean (Kangxi: 憔悴瘦也), anxious and starving; _ts'iu sui_ (ciu4 seoi6 憔悴) distressed from a want of food. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𭵼 to 憔. The incomprehensible character looks like ⿱䒑用. 【憔】 (dzjew) Used in 憔悴. 【憔悴】 (dzjew dzwijH) (_adjective-like_) languishing
U+6641 鼂 (F2.5 鼂) 姓 (F2.5 晁) 同上 Fan Wan entry: Fixed incorrectly crossing strokes in headword 鼂. A surname (Kangxi 鼂: 姓, 或作晁).

U+671D 晁 (F2.5 朝) 朝廷朝覲 (Page 25* 朝) A court; the court, the palace; to attend an audience, to go to court, to show fealty (Kangxi: 陪朝也, 臣見君曰朝); to have an audience, to hold a levee (Kangxi: 天子無事與諸侯相見曰朝); an audience; a dynasty; courtly, fashionable; to visit a father or elder; _ch'iu t'ing_ (ciu4 ting4 朝廷), the court; his majesty; _ch'iu kin_ (ciu4 gin3 朝見) to see his majesty; _sheung ch'iu_ (soeng5 ciu4 上朝), to go up to court; _ching tun ch'iu kong_ (zing2 deon6 ciu4 gong1 整頓朝綱) to strengthen the government; _ch'iu fan_ (ciu4 faan1 朝幡), to salute the banner, a religious practice; _shi ch'iu_ (si6 ciu4 侍朝), H. I. M. entering court; _t'in ch'iu_ (tin1 ciu4 天朝), the celestial dynasty; _ch'iu kung_ (ciu4 gung3 朝貢) to hand up tribute; _chun ch'iu_ (zyun2 ciu4 轉朝), to change the dynasty; _yat ch'iu t'in tsz' yat ch'iu shan_ (jat1 ciu4 tin1 zi2 jat1 ciu4 san4 一朝天子一朝臣), each monarch has his own set of ministers; _ch'iu fong_ (ciu4 fong4 朝房), an antechamber of the audience-room; _ch'iu fuk_ (ciu4 fuk6 朝服) court dress; _ch'iu hau_ (ciu4 haau2 朝考), examination for Hanlin (翰林). See 朝 ziu1 for commentary on the reading of 朝 in 朝鮮. 【朝】 (drjew) (_noun-like_) audience, (imperial) court (_verb-like_) to attend audience, to attend court (_noun-like_) dynasty

ziu1.text: U+671D 晁 (F2.2 朝) 旦也早也又朝鮮國名 (Page 23* 朝晁) Dawn, morning (Kangxi: 旦也, 朝早也); early; _chiu man_ (ziu1 maan5 朝晚), morning and evening, early and late; _yat chiu_ (jat1 ziu1 一朝), in a morning, quickly, in a trice; _chiu t'au tso_ (ziu1 tau4 zou2 朝頭早), early in morning; _chiu fan_ (ziu1 faan6 朝飯) breakfast; _chiu shan_ (ziu1 san4 朝晨), morning time; _chiu chiu_ (ziu1 ziu1 朝朝) every morning; _Chiu sin kwok_ (ziu1 sin1 gwok3 朝鮮國) Corea (Kangxi: 朝鮮國名); _yung chiu mi_ (jung6 ziu1 mei6 用朝未) have you breakfasted? _yau chiu mo man_ (jau5 ziu1 mou4 maan5 有朝無晚), there is a morning but no evening, the day is very short; _tai pun chiu loi_ (daai6 bun3 ziu1 loi4 大半朝來), come before breakfast. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 且 to 旦. Williams' 朝鮮國: Opinion is divided on whether 朝 in 朝鮮 should be read _chiu_ (ziu1) or _ch'iu_ (ciu4). _chiu_ (ziu1) is recorded in the dictionaries: 《廣韻》陟遥切 朝 早也...又朝鮮國名... 《康熙字典》朝 古文晁 《唐韻》《廣韻》《集韻》《類篇》《韻會》𠀤陟遙切音昭... 又朝鮮國名... _ch'iu_ (ciu4) is recorded in 《史記索隱》, specifically as a note under 朝鮮列傳第五十五: 案朝音潮直驕反鮮音仙以有汕水故名也汕一音訕 Corrected _sin_ to _sin_. Williams' 朝飯, 朝晨, 用朝未: Dated. 【朝頭早】 (ziu1 tau4 zou2) The morning. 朝頭早沖涼 (ziu1 tau4 zou2 cung1 loeng4): "I shower in the morning." 【朝早】 (ziu1 zou2) Morning. 聽朝早 (ting1 ziu1 zou2): "Tomorrow morning." 琴日朝早 (kam4 jat6 ziu1 zou2): "Yesterday morning". 朝早八點 (ziu1 zou2 baat3 dim2): "Eight o'clock in the morning."

ciu4.text: U+6A35 (F2.5 樵) 采薪曰樵 (Page 581 樵) Wood fit for fuel; scattered wood (Kangxi: 散木也); to cut fuel (Kangxi: 采薪曰樵); _~~tsiu~~ ``ts'iu`` fu_ (ciu4 fu1 樵夫), a woodcutter; _ts'iu lau_ (ciu4 lau4 樵樓), a watchman's tower; _ts'iu king_ (ciu4 ging3 樵徑) a bridle path, a goat path; _ts'oi ts'iu_ (coi2 ciu4 採樵), to cut wood; _Sai Ts'iu_ (sai1 ciu4 西樵), a hilly district southwest of Canton. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𬋈 to 樵. Williams' 樵夫: Corrected _tsiu_ (ziu4) to _ts'iu_ (ciu4). 【樵】 (dzjew) (_noun-like_) firewood (_verb-like_) (_met._) to cut firewood, to burn firewood

U+6F6E (F2.5 潮) 海水旋流每日消長朝曰潮夕曰汐 (Page 25* 潮) The morning tide (Kangxi: 隨月消長早曰潮晚曰汐); moist, damp; _cheung ch'iu_ (zoeng2 ciu4 長朝), tide rising; _shing ch'iu_ (sing4 ciu4 乘潮), to go with the tide; _ch'iu shap_ (ciu4 sap1 潮濕) damp ~~ground~~; _ch'iu t'in_ (ciu4 tin4 潮田), tidal grounds; _fan ch'iu_ (faan1 ciu4 返潮), becoming damp again; _Ch'iu chau fu_ (ciu4 zau1 fu2 潮州府), a prefecture in the east of Kwangtung (廣東); _ch'iu hi_ (ciu4 hei3 潮氣) dull, stupid, owlish. Fan Wan entry: Normalised ⿱一毋 to 每, and corrected 汝 to 汐. Williams' 潮濕: Not necessarily of the ground. 【潮】 (drjew) (_noun-like_) (morning) tide
U+77A7 (Page 581 瞧) To look at, to see; to look at furtively (Kangxi: 偸視貌), to glance at; _ts'iu yat ts'iu_ (ciu4 jat1 ciu4 瞧一瞧), I had a look at it. Chiefly used in the court dialect.

co1.text: U+509E so1 (alternative per 《廣韻》素何切) (F2.58 傞) 舞不止也 (Page 583 傞) The drunken frolics of a man in liquor (Kangxi: 醉舞貌); _ts'o ts'o_ (co1 co1 傞傞), drunken antics. Williams' "frolics": Specifically, dancing. 【傞】 (tsha, sa) Used in 傞傞. 【傞傞】 (tsha tsha, sa sa) (_adverb-like_) (dancing) drunkenly, on and on

U+521D (F2.58 初) 始也先也 (Page 26* 初) To cut out garments (Kangxi: 裁衣之始也); to begin, to commence (Kangxi: 始也); the first, the beginning; incipient, at first; _a ch'o ko_ (aa3 co1 go1 阿初哥), a beginner, raw; _ch'o kan_ (co1 gaan1 初間), during the first decade; _ch'o ch'o_ (co1 co1 初初), at first; _ch'o t'au fat shai_ (co1 tau4 faat3 sai3 初頭發勢) a great show at starting; _ch'o loi po to_ (co1 loi4 bou6 dou3 初來步到) unacquainted with things; _ch'o tso_ (co1 zou6 初做) to begin; _ch'o shang_ (co1 sang1 初生), a ~~first-born~~ ``newborn``; _ch'o ts'un_ (co1 ceon4 初旬), the first decade; _ch'o hoi fau_ (co1 hoi1 fau6 初開埠) to found a mart; _ch'o ki_ (co1 gei2 初幾), which day of the first decade is it? Williams' "To cut out garments": Specifically, the beginning of producing clothes. Williams' 初間, 初旬, 初幾: Here, "decade" means ten days. Williams' 初來步到: Corrected _ch'o_ (co4) to _ch'o_ (co1). 【初】 (tsrhjo) (_noun-like_) beginning (_adjective-like_) initial 【初初】 (co1 co1) At first, initially. 【初哥】 (co1 go1) A beginner, a novice.
U+5528 (Page 26* 唨) A colloquial, final particle. To stop, to desist; _ni mai hu tsz' ch'o_ (nei5 mai5 heoi3 zi6 co1 你咪去？唨), don't you go! _ni tang ha ch'o_ (nei5 dang2 haa5 co1 你等吓唨), stop a little!

U+5D6F co4 (per 《廣韻》昨何切) (F2.58 嵯) 嵯峨 (Page 583 嵯) ~~Uneven hills;~~ ~~_ts'o ngo_ (co1 ngo4 嵯峨), the inequalities of hills, the undulating outline of mountains.~~ The reading _ts'o_ (co1) is not unattested; Kangxi has 《集韻》《韻會》《正韻》𠀤倉何切音磋《說文》嵯峨山石貌. Williams' "Uneven hills" etc.: These explanations only apply to the reading 楚宜切 _ch'i_ (ci1) for use in the phrase 㠁嵯, which is the mountain version of 參差. Used in 嵯峨 (lofty, mountainous).

U+7473 co2 (alternative per 《廣韻》千可切) (F2.58 瑳) 玉鮮白色 (Page 583 瑳) A white, lustrous, stone (Kangxi: 玉色鮮白也); fresh, beautiful (Kangxi: 凡物鮮盛亦曰瑳, 鮮盛貌); to smile, to look fascinating (Kangxi: 笑貌, 瑳巧笑貌). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 解 to 鮮 (notwithstanding the olden dictionaries read 玉色鮮白). Kangxi prescribes the rising-tone reading for the "brilliant" (鮮盛貌) and "smiling" (笑貌) senses. 【瑳】 (tsha, tshaX) (_adjective-like_) brilliant-white

U+78CB 瑳 (F2.58 磋) 切磋所以治骨角 (Page 583 瑳) Used for the next (磋) (Kangxi 瑳: 與磋同). (Page 583 磋) To polish, to work on (Kangxi: 磨治也, 治象曰磋); to cut and polish; to work at, to make good, as composition; _ts'it ts'o_ (cit3 co1 切磋), to cut and polish. 【磋】 (tsha) (_verb-like_) to polish

U+82BB (F2.58 芻) 草也 (Page 26* 芻) To cut grass (Kangxi: 刈草也); hay, fodder, grass for animals (Kangxi: 芻者飼牛馬之草, 草食曰芻); _yat ch'uk ch'o_ (jat1 cuk1 co1 一束芻), a bundle of hay; _ch'o iu_ (co1 jiu4 芻蕘), grass; _met._ the people. 【芻】 (tsrhju) (_noun-like_) hay, fodder
U+8E49 (F2.58 蹉) 蹉跎不及也 (Page 583 蹉) To slip (Kangxi: 蹉跌也); to err (Kangxi: 蹉過也); _ts'o t'o_ (co1 to4 蹉跎), to miss the time, to lose the opportunity (Kangxi: 蹉跎失時也).

U+96DB co4 (per 《廣韻》仕于切) (F2.60 雛 _Ch'o_ (co4)) 鳥子曰雛 (Page 26* 雛 _Ch'o_ (co4); here normalised to _Ch'o_ (co1)) A chick, a fledgeling (Kangxi: 雞子也); the callow young of birds; _ch'o kai_ (co1 gai1 雛雞), a chicken. 【雛】 (dzrju) (_noun-like_) chick

co4.text: U+96DB (F2.60 雛) 鳥子曰雛 (Page 26* 雛)

co2.text: U+61B7 (F2.60 憷) 辛苦痛也 (Page 26* 憷)

U+695A 憷 (for "pain/suffering" senses) (F2.60 楚) 國名大也高起也又夏楚荊條也 (Page 26* 楚) A coppice, a bramble-bush, a thicket (Kangxi: 叢木也); a clump of trees; spinous (Kangxi: 楚楚茨棘貌); distinct, clear, orderly (Kangxi: 楚楚鮮明貌); well done, properly finished; painful (Kangxi: 辛楚痛也), sharp; a feudal state (Kangxi: 國名) in the Chau (周) dynasty now the chief part of Hunan (湖南) and Hupeh (湖北); _ts'ing ch'o_ (cing1 co2 清楚), clear, fresh, completed, well done; _ch'o ch'o_ (co2 co2 楚楚), very good, carefully; _king ch'o_ (ging1 co2 荊楚), a severe castigation; _fu ch'o_ (fu2 co2 苦楚), distressing, suffering. (Page 26* 憷) Interchanged with the last (楚), in the senses of painful, suffering (Kangxi 憷: 通作楚). A rod or cane used to beat offenders (Kangxi: 楚荆也扑撻犯禮者). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【楚】 (tsrhjoX) (_noun-like_) thorn-tree, thorn, (_met._) ferule (_noun-like_) pain, suffering (_adjective-like_) thickset (_adjective-like_) neat, splendid Used in 萇楚.
U+790E (Page 26* ~~？~~``礎``) The stone base of a pillar or column (Kangxi: 柱下石, 礩也); a pedestal; a plinth.

U+811E co3 (per Fan Wan) (F2.61 脞 _Ts'o_ (co3)) 叢脞又細碎無大畧也 (Page 583 脞 _Ts'o_ (co3); here normalised to _Ts'o_ (co2)) To mince up meat (Kangxi: 一曰切肉爲脞); broken to bits, minced; small (Kangxi: 小也); to attend to trifles (Kangxi: 叢脞細碎無大略). 【脞】 (tshwaX) (_noun-like_) mince, (_met._) trifle

co3.text: U+811E (F2.61 脞) 叢脞又細碎無大畧也 (Page 583 脞)
U+5249 (F2.61 剉) 折也 (F2.61 銼) 與剉同 (Page 583 剉) To cut, to lop off the corners and sharp points, to trim and prune (Kangxi: 折傷也, 斫也, 斬截也); to cut up, to cut to pieces.

U+632B zo3 (per 《廣韻》則臥切) (F2.61 挫) 摧折也打也 (Page 583 挫) To break, to wound (Kangxi: 摧也, 挫折也); to take off the edge, to humble; to oppress, to treat harshly; ~~to trim, to file off;~~ _ts'o k'i yui hi_ (co3 kei4 jeoi6 hei3 挫其銳氣) to bring down his valor; _ts'o chit_ (co3 zit3 挫折) to improve, to polish rude ways; tried by afflictions. 【挫】 (tswaH) (_verb-like_) to break, to defeat, (_met._) to dampen (_verb-like_) to push

U+92BC 剉 co4 (per 《廣韻》昨禾切 for "boiler" sense) zuk6 (per 《廣韻》昨木切 for "boiler" sense) (F2.61 剉) 折也 (F2.61 銼) 與剉同 (Page 583 剉) Often used for the next (銼). (Page 583 銼) An iron pan or boiler (Kangxi: 鍑也); a file, a rasp; to rasp, to file, to smooth off the sharp or rough points; to rub down; _yat pa ts'o_ (jat1 baa2 co3 一把銼) one file; _ts'o wat_ (co3 waat6 銼滑) file it smooth; _kai ch'un ts'o_ (gai1 ceon1 co3 雞膥銼) to file eggs, or _chi ma ts'o_ (zi1 maa4 co3 芝麻銼) to scrape sesamum seeds, both mean a skinflint; _ts'o kok_ (co3 gok3 銼角) to file off corners. Williams' 雞膥銼, 芝麻銼: More accurately rendered as nouns, i.e. "chicken-egg file" and "sesame file".

U+932F (Page 583 錯) In disorder, confused; to mistake, to err, to do wrong; to slip; erroneous, mixed, wrong; _mo ts'o_ (mou5 co3 冇錯) no mistake, right; _ts'o ngam_ (co3 ngaam1 錯啱), a lucky hit, an error which is fortunate; _'m ying ts'o_ (m4 jing6 co3 唔認錯) will not confess the wrong; _ts'o keuk nan fan_ (co3 goek3 naan4 faan1 錯腳難翻) a slip may be hard to retrieve; _ts'o kwo pit koi_ (co3 gwo3 bit1 goi2 錯過必改), error must be corrected; _se ts'o_ (se2 co3 寫錯) written wrong. The classical reading of the "erroneous" and "mixed" senses is entering-tone _ts'ok_ (cok3). Williams' 錯啱: Corrected _ngam_ (ngaam5) to _ngam_ (ngaam1). 【唔錯】 (m4 co3) Not bad (in quality). 唔錯吖 (m4 co3 a1): "Not bad!" 【冇錯】 (mou5 co3) Correct. 冇錯喇 (mou5 co3 la3): "That's correct."

cok3.text: U+932F 剒 (for "cut/work" sense) 厝 (for "whetstone" sense) (F1.102 錯) 舛也乖也誤也亂也 (Page 588 錯 _Ts'o_ (cou3); here corrected to _Ts'ok_ (cok3)) To cut or work stones (Kangxi: 治玉石曰錯); (Page 592 錯) To ~~wash~~ ``inlay`` or plate with gold (Kangxi: 金涂也); the stripes, striae, streaks, &c., in stones or fabrics (Kangxi: 錯衡文衡也); to file or polish (Kangxi: 鑢也, 治玉石曰錯); to tattoo (Kangxi: 錯臂亦文身謂以丹靑錯畫其臂); a stone for grinding gems or glass (Kangxi: 厲石也, 錯石也可以琢玉); mixed, crossed, barred, confused (Kangxi: 雜也, 亂也); _ts'ok pi_ (cok3 bei3 錯臂) to tattoo the arm. 【錯】 (tshak) (_noun-like_) whetstone (_verb-like_) to inlay, to tattoo (_verb-like_) to cross (diagonally) (_adjective-like_) mixed, erroneous, confused (_adjective-like_) diagonal (_adjective-like_) wary

cou3.text: U+932F (F2.16 錯 _Ts'u_) 捨置也又倉入聲礪石也 (Page 588 錯) Interchanged with the two last (厝, 措). to place (Kangxi: 錯置也); to hide, to store away (Kangxi: 藏也).

co4.text: U+924F (F2.60 鋤) 冲所切田器也 (F2.60 鉏) 同上 (Page 26* 鉏鋤) Fan Wan entry: The supplied dissection 冲所切 is incorrect.

U+92E4 鉏 (F2.60 鋤) 冲所切田器也 (F2.60 鉏) 同上 (Page 26* 鉏鋤) A hoe, a mattoc (Kangxi 鉏: 立薅所用也, 助也去穢助苗也); to hoe, to cultivate (Kangxi 鉏: 治田也); _ch'o t'in_ (co4 tin4 鋤田), to dig; _ch'o t'au_ (co4 tau4-2 鋤頭), a hoe or mattoc. Fan Wan entry: The supplied dissection 冲所切 is incorrect. 【鋤】 (dzrjo) (_noun-like_) hoe 【鋤大弟】 (co4 daai6 di2) Big two (the card game). To play big two. 【鋤弟】 (co4 di2) Short for 鋤大弟.

zo6.text: U+5750 co5 (verb-like) (F2.62 坐) 止也居也 (Page 582 坐) To sit (Kangxi: 行之對也); to sit on the heels; sitting, remaining; a session; to maintain, to hold (Kangxi: 猶守也); involved in, implicated; ~~to sit in judgement on,~~ to judge; to place, to set down; to kneel (Kangxi: 古者謂跪爲坐); _ts'ing ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (cing2 co5 請坐) please be seated; _tso iu_ (zo6 jiu1 坐腰), to curtsey; _ni ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (nei5 co5 你坐) or _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` che_ (co5 ze3 坐嗻) good bye, said to the host; _tso li_ (zo6 lei5 坐𢃇), to let down sail; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` kiu_ (co5 giu6-2 坐轎) to ride in a sedan; _tso tsui u ni_ (zo6 zeoi6 jyu1 nei5 坐罪於你), to bring the punishment upon you; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` ut_ (co5 jyut6-2 坐月) the month after confinement; _tso wai_ (zo6 wai6-2 坐位) a seat, a station; _tso shat_ (zo6 sat6 坐實) put it in securely; _tso sok_ (zo6 sok3 坐索) to quarter on one to force him to pay; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` 'm lok_ (co5 m4 lok6 坐唔落) no place to sit in; _tso heung_ (zo6 hoeng3 坐向) the prospect, as of a grave; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` ha t'im_ (co5 haa5 tim1 坐吓添), sit a while longer; _t'it tso_ (tit3 zo6 鐵坐) to squat, as a tailor; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` kam_ (co5 gaam1 坐監), in prison; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` sheung_ (co5 soeng5 坐上) sit up higher; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` t'ong_ (co5 tong4-2 坐堂), in court, on the bench; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` lang fan kom ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (co5 laang5 faan6 gam2 co5 坐冷飯噉坐) to sit till the rice is cold, _i. e._ to take ~~Freuch~~ ``French`` leave; _ta tso ts'am ~~shin~~ ``shim``_ (daa2 zo6 caam1 sim4 打坐參禪), to ~~squat~~ ``sit in the lotus position`` and contemplate, as Budhists do. The rising-tone reading _ts'o_ (co5) is typically only heard in vernacular usage for verb-like occurrences, leading to the perception that it is non-literary. In fact, the distinction in Cantonese between aspirated rising _ts'o_ (co5) and unaspirated departing _tso_ (zo6) actually preserves the two readings of 坐 in Middle Chinese (also true of 斷, 淡, 近, 重, etc.). Kangxi has an extended note, 按坐有上去二音 字韻諸書訓註皆同 惟轉注古音坐註 引史記高帝紀遂坐上坐 正義云前坐字在果反後坐字在臥反 the short version of which is, that the quoted line 遂坐上坐 "Then he sat upon the upper seat" should be read _sui ts'o sheung tso_ (seoi6 co5 soeng6 zo6), with the first 坐 ("sat") rising and the second 坐 ("seat") departing. Williams' _tso_ (zo6 去聲) has been corrected to _ts'o_ (co5 上聲) when appropriate. Williams' 坐月: Here, "confinement" means childbirth. Confusingly, in more modern English, the act of staying home for a month afterward is also called confinement. Williams' 坐上: Corrected _sheung_ (soeng6 去聲) to _sheung_ (soeng5 上聲). Williams' 坐冷飯噉坐: Corrected _kom_ (gam3) to _kom_ (gam2). Williams' 打坐參禪: Changed _shin_ (sin4) to common reading _shim_ (sim4). 【坐-verb】 (dzwaX) (_verb-like_) to sit 【坐-noun】 (dzwaH) (_noun-like_) seat 【坐】 (co5) (_Chinese chess_) Used to describe moves of the general. 坐起 (co5 hei2): "Sit raised" (i.e. move the general up). 坐上 (co5 soeng5): "Sit up" (i.e. move the general up). 坐上山頂 (co5 soeng5 saan1 deng2): "Sit up to the mountain top" (i.e. move the general up to the top row of the palace). 坐落 (co5 lok6): "Sit down" (i.e. move the general down). 坐出 (co5 ceot1): "Sit out" (i.e. move the general out of cover of the centre column). 坐入 (co5 jap6): "Sit in" (i.e. move the general back into cover of the centre column). 坐歪 (co5 me2): "Sit off-centre" (i.e. move the general off the uncovered centre column). 坐正 (co5 zeng3): "Sit right" (i.e. move the general back onto the uncovered centre column). 坐咗佢 (co5 zo2 keoi5): "Sit over it" (i.e. capture with the general). 【坐低】 (co5 dai1) To sit down. 【坐穩機】 (co5 wan2 gei1) A farewell wish for a safe flight.

coek3.text: U+502C zoek3 (per initial of 《廣韻》竹角切) (F1.94 倬) 著也大也詩倬彼雲漢 (Page 33|n76 倬) Extensive~~, distant~~; large (Kangxi: 大也); to show, to manifest (Kangxi: 著也, 明貌); _ming ch'euk u tsui_ (ming4 coek3 jyu5 zeoi6 明倬汝罪) to make your crime known. 【倬】 (traewk) (_adjective-like_) grand, illustrious

U+5353 zoek3 (per initial of 《廣韻》竹角切) (F1.94 卓) 特立也高也遠也望也 (Page 33|n76 卓) Firm, stable; lofty (Kangxi: 高也) and profound; distant; to establish; to expect; to surpass (Kangxi: 超卓也); eminent in, raised above others; ~~at, reached, as a time;~~ _ch'iu ch'euk_ (ciu1 coek3 超卓) preeminent, superior to common; _ch'euk ut yan kw'an_ (coek3 jyut6 jan4 kwan4 卓越人群), excelling other men; _ch'euk lap_ (coek3 lap6 卓立) to erect, upright; _ch'euk i_ (coek3 ji6 卓異) meritorious, said of officers at the quinqennial trial. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【卓】 (traewk) (_adjective-like_) lofty (_adverb-like_) aloft
U+6233 cok3 (alternative for "stamp" sense) (Page 33|n76 戳) To stab or spear (Kangxi: 槍戳也); a seal used by constables; _k'oi ch'euk_ (koi3 coek3 蓋戳) to stamp a document.
U+684C 棹 zoek3 (per initial of 《廣韻》竹角切) (Page 33|n76 棹; here normalised to 桌) A table (Kangxi 桌: 俗呼几案曰桌); a stand; _ch'euk tsz'_ (coek3 zi2 桌子) a table; _i ch'euk_ (ji2 coek3 椅桌); tables and chairs.
U+68F9 zoek6 (per initial of Kangxi 直角切) (Page 33|n76 棹) a kind of tree (Kangxi: 木名);

zaau6.text: U+68F9 掉 櫂 (F2.42 棹 _Chau_ (zaau3)) 進船之器 (F2.43 櫂 _Chau_ (zaau3)) 同上 (Page 29|n72 櫂棹掉 _Chau_ (zaau3); here corrected to _Chau_ (zaau6)) To row (Kangxi 櫂: 言使舟櫂進也); to shoot an arrow; to throw away; a long oar (Kangxi 櫂: 所以進船也, 短曰檝長曰櫂); _chau keuk_ (zaau6 goek3 棹腳) to limp, as the lame do; _chau chung_ (zaau6 zung3 棹中) to hit the bull's-eye; _chau tseung_ (zaau6 zoeng2 棹槳), to pull an oar; _chau mai t'au_ (zaau6 maai4 tau4 棹埋頭), to pull ashore; _chau kwo hoi_ (zaau6 gwo3 hoi2 棹過海), to pull across the river; _ch'ut lik chau_ (ceot1 lik6 zaau6 出力棹) row harder; _chau ch'ut kai_ (zaau6 ceot1 gaai1 棹出街), throw it out in the street. The ~~two~~ last of these characters (~~棹,~~ 掉) ~~are~~ ``is`` improperly used in Canton for the first (櫂).

coek3.text: U+707C 焯 (for "manifest/clear" sense and vernacular "blanch" sense) zoek3 (per 《廣韻》之若切) (F1.94 灼 _Cheuk_ (zoek3)) 燒也炙也昭也又灼灼華盛貌 (Page 32|n75 灼 _Cheuk_ (zoek3); here normalised to _Ch'euk_ (coek3)) To burn (Kangxi: 炙也, 熱也); to singe, to cauterize ~~with moxa~~ (Kangxi: 熱也); alarmed (Kangxi: 灼驚也); clear, distinct (Kangxi: 明也); luxuriant, as flowers (Kangxi: 灼灼花盛貌); _~~cheuk~~ ``ch'euk`` kwai_ (coek3 gwai1 灼龜), to singe a terrapin's shell; _~~cheuk~~ ``ch'euk`` lan_ (coek3 laan6 灼爛) to raise a blister; _~~cheuk~~ ``ch'euk`` ngai_ (coek3 ngaai6 灼艾) to burn the moxa; _~~cheuk cheuk~~ ``ch'euk ch'euk``_ (coek3 coek3 灼灼) splendid, as flowers. 【灼】 (tsyak) (_verb-like_) to burn, to scorch (_adjective-like_) manifest, (_met._) bright 【灼】 (coek3) To cook (briefly) in boiling water, to blanch. 白灼蝦 (baak6 coek3 haa1): "Plain boiled prawns." 灼一灼 (coek3 jat1 coek3): "Give it a blanch."

zoek3.text: U+707C (F1.94 灼) 燒也炙也昭也又灼灼華盛貌 (Page 32|n75 灼)

coek3.text: U+712F zoek3 (per 《廣韻》之若切) Clear, bright.

U+7DBD (F1.94 綽) 寬也緩也 (Page 33|n76 綽) Large, ample (Kangxi: 緩也, 綽綽寬也, 綽猶多也); ~~slow, leisurely;~~ _ch'euk yeuk_ (coek3 joek3 綽約) delicate, as a girl; _fun ch'euk_ (fun1 coek3 寬綽) broad, liberal; _ch'euk ch'euk yau u_ (coek3 coek3 jau5 jyu4 綽綽有餘), an overplus, more than enough. Williams' "slow, leisurely": This is a misreading of Kangxi 緩也. 【綽】 (tsyhak) (_adjective-like_) generous, ample (_adjective-like_) gentle

U+828D zoek3 (per 《廣韻》張略切) (F1.94 芍 _Cheuk_ (zoek3)) 芍藥花名 (Page 32|n75 芍 _Cheuk_ (zoek3); here normalised to _Ch'euk_ (coek3)) The peony (Kangxi: 芍藥猶綽約也); also the dahlia; _~~cheuk~~ ``ch'euk`` yeuk_ (coek3 joek6 芍藥) peony roots, a medicine.

zoek3.text: U+828D (F1.94 芍) 芍藥花名 (Page 32|n75 芍)

coek3.text: U+8E14 (Page 29|n72 踔) Read _ch'euk_ (coek3); to leap far and high (Kangxi: 踔高遠也); to walk lamely (Kangxi: 踸踔跛者行也).

zaau3.text: U+8E14 caau3 (per 《廣韻》丑敎切) (Page 29|n72 踔) To tread on (Kangxi: 踶也); to stretch ahead in running (Kangxi: 踔走也, 踔踰也, 踔猶越也); to jump over.

coek3.text: U+9D72 (F1.94 鵲) 喜鵲 (Page 565 鵲)

zoek3.text: U+9D72 coek3 (per 《廣韻》七雀切) zoek3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.94 鵲 _Ts'euk_ (coek3)) 喜鵲 (Page 565 鵲 _Ts'euk_ (coek3); here normalised to _Tseuk_ (zoek3)) A magpie, a jackdaw (Kangxi: 鳥名喜鵲也); a jay (Kangxi: 練鵲); _hi ~~ts'euk~~ ``tseuk``_ (hei2 zoek3 喜鵲) or _kon ~~ts'euk~~ ``tseuk``_ (gon1 zoek3 乾鵲) a magpie; _shan hi ~~ts'euk~~ ``tseuk``_ (saan1 hei2 zoek3 山喜鵲) a magpie with a gradated tail; _u ~~ts'euk~~ ``tseuk``_ (wu1 zoek3 烏鵲) a raven.

coeng1.text: U+5021 (F1.90 倡) 倡導倡率先之也 (Page 36|n79 倡)

coeng3.text: U+5021 coeng1 (per Fan Wan, common) (F1.90 倡 _Ch'eung_ (coeng1)) 倡導倡率先之也 (Page 36|n79 倡 _Ch'eung_ (coeng1)) To lead, to go before (Kangxi: 倡發歌句也), to seduce or induce; a leader; an example, a guide; _ch'eung to_ (coeng1 dou6 倡導) to show the way; _ch'eung in_ (coeng1 jin4 倡言), to speak first; _ch'eung sut_ (coeng1 seot1 倡率) to lead on; _ch'eung t'au_ (coeng1 tau4 倡頭), a leader; _ch'eung lun_ (coeng1 lyun6 倡亂) to head a riot. 【倡】 (tsyhangH) (_verb-like_) to lead song

coeng1.text: U+5A3C 倡 (F1.90 娼) 娼妓又娼優女樂 (Page 36|n79 倡) used for the (...) next (娼) (Kangxi 倡: 別作娼); (Page 36|n79 娼) A singing woman (Kangxi 倡: 倡優女樂...別作娼); a courtesan; _ch'eung mun_ (coeng1 mun4 娼門), public women; _wo ch'eung_ (wo1 coeng1 窩娼), house of assignation; _ch'eung fu_ (coeng1 fu5 娼婦), a strumpet; _ch'eung ki_ (coeng1 gei6 娼妓) a whore; _ch'eung liu_ (coeng1 liu4 娼寮), a brothel; _ch'eung ka_ (coeng1 gaa1 娼家), a bagnio; _yau ch'eung_ (jau1 coeng1 優娼), public singers; _tong ch'eung_ (dong1 coeng1 當娼), to be a whore.
U+5C07 To request, to ask (Kangxi: 請也幾願辭也). (_onomatopoeia_) The sound of tinkling (Kangxi: 聲也). Stern and proper (Kangxi: 嚴正貌). Collected (Kangxi: 集也). 【將】 (tshjang) (_verb-like_) to entreat

zoeng1.text: U+5C07 (F1.89 將) 事之未必就時也又奉也送也又干將劍名 (Page 565 將) To take, to hold in the hand (Kangxi: 扶持也); sign of the future, shall, will, about to go (Kangxi: 將甫始之辭); sometimes a form of the accusative; then, soon, presently; to help, to accommodate (Kangxi: 助也); to use, to avail of, to go with, to escort, to accompany (Kangxi: 送也); great (Kangxi: 大也)~~, stout~~; long (Kangxi: 長也)~~, large~~; to receive and act upon, endued with (Kangxi: 承也奉也行也); to act``, to perform, to implement`` (Kangxi: 將行也); to progress, ~~to increase~~, to approach, to advance (Kangxi: 進也) ~~towards a mark~~; to follow (Kangxi: 從也隨也); going, passing, as time (Kangxi: 去也 etc.); the side (Kangxi: 側也); _tseung loi_ (zoeng1 loi4 將來) presently; _tseung sz'_ (zoeng1 si2 將死) dying; _tseung kan an chau_ (zoeng1 gan6 aan3 zau3 將近晏晝) it is nearly noon; _tseung ho ch'ut_ (zoeng1 ho2 ceot1 將可出) then he can go; _tseung kwan_ (zoeng1 gwan1 將軍), a commander-in-chief, all of whom are Manchus; _met._ the best, the principal; _tseung u chi ho_ (zoeng1 jyu4 zi1 ho4 將如之何), how shall we then act? _tseung loi ch'ung kung_ (zoeng1 loi4 cung1 gung1 將來充公), to confiscate effects; _tseung kam pok po_ (zoeng1 gam1 bok3 bou2 將金博寶), to value a rarity with gold; _tseung pun k'au li_ (zoeng1 bun2 kau4 lei6 將本求利) to make money on a capital; _shu tseung_ (syu1 zoeng1 輸將), to aid the government; _tseung iu in ch'un po_ (zoeng1 jiu4 jin4 cyun4 bou3 將謠言傳布) to avail of strange words to make it known. Williams' 將軍: In the non-Manchu context, a general; in Chinese chess, to check. Or (Kangxi: 抑然之辭). And, also (Kangxi: 且也). To provide for, to support (Kangxi: 養也). Strong, vigorous (Kangxi: 壯也). To recuperate. (_Chinese chess_) Short for 將軍 (to check).

zoeng3.text: U+5C07 (F1.93 將) 將帥統兵之官也 (Page 566 將) To take charge of troops, to lead troops into the field, to act as a general (Kangxi: 將之也); a general, a leader (Kangxi: 將帥也); _lo tseung_ (lou5 zoeng3 老將) a``n old`` soldier``, a veteran``. Williams' 老將: Corrected _lo_ (lou2) to _lo_ (lou5).

coeng1.text: U+220D1 (F1.90 ⿰巾昌) 披衣不帶曰𢃑 (Page 36|n79 ~~帽~~``𢃑``)

U+65A8 (F1.89 斨) 方銎斧也 (Page 567 斨) A sort of broad-ax (Kangxi: 斨卽斧也); a ~~pole-ax made square, a bill~~ ``square-eyed axe`` (Kangxi: 方銎斧也); to hack, to chop (Kangxi: 斨戕也); _Shu ts'eung_ (syu4 coeng1 殳斨), a statesman in the days of Shun (舜). Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿺方丶 to 方, ⿱⿰工几金 to 銎, and 釜 to 斧. 【斨】 (tshjang) (_noun-like_) square-eyed-axe

U+660C (F1.90 昌) 盛也顯也善也 (Page 35|n78 昌) Light of the sun (Kangxi: 一曰日光也); fine words (Kangxi: 美言也); elegant, flourishing, prosperous (Kangxi: 盛也, 昌盛壯貌); increasing in wealth or peace; powerful; effulgent; good (Kangxi: 當也, 昌狡好貌); rich, affluent; to increase; ~~to illuminate~~ a thing (Kangxi: 物也); _ch'eung shing_ (coeng1 sing6 昌盛) prosperous, well-off; _kat ch'eung_ (gat1 coeng1 吉昌), happy, successful; _shun t'in che ch'eung_ (seon6 tin1 ze2 coeng1 順天者昌), he will prosper who obeys heaven; _kwong ch'eung_ (gwong1 coeng1 光昌), splendid, gay. A surname. 【昌】 (tsyhang) (_adjective-like_) splendid (_adjective-like_) flourishing, abundant
U+7255 May appear as either ⿰片⿱囪心 or ⿰片⿱囱心 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.90 窓) 高曰窓低曰牗 (F1.90 牕) 同上 (Page 36|n79 窓窗牕)

U+7316 倡 (F1.90 猖) 猖狂縱橫也 (Page 36|n79 猖) ~~A herd of animals fleeing;~~ _ch'eung kw'ong_ (coeng1 kwong4 猖狂), ungovernable, unrestrained (Kangxi: 猖狂). (Page 36|n79 倡) used for the last (猖) (Kangxi 倡: 亦作猖) (...); Williams' "A herd of animals fleeing": This is a misreading of Kangxi 狂駭也.

U+7472 (Page 593 瑲 _Ts'ong_ (cong1); here corrected to _Ts'eung_ (coeng1)) a musical sound (Kangxi: 玉聲也, 一曰樂聲); _~~ts'ong ts'ong~~ ``ts'eung ts'eung``_ (coeng1 coeng1 瑲瑲), the jingling of stones. 【瑲】 (tshjang) (_onomatopoeia_) ting

cong1.text: U+7472 (F1.95 瑲) 玉名 (Page 593 瑲) Color of gems (Kangxi: 玉色);

coeng1.text: U+7A93 (F1.90 窓) 高曰窓低曰牗 (F1.90 牕) 同上 (Page 36|n79 窓窗牕)

U+7A97 May appear as either ⿱穴囪 (assumed here) or ⿱穴囱, depending on the font. 牕 窓 窻 (F1.90 窓) 高曰窓低曰牗 (F1.90 牕) 同上 (Page 36|n79 窓窗牕) A window, an aperture to admit light (Kangxi: 助戸爲明); a sash; a venetian blind, a shutter; a school; a student; _ch'eung mun_ (coeng1 mun4-2 窗門), a window; _t'in ch'eung_ (tin1 coeng1 天窗), a sky-light; _ch'eung mun ~~ch'an~~ ``ch'am``_ (coeng1 mun4-2 caam5 窗門棖), a window-sill; _shu ch'eung_ (syu1 coeng1 書窗), or _kai ch'eung_ (gai1 coeng1 雞窗), a schoolroom; _t'ong ch'eung_ (tong3 coeng1 摥窗), to pull the sash to; _hon ch'eung_ (hon4 coeng1 寒窗), a poor student; _ch'eung at_ (coeng1 aat3 窗壓) a window-bar; _t'ung ch'eung_ (tung4 coeng1 同窗), or _ch'eung yau_ (coeng1 jau5 窗友), chums, fellow-students; _ch'eung ha_ (coeng1 haa6 窗下) at school, while at his studies. Williams' "sash": In the sense of the sliding panel in a sash window. Williams' 窗門棖: Changed uncommon reading _ch'an_ (caan5) to _ch'am_ (caam5). 【窗】 (tsrhaewng) (_noun-like_) window
U+7ABB May appear as either ⿱穴⿱囪心 or ⿱穴⿱囱心 (assumed here), depending on the font.
U+83D6 (F1.90 菖) 菖蒲草名 (Page 36|n79 菖) The flag _(Acorus)_ (Kangxi: 菖蒲蒲類之昌盛者); _ch'eung p'o_ (coeng1 pou4 菖蒲), sweet flag, hung on door lintels to drive away maleficent influences.
U+88EE 𢃑 (F1.90 ⿰巾昌) 披衣不帶曰𢃑 (Page 36|n79 ~~帽~~``𢃑``; here normalised to 裮) Loose flowing garments thrown on one without a cincture (Kangxi 裮: 披裮, 披衣不帶也).

U+8E4C 蹡 (F1.89 蹌) 動也 (Page 567 蹌) To walk rapidly (Kangxi: 恐動趨步, 蹌巧趨貌); to move ~~towards a superior quickly~~ ``dignifiedly`` (Kangxi: 士大夫之威儀也); to gambol, to skip about (Kangxi: 舞貌). (Page 567 蹡) Similar to the preceding (蹌); 【蹌】 (tshjang) (_verb-like_) to move, to fleet, (_met._) dignified
U+8E61 (F1.89 蹡) 行貌 (Page 567 蹡)

coeng3.text: U+8E61 蹌 (Page 567 蹡 _Ts'eung_ (coeng1); here corrected to _Ts'eung_ (coeng3)) to move irregularly, to go across, to walk awry (Kangxi: 同蹌行不正貌).

coeng1.text: U+93D8 鎗 (F1.89 鏘) 鏗鏘鐘鼓之聲 (Page 567 鏘) The sound of stones, bells and drums (Kangxi: 玉聲也一曰樂聲); a noise (Kangxi: 鏘聲也); a tinkling; _hang ts'eung_ (hang1 coeng1 鏗鏘), the ring of metallic things. (Page 567 鎗) Interchanged with the last (鏘); 【鏘】 (tshjang) (_onomatopoeia_) ting
U+95B6 (F1.90 閶) 閶闔天門也 (Page 36|n79 閶) The door of heaven (Kangxi: 天門也); a gate of Suchau (蘇州); _ch'eung hop_ (coeng1 hap6 閶闔) door of paradise, kept by Kwanti (關帝) or Mars.

U+9BE7 䱽 Effectively, 鯧 and 䱽 are the same semantic word "pomfret", but: 鯧 is classical with reading _ch'eung_ (coeng1); and 䱽 is vernacular with reading _ts'ong_ (cong1) (see 䱽 cong1). The pomfret (Kangxi: 鯧鯸魚名, 閩人譌爲䱽魚).

U+9DAC (F1.95 鶬) 鞗革聲之和也又音倉鶬鶊鳥名 Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱𥫗革 to 鞗 and corrected ⿰肛鳥 to 鶊. (_onomatopoeia_) The sound of harmonising tinkling (Kangxi: 聲和也). Adorned with metal (Kangxi: 金飾貌). 【鶬-onomatopoeia】 (tshjang) (_onomatopoeia_) ting
