cong1.text: U+9DAC 倉 (F1.95 鶬) 鶬鶊鳥名 (Page 594 鶬) A kind of crane (Kangxi: 今呼鶬鴰, 鶬大如鶴), plumage ashcolored (Kangxi: 靑蒼色亦有灰色者), and cheeks red (Kangxi: 兩頰紅); _ts'ong ~~kut~~ ``k'ut``_ (cong1 kut3 鶬鴰) the black crane, called _t'in tam kai_ (tin4 dam1 gai1 田揼雞), or field tank hen. Williams' 鶬鴰: Changed _kut_ (gut3) to common reading _k'ut_ (kut3). Used in 鶬鶊 (oriole) (Kangxi: 鶬鶊鳥名本作倉). 【鶬-noun】 (tshang) (_noun-like_) crane Used in 鶬鶊. 【鶬鶊】 (tshang kaeng) (_noun-like_) oriole

coeng3.text: U+5531 (F1.93 唱) 歌唱 (Page 37|n80 唱) To sing in recitative (Kangxi: 發歌也); to cry out, to give the word; to lead, to conduct, as singing (Kangxi: 導也); to crow; _ts'ing ch'eung_ (cing1 coeng3 清唱) singing with a lute; _ch'eung ham_ (coeng3 haam4 唱銜), to call out the titles, as at a levee; _ch'eung ko_ (coeng3 go1 唱歌), to sing songs; _ch'eung nam ts'z'_ (coeng3 naam4 ci4 唱南詞) to sing southern ditties, _ch'eung pong tsz' hong_ (coeng3 bong1 zi2 hong1 唱梆子腔), theatrical singing; _ch'eung hi_ (coeng3 hei3 唱戲) to recite plays; _ch'eung muk u_ (coeng3 muk6 jyu4-2 唱木魚), to sing in the Canton dialect. 【唱】 (tsyhangH) (_verb-like_) to lead song

zoeng3.text: U+60B5 coeng3 (per 《廣韻》丑亮切) (F1.92 悵) 惆悵 (Page 34|n77 悵) Disappointment (Kangxi: 望恨也); vexed; _cheung cheung_ (zoeng3 zoeng3 悵悵) longed for; _cheung mong_ (zoeng3 mong6 悵望) sick at heart from delayed hopes.

cong2.text: U+6636 (F1.98 昶) 日長也明也通也舒也 (Page 29* 昶) A long day (Kangxi: 日長也); bright (Kangxi: 明久也), thorough (Kangxi: 通也), extended (Kangxi: 舒也);

coeng3.text: U+66A2 昶 鬯 (F1.93 暢) 遂也通也達也 (Page 37|n80 暢) ~~The inner qualities developing;~~ penetrating, thorough, spreading (Kangxi: 通暢又達也); to fill (Kangxi: 暢充也); joyous, contented, in good spirits; remote, long (Kangxi: 長也); exhilirating; joy; _ch'eung ut_ (coeng3 jyut6 暢月) the eleventh month; _fai ch'eung_ (faai3 coeng3 快暢) happy; _ch'eung tat_ (coeng3 daat6 暢達) to permeate; _ch'eung mau_ (coeng3 mau6 暢茂) flourishing. (Page 29* 昶 _Ch'ong_ (cong2); _Ch'eung_ (coeng3) implied) Interchanged with 暢 (Kangxi 昶: 昶與暢同). filled. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 逹 to 達. Williams' "The inner qualities developing": This is a misreading of a Kangxi 《易坤卦》美在其中而暢於四支《疏》有美在中必通暢於外 as a definition, when it is actually an example. 【暢】 (trhjangH) (_verb-like_) to permeate 【暢】 (coeng3) To exchange cash so as to break it up. 暢錢 (coeng3 cin4-2): "Break up cash." 暢散紙 (coeng3 saan2 zi2): "Exchange for smaller denominations." 暢散張廿蚊 (coeng3 saan2 zoeng1 jaa6 man1): "Break up the twenty-buck note."
U+97D4 鬯 (F1.93 韔) 弓囊也 (Page 37|n80 韔) A bow case (Kangxi: 弓衣也); to put a bow into its case (Kangxi: 韔韜也). 【韔】 (trhjangH) (_noun-like_) bow-case

U+9B2F (F1.93 鬯) 用秬黍爲酒以香草和之爲鬯 (Page 37|n80 鬯) Sacrificial spirits, made by infusing millet and fragrant herbs (Kangxi: 以秬釀𩰪艸芬芳條暢以降神也, 鬯香酒奉宗廟之盛也); mixed wine; _ku ch'eung_ (geoi6 coeng3 秬鬯) odoriferous spirits; _chu ch'eung_ (zyu2 coeng3 主鬯) a man who prepares the libations. The 192nd radical ("sacrificial-wine"). 【鬯】 (trhjangH) (_noun-like_) fragrant-wine

coeng4.text: U+5834 塲 (F1.91 塲) 祭神之所築塲爲壇除地爲塲又疆場 (Page 37|n80 塲; here normalised to 場) An arena (Kangxi 場: 戰爭之地曰戰場), an area, a lot, a field (Kangxi 場: 收禾圃曰場); an altar, a sacrificial ground (Kangxi 場: 祭神道也); a playhouse; the society of, a company; a classifier of affairs; a fit, a spell; _ta tai ch'eung k'u_ (daa2 daai6 coeng4 keoi5 打大場佢), give him a sound drubbing; _chin ch'eung_ (zin3 coeng4 戰場) field of battle; _uk ch'eung_ (uk1 coeng4 屋場), a building lot; _kun ch'eung_ (gun1 coeng4 官場), the fashion of officials; _kau ch'eung_ (gaau3 coeng4 校場), a paradeground; _fat ch'eung_ (faat3 coeng4 法場), execution-ground; _t'an ch'eung_ (taan4 coeng4 壇場), an altar; _t'iu ch'ut shi fi ch'eung_ (tiu3 ceot1 si6 fei1 coeng4 跳出是非場), I'll have nothing more to do with it; _hoi t'an ch'eung_ (hoi1 taan1 coeng4 開攤場), to open a gambling-house; _yap fo ch'eung_ (jap6 fo1 coeng4 入科場), to enter the lists for a degree of _kujin_ (舉人); _kun ha ch'eung_ (gun1 haa5 coeng4 觀吓場), made a trial; in the examination; _man ch'eung_ (man4 coeng4 文場), resorts of students; trials for degrees; _t'au ch'eung_ (tau4 coeng4 頭場), the first trial; _lok ch'eung_ (lok6 coeng4 落場), to go to the gambling-table; _yat ch'eung ho sam_ (jat1 coeng4 hou2 sam1 一場好心), a good action; _tai mung yat ch'eung_ (daai6 mung6 jat1 coeng4 大夢一場), a great dream, life. Williams' 跳出是非場: Corrected _shi_ (si4) to _shi_ (si6). 【場】 (drjang) (_noun-like_) arena (_noun-like_) field
U+5872 (F1.91 塲) 祭神之所築塲爲壇除地爲塲又疆場 (Page 37|n80 塲)
U+58BB (F1.91 牆) 卑曰垣高曰牆 (F1.91 墻) 同上 (Page 568 牆墻)
U+5B19 (F1.91 嬙) 嬪嬙女官名 (Page 568 嬙) Female officers in the imperial hareem (Kangxi: 嬪嬙婦官名); _pan ts'eung_ (ban3 coeng4 嬪嬙), a lady of the bedchamber.

U+5EA0 (F1.91 庠) 學宮名以養老爲義 (Page 567 庠) An asylum for aged people in ancient times (Kangxi: 禮官養老處也), a village school, a college, a gymnasium, an academy (Kangxi: 上庠右學大學也下庠左學小學也); to teach (Kangxi: 庠者養也); _ts'eung shang_ (coeng4 sang1 庠生), a siuts'ai (秀才); _tsun ts'eung_ (zeon3 coeng4 進庠), to become a siuts'ai (秀才); _yap ts'eung_ (jap1 coeng4 邑庠), the graduates of a district. 【庠】 (zjang) (_noun-like_) academy

U+6215 (F1.91 戕) 傷也害也 (Page 568 戕) ~~A spear, a wooden lance;~~ to kill; ~~to rob,~~ to maltreat (Kangxi: 槍也, 戕者卒暴之名); _ts'eung sheung_ (coeng4 soeng1 戕傷), to wound; _ts'eung ts'ak_ (coeng4 caak6 戕賊) to kill ~~and rob~~ ``or injure``. Williams' "A spear, a wooden lance": This is a misreading of Kangxi 槍也. According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》 (which has 搶也 originally), the intended meaning is 槍者歫也歫謂相抵爲害 (to do harm by collision), with an annotation of the Classic of Poetry reading 戕殘也 (to harm cruelly). 【戕】 (dzjang) (_verb-like_) to kill, to brutalise

zong1.text: U+6215 (Page 568 戕 _Ts'eung_ (coeng4); here corrected to _Tsong_ (zong1)) a post (Kangxi: 戕戙𣏾也);

coeng4.text: U+6AA3 (F1.91 檣) 船桅也 (Page 568 檣) A mast, any spar or mast which sustains the sails (Kangxi: 船檣帆柱也); _fan ts'eung_ (faan4 coeng4 帆檣), masts and sails.

U+7246 墻 (F1.91 牆) 卑曰垣高曰牆 (F1.91 墻) 同上 (Page 568 牆墻) A wall made of stone, brick, or mud (Kangxi: 垣蔽也, 牆謂之墉); a defense or wall (Kangxi: 牆所以防非常); _chun ts'eung_ (zyun1 coeng4 磚牆), a brick wall; _nai ts'eung_ (nai4 coeng4 泥牆), an adobie wall; _chuk ts'eung_ (zuk1 coeng4 築牆), to build a wall; _tong ts'eung_ (dong6 coeng4 盪牆), to plaster a wall; _mi shui chung ts'eung_ (mei6 seoi2 zung1 coeng4 寐水舂牆), to crawl under water, and build a wall, _met._ hard labor; _tiu ts'eung_ (diu1 coeng4 雕牆), to adorn and paint a wall; _ts'eung pik_ (coeng4 bik1 牆壁) a wall; _yat t'iu ts'eung kwo_ (jat1 tiu4 coeng4 gwo3 一條牆過) a single back wall for several houses. 【牆】 (dzjang) (_noun-like_) wall

U+7965 (F1.91 祥) 祥瑞言祥禍也善也又喪服有大祥小祥謂除凶從吉也 (Page 567 祥) Happiness, felicity, fortune (Kangxi: 福也), goodness (Kangxi: 一云善也); an omen, a sign, a prognostic (Kangxi: 凡吉凶之兆皆曰祥, 祥詳也, 祥者善惡之徵); _ts'eung sui_ (coeng4 seoi6 祥瑞) lucky omens; _kat ts'eung_ (gat1 coeng4 吉祥), a happy sign; _siu ts'eung_ (siu2 coeng4 小祥) and _tai ts'eung_ (daai6 coeng4 大祥), are the sacrifices to ancestors at the end of one and two years, when the mourning is altered (Kangxi: 祭名 etc.); _pat ts'eung chi ~~chiu~~ ``shiu``_ (bat1 coeng4 zi1 siu6 不祥之兆) a most unlucky omen; _ts'z' ts'eung_ (ci4 coeng4 慈祥), kind-hearted. Williams' 不祥之兆: Changed _chiu_ (ziu6) to common reading _shiu_ (siu6). 【祥】 (zjang) (_adjective-like_) auspicious (_noun-like_) auspice

U+7FD4 (F1.91 翔) 翺翔飛也 (Page 568 翔) To soar, to hover over (Kangxi: 回飛也); to look back (Kangxi: 翔廻顧也); dignified, severe, stern (Kangxi: 莊敬貌); _ui ts'eung_ (wui4 coeng4 回翔), to look back regretfully. 【翔】 (zjang) (_verb-like_) to fly about, to soar

U+8178 coeng4-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.91 腸) 腸臟 (Page 36|n79 腸) The bowels (Kangxi: 大小腸藏府之二名也); the intestines; tripe; _met._ the feelings, the affections; _wang a ch'eung_ (waang4 aa1 coeng4-2 橫丫腸), cunning; _ho ch'eung t'o_ (hou2 coeng4 tou5 好腸肚), clever, learned; _siu lun ch'eung_ (siu3 lyun1 coeng4-2 笑攣腸), laughed till his side ached; _chik ch'eung_ (zik6 coeng4 直腸), honest, trustworthy; _ngau ch'eung_ (ngau4 coeng4-2 牛腸), tripe; _tai ch'eung_ (daai6 coeng4-2 大腸), the colon; _siu ch'eung_ (siu2 coeng4-2 小腸), lesser intestines; _ch'eung tsong_ (coeng4 zong6 腸臟) the intestines, the inwards; _sam ch'eung_ (sam1 coeng4 心腸), the disposition, feelings; _mo ch'eung kung tsz'_ (mou4 coeng4 gung1 zi2 無腸公子) a crab. Williams' "tripe", 牛腸: Only tripe in the sense of entrails (specifically the intestines). 【腸】 (drjang) (_noun-like_) bowel 【腸】 (coeng4-2) Short for 腸粉. 蝦腸 (haa1 coeng4-2): "Prawn rice-noodle rolls." 齋腸 (zaai1 coeng4-2): "Plain rice-noodle rolls." 【腸粉】 (coeng4-2 fan2) Rice-noodle rolls. 【豬腸粉】 (zyu1 coeng4 fan2) Rice-noodle rolls, especially the tighter-rolled street food variant (smaller radius with no filling).

U+8407 (F1.91 萇) 萇楚今羊桃也 (Page 36|n79 萇) A sort of fruit, the carambola _(Averrhoa)_ (Kangxi: 萇楚, 今羊桃也), also called the _yeung t'o_ (joeng4 tou4 羊桃), or sheep's peach. 【萇】 (drjang) Used in 萇楚. 【萇楚】 (drjang tsrhjoX) (_noun-like_) carambola
U+8594 (F1.91 薔) 薔薇花名 (Page 568 薔) A rose; _ts'eung mi fa_ (coeng4 mei4 faa1 薔薇花), the cinnamon rose.

U+8A73 祥 (F1.91 詳) 詳備詳悉細密也 (Page 567 詳) To examine carefully, to learn and report upon, to inquire fully into (Kangxi: 審議也); to discourse upon (Kangxi: 論也), to reason upon (Kangxi: 諟也); to state the circumstances, to minutely narrate (Kangxi: 詳言之也, 詳悉也); the detail, the particulars, the facts, the arguments; minutely, fully; _ts'eung ha_ (coeng4 haa5 詳吓), examine into it; _ts'eung sai kong_ (coeng4 sai3 gong2 詳細講), told every particular; _ts'eung man_ (coeng4 man4 詳文), an official report; _'m chi ts'eung sai_ (m4 zi1 coeng4 sai3 唔知詳細) I don't know all the facts; _ts'eung sik_ (coeng4 sik1 詳悉) a luminous account of, intelligible. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰亻⿱十用 to 備. 【詳】 (zjang) (_verb-like_) to scrutinise (_adjective-like_) detailed (_verb-like_) to detail, to elaborate

U+9577 coeng4-2 (rising second in vernacular 長長地) (F1.91 長) 長久永遠也 (Page 36|n79 長) Long in time (Kangxi: 久也) or distance (Kangxi: 短之對也), far off (Kangxi: 遠也); constantly, regularly (Kangxi: 常也); always, continual; superiors (Kangxi: 長弟猶云先後也); skilled, used to, practiced; direct, straight; the 168th radical ("long"); _ch'eung un_ (coeng4 jyun5 長遠), durable; _kok yau sho ch'eung_ (gok3 jau5 so2 coeng4 各有所長), in what each excels; _ch'eung kwo t'au_ (coeng4 gwo3 tau4 長過頭), too long; _ch'eung shang_ (coeng4 sang1 長生) long life; a coffin; _ch'eung shi yung_ (coeng4 si4 jung6 長時用) in constant use; _ch'eung kau_ (coeng4 gau2 長久), old, a long time; _ch'eung hi_ (coeng4 hei3 長氣) longwinded, as a talker; _ch'eung hung_ (coeng4 hung4 長紅), a hand-bill; _yat mo sho ch'eung_ (jat1 mou4 so2 coeng4 一無所長), changeful, not persevering; _ch'eung kau fo ki_ (coeng4 gau2 fo2 gei3 長久伙記) an old comrade; _ch'eung shang pat lo_ (coeng4 sang1 bat1 lou5 長生不老), ~~a green old age~~ ``immortal``; _ch'eung un shai kai_ (coeng4 jyun5 sai3 gaai3 長遠世界) long continued, as a family; _ch'eung tun_ (coeng4 dyun2 長短), the length of, the traits of character; _ch'eung k'u_ (coeng4 keoi1 長驅), go directly there. 【長-level】 (drjang) (_adjective-like_) long, (_met._) everlasting 【長氣】 (coeng4 hei3) Long-winded. 咪咁長氣啦 (mai5 gam3 coeng4 hei3 la1): "Don't be so long-winded!"

zoeng2.text: U+9577 (F1.91 長) 尊也大也 (Page 34|n77 長) Old, senior, superior, greater (Kangxi: 長雄長之長); to excel, to increase (Kangxi: 進也); to grow (Kangxi: 生長也), to extend, to advance; to prosper; to elevate, to think highly of; an elder (Kangxi: 齒高也), a superior (Kangxi: 位高也); _po cheung_ (bou2 zoeng2 保長), a constable; _ka cheung_ (gaa1 zoeng2 家長), a paterfamilias; _ngo kau cheung ni_ (ngo5 gau2 zoeng2 nei5 我久長你), I'm somewhat older than you; _kwai kang to cheung_ (gwai3 gang1 do1 zoeng2 貴庚多長), how old are you? _fo cheung_ (fo2 zoeng2 伙長), mates in a ship; _cheung tai_ (zoeng2 daai6 長大) to grow older; _cheung tsz'_ (zoeng2 zi2 長子) or _cheung fong_ (zoeng2 fong4 長房), the eldest son ``and his household``; _cheung lo_ (zoeng2 lou5 長老), a Budhist priest; _tsun cheung_ (zyun1 zoeng2 尊長) or _cheung che_ (zoeng2 ze2 長者), an old man; _nin cheung_ (nin4 zoeng2 年長), old, older; _pat cheung tsun_ (bat1 zoeng2 zeon3 不長進) ~~untrustworthy~~ ``unimproving, not progressing``. Used in 長孫 (a compound surname). 【長-rising】 (trjangX) (_verb-like_) to grow (_noun-like_) elder, leader (_verb-like_) to lead

zoeng6.text: U+9577 Excess or surplus (Kangxi: 餘也, 多也宂也剰也). 【長-departing】 (drjangH) (_adjective-like_) surplus

coi1.text: U+554B (Page 590 啋) A colloquial word. An interjection of contempt, tush! pish! to pish at; _ts'oi, fat ngam wa me_ (coi1, faat3 ngaam6 waa6-2 me1 啋、發譫話咩), pshaw, what a lie! said by women; _ts'oi, wan ni_ (coi1 wan1 nei5 啋瘟你), pox take you! 【啋】 (coi1) An interjection of rebuke for mentioning a bad or inauspicious outcome. 啋 (coi1): "Pish!" 啋過你 (coi1 gwo3 nei5): "Pish unto you for saying that!"

coi2.text: U+5BC0 采 (F2.45 寀) 寮寀 (Page 591 寀) Lands or cities allotted for the revenue of officers (Kangxi: 謂寀地); _ts'oi ti_ (coi2 dei6 寀地) territory set apart for grandees; _liu ts'oi_ (liu4 coi2 寮寀), officers exercising jurisdiction over the same circuit (Kangxi: 寀寮官也, 官地爲寀同官爲寮).

U+5F69 采 (F2.44 彩) 光彩 (Page 591 彩) Variegated, mixed or ornamented with different colors; elegant, gay, colored; beautiful, as the figures in silks or gems (Kangxi: 文章也); lucky, pleased; _kwong ts'oi_ (gwong1 coi2 光彩), brilliant; smooth, as a shaven head; _ho ts'oi_ (hou2 coi2 好彩), fortunate, a good chance; _mo ts'oi_ (mou5 coi2 冇彩), unlucky, unprosperous; _lo ts'oi_ (lo2 coi2 攞彩), to get eclat, as actors do; _hot ts'oi_ (hot3 coi2 喝彩), to clap, to encore actors; _kam fa ts'oi hung_ (gam1 faa1 coi2 hung4 金花彩紅), tinsel and red silk, such as is used on joyful occasions; _hi ~~kwo~~ ``ko`` ts'oi_ (hei2 go3 coi2 起個彩), let us have a rejoicing, as after one is out of prison; _tut ts'oi_ (dyut6 coi2 奪彩), to get to the goal, to win the praise; _ts'oi ts'in_ (coi2 cin4 彩錢), the winner's douceur, the lucky man's present; _ts'oi k'i_ (coi2 kei4 彩旗), variegated banners, as those used at weddings; _'ng ts'oi_ (ng5 coi2 五彩), variegated, colored. Williams' 起個彩: Corrected _kwo_ (gwo3) to _ko_ (go3). 【彩】 (tshojX) (_noun-like_) colour, variegation, brilliance 【彩數】 (coi2 sou3) Luck, fortune. 【好彩】 (hou2 coi2) Lucky, fortunate. 好彩冇落雨 (hou2 coi2 mou5 lok6 jyu5): "Fortunately it didn't rain." 唔好彩 (m4 hou2 coi2): "Unlucky."

U+63A1 采 (F2.44 採) 取也 (Page 591 採) To select, to choose, to pick, to pluck, as leaves (Kangxi: 摘也取也㩜也); to take with the hand; _ts'oi song_ (coi2 song1 採桑), to pick mulberry leaves; _ts'oi mai_ (coi2 maai5 採買), to buy the choicest; _ts'oi fa_ (coi2 faa1 採花), to gather flowers. 【採】 (tshojX) (_verb-like_) to pick

U+68CC coi3 (per 《廣韻》倉代切) (F2.45 棌) 木名 (Page 591 棌) A sort of tree (Kangxi: 槲也), whose wood is hard and durable, like that of the chestnut or locust. The reading _ts'oi_ (coi2) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 此宰切, explained 木也 with example 漢書唐虞棌椽.
U+776C (Page 591 睬) To pay attention to, to notice, to greet (Kangxi: 偢睬俗言也); _'m ts'au 'm ts'oi_ (m4 cau1 m4 coi2 唔瞅唔睬) the cut direct, not to recognize; _'m ts'oi_ (m4 coi2 唔睬), not to acknowledge.

U+7DB5 彩 (F2.44 綵) 文色也繪也 (Page 591 綵) Interchanged with the last (彩). Variegated, colored silk (Kangxi: 五綵備); particolored; _kit ts'oi_ (git3 coi2 結綵), or _kwa ts'oi ch'au_ (gwaa3 coi2 cau4 掛綵綢), to hang festoons of silk, as is done at the _ta tsiu_ (daa2 ziu3 打醮); _ts'oi k'au_ (coi2 kau4 綵球), a many colored ball; _ling ts'oi_ (ling4 coi2 綾綵), colored sarsnet, used for linings. 【綵】 (tshojX) (_noun-like_) colourful-silk

U+91C7 埰 (for "fief" sense) coi3 (alternative for "fief" sense) (F2.44 采) 文采又采取 (Page 590 采) To be distinguished from 釆 _pin_ (bin6) to divide. To pluck, to choose, to take with the hand (Kangxi: 捋取也, 擇也); variegated, adorned with colors (Kangxi: 采色, 物采); cities allotted to officers for revenue (Kangxi: 采地); in these senses the three next (採, 寀, 彩) are used; a business, an employment; _ts'oi yap_ (coi2 jap1 采邑) territory set apart for the support of grandees, an ancient usage; _nap ts'oi_ (naap6 coi2 納采), to choose and send the betrothal presents. Note that the "fief" sense is recorded in Kangxi under *both* rising _ts'oi_ (coi2) and departing _ts'oi_ (coi3): 《康熙字典》采 《唐韻》倉宰切《集韻》《韻會》此宰切𠀤音採... 又采地《禮禮運》大夫有采以處其子孫《前漢地理志》大夫韓武子食采於韓原... 又《韻會》《正韻》𠀤倉代切音菜臣食邑 《周禮天官八則註》公卿大夫采邑音菜 《孟子元士受地視子男註》所受采地之制音菜 《前漢食貨志註》采官也因官食地故曰采地... Prescription of the departing-tone reading _ts'oi_ (coi3) is a result of zealously following 《廣韻》, which only has the departing reading 倉代切 for the alternative form 埰. Williams' 采邑: Corrected _yap_ (jap6) to _yap_ (jap1).

coi3.text: U+585E (F2.45 塞 _Soi_ (soi3)) 邊界也 (Page 591 塞) A boundary, a frontier, a limit (Kangxi: 邊界也); _ch'ut ts'oi_ (ceot1 coi3 出塞) to travel abroad; _ts'oi ngoi_ (coi3 ngoi6 塞外) beyond the frontier. 【塞】 (sojH) (_noun-like_) frontier

U+83DC (F2.45 菜) 蔬菜 (Page 591 菜) Vegetables, edible herbs, greens (Kangxi: 草之可食者, 蔬也); food, viands; _ho ts'oi_ (hou2 coi3 好菜) good eating; _ts'eng ts'oi_ (ceng1 coi3 青菜) greens in general; _sung ts'oi_ (sung3 coi3 餸菜) dishes on the table, not rice; _ts'oi shik_ (coi3 sik1 菜色) lank, half starved, like one who has lived on greens (Kangxi: 食菜之饑色); _hu mai ts'oi_ (heoi3 maai5 coi3 去買菜) gone to market; _pak ts'oi_ (baak6 coi3 白菜) ~~broccoli,~~ the pehtsai (白菜); _ham ts'oi_ (haam4 coi3 鹹菜) sour crout; _shang ts'oi_ (saang1 coi3 生菜) lettuce; _ye ts'oi_ (je4 coi3 椰菜) cabbage; _tam ts'oi_ (daam6 coi3 淡菜) a small kind of oyster; _ts'oi yau_ (coi3 jau4 菜油), rape-seed oil; _nga ts'oi_ (ngaa4 coi3 芽菜) bean sprouts; _ts'oi fa_ (coi3 faa1 菜花), a kind of rape of which the _ts'oi yau_ (coi3 jau4 菜油), or herb oil``,`` is pressed; _siu ts'oi_ (siu2 coi3 小菜) condiments of leaves and greens; _hoi ts'oi_ (hoi2 coi3 海菜) seaweed; limpets. Williams' 生菜: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). 【菜】 (tshojH) (_noun-like_) vegetable 【菜】 (coi3) (_derogatory_, _objectification_) Girlfriend. 你條菜呢 (nei5 tiu4 coi3 ne1): "Where's your chick?"

U+8521 saat3 (per 《集韻》桑割切 for "banish" and "kill" senses) (F2.45 菜) 國名又大龜也又姓 (Page 591 蔡) Grass, herbs, esculent herbs (Kangxi: 草也); ~~a law, a rule~~ ``punishment by banishment`` (Kangxi: 蔡法也); name of a feudal state, now Juning fu (汝寧府) in the southeast of Honan (河南). To put down, to kill. A great turtle (Kangxi: 蔡國君之守龜 etc.). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【蔡】 (sat) (_verb-like_) to banish, to put down

U+8CFD (F2.45 賽 _Soi_ (soi3)) 比併也又報賽 (Page 592 賽) To recompense, to thank (Kangxi: 報也); to strive to surpass, to contest for, to emulate, to match; excelling, contesting; ~~to announce~~ a thank offering, to make an offering (Kangxi: 今俗報祭曰賽神); a show of; _ts'oi tang_ (coi3 dang1 賽燈), to show lanterns, on the festival of lanterns, at 15th of 1st moon; _ts'oi shan_ (coi3 san4 賽神), to get up religious processions; _ts'oi shik_ (coi3 sik1 賽色) to show figures, to emulate in making a fine display in processions; _to ts'oi_ (dou2 coi3 賭賽) to wager. Williams' "to announce": This is a misreading of 報 in Kangxi 今俗報祭曰賽神.

coi4.text: U+624D 材 財 coi4-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun in 秀才) (F2.44 才) 能也功也畧也 (Page 590 才) talents, capacity, ability, powers (Kangxi: 一曰能也, 質也力也); endowments, genius, gifts; a person, a graduate; _ts'oi tsz'_ (coi4 zi2 才子) a genius; _man ts'oi_ (man4 coi4 文才), literary talents; _sam ts'oi_ (saam1 coi4 三才), the three powers---are heaven, earth, man (Kangxi: 天地人爲三才), who rule all things; _yat piu yan ts'oi_ (jat1 biu2 jan4 coi4 一表人才), a fine looking man; _no ts'oi_ (nou4 coi4 奴才), a slave; _ts'oi ku_ (coi4 geoi6 才具) parts, talents; _ts'oi nang_ (coi4 nang4 才能), ability; _leung ts'oi_ (loeng4 coi4 良才), goodness, rectitude; _ts'oi chi_ (coi4 zi3 才智) talents and knowledge; _ch'un ts'oi_ (ceon2 coi4 蠢才), you goose! _wai ts'oi_ (wai4 coi4 遺才), the unsuccessful siuts'ai (秀才); _ho hau ts'oi_ (hou2 hau2 coi4 好口才), eloquent. 【才】 (dzoj) (_noun-like_) talent, capability

U+6750 才 財 (F2.44 材) 人之可用者曰賢材木之可用者曰材料 (Page 590 才) Materials, stuff, or the substance of things, for which the next (材) is used; (Page 590 材) Materials of which things are made (Kangxi: 五材金木水火土也 etc.); nature, qualities; timber, lumber, wood; materiel, stuff; _ts'oi liu_ (coi4 liu6-2 材料) materials, as wood, stone, &c.; ingredients; _leung ts'oi_ (loeng4 coi4 良材), good stuff; _met._ upright ministers; _ho shan ts'oi_ (hou2 san1 coi4 好身材), likely, well formed; _'ng ts'oi_ (ng5 coi4 五材), the five elements. 【材】 (dzoj) (_noun-like_) timber (_noun-like_) material

U+7E94 才 裁 財 (F2.44 纔) 暫也僅也又剛纔方纔 (F2.44 ？) 同上 (Page 590 纔) An adverb of time; near to, at hand, nearly reaching to; in a short time, thereupon, presently, then, just then, just now, scarcely (Kangxi: 僅也); _fong ts'oi_ (fong1 coi4 方纔), then, a little while ago; _ts'oi ho_ (coi4 hou2 纔好), then it will do; _ts'oi hu_ (coi4 heoi3 纔去) just gone. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 謹 to 僅, and corrected 土 to 上.

U+88C1 才 財 (F2.44 裁) 裁衣又裁度裁製 (Page 590 裁) To make clothes, to cut out garments (Kangxi: 制衣也); to tear, to cut off (Kangxi: 裂也); to moderate, to reduce, to deny, to diminish (Kangxi: 節也); to guess, to plan; to regulate; _ts'oi fung lo_ (coi4 fung4-2 lou2 裁縫佬), a tailor; _tsz' ts'oi_ (zi6 coi4 自裁), suicide (Kangxi: 自裁自殺也); _ts'oi i_ (coi4 ji1 裁衣), to cut out clothes; _ts'oi tsin_ (coi4 zin2 裁剪), to cut off with shears; _siu ts'oi_ (siu2 coi4 小裁) and _tai ts'oi_ (daai6 coi4 大裁), a scant pattern, a full pattern. Williams' 自裁: Literally, to "self-resolve". To judge, to adjudicate, to make a ruling (Kangxi: 鑒別也). 【裁】 (dzoj) (_verb-like_) to cut, to tailor (_verb-like_) to rule (on), to resolve

U+8CA1 (F2.44 財) 寶也又貨也 (Page 590 財) Wealth, riches, valuables, property, possessions, goods, whatever men can use (Kangxi: 人所寶也, 可入用者也, etc.); ~~profits;~~ ~~bribes;~~ presents (Kangxi: 賂也); _ts'oi chu lo_ (coi4 zyu2 lou2 財主佬), a rich man; _ts'oi tung_ (coi4 dung1 財東), a capitalist, a moneyed man; _k'ap ts'oi_ (kap1 coi4 吸財), quick at money making; _fat ts'oi_ (faat3 coi4 發財), to get rich, to make money; _t'in ts'oi_ (tin1 coi4 天財), good luck, as a prize in a lottery; _ts'oi pak sing kwan_ (coi4 baak6 sing1 gwan1 財帛星君), or _ts'oi shan_ (coi4 san4 財神), god of wealth, Plutus, Mammon; _t'au ka ts'oi_ (tau1 gaa1 coi4 偷家財), a thief of a cook; _ts'oi lai_ (coi4 lai5 財禮), presents, complimentary gifts; _p'o ts'oi tong tsoi_ (po3 coi4 dong2 zoi1 破財擋災), to lavish money to ward off (or remedy) calamity; _ts'oi ho i t'ung shan_ (coi4 ho2 ji5 tung1 san4 財可以通神), money can move the gods; _ni 'm hai ts'oi shan_ (nei5 m4 hai6 coi4 san4 你唔係財神), you've bought nothing of me, you've given me no custom. Williams' "bribes": This is a misreading of Kangxi 賂也. Williams' 財可以通神: Corrected _i_ (ji2) to _i_ (ji5). 【財】 (dzoj) (_noun-like_) wealth

cok3.text: U+5252 錯 (for vernacular "choppiness" and "jolt" senses) cok3-2 (rising second in vernacular 剒剒地) (Page 592 錯; here normalised to 剒) A colloquial word. A hacking in the throat, a difficulty or stoppage in the throat; _ts'ok hi_ (cok3 hei3 剒氣) the heart in the throat; _ch'au ts'ok_ (cau1 cok3 抽剒) a retching cough; _ts'ok ts'ok ngok ngok_ (cok3 cok3 ngok6 ngok6 剒剒愕愕) amazed, alarmed and amused. To work, to cut, to polish (Kangxi: 治樸之名, 亦作錯). To chop off. 【剒】 (cok3) Choppiness in the airways, unsmoothness in breathing. 剒住條氣 (cok3 zyu6 tiu4 hei3): "Choppiness in the airways." To shake, to jolt. 剒車 (cok3 ce1): "Jerking car." 剒散咗 (cok3 saan2 zo2): "Shaken to pieces."

cou3.text: U+539D (F2.16 措 _Ts'u_) 處置也安着也施布也又委置也 (F2.16 厝 _Ts'u_) 同上又安頓也 (Page 587 厝) ~~A large flat stone, fit for a gravestone;~~ to place (Kangxi: 厝置也), to put a body away properly; interchanged with the next (措) (Kangxi: 同措); _on ts'o_ (on1 cou3 安厝) to put a coffin in the grave or under a lodge; quietly reposing; _ts'o chu_ (cou3 zyu3 厝註) ~~to cut~~ an epitaph. Williams' "large flat stone, fit for a gravestone" and "to cut": Williams has conflated the "whetstone" sense of 厝, which should be read in entering tone _ts'ok_ (cok3), with the "placement" sense _ts'o_ (cou3). Unlike the "placement" sense, the "whetstone" sense has no connotations with burial.

cong1.text: U+5009 蒼 (in 倉皇 spelt 蒼黃) cong2 (common in 倉卒) (F1.95 倉) 倉廪藏谷之所又倉皇言懼也 (Page 593 倉) A granary (Kangxi: 穀藏也) of the government; a storehouse; a box, a bin, a compartment, a pigeon-hole; _kuk ts'ong_ (guk1 cong1 穀倉), a rice storehouse; _ts'ong seung_ (cong1 soeng1 倉箱), bins or partitions in a granary; _fo ts'ong_ (fo3 cong1 貨倉), a pack-house, a go-down; _hoi ts'ong_ (hoi1 cong1 開倉), to dispense rice from the granaries; _yat ts'ong ts'ong_ (jat1 cong1 cong1 一倉倉), ~~one bin or stall~~ ``individual bins``; _ts'ong ts'ut_ (cong1 cyut3 倉卒) ~~flurried, perturbed, alarmed~~ ``hurried, hasty``. (Page 593 蒼; here normalised to 倉) Occurs used for the last (倉), hastily; _ts'ong wong_ (cong1 wong4 倉皇), hurried, flighty (Kangxi 蒼: 蒼黃急遽貌); 【倉】 (tshang) (_noun-like_) granary

U+5275 刅 戧 cong3 (common) (Page 28* 創刅) To wound, to cut and injure (Kangxi: 傷也); to butt, to gore; a wound, a cut; wounded, gashed; _shan pi to ch'ong_ (san1 bei6 do1 cong1 身被多創) he received many wounds. Whilst the classical reading for the "wound" sense is level-tone _ch'ong_ (cong1), the common reading departing-tone _ch'ong_ (cong3) is not unattested; Kangxi has 又《唐韻》初亮切瘡去聲始也造也...又傷也《前漢匈奴傳》人民創艾戰鬭. 【創-level】 (tsrhjang) (_verb-like_) to wound

cong3.text: U+5275 刱 (F1.100 創) 創造創始又懲創儆也 (F1.100 ？) 同上 (Page 28* 創刱) To begin, to invent (Kangxi: 始也造也); to create, to found, to lay the foundation of; to take measures for; ~~to reprove;~~ the commencement, first; _tso ch'ong_ (zou6 cong3 造創) to invent; _ch'ong ch'i_ (cong3 ci2 創始), to begin; _ch'ong ip_ (cong3 jip6 創業) to found a family or a settlement; _t'in ti hoi ch'ong_ (tin1 dei6 hoi1 cong3 天地開創) the creation; _ch'ong lap ma t'au_ (cong3 lap6 maa5 tau4 創立碼頭), to build a ferry or jetty; _ch'ing ch'ong_ (cing4 cong3 懲創) to blame. To take heed or warning (Kangxi: 懲也). 【創-departing】 (tsrhjangH) (_verb-like_) to create, to initiate (_verb-like_) to take heed

cong1.text: U+6EC4 (F1.95 滄) 滄浪水名又滄海也 (Page 593 滄) Vast like the sea; name of a river and lake (Kangxi: 水名, 河名, 湖名); a superior district in Tientsin fu (天津府); _ts'ong hoi chi leung_ (cong1 hoi2 zi1 loeng6 滄海之量) to drink like the sea, able to drink much. Cold (Kangxi: 寒也).

U+7621 創 (F1.96 瘡) 瘡疾傷也 (Page 28* 瘡) A sore, ulcer, boil, or eruption (Kangxi: 痏也, 瘍也); _ch'ong na_ (cong1 naa1 瘡𤶸), a scar; _ch'ong ch'un_ (cong1 cyun1 瘡穿), to open a boil; _ch'ong lau_ (cong1 lau6 瘡瘻) the piles; _shang ch'ong_ (saang1 cong1 生瘡), to have a sore; _fo ~~ting~~ ``teng`` ch'ong_ (fo2 deng1 cong1 火釘瘡), a boil; _wat yuk lo ch'ong shang_, (waat3 juk6 lo2 cong1 saang1 挖肉攞瘡生) he scrapes the flesh to make a sore --- he ~~trumps up a cause against me~~ ``brings unnecessary trouble to himself``; _ki ch'ong_ (gei3 cong1 寄瘡), to give away a boil. Williams' 生瘡, 挖肉攞瘡生: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). Williams' 火釘瘡: Changed _ting_ (ding1) to vernacular reading _teng_ (deng1). 【瘡】 (tsrhjang) (_noun-like_) boil

U+8259 (Page 594 艙) An unauthorized character. The compartments in a ship's hold; a hold; _shun ts'ong_ (syun4 cong1 船艙), the hold of a vessel; _hoi ts'ong_ (hoi1 cong1 開艙) to begin to unlade; _so ts'ong_ (sou3 cong1 掃艙), the last lighter; _ts'ing ts'ong_ (cing1 cong1 清艙), cargo all discharged; _fung ts'ong_ (fung1 cong1 封艙), to forbid trade; _tai t'o ts'ong_ (daai6 tou5 cong1 大肚艙), the main hold; _ts'ong wai_ (cong1 wai6-2 艙位) the stowage. Williams' 大肚艙: Corrected _t'o_ (tou2) to _t'o_ (tou5).

U+84BC 倉 (F1.95 蒼) 靑色又蒼天 (Page 593 蒼) the green tint of plants (Kangxi: 草色也), or the azure of the sky; flourishing, prospering (Kangxi: 茂也); hoary, aged, old in service (Kangxi: 老也); _ts'ong t'au_ (cong1 tau4 蒼頭), soldiers, so called from their green caps (Kangxi: 蒼頭, 士卒靑帛巾); an old slave; _ts'ong ts'ong che t'in_ (cong1 cong1 ze2 tin1 蒼蒼者天), the empyrean; _met._ Providence; _ts'ong lo_ (cong1 lou5 蒼老), old, waning; an old man; _ts'ong shang_ (cong1 sang1 蒼生) the people, living things; _ts'ing ts'ong_ (cing1 cong1 青蒼), a greenish bluish color. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【蒼】 (tshang) (_adjective-like_) azure, verdant
U+468E (Page 27* 䚎 _Chong_ (zong1); here corrected to _Ch'ong_ (cong1)) To see indistinctly (Kangxi: 視不明也); to look at fixedly (Kangxi: 一曰直視); ~~to watch, as one does a new hand.~~

zong1.text: U+468E 𥅾 𥊙 (Page 27* 䚎) A colloquial word. To peep through a crack; to hide one away; _chong k'am chong shuk_ (zong1 kam4 zong1 suk1 䚎擒䚎縮) to peep in and out; _chong ha k'u tim_ (zong1 haa5 keoi5 dim2 䚎吓佢點), see how he acts; _chong k'u ts'o ch'u_ (zong1 keoi5 co3 cyu3 䚎佢錯處) mark his errors. 【䚎】 (zong1) To look whilst trying not to be seen. 唔准䚎人 (m4 zeon2 zong1 jan4): "No peeping at others!"

cong1.text: U+4C7D 鯧 (F1.95 䱽) 魚名 (Page 594 鯧䱽) The second character (䱽) is unauthorized. The pomfret; _pak ts'ong u_ (baak6 cong1 jyu4-2 白䱽魚), the silver pomfret _(Stromateus argenteus)_; _hak ts'ong_ (hak1 cong1 黑䱽), the black pomfret _(Stromateus niger)_; _wong lap ts'ong_ (wong4 laap6 cong1 黃鱲䱽), the yellow pomfret _(Trachinotus auratus)_; _fa ts'ong_ (faa1 cong1 花䱽), small pomfret _(Caranx malabaricus)_; _kwa tsz' ts'ong_ (gwaa1 zi2 cong1 瓜子䱽), long-finned pomfret _(Trachinotus asper)_. Effectively, 鯧 and 䱽 are the same semantic word "pomfret", but: 鯧 is classical with reading _ch'eung_ (coeng1) (see 鯧 coeng1); and 䱽 is vernacular with reading _ts'ong_ (cong1). Williams' 黃鱲䱽: Changed _lap_ (lap6) to longer syllable _lap_ (laap6).

cong2.text: U+53B0 (F1.98 厰) 屋無壁 (Page 29* 厰) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿸厂敝 to 厰.

U+5EE0 厰 (F1.98 厰) 屋無壁 (Page 29* 厰; here normalised to 廠) An open shed (Kangxi 廠: 露舍也); a covered place not walled in (Kangxi 廠: 屋無壁也); a place to receive taxes, broad, spacious; a depository; a large workshop; _mui t'an ch'ong_ (mui4 taan3 cong2 煤炭廠), a coal depot; _siu ch'ong_ (siu1 cong2 硝廠), a saltpetre gabel; _p'ung ch'ong_ (pung4 cong2 篷廠) a mat shed; _ko kan uk ho ch'ong_ (go2 gaan1 uk1 hou2 cong2 嗰間屋好廠), that's a large house; _ch'ong t'eng_ (cong2 teng1 廠廳), a summer arbor. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿸厂敝 to 厰. Williams' 嗰間屋好廠: Changed _ko_ (go3) to modern _ko_ (go2) and corrected _kan_ (gaan4) to _kan_ (gaan1).

U+60DD 𢠵 tong2 (alternative per 《集韻》坦㓪切) (F1.98 𢠵) 𢠵恍也驚貌 (Page 29* 𢠵; here normalised to 惝) Alarmed (Kangxi 𢠵: 𢠵怳驚貌或省作惝); frightened; _ch'ong fong_, (cong2 fong2 惝怳) apprehensive, disappointed (Kangxi 惝: 惝怳失意不悅貌). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 𢠳 to 𢠵.
U+6134 (F1.98 愴) 悽愴言傷心也 (Page 29* 愴) Used in 愴怳 (disappointed) (Kangxi: 愴怳失意貌).

cong3.text: U+6134 cong2 (per Fan Wan) (F1.98 愴 _Ch'ong_ (cong2)) 悽愴言傷心也 (Page 29* 愴 _Ch'ong_ (cong2); here normalised to _Ch'ong_ (cong3)) Grief (Kangxi: 傷也); _ts'ai ch'ong_ (cai1 cong3 悽愴), sorrow; to pity; _ch'ong ch'ak_ (cong3 cak1 愴惻) a wounded heart, distressed. 【愴】 (tsrhjangH) (_adjective-like_) grieved

cong2.text: U+22835 (F1.98 𢠵) 𢠵恍也驚貌 (Page 29* 𢠵) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 𢠳 to 𢠵.

U+95D6 cam3 (per 《廣韻》丑禁切) cong3 (per 《說文注》俗語轉若刱) (F1.98 闖) 闖進出頭貌又音滲 (Page 29* 闖) A horse going out of a door (Kangxi: 馬出門貌); to thrust out the head, to appear (Kangxi: 出頭貌); to bolt out; suddenly, forcibly, abruptly; _ch'ong wo_ (cong2 wo6 闖禍) to induce calamities; _ch'ong kwan_ (cong2 gwaan1 闖關), to slip by the custom-house; _ch'ong ~~tsak~~ ``ts'ak`` loi_ (cong2 caak6 loi4 闖賊來), the rover Ch'ong (闖) is on us! Also read _sham_ (sam1) (Kangxi: 《說文》...讀若郴). Williams' 闖賊來: Corrected _tsak_ (zaak6) to _ts'ak_ (caak6). Williams' "Also read ...": Or _ch'am_ (cam1) per 《廣韻》丑林切 for 郴.

cong3.text: U+5231 (F1.100 創) 創造創始又懲創儆也 (F1.100 ？) 同上 (Page 28* 創刱)

cong4.text: U+5E62 (Page 28* 幢 _Chong_ (zong4); here corrected to _Ch'ong_ (cong4)) Long embroidered scrolls or streamers (Kangxi: 旌旗之屬) in temples, suspended from the roof, and having a tilt at top.
U+5E8A (F1.97 床) 臥榻 (F1.97 牀) 同上 (Page 28* 床牀) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 㮼 to 榻.

U+7240 床 (F1.97 床) 臥榻 (F1.97 牀) 同上 (Page 28* 床牀) A bed, a couch (Kangxi 牀: 安身之坐者, 人所坐臥曰牀); a settee; a suit of bed clothes; the boards which make a bed; a well-curb; a measure of 8 cubits (Kangxi 牀: 八尺曰牀); _yat ch'ong p'i yuk_ (jat1 cong4 pei5 juk6 一牀被褥) mattrass, sheets, all complete; _p'o ch'ong_ (pou1 cong4 鋪牀), make the bed; _yat cheung ch'ong_ (jat1 zoeng1 cong4 一張牀), a bed; _tai ch'ong_ (daai6 cong4 大牀), a double bedstead; _k'ong ch'ong_ (kong3 cong4 匟牀), the couch of honor in a hall; _ch'ong p'o_ (cong4 pou1 牀鋪), bedding; _kwai fi ch'ong_ (gwai3 fei1 cong4 貴妃牀), a couch with rolling ends; _sheung ch'ong_ (soeng5 cong4 上牀), to go to bed. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 㮼 to 榻. 【牀】 (dzrjang) (_noun-like_) bed

zong1.text: U+81E7 (F1.95 臧) 善也厚也又奴婢曰臧獲 (Page 593 臧) Good (Kangxi: 臧善也), generous (Kangxi: 厚也); virtuous, faithful; to scold ``male`` slaves (Kangxi: ...罵奴曰臧罵婢曰獲); _tsong wok_ (zong1 wok6 臧獲) to berate slaves. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𫉬 to 獲. Williams' 臧獲: Corrected _wok_ (wok3) to _wok_ (wok6). To commend, to praise. A slave. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

cong4.text: U+85CF 臧 (F1.97 藏) 匿也隱也蓄也 (Page 594 藏) To store away (Kangxi: 蓄也), to put in safety; to contain; to hide, to conceal (Kangxi: 匿也); to accumulate; to gather knowledge; _ts'ong mai_ (cong4 maai4 藏埋), to lay by; _ka ts'ong_ (gaa1 cong4 家藏), heirlooms laid by, family treasures; _mo ts'ong t'au_ (mou5 cong4 tau4-2 冇藏頭), no chance for profit; _ts'ong pan_ (cong4 baan2 藏版), to keep the blocks, to publish a book; _pau ts'ong_ (baau1 cong4 包藏), to keep from, not to divulge; _shau ts'ong_ (sau1 cong4 收藏), to receive, as in trust; _'m hai ts'ong lung chi sho_ (m4 hai6 cong4 lung4 zi1 so2 唔係藏龍之所), I'm not to be cribbed in this place; _yan ts'ong_ (jan2 cong4 隱藏), to conceal; _ts'ong t'au lo mi_ (cong4 tau4 lou6 mei5 藏頭露尾), to give a partial account of, an inkling. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 慝 to 匿, and corrected malformed 蓄. 【藏】 (dzang) (_verb-like_) to store (away)

zong6.text: U+85CF 臧 (F1.101 藏) 收貨財處曰藏又過貪曰無盡藏 (Page 593 藏) A store-house, a retreat; a strong-box, a casket; _mo tsun tsong_ (mou4 zeon6 zong6 無盡藏) exceedingly avaricious or greedy; _sai tsong_ (sai1 zong6 西藏) Tibet; _yap tsong_ (jap6 zong6 入藏) to lie torpid; to keep out of sight; _ti tsong wong_ (dei6 zong6 wong4 地藏王), name of a deified Budhist.

cou1.text: U+64CD (F2.9 操 _Ts'u_) 操持 (F2.9 撡 _Ts'u_) 同上 (Page 586 操) To take, to hold, to grasp (Kangxi: 把持也); to manage; to exercise, to drill; expert, used to; to maintain, to restrain one's self, to moderate one's desires; _ts'o in_ (cou1 jin2 操演) or _ha ts'o_ (haa6 cou1 下操), to drill troops; _t'ai ts'o_ (tai2 cou1 睇操), to see a review; _tai ts'o_ (daai6 cou1 大操), the triennial review; _ts'o ch'i_ (cou1 ci4 操持), resolute, adhering to right; _ts'o sam_ (cou1 sam1 操心), anxious, careworn. Williams' 下操: Changed _ha_ (haa5) to modern _ha_ (haa6). 【操-verb】 (tshaw) (_verb-like_) to hold, to handle

cou3.text: U+64CD (Page 586 操 _Ts'o_ (cou1); here corrected to _Ts'o_ (cou3)) a purpose, a design, self-imposed rule (Kangxi: 所守也持念也, 所執持之志行也); consistent, principled; Zither music (Kangxi: 琴曲也). 【操-noun】 (tshawH) (_noun-like_) integrity, moral-character

cou1.text: U+7C97 麤 (F2.9 粗 _Ts'u_) 不精也臭惡也 (F2.9 麄 _Ts'u_) 同上 (F2.9 麤 _Ts'u_) 同上 (Page 586 粗麤) Coarse (Kangxi 粗: 略也疏也), uncleaned, dirty, as rice just thrashed; large, open, coarse (Kangxi 粗: 大也...物不精也); rude, vulgar, boisterous, unpolished; gross, indecent, dirty; harsh, as a voice; partially, rudely; _ts'o yau_ (cou1 jau3 粗幼) coarse or fine, as cloth; _ts'o tsuk yan_ (cou1 zuk6 jan4 粗俗人), a rough vulgar man; _ts'o lo_ (cou1 lou5 粗魯), rough, unmannerly; _ts'o chi_ (cou1 zi1 粗知), partly knew it; _ts'o wa_ (cou1 waa6-2 粗話) obscene language; _ts'o shik_ (cou1 sik6 粗食) coarse food; a gross eater; _ts'o un_ (cou1 jyun4 粗完), nearly done; _shau ts'o_ (sau2 cou1 手粗), a large hand; _ts'o sam fau hi_ (cou1 sam1 fau4 hei3 粗心浮氣) light, inattentive, giddy; _ts'o shai tai yung_ (cou1 sai2 daai6 jung6 粗使大用) careless in spending money; ~~_u ts'o_ (wu1 cou1 污粗), dirty, unwashed.~~ Williams' 污粗: Should instead appear under 糟 as 污糟. Cylindrically thick. 【粗】 (tshu) (_adjective-like_) coarse, thick
U+9EA4 (F2.9 粗 _Ts'u_) 不精也臭惡也 (F2.9 麄 _Ts'u_) 同上 (F2.9 麤 _Ts'u_) 同上 (Page 586 粗麤) The second form (麤) also means to take long leaps as a deer (Kangxi 麤: 行超遠也); to take precautions (Kangxi 麤: 警防也).

cou3.text: U+61C6 cou2 (per 《廣韻》采老切 for "distressed" sense) (F2.16 懆 _Ts'u_) 憂貌 (Page 587 懆) Chagrined, thoughtful, sad, vexed (Kangxi: 愁不安也); covetous (Kangxi: 貪也); affected by; _tsiu ts'o_ (ziu1 cou3 焦懆) discontented and anxious. The reading _ts'o_ (cou3) is not unattested for the "distressed" sense; 《集韻》 has the reading 七到切, explained 貪也不安也 (covering both the "greed" and "distressed" senses). 【懆】 (tshawX) (_adjective-like_) distraught

cou2.text: U+8278 (F2.14 草 _Ts'u_) 草木 (F2.14 艸 _Ts'u_) 同上 (Page 587 草艸) the 140th radical ("grass") of characters relating to herbs;

U+8349 艸 (F2.14 草 _Ts'u_) 草木 (F2.14 艸 _Ts'u_) 同上 (Page 587 草艸) Herbs, grasses, plants with herbaceous stems (Kangxi 草: 百卉也); plants in general; hastily, carelessly (Kangxi 草: 苟簡曰草草); the running hand; a rough copy, original draft; to cut grass, to mow (Kangxi 草: 草艾謂艾取草也); _ts'o muk_ (cou2 muk6 草木) plants; _ta ts'o ts'am she_ (daa2 cou2 cam4 se4 打草尋蛇), to beat the grass for snakes, _i. e._ to devise evil; _ts'o ts'o liu sz'_ (cou2 cou2 liu5 si6 草草了事) to finish up jobs heedlessly; _ts'o shu_ (cou2 syu1 草書), run``n``ing hand; _ts'o ts'o_ (cou2 cou2 草草), toilsome``,`` troublesome, careless, lenient; _ts'o ko_ (cou2 gou2 草稿), first proof, a rough copy; _ts'o pau_ (cou2 baau1 草包), an irascible man; _wo ts'o_ (wo4 cou2 禾草), rice straw; _ts'o kan fui_ (cou2 gan1 fui1 草根灰), mortar with straw; _fa ts'o_ (faa1 cou2 花草), flowers, adorned with plants; _'m ho fa ts'o_ (m4 hou2 faa1 cou2 唔好花草), not well carved; _ts'o p'ang_ (cou2 paang4 草棚), a thatched roof, any thatch; _ts'o ~~kun~~ ``kan`` yan meng_ (cou2 gaan1 jan4 meng6 草菅人命) to trifle with people's lives, as charlatans do. Williams' 草菅人命: Corrected _kun_ (gun2) to _kan_ (gaan1). Williams has likely misread 菅 as 管. 【草】 (tshawX) (_noun-like_) grass, herbage (_adjective-like_) rough, careless (_adjective-like_) travailed 【草花頭】 (cou2 faa1 tau4-2) The character component 艹. (Literally, "grass flower atop".)

cou3.text: U+566A 喿 (F2.16 噪 _Ts'u_) 群鳥鳴聲 (Page 587 噪) The chirping and singing of many birds (Kangxi 喿: 鳥羣鳴也, 或作噪𠹏); the hum of men; to cry out (Kangxi 噪: 呼噪也).

U+63AA 厝 (for "place", "bury", "conceal" senses) 錯 (for "place", "bury", "conceal" senses) (F2.16 措 _Ts'u_) 處置也安着也施布也又委置也 (F2.16 厝 _Ts'u_) 同上又安頓也 (Page 587 措) To cast away, to throw down, to place, to put in its place (Kangxi: 措置也, 投也); to make known, to show abroad (Kangxi: 舉也); to conceal, to set aside; to bury; ~~to take at unawares;~~ to use, to employ (Kangxi: 施布也, 措猶用也); to ~~relinquish~~ ``abandon`` (Kangxi: 委置也); arranged, well ordered; a rule; _mo sho ts'o shau tsuk_ (mou4 so2 cou3 sau2 zuk1 無所措手足) no place for my hands or feet, _i.e._ at a loss, perplexed; _ts'o ts'z' tai nga_ (cou3 ci4 daai6 ngaa5 措辭大雅), elegant phraseology, words well spoken. 【措】 (tshuH) (_verb-like_) to place, to put, to set (_verb-like_) to apply

zaak3.text: U+63AA (Page 587 措) Read _~~ch'ak~~ ``chak``_ (zaak3). To pursue after, to chase, to ferret out (Kangxi: 追捕也).

cou3.text: U+6FA1 zou2 (per 《廣韻》子晧切) (F2.13 澡 _Tsu_ (zou2)) 洗滌 (Page 584 澡 _Tso_ (zou2); here normalised to _Ts'o_ (cou3)) To bathe, to wash the person (Kangxi: 澡洗也); to cleanse the heart, to reform (Kangxi: 借作修潔意); _sai ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (sai2 cou3 洗澡), to bathe. To wash the hands (per 《說文》洒手也). 【澡】 (tsawX) (_verb-like_) to wash

zou2.text: U+6FA1 (F2.13 澡 _Tsu_) 洗滌 (Page 584 澡)

cou3.text: U+71E5 cou3-2 (rising second in vernacular 燥燥地) (Page 587 燥) Dry, scorched, chapped, parched (Kangxi: 乾也); to dry by the fire; _kon ts'o_ (gon1 cou3 乾燥) parched up, drought; _fung ko ts'o mat_ (fung1 gou1 cou3 mat6 風高燥物) the north wind dries or splits things; _ts'au ts'o_ (cau1 cou3 秋燥) heats of autumn. Williams' 風高燥物: Usually 風高物燥. 【燥】 (sawH, sawX) (_adjective-like_) parched

U+7CD9 May appear as either ⿰米⿺辶吿 or ⿰米⿺辶告 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.16 糙 _Ts'u_) 米糙 (Page 587 糙) Coarse rice (Kangxi: 粗米未舂)~~, paddy which has only been hulled, and not cleaned~~; _ts'o mai lan_ (cou3 mai5 laan1 糙米欄), Coarse Rice street, in Canton.

U+8B5F 噪 (Page 728 譟) The noise of a crowd (Kangxi: 羣呼煩擾也, 聒也, 羣呼曰譟); a clamor, a disturbance (Kangxi: 擾也); _yan to ku ts'o_ (jan4 do1 gu2 cou3 人多鼓譟) a great hallooing. Williams' 人多鼓譟: Corrected _ts'o_ (with an apostrophe) to _ts'o_ (with an aspirate). 【譟】 (sawH) (_verb-like_) to clamour

U+8E81 燥 趮 zou3 (per 《廣韻》則到切) (F2.16 躁 _Ts'u_) 浮躁妄動也急進也 (Page 587 燥; here normalised to 躁) fierce, untameable; (Page 587 躁) Hasty, heedless, precipitate; to go quickly (Kangxi: 疾也); dried up by the heat, and therefore light (Kangxi: 躁燥也物燥乃動而飛揚也); fierce, violent; _ts'o po_ (cou3 bou6 躁暴) outrageous, prone to anger; bustling, fussy; _fau ts'o_ (fau4 cou3 浮躁) light, testy, wavering. To move, to stir. 【躁】 (tsawH) (_adjective-like_) rash (_verb-like_) to move (hastily), to stir

U+9020 May appear as either ⿺辶吿 or ⿺辶告 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.16 造 _Ts'u_) 至也詣也進也又造次 (Page 587 造) To go to, to arrive (Kangxi: 就也, 詣也); to advance, to enter (Kangxi: 進也); to contain (Kangxi: 納也, 造猶內也內與納同); _ts'o ts'z'_ (cou3 ci3 造次) hurried (Kangxi: 造次急遽也), thoughtless. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰音旨 to 詣. 【造】 (tshawH) (_verb-like_) to go unto, to attain (_verb-like_) to contain Used in 造次. 【造次】 (tshawH tshijH) (_adjective-like_) abrupt

cou5.text: U+9020 May appear as either ⿺辶吿 or ⿺辶告 (assumed here), depending on the font. 儲 (Page 587 造) A colloquial word. To collect, to lay up, to heap up; to lay by, to pay instalments, to pay in savings; _yat ts'in ts'o yat ts'in_ (jat1 cin4 cou5 jat1 cin4 一錢造一錢), for one mace lay up another; _siu ts'in ts'o tai ts'in_ (siu2 cin4 cou5 daai6 cin4 小錢造大錢), a little farthing will grow to a large penny; _ts'o mai_ (cou5 maai4 造埋), to lay up. Used in 新造 (the name of a town in Canton). 【造】 (cou5) To collect, to gather, to accumulate. 造錢 (cou5 cin4-2): "Save up money." 造銀仔 (cou5 ngan4-2 zai2): "Collect coins."

zou6.text: U+9020 May appear as either ⿺辶吿 or ⿺辶告 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.18 造 _Tsu_) 建也作也爲也始也 (Page 585 造) To build, to make, to construct (Kangxi: 建也作也爲也); to create; to act; to do (Kangxi: 造爲也); to begin, to commence (Kangxi: 始也); a crop; _kin tso_ (gin3 zou6 建造) to build; _tso fa_ (zou6 faa3 造化) to create; _ho tso fa_ (hou2 zou6 faa3 好造化) good fortune; _kot wo tso_ (got3 wo4 zou6 割禾造) to cut the rice harvest; _shang tso ch'ut lai_, (saang1 zou6 ceot1 lai4 生造出嚟) an entire fabrication, made out of whole cloth; _tsoi tso_ (zoi3 zou6 再造) do it over again; _tso pun_ (zou6 bun2 造本), to get some capital; _mut tso tong san ch'ut_ (mut6 zou6 dong3 san1 ceot1 末造當新出) the last of the season is as good as a new crop; _tso tso man tso_ (zou2 zou6 maan5 zou6 早造晚造) the early crop, the late crop; _hau tso_ (haau2 zou6 巧造) skillful work. Williams' 生造出嚟: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1).

zok6.text: U+9162 (F1.103 酢) 酧酢 (Page 592 酢) To pledge a host, to return health (Kangxi: 客酌主人也, 酬酢); to recompense, to return a compliment (Kangxi: 酢報也).

cou3.text: U+918B 酢 (F2.16 醋 _Ts'u_) 酒醋 (Page 588 醋) Vinegar (Kangxi 酢: 今人...反以醋爲酢字, 酸也); pickle; pickled; _tsau ts'o_ (zau2 cou3 酒醋) vinegar of spirits; _pak ts'o_ (baak6 cou3 白醋) clear vinegar; _hap ts'o_ (haap3 cou3 呷醋) female rivalry, as between two concubines; _lok tik im ts'o_ (lok6 dik1 jim4 cou3 落滴鹽醋) be more severe with him, as with a pupil; _tso ts'o 'm sun_ (zou6 cou3 m4 syun1 做醋唔酸), your vinegar has no sourness, *you* can do nothing; _yak keung ts'o_ (jaak3 goeng1 cou3 吃薑醋) to bear a child, because ginger and vinegar are given to the mother; _yeung ts'o_ (joeng4 cou3 洋醋) foreign vinegar. 【呷醋】 (haap3 cou3) To be jealous of a romantic rival.

cou4.text: U+5608 cou4-2 (rising second in vernacular 嘈嘈地) (F2.12 嘈 _Ts'u_) 喧也又胡酒也 (Page 586 ~~？~~``嘈``) Noise, clamor, hubbub (Kangxi: 聲也); an outcry (Kangxi: 喧嘈); to make a din; to vociferate; _ts'o nau_ (cou4 naau6 嘈鬧) wrangling, squabbling; _hu kom ts'o_ (heoi1 gam3 cou4 墟咁嘈), noisy as a market; _ts'o ts'o pai_ (cou4 cou4 bai3 嘈嘈閉) a brawl; _mai ts'o yan i_ (mai5 cou4 jan4 ji5 咪嘈人耳), don't deafen people with your noise; _lo ts'o_ (lou4 cou4 嘮嘈), rude, rough, unmannerly; _shap fan ts'o hi_ (sap6 fan1 cou4 hei3 十分嘈氣) panting after an angry brawl; _ts'o ts'o ch'au ch'au_ (cou4 cou4 caau2 caau2 嘈嘈吵吵), a tumult, squalling, as tanka women do; _'m ho ts'o_ (m4 hou2 cou4 唔好嘈), don't make a noise. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰口冋 to 喧.

U+5F82 (F2.12 徂 _Ts'u_) 往也落也 (Page 586 徂) To go up; to proceed, to travel (Kangxi: 往也); to preserve (Kangxi: 存也), to lay up; _ts'o tse_ (cou4 ze6 徂謝) to fade, as flowers. 【徂】 (dzu) (_verb-like_) to go (unto)

U+66F9 (F2.12 曹 _Ts'u_) 局也衆也軰也又同事曰曹 (Page 586 曹) ~~A sort of revising judge~~; ~~a judge of appeals;~~ a place where officers meet (Kangxi: 曹羣也); a company, a class``, a herd`` (Kangxi: 曹輩也); denotes the plural of persons; _luk ts'o_ (luk6 cou4 六曹), the six Boards; _t'in ts'o_ (tin1 cou4 天曹) and _yam ts'o_ (jam1 cou4 陰曹), gods and devils; _i ts'o_ (ji5 cou4 爾曹), you; _kun ts'o_ (gun1 cou4 官曹), officers; _Ts'o Ts'o_ (cou4 cou1 曹操), a famous general``, who died`` A.D. ~~250~~~``220``. Williams' "A sort of revising judge", "a judge of appeals": These are misreadings of Kangxi 獄之兩曹也在廷東从㯥治事者从曰. Here, 獄 means "litigation" rather than "gaol", and 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》 interpret 㒳曹 as referring to the plaintiff and the defendant (㒳曹今俗所謂原吿被吿也). Note that the two characters "从曰" are missing in Kangxi; their presence in 《說文》 implies that 治事者 is just the explanation of the bottom component 曰, rather than the definition of the character as a whole (as Williams has taken it to be). Plaintiff and defendant (Kangxi: 獄之兩曹也 etc.). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【曹】 (dzaw) (_noun-like_) company, herd

U+69FD (F2.12 槽 _Ts'u_) 馬槽滿槽 (Page 586 槽) A trough, a log channeled out, a manger (Kangxi: 畜獸之食器); a vat, a tub for spirits (Kangxi: 酒槽酒坊也); a sort of fruit (Kangxi: 枸槽子果名); _ma ts'o_ (maa5 cou4 馬槽), a manger; _tsau ts'o_ (zau2 cou4 酒槽), a wine vat; _ts'o fong_ (cou4 fong1 槽坊), a grogshop; _shui ts'o_ (seoi2 cou4 水槽), an eaves-trough, a horse-trough. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰冫㒼 to 滿.

U+6B82 徂 (F2.12 殂 _Ts'u_) 死也㓕也 (Page 586 殂) To die (Kangxi: 往死也); ~~dead;~~ ~~a path to the grave;~~ _ts'o lok_ (cou4 lok6 殂落) to pass away, to die. 【殂】 (dzu) (_verb-like_) to go on, (_met._) to pass away

U+6F15 zou6 (alternative per 《廣韻》在到切 for "water-transport" sense) (F2.12 漕 _Ts'u_) 衞邑也又以水運糧曰漕運 (Page 586 漕) A mill-race, a sluice through which water runs (Kangxi: 水轉穀也); ~~a gulf, a gorge;~~ a canal; to lead on water, to turn a water course (Kangxi: 水運曰漕); to convey revenue to Peking (北京); ~~occurs used for the next (艚);~~ _ts'o wan_ (cou4 wan6 漕運) to transport grain, to take it to the army; _ts'o ho_ (cou4 ho4 漕河), the Grand Canal; _ts'o wai_ (cou4 wai6 漕衛) a fortified city.
U+825A (F2.12 艚 _Ts'u_) 艚船 (Page 586 艚) A junk, a smack (Kangxi: 小船也); _pak ts'o shun_ (baak6 cou4 syun4 白艚船), sea-going junks; _ts'o tsai_ (cou4 zai2 艚仔), small junks.

U+87AC (F2.12 螬 _Ts'u_) 虫名 (Page 587 螬) ~~Maggots~~ ``Scarabs`` in ordure (Kangxi: 蟦蠐螬, 在糞土中者); _ts'ai ts'o_ (cai4 cou4 蠐螬), grubs found in filth, which curl themselves up. 【螬】 (dzaw) (_noun-like_) grub Used in 蠐螬.

cou5.text: U+5132 May appear with 者 dotless (+15 assumed here) or dotted (+16), depending on the font.

cyu4.text: U+5132 May appear with 者 dotless (+15 assumed here) or dotted (+16), depending on the font. (Page 32* 儲)

cyu5.text: U+5132 May appear with 者 dotless (+15 assumed here) or dotted (+16), depending on the font. cyu4 (per 《廣韻》直魚切) (Page 32* 儲 _Ch'u_ (cyu4); here normalised to _Ch'u_ (cyu5)) To collect (Kangxi: 偫也); to provide for need; furnished; to assist, to act as second to (Kangxi: 副也); _ch'u wai_ (cyu5 wai6 儲位) an heir-apparent; _ch'u kwan_ (cyu5 gwan1 儲君), ~~son of~~ a prince. Williams' 儲君: Not a son of a prince, but an heir to a monarch. To await. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【儲】 (drjo) (_verb-like_) to collect

cuk1.text: U+4FC3 趣 (for "urge" sense) (Page 600 促) To constrain (Kangxi: 局促小貌); near, short, close (Kangxi: 迫也近也密也); urgent, pressed upon (Kangxi: 刺促); small, shortened; hampered; _kuk ts'uk_ (guk6 cuk1 侷促) a narrow, small space; _ts'uk sat t'am sam_ (cuk1 sat1 taam4 sam1 促膝談心), to draw together and talk, as long absent friends.

U+6149 huk1 (per 《廣韻》許竹切) (F1.64 慉) 亦養也 (Page 36* 慉) To sustain (Kangxi: 慉養也); to encourage (Kangxi: 起也); ~~_ch'uk kiu_ (cuk1 giu1 慉驕), to excite pride;~~ ~~_ch'uk no_ (cuk1 nou6 慉怒) to stir up anger.~~ Broadly, there are two interpretations of this character: To uplift (《說文》起也, defended by 段玉裁's annotations); and To sustain or to cherish (per 《朱傳》, 《箋》, 《正義》 as appears in Kangxi). Williams' 慉驕: This is a misreading of Kangxi 慉驕也, which is an explanation of meaning (to be punctuated 慉、驕也), rather than an example of a two-character phrase. 【慉】 (xjuwk) (_verb-like_) to cherish, to uplift
U+6410 (F1.64 搐) 宰制也 (Page 36* 搐) To drag along, to pull by force, as one drags an ox (Kangxi: 牽制也); to shake rudely (Kangxi: 搐謂動而痛也).

U+675F (F1.64 束) 束縛約束又束脩束帶 (Page 36* 束) To bind or bundle a number of things (Kangxi: 縛也); to tie up in a sheaf; to coerce, to restrain; a bundle, a sheaf; _ch'uk ~~fok~~ ``pok``_ (cuk1 bok3 束縛) to cord up; _kun ch'uk_ (gun2 cuk1 管束) to restrain those under one; _ch'uk sau_ (cuk1 sau1 束修), a teacher's pay; _ch'uk shau toi pai_ (cuk1 sau2 doi6 bai6 束手待斃) in the utmost distress, no work. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 朿 to 束. Williams' 束縛: Changed _fok_ (fok3) to common reading _pok_ (bok3). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【束】 (syowk) (_verb-like_) to bundle, to bind

U+6A15 𣙙 (F1.64 𣙙) 樸𣙙小木也 (Page 600 𣙙; here normalised to 樕) A small tree, a shrub, a sapling (Kangxi: 樸樕小木也); _puk ts'uk_ (buk6 cuk1 樸樕) small trees, suitable for posts. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰木敇 to 𣙙. 【樕】 (suwk) (_noun-like_) oak (_noun-like_) shrub
U+23659 (F1.64 𣙙) 樸𣙙小木也 (Page 600 𣙙) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰木敇 to 𣙙.

U+755C (F1.64 畜) 畜養 (Page 36* 畜) To rear, to feed, to raise (Kangxi: 養也); to domesticate, to herd; to cultivate; to restrain (Kangxi: 畜止也); to bear with (Kangxi: 容也); to obey (Kangxi: 順也); domestic animals (Kangxi: 六畜); _ch'uk shang_ (cuk1 sang1 畜牲), you brute! _luk ch'uk_ (luk6 cuk1 六畜) the six domestic animals; _ch'uk yeung_ (cuk1 joeng5 畜養), to raise cattle. (Page 36* 蓄; here corrected to 畜) to nourish, to rear; _ch'uk a t'au_ (cuk1 aa1 tau4 畜丫頭), to bring up slave girls. Much confusion exists in relation to the Middle Chinese readings of this character. 《廣韻》 has two headline readings: 丑救切 _ch'au_ (cau3) for the "livestock" sense; and 許竹切 _huk_ (huk1) for the "rear" sense. But within these two entries, it also provides alternative readings (又許宥許六丑六三切 and 又丑六許救二切 respectively), and it is unclear whether these are intended as cross-references or as genuine alternatives for the senses given. Kangxi has a hodgepodge: 丑六切 _ch'uk_ (cuk1) for "rearing-field" (田畜也), "gather" (積也), and "restrain" (止也), among other senses; 許六切 _huk_ (huk1) for "rear" (養也), "accommodate" (容也), "livestock" (六畜) (with a note that 今人𠀤讀作昌六反), and "obey" (順也), among other senses; 許救切 _yau_ (jau3) for "non-working livestock" (養之曰畜用之曰牲); and 丑救切 _ch'au_ (cau3) for "domesticated animal" (在野曰獸在家曰畜). Modern Cantonese simply merges all readings into _ch'uk_ (cuk1). 【畜-noun】 (trhjuwH, xjuwk) (_noun-like_) livestock 【畜-verb】 (xjuwk) (_verb-like_) to rear, to nurture (_verb-like_) to accommodate

U+77D7 May appear with components as the right component of 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.64 矗) 聳上也高起也又直也齊也 (Page 36* 矗) High, eminent (Kangxi: 聳上貌, 高起也); equal, even (Kangxi: 齊也), straight (Kangxi: 直也, 長直貌); to raise; luxuriant growth (Kangxi: 草木盛也).

U+7C0C 𫂙 (Page 600 𫂙; here normalised to 簌) To sift, and cause to shower down (Kangxi: 篩也); close, thickset (Kangxi: 茂密貌); showering down, like autumnal leaves blown by the wind. Fan Wan entry: The headword 𫂙 is a misprint of 蔌.
U+2B099 (Page 600 𫂙) Fan Wan entry: The headword 𫂙 is a misprint of 蔌.

U+84C4 畜 (F1.64 蓄) 積也藏也 (Page 36* 畜; here normalised to 蓄) to gather (Kangxi 畜: 積也); (Page 36* 蓄) To collect, to accumulate, to hoard (Kangxi: 積也, 聚也藏也); _ch'uk tsik_ (cuk1 zik1 蓄積) to collect together; Used in 蓄意 (premeditated, deliberate). 【蓄】 (xjuwk, trhjuwk) (_verb-like_) to hoard

U+850C (F1.64 蔌) 果名 (F1.64 蔌) 窶陋也 (Page 600 蔌) Vegetables, legumes, kitchen herbs (Kangxi: 菜謂之蔌, 蔌者菜茹之總名); _ts'oi ts'uk_ (coi3 cuk1 菜蔌) vegetables; _ts'uk ts'uk_ (cuk1 cuk1 蔌蔌) mean (Kangxi: 蔌蔌陋也); _ts'uk ts'uk fung wai_ (cuk1 cuk1 fung1 wai1 蔌蔌風威), the stern wind whistling along (Kangxi: 風聲勁疾之貌). Fan Wan entry: The original entry with headword 蔌, explained 果名, is erroneous. Corrected headword 𫂙 to 蔌. 【蔌】 (suwk) (_noun-like_) vegetable (_adjective-like_) base, mean

U+89EB (F1.65 觫) 觳觫恐懼貌 (Page 600 觫) Horns beginning to start; to start, to tremble; _huk ts'uk_ (huk6 cuk1 觳觫) frightened, as oxen are in presence of lions; in a terrible fright (Kangxi: 觳觫懼死貌). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰角朿 to 觫.
U+89F8 (F1.64 觸) 突也犯也抵觸惑也 (F1.64 触) 同上 (Page 36* 觸)

zuk1.text: U+89F8 cuk1 (per 《廣韻》尺玉切) (F1.64 觸 _Ch'uk_ (cuk1)) 突也犯也抵觸惑也 (F1.64 触 _Ch'uk_ (cuk1)) 同上 (Page 36* 觸 _Ch'uk_ (cuk1); here normalised to _Chuk_ (zuk1)) To gore, to push as an ox (Kangxi: 牴也); to oppose, to excite; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` fan_ (zuk1 faan6 觸犯) to insult; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` no_ (zuk1 nou6 觸怒) to irritate; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` hi_ (zuk1 hei2 觸起), startling, to recollect suddenly; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` muk sheung sam_ (zuk1 muk6 soeng1 sam1 觸目傷心), distressing and affecting; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` hon_ (zuk1 hon4 觸寒), to take cold; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` shan chi lik_ (zuk1 saan1 zi1 lik6 觸山之力) extraordinary strength.

cuk1.text: U+8E59 顣 cik1 (per 《集韻》倉厯切 for "contracted" sense) zuk1 (per 《廣韻》子六切) (F1.64 蹙) 頻蹙愁貌又急也廹也 (F1.64 顣) 同上 (Page 600 蹙) Pressed upon, urged, impelled (Kangxi: 迫也, 近也); cramped, cribbed; ~~embarrassed;~~ to wrinkle, to rumple, to draw in; retractile; disquieted, uneasy, reduced to extremity; _pik ts'uk_ (bik1 cuk1 迫蹙) to press or urge vehemently; _ts'uk ts'uk_ (cuk1 cuk1 蹙蹙) contracted (Kangxi: 蹙蹙縮小貌, 蹙蹙小之貌). (Page 600 顣) Interchanged with the last (蹙), to frown (Kangxi 顣: 顣頞鼻頤促貌); to wrinkle the forehead; anxious, careworn. 【蹙】 (tsjuwk) (_verb-like_) to compress, to constrain (_verb-like_) to scowl

U+8E74 蹙 蹵 zuk1 (alternative per 《廣韻》子六切) zuk6 (per 《集韻》就六切 for "respectful" sense) (F1.65 蹴) 踐踏也 (Page 600 蹴) ~~Similar to the last two (蹙, 顣).~~ To tread on, to press with the foot (Kangxi: 躡也); to kick; _ts'uk in_ (cuk1 jin4 蹴然), reverential (Kangxi: 蹴然敬貌). 【蹴-voiced】 (dzjuwk) (_adjective-like_) reverential 【蹴-unvoiced】 (tshjuwk, tsjuwk) (_verb-like_) to trample, to kick

U+901F 遬 (F1.64 速) 急也廹也 (Page 600 速) Hasty, quick, hurried (Kangxi: 疾也); fleet, as a deer; with haste; to call, to invite (Kangxi: 召也); _tsik ts'uk_ (zik1 cuk1 即速) instantly, presently; _ts'uk hu_ (cuk1 heoi3 速去) go quickly; _ts'uk ts'uk_ (cuk1 cuk1 速速) no connection with (Kangxi: 速速不相親附之貌); friendless; _'m tsoi kom mong ts'uk_ (m4 zoi6 gam3 mong4 cuk1 唔在咁忙速) don't be so precipitate, flurried; _ts'uk yuk_ (cuk1 juk6 速玉) to hurry the steps of a guest; _fo ts'uk_ (fo2 cuk1 火速) as quick as possible. 【速】 (suwk) (_verb-like_) to invite (_verb-like_) to impel (_adjective-like_) speedy

U+906C (F1.65 遬) 齊遬 (Page 600 遬) ~~Similar to the next (蹙).~~ To step smartly to one's place; to dispatch, to hurry through; attentive, respectful (Kangxi: 猶蹙蹙也); alert, speedy (Kangxi: 剽遬疾也). Williams' "smartly": In the sense of "quickly". 【遬】 (suwk) (_adjective-like_) reverent
U+9863 (F1.64 蹙) 頻蹙愁貌又急也廹也 (F1.64 顣) 同上 (Page 600 顣)

U+9917 (F1.64 餗) 鼎實也易覆餗 (Page 600 餗) The prepared contents of a boiler or kettle (Kangxi: 鼎實也); pot-luck, dressed food. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰飠朿 to 餗. 【餗】 (suwk) (_noun-like_) cauldron-stuff

cung1.text: U+5145 May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+3) or 4 strokes (+4 assumed here), depending on the font. The two forms are actually separate characters in Kangxi (p.123#10 and p.124#1 respectively). Kangxi considers the first (𠫓 having 3 strokes) to be orthodox. 𠑽 (F1.58 充) 滿也塞也實也 (Page 40* 充𠑽) To fill (Kangxi 充: 實之也), to satiate (Kangxi 充: ...大充...過飽也)~~, to extend~~; to carry out, to continue (Kangxi 充: 行也滿也); to act, to fill an office (Kangxi 充: 充當也); to stop up, to stuff (Kangxi 充: 塞也); sufficient (Kangxi 充: 備也); fine (Kangxi 充: 美也), ~~long~~ ``grown``, high (Kangxi 充: 長也高也); _ch'ung ka fo_ (cung1 gaa2 fo3 充假貨) to foist in a poor quality of goods; _ch'ung mun ch'a u_ (cung1 mun5 caa4 wu4-2 充滿茶壺), fill up the tea-pot; _hau ngoi ch'ung kwan_ (hau2 ngoi6 cung1 gwan1 口外充軍), banishment beyond the frontiers; _ch'ung shat_ (cung1 sat6 充實) stuff it solid; _ch'ung ui_ (cung1 wui6-2 充會) to contribute to a society. Williams' 充實: Also "fulfilling" or "rich". To feign. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【充】 (tsyhuwng) (_verb-like_) to fill, to stuff, (_met._) to serve as (_adjective-like_) full, stuffed

U+51B2 (F1.58 沖) 和也又相沖 (F1.58 冲) 幼小曰冲年又相冲也 (F1.58 沖) 搖也 (Page 41 冲沖) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 忡 to 沖, and corrected 枂 to 相. Note that a separate entry with headword 沖 also exists, explained 搖也.

U+5306 忩 怱 悤 (F1.58 怱) 怱忙急遽也 (F1.58 匆) 匆匆喜色 (Page 608 悤; here normalised to 匆) Hurried (Kangxi 悤: 悤悤急遽也, 亦作忩俗作匆非), alarmed, agitated; _no hi ts'ung ts'ung_ (nou6 hei3 cung1 cung1 怒氣匆匆), excited to anger, exasperated; _ts'ung mong_ (cung1 mong4 匆忙), precipitate. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 忽 to 怱, and corrected headword 勿 to 匆.

U+5FE1 (Page 40* 忡) Sorrowful, grieved (Kangxi: 憂也); _ch'ung ch'ung_ (cung1 cung1 忡忡), distressed in mind. Fan Wan entry: The headword 忡 is a misprint of 沖. 【忡】 (trhjuwng) (_adjective-like_) grieved, distressed
U+6031 (F1.58 怱) 怱忙急遽也 (F1.58 匆) 匆匆喜色 Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 忽 to 怱, and corrected headword 勿 to 匆.
U+391D May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+5) or 4 strokes (+6 assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.58 㤝) 心動貌 (Page 40* 㤝) The mind ~~much~~ moved (Kangxi: 心動也); perturbed, agitated.
U+60A4 May appear as either ⿱囪心 (assumed here) or ⿱囱心, depending on the font. (Page 608 悤)
U+61A7 (F1.58 憧) 意不定也 (Page 41 憧) Unsettled in mind, disturbed, undecided (Kangxi: 意不定也); irresolute. 【憧】 (tsyhowng) (_adjective-like_) unsettled, undecided

zong6.text: U+61A7 Foolish (Kangxi: 一曰戇憧愚貌).

cung1.text: U+6A05 zung1 (alternative per 《廣韻》即容切) (F1.58 樅 _Tsung_ (zung1)) 植木以懸鐘磬者 (Page 607 樅 _Tsung_ (zung1); here normalised to _Ts'ung_ (cung1)) A sort of tall thuja or yew (Kangxi: 松葉柏身), which furnishes timber (Kangxi: 今太廟梁材用此); to beat, as a drum (Kangxi: 撞也). Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing and incomplete strokes in 鐘. Having teeth whereupon bells are hung (Kangxi: 言崇牙之貌樅樅然也). 【樅】 (tshjowng, tsjowng) (_noun-like_) fir (_adjective-like_) toothed

zung1.text: U+6A05 (F1.58 樅) 植木以懸鐘磬者 (Page 607 樅) Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing and incomplete strokes in 鐘. Used in 樅陽.

cung1.text: U+6C96 冲 翀 (for "soar" sense) cung4 (per 《廣韻》直弓切) (F1.58 沖) 和也又相沖 (F1.58 冲) 幼小曰冲年又相冲也 (F1.58 沖) 搖也 (Page 41 冲沖) Young, delicate (Kangxi 沖: 幼小也, 沖童也); harmonious (Kangxi 沖: 和也); deep (Kangxi 沖: 深也), (...); to rise in the air (Kangxi 沖: 飛也); to agitate, to move; to strike against (as things do in the water) (Kangxi 沖: 涌搖也); to dart against, to rush; to send (as a letter); _ch'ung ch'ong_ (cung1 cong3 沖？) to offend, to answer back; _ying ch'ung hak hoi_ (jing4 cung1 haak3 hoi6 迎沖客害) inauspicious calculations; _hi ch'ung tau ngau_ (hei3 cung1 dau2 ngau4 氣沖牛斗), his spirit rises to the skies; _ch'ung ch'ung_ (cung1 cung1 沖沖), sound of ornaments or other things tinkling against each other (Kangxi 沖: 聲也). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 忡 to 沖, and corrected 枂 to 相. Note that a separate entry with headword 沖 also exists, explained 搖也. Hanging down (Kangxi 沖: 垂飾貌). To offset, to counterbalance. Conflicting, incompatible. 【沖】 (drjuwng) (_verb-like_) to soar (_adjective-like_) childly (_adjective-like_) hanging (down) (_onomatopoeia_) crack

U+6D8C (F1.58 涌) 涌𣽸小海者也 (Page 40* 涌) A creek, a side stream coming into a river; _ch'ung kau_ (cung1 gaau3 涌𣽸) a creek (Fan Wan: 涌𣽸小海者也); _Lau po ch'ung_ (lau5 bo1 cung1 柳波涌), a creek near Kamli-fau (金利埠). Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰氵⿱㓁舌 to 𣽸.
U+249DA (F1.58 𤧚) 石似玉 (Page 608 𤧚) Fan Wan entry: Fixed incomplete stroke in headword 𤧚.

U+7481 May appear as either ⿰𤣩⿱囪心 (assumed here) or ⿰𤣩⿱囱心, depending on the font. 𤧚 (F1.58 𤧚) 石似玉 (Page 608 𤧚; here normalised to 璁) A pebble or stone, resembling a precious gem (Kangxi 璁: 石之似玉者). Fan Wan entry: Fixed incomplete stroke in headword 𤧚.

U+76C5 冲 沖 cung4 (alternative per 《廣韻》直弓切) (Page 39* 盅 _Chung_ (zung1); here corrected to _Ch'ung_ (cung1)) (...) a hollow vessel (Kangxi: 器虛也, 通作沖) (...); (Page 41 冲沖; here normalised to 盅) (...), hollow (Kangxi 沖: 虛也); 【盅】 (drjuwng, trhjuwng) (_adjective-like_) empty

zung1.text: U+76C5 鍾 (F1.57 鍾) 茶鍾酒鍾又聚也又姓 (Page 39* 鍾; here normalised to 盅) a cup or goblet; _tsau chung_ (zau2 zung1 酒盅), a wine-cup. (Page 39* 鐘; here corrected to 鍾 and normalised to 盅) _kuk chung_ (guk6 zung1 焗盅), a covered cup for tea; _yat chung ch'a_ (jat1 zung1 caa4 一盅茶), a cup of tea; (Page 39* 盅) A cup, (...), generally small and covered; _ngau yau chung_ (ngau4 jau4 zung1 牛油盅), a butter-cup; _ch'a chung_ (caa4 zung1 茶盅), a tea-cup and cover; _kat shi chung_ (gat1 si4 zung1 吉時盅), a custard-cup. Williams' 吉時盅: Custard is now 吉士 (gat1 si6-2) rather than 吉時 (gat1 si4).

cung1.text: U+7F7F tung4 (alternative per 《廣韻》徒紅切) (F1.58 罿) 掩兔之車 (Page 41 罿) A net to take birds, with a frame (Kangxi: 罬也, 幡車罔); the Fan Wan says, a frame to cover rabbits (Fan Wan: 掩兔之車). Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿱罓童 to 罿, and corrected ⿰犭奄 to 掩. 【罿】 (tsyhowng, duwng) (_noun-like_) bird-net

U+7FC0 cung4 (per 《廣韻》直弓切) (F1.58 翀) 直飛上也 (Page 40* 翀) To fly up (Kangxi: 直上飛也); to ascend to the clouds; _chi ho ch'ung siu_ (zi3 ho2 cung1 siu1 志可翀霄), aspiring talents. The reading _ch'ung_ (cung1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 敕中切, explained 上飛也.
U+8066 (F1.58 聦) 察也明也耳力也 (F1.58 聰) 同上 (Page 608 聰聦) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 𦖟 to 聦.

U+8070 May appear as either ⿰耳⿱囪心 (assumed here) or ⿰耳⿱囱心, depending on the font. 悤 聡 聦 (F1.58 聦) 察也明也耳力也 (F1.58 聰) 同上 (Page 608 聰聦) Quick at hearing, astute, ready, quickwitted; to perceive clearly (Kangxi 聰: 聞也明也通也聽也); to discriminate intelligently (Kangxi 聰: 察也, 耳之所聞旣順且審故謂之聰); _ts'ung ming_ (cung1 ming4 聰明), perspicacious, clever; _t'in ts'ung_ (tin1 cung1 天聰), natural gifts; _i ts'ung_ (ji5 cung1 耳聰) quick at hearing; _sing ts'ung_ (sing3 cung1 性聰) ready at learning. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 𦖟 to 聦. Hearing. 【聰】 (tshuwng) (_adjective-like_) hearing, well-hearing, (_met._) intelligent

U+8525 May appear as either ⿱艹⿱囪心 (assumed here) or ⿱艹⿱囱心, depending on the font. 葱 (Page 608 葱; here normalised to 蔥) An onion~~, garlic~~; a general name for alliaceous vegetables (Kangxi 葱: 菜也 etc.); a leek or light green (Kangxi 葱: 靑謂之蔥); ~~a vent, a tubular passage, a draft;~~ _ts'ung t'au_ (cung1 tau4 蔥頭), onions; _ts'ung luk_ (cung1 luk6 蔥綠) a pea green; _u ts'ung_ (wu4 cung1 胡蔥), small onions; _ts'ung pak_ (cung1 baak6 蔥白) the onion bulb; _ts'ung ~~ling~~ ``leng``_ (cung1 leng5 蔥嶺), the ~~Karakorum~~ ``Pamir`` Mts (Kangxi: 山名 etc.). Williams' "a vent, a tubular passage, a draft": This is a misreading of either Kangxi 《本草》蔥从悤外直中空有悤通之象也 (which is a description of what is probably the spring-onion) or Kangxi 氣通達也 (which is an adjective sense, not a noun sense). Williams' 蔥頭: The preferred term for "onion" in Cantonese is 洋蔥. Williams' 蔥嶺: Changed _ling_ (ling5) to vernacular reading _leng_ (leng5). Ventilated (Kangxi: 氣通達也). 【蔥】 (tshuwng) (_noun-like_) spring-onion (_adjective-like_) spring-onion-green

U+885D cung3 (possibly from Mandarin-matching for "toward" and "fierce" senses) (F1.58 衝) 向也水直撞也又衝要通衢也 (Page 41 衝) A path, a crossway (Kangxi: 通道也 etc.); ~~to support;~~ to move, to excite; to rise or rush against (Kangxi: 當也向也突也)~~, to overflow~~; abrupt; towards; a ~~car~~ ``battering-chariot`` (Kangxi: 車也 etc.); _ch'ung iu_ (cung1 jiu3 衝要) a thoroughfare; _shui ch'ung_ (seoi2 cung1 水衝), a canal in a city; _ch'ung to_ (cung1 dou2 衝倒), to overthrow; _ch'ung fan_ (cung1 faan4 衝繁), a place of great concourse. Fan Wan entry: The character 水 is likely erroneous. 【衝】 (tsyhowng) (_noun-like_) crossroad, thoroughfare (_verb-like_) to charge 【衝】 (cung1) To run a traffic light. 衝紅燈 (cung1 hung4 dang1): "Running a red light." 衝黃燈 (cung1 wong4 dang1): "Running an amber light."

U+8877 zung1 (per 《廣韻》陟弓切) (F1.58 衷) 心曲也情也 (Page 40* 衷) Inner garments (Kangxi: 裏褻衣也); the heart; ~~to adjust;~~ sincerity (Kangxi: 誠也), rectitude (Kangxi: 中也), (...); full knowledge of; _pat ch'ung_ (bat1 cung1 不衷), insincere; _ch'ung ch'eung_ (cung1 coeng4 衷腸), the feelings. Goodness (Kangxi: 善也). 【衷】 (trjuwng) (_noun-like_) inner-garment (_noun-like_) inner-feeling (_noun-like_) goodness (_adjective-like_) sincere

zung3.text: U+8877 (Page 40* 衷 _Ch'ung_ (cung1); here corrected to _Chung_ (zung3)) (...), equity; just, as a sentence (Kangxi: 不輕不重也通作中); Williams' "equity": As in compromise (Kangxi: 折衷平也).

cung1.text: U+9A18 (F1.58 騘) 鐵色馬 (Page 608 騘) Fan Wan entry: Fixed incomplete stroke in headword 騘, and corrected malformed 鐵.

U+9A44 May appear as either ⿰馬⿱囪心 (assumed here) or ⿰馬⿱囱心, depending on the font. 騘 (F1.58 騘) 鐵色馬 (Page 608 騘; here normalised to 驄) A dapple-gray horse (Kangxi: 馬靑白雜毛也 etc.)~~, a bay horse~~; _'ng fa ts'ung_ (ng5 faa1 cung1 五花驄), a piebald, fancy horse. Fan Wan entry: Fixed incomplete stroke in headword 騘, and corrected malformed 鐵.

cung2.text: U+51A2 塚 (for "mound" sense) zung2 (per 《廣韻》知隴切) (F1.61 冢) 大也長子長婦俱曰冢 (Page 41 ~~冡~~``冢``) The peak of a hill (Kangxi: 山頂也); a mound or tumulus (Kangxi: 墓之墳者亦曰冢); great, honorable, first, the eldest (Kangxi: 大也); _ch'ung tsz'_ (cung2 zi2 冢子) the eldest son; _ch'ung fu_ (cung2 fu5 冢婦), the wife; _ch'ung tsoi_ (cung2 zoi2 冢宰), the highest cabinet ministers. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 冡 to 冢. A high mound (Kangxi: 高墳也 etc.). A raised mound for an altar (Kangxi: 封土爲社). 【冢】 (trjowngX) (_noun-like_) mound (_adjective-like_) great, (_met._) prime

U+585A zung2 (per 《廣韻》知隴切) (F1.61 塚) 墓封曰塚 (Page 41 ~~塜~~``塚``) A grave, a tomb (Kangxi: 冢俗作塚); _fong ch'ung_ (fong1 cung2 荒塚), a deserted, unlucky grave; _wat ch'ung_ (waat3 cung2 挖塚), to rifle a grave; _ch'ung mo_ (cung2 mou6 塚墓) a grave; _ming ch'ung_ (ming4 cung2 明塚), a vaulted grave. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 塜 to 塚.

U+5BF5 𠖥 龍 (F1.61 寵) 愛也恩也尊榮也寵 (Page 41 𠖥; here normalised to 寵) Grace, kindness, affection (Kangxi: 一曰愛也恩也), regard, friendship; favor of superiors; to esteem (Kangxi: 尊榮也); to indulge to allow; _ch'ung oi_ (cung2 oi3 寵愛) delight in; _ch'ung fi_ (cung2 fei1 寵妃), a favorite concubine; _ch'ung sik_ (cung2 sik1 寵錫) a special favor of the king; _yan ch'ung_ (jan4 cung2 仁寵), loving-kindness. (Page 713 𠖥寵) The second form (寵) of this character is the most correct. _Ling ch'ung_ (ling6 cung2 令寵), your concubine; _tak ch'ung_ (dak1 cung2 得寵), to win a hushand's love. 【寵】 (trhjowngX) (_verb-like_) to favour, to adore

cung3.text: U+39E4 May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+5) or 4 strokes (+6 assumed here), depending on the font. (Page 713 㧤) To leap, to jump (Kangxi: 跳也). A colloquial word. At once, altogether; to push, to hit; _ch'ung ts'an_ (cung3 can1 㧤嚫), to run upon, to thump against; _yat ch'ung sam tau yau_ (jat1 cung3 saam1 dau2 jau4 一㧤三斗油), I don't know why he is all at once so very angry.

U+9283 May appear with 𠫓 having 3 strokes (+5) or 4 strokes (+6 assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.62 銃) 火炮 (Page 41 銃) ~~A piece of iron bored~~ ``The eye of an axe`` (Kangxi: 銎也, 斧穿也); a blunderbuss, a ginjall, a gun; _ch'ung p'au_ (cung3 paau3 銃炮) fire-arms; _ch'ung shau_ (cung3 sau2 銃手), musketeers; _shau ch'ung_ (sau2 cung3 手銃) small guns.

cung4.text: U+53E2 樷 (F1.60 叢) 聚也又林木茂盛也 (F1.60 樷) 古文叢字 (Page 609 叢) A thicket, a bushy place; crowded, as trees; collected, assembled (Kangxi: 聚也) ~~in numbers~~; bushy; _shang ts'ung kom to_ (sang1 cung4 gam3 do1 生叢咁多), crowded like a grove, jammed, crowded; _ts'ung lam_ (cung4 lam4 叢林), a wood, a grove, a place of concours; _sz' tai ts'ung lam_ (sei3 daai6 cung4 lam4 四大叢林), the four large Budhist temples in Canton; _lam lo yap fa ts'ung_ (lam4 lou5 jap6 faa1 cung4 臨老入花叢), to become depraved when old. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 占 to 古. 【叢】 (dzuwng) (_verb-like_) to collect (_noun-like_) thicket

U+5F9E 从 (F1.60 從) 隨也順也就也 (Page 608 從) To follow, to listen to (Kangxi: 相聽也), to comply with, to agree; to believe in; commencing from, the place of origin; a preposition, from, by, through, in; since, whence (Kangxi: 自也); a way, a manner; compliance; a follower; _ts'ung sam sho yuk_ (cung4 sam1 so2 juk6 從心所欲) what my heart desires; _'m ts'ung_ (m4 cung4 唔從), uncompliant, obstinate; _ts'ung loi_ (cung4 loi4 從來), heretofore; _mo ts'ung yap shau_ (mou4 cung4 jap6 sau2 無從入手), no way to begin; _yam ts'ung ni_ (jam6 cung4 nei5 任從你), as you please, do as you like; _i ts'ung_ (ji1 cung4 依從), according with; _~~huk~~ ``k'uk`` ts'ung_ (kuk1 cung4 曲從), constrained to obey; _ts'ung yau_ (cung4 jau4 從遊), to follow about, as a teacher; _ts'ung kam i hau_ (cung4 gam1 ji5 hau6 從今以後) hereafter; _ts'ung mi_ (cung4 mei6 從未) never, not at all; _ts'ung leung_ (cung4 loeng4 從良), a reformed prostitute, one who enters a family; _ts'ung siu_ (cung4 siu2 從小), since childhood; _sam ts'ung_ (saam1 cung4 三從), three compliances of a female to her father, husband``,`` and son. Williams' 曲從: Changed _huk_ (huk1) to common reading _k'uk_ (kuk1). To pursue, to partake in. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【從-follow】 (dzjowng) (_verb-like_) to follow, to pursue (_preposition-like_) from

zung1.text: U+5F9E

zung6.text: U+5F9E (F1.63 從) 隨從首從 (Page 608 從) A clan or family (Kangxi: 同宗也)~~, a succession of, as posterity~~; ~~to be second to, to follow after;~~ ~~attached to;~~ subordinate, secondary in rank; _ts'ui tsung_ (ceoi4 zung6 隨從) an aid or follower; _tsung kau pan_ (zung6 gau2 ban2 從九品), secondary ninth grade; _tsung pak_ (zung6 baak3 從伯) uncles descended from the same great-grandfather; _shau tsung_ (sau2 zung6 首從) principal and accessories; _tsung che_ (zung6 ze2 從者), followers. A follower.

cung4.text: U+60B0 zung1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.57 悰 _Tsung_ (zung1)) 情懷也 (Page 606 悰 _Tsung_ (zung1); here normalised to _Ts'ung_ (cung4)) To rejoice; ~~pleasure, delight~~ ``joy`` (Kangxi: 樂也); ~~to think over, to consider.~~ Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰忄⿳亠罒⿱十衣 to 懷. Mood, state of mind (Fan Wan: 情懷也).

zung1.text: U+60B0 (F1.57 悰) 情懷也 (Page 606 悰) Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰忄⿳亠罒⿱十衣 to 懷.

cung4.text: U+677E (F1.60 松) 木名 (Page 609 松) The fir tree (Kangxi: 木也 etc.); an emblem of longevity; _ts'ung cham_ (cung4 zam1 松針), pine leaves; _ts'ung heung_ (cung4 hoeng1 松香) or _ts'ung shu kau_ (cung4 syu6 gaau1 松樹膠), rosin, pitch; _ts'ung ming_ (cung4 ming4 松？), concrete pieces of rosin picked from pines; _ts'ung mau_ (cung4 maau4 松茅), bunches of pine leaves; _shui ts'ung k'eung_ (seoi2 cung4 koeng5-2 水松蔃), corky roots of cedar; _ts'ung tsz'_ (cung4 zi2 松子) pine aments; _ts'ung chuk mui_ (cung4 zuk1 mui4 松竹梅), the fir, bamboo, and plum, _met._ like three friends; _Ts'ung kong fu_ (cung4 gong1 fu2 松江府), the prefecture of Sungkiang (松江), in which Shanghai (上海) lies. A surname. 【松】 (zjowng) (_noun-like_) pine-tree, pine
U+6A37 (F1.60 叢) 聚也又林木茂盛也 (F1.60 樷) 古文叢字

zung1.text: U+6DD9 cong4 (per 《廣韻》士江切 for "water-flow" sense) cung4 (per 《廣韻》藏宗切 for "water-sound" sense) song3 (per 《廣韻》色絳切 for "water-emerging" sense) (F1.58 淙) 水聲 (Page 606 淙) The noise of water (Kangxi: 水聲也); murmuring, flowing water (Kangxi: 一曰水流貌); bubbling as a fountain (Kangxi: 水出貌).

cung4.text: U+6DDE (F1.60 淞) 水名 (Page 609 淞)

U+6F40 潨 zung1 (alternative per 《廣韻》職戎切) (F1.60 潨) 會也小水入大水也 (Page 609 潨; here normalised to 潀) Smaller streams flowing into the main river (Kangxi 潨: 小水入大水曰潨); ~~tributaries;~~ the meeting of waters (Kangxi 潨: 潨水會也). 【潨】 (dzuwng, tsyuwng, dzowng) (_noun-like_) confluence
U+6F68 (F1.60 潨) 會也小水入大水也 (Page 609 潨)
U+721E 蟲 tung4 (per 《廣韻》徒冬切) Intense heat (Kangxi: 熏也熱也, 旱熱也, 或作蟲, 爞爞炎炎熏也). 【爞】 (drjuwng, downg) (_adjective-like_) torrid

U+742E zung1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.58 琮 _Tsung_ (zung1)) 瑞玉 (Page 606 琮 _Tsung_ (zung1); here normalised to _Ts'ung_ (cung4)) An ancient badge ~~of princely dignity~~, eight or nine inches wide, octagonal, and having a hole in it (Kangxi: 瑞玉大八寸似車釭, 謂其狀外八角而中圓也).

zung1.text: U+742E (F1.58 琮) 瑞玉 (Page 606 琮)

cung4.text: U+79CD (F1.58 种 _Ch'ung_ (cung1)) 稚也又姓 (Page 40* 种 _Ch'ung_ (cung1); here corrected to _Ch'ung_ (cung4)) Young grain (Kangxi: 稚也); delicate, tender; _ch'ung chi_ (cung4 zi6 种稚) immature, beautiful, as children. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).
U+866B (F1.60 虫) 虫蟻 (F1.60 蟲) 同上 (Page 41 虫蟲)

U+87F2 虫 (F1.60 虫) 虫蟻 (F1.60 蟲) 同上 (Page 41 虫蟲) Term for featherless and hairless animals, including insects, frogs, snails, &c.; usually applied to insects only; _pak ch'ung_ (baak3 cung4 百蟲), insects generally. Legged animals (Kangxi: 有足謂之蟲無足謂之豸). 【蟲】 (drjuwng) (_noun-like_) insect, creature

U+91CD (F1.60 重) 復也叠也 (Page 41 重) To duplicate, to repeat, to reiterate (Kangxi: 複也疊也); to add; _ch'ung tip_ (cung4 dip6 重疊) to increase, numerous; _ki ch'ung_ (gei2 cung4 幾重), several, a series; _ch'ung ch'ung_ (cung4 cung4 重重), a succession, as peaks of hills; _ch'ung wai_ (cung4 wai4 重圍), to encompass; _ch'ung sheng_ (cung4 seng4 重城), a double wall, such as are at city gates. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 叠. 【重-level】 (drjowng) (_verb-like_) to repeat, to layer (_adverb-like_) once more

cung5.text: U+91CD cung5-2 (rising second in vernacular 重重地) (Page 40* 重 _Chung_ (zung6); here corrected to _Ch'ung_ (cung5)) Heavy, weighty (Kangxi: 毛氏曰凡物不輕而重則上聲); severe, heinous; _yau ki ~~chung~~ ``ch'ung``_ (jau5 gei2 cung5 有幾重) how heavy is it? _~~chung~~ ``ch'ung`` ta_ (cung5 daa2 重打), to beat severely; Williams (following Fan Wan) does not provide separate entries for rising-tone _ch'ung_ (cung5) and departing-tone _chung_ (zung6), whose distinction is still well preserved in modern Cantonese. I have split things up accordingly, noting that the "severe, heinous" sense is able to fit both readings (and, in particular, is rising in 重病, 重刑, etc.). Kangxi has commentary claiming that the rising and departing readings were anciently interchangeable, and that maintaining a distinction between them is 拘泥之過 an "error of clinging" on the part of Sung Dynasty scholars: 按說文柱用切厚也卽與輕重義同 集韻柱勇切愼也卽與鄭重義同 上去雖有二音𠀤無二義 古人三聲通用 必謂上去異訓不可通押此宋人拘泥之過也 However, both Zhengzhang and Baxter--Sagart reconstruct *three* readings for 重 in Old Chinese, corresponding to Middle Chinese level, rising, and departing. 【重-rising】 (drjowngX) (_adjective-like_) weighty, heavy (_verb-like_) to weigh

zung6.text: U+91CD 仲 (for Cantonese "still" and "and also" senses) (F1.63 重) 厚也又愼重輕重 (Page 40* 重) momentous (Kangxi: 毛氏曰...因其可重而重之與再重鄭重皆去聲); severe, heinous; decorous, grave; to regard, to give importance to (Kangxi: 尊也, 尚也); a sign of the comparative; (...) _chung ho_ (zung6 hou2 重好), still better; _shan chung_ (san6 zung6 慎重) ~~correct, well principled~~ ``prudent, careful``; _tsz' chung_ (zi6 zung6 自重) self-respect; _chung fu hi p'an_ (zung6 fu3 hei1 pan4 重富欺貧), to court the rich and despise the poor. Williams (following Fan Wan) does not provide separate entries for rising-tone _ch'ung_ (cung5) and departing-tone _chung_ (zung6), whose distinction is still well preserved in modern Cantonese. I have split things up accordingly, noting that the "severe, heinous" sense is able to fit both readings (and, in particular, is departing in 嚴重). Kangxi has commentary claiming that the rising and departing readings were anciently interchangeable, and that maintaining a distinction between them is 拘泥之過 an "error of clinging" on the part of Sung Dynasty scholars: 按說文柱用切厚也卽與輕重義同 集韻柱勇切愼也卽與鄭重義同 上去雖有二音𠀤無二義 古人三聲通用 必謂上去異訓不可通押此宋人拘泥之過也 However, both Zhengzhang and Baxter--Sagart reconstruct *three* readings for 重 in Old Chinese, corresponding to Middle Chinese level, rising, and departing. Further (Kangxi: 更爲也, 重再也). 【重-departing】 (drjowngH) (_adjective-like_) weighty (_verb-like_) to weight, (_met._) to esteem 【重】 (zung6) Even more, still. 重高過我 (zung6 gou1 gwo3 ngo5): "Even taller than me." 噉重危險 (gam2 zung6 ngai4 him2): "This is even more dangerous." 噉重污糟 (gam2 zung6 wu1 zou1): "This is even dirtier." 比佢重平 (bei2 keoi5 zung6 peng4): "Cheaper still, compared to that." Yet, still. 重嚟 (zung6 lai4): "Still coming at me?" 重污糟 (zung6 wu1 zou1): "Still dirty." 重未到 (zung6 mei6 dou3): "Still yet to arrive." 重有飯 (zung6 jau5 faan6): "There's still rice." And also, as well. 重有甜品送 (zung6 jau5 tim4 ban2 sung3): "And also there's dessert given for free." All these senses of 重 are commonly spelt 仲.

cyu2.text: U+51E6 (F1.46 處) 止居也又處置 (F1.46 䖏) 同上 (F1.46 処) 同上 (Page 32* 處処)
U+696E May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font.

cyu5.text: U+696E May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. cyu2 (per 《廣韻》丑吕切) (Page 33* 楮) A mulberry _(Broussonetia papyfera)_ (Kangxi: 榖也) from whose bark paper and cloth can be made (Kangxi: 江南人績其皮以爲布又擣以爲紙); _ch'u pai_ (cyu5 bai6 楮幣) paper money, a note; _ch'u i_ (cyu5 ji4 楮儀), money given to defray funerals; _ch'u ts'in_ (cyu5 cin4 楮錢), fancy paper burned in worship.

cyu2.text: U+8655 処 䖏 cyu5 (common in certain usages) (F1.46 處) 止居也又處置 (F1.46 䖏) 同上 (F1.46 処) 同上 (Page 32* 處処) To dwell, to reside (Kangxi: 居也); to be, to act, to occupy the place of; while in; to distinguish (Kangxi: 分別也), to manage, to judge, to decide on, to attend to, to do what is proper; to place; _ch'u tsz'_ (cyu5 zi2 處子) a young lady; _ch'u chi t'a_ (cyu2 zi6 taa1 處治他), do it for him, settle him, fix him; _seung ch'u_ (soeng1 cyu5 相處), familiar, friendly; _ch'u kwan shan_ (cyu5 gwan1 san4 處君臣), to be an officer; _nan ch'u_ (naan4 cyu2 難處), not easy to judge; _ch'u ~~kut~~ ``k'ut``_ (cyu2 kyut3 處決) execute him; _ch'u fu fu_ (cyu5 fu1 fu5 處夫婦) when married. I have changed _ch'u_ (cyu2) to the common reading _ch'u_ (cyu5) when it seems appropriate (although I am not aware of any logic that can be used to determine when this is the case). From personal experience: The classical reading _ch'u_ (cyu2) seems mandatory in 處決, 處置. The common reading _ch'u_ (cyu5) seems preferred in 共處, 判處, 懲處, 獨處, 相處, 處分, 處境, 處女, 處斬, 處方, 處死, 處罰. Either reading seems acceptable in 處世, 處事, 處理. Williams' 處決: Changed _kut_ (gyut3) to common reading _k'ut_ (kyut3). 【處-rising】 (tsyhoX) (_verb-like_) to reside, to stay (_verb-like_) to place

cyu3.text: U+8655 処 syu3 (vernacular for "place" sense) (F1.48 處) 處所 (Page 33* 處) A place, a spot (Kangxi: 所也); a circumstance, a condition; added to nouns, it often forms the concrete, as _ching kin t'a ling ch'u_ (zing3 gin3 taa1 ling4 cyu3 正見它靈處) we now see its spirituality; _pit ch'u_ (bit6 cyu3 別處) elsewhere; _ch'u ch'u_ (cyu3 cyu3 處處) everywhere; _ko ch'u_ (go2 cyu3 嗰處) there; _pin ch'u_ (bin1 cyu3 邊處) where? _tun ch'u_ (dyun2 cyu3 短處) a failing; _ho ch'u_ (hou2 cyu3 好處) well off. Williams' 嗰處: Changed _ko_ (go3) to modern _ko_ (go2). 【處-departing】 (tsyhoH) (_noun-like_) place 【處】 (cyu3, syu3) Here. 佢響處 (keoi5 hoeng2 cyu3): "He is here." 唔喺處 (m4 hai2 syu3): "It isn't here."

cyu4.text: U+8E87 May appear with 者 dotless (+12 assumed here) or dotted (+13), depending on the font. ceoi4 (uncommon) (F1.45 躇) 躊躇猶豫也 (Page 32* 躇) At a loss, perplexed; _ch'au ch'u_ (cau4 cyu4 躊躇), embarrassed, hesitating, stopping to consider (Kangxi: 躊躇猶豫也). Fan Wan entry: Corrected illegible character to 豫. Williams' "embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed".

cyu5.text: U+4F47 宁 竚 (F1.47 佇) 立也 (F1.47 竚) 久立又企也 (Page 32* 佇竚) To stand a long time (Kangxi: 佇久立也); to wait, to look for; _ch'u lap_ (cyu5 lap6 佇立) to stand up; _ch'u mong_ (cyu5 mong6 佇望) to be hoping, expecting. 【佇】 (drjoX) (_verb-like_) to stand long
U+5B81 cyu4 (alternative per 《廣韻》直魚切) (F1.47 宁) 門屏之間曰宁 (Page 32* 宁) A porch, a space between the gate and the screen (Kangxi: 門屛之閒謂之宁); 【宁】 (drjoX, drjo) (_noun-like_) entrance-space
