U+6775 cyu2 (per 《廣韻》昌與切) (F1.47 杵) 舂杵 (Page 33* 杵) A pestle, a beater (Kangxi: 舂杵也); _yat t'iu chung ch'u_ (jat1 tiu4 zung1 cyu5 一條舂杵), a single pestle; _ch'u k'au_ (cyu5 kau5 杵臼), mortar and pestle. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 春 to 舂. 【杵】 (tsyhoX) (_noun-like_) pestle

U+677C (F1.47 杼) 杼柚機之緯也 (Page 33* 杼) A shuttle (Kangxi: 機之持緯者); thin, as a wheel (Kangxi: 薄也); a fruit like a chestnut (Kangxi: 或作芧栗屬); _mun ch'u king lun_ (mun5 cyu5 ging1 leon4 滿杼經綸), learned as a full shuttle. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 袖 to 柚 and corrected 𢴰 to 機. 【杼】 (drjoX) (_noun-like_) shuttle (_adjective-like_) thin, tapered

U+67F1 (F1.47 柱) 屋之棟也 (Page 33* 柱) A post, a pillar, an upright support~~, a joist~~ (Kangxi: 楹也); a main dependance, a statesman; _kwok ch'u_ (gwok3 cyu5 國柱), a high officer; _k'ing t'in ch'u_ (king4 tin1 cyu5 擎天柱), an atlas of the state; _ch'u tan_ (cyu5 dan2 柱墩), the pedestal; _leung ch'u_ (loeng4 cyu5 梁柱), beams and columns, _met._ statesmen. Williams' 柱墩: Changed _tan_ (dan1) to _tan_ (dan2). 【柱】 (drjuX) (_noun-like_) pillar, post
U+7ADA (F1.47 佇) 立也 (F1.47 竚) 久立又企也 (Page 32* 佇竚)

U+7D35 苧 (F1.47 紵) 絲屬 (Page 32* 紵) ~~Contracted to ⿰糸⿳宀一 (紵 with the last stroke unwritten), unless it is used for the private name of Hienfung (咸豐).~~ A ~~coarse~~ kind of hemp (Kangxi: ~~檾屬細者爲絟粗者爲紵~~, 紵亦麻也); a ~~coarse~~ covering. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𮈔 to 絲. Williams' "coarse": According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, 粗者爲紵 should be replaced with 布白而細曰紵. Williams' "Contracted to ⿰糸⿳宀一 ": Likely a mistake. The personal name of Emperor 咸豐 is 奕詝, not 奕紵. 【紵】 (drjoX) (_noun-like_) ramie
U+7F9C (F1.47 羜) 未成羊也 (Page 32* 羜) A lamb five months old (Kangxi: 五月羔也); _fi ch'u_ (fei4 cyu5 肥羜), a fat lamb. 【羜】 (drjoX) (_noun-like_) five-month-lamb
U+82E7 (F1.47 苧) 苧麻 (Page 33* 苧) A plant _(Boehmeria tenacissima)_ grown for its fibres (Kangxi: 草名可爲繩); _ch'u ma_ (cyu5 maa4 苧麻), the grasscloth nettle; _ch'u kan_ (cyu5 gan1 苧根), its roots.

U+8CAF 宁 zyu2 (per 《廣韻》丁吕切) (F1.47 貯) 積貯 (Page 32* 宁; here normalised to 貯) to accumulate (Kangxi: 宁辨積物也). (Page 33* 貯) To hoard, to store up, to lay by in safety (Kangxi: 積也, 藏也); a hoard, a treasure; _ch'u tsik_ (cyu5 zik1 貯積) to accumulate; _ch'u tun_ (cyu5 deon6 貯頓) to store up goods; _ts'un ch'u_ (cyun4 cyu5 存貯) or _ch'u mai_ (cyu5 maai4 貯埋), to lay by. 【貯】 (trjoX) (_verb-like_) to stockpile

cyun1.text: U+5DDD 巛 (F2.19 川) 山川衆流也又川穿也謂穿地而流也 (Page 38* 川) ~~Mountain~~ streams, ~~perennial~~ brooks (Kangxi: 穿也穿地而流也); _ch'un lau pat sik_ (cyun1 lau4 bat1 sik1 川流不息) perennial, unceasing; _ch'un ngai_ (cyun1 ngaai4 川崖), a cascade. Williams' 川崖: Corrected _ngai_ (ngai4) to _ngai_ (ngaai4). Short for 四川. 【川】 (tsyhwen) (_noun-like_) stream

U+6751 邨 (F2.20 村) 鄕落曰村 (F2.20 邨) 同上 (Page 604 村邨) A hamlet, a village (Kangxi: 墅也, 聚落也); _heung ts'un_ (hoeng1 cyun1 鄉村), a village, the country; _ts'un tsuk_ (cyun1 zuk6 村俗) rustic, unpolished; _ts'un fu_ (cyun1 fu1 村夫), a villager, an uneducated man.

U+7A7F cyun3 (per 《廣韻》尺絹切 for "thorough" sense) (F2.19 穿) 貫也鑿也 (Page 38* 穿) To bore through (Kangxi: 委曲入也鑽也鑿也), to perforate (Kangxi: 通也穴也); to clothe, to put on; to string; _ch'un tsok_ (cyun1 zok6 穿鑿) to open a hole; _ch'un fong shai ~~fun~~ ``un``_ (cyun1 fong4 sai2 wun6 穿房使喚) a servant of all work; _ch'un sha po_ (cyun1 saa1 bou1 穿砂煲), to show the falsity of; _ch'un kam tai ngan_ (cyun1 gam1 daai3 ngan4 穿金戴銀), she wears gold and robes in silver; _ch'un chu_ (cyun1 zyu1 穿珠), to string beads; _ch'un hai_ (cyun1 haai4 穿鞋), to pull on shoes; _ch'un shan kap_ (cyun1 saan1 gaap3 穿山甲) the manis. Note that the (now obsolete) departing-tone reading is cognate with 串 cyun3. Fan Wan entry: Normalised ⿱⿰凿殳金 (with a stroke missing) to 鑿. Williams' 穿房使喚: Changed _fun_ (fun3) to modern _un_ (wun6). Williams' 穿砂煲: Now simply 穿煲. Williams' 穿山甲: Here, "manis" means pangolin. Thorough (in understanding) (Kangxi: 貫也). 【穿】 (tsyhwen) (_verb-like_) to bore through 【穿煲】 (cyun1 bou1) To have a secret or embarrassing information exposed. 【穿窿】 (cyun1 lung4-1) To develop a hole.
U+90A8 (F2.20 村) 鄕落曰村 (F2.20 邨) 同上 (Page 604 村邨) Commonly used in housing estate names in Hong Kong (e.g. 彩虹邨).

cyun2.text: U+5598 端 (F2.21 喘) 氣緊 (Page 38* 喘) To pant, to gasp (Kangxi: 疾息也, 喘息); the breath, the life; _ts'an ch'un_ (caan4 cyun2 殘喘), my poor life, ready to perish; _ch'un fai_ (cyun2 fai6 喘吠) to wheeze and cough. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱⿰臣⿱𠂉一糸 to 緊. To speak quietly or subtly (Kangxi: 微言). 【喘】 (tsyhwenX) (_verb-like_) to gasp

U+5FD6 (F2.22 忖) 村上聲思也度也 (Page 605 忖) To consider, to reflect on (Kangxi: 思也); to guess, to surmise (Kangxi: 度也); _ts'un tok_ (cyun2 dok6 忖度) to conjecture, to calculate. 【忖】 (tshwonX) (_verb-like_) to consider
U+651B (F2.21 攛) 擲也 (Page 38* 攛) Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword.

cyun3.text: U+651B 竄 (for "tempt" sense) cyun1 (per Kangxi 七丸切 for "tempt" sense) cyun2 (per Fan Wan) (F2.21 攛 _Ch'un_ (cyun2)) 擲也 (Page 38* 竄 _Ch'un_ (cyun2); here normalised to 攛 _Ts'un_ (cyun3)) to seduce, to beguile (Kangxi 竄: 誘人爲惡曰竄俗曰攛掇); (Page 38* 攛 _Ch'un_ (cyun2); here normalised to _Ts'un_ (cyun3)) To fling away (Kangxi 攛: 擲也); to tempt, to seduce to evil (Kangxi 攛: 俗謂誘人爲非曰攛掇); to get one to consent; _~~ch'un~~ ``ts'un`` chut_ (cyun3 zyut3 攛掇) to entice, to inveigle. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword.

cyun2.text: U+7AC4 (F2.21 竄) 也竄驅逐也 (Page 38* 竄) Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword. The first two characters 也竄 of the explanation are erroneous.

cyun3.text: U+7AC4 cyun2 (per Fan Wan) (F2.21 竄 _Ch'un_ (cyun2)) 也竄驅逐也 (Page 38* 竄 _Ch'un_ (cyun2); here normalised to _Ts'un_ (cyun3)) To sneak away, to hide, to skulk (Kangxi: 匿也逃也); ~~to kill~~ ``to exile, to punish`` (Kangxi: 放也誅也); to fumigate (Kangxi: 竄以藥薰之也); to secrete (Kangxi: 藏也隱也); ~~petty, weak, pusillanimous;~~ _~~ch'un~~ ``ts'un`` nik_ (cyun3 nik1 竄匿) to hide away; _t'o ~~ch'un~~ ``ts'un``_ (tou4 cyun3 逃竄), to run off; _~~ch'un~~ ``ts'un`` sit_ (cyun3 sit3 竄竊) to pilfer. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword. The first two characters 也竄 of the explanation are erroneous. In secret (Kangxi: 竄微也). To alter (Kangxi: 改易也). To put, to place. 【竄】 (tshwanH) (_verb-like_) to skulk (_verb-like_) to exile

cyun2.text: U+821B 踳 (F2.21 舛) 差謬也錯亂也 (Page 38* 舛) Erroneous (Kangxi: 舛錯也, 錯亂也), opposed (Kangxi: 舛偝也 etc.); to contradict; the 136th radical ("oppose")``;`` _ch'un ts'ok_ (cyun2 cok3 舛錯) wrong; _ch'un mau_ (cyun2 mau6 舛謬) ~~to deceive~~ ``absurd``. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 錯, and corrected malformed 亂. Williams' 舛錯: Now more commonly read _ch'un ts'o_ (cyun2 co3). Difficult (as a situation). 【舛】 (tsyhwenX) (_adjective-like_) contrary

cyun4.text: U+9044 cyun2 (per Williams) (F2.56 遄 _Ch'ui_ (ceoi2)) 速也 (Page 38* 遄 _Ch'un_ (cyun2); here normalised to _Ch'un_ (cyun4)) To go quickly (Kangxi: 往來數也, 疾也速也); haste, speed; ~~to go and come.~~ ~~Also read _chui_ (zeoi3) with the same meaning.~~ Williams' "to go and come": This is a misreading of Kangxi 往來數也. Here, 數 is to be read in entering tone, _shok_ (sok3), meaning "frequently". Thus, 往來數也 should be rendered "to go and come frequently", and defines the same sense as 疾也速也. 【遄】 (dzywen) (_adverb-like_) quickly

cyun3.text: U+4E32 (F2.23 串) 穿也貫也 (Page 38* 串) To string together (Kangxi: 物相連貫也與穿讀去聲通穿亦作串); to connect; leagued or banded; a string of; _yat ch'un ts'in_ (jat1 cyun3 cin4 一串錢), a string of cash; _yat ch'un p'au_ (jat1 cyun3 paau3 一串炮) a string of crackers; _ch'un kai_ (cyun3 gai3 串計) to intrigue; _ch'un t'au_ (cyun3 tau4 串頭), a plan to swindle; _ch'un mai_ (cyun3 maai4 串埋), to make a party or band. Cognate with the (now obsolete) departing-tone reading of 穿. To play a role, to perform. 【串】 (cyun3) To spell (of words in an alphabetic language). 點串啊 (dim2 cyun3 a3): "How do you spell it?" 唔識串 (m4 sik1 cyun3): "I don't know how to spell it." Arrogant, cocky.

U+5BF8 (F2.23 寸) 分寸 (Page 606 寸) The Chinese inch (Kangxi: 十分也), regarded as long as the middle joint of the finger; the tenth of a foot, and varies according to the _ch'ek_ (cek3 尺) (Kangxi: 十寸爲一尺); a very little; the 41st radical ("inch"); _ts'un sam_ (cyun3 sam1 寸心) or _fong ts'un_ (fong1 cyun3 方寸) the heart; _ts'un hau_ (cyun3 hau2 寸口), place of the pulse (Kangxi: 人手卻一寸動脈謂之寸口); _sik ts'un yam_ (sik1 cyun3 jam1 惜寸陰), improve every moment of time; _yeuk yau ts'un tsun_ (joek6 jau5 cyun3 zeon3 若有寸進) have got on a little --- a depreciating phrase; _ts'un_ (cyun3 寸), _kwan_ (gwaan1 關), _ch'ek_ (cek3 尺) are three locations of the pulse; _tak sau ts'un tsz'_ (dak6 sau1 cyun3 zi6 特修寸字) I have written a little note; _lo ch'ek ts'un_ (lo2 cek3 cyun3 攞尺寸) get its measure. In accounts, often used as a contraction of _sun_ (syun3) 算 to reckon. Williams' "improve": In the (now) dated sense of employing to good purpose. Williams' 特修寸字: Corrected _tak_ (dak1) to _tak_ (dak6). Williams' 攞尺寸: Corrected _lo_ (lo5) to _lo_ (lo2). 【寸】 (tshwonH) (_noun-like_) inch

U+7228 May appear with interior of the top-middle component as ⿱一丅 (+25 assumed here), ⿱一コ (+25), or ⿱一口 (+26), depending on the font. 㸑 cyun1 (alternative per 《集韻》七丸切 for verb sense) (F2.22 㸑 _Ts'un_ (cyun2)) 炊也 (Page 605 㸑 _Ts'un_ (cyun2); here corrected to _Ts'un_ (cyun3) and normalised to 爨) A furnace or cooking-place (Kangxi: 竈也 etc.); a mess, a table; to cook ``over a fire`` (Kangxi: 炊也) ~~gently~~; ~~to steam;~~ _ch'ui ts'un_ (ceoi1 cyun3 炊爨) to cook; _tsz' ki hoi ts'un_ (zi6 gei2 hoi1 cyun3 自己開爨), eats by himself; _fan ts'un_ (fan1 cyun3 分爨), to eat at separate tables; _t'ung ts'un_ (tung4 cyun3 同爨), at the same table, messmate; _tap ts'un_ (daap3 cyun3 搭爨), a boarder. 【爨】 (tshwanH, tshwan) (_noun-like_) cookstove 【開火爨】 (hoi1 fo2 cyun3) To start a fire stove (and, by extension, to cook).

U+91E7 cyun1 (common) (F2.23 釧) 釵釧手鈪也 (Page 38* 釧) A bracelet or armlet (Kangxi: 臂環也 etc.); ~~an amulet on the arm,~~ also called _t'iu t'ut_ (tiu4 tyut3 條脫) ~~a warder off~~. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing dots in 釵.

cyun4.text: U+50B3 (F2.21 傳) 傳授傳布傳遞又續也 (Page 38* 傳) To transfer (Kangxi: 轉也), to transmit, to hand down, to perpetuate (Kangxi: 授也續也); to deliver an order; to propagate (Kangxi: 布也); ~~to carry over, as a balance;~~ ~~to narrate, to record;~~ _ch'un shau_ (cyun4 sau6 傳授) to deliver to; _ch'un kau_ (cyun4 gaau3 傳教) to instruct in; _ch'un san man_ (cyun4 san1 man4 傳新聞), to tell the news; _ch'un in_ (cyun4 jin4 傳言), to send a message; _ch'un kung_ (cyun4 gung1 傳供), a local interpreter in a government office; _ch'un shut_ (cyun4 syut3 傳說) a rumor. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 授 and 遞. Williams' 傳教: Now to proselytise (especially Christianity). Williams' 傳言: Also a rumour. Williams' 傳說: Also a legend, a tale. To shift (Kangxi: 移也). To summon, to call. 【傳-verb】 (drjwen) (_verb-like_) to transmit (_verb-like_) to summon

zyun3.text: U+50B3 A relay station (Kangxi: 傳舍 etc.). A relay carriage (Kangxi: 遽也驛遞曰傳 etc.).

zyun6.text: U+50B3 zyun6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun for "biography/narrative/legend" sense) (F2.23 傳) 執愿切古書曰傳 (Page 38* 傳) Records or precepts of ancient times (Kangxi: 訓也賢人之書曰傳又紀載事迹以傳於世亦曰傳諸史列傳是也, 傳傳也以傳示後人也); _chun man_ (zyun6 man4 傳文), a record or story, an account of. Fan Wan entry: The supplied dissection 執愿切 is incorrect, as it results in the 陰去 reading _chun_ (zyun3), rather than the intended 陽去 reading _chun_ (zyun6). Corrected headword ⿰亻⿳甫一寸 to 傳. The pronunciation of 傳 in the term 傳奇 "legend" is contentious in Cantonese. The choices are: cyun4 kei4, held by a few who assert that 傳奇 means "transmitted oddity" from etymological considerations; and zyun6 kei4, which is defensible as "chronicled oddity", and is by far the accepted common reading in Hong Kong, as attested in the following songs: 似是故人來 (1991) sung by Anita Mui. 早班火車 (1992) sung by Beyond. A chronicle. A commentary. A biography, a narrative, a legend. Successively (Kangxi: 續也).

cyun4.text: U+5168 (F2.21 全) 完備也 (Page 604 全) Complete, entire, unbroken, perfect (Kangxi: 完也); all, the whole (Kangxi: 具也); to finish, to do all that is required; _ts'un nang_ (cyun4 nang4 全能), almighty; plenipotentiary, having full powers; _ts'un fu tsing shan_ (cyun4 fu3 zing1 san4 全副精神), with undivided energies; _ts'un sai_ (cyun4 saai3 全嗮) all complete; _ts'un ho_ (cyun4 hou2 全好), all right; _ts'ai ts'un_ (cai4 cyun4 齊全), complete throughout; _ts'un ka li sz'_ (cyun4 gaa1 lei6 si6 全家利事) may your whole family prosper; Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 備. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【全】 (dzjwen) (_adjective-like_) complete, whole

U+5B58 (F2.21 存) 留也在也 (Page 605 存) To preserve, to defend from injury; alive, to exist (Kangxi: 存在也); to lay by; to take care of, as of one's life; to inquire after (Kangxi: 恤問也), as one's health; to heedfully look after (Kangxi: 省也); _ts'un ha_ (cyun4 haa6 存下) lay this aside; _ts'un sam_ (cyun4 sam1 存心), to ~~be of the same~~ ``keep in the`` mind; _ts'un fan yat tim hau yui_ (cyun4 faan1 jat1 dim2 hau6 jeoi6 存返一點後裔) there remains one descendant only; _ts'un mong_ (cyun4 mong4 存亡), to preserve and to ruin; to continue and to lose; _i ts'un_ (ji3 cyun4 意存), a settled purpose; _ts'un lau_ (cyun4 lau4 存留), to detain, to keep back; _ko ts'un_ (gou3 cyun4 告存), to inform the emperor of one's health, as old or octogenarian officers should. Williams' 存留: Corrected _ts'un_ (cyun5) to _ts'un_ (cyun4). 【存】 (dzwon) (_verb-like_) to exist, to be (_verb-like_) to preserve
U+62F4 cyun1 (per 《廣韻》此縁切) (F2.20 拴 _Sun_ (syun4)) 揀也 (Page 605 拴) To select from, to pick out from with the hand (Kangxi: 揀也).

U+6522 攅 櫕 欑 zaan2 (possibly from Mandarin-matching in 積攢) zyun6 (per 《廣韻》在玩切 for "gather" sense) (F2.21 攢) 聚也又攢盒 (Page 605 ~~？~~``攅``; here normalised to 攢) ~~Interchanged with 欑;~~ to collect, to come together, to assemble (Kangxi 攢: 族聚也); to lay a coffin under a shed, and not bury it (Kangxi 攢: 不葬而掩其柩曰攢亦作欑); _ts'un hop_ (cyun4 hap6-2 攢盒) a box with partitions, used for sweetmeats``.`` The reading _ts'un_ (cyun4) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 徂官切, explained 族聚也 etc. The reading _tsan_ (zaan2) is used in 積攢 "accumulate/save". Whilst this reading appears in 《集韻》 as 子䍐切, the explanation 折也 is unrelated in meaning. Therefore, I would wager that zaan2 is from analogy with the Mandarin reading zan in 積攢. Williams' "Interchanged with 欑": The character 攢 can be written as 欑, but not the other way round.
U+6B11 (Page 605 ~~？~~``攅``; here corrected to 欑) ~~Interchanged with 欑;~~ poles and thatch to cover a coffin (Kangxi 欑: 輴盛柩之車欑猶菆也菆木于輴之四面至于棺上以泥塗之); (...)``.`` 【欑】 (dzwan) (_noun-like_) pole-heap

U+6CC9 (F2.21 泉) 源泉有源之水也 (Page 605 泉) A fountain, a spring (Kangxi: 濫泉正出正出涌出也 etc.); the headwaters of a river (Kangxi: 水原也); a department in Fuhkien (福建), Ts'iuen-chau fu (泉州府) or Chinchew (泉州); _~~tsing~~ ``tseng`` ts'un_ (zeng2 cyun4 井泉), a well; _lap ts'un_ (lap6 cyun4 立泉) a cascade (Kangxi: 瀑布曰立泉); _wong ts'un_ (wong4 cyun4 黃泉), elysiun; _kau ts'un_ (gau2 cyun4 九泉), hades; _fo ts'un_ (fo3 cyun4 貨泉), money, cash (Kangxi: 貨泉卽錢也); _ts'un lo_ (cyun4 lou6 泉路) ~~the shades~~ ``the underworld``; _lau ts'un yeung lo_ (lau4 cyun4 joeng5 lou5 流泉養老), to cherish one's old age. Williams' 井泉: Changed _tsing_ (zing2) to vernacular reading _tseng_ (zeng2). Williams' 泉路: Not "the shades", which are souls of the dead in Greek mythology, but their abode, i.e. the underworld. 【泉】 (dzjwen) (_noun-like_) spring, springwater

U+75CA 全 (in 痊癒 spelt 全愈) cyun1 (per 《廣韻》此縁切) (F2.20 痊 _Sun_ (syun4)) 病愈曰痊 (Page 604 全; here normalised to 痊) _ts'un u_ (cyun4 jyu6 全愈) entirely well. (Page 605 痊) Cured, healed, convalescent, recovered (Kangxi: 病瘳也, 病除也); _peng ts'un_ (beng6 cyun4 病痊), recovered from illness.

U+7B4C cyun1 (per 《廣韻》此縁切) (F2.20 筌 _Sun_ (syun4)) 裝魚之器 (Page 605 筌) A crab-trap; a bamboo trap for fish (Kangxi: 取魚竹器, 捕魚器今之斗回也), having a small entrance; _tak u mong ts'un_ (dak1 jyu4 mong4 cyun4 得魚忘筌) to get the fish and then forget the trap---ungrateful for benefits. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱⿰共士衣 to 裝. 【筌】 (tshjwen) (_noun-like_) fish-trap

U+8A6E cyun1 (per 《廣韻》此縁切) (F2.20 詮 _Sun_ (syun4)) 懌言也解喻也評論事理也 (Page 605 詮) To explain, to comment on (Kangxi: 解喩也); ~~select sayings;~~ to illustrate, to expound (Kangxi: 具也 etc.) ~~books~~; to discourse upon, to set forth and enforce; _chu ts'un_ (zyu3 cyun4 註詮), explained by notes. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 解, and corrected 愉 to 喻. Williams' "select sayings": This is a direct rendering Kangxi 擇言也, but does not sit right, given it is part of the "explain" sense.

U+8DE7 zaan1 (per 《廣韻》阻頑切 for "prostrate" sense) zeon1 (per 《廣韻》將倫切 for "stooped/curled" sense) zyun1 (per 《廣韻》莊緣切 for "kick", "stooped/curled", and "crawl" senses) (F2.20 跧 _Sun_ (syun4)) 伏也 (Page 605 跧) Stooping, creeping, bent down (Kangxi: 一曰卑也絭也); to kick (Kangxi: 蹴也); to go along doubled over (Kangxi: 跧莊匍匐也); ~~decrepit.~~ Prostrate (Kangxi: 伏也). To walk crookedly (Kangxi: 行曲).

U+8E72 ceon1 (per 《集韻》七倫切 for "dancing" sense) cyun2 (per 《集韻》粗本切 for "gather" sense) deon1 (possibly from Mandarin-matching) zeon1 (common) (F2.21 蹲) 踞也坐也又舞貌 (Page 605 蹲) To sit cross-legged; to sit cuddled up, to sit on the heels (Kangxi: 踞也); ~~to make antics~~ ``dancing, moving with regularity`` (Kangxi: 蹲蹲舞貌, 蹲蹲行有節); to sit haughtily; ~~placed together~~ ``gathered``, as scales of armor (Kangxi: 聚也). 【蹲-squat】 (dzwon) (_verb-like_) to squat 【蹲-dancing】 (tshwin) (_adverb-like_) (dancing) rhythmically

U+9293 cyun1 (per 《廣韻》此縁切) (F2.20 銓 _Sun_ (syun4)) 衡也度也 (Page 604 銓) To weigh, to measure (Kangxi: 衡也, 量也次也度也); to estimate the quantity of things, to value; to choose according to merit or value; a carpenter's plane (Kangxi: 平木器); _ts'un sun_ (cyun4 syun2 銓選), to select, as proper officers. 【銓】 (tshjwen) (_verb-like_) to weigh

cyun5.text: U+9C52 zyun1 (common) zyun6 (recast to departing) (F2.22 鱒) 赤目魚 (Page 605 鱒) A fish like the roach, with small scales ``and red eyes`` (Kangxi: 鱒似鯶魚而鱗細於鯶赤眼). In Japan, this character denotes the salmon. Williams' "In Japan, ... denotes the salmon": I am not knowledgeable enough in either Japanese or biology to say whether this is unreasonable. Note that both {lang=ja}鮭 _sake_ (salmon?) and {lang=ja}鱒 _masu_ (trout?) belong to the Salmonidae family. The trout. 【鱒】 (dzwonX, dzwonH) (_noun-like_) redeye-carp

cyut3.text: U+5352 (F2.24 卒) 急也速也 (Page 610 卒) An adverb, hastily, suddenly, abruptly (Kangxi: 急也, 匆遽之貌); urgently; _ts'ut kap_ (cyut3 gap1 卒急) in a hurry; _ts'ut ts'ut_ (cyut3 cyut3 卒卒) expeditiously; _ts'ut in loi to_ (cyut3 jin4 loi4 dou3 卒然來到) he suddenly came upon one; _ts'ut in kan_ (cyut3 jin4 gaan1 卒然間), all in a hurry. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𬨮 to 速. 【卒-abruptly】 (tshwot) (_adverb-like_) abruptly

zeot1.text: U+5352 (F2.54 卒) 終也又兵卒 (Page 609 卒) Those who execute orders (Kangxi: 隸人給事者); lictors, soldiers, underlings, menials; to conclude, to finish (Kangxi: 盡也, 終盡也); to die, to come to an end (Kangxi: 大夫死曰卒); to the end; _ping tsut_ (bing1 zeot1 兵卒) soldiers; _siu tsut_ (siu2 zeot1 小卒) camp followers; _peng tsut_ (beng6 zeot1 病卒) ~~disease is mortal~~ ``died of disease``; _tsut wai shin sz'_ (zeot1 wai4 sin6 si6 卒為善士) to the last he was a good man. 【卒-end】 (tswit) (_verb-like_) to end, to conclude (_adverb-like_) finally, completely 【卒-serviceman】 (tswot) (_noun-like_) serviceman (_noun-like_) troops 【卒之】 (zeot1 zi1) Finally, eventually.

U+637D cyut3 (common for literary senses) zyut6 (per 《廣韻》昨没切 for literary senses) (Page 610 捽 _Ts'ut_ (cyut3)) To grasp, to clutch, to seize (Kangxi: 手持也), as by the nape; to butt, to push (Kangxi: 觸也); to pluck out (Kangxi: 拔取也); _ts'ut k'i fat_ (cyut3 kei4 faat3 捽其髮) to clench his hair; _ts'ut keng_ (cyut3 geng2 捽頸), to take by the nape. The reading _tsut_ (zeot1) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 卽律切, explained 說文持頭髪也 etc. To conflict, to clash (Kangxi: 交對也). 【捽】 (dzwot) (_verb-like_) to grasp (_verb-like_) to pick (_verb-like_) to clash, to conflict 【捽】 (zeot1) To rub. 捽乾淨 (zeot1 gon1 zeng6): "Rub it clean." 捽瘀血 (zeot1 jyu2 hyut3): "Rub a bruise." 唔好捽眼 (m4 hou2 zeot1 ngaan5): "Don't rub the eyes."

cyut3.text: U+64AE zyut3 (per 《廣韻》子括切 for "pull" sense) (F2.24 撮) 兩指蹙聚而取曰撮又四圭曰撮 (Page 610 撮) To take a pinch with two or three fingers (Kangxi: 一曰兩指撮也, 三指取也); a pinch, a pugil; to take up a little; ~~to snatch,~~ to bring together (Kangxi: 撮摠取也); a little, a handfull; a measure of 256 (some say 60) grains of millet (Kangxi: 四圭曰撮三指撮之也六十四黍爲圭); brought together, selected (Kangxi: 蹙聚而捎取之也); _ts'ut iu_ (cyut3 jiu3 撮要) a selection of what is important; _yat ts'ut kom to_ (jat1 cyut3 gam3 do1 一撮咁多), as much as a pinch, a little; _yat ts'ut t'o_ (jat1 cyut3 tou2 一撮土), a piece of land; _ts'ut mai yat po_ (cyut3 maai4 jat1 bou6 撮埋一部) make them all into one book; _ts'ut sheung kiu_ (cyut3 soeng5 giu6-2 撮上轎), to force one into a sedan; _ts'ut hop_ (cyut3 hap6 撮合) to make~~,~~ an affiance, a partnership. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰扌⿳冖一取 to 撮, and fixed missing strokes in 蹙聚. Williams' "(some say 60) grains": Per 《孫子算經》六粟為一圭十圭為一撮. Williams' 撮上轎: Corrected _kiu_ (giu6-5) to _kiu_ (giu6-2). To extract. To pull (Kangxi: 挽也). 【撮】 (tshwat) (_noun-like_) pinch (_noun-like_) small-cap

U+731D 卒 (for "abruptly/hastily" senses) (F2.24 猝) 倉猝暴疾貌 (Page 610 猝) Similar to the last (卒). A dog rushing from a cover (Kangxi: 犬从艸暴出逐人也); abrupt (Kangxi: 言倉卒...突也); fierce, precipitate (Kangxi: 暴疾也); _ts'ong ts'ut_ (cong1 cyut3 倉猝) impetuous; ~~perplexed driven by many affairs~~.
U+855D May appear as either ⿱艹絕 (assumed here) or ⿱艹絶, depending on the font. (Page 610 ~~？~~``蕝``蕞)

zyut3.text: U+855D May appear as either ⿱艹絕 (assumed here) or ⿱艹絶, depending on the font. 蕞 cyut3 (per Williams) zyut6 (common) (Page 610 ~~？~~``蕝``蕞 _Ts'ut_ (cyut3); here normalised to _Tsut_ (zyut3)) A bundle or sheaf of coarse sedge used for straining or pouring spirits through (Kangxi 蕝: 蕝謂束茅而立之所以縮酒, 通蕞). Williams' reading of _ts'ut_ (cyut3) takes after the Fan Wan reading for 蕞 in the unrelated sense of "small". See 蕞 cyut3. Williams' "used for straining or pouring spirits through": This is a faithful rendering of Kangxi 所以縮酒. However, Kangxi does not give the sense from 《說文》, supplemented below. Bundled thatch used to indicate the place of attendees in a meeting (per 《說文》朝會束茅表位曰蕝).

zeoi3.text: U+855E cyut3 (per Fan Wan) zeoi6 (per 《廣韻》才外切) (F2.24 蕞 _Ts'ut_ (cyut3)) 小國曰蕞爾 (Page 610 ~~？~~``蕝``蕞) The second (蕞) is otherwise read _tsui_ (zeoi3); small, vile, base, insignificant (Kangxi 蕞: 小貌); ~~_tsui tsu_ (zeoi3 zeoi6 蕞聚) to collect together.~~

e6.text: U+35A1 【㖡】 (e6) A filler used to express uncertainty or hesitation. 飲㖡香片啦 (jam2 e6... hoeng1 pin3-2 la1): "We'll drink erm... jasmine tea."

faa1.text: U+82B1 華 (F2.73 花) 草木之葩 (F2.73 華) 華美亦作花 (Page 42 花華) A blossom, a flower (Kangxi 花: 草木之葩也); pleasure, vice; variegated, ornamental, carved; to exaggerate; _yat to fa_ (jat1 do2 faa1 一朵花), a flower; _yat chat fa_ (jat1 zaat3 faa1 一紮花), a bouquet; _ming fa_ (ming4 faa1 名花), ornamental flowers; _fa shang_ (faa1 sang1 花生), the ground-nut; _fa lo shui_ (faa1 lou6 seoi2 花露水), ~~lavendar~~ ``lavender`` water; _ang fa_ (ang2 faa1 㾘花), to enchase on metals; _fa kap tsz'_ (faa1 gaap3 zi2 花甲子) the sexagenary cycle; _fa tsz'_ (faa1 zi2 花子) a beggar; _fa lam_ (faa1 lam4 花林), a brothel; _fa fai_ (faa1 fai3 花費) to squander in dissipation; _fa nu_ (faa1 neoi5-2 花女), a girl; _fa tan_ (faa1 daan2 花旦), an actor who personates women; _fa min_ (faa1 min6 花面) to paint the face for acting; _fa fa shai kai_ (faa1 faa1 sai3 gaai3 花花世界) a thoughtless age; _fa fa kung tsz'_ (faa1 faa1 gung1 zi2 花花公子) a profligate rake; _fa wong_ (faa1 wong4 花王), the god who protects children, worshiped under the bed; _fa po_ (faa1 bou3 花布) chintz; _fa kai_ (faa1 gaai1 花街), an illuminated street; _fa wa_ (faa1 waa6 花話) exaggerated talk; _fa meng_ (faa1 meng4-2 花名), a nickname; _Fa k'i kwok_ (faa1 kei4 gwok3 花旗國) the United States; _fa ti_ (faa1 dei6-2 花地) the Flower Gardens near Canton. Williams' 一朵花: Corrected _to_ (do1) to _to_ (do2). Williams' 一紮花: Corrected _chat_ (zat1) to _chat_ (zaat3). Williams' 名花: Corrected _ming_ (ming1) to _ming_ (ming4). Williams' 花費: Also simply to expend, without the negative connotation of excess. Williams' 花面: Corrected _min_ (min3) to _min_ (min6). Williams' 花花公子: Now used as the translation of Playboy. Williams' 花王: Corrected _wong_ (wong1) to _wong_ (wong4). Williams' 花話: Corrected _wa_ (waa3) to _wa_ (waa6). To flower. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【花】 (xwae) (_noun-like_) flower, blossom 【花】 (faa1) Scratched. 揩花咗 (haai1 faa1 zo2): "Scratched from being brushed against." Messy or chaotic (like the shape of Mr Messy). 畫花嗮 (waak6 faa1 saai3): "All messed-up from being drawn over." (_mahjong_) The flower tiles, usually the four flora (梅蘭菊竹) and the four seasons (春夏秋冬). 正花 (zeng3 faa1): "A correct flower." 爛花 (laan6 faa1): "A bad flower." 唔打花 (m4 daa2 faa1): "Not playing with flower-tiles." A flush (card hand). 【花碼】 (faa1 maa5) Suzhou numerals (〇〡〢〣〤〥〦〧〨〩). 【花面貓】 (faa1 min6 maau1) Messy in the face.
U+83EF (F2.73 花) 草木之葩 (F2.73 華) 華美亦作花 (Page 42 花華)

faa3.text: U+5316 (F2.76 化) 變化王化敎化 (Page 43 化) To change, to influence so as to alter, to transform (Kangxi: 化敎行也 etc.); to melt, to digest; to convert; to pass into metempsychosis; to create; to transmute (Kangxi: 革物曰化); to barter; to exchange (Kangxi: 貨賄貿易曰化); to reform; _kau fa_ (gaau3 faa3 教化) to improve by instruction; _fa shan_ (faa3 san1 化身), to burn a priest's corpse; _~~ts'o~~ ``tso`` fa_ (zou6 faa3 造化) fate, nature; _ho ~~ts'o~~ ``tso`` fa_ (hou2 zou6 faa3 好造化) good luck; _wong fa_ (wong4 faa3 王化) the influence of law; _fa shang_ (faa3 sang1 化生), produced by metamorphosis, as insects; _fa yat_ (faa3 jat6 化日) peaceful times; _ch'au fa_ (caau1 faa3 抄化) to subscribe for a Budhist mass; _'m fa_ (m4 faa3 唔化) did not subscribe; also, indigestible; _shin ts'oi nan fa_ (sin6 coi4 naan4 faa3 善財難化) avaricious, niggardly. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 敎. Williams' 化身: Also reincarnation. Williams' 造化: Corrected _ts'o_ (cou3) to _tso_ (zou6). Williams' 好造化: Corrected _ho_ (hou3) to _ho_ (hou2), and corrected _ts'o_ (cou3) to _tso_ (zou6). Williams' 抄化: More specifically, for a monk to subscribe an alms-giver. To burn (paper offerings). 化元寶 (faa3 jyun4 bou2): "Transform paper-ingots by fire." 【化】 (xwaeH) (_verb-like_) to transform

faai3.text: U+5080 kui2 (per 《廣韻》口猥切) (F2.28 傀) 傀儡鬼戲人也 (Page 44 傀) _fai lui_ (faai3 leoi5 傀儡) puppets (Kangxi: 今爲木偶戲曰傀儡), vulgarly called _kwai tsai hi_ (gwai2 zai2 hei3 鬼仔戲). Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing component 厶 in 鬼. Williams' 傀儡: Corrected _lui_ (leoi6) to _lui_ (leoi5).

U+5672 (F2.28 快) 稱意也來也喜也又疾速也 (F2.28 噲) 燕王名又咽也又與快同 (Page 44 噲) To swallow, to drink ~~with avidity~~ (Kangxi: 一曰嚵噲也); ~~voracious;~~ the throat (Kangxi: 咽也) ~~stopped~~; ~~clamor of voices.~~ Name of a king (Kangxi: 人名燕王噲). Williams' "with avidity", "voracious": These are misreadings of Kangxi 一曰嚵噲也. In the source 《說文》 of this explanation, 嚵 means "sip/taste" or "beak". It is *not* used for 饞, gluttonous. Williams' "Name of a king": Kangxi puts this under a separate reading, _kwai_ (gwaai3), per 《集韻》古邁切 .

U+584A 凷 墤 (F2.28 塊) 片也片也 (Page 44 塊) A clod (Kangxi: 墣也), a lump; a piece of, a fraction; a classifier of ~~small~~ things which are flattish and usually shapeless, as boards, panes, slices, &c.; doltish; _yat fai yuk_ (jat1 faai3 juk6 一塊肉) a slice of meat; _yat fai ti_ (jat1 faai3 dei6 一塊地) a piece of land; _hom fai_ (ham6 faai3 冚塊) the whole space; _tai fai_ (daai6 faai3 大塊) the globe, nature (Kangxi: 造物之名曰大塊); _fai in mo chi_ (faai3 jin4 mou4 zi1 塊然無知), stupid. Used for the pronoun I (Kangxi: 一曰楚人謂我曰塊). Fan Wan entry: The headword 塊 is printed with the top dot missing in the component 鬼. Solitary (Kangxi: 孑也). 【塊】 (khwojH, khweajH) (_noun-like_) clod

U+5FEB 噲 (F2.28 快) 稱意也來也喜也又疾速也 (F2.28 噲) 燕王名又咽也又與快同 (Page 44 快) Contented (Kangxi: 稱心也可也), glad (Kangxi: 喜也); pleasure, cheerfulness; quick, hasty (Kangxi: 爽快也急疾也); alacrity; prompt; _fai lok_ (faai3 lok6 快樂) happy; _pat shong fai_ (bat1 song2 faai3 不爽快) indisposed, out of sorts; _fai ut_ (faai3 wut6 快活) delighted, good spirits; _fai fai_ (faai3 faai3 快快) quick! _fai tsz' shau_ (faai3 zi2 sau2 快子手), a headsman; _fai t'eng_ (faai3 teng5 快艇), a fast-boat; _fai ma lai_ (faai3 maa5 lai4 快馬嚟), bring it quickly; _fai pan_ (faai3 baan1 快班), a lictor in a magistrate's office; _fai tik fan loi_ (faai3 dik1 faan1 loi4 快滴返來), come back quick; _fai ma_ (faai3 maa5 快馬), a courier; _fai ts'ui_ (faai3 ceoi3 快脆) speedy. Williams' 快子手: Probably related to 劊子手. Frank, straightforward. Sharp. Soon. 【快】 (khwaejH) (_adjective-like_) pleased 【快脆】 (faai3 ceoi3) Quick. 咁快脆 (gam3 faai3 ceoi3): "That quick?" 行快脆啲 (haang4 faai3 ceoi3 di1): "Walk quicker."
U+7B77 快 (Page 44 快; here normalised to 筷) _fai tsz'_ (faai3 zi2 筷子) chopsticks; Used without the suffix 子 in certain compounds, e.g. 公筷 "public chopsticks" and 起筷 "raise chopsticks to start eating".

faak3.text: U+22AE6 𢪎 (Page 713 _Fak_ (faak3) implied; here assigned to 𢫦) A colloquial word. To whip; to beat with a flat board; to beat up, as eggs or jelly; _fak kon tseng_ (faak3 gon1 zeng6 𢫦乾淨) thrash it clean, as a carpet; _fak t'ung_ (faak3 tung2 𢫦桶), the tub into which grain is thrashed. I have chosen 𢫦 as canonical over 𢪎 for the better font support. 【𢫦】 (faak3) To whip, to swing abruptly, to flap violently. 𢫦甩啲塵 (faak3 lat1 di1 can4): "Flap off the dust." To whisk. 𢫦蛋 (faak3 daan6-2): "Whisk eggs."

faan1.text: U+53CD 仮 (for an octave lower in the musical sense) 𢓉 (for an octave higher in the musical sense) faan2 (alternative for phonetics sense) (Page 48 反 _Fan_ (faan2); here normalised to _Fan_ (faan1)) _fan ~~tsit~~ ``ts'it``_ (faan1 cit3 反切), to spell, to combine sounds; Williams' 反切: Corrected _tsit_ (zit) to _ts'it_ (cit3). Used in 反切 (back-dissection) or alone, referring to the method of indicating (Middle) Chinese pronunciation by two characters, marking the initial (consonant) and the final (rime) respectively. 【反-level】 (phjon) (_verb-like_) to flip, to overturn Used in 反切. 【反切】 (phjon tshet) (_noun-like_) back-dissection 【反】 (faan1) (_music_) Rough equivalent of solfege _fa_ half-sharp in 工尺譜 (Gongche notation).

faan2.text: U+53CD 阪 (F2.69 反) 反覆不順也 (Page 48 反) To turn back, to return (Kangxi: 反謂回還也); to be contrary, to rebel; to combine against; opposed to, but, contrary; again; _fan fuk_ (faan2 fuk1 反覆) repetition, to and fro, to retract; _seung fan_ (soeng1 faan2 相反), discordant; ``opposite, contrariwise;`` _tsok fan_ (zok3 faan2 作反), to rebel; _fan chiu_ (faan2 ziu3 反照) to reflect light; _fan min_ (faan2 min6-2 反面) to turn a cold shoulder; _fan kwat_ (faan2 gwat1 反骨) to backbite; _fan chun_ (faan2 zyun3 反轉), to turn over; _fan tau_ (faan2 dau2 反斗) clamor and play of children; _fan hau_ (faan2 hau2 反口) to unsay, to recant; _fan wai it shik_ (faan2 wai6 jit3 sik6 反胃噎食) the stomach rejects food. Williams' 反覆: Corrected _fuk_ (fuk6) to _fuk_ (fuk1). Williams' 反照: Corrected _chiu_ (ziu6) to _chiu_ (ziu3). Williams' 反轉: Corrected _chun_ (zyun2) to _chun_ (zyun3). Williams' 反斗: Corrected _tau_ (dau3) to _tau_ (dau2). Hence 玩具反斗城 for Toys "R" Us (with 反斗 being a pun on the flipped R in English). Williams' 反胃噎食: Corrected _it_ (jit6) to _it_ (jit3). To overturn (Kangxi: 覆也). Contrariwise. To reflect. 【反-rising】 (pjonX) (_verb-like_) to turn (over), to turn (back) (_verb-like_) to revolt (_adverb-like_) contrariwise

faan6.text: U+53CD faan3 (alternative per 《集韻》方願切) Used in 反反 (solemn, prudent). 【反-voiced】 (baenX) (_adjective-like_) solemn, prudent

faan1.text: U+5E61 (F2.66 幡) 慢也又幡然攺 (Page 47 幡) A banner, or streamer (Kangxi: 一曰幟也) with sentences, hung in temples or carried in processions; a duster or napkin (Kangxi: 幡書兒拭觚布也); ~~to move;~~ _ch'eung fan yat tui_ (coeng4 faan1 jat1 deoi3 長幡一對) a pair of streamers; _fan in_ (faan1 jin4 幡然), forthwith. (Page 47 旛; here corrected to 幡) (...) _wan fan_ (wan4 faan1 魂幡), a banner carried at funerals. ~~Used for the preceding (幡).~~ Fan Wan entry: The explanation 慢也 is erroneous. If it is a typo for 幔也, it is still not quite correct (a banner is not a curtain/screen). Williams' "duster or napkin": Specifically for 觚 "wooden writing-slips". According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, 拭 ("wipe", which is not present as a headword) is a vulgar substitute for 飾 ("decorate"), and this sense should instead be understood as "decorative cloth". Williams' "forthwith": Abrupt like an about-face (see supplemented sense below). Williams' "a banner carried at funerals": Appears erroneously as 魂旛 under 旛. A reversal, an about-face (Kangxi: 幡反也). 【幡】 (phjon) (_verb-like_) to flip (_verb-like_) to flap (about)

U+65DB faan4 (alternative per 《廣韻》附袁切) (F2.66 旛) 旗屬 (Page 47 旛) A streamer (Kangxi: 幅胡也, 旛幡也); _ts'ing fan_ (cing1 faan1 青旛), ~~or _wan fan_ (wan4 faan1), a banner carried at funerals~~. ~~Used for the preceding (幡).~~ Williams' 青旛: These are banners used in springtime rites, distinct from 魂幡 used in funerals, which should appear under 幡. Banners or flags in general (Kangxi: 旌旗總名也). 【旛】 (phjon, bjon) (_noun-like_) banner

U+756A 蕃 (for "foreign" sense) (F2.66 番) 夷狄之稱 (Page 47 番) a time, a turn, a repetition of (Kangxi: 數也遞也); ~~to reckon, to change;~~ ~~a tribe on the south, now applied to all~~ foreigners; _ki fan_ (gei2 faan1 幾番), several times; _fan wa_ (faan1 waa6-2 番話) foreign talk; _fan yan_ (faan1 jan4 番人), foreigners; _lo fan_ (lou5 faan1 老番) a foreigner. (_mahjong_) Points used to score a winning hand. 三番起糊 (saam1 faan1 hei2 wu4-2): "Three points minimum for a win." 【番】 (phjon) (_noun-like_) turn 【番子】 (faan1 zi2) (_mahjong_) The honours tiles (i.e. the unnumbered suit), consisting of the four winds (東南西北), the three duxes (中發白), and (optionally) the flower tiles.

U+7C53 faan4 (per Fan Wan) (F2.67 籓 _Fan_ (faan4)) 大箕也又蔽也 (Page 48 籓 _Fan_ (faan4); here normalised to _Fan_ (faan1)) A dust basket or large sieve (Kangxi: 大箕); to hide, to cover, to shade (Kangxi: 一曰蔽也).

faan4.text: U+7C53 (F2.67 籓) 大箕也又蔽也 (Page 48 籓)

faan1.text: U+7E59 (Page 47 繙) ~~To spread out~~; ~~to agitate, or wave, as the wind does~~ a flag (Kangxi: 𦆯繙風吹旗也); Williams' "To spread out": This is probably a rendering of Kangxi 《說文》寬也, which is a typo for 冕也, which perhaps should be 繙冤也 (neither of which I comprehend). In any case, this sense does not belong here, as the reading is _fan_ (faan4) per 《廣韻》附袁切. Williams' "to agitate, or wave, as the wind does": This is a misreading of Kangxi 𦆯繙風吹旗也. In, 𦆯繙 (same as 繽繙), 繙 means flag. It is 𦆯 (same as 繽, literally "disorderly") that supplies the meaning of "wave" or "flap".

U+7FFB 反 幡 (for "reverse/flip" senses) 繙 飜 (F2.66 翻) 翩翻羽飛貌 (Page 47 繙; here normalised to 翻) to translate; to explain; _fan yik_ (faan1 jik6 翻譯) to translate. (Page 47 翻) To fly (Kangxi: 飛也) to and fro; (...), to change (Kangxi: 反覆也通作幡又通作反); to revise a case; _fan fuk_ (faan1 fuk1 翻覆) changeable; vacillating; _fan on_ (faan1 on3 翻案) to rejudge a case; _tiu fan shang_ (diu6 faan1 saang2 調翻省); ~~to~~ to carry a case to the capital; To reverse, to flip, to turn around.

faan4.text: U+8543 (F2.67 蕃) 茂也滋也息也 (Page 48 蕃) Plants growing luxuriantly (Kangxi: 草茂也, 蕃滋也, 蕃息也); flourishing; plenty; many; ~~to settle;~~ 【蕃】 (bjon) (_adjective-like_) luxuriant, flourishing

U+85E9 蕃 faan1 (per 《廣韻》甫煩切) (F2.67 藩) 藩籬藩屏藩垣 (Page 48 藩~~轓~~) A fence (Kangxi 藩: 籬也), hedge, boundary; a frontier; ~~to ward off,~~ to protect``, to screen``; _fan li_ (faan4 lei4 藩籬), a wattle or hurdle; _fan wik_ (faan4 wik6 藩域) the frontiers; _fan t'oi_ (faan4 toi4 藩臺), the treasurer of a province; _fan un_ (faan4 wun4 藩垣), a wall inclosing; _fan p'ing_ (faan4 ping4 藩屏), a screen; a statesman. A screen (Kangxi 藩: 與蕃通屛也). 【藩】 (pjon) (_noun-like_) fence (_noun-like_) screen

faan1.text: U+8F53 藩 (Page 48 藩轓 _Fan_ (faan4); here corrected to _Fan_ (faan1)) a cover for a chariot (Kangxi 轓: 轓車之蔽也); a covered car; A large storage compartment of a chariot (Kangxi 轓: 車大箱也). 【轓】 (pjon, phjon) (_noun-like_) chariot-cover

U+8FD4 番 翻 (Page 47 翻; here normalised to 返) to return, (...); _fan shang_ (faan1 saang1 返生), resuscitated; _fan hu kwai_ (faan1 heoi3 gwai1 返去歸); to go home; _fai fan kwai_ (faai3 faan1 gwai1 快返歸) come home soon. Williams' 返生: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). 【返】 (faan1) To return, to go back. 返去 (faan1 heoi3): "Go back." 返屋企 (faan1 uk1 kei2): "Go home." 唔返 (m4 faan1): "Not going back." To revert to. 返發 (faan1 faat3): "Reoccur." 返生 (faan1 saang1): "Revive." To go to (in recurring attendance). 返工 (faan1 gung1): "Go to work." 返學 (faan1 hok6): "Go to school." 返廠 (faan1 cong2): "Go to the factory." 返寫字樓 (faan1 se2 zi6 lau4): "Go to the office." 冇工返 (mou5 gung1 faan1): "No work to go to." Back, in return (in reciprocation). 打返畀佢 (daa2 faan1 bei2 keoi5): "Call him back." 還返畀佢 (waan4 faan1 bei2 keoi5): "Return it back to him." 踢返畀佢 (tek3 faan1 bei2 keoi5): "Kick it back to him." 可以打返我 (ho2 ji5 daa1 faan1 ngo5): "You can hit me back." 找返兩蚊 (zaau2 faan1 loeng5 man1): "Two bucks' change in return." Back, again (in restoration of a good state). 好返 (hou2 faan1): "Well again" (after being sick). 搵返 (wan2 faan1): "Found it back" (after having lost it). 惜返 (sek3 faan1): "Kiss you back to being OK." 寫返好啲 (se2 faan1 hou2 di1): "Rewrite it better." 企返起身 (kei5 faan1 hei2 san1): "Stand back up." Suffixed after a verb to indicate a fresh instance of that action. 沖返個正涼 (cung1 faan1 go3 zeng3 loeng4): "Have a nice shower." 瞓返個晏覺 (fan3 faan1 go3 aan3 gaau3): "Take an afternoon nap." Suffixed after a verb to indicate a remainder. 留返畀佢食 (lau4 faan1 bei2 keoi5 sik6): "Leave it aside for him to eat." 得返一個 (dak1 faan1 jat1 go3): "Only one left." 【返轉頭】 (faan1 zyun3 tau4) To go back. 返轉頭攞嘢 (faan1 zyun3 tau4 lo2 je5): "Go back to get some stuff." 冇得返轉頭 (mou5 dak1 faan1 zyun3 tau4): "There's no going back." Back, retracing backwards. 行返轉頭 (haang4 faan1 zyun3 tau4): "Walking back." 做返轉頭 (zou6 faan1 zyun3 tau4): "Doing something but going backwards." Back, in return (in reciprocation). 踢返佢轉頭 (tek3 faan1 keoi5 zyun3 tau4): "Kick him back." Note that the reading _fan_ (faan1) for 返 is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 孚袁切, explained 回行也.

faan2.text: U+8FD4 反 (F2.69 返) 還也 (Page 48 返) To return, to come back (Kangxi: 還也, 復也); to go back; _fan hiu heung ha_ (faan2 hiu1 hoeng1 haa6-2 返嘵鄉下), gone to his village; _fan hu kwai_ (faan2 heoi3 gwai1 返去歸), gone home; _wong fan ki yat_ (wong5 faan2 gei2 jat6 往返幾日) how many days will you be gone? Williams' 返嘵鄉下: Corrected _hiu_ (hiu4) to _hiu_ (hiu1) and changed _ha_ (haa5) to modern _ha_ (haa6). Williams' 返去歸: Corrected _kwai_ (gwai4) to _kwai_ (gwai1). Williams' 往返幾日: Corrected _wong_ (wong2) to _wong_ (wong5). All three examples read like they belong under 返 faan1 instead. 【返】 (pjonX) (_verb-like_) to turn (back)

U+3906 faan3 (per 《廣韻》芳万切 and 《集韻》孚萬切) (F2.69 㤆) 㤆悔謂事旣成而生背心 (Page 48 㤆) Regret (Kangxi: 悔也); _fan fui_ (faan2 fui3 㤆悔) to regret an act; _fan chun min p'i_ (faan2 zyun2 min6 pei4 㤆轉面皮), to change the countenance; _fan sam_ (faan2 sam1 㤆心), sorry for. The other definitions given in Kangxi (惡心也急性也 and 急也) are not particularly illuminating.

faan3.text: U+6C3E faan6 (common) (F2.70 泛) 廣也泛濫也 (F2.70 汎) 廣也又汎舟 (F2.70 氾) 氾濫水溢也 (Page 49 氾) To overflow (Kangxi: 水延漫也), to fluctuate; in motion, agitated (Kangxi: 搖動貌); to float; _fan lam_ (faan3 laam6 氾濫) wide, as a deluge. Name of a river (Kangxi: 水名) in Honan (河南). Unsteady, bobbing (Kangxi: 不定之辭). 【氾】 (phjomH) (_verb-like_) to flood

faan4.text: U+6C3E A place name (Kangxi: 地名). A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

faan3.text: U+6C4E fung4 (alternative per 《廣韻》房戎切) (F2.70 泛) 廣也泛濫也 (F2.70 汎) 廣也又汎舟 (F2.70 氾) 氾濫水溢也 (Page 48 汎) To float (Kangxi: 浮貌); driven by the wind (Kangxi: 一曰任風波自縱也); name of a river (Kangxi: 水名). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 㲹 to 汎. 【汎】 (phjomH, bjuwng) (_verb-like_) to float (about)

U+6CDB 氾 汎 faan6 (common) (F2.70 泛) 廣也泛濫也 (F2.70 汎) 廣也又汎舟 (F2.70 氾) 氾濫水溢也 (Page 48 汎) Used for the next character (泛). (Page 49 泛汎) To float, to flow down (Kangxi 泛: 浮也一曰流也通作汎); to transport; extensive (Kangxi 氾: 普博也, 氾廣也); to spill over; _fau fan_ (fau4 faan3 浮泛) to float, superficial; _fan in_ (faan3 jin4 泛言), vague words; _fan chau_ (faan3 zau1 泛舟), to sail in a boat; _fan in_ (faan3 jin4 泛？), lightminded. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 㲹 to 汎.

U+7548 畚 (for vernacular "evil supernatural influences" sense) (Page 49 畚; here normalised to 畈) <ins> A colloquial word. </ins> Ill-luck, evil influences; ill-starred; things to be avoided when commencing anything; _fan cheuk_ (faan3 zoek6 畈着) to bring ill luck; _fan shan_ (faan3 san4 畈神), to exorcise, to expel or induce evil spirits to depart; _hi fan_ (hei2 faan3 起畈) to adore the spirits of the threshold. The Williams entry should be marked "A colloquial word", as it pertains to vernacular Cantonese. Williams' choice to spell it 畚 is a poor one, as that character is otherwise read _pun_ (bun2). I have normalised to 畈 for phonetic defensibility. Williams' 畈着: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). A field (Kangxi: 田畈, 田也, 平疇也).

U+8CA9 faan3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) faan5 (common) faan6 (common) (F2.70 販) 賤買貴賣曰販 (Page 49 販) To traffic, to deal in; _fan mai yan hau_ (faan3 maai6 jan4 hau2 販賣人口), to deal in men; _kwai fan_ (gwaai2 faan3 拐販) to get away children to sell; _fan tsai_ (faan3 zai2 販仔) or _fan fu_ (faan3 fu1 販夫), a chapman, a peddler; _fu fan_ (fu6 faan3 負販) to peddle about. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 責 to 貴 and 販曰 to 曰販. Williams' 販賣人口: Now rendered "human trafficking". A seller, a dealer (Kangxi: 買賤賣貴者). 【販】 (pjonH) (_verb-like_) to trade (in) (_noun-like_) trader

faan4.text: U+51E1 凢 (F2.67 凢) 皆也大槩也又庸常也今人鄙人爲凢夫輕賤之稱也 (Page 47 凡凢) All, everybody (Kangxi 凡: 最括也, 非一也, 皆也); common, vulgar (Kangxi 凡: 輕微之稱), usual (Kangxi 凡: 常也); generally, for the most part (Kangxi 凡: 大槪也); _fan yan_ (faan4 jan4 凡人), men; _fan kan_ (faan4 gaan1 凡間), the world; _chu fan_ (zyu1 faan4 諸凡), every one; _fi fan_ (fei1 faan4 非凡), extraordinary, clever; _tai fan sho yau_ (daai6 faan4 so2 jau5 大凡所有), people generally have it; _ha fan_ (haa6 faan4 下凡); to enter the world; _sz' fan_ (si1 faan4 思凡); to think of marrying (said of a bonze). Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 槩 and 鄙. Williams' 凡間: The mortal world, as opposed to the heavenly realm. Williams' 下凡: Changed _ha_ (haa5) to modern _ha_ (haa6). Specifically, to descend into the mortal world from the heavenly realm above. To total (Kangxi 凡: 計數也, 凡最計也). Mortal, earthly. 【凡】 (bjom) (_determiner-like_) all, every (_preposition-like_) in total (_adjective-like_) common
U+51E2 (F2.67 凢) 皆也大槩也又庸常也今人鄙人爲凢夫輕賤之稱也 (Page 47 凡凢) Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 槩 and 鄙.

U+58A6 (F2.67 墦) 墦間塚也 (Page 48 墦) A grave (Kangxi: 墦埌冢也); _fan kan_ (faan4 gaan1 墦間), at the graves, a sepulchre. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 間, and corrected 塜 to 塚. 【墦】 (bjon) (_noun-like_) grave

U+5E06 faan6 (per 《廣韻》扶泛切 for verb sense) (F2.68 帆) 風帆 (Page 48 帆) A sail of canvas (Kangxi: 舟上幔以帆風); _kwa fan_ (gwaa3 faan4 掛帆), to hoist sail; _fan po_ (faan4 bou3 帆布) canvas. 【帆-noun】 (bjom) (_noun-like_) sail 【帆-verb】 (bjomH) (_verb-like_) to sail

faan6.text: U+68B5 faan4 (common) (F2.71 梵) 羗戎吟聲也佛家經聲曰梵音 (Page 49 梵) Name of a bonze; the country of Magadha, whence Budha came; the language of the Budhists, Pali or Sanscrit; _fan yam_ (faan6 jam1 梵音), to chant prayers (Fan Wan: 佛家經聲曰梵音); _fan in_ (faan6 jin4 梵言), Pali; _fan ~~ch'at~~ ``shat``_ (faan6 saat3 梵剎) a monastery. Williams' 梵剎: Normalised _ch'at_ (caat3) to _shat_ (saat3). Tranquil, quiet (Kangxi: 華言淸淨正言寂靜).

faan4.text: U+7169 faan4-2 (rising second in vernacular 煩煩地) (F2.68 煩) 勞也擾也 (Page 48 煩) Troubled, annoyed, perplexed, heated (Kangxi: 熱頭痛也); to trouble, to intrude on (Kangxi: 干煩也, 煩勞也); troublesome, impertinent; grieved, sorry (Kangxi: 悶也); _to fan ni_ (do1 faan4 nei5 多煩你), I trouble you; _fan tai_ (faan4 daai3 煩帶) I trouble you to take this; _fan lo_ (faan4 lou4 煩勞), to trouble one to do a thing; _fan mun_ (faan4 mun6 煩悶) perplexed, grieved; _fan iu_ (faan4 jiu2 煩擾), interrupted, annoyed. (Page 48 繁; here corrected to 煩) _'m noi fan_ (m4 noi6 faan4 唔耐煩), unwilling to be troubled, can not endure; Williams' 煩擾: Changed _iu_ (jiu5) to common reading _iu_ (jiu2). Complex (Kangxi: 不簡也). 【煩】 (bjon) (_verb-like_) to vex, to bother (_adjective-like_) vexed, bothered (_adjective-like_) vexatious, bothersome

U+71D4 (F2.67 燔) 熱炙也 (Page 48 燔) _fan chek_ (faan4 zek3 燔炙) to roast meat. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𤍠 to 熱. Williams' 燔炙: Corrected _chek_ (zek6) to _chek_ (zek3). To roast, to burn (Kangxi: 爇也, 燒也, 炙也). 【燔】 (bjon) (_verb-like_) to roast, to burn

U+792C (F2.68 礬) 白礬 (Page 48 礬) Mineral salts proper four painting or dyeing (Kangxi: 礬石); _pak fan_ (baak6 faan4 白礬), alum; _ts'ing fan_ (cing1 faan4 青礬), copperas; _fan shek_ (faan4 sek6 礬石) alum shale; _tam fan_ (daam2 faan4 膽礬), blue vitriol; _~~fu~~ ``fui`` fan_ (fui1 faan4 灰礬), alum ash. Williams' 白礬: Corrected _pak_ (baak3) to _pak_ (baak6). Williams' 灰礬: Corrected _fu_ (fu1) to _fui_ (fui1).

U+7E41 煩 䋣 蕃 (F2.68 繁) 多也 (Page 48 蕃; here normalised to 繁) _fan mau_ (faan4 mau6 繁茂), abundant, prosperous; _fan in_ (faan4 jin2 繁衍), numerous progeny. (Page 48 繁) Much, numerous (Kangxi: 繁多也); many, confused, multitudinous (Kangxi: 繁雜也); a variety of affairs; _fan nan_ (faan4 naan4 繁難), wearisome; _fan wa_ (faan4 waa4 繁華), pomp, show; _fan fai_ (faan4 fai3 繁費) expensive. Complex. Abundant, flourishing (Kangxi: 繁猶盛也). 【繁】 (bjon) (_adjective-like_) abundant, manifold

U+81B0 燔 㸋 (F2.67 膰) 祭餘肉也 (Page 48 燔; here normalised to 膰) To roast meat for sacrifices (Kangxi 燔: 與膰通 etc.); (Page 48 膰) Meat roasted for sacrifice (Kangxi: 宗廟火熟肉, 膰祭肉); the remnants of a sacrifice, sent to princes (Kangxi: ...以饋同姓諸侯). 【膰】 (bjon) (_noun-like_) sacrificial-roast

U+8629 (F2.68 蘩) 草名白蒿也 (Page 48 蘩) A kind of southernwood or Artemisia (Kangxi: 白蒿也, 蘩皤蒿), whose decoction is sprinkled on silkworm eggs to hasten their hatching (Kangxi: 蘩所以生蠶, 蠶未出煑蘩以沃之則易出); _fan ho_ (faan4 hou1 蘩蒿), a plant grown like celery, and pickled in winter. Williams' 蘩蒿: This is dubious. 【蘩】 (bjon) (_noun-like_) hoary-mugwort

U+881C (F2.67 蠜) 虫名 (Page 48 蠜) A kind of cricket or grasshopper (Kangxi: 蛗螽蠜)~~, which goes by night~~. Williams' "which goes by night": This is a dubious reading of Kangxi 《本草》行夜一名氣蠜, which in any case is separate to the main sense.

U+8E6F 番 (Page 47 番 _Fan_ (faan1); here corrected to _Fan_ (faan4) and normalised to 蹯) A beast's foot~~step~~ (Kangxi 番: 獸足謂之番, 或作𨆌蹯); (Page 714 蹯) A paw of a plantigrade animal (Kangxi: 《說文》作番獸足也), as a badger; _hung fan_ (hung4 faan4 熊蹯), a bear's paw, which is esteemed a delicacy. 【蹯】 (bjon) (_noun-like_) paw

faan6.text: U+72AF faan6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.71 犯) 觸犯干犯侵犯 (Page 49 犯) To rush against (Kangxi: 抵觸也); to offend (Kangxi: 干也); to transgress (Kangxi: 僭也); to invade (Kangxi: 侵也, 勝也); to violate, to resist, to oppose; a criminal; _fan tsui_ (faan6 zeoi6 犯罪) to transgress; _fan fat_ (faan6 faat3 犯法) to break the laws; _fan yan_ (faan6 jan4 犯人), a prisoner``, a criminal``; _ts'au fan_ (cau4 faan6-2 囚犯) ~~to cage a criminal~~ ``a prisoner``; _ts'am fan_ (cam1 faan6 侵犯) to encroach on, to usurp; _yau fan tsun ngan_ (jau5 faan6 zyun1 ngaan4 有犯尊顏) I have offended you. Fan Wan entry: The headword 犯 is printed ⿰犭巳. Williams' 犯罪: Corrected _tsui_ to _tsui_. To face, to brave. 【犯】 (bjomX) (_verb-like_) to offend (against) (_verb-like_) to confront, to face (_verb-like_) to go over

U+7BC4 范 (F2.71 範) 法也式也模也則也隄防也 (Page 49 范) Used for the following (範). (Page 49 範) A rule, a guide; a custom, a usage; a mold, a pattern (Kangxi: 法也式也模也 etc.); to imitate; _mo fan_ (mou4 faan6 模範) a pattern, an exemplar (Kangxi: 範謂模範); _fong fan_ (fong4 faan6 防範) rule for guarding; _fung fan_ (fung1 faan6 風範) a popular custom. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing dot in 式. The headword 犯 is printed ⿱𥫗⿰車巳. Williams' 風範: Better rendered "manner" or "air". Bounds. To bound, to guard against. 【範】 (bjomX) (_noun-like_) mould

U+8303 (F2.71 范) 草名又姓 (Page 49 范) Herbs, grass (Kangxi: 草也); a bee or wasp (Kangxi: 蠭也); a surname (Kangxi: 姓). Williams' "Herbs, grass": More likely a specific *type* of grass. 【范】 (bjomX) (_noun-like_) bee

U+98EF faan5 (per 《廣韻》扶晚切 rising for verb sense) (F2.71 飯) 米飯 (Page 49 飯) Cooked rice; a meal; _shik fan_ (sik6 faan6 食飯) to eat; _tso fan_ (zou6 faan6 做飯) to cook; _tso fan_ (zou2 faan6 早飯) breakfast; _pin fan_ (bin6 faan6 便飯) a common meal; _shik fan 'm ts'ang_ (sik6 faan6 m4 cang4 食飯唔曾), have you eaten, (i. e. how do you do? the ans. is, _yau p'in_ (jau5 pin1 有偏), thank you.) _chong fan_ (zong1 faan6 裝飯) dish up the rice. Williams' 做飯: The preferred term for "cooking" in Cantonese is 煮飯. Williams' 早飯: Corrected _fan_ (faan3) to _fan_ (faan6). To eat (Kangxi: 餐飯也), to feed. The base of the thumb (Kangxi: 指本, 飯大擘指本也). 【飯-verb】 (bjonX) (_verb-like_) to eat, to feed 【飯-noun】 (bjonH) (_noun-like_) cooked-rice, rice, meal

faat3.text: U+6CB7 fat1 (alternative per Kangxi 敷勿切) (Page 714 沷) Cold (Kangxi: 寒也); to open sluices, to let water in over fields, for irrigating them (Kangxi: 一曰渫也通流也, 沷灌也).

U+6CD5 灋 (F2.72 法) 法度法則又刑法方法 (F2.72 灋) 古法字 (Page 51 法) A rule, a law, usage (Kangxi: 法常也, 法偪也偪而使有所限也, etc.); regulations, precepts; a punishment (Kangxi: 𠛬法也) or legal infliction; an art; skill, industry; ~~sect;~~ _fat to_ (faat3 dou6 法度) rule, regulation; _ying fat_ (jing4 faat3 刑法) penal laws; _fu fat_ (fu4 faat3 符法) to lay a spell; _fat tsak_ (faat3 zak1 法則) a guide; _fat mun_ (faat3 mun4 法門), the ~~Budhists~~ ``Dharmic gateway of Budhism``; _ch'ak fat_ (caak3 faat3 拆法) mode of concluding or repairing; _to fat_ (dou1 faat3 刀法) rules for carving. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed headword (with 虫 instead of ⿱灬去) to 灋. Williams' 刀法: Also (single-edged) swordsmanship. To emulate (Kangxi: 效法也). A method. Religious, supernatural, or sorcerous practices. A divisor. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【法】 (pjop) (_noun-like_) law (_noun-like_) emulate (_noun-like_) method, fashion (_noun-like_) divisor
U+704B (F2.72 法) 法度法則又刑法方法 (F2.72 灋) 古法字

U+767C 彂 (F2.72 發) 起也揚也舒也遣也 (F2.72 彂) 同上 (Page 50 發) To issue, to appear, to send forth, to germinate; to cause to go, to dispatch (Kangxi: 遣也)~~, throw out~~; to ferment; to show forth (Kangxi: 舒也揚也, 發越也, 宣發也, 見也), to manifest (Kangxi: 明也); ~~the~~ spring ``and summer`` (Kangxi: 春夏曰發秋冬曰斂); _fat ts'oi_ (faat3 coi4 發財), to make money; _fat shi_ (faat3 si5 發市), to have customers; _fat hak_ (faat3 haak3 發客) or _fat mai_ (faat3 maai6 發賣) for sale; _fat sam_ (faat3 sam1 發心), to give in charity; _fat shai_ (faat3 sai6 發誓) to take an oath; _fat tin_ (faat3 din1 發癲), crazed, beside himself; _ni fat wan me?_ (nei5 faat3 wan1 me1 你發瘟咩) are you crazy? _fat hi loi_ (faat3 hei2 loi4 發起來), to rise (as dough), to succeed, to get on; _fat fan un tsik_ (faat3 faan1 jyun1 zik1 發？？？) to dismiss home; _fat tan_ (faat3 daan1 發單), to issue ~~advertisements~~ ``an invoice``; _fat ch'iu_ (faat3 ciu4 發潮), to get damp; _ta fat_ (daa2 faat3 打發) to send away; _fat hi_ (faat3 hei3 發氣) angry; _fat fo_ (faat3 fo1 發科), to become a _kuyan_ (舉人); _fat kap_ (faat3 gaap3 發甲) to become a _tsunsz'_ (進士); _fat ho_ (faat3 hou6 發號) to make a signal; _fat p'ui_ (faat3 pui3 發配) banished; _fat fung_ (faat3 fung1 發瘋), leprous; _fat ting_ (faat3 ding1 發丁), to have descendants; _fat chi_ (faat3 zi2 發紙), to wet paper. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 楊 to 揚. Williams' 發客: Very dated. Williams' 發丁: Specifically, male descendants. To shoot (Kangxi: 䠶發也 etc.). To rise (Kangxi: 起也). To open (Kangxi: 開也). To go, to depart (Kangxi: 進也行也, 去也, 謂起屨也). To raise (Kangxi: 舉也, 興也). To till, to hoe (Kangxi: 伐也, 以耜擊伐). 【發】 (pjot) (_verb-like_) to send forth (_verb-like_) to start (up) (_verb-like_) to go forth (_verb-like_) to shew forth (_adjective-like_) fierce

U+9AEE (F2.72 髮) 毛髮 (Page 51 髮) Hair of the head (Kangxi: 首上毛也); _met._ herbs, trees, moss (Kangxi: 毛草也地理書云山以草木爲髮); _ch'uk fat_ (cuk1 faat3 束髮) to bind up the hair; _p'i t'au san fat_ (pei1 tau4 saan2 faat3 披頭散髮) disheveled hair; _seuk fat_ (soek3 faat3 削髮) to shave the whole head; _kit fat_ (git3 faat3 結髮) marriage of virgins; _shek fat_ (sek6 faat3 石髮) mosses; _fat ts'oi_ (faat3 coi3 髮菜) an algae used for food; _fat mong_ (faat3 mong5 髮網), female's false hair; _fi fat_ (fei1 faat3 飛髮) to clip the hair with a razor; _lau t'au pat lau fat_ (lau4 tau4 bat1 lau4 faat3 留頭不留髮) ~~married~~ ``to retain your head, do not retain your hair``. Williams' 削髮: Especially in preparation for becoming a Buddhist monk or nun. Williams' 髮網: Now "hairnet". Williams' 留頭不留髮: Clearly not "married". Instead, one of the slogans allegedly used during enforcement of the Ts'ing Dynasty policy for Han Chinese men to adopt Manchurian hairstyle (a shaved forehead plus a queue). The second half is 留髮不留頭, "retain your hair and you will not retain your head". 【髮】 (pjot) (_noun-like_) hair

fai1.text: U+5FBD (F1.27 徽) 美也 (Page 44 徽) A cord of three strands (Kangxi: 三糾繩也); a string; ~~queenly garments;~~ ~~a kind of sash;~~ good, beautiful (Kangxi: 善也, 徽者美善也); a banner; the stops on a guitar (Kangxi: 琴節曰徽); _Fai mak_ (fai1 mak6 徽墨) ink from Hwuichau (徽州); _fai yam_ (fai1 jam1 徽音), fine sounds; _fai ho_ (fai1 hou6 徽號) a flag. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿲彳⿱山糸攵 to 徽. Williams' "queenly garments": This is dubious. Although Kangxi has 與褘通, the character 褘 has two readings, and Kangxi appears to be referring to the second reading: 許歸切 _fai_ (fai1), for a queen's attire for sacrificial rites (Kangxi 褘: 后祭服也); and 雨非切 _wai_ (wai4), for a woman's bag(?) (囊也) or for a knee-cover (蔽膝也). Williams' "a kind of sash": This is a misreading of Kangxi 衺幅也. Here, 幅 is to be read _pik_ (bik1). Per 赤芾在股邪幅在下 from the Classic of Poetry, this is a type of binding applied to the lower leg (i.e. from the knee downward). Williams' "the stops on a guitar": Note that the Chinese zither (琴) is fretless, so these are not frets, but a set of inlaid markings away from the strings (providing a visual cue but no physical stoppage). Williams' 徽號: Now "emblem" or "badge". 【徽】 (xjw+j) (_noun-like_) three-stranded-cord (_adjective-like_) good, beautiful (_noun-like_) banner

U+63EE (F1.27 揮) 指揮發揮又揮霍 (Page 43 揮) To move, to shake (Kangxi: 振也動也); to be agitated (Kangxi: 奮也); to animate; to sprinkle (Kangxi: 灑也, 揮湔也); to scatter~~, to throw away~~; _fai ch'un_ (fai1 ceon1 揮春), ~~to write~~ new-year's inscriptions; _fai ho_ (fai1 hou4 揮毫), to write; _fai kam u t'o_ (fai1 gam1 jyu4 tou2 揮金如土), to spend money like dirt; _fai fok_ (fai1 fok3 揮霍) extravagant; _chi fai_ (zi2 fai1 指揮), a major in a Manchu corps, to point with the hand; _fai sha_ (fai1 saa2 揮灑), to sprinkle, to spend fast; _fai tsau_ (fai1 zau6 揮袖) written. Also read _wan_ (wan4), in the phrase _wan lun_ (wan4 leon4 揮掄), entire, unbroken (Kangxi: 揮掄全而不破也). Williams' 揮毫: Corrected _ho_ (hou1) to _ho_ (hou4). To direct, to command (Kangxi: 指揮也). To brandish. 【揮】 (xjw+j) (_verb-like_) to shake (_verb-like_) to brandish 【揮春】 (fai1 ceon1) New Year calligraphic banners. 寫揮春 (se2 fai1 ceon1): "Write New Year banners."

U+649D May appear as either ⿰扌為 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰扌爲 (+12), depending on the font. (F1.27 撝) 指也又發撝 (Page 43 撝) To rend open, to tear off (Kangxi: 裂也); to point ~~out~~; humble, unassuming; ~~used for the preceding (翬).~~ Williams' "humble, unassuming": Note that Kangxi's example 《易謙爻》撝謙 should *not* be read as defining 撝 as "humility" (even though the "humility" sense is probably derived from this). To signal, to direct, to command (Kangxi: 一曰手指麾也). 【撝】 (xjwe) (_verb-like_) to signal
U+6689 (F1.27 輝) 光輝 (F1.27 煇) 火光也 (F1.27 暉) 日光 (Page 43 暉) Same as the two preceding (輝, 煇); effulgent, glorious (Kangxi: 光也), as the sun. 【暉】 (xjw+j) (_noun-like_) sunlight, (_met._) brightness
U+7147 (F1.27 輝) 光輝 (F1.27 煇) 火光也 (F1.27 暉) 日光 (Page 43 煇)

U+7762 zeoi1 (per Williams) (Page 594 睢 _Tsu_ (zeoi1); here normalised to _Fai_ (fai1)) To stare at (Kangxi: 大視也) ~~in a fright~~, to gaze up at (Kangxi: 仰目也, 仰目視貌), to look angrily at (Kangxi: 恣睢恣意怒視也, 睢仰目怒貌). Williams' "in a fright": This is a misreading of Kangxi 《前漢五行志》萬眾睢睢驚怪連日 as a definition, when it is actually an example.

zeoi1.text: U+7762 (Page 594 睢) The reading _tsu_ (zeoi1) for 睢 is not attested in the Middle Chinese rime books. It is possible that Williams has mistaken it for 雎, which is what is present in Fan Wan.

fai1.text: U+7FEC (F1.27 翬) 雉也 (Page 43 翬) To fly (Kangxi: 大飛也) with noise ``quickly`` (Kangxi: 翬翬其飛疾羽聲也); a kind of colored pheasant (Kangxi: 一曰伊洛而南雉五采皆備曰翬 etc.); colored, adorned; _fai fai_ (fai1 fai1 翬翬), to fly up and show the plumage. 【翬】 (xjw+j) (_noun-like_) colourful-pheasant (_verb-like_) to fly

U+8F1D 暉 煇 (F1.27 輝) 光輝 (F1.27 煇) 火光也 (F1.27 暉) 日光 (Page 43 輝) Brilliant, refulgent, glorious (Kangxi: 光也火之光也), like the sun; to glisten; _fai in_ (fai1 jin4 輝然), spruced up, pleased; _fai in_ (fai1 jin6 輝現) exceedingly glorious. (Page 43 煇; here normalised to 輝) Luminous, splendid (Kangxi 煇: 光也, 燿光也), like fire (Kangxi 煇: 火之光); _fai kwong_ (fai1 gwong1 輝光), lustrous, gorgeous, like many lamps; _fai wong_ (fai1 wong4 輝煌), illuminated. 【輝】 (xjw+j) (_noun-like_) brightness

U+96B3 墮 墯 隓 (F1.27 隳) 敗也 (Page 43 ~~？~~``隳``) To destroy, to overth``r``ow, to break (Kangxi 隓: 俗作隳非, 廢也毀也損也, 亦作墮); to injure; _fai pai_ (fai1 baai6 隳敗) to destroy ~~and scatter~~. ~~Also read _to_ (do6), and~~ (...). Williams' "Also read ...": The reading _to_ (do6) is for a different sense (alternative form of 墮 "fall"), so the qualifier "Also" is incorrect. Kangxi 又通作墮 indicates that 隳 _fai_ (fai1) can be spelt 墮. It does *not* indicate a reading of _to_ (do6) for 隳 "destroy/collapse". A collapsed wall (Kangxi 隓: 敗城阜曰隓). 【隳】 (xjwie) (_verb-like_) to destroy

U+9EBE 戲 (F1.27 麾) 驅也旌旗所以指揮 (Page 43 麾) A signal or marking flag (Kangxi: 旌旗之屬所以指麾也, 或作戲, 戲大將之旗); to make a signal, to motion to (Kangxi: 與撝同以手指麾也); quick, hasty (Kangxi: 快也); _fai ha_ (fai1 haa6 麾下) your honor! (used by soldiers.) Fan Wan entry: Corrected missing strokes in 驅. 【麾】 (xjwe) (_noun-like_) signal-flag (_verb-like_) to signal (_adjective-like_) quick

fai3.text: U+75BF 痱 fai3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) fai6 (per 《廣韻》扶沸切 for 痱) (Page 713 疿) Pimples, small boils~~;~~``,`` sores from heat (Kangxi: 熱生小瘡 etc.); _it fai_ (jit6 fai3-2 熱疿) or _fai tsz'_ (fai3-2 zi2 疿子) prickly heat. The original departing reading fai3 is almost never heard, having been supplanted by vernacular rising fai3-2 in all senses. In modern usage, the spelling 痱 is much more common. However, this appears to be a merger of two apparently distinct (but synonymous) words in Middle Chinese: 疿, read 方味切 _fai_ (fai3), explained 熱生小瘡; and 痱, read 扶沸切 _fai_ (fai6), explained 熱瘡. In Cantonese, the distinction is hidden by the vernacular rising tone. In Mandarin, there is no distinction between 陰去 and 陽去 (both become fei). Of the two characters, 疿 is the older, as attested in 《黃帝素問》汗出見濕乃生痤疿. Whilst it is 痱 that appears in 《說文》, it is for the different sense of "stroke/paralysis", to be read 符非切 _fi_ (fei4). See 痱 fei4. Williams' "small boils": Corrected the following semicolon to a comma. There is only one sense to be defined here (i.e. the pimples and small boils are also from heat, not standalone). 【疿】 (pj+jH) (_noun-like_) heat-rash 【熱痱】 (jit6 fai3-2) Miliaria, heat rash.

fei4.text: U+75F1 Stroke or paralysis (Kangxi: 風病也). 【痱】 (bj+j) (_noun-like_) stroke

fai3.text: U+5EE2 fai3-2 (rising second in vernacular 廢廢地) (F1.32 廢) 絶也棄也 (Page 44 廢) A house in ruins (Kangxi: 屋頓也); to abandon, to throw aside (Kangxi: 廢舍也, 舍放置), to destroy, to lose; to repudiate, to disinherit; to stop (Kangxi: 止也, 止住也), to fail; obsolete, null; spoiled, corrupt; to depose; useless, degraded; _fai hu_ (fai3 heoi3 廢去) thrown aside, rejected; _fai mat_ (fai3 mat6 廢物) a useless fellow, good for nothing; _pun t'o i fai_ (bun3 tou4 ji4 fai3 半途而廢) to ~~reject~~ ``abandon`` when half done; _fai tsat_ (fai3 zat6 廢疾) disabled, incurable. To fall (Kangxi: 墮也). 【廢】 (pjojH) (_verb-like_) to cast away, to abandon, to abolish (_verb-like_) to waste (_verb-like_) to fall

U+6CB8 fat1 (per 《集韻》分物切 for "spring-bubbling" sense) (F1.32 沸) 火煮水滚又泉湧出曰沸 (Page 44 沸) To bubble, as boiling water (Kangxi: 涫也) or a spring (Kangxi: 觱沸泉出貌); _shui fai ch'ung ch'a_ (seoi2 fai3 cung1 caa4 水沸沖茶), the water boils, pour it on the tea. Also read _fat_ (fat1). Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 滚. Williams' 水沸沖茶: Corrected _shui_ (seoi3) to _shui_ (seoi2). Bustling. 【沸-departing】 (pj+jH) (_verb-like_) to bubble 【觱沸】 (pjit pjut) (_verb-like_) to bubble 【沸-entering】 (pjut) Used in 觱沸.

fei6.text: U+72D2 fai3 (common) fai6 (alternative) fat1 (common) (Page 53 狒) An animal of the monkey tribe, large and monstrous (Kangxi: 狒狒如人被髮迅走食人, 梟羊也 etc.); _fi fi_ (fei6 fei6 狒狒) the orang-outang or chimpanzee. Williams' 狒狒: Now refers to the baboon.

fai3.text: U+80BA (F1.32 肺) 肺肝 (Page 44 肺) The lungs (Kangxi: 金藏也 etc.); they are connected with metal; _fai ka_ (fai3 gaa1 肺家), the lungs. 【肺】 (phjojH) (_noun-like_) lung

U+82BE 茀 bui3 (alternative per 《廣韻》博蓋切 for "small" sense) (F1.32 芾) 蔽芾木盛也 (Page 44 芾) Luxuriant (Fan Wan: 蔽芾木盛也); small (Kangxi: 蔽芾小貌); 【芾-departing】 (pj+jH, pajH) (_adjective-like_) small (_adjective-like_) luxuriant

fat1.text: U+82BE 紼 (for "knee-cover" sense) (F1.86 芾) 芾冕諸侯服也 (Page 44 芾) read _fat_ (fat1) as _fat fat_ (fat1 fat1 芾芾) foliage abundant (Kangxi: 草木翳薈也, 芾芾茂也). (Page 49 紼; here normalised to 芾) a ~~screen~~ ``covering for the knees`` (Kangxi 紼: 與芾通, 紼者蔽也行以蔽前天子朱紼諸侯赤紼); Fan Wan entry: Removed extra dot atop 冕, and corrected missing and incomplete strokes in 服. 【芾-entering】 (pjut) (_adjective-like_) flourishing (_noun-like_) knee-cover

U+8300 祓 (for "good-fortune" sense) fuk1 (for "good-fortune" sense) (F1.86 茀) 轎簾也 (Page 49 茀) Brambles, or luxuriant herbage, concealing the path (Kangxi: 道多草不可行, 茀茀茂也); happiness, luck (Kangxi: 福也); ~~to~~ ``a`` screen (Kangxi: 婦人乗車不露見車之前後設障以自蔽隱謂之茀); to clear away, to open (Kangxi: 治也); _fat ~~tso~~ ``ts'o``_ (fat1 cou2 茀草), to clear away grass. Williams' 茀草: Corrected _tso_ (zou2) to _ts'o_ (cou2). Strong, vigorous (Kangxi: 强盛也). 【茀】 (phjut) (_adjective-like_) flourishing, vigorous (_noun-like_) screen (_verb-like_) to clear (away) (_noun-like_) fortuity

fai3.text: U+8CBB (F1.32 費) 費用又姓 (Page 44 費) To spend, to use (Kangxi: 散財用也); to exert; to dissipate (Kangxi: 損也耗也); expense, cost, trouble, outlay (Kangxi: 用也); waste, lavishing; squandering; _shai fai_ (sai2 fai3 使費) to spend, to expend; _fai sz'_ (fai3 si6 費事) troublesome, to interrupt; _fai sam_ (fai3 sam1 費心), to remember one; _long fai_ (long6 fai3 浪費) wasteful; _fai shi_ (fai3 si4 費時), waste one's time; _kwo fai_ (gwo3 fai3 過費) "great outlay," politely said when sitting at a feast; _p'o fai_ (po3 fai3 破費) 'lost your outlay,' needless expense, politely said by one who gets a present; _chai fai_ (zai3 fai3 ？費) heedless, witless. 【費-unvoiced】 (phj+jH) (_verb-like_) to squander 【費事】 (fai3 si6) Not to be bothered (to do something). 費事煮 (fai3 si6 zyu2): "Can't be bothered cooking." 費事行 (fai3 si6 haang4): "Can't be bothered walking." Used before a negative consequence to be prevented or avoided. Like English "lest", but informal. 費事遲到 (fai3 si6 ci4 dou3): "So that we won't arrive late." 費事冷嚫 (fai3 si6 laang5 can1): "So that I don't catch a cold."

fai6.text: U+8CBB fei6 (alternative) Broad in application (Kangxi: 費用之廣也). A surname (Kangxi: 姓也). 【費-voiced】 (bj+jH) (_adjective-like_) general-use
U+5420 (F1.34 吠) 犬聲 (Page 44 吠) The bark of a dog (Kangxi: 犬吠鳴也, 犬聲); _lun fai kau_ (lyun6 fai6 gau2 亂吠狗), a dog which barks at everybody. 【吠】 (bjojH) (_verb-like_) to bark

fei6.text: U+5C5D 菲 fai6 (alternative) fei2 (per Williams for 菲, common) (Page 53 菲 _Fi_ (fei2); here normalised to 屝 _Fi_ (fei6)) straw sandals (Kangxi 菲: 與屝通, 菲草履); Or made from hemp, which also is coarser than silk (Kangxi: 屝麤屨也絲作曰履麻作曰屝). 【屝】 (bj+jH) (_noun-like_) straw-sandal

fei1.text: U+83F2 (Page 53 菲 _Fi_ (fei2); here corrected to _Fi_ (fei1)) fragrant (Kangxi: 菲菲香也), ~~beautiful~~ ``luxuriant`` (Kangxi: 草茂貌, 芳菲也); _fong fi_ (fong1 fei1 芳菲) grassy. Mixed, assorted (Kangxi: 雜也). 【菲-level】 (phj+j) (_adjective-like_) fragrant (_adjective-like_) luxuriant

fei2.text: U+83F2 棐 (for "scant" sense) fai6 (alternative per 《集韻》父沸切 for vegetable sense) fei6 (alternative per 《集韻》父沸切 for vegetable sense) (F1.39 菲) 薄也 (Page 53 菲) A red marshy plant (Kangxi: 菜名, 芴也, 菲草生下濕地似蕪菁華紫赤色可食); pot-herbs, greens; sparing, frugal (Kangxi: 薄也); uneasy; _fi pok_ (fei2 bok6 菲薄) poor, valueless; _fi i_ (fei2 ji4 菲儀), trifling, little of; _fi mat_ (fei2 mat6 菲物) of no moment---said of presents; _fi nik_ (fei2 nik6 菲惄) grieved (Kangxi: 菲惄悵也); Fan Wan entry: Fixed incomplete strokes in 薄. Williams' 菲惄: Normalised _nik_ (nik1) to _nik_ (nik6). 【菲-rising】 (phj+jX) (_adjective-like_) scant

fan1.text: U+5206 匪 (F1.80 分) 分别分離 (Page 45 分) To separate, to divide (Kangxi: 別也); to halve (Kangxi: 半也); to distribute (Kangxi: 賦也施也), to divide amongst; to give, to partake with (Kangxi: 與也); a candareen, a tenth in decimal notation. _P'ing fan_ (ping4 fan1 平分), to divide evenly; _fan hoi_ (fan1 hoi1 分開), to separate; _fan pit_ (fan1 bit6 分別) to divide, to leave; ``separately;`` _fan pun_ (fan1 bun6 分伴) to dissolve partnership; _fan ch'ing sho_ (fan1 cing4 sou3 分程數) to pay in proportion; _fan ka_ (fan1 gaa1 分家), to divide the estate; _fan ~~pai~~ ``p'ai``_ (fan1 paai3 分派) to give amongst; _yat fan hong fo, yat fan ts'in_ (jat1 fan1 hong4 fo3 jat1 fan1 cin4 一分行貨一分錢), each kind of goods has its own price; _fan kom t'ung mi_ (fan1 gam1 tung4 mei6 分柑同味) same taste as the carpels of an orange; _fan un_ (fan1 jyun4 分？), a deputy district magistrate. Williams' "a candareen": That is, a tenth of a mace (錢), or a hundredth of a tael (両). Williams' 分別: Corrected _pit_ (bit3) to _pit_ (bit6). Williams' 分伴: Corrected _pun_ (bun3) to _pun_ (bun6). Williams' 分派: Corrected _pai_ (baai3) to _p'ai_ (paai3). Williams' 分柑同味: So spelt because of his reference to "orange". The more common form is 分甘同味, with "sweetness" replacing "orange". To distinguish (Kangxi: 判也). A minute. A point, a mark (in scoring). A tenth of a Chinese inch (寸). 【分-level】 (pjun) (_verb-like_) to separate, to split, (_met._) to dispute (_verb-like_) to distinguish (_verb-like_) to share (_noun-like_) tenth

fan6.text: U+5206 (Page 45 分 read _Fan_ (fan3); here corrected to _Fan_ (fan6)) Read _fan_ (fan6) a portion (Kangxi: 均也分劑也), a duty, a part (Kangxi: 分位也); _sam fan chi yat_ (saam1 fan6 zi1 jat1 三分之一) one third. In Cantonese, the fraction-building sense of 分 is a noun, not a verb. The construct 三分之一 to be parsed as "one of three shares (or portions)". As such, 分 is to be read in the departing tone, _fan_ (fan6). It is *not* to be read in the level tone, as in Mandarin. By extension, the "fraction" sense of 分 is also to be read in the departing tone in Cantonese. This distinguishes 分數 fan6 sou3 "fraction" from 分數 fan1 sou3 "score/points". A fraction (of mathematics). 分數 (fan6 sou3): "Fraction." 約分 (joek3 fan6): "Reducing fractions." 【分-departing】 (bjunH) (_noun-like_) share, fraction (_noun-like_) allotted duty

fan1.text: U+52DB (F1.80 勳) 功勞也 (F1.80 勛) 同上 Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿺熏力 to 勳.

U+52F3 勛 薰 (F1.80 勳) 功勞也 (F1.80 勛) 同上 (Page 45 ~~？~~``勳``) Loyal merit (Kangxi: 能成王功也 etc.), meritorious; to spend strength for one's king; _fan lo_ (fan1 lou4 勳勞), merit obtained in serving the state; _fan shan_ (fan1 san4 勳臣), a patriotic statesman. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿺熏力 to 勳. 【勳】 (xjun) (_noun-like_) merit
U+532A (Page 53 匪) Read _fan_ (fan1), to distribute (Kangxi: 匪分也, 匪讀爲分).

fei2.text: U+532A (F1.39 匪) 非也 (Page 53 匪) not right, illegal; banditti, vagabonds, heretics; _fi lui_ (fei2 leoi6 匪類) or _fi ~~to~~ ``t'o``_ (fei2 tou4 匪徒), vagabonds; _fi tong_ (fei2 dong2 匪黨), a band of villains; _fi shik_ (fei2 sik1 匪色) variegated. Williams' 匪徒: Corrected _to_ (dou4) to _t'o_ (tou4). Not (Kangxi: 一曰非也). 【匪】 (pj+jX) (_adverb-like_) not

fan1.text: U+5429 (F1.80 吩) 吩咐 (Page 46 吩) To direct; _fan fu_ (fan1 fu3 吩咐), to bid to do, to order. Williams' 吩咐: Corrected 吩呼 _fan fu_ (fan1 fu1) to 吩咐 _fan fu_ (fan1 fu3). Note that Williams has no entry for the character 咐.

U+5A5A 㛰 昏 (F1.80 婚) 婚姻 (Page 45 婚) Marriage (Kangxi: 婚姻嫁也); to take a wife; _fan yan_ (fan1 jan1 婚姻), ~~nuptials~~ ``marriage``; _mai fan_ (maai4 fan1 埋婚), to complete a marriage; _lin fan_ (lin4 fan1 連婚) (Kangxi: 婚嫁之婚親曰連婚), ~~to marry relatives~~ ``relatives by marriage, _i. e._ in-laws``; _fan p'ui_ (fan1 pui3 婚配) to contract a marriage. Williams' 連婚: Corrected _lin_ (lin1) to _lin_ (lin4). 【婚】 (xwon) (_noun-like_) marriage (_noun-like_) in-law
U+5E09 紛 A wiping-cloth (Kangxi: 拭物巾也, 楚謂大巾曰帉, 又通作紛). 【帉】 (phjun) (_noun-like_) wiping-cloth

U+60DB 惽 fan3 (per 《廣韻》呼悶切 for "forgetful" sense) man5 (per 《集韻》弭盡切 for "confused" sense) (F1.80 惛) 心不明也 (Page 45 惛) Stupid (Kangxi: 不憭也, 不明了也, 心不明也), forgetful (Kangxi: 迷忘也); confused ~~recollection~~ (Kangxi: 昏意也). 【惛】 (xwon) (_adjective-like_) muddled, muddle-headed

U+660F 昬 (F1.80 昏) 日暮也暗也昧也 (Page 45 昏) The sun setting (Kangxi: 日冥也); ``evening`` twilight; dusk (Kangxi: 昏代也, 代明也, 日入後二刻半爲昏昏來則明往故云代明); obscure, confused, dull (Kangxi: 昏亂也); in disorder; ~~to oblige to do;~~ _wong fan_ (wong4 fan1 黃昏), dusk; _fan om_ (fan1 am3 昏暗) dark, dull; _fan lun_ (fan1 lyun6 昏亂) confused; _fan mui_ (fan1 mui6 昏昧) unintelligible; _fan ye_ (fan1 je6 昏夜) ``a gloomy`` night; _fan fa_ (fan1 faa1 昏花), motes in the eye. To endeavour, to make an effort (Kangxi: 昏彊也). Not yet named (Kangxi: 未名曰昏). To lose consciousness. 【昏】 (xwon) (_noun-like_) dusk (_adjective-like_) adusk, (_met._) blind

U+66DB 熏 纁 (for "dusklight" sense) (F1.80 曛) 日没曰曛 (Page 45 熏; here normalised to 曛) evening ``twilight``; _fan tsik_ (fan1 zik6 曛夕) ``evening`` twilight; (Page 45 曛) ~~Twilight~~ ``Evening twilight, dusk`` (Kangxi: 黃昏時); the light reflected after sunset (Kangxi: 日入餘光); ~~exhilarated;~~ _shan ham fan ~~yeung~~ ``ying``_ (saan1 haam4 fan1 jing2 山涵曛映), the hills are tinged with the setting sun. Williams' 山涵曛映: Corrected _yeung_ (joeng2) to _ying_ (jing2).
U+678C (F1.80 枌) 枌榆木名 (Page 46 枌)

fan4.text: U+678C fan1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.80 枌 _Fan_ (fan1)) 枌榆木名 (Page 46 枌 _Fan_ (fan1); here normalised to _Fan_ (fan4)) A tree resembling the elm with white wood (Kangxi: 楡白枌); _fan u she_ (fan4 jyu4 se5 枌榆社), an agricultural ~~feast~~ ``altar`` (Kangxi: 枌楡社名). 【枌】 (bjun) (_noun-like_) white-elm

fan1.text: U+68FB fan4 (alternative per 《廣韻》符分切) (F1.80 棻) 香木也 (Page 46 棻) A fragrant wood (Kangxi: 香木也) burned for its perfume. Flourishing, luxuriant.

U+68FC fan4 (per Kangxi 符分切) (F1.80 紛) 紛紜雜也衆也紛亂也 (F1.80 棼) 同上 (Page 46 棼) confused, disordered, complicated (Kangxi 棼: 亂也); Kangxi has this sense under the reading 符分切 _fan_ (fan4). In my opinion, this is dubious, as 《廣韻》符分切 is explained 複屋棟也, which is unrelated to confusion/disorder. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 衆 and 亂.

fan4.text: U+68FC 枌 (for "roof-beam" sense) (Page 46 枌 _Fan_ (fan1); here corrected to _Fan_ (fan4) and normalised to 棼) a beam in a house (Kangxi 枌: 通棼複屋棟也); (Page 46 棼 _Fan_ (fan1); here corrected to _Fan_ (fan4)) A beam or ridge-pole of a roof (Kangxi: 複屋棟也, 複屋背重梁); hempen covering (Kangxi: 薠麻謂之棼, 棼麻布也). 【棼】 (bjun) (_noun-like_) roof-beam (_noun-like_) hemp-cloth

fan1.text: U+6C1B 雰 fan4 (alternative per 《廣韻》符分切) (F1.80 氛) 沃氛 (Page 46 氛) Fume, vapor (Kangxi: 氣也); shadowy signs, a will-o'-the-wisp, airy omens (Kangxi: 氛氳祥氣也, 氛祲妖氣也); _fan wan_ (fan1 wan1 氛氳), aerial influences; _iu fan_ (jiu2 fan1 妖氛), noxious influences; _hoi fan_ (hoi2 fan1 海氛), pirates; _kwok fan_ (gwok3 fan1 國氛), ~~demagogues, disturbers~~ ``disturbings`` of the peace. 《廣韻》 has 符分切 _fan_ (fan4) for 氛氲祥氣 "auspicious ambience", and 撫文切 _fan_ (fan1) for 氛侵妖氣 "malign ambience". Modern usage prefers the latter reading, and it is debatable whether the distinction was necessary in the first place. 【氛】 (bjun, phjun) (_noun-like_) ambience
U+6C7E (F1.80 汾) 水名 (Page 46 汾)

fan4.text: U+6C7E fan1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.80 汾 _Fan_ (fan1)) 水名 (Page 46 汾 _Fan_ (fan1); here normalised to _Fan_ (fan4)) A river in Shansi (山西), a branch of the Yellow R (Kangxi: 水名 etc.). _Fan yeung wong_ (fan4 joeng4 wong4 汾陽王), a personage in the Tang (唐) dynasty, whose name is a synonym for happiness; as _Fan yeung tim hom_ (fan4 joeng4 dim2 ham5 汾陽點頷), Fanyeung's king nodding his chin (because he did not know all his descendants). Williams' "A river in Shansi": Note that Kangxi 水出太原晉陽山西南入河 is to be punctuated as "水出太原晉陽山、西南入河". That we know it is in Shansi is due to "太原晉陽山", not "山、西".

fan1.text: U+6DBD 湣 (F1.80 涽) 齊涽王 (Page 45 涽) Unsettled, unstable, like water (Kangxi: 涽涽未定貌); Fan Wan entry: The posthumous-name sense should be read _man_ (man5) and spelt 湣. See 湣 man5 (TODO).

U+7104 (F1.80 焄) 香氣也禮云焄蒿悽愴 (Page 45 ~~蔒~~焄) fumes from cooked vegetables; ~~savory~~ odors (Kangxi: 香臭之氣也); _fan ho_ (fan1 hou1 焄蒿), steam from viands; odorous exhalations. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 愴. Williams considers 蔒 and 焄 interchangeable. This is only true in the sense that both are alternative forms of 葷. Williams' "savory odors": This is a misreading of Kangxi 香臭, which refers odours in general (whether good or bad). Williams' 焄蒿: Corrected _ho_ (ho1) to _ho_ (hou1). 【焄】 (xjun) (_noun-like_) fume, odour

U+718F 焄 燻 (for smoke-related senses) 薰 (F1.80 熏) 烟也又熱氣蒸也 (Page 45 熏) Vapor, exhalations, steam (Kangxi: 火氣盛貌); smoke rising (Kangxi: 火煙上出也); to smoke, to fumigate, to heat (Kangxi: 熱也), to parch (Kangxi: 熏灼也); to offend; to becloud; _fan fung_ (fan1 fung1 熏風), warm wind (Kangxi: 東南曰熏風); _fan fo t'ui_ (fan1 fo2 teoi2 熏火腿), to smoke hams; _fan fan_ (fan1 fan1 熏熏)~~, uneasy, fidgetty~~; _fan kon_ (fan1 gon1 熏乾), to dry at the fire; _fan chek_ (fan1 zek3 熏炙) to cauterize (Kangxi 焄: 焄猶熏炙之也). (Page 45 ~~蔒~~焄) Vapor or steam (Kangxi 焄: 火上出也, 亦作熏); Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𤍠 to 熱. Williams' "uneasy, fidgetty": This is a rendering of Kangxi 熏熏坐不安之貌, which is one of the explanations for 《詩大雅》公尸來止熏熏, but not a very good one, in my opinion. The other explanations (熏熏和悅也 and 熏說文作醺醉也) are better, and of these, the second takes 熏 to be an alternative form of 醺. Williams' 熏炙: Corrected _chek_ (zek6) to _chek_ (zek3). Amiable and cheerful (Kangxi: 熏熏和悅也). Warm. 【熏】 (xjun) (_verb-like_) to smoke, to fume, (_met._) to scorch Used in 熏熏. 【熏熏】 (xjun xjun) (_adjective-like_) elated
U+71FB
U+736F 葷 薰 (F1.80 獯) 獯鬻狄人號也 (Page 45 獯) A tribe of aborigines, called _fan yuk_ (fan1 juk6 獯鬻) (Kangxi: 獯鬻 etc.); they dwelt near Shensi (陝西).

U+7D1B 分 (for "confused" and "many" senses) 棼 (for "confused" and "many" senses) 汾 (for "confused" and "many" senses) 芬 (for "confused" and "many" senses) (F1.80 紛) 紛紜雜也衆也紛亂也 (F1.80 棼) 同上 (Page 46 紛) A variegated ribbon (Kangxi: 紛如綬有文而狹者也); confused, perplexed (Kangxi: 亂也); raveled; to mix up; many things at once (Kangxi: 紛紛衆也, 紛衆也, 紛紛言其多); hurry, bustle, clamor; _fan lun_ (fan1 lyun6 紛亂) hubbub; _fan fan to sz'_ (fan1 fan1 do1 si6 紛紛多事) distracted by business; _fan fa lau lun_ (fan1 faa1 lau5 lyun6 紛花柳亂) all in disorder; _fan wan_ (fan1 wan4 紛紜), confused. (Page 46 芬; here normalised to 紛) Numerous (Kangxi 芬: 芬謂衆多), (...). Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 衆 and 亂. A horse-tail tie (Kangxi: 馬尾韜也). Dispute. To disturb. 【紛】 (phjun) (_adjective-like_) confused (_adjective-like_) numbersome (_noun-like_) lace-ribbon
U+7E81 (F1.80 纁) 淡紅色也 (Page 713 纁) A light red (Kangxi: 淺絳也), produced by a trine immersion in the dye (Kangxi: 三染謂之纁); a scarlet tint. 【纁】 (xjun) (_adjective-like_) light-red, pink

U+82AC (F1.80 芬) 芬芳 (Page 46 芬) Budding and blossoming; fragrance from opening plants (Kangxi: 草初生香分布也); _fan fong_ (fan1 fong1 芬芳), spreading fragrance; _fan fan_ (fan1 fan1 芬芬), odoriferous (Kangxi: 芬芬香也). (...), harmonious (Kangxi: 和也). Kangxi has the "mound-like, bulging" sense (supplemented below) under the main reading 撫文切 _fan_ (fan1). But if this is the same word as 墳 "moundy", then it should be read 符分切 _fan_ (fan4) instead. Mound-like, bulging (Kangxi: 芬然壤起貌). 【芬】 (phjun) (_adjective-like_) fragrant

U+8477 焄 蔒 薰 (F1.80 葷) 臭草又不食齋曰葷 (Page 45 葷) Leeks, onions, and strong flavored vegetables (Kangxi: 蔥薤也, 葷臭菜也 etc.); animal food; _fan su_ (fan1 sou3 葷素) meats or vegetables; _pat u fan_ (bat1 jyu4 fan1 不？葷), not to eat meat; _fan seng_ (fan1 seng1 葷腥), ~~savory~~ ``meaty`` odors of food. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 齋. 【葷】 (xjun) (_noun-like_) aromatic-vegetable
U+8512 (Page 45 蔒~~焄~~) Williams considers 蔒 and 焄 interchangeable. This is only true in the sense that both are alternative forms of 葷.

U+85B0 (F1.80 薰) 香草 (Page 45 薰) Fragrant plants (Kangxi: 香草也), fragrance; used ~~in houses~~ to expel demons (Kangxi: 古人祓除以此草薰之故謂之薰); a savory smell. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in headword 薰. Williams' "Fragrant plants": More likely a specific *type* of fragrant plant. To influence. 【薰】 (xjun) (_noun-like_) fragrance-grass
U+91BA 熏 (F1.80 醺) 微醉也 (Page 45 醺) Intoxicated, drunk (Kangxi: 醉也 etc.); smelling of liquor; _tsui fan fan_ (zeoi3 fan1 fan1 醉醺醺), gloriously drunk. 【醺】 (xjun) (_adjective-like_) intoxicated

U+95BD 𨵽 (F1.80 閽) 閽人守門之役 (Page 45 閽) ~~To shut~~ ``The lowly servant who shuts`` the door at evening (Kangxi: 常以昏閉門隸也); a ``lowly`` porter (Kangxi: 閽者守門之賤者也); _fan yan_ (fan1 jan4 閽人), a doorkeeper, an eunuch who keeps the hareem; _k'au fan_ (kau3 fan1 叩閽), to visit the palace (Kangxi: 凡吏民冤抑詣闕自愬者曰叩閽). A palace gate. 【閽】 (xwon) (_noun-like_) doorkeeper

U+96F0 (F1.80 雰) 下雲貌 (Page 46 雰) A frosty mist (Kangxi: 霧氣也); hoarfrost (Kangxi: 潤氣著草木遇寒凍色白曰雰); _u fan fan_ (jyu5 fan1 fan1 雨雰雰), a snowy, sleety, rain. Snowy (Kangxi: 雰雰雪貌). 【雰】 (phjun) (_adjective-like_) snowy
U+9934 May appear as either ⿰飠⿱大卉 (+8 assumed here) or ⿰飠⿱大𠦄 (+9), depending on the font. (F1.80 餴) 蒸熟米以水沃之乃再蒸也 (Page 45 餴)

U+9959 May appear with 艹 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. 餴 (F1.80 餴) 蒸熟米以水沃之乃再蒸也 (Page 45 餴; here normalised to 饙) Rice steamed thoroughly; to steam rice (Kangxi 饙: ...然則蒸米爲之饙饙必餾而熟之...). Kangxi's treatment is confusing. For the form 餴, it gives a single sense, defined 半蒸飯也與饙同 (half-steamed-rice, same as 饙), along with the commentary 餴蒸米一熟而以水沃之乃再蒸也 (to steam rice till cooked, then add water, then steam again). This is consistent with Williams' "Rice steamed thoroughly". For the form 饙, it splits things up into: The "half-steamed-rice" sense (半蒸飯); and What appears to be the "thoroughly cooked" sense, based on a heap of commentary on 《爾雅釋言》饙餾稔也.

fan6.text: U+4EFD 分 fan6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun for "share/part" senses) (Page 46 份) A portion, a dividend, a share; _fan fan_ (fan1 fan6-2 分份), to divide the profits; _mai fan_ (maai4 fan6-2 埋份), to put in shares; _fan tsz'_ (fan6-2 zi2 份子) a share. A vulgar character. (Page 713 份) (...) The proper sound in colloquial is _fan_ (fan6), though sometimes read _fan_ (fan6-2). It is the same word as 分 (fan6) when used as a noun. Classifier for documents. Classifier for gifts. Classifier for jobs. Suffixed after 年, 月, 省, etc. to indicate an interval (in time) or division (in space). 【份】 (fan6-2) A part, a partaking. 有份出席 (jau5 fan6-2 ceot1 zik6): "Have a part in attending." 冇佢份 (mou5 keoi5 fan6-2): "There's no part for him" (i.e. he is excluded). 【兩份】 (loeng5 fan6-2) Shared between two. 兩份飲 (loeng5 fan6-2 jam2): "Drink it between the two of us."

fan2.text: U+7C89 fan3 (per 《集韻》方問切 for "apply" sense) (F1.83 粉) 米末也又粉飾 (Page 46 粉) Rice broken to pieces (Kangxi: 米粉, 分也硏米使分散也); a flour of any grain (Kangxi: 豆屑也); pigment, to adorn (Kangxi: 傅也飾也); to whitewash or color (Kangxi: 設采潤色謂之粉澤 etc.); _mai fan_ (mai5 fan2 米粉) rice flour; ``rice vermicelli;`` _suk mai fan_ (suk1 mai5 fan2 粟米粉), indian-meal; _fan sz'_ (fan2 si1 粉絲) or _fan tsai_ (fan2 zai2 粉仔), vermicelli; _min fan_ (min6 fan2 面粉), a cosmetic; _fan tseung_ (fan2 zoeng1 粉漿), starch; _fan toi_ (fan2 doi6-2 粉袋) a tailor's chalk-bag; _leung fan_ (loeng4 fan2 涼粉), a sort of jelly; _fan sui_ (fan2 seoi3 粉碎) smashed to shivers; _fan shik t'ai p'ing_ (fan2 sik1 taai3 ping4 粉飾太平), a specious peace; _fan ~~pai~~ ``p'ai``_ (fan2 paai4 粉牌) a writing-board; _fan pik_ (fan2 bik1 粉壁) to whitewash a wall; _un fan_ (jyun4 fan2 鉛粉), white lead.
