(Page 713 粉) The white pus of sores; _fan lau_ (fan2 lau4-2 粉瘤), the sloughing from tumors. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 未 to 末. Williams' 米粉, 粟米粉: Corrected _mai_ (mai2) to _mai_ (mai5). Williams' 粉絲: Now more commonly known as glass/cellophane noodles. Williams' 面粉: Not to be confused with 麪粉. Williams' 涼粉: Corrected _leung_ (loeng1) to _leung_ (loeng4). Specifically, grass jelly. Williams' 粉碎: Corrected _sui_ (seoi6) to _sui_ (seoi3). Williams' 粉牌: Corrected _pai_ (baai4) to _p'ai_ (paai4). Cosmetics (Kangxi: 傅面者也). Powder (Kangxi: 凡物磑之如屑者皆爲粉粉通稱非獨米也). To powder. Flour- or starch-based foods (especially staples). 【粉】 (pjunX) (_noun-like_) powder (_noun-like_) rice-flour

fan3.text: U+7793 (Page 47 瞓) To sleep, to rest; _ni fan wan_ (nei5 fan3 wan1 你瞓瘟), you are half asleep; _ngan fan_ (ngaan5 fan3 眼瞓) sleepy. A colloquial word, for which this character is often made. 【瞓】 (fan3) To sleep. 唔夠瞓 (m4 gau3 fan3): "Not enough sleep." 喺度瞓 (hai2 dou6 fan3): "Sleep here." 打側瞓 (daa2 zak1 fan3): "Sleep sideways." 瞓廳 (fan3 teng1): "Sleep in the living-room." 瞓地 (fan3 dei6): "Sleep on the floor." 瞓街 (fan3 gaai1): "Sleep on the streets" (i.e. be homeless). To lie, to recline. 瞓低 (fan3 dai1): "Lie down." 瞓低嗮 (fan3 dai1 saai3): "All toppled." 【瞓覺】 (fan3 gaau3) To sleep. 瞓緊晏覺 (fan3 gan2 aan3 gaau3): "Taking an afternoon nap." 瞓着覺 (fan3 zoek6 gaau3): "Fallen asleep." 【瞓身】 (fan3 san1) To go all in. 【瞓着】 (fan3 zoek6) To fall asleep. 未瞓着 (mei6 fan3 zoek6): "Not yet asleep." 瞓唔着 (fan3 m4 zoek6): "Can't fall asleep." 瞓着咗 (fan3 zoek6 zo2): "Fallen asleep." 【蚊都瞓】 (man1 dou1 fan3) Way too late. (Literally, "even mosquitoes are going to sleep".) 【蚊瞓】 (man1 fan3) Short for 蚊都瞓. 【眼瞓】 (ngaan5 fan3) Sleepy.

U+7CDE (F1.85 糞) 糞穢又壅也 (Page 47 糞) Ordure, filth, muck (Kangxi: 穢也), excrement; to manure (Kangxi: 治也培也); _fan hang_ (fan3 haang1 糞坑), a privy; _fan mun_ (fan3 mun4 糞門), the anus; _lok fan_ (lok6 fan3 落糞) to manure; _fan liu_ (fan3 liu6-2 糞料) muck prepared for sale; _fan ti_ (fan3 dei6 糞地) a public necessary. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 穢. To remove, to sweep away (Kangxi: 掃除也, 糞除也). 【糞】 (pjunH) (_noun-like_) manure, muck (_verb-like_) to sweep (away)

U+8A13 (F1.85 訓) 敎訓也又誡也 (Page 47 訓) To instruct (Kangxi: 說敎也, 導也), to teach (Kangxi: 誨也)~~, to exhort~~; to explain (Kangxi: 訓者釋所言之理也); a doctrine, precept (Kangxi: 古言可爲法也); ~~definition;~~ _fan kau_ (fan3 gaau3 訓教) to instruct; _fan lin_ (fan3 lin6 訓練) ~~to teach the manual~~ ``to train``; _fan to_ (fan3 dou6 訓導) the ~~snperintendent~~ ``superintendent`` of education in each department; _ku fan_ (gu2 fan3 古訓) tradition. To caution (Kangxi: 誡也). To comply with, to go along with (Kangxi: 順也). To train. 【訓】 (xjunH) (_verb-like_) to instruct (_verb-like_) to explain (_verb-like_) to comply (with)

fan4.text: U+58B3 May appear with 艹 in 賁 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. (F1.81 墳) 墳墓又墳典言書籍也 (Page 46 墳) A grave, a tomb (Kangxi: 墓也), a tumulus (Kangxi: 土之高者曰墳); river banks, an embankment (Kangxi: 水涯曰墳大防也所以扞水); great, vast (Kangxi: 大也); _Fan mo_ (fan4 mou6 墳墓) a tomb; _so fan_ (sou3 fan4 掃墳), to worship ``at`` the graves; _fong fan_ (fong1 fan4 荒墳), a deserted grave; _sam fan_ (saam1 fan4 三墳), the distinction of heaven, earth, man (Kangxi: 三墳分也論三才之分天地人之始也). Williams' 掃墳: Corrected _fan_ (fan1) to _fan_ (fan4). A classical book or tome. 【墳-level】 (bjun) (_noun-like_) mound (_noun-like_) embankment (_adjective-like_) moundy, (_met._) large

fan5.text: U+58B3 May appear with 艹 in 賁 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. bun6 (per 《集韻》部本切 for "raised" sense) (Page 46 墳 read _Fan_ (fan3); here corrected to _Fan_ (fan5)) rich soil (Kangxi: 土膏肥也); read _fan_ (fan5). Raised (Kangxi: 土沸起也). 【墳-rising】 (bjunX) (_adjective-like_) rich

fan4.text: U+5E69 May appear with 艹 in 賁 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. (F1.82 幩) 鑣之飾也 (Page 46 幩) An ornament on the bit of a bridle (Kangxi: 馬纏鑣扇汗也, 幩飾也 etc.). 【幩】 (bjun) (_noun-like_) bridle-ornament

U+6FC6 May appear with 艹 in 賁 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. (F1.81 濆) 水涯 (Page 46 濆) A river overflowing and making streamlets; a river bank (Kangxi: 水厓也); a river in Honan (Kangxi: 汝水逕奇雒城西北濆水出焉). Williams' "A river overflowing and making streamlets": Kangxi 大水溢出別爲小水之名 is commentary for 《爾雅釋水》汝爲濆, in which a great river (named 汝) makes (爲) a streamlet (named 濆). It perhaps should not be read as a definition. 【濆】 (bjun) (_noun-like_) riverbank, bank

U+711A (F1.81 焚) 火燒曰焚 (Page 46 焚) To burn, to light, to set on fire (Kangxi: 燒也, 火灼物也); _fan heung_ (fan4 hoeng1 焚香), to light incense sticks; _fan fa_ (fan4 faa3 焚化) to burn up; ``to cremate, to incinerate;`` _fan sau_ (fan4 sau1 焚修), to burn ~~a bonze~~ ``incense in the pursuit of Budhist cultivation``; _fan shu_ (fan4 syu1 焚書), burning of the books by Tsin (秦). Williams' 焚化: Corrected _fan_ (fan1) to _fan_ (fan4). Williams' 焚書: Corrected _shu_ (syu4) to _shu_ (syu1). 【焚】 (bjun) (_verb-like_) to burn

U+8561 May appear with 艹 in 賁 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. (F1.81 蕡) 草木多實曰蕡 (Page 46 蕡) Fruitful (Kangxi: 草木多實也), flourishing plants (Kangxi: 蕡藹), fragrant flowers growing together (Kangxi: 雜香草也); _ma fan_ (maa4 fan4 麻蕡), hemp seed (Kangxi: 蕡麻子也); _fan shat_ (fan4 sat6 蕡實) bearing much seed. 【蕡】 (bjun) (_adjective-like_) fruitful (_noun-like_) hemp-seed

U+8CC1 May appear with 艹 having 3 strokes (+5 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+6), depending on the font. Great (Kangxi: 宏賁皆大也). A three-legged tortoise (Kangxi: 龜三足賁). 【賁】 (bjun) (_adjective-like_) large

fan5.text: U+8CC1 May appear with 艹 having 3 strokes (+5 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+6), depending on the font. (Page 352 賁) Also read _Fan_ (fan5), to be filled with rage (Kangxi: 與憤同, 賁讀爲憤憤怒氣充實也); to bubble or effervesce as a spring (Kangxi: 沸也);

fei4.text: U+8CC1 May appear with 艹 having 3 strokes (+5 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+6), depending on the font. The modern surname is read _pan_ (ban1) (Kangxi: 前書賁赫音肥今姓音奔). See 賁 ban1 (TODO). An olden surname (Kangxi: 姓也 etc.).

fan5.text: U+9F22 蚡 fan4 (alternative per 《廣韻》符分切) (Page 47 鼢) A mole or field rat (Kangxi: 地行鼠 etc.); called _lai shu_ (lai4 syu2 犁鼠) (Kangxi: 所謂犁鼠者卽此也), a plough rat.

U+50E8 May appear with 艹 in 賁 having 3 strokes (+12 assumed here) or 4 strokes (+13), depending on the font. 焚 賁 fan3 (per 《廣韻》方問切) (F1.83 僨) 覆敗也 (Page 47 僨) ~~Prostrate;~~ to overthrow, to ruin (Kangxi: 覆敗也); to fall on one's back (Kangxi: 僵也, 仰偃也); _yat in fan sz'_ (jat1 jin4 fan5 si6 一言僨事) a word will ruin an affair; _fan to ti_ (fan5 dou2 dei6 僨倒地) to tumble to the ground. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 覆. Moved, excited (Kangxi: 僨動也). 【僨】 (pjunH) (_verb-like_) to fall (back), to ruin

U+596E fan3 (per 《廣韻》方問切) (F1.83 奮) 發動也 (Page 47 奮) To endeavor after, to excite, to rouse (Kangxi: 震動也); to spread abroad (Kangxi: 揚也) or reach to; to ~~brush away~~ ``shake off``; to lift; prompt, impetuous, rapid; _fan chi_ (fan5 zi3 奮志) to animate one's self; _fan lik_ (fan5 lik6 奮力) to put forth energy; _fan i_ (fan5 ji1 奮衣), to ~~lift up the skirt~~ ``shake the dust off the clothes``. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 奮, which is printed with the second stroke of 大 conjoined with the first stroke of 隹. To spread the wings (Kangxi: 翬也, 鳥張羽奮奞也). To shake off dust (Kangxi: 振去塵也). 【奮】 (pjunH) (_verb-like_) to spread the wings, to spread (_verb-like_) to shake (_verb-like_) to rouse, to start, to exert

U+5FFF fan2 (per 《廣韻》敷粉切) (F1.83 忿) 怒也 (Page 47 忿) Anger, resentment (Kangxi: 悁也, 恨也怒也); _fan no_ (fan5 nou6 忿怒) angry, vexed; _fan fan pat kik_ (fan5 fan5 bat1 gik1 忿忿不？) perturbed, cross; _fan han_ (fan5 han6 忿恨) hatred, malice. Classically, 忿 is to be distinguished from 憤. Specifically, 忿 is an irrational anger, whereas 憤 is an internal build-up of *either* anger or ardour. In modern usage, 忿 is indistinguishable from 憤 for the "anger" sense. 【忿】 (phjunX, phjunH) (_verb-like_) to anger

fan6.text: U+5FFF 分 fan3 (per Kangxi 芳問切) Generally only used in the negative (e.g. 不忿, 唔忿氣). Accepting (of an adverse result or occurrence), as opposed to discontented (Kangxi: 通作分, 不分者不平之意與忿同). 深深不忿 (sam1 sam1 bat1 fan6): "Deeply discontented." 【忿氣】 (fan6 hei3) Accepting (of an adverse result or occurrence), as opposed to discontented. 唔忿氣輸咗 (m4 fan6 hei3 syu1 zo2): "Discontented at losing."

fan5.text: U+61A4 忿 (for "anger" sense) 賁 (F1.83 憤) 心求通而未能也又發憤又怒也 (Page 47 憤) Impatient desire (Kangxi: 懣也), zeal, ardor; anger, violent feeling; _fat fan_ (faat3 fan5 發憤), excited, zealous after; _fan lik_ (fan5 lik6 憤力) to put forth one's energies; ~~_fan kit_ (fan5 git3)~~ ``_fan lit_ (fan5 lit6 憤烈)`` wrought up by passion. (Page 713 憤) Under this character, for _fan kit_ (fan5 git3) read _fan lit_ (fan5 lit6 憤烈). See commentary at 忿 on the distinction from 憤. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 通. Williams' 發憤, 憤力: These are effectively alternative forms of 發奮, 奮力. 【憤】 (bjunX) (_adjective-like_) disgruntled

fang4.text: U+63C8 fang6 (vernacular alternative) 【揈】 (fang4, fang6) To hit hard. 揈佢兩錘 (fang4 keoi5 loeng5 ceoi4): "Hit him hard with two blows." Olden attestation for 揈 _fang_ (fang4) meaning "hit": 《集韻》 乎萌切 揈 擊也

fing1.text: U+63C8 捹 (Page 714 _Fing_ (fing1); here assigned to 揈) A colloquial word. Confused, tangled; _fing kom lun_ (fing1 gam3 lyun6 揈咁亂) all in disorder, as one moving; touzled, as hair.

fing3.text: U+63C8 捹 (Page 714 _Fing_ (fing3); here assigned to 揈) A colloquial word. A slap with the hand; _tai pa fing_ (daai6 baa1 fing3 大巴揈) a smart slap. Williams' 大巴揈: Corrected _pa_ (ba2) to _pa_ (baa1).

fing6.text: U+63C8 捹 fang6 (alternative mostly for "spend recklessly" sense) (Page 53 捹; here normalised to 揈) To swing; to shake in the wind; _~~t'iu t'iu~~ ``tiu tiu`` fing_ (diu3-4 diu3-2 fing6 吊吊揈) to hang and swing; _fing tik shui lai_ (fing6 dik1 seoi2 lai4 揈滴水嚟), sprinkle a little water; _fing lat_ (fing6 lat1 揈甩) shake it off. (Page 714 _Fing_ (fing6); here assigned to 揈) A colloquial word. To vibrate, to swing, to dangle, as the end of a rope; to sprinkle; to snap away; to shake off, as ink out of a pen; _fing kwo lai_ (fing6 gwo3 lai4 揈過嚟), yerk it (as the cue) forward. (Page 714 after _Fing_ (fing6)) The character 捹, has no authority; ~~it is read _pan_ (ban3);~~ ~~the hands rapidly moving.~~ Williams' 吊吊揈: Corrected _t'iu t'iu_ (tiu4 tiu4) to _tiu tiu_ (diu3-4 diu3-2). Williams' 揈滴水嚟: Corrected _lai_ (lai1) to _lai_ (lai4). Williams' "read _pan_": This and the subsequent explanation are misreadings of 《集韻》蒲悶切音坌手亂貌 in Kangxi. See 捹 ban(TODO) 【吊吊揈】 (diu3-4 diu3-2 fing6) Hanging and swinging about. 【揈】 (fing6, fang6) To swing, to flap, to shake. 揈身揈勢 (fing6 san1 fing6 sai3): "Swinging the body to and fro" (i.e. not staying still). 揈手揈腳 (fing6 sau2 fing6 goek3): "Flapping about the hands and feet." 揈甩啲塵 (fing6 lat1 di1 can4): "Flap off the dust." 揈乾隻手 (fing6 gon1 zek3 sau2): "Shake the hand dry." To whirl. 揈乾 (fing6 gon1): "Spin dry." To spend recklessly. 揈錢 (fang6 cin4-2): "Flinging money around." 【揈頭丸】 (fing6 tau4 jyun4-2) Ecstasy, the tablet form of MDMA. (Literally, "head-whirling pills".)

fang4.text: U+8A07 The choice of the character 訇 is an arbitrary spelling by me. I am not aware of any better alternatives. 【訇】 (fang4) To rebuke, especially sternly. 畀先生訇 (bei2 sin1 saang1 fang4): "Told off by the teacher." 成日訇人 (seng4 jat6 fang4 jan4): "Always telling others off."

fat1.text: U+4F5B

fat6.text: U+4F5B 仸 (F1.88 佛) 仙佛以空虚寂滅爲敎 (F1.88 仸) 同上 (Page 50 佛) Budha``, the enlightened one`` (Kangxi: 佛佗佛者覺也); _Fat kau_ (fat6 gaau3 佛教) ~~Budhists~~ ``Budhism``; _Fat tso_ (fat6 zou2 佛祖), Budha; _Fat fat_ (fat6 faat3 佛法) ~~Budhism~~ ``Budhist methodologies``; _fat shau_ (fat6 sau2 佛手), citron; _Fat t'ong_ (fat6 tong4 佛堂), Budhist temples; _fat hau she sam_ (fat6 hau2 se4 sam1 佛口蛇心), a villainous hypocrite. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱宀⿰未又 to 寂 and corrected 𬇠 to 滅. Williams' 佛法: Corrected _Fat_ (fat1) to _Fat_ (fat6) and corrected _fat_ (faat6) to _fat_ (faat3). 【佛】 (bjut) (_noun-like_) buddha

fat1.text: U+5DFF This character has a single vertical stroke through the centre. Not to be confused with 市, which has a dot at the top.

U+5F17 (F1.86 弗) 不也 (Page 49 弗) A negative (Kangxi: 不也); not, it should not be, not permissible (Kangxi: 不可也不然也); contrary (Kangxi: 違也), distorted (Kangxi: 不正也); _fat loi_ (fat1 loi4 弗來), he will not come; _fat tsai sz'_ (fat1 zai3 si6 弗濟事) it will not be done. To rid, to remove (Kangxi: 去也). 【弗】 (pjut) (_adverb-like_) not (_adjective-like_) gusty

U+5F7F 佛 拂 髴 (F1.86 彿) 彷彿猶似樣也 (F1.86 髴) 髣髴猶近似也 (Page 49 彿) Like, as if; used in the phrase _fong fat_ (fong2 fat1 彷彿) resembling (Kangxi: 彷彿也). (Page 50 髴; here normalised to 彿) like, nearly (Kangxi 髴: 若似也, 或作彿), applied to things. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 依 to 似. 【彷彿】 (phjangX phjut) (_verb-like_) to seem (like), to appear like 【彿】 (phjut) Used in 彷彿.

U+5FFD 曶 芴 (F1.86 忽) 倐倐急遽也又滅也輕也忘也 (Page 50 忽) A single floss of silk (Kangxi: 輕也一蠶爲一忽十忽爲一絲, 忽蜘蛛網也); the smallest fraction in notation, a ~~millionth~~ ``hundred thousandth``; to forget (Kangxi: 忘也忽忽不省事也), to disregard (Kangxi: 慢忽也); to make light of~~,~~``;`` to extinguish``, to obliterate`` (Kangxi: 滅也); neg``l``ectful; suddenly, abrupt (Kangxi: 倏忽也), unexpectedly, all at once; _fat in_ (fat1 jin4 忽然), suddenly; _fat yau yan loi_ (fat1 jau5 jan4 loi4 忽有人來), just then a man came; _~~heng~~ ``hing`` fat_ (hing1 fat1 輕忽) to make light of``, to neglect``; _fat leuk_ (fat1 loek6 忽略) to forget; _fat shi_ (fat1 si4 忽時), not punctual; _fat ming fat mit_ (fat1 ming4 fat1 mit6 忽明忽滅) it appeared and disappeared suddenly. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing and incomplete strokes in 遽 and 滅, and normalised ⿱亾心 to 忘. Williams' "millionth": 忽 is a millionth of a 尺, but a hundred thousandth of a 寸. The progression of length units is 忽絲毫釐分寸尺. Since the convention is to identify 分 with a tenth, 釐 with a hundredth, etc., the base should be 寸, not 尺, whence "hundred thousandth" instead of "millionth". Williams' 輕忽: Corrected _heng_ (heng1) to _hing_ (hing1). Williams' 忽明忽滅: Corrected _ming_ (ming1) to _ming_ (ming4). 【忽】 (xwot) (_adjective-like_) sudden (_verb-like_) to neglect (_verb-like_) to obliterate (_noun-like_) floss
U+60DA 忽 曶 (F1.86 惚) 恍惚 (Page 50 惚) Minute, abstruse (Kangxi: 怳惚微妙不測貌); inexplicable; that which dazzles or flutters the mind. 【惚】 (xwot) (_adjective-like_) confused

U+62C2 (F1.86 拂) 拭也 (F1.86 拂) 拂拭又蠅拂 (F1.88 拂 _Pat_ (bat6)) 去也拭也除也擊也矯也逆也 (Page 49 拂) To brush, to wipe, to dust (Kangxi: 去也拭也除也, 拂去塵埃也); to push away, (...); to expel, (...); ~~a sort of flail;~~ _fat ch'an_ (fat1 can4 拂塵), to brush away dust; _fat shik_ (fat1 sik1 拂拭) to brush away; _ying fat_ (jing4 fat1 蠅拂) a fly whip; Fan Wan entry: The headword 拂 appears twice under the reading _fat_ (fat1). To strike obliquely, to glance (Kangxi: 過擊也, 擊而過之也). A duster (Kangxi: 拂塵具). 【拂-unvoiced】 (phjut) (_verb-like_) to glance (_verb-like_) to brush (off), to wipe (off)

fat6.text: U+62C2 佛 bat6 (per Fan Wan) fat1 (per 《正韻牋》敷勿切) (F1.88 拂 _Pat_ (bat6)) 去也拭也除也擊也矯也逆也 (Page 49 拂 _Fat_ (fat1); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) (...), to oppose (Kangxi: 與咈通違也戾也); (...), to contradict; perverse, disobedient; _fat yan sing_ (fat6 jan4 sing3 拂人性) to thwart another's wishes. (Page 50 佛; here normalised to 拂) Contrary, unreasonable (Kangxi 佛: 逆也戾也, 與拂同); to turn aside (Kangxi 佛: 捩也); (Page 360 拂 _Pat_ (bat6)) _pat lu_ (bat6 leoi6 拂戾) perverse, obstinate; _pat yan chi sing_ (bat6 jan4 zi1 sing3 拂人之性) to act contrary to other people. Usually read _fat_ (fat1). 【拂-voiced】 (bjut) (_verb-like_) to oppose, to turn aside

fat1.text: U+39FE (Page 50 㧾) To take up refuse, to clean away; to bale out, to dip up; _fat tau_ (fat1 dau2 㧾斗), a dust-board; _fat shui_ (fat1 seoi2 㧾水), to bale water; _fat lap sap_ (fat1 laap6 saap3 㧾垃圾) to take up rubbish. Williams' 㧾垃圾: Corrected _lap_ (lap6) to _lap_ (laap6). To strike, to hit (Kangxi: 擊也, 南楚相推搏曰㧾). To dust (Kangxi: 一曰去塵也).
U+6612 㫚 mat6 (alternative per 《廣韻》文弗切) Morning twilight, dawn (Kangxi: 尚冥也, 旦明也, 謂日尚冥蓋未明之時也).
U+3ADA This character is ⿱勿日 under the sun radical (Kangxi p.492#2). For the visually similar character under the say radical, see 曶 fat1.
U+66F6 This character is ⿱勿曰 under the say radical (Kangxi p.502#8). For the visually similar character under the sun radical, see 㫚 fat1.

U+7953 弗 fai3 (alternative per 《廣韻》方肺切) (F1.86 祓) 除也 (Page 50 祓) ~~Clean, pure~~ ``To cleanse, to purify`` (Kangxi: 潔也); to disperse; to drive off; to wash away; a sacrifice or baptism to obtain good (Kangxi: 除災求福也); _fat ch'u_ (fat1 ceoi4 祓除), to ward off. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 袚 to 祓. Williams' "Clean, pure": These should be rendered as verbs instead. To remove (除也, 通作弗, 弗之言祓也). Good fortune (Kangxi: 通作茀, 祓福也). 【祓】 (phjut, pjojH) (_verb-like_) to remove, to purify

U+7A9F gwat6 (uncommon) wat1 (uncommon) (F1.86 窟) 孔穴也 (Page 50 窟) A hole in the ground (Kangxi: 窟室也孔穴也); a stable or sty dug out of the earth; _fat lung_ (fat1 lung4-1 窟窿), a hole, a grotto; _shu fat_ (syu2 fat1 鼠窟) a rat-hole; _kau t'o sam fat_ (gaau2 tou3 saam1 fat1 狡兔三窟) a cunning rabbit has three holes; _shi fat_ (si2 fat1 屎窟) buttocks. Williams' 窟窿, 鼠窟, 狡兔三窟: Corrected _fat_ (fat6) to _fat_ (fat1). Williams' 窟窿: Corrected _lung_ (lung5) to _lung_ (lung4-1). 【窟】 (khwot) (_noun-like_) cave (_noun-like_) hole 【屎窟】 (si2 fat1) Arse (mostly the buttocks, sometimes the anus). 屎窟窿 (si2 fat1 lung4-1): "The arsehole." 屎窟痕 (si2 fat1 han4): "His arse must have been itchy" (i.e. he had no good reason to do that). 正屎窟鬼 (zing3 si2 fat1 gwai2): "What a right arsehole." 生仔冇屎窟 (saang1 zai2 mou5 si2 fat1): "Have a kid without an arse" (as a divine punishment for unethical behaviour).

U+7B0F 曶 (F1.86 笏) 簪笏牙簡也 (Page 50 笏) A tablet of ivory, bamboo, gem, or wood, used at audiences in former times (Kangxi: 公及士所搢也 etc., 笏一名手版品官所執); meant for making memoranda (Kangxi: 笏忽也備忽忘也), and then used as ornaments; they were held before the breast; _chap fat i ch'iu_ (zap1 fat1 ji5 ciu4 執笏以朝), to take the tablet in court; _ching fat_ (zing3 fat1 正笏) to hold the tablet. Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 簪. 【笏】 (xwot) (_noun-like_) tablet

U+7D31 紼 韍 黻 (F1.86 紱) 綬也 (Page 50 紱) A ribbon to hold a seal (Kangxi: 綬也, 紱或作紼); ~~used for the two preceding (茀, 紼).~~ (Page 50 韍; here normalised to 紱) a string on a seal (Kangxi 韍: 璽之組也). Williams' "used for the two preceding": Not the case for 茀, and the other way round for 紼 (i.e. 紼 is used for 紱). 【紱】 (pjut) (_noun-like_) seal-ribbon

U+7D3C 綍 茀 (F1.86 茀) 繫印組也又引柩索 (Page 49 紼) Raveled silk (Kangxi: 亂糸也); a trace to drag a bier (Kangxi: 從前引之曰紼紼發也發車使前); _chap fat_ (zap1 fat1 執紼) to accompany a funeral. Williams' "Raveled silk": 《說文》 actually has 亂系也, with 段玉裁's annotations saying this should instead be 亂枲也, "ravelled hemp". A rope (Kangxi: 紼繂也, 紼縳也). A coffin-rope (for burial) (Kangxi: 引棺索也). 【紼】 (pjut) (_noun-like_) rope, coffin-rope

U+7D8D but6 (per Fan Wan) (F2.80 綍 _Put_ (but6)) 綸綍王言之 (Page 402 綍 _Put_ (but6); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat1)) Silken cords which are used to carry coffins by (Kangxi: 同紼, 綍舉棺索也); _met._ fine, elegant, applied to the emperor's words; _lun ~~put~~ ``fat``_ (leon4 fat1 綸綍) eloquent, persuasive, as imperial sayings. 【綍】 (pjut) (_noun-like_) rope, coffin-rope

U+889A but3 (per 《廣韻》北末切 for "barbarian-clothing" and "knee-cover" senses) fai3 (per 《集韻》方未切 for "knee-cover" sense) (Page 50 袚) A covering for the knees (Kangxi: 蔽膝也 etc.); ~~a short tunic worn in southern China~~ ``clothing worn by south-eastern barbarians`` (Kangxi: 蠻夷服也). The reading _fat_ (fat1) is not unattested for the "barbarian-clothing" sense; 《集韻》 has the reading 敷勿切, explained 蠻夷衣. Fan Wan entry: The headword 袚 is a misprint of 祓. Williams' "short tunic worn in southern China": This is a poor rendering of Kangxi 蠻夷服也. Clothing for small children (Kangxi: 褿袚䙁褯也).
U+97CD 巿 紱 芾 黻 (F1.86 韍) 以韋爲之所以蔽膝 (Page 50 韍) A knee-pad of leather (Kangxi: 本作巿韠也); a cover or defense for the bosom; Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 蔽 and 膝. 【韍】 (pjut) (_noun-like_) knee-cover
U+9AF4 fai3 (per Kangxi 芳味切 for "dishevelled" sense) (F1.86 髴) 髣髴猶近似也 (Page 50 髴) Disheveled hair (Kangxi: 髮亂貌); head ornaments (Kangxi: 婦人首飾);

U+9EFB (F1.86 黻) 黼黻 (Page 50 黻) ~~Variegated,~~ ``An embroidered symbol in`` black and azure mixed (Kangxi: 黑與靑相次文, 黼黻彰也, etc.); to embroider in colors; elegant, flowery (as writing); a variegated garment (Kangxi: 黻紩也畫黻紩文綵於衣也 etc.); _fat min_ (fat1 min5 黻冕), an embroidered coronet (Kangxi: 黻冕 etc.). Fan Wan: Fixed missing strokes in 黼. The symbol (文, 彰) referred to by Kangxi is like two 弓 back-to-back, or 亞 with the horizontal strokes disconnected. 【黻】 (pjut) (_noun-like_) embroidered-emblem

fat6.text: U+4E4F (F2.73 乏 _Fat_ (faat6)) 貧乏窮乏匱也 (Page 51 乏 _Fat_ (faat6); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) Want~~, failure, defective~~; without (Kangxi: 無也); ``a vacancy (Kangxi: 匱也);`` to fail; to spoil (Kangxi: 廢也)~~, to injure~~; empty, poor, fatigued; _fat shik_ (fat6 sik6 乏食) in want of food; _p'an fat_ (pan4 fat6 貧乏) beggared. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱宀躬 to 窮, and corrected ⿷匚責 to 匱. A safety screen used in archery (Kangxi: 射者所蔽). 【乏】 (bjop) (_verb-like_) to lack (_noun-like_) vacancy (_verb-like_) to waste (_noun-like_) safety-screen

U+4F10 (F2.72 伐 _Fat_ (faat6)) 征伐殺伐又誇功也 (Page 51 伐 _Fat_ (faat6); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) To destroy, to strike``, to smite`` (Kangxi: 攻殺擊刺也)~~, to fight~~; to punish, to subdue (Kangxi: 征伐)~~, to desolate~~; to cut down (Kangxi: 斫木也); to brag (Kangxi: 自稱其功曰伐); meritorious deeds (Kangxi: 伐功也); a go-between; _shat fat li hoi_ (saat3 fat6 lei6 hoi6 殺伐利害) famous for prowess, invincible; _fat tsui_ (fat6 zeoi6 伐罪) to punish crime; _tsok fat_ (zok3 fat6 作伐) to act as go-between. Williams' 殺伐利害: Corrected _shat_ (sat1) to _shat_ (saat3). Williams' 伐罪: Corrected _tsui_ to _tsui_. To hit, to strike (Kangxi: 考擊鐘鼓也). 【伐】 (bjot) (_verb-like_) to smite, to strike, to fell (_verb-like_) to boast
U+5488 佛 拂 To oppose, to go against (Kangxi: 違也, 戾也, 通作佛拂). 【咈】 (bjut) (_verb-like_) to oppose, to go against

U+6A43 (Page 51 筏 _Fat_ (faat6); here corrected to 橃 and normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) a large ship ~~like a raft for size~~ (Kangxi 橃: 海中大船, 俗作筏非是); an ark. According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, "large ship" is an olden sense of 橃 that is distinct from 筏 "raft". Williams (following Fan Wan and to some extent Kangxi 筏) includes the "large ship" sense under 筏, and does not provide a separate entry for 橃. I have split things up accordingly.
U+7782 伐 A shield (Kangxi: 盾也, 通作伐). 【瞂】 (bjot) (_noun-like_) shield

U+7B4F 橃 (F2.72 筏 _Fat_ (faat6)) 海中大船 (Page 51 筏 _Fat_ (faat6); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) A bamboo raft (Kangxi: 編竹渡水曰筏); According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, "large ship" is an olden sense of 橃 that is distinct from 筏 "raft". Williams (following Fan Wan and to some extent Kangxi 筏) includes the "large ship" sense under 筏, and does not provide a separate entry for 橃. I have split things up accordingly.

U+7F70 (F2.73 罰 _Fat_ (faat6)) 責也治也 (Page 51 罰 _Fat_ (faat6); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) A peccadillo (Kangxi: 辠之小者 etc.), a crime; to punish, to castigate, to flog (Kangxi: 罰謂撻擊之也); to fine (Kangxi: 出金贖罪); a forfeit; _fat chak_ (fat6 zaak3 罰簀) to bamboo; _fat tsau_ (fat6 zau2 罰酒), a forfeit of wine; _fat fung_ (fat6 fung6-2 罰俸), to mulct an officer's salary; _fat ngan_ (fat6 ngan4 罰銀), to fine, a forfeit. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 責 and 治. Williams' 罰簀: Normalised _chak_ (zak1) to _chak_ (zaak3). 【罰】 (bjot) (_verb-like_) to punish (_verb-like_) to fine

U+95A5 伐 (F2.72 閥 _Fat_ (faat6)) 閥閱自序也積其功曰閥明其等曰閱 (Page 51 閥 _Fat_ (faat6); here normalised to _Fat_ (fat6)) A door on the left (Kangxi: 門在左曰閥在右曰閱); meritorious services, to set forth one's merit (Kangxi: 明其等曰閥積日曰閱); _fat ut_ (fat6 jyut6 閥閱) the side doors of a gateway; worthy services, accumulated merit.

fau1.text: U+574F (F1.50 坏) 一坏之土 (Page 51 坏) A hillock of earth.
U+6294 (F1.50 抔) 以手掬物也 (Page 51 抔)

fau4.text: U+7D11 fau1 (per 《廣韻》匹尤切 and 甫鳩切) fau2 (per 《廣韻》芳否切) fu1 (per 《集韻》芳無切 for "bright-coloured" sense) (F1.52 紑) 衣鮮絜貌 (Page 51 紑) ~~New silken~~ ``Fresh, clean, as`` garments (Kangxi: 白鮮衣貌, 紑絜鮮貌); ~~elegant,~~ lustrous``, as silk`` (Kangxi: 繒色鮮). Fan Wan entry: Corrected malformed 絜. 【紑】 (phjuw, phjuwX, pjuw) (_adjective-like_) fresh, bright

fau2.text: U+5426 (F1.53 否) 不然之詞 (Page 52 否) Not so, ~~ought~~ not (Kangxi: 不也 etc.); used at the end of sentences as a negative interrogation; else, whether, otherwise; _ut fau_ (jyut6 fau2 ？否), it is not; _ho fau_ (ho2 fau2 可否), will or can it do, or not; _shi fau_ (si6 fau2 是否), is it so? _fau tsak wai chi_ (fau2 zak1 wai1 zi1 否則威之), if ~~it be bad~~ ``not``, then ~~frown it away~~ ``awe them``. Williams' "a negative interrogation": Specifically, to present an alternative in the negative, i.e. "or not". Williams' 否則威之: Corrected _wai_ (wai4) to _wai_ (wai1). This is Kangxi's example from 《書益稷》. 【否】 (pjuwX) (_adverb-like_) nay (_adverb-like_) not

U+7F36 (F1.50 缶 _Fau_ (fau1)) (Page 51 缶 _Fau_ (fau1); here corrected to _Fau_ (fau2)) Earthenware in general (Kangxi: 瓦缶之器); a vessel used to contain wine (Kangxi: 瓦器所以盛酒漿); one on which time was ~~noted~~ ``beaten`` (Kangxi: 秦人鼓之以節歌); a jar (Kangxi: 盎謂之缶, 缶卽盎也大腹而斂口); _nga fau_ (ngaa5 fau2 瓦缶), earthenware. The 121st radical ("jar"). 【缶】 (pjuwX) (_noun-like_) earthenware

fau4.text: U+6D6E pou4 (vernacular) (F1.52 浮) 泛也溢也 (Page 51 浮) To float (Kangxi: 氾也), to drift (Kangxi: 順流曰浮); to overflow, to exceed (Kangxi: 溢也過也), to inundate; ~~past time;~~ light, unsteady, fleeting; excessive; _fau fan_ (fau4 faan3 浮泛) floating; _vulg._ _~~po po pan~~ ``p'o p'o p'an``_ (pou4 pou4 paan3 浮浮泛); _fau in_ (fau4 jin4 浮言), unmeaning words; _fau ts'oi_ (fau4 coi4 浮財), floating property; _fau yau tsoi shai_ (fau4 jau4 zoi6 sai3 浮遊在世) engaged in worldly affairs; _fau shek_ (fau4 sek6 浮石) pumice; _kom fau_ (gam2 fau4 敢浮), heedless, flagitious; _fau t'o_ (fau4 tou4 浮屠), a pagoda; _fau shang_ (fau4 sang1 浮生), the living; _fau fau_ (fau4 fau4 浮浮), vain, much. Williams' "past time": This is a misreading of Kangxi 先時曰浮. The example given is 《書盤庚》鮮以不浮于天時. Reading 浮 as the noun "past time" clearly does not make sense. Instead, 浮 should be read as a verb, "preempt". Williams' 浮浮泛: Corrected _po po pan_ (bou4 bou4 baan3) to _p'o p'o p'an_ (pou4 pou4 paan3). Williams' 浮浮在世: Corrected _shai_ (sai6) to _shai_ (sai3). Flippant, levitous (Kangxi: 輕也). Falling heavily (Kangxi: 雨雪盛貌). To punish with drink as forfeit (Kangxi: 浮罰也謂罰爵也). 【浮】 (bjuw) (_verb-like_) to float (_verb-like_) to penalise (_adjective-like_) (_met._) heavy, strong 【浮面】 (pou4 min6-2) On the surface. 浮面𢳂油 (pou4 min6-2 bat1 jau4): "Skim the oil along the surface." 【浮頭】 (pou4 tau4) To surface.

U+7F58 (F1.50 罘 _Fau_ (fau1)) 罘罳屏風也 (Page 51 罘 _Fau_ (fau1); here corrected to _Fau_ (fau4)) A rabbit net (Kangxi: 兔罟, 罘罝也); a ~~wooden~~ screen before an official residence (Kangxi: 罘罳 etc.). Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿱𠔿不 to 罘, and normalised ⿱𠔿思 to 罳. 【罘】 (bjuw) (_noun-like_) rabbit-net

U+82A3 (F1.53 芣 _Fau_ (fau2)) (Page 52 芣 _Fau_ (fau2); here corrected to _Fau_ (fau4)) The plantago or plantain leaf, called _fau i_ (fau4 ji5 芣苡) (Kangxi: 芣苢馬舄馬舄車前); _ch'e ``t``s'in ts'o_ (ce1 cin4 cou2 車前草) is the common name; it is eaten as greens. Williams' 車前草: Inferred incompletely printed letter _t_ in _ts'in_. 【芣】 (bjuw) Used in 芣苡. 【芣苡】 (bjuw yiX) (_noun-like_) plantain

U+8709 (F1.52 蜉) 蜉蝣虫名 (Page 52 蜉) A large ant, called _p'i fau_ (pei4 fau4 蚍蜉) (Kangxi: 蚍蜉大螘); _fau yau_ (fau4 jau4 蜉蝣), an ephemera, produced from muck (Kangxi: 蜉蝣渠略也 etc.). 【蜉】 (bjuw) Used in 蜉蝣. 【蜉蝣】 (bjuw yuw) (_noun-like_) up-wing fly

fau6.text: U+57E0 bou6 (per Kangxi 同步) (F1.56 埠) 舶船埠頭 (Page 52 ~~？~~``埠``) A landing where trade is carried on, a mart on the seaside, an unwalled settlement; _fau t'au_ (fau6 tau4 埠頭), a port; _kuk fau_ (guk1 fau6 穀埠) a grain market; _tsz' fau_ (zi2 fau6 子埠) a sub salt depot where no duties are levied; _pan fau_ (baan6 fau6 辦埠) manager of a salt depot; _kwo fau_ (gwo3 fau6 過埠) to go from port to port. Cognate with 埗 bou6 (TODO). Williams' 埠頭: Corrected _t'au_ (tau5) to _t'au_ (tau4).
U+86D7 𧌓 (Page 714 𧌓; here normalised to 蛗) A kind of grasshopper or cricket; _fau chung_ (fau6 zung1 蛗螽), a sort of green locust; _met._ many children.

U+961C 阝 (as a character component on the left) 𨸏 (F1.56 阜) 山無石曰阜又曲阜地名又民豐物阜 (Page 52 阜阝) A ``great`` mound or hill of earth (Kangxi: 大陸曰阜, 山無石者); ``the 170th ``radical ("mound") of elevations, hills, &c.; great (Kangxi: 大也), large (Kangxi: 肥也), high, abundant (Kangxi: 盛也), numerous (Kangxi: 多也); _mat fau_ (mat6 fau6 物阜) plenty of things; _fung fau_ (fung1 fau6 豐阜) abundant, as a harvest. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 豐. 【阜】 (bjuw) (_noun-like_) great-mound (_adjective-like_) great, large, abundant

fe1.text: U+5561 fei1 (uncommon) 【啡】 (fe1) Short for 咖啡 (coffee). 齋啡 (zaai1 fe1): "Straight coffee." Brown. 【啡色】 (fe1 sik1) Short for 咖啡色. 【咖啡色】 (gaa3 fe1 sik1) Brown.

fe4.text: U+6DDD 啡 fe4-2 (rising second in 淝淝) fe6 (alternative for "spray" sense) 【淝】 (fe4, fe6) To spray. 淝濕咗 (fe4 sap1 zo2): "Sprayed it wet." 淝緊水 (fe4 gan2 seoi2): "It's spraying water" (i.e. the sprinkler is on). To shoot. 淝中咗 (fe4 zung3 zo2): "Shot and hit." 【淝淝】 (fe4 fe4-2) Diarrhoea. Commonly spelt 啡啡. 屙淝淝 (o1 fe4 fe4-2): "Have diarrhoea."

fei4.text: U+6DDD 肥 (F1.39 淝) 地名又水名 (Page 714 淝) Diverging streams (Kangxi: 所出同所歸異曰肥泉); a river in Nganhwui (安徽) running into Lake Ch'au (巢湖) by Luchau fu (廬州府) (Kangxi: 水名 etc.). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 各 to 名.

fei1.text: U+5983 (F1.35 妃) 妃嬪 (Page 52 ~~？~~``妃``) Royal concubines (Kangxi: 女與己身儔也古嬪御之貴次于后者曰妃); _wong fi_ (wong4 fei1 王妃), wife of the heir apparent; _ching fi_ (zing3 fei1 正妃), the empress; _t'in fi_ (tin1 fei1 天妃), the Amphitrite of the Chinese (Kangxi: 天妃水神); _fi tsz' siu_ (fei1 zi2 siu3 妃子笑) the best sort of laichi (荔枝). Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿰女巳 to 妃. A spouse (Kangxi: 匹也). 【妃】 (phj+j) (_noun-like_) consort
U+6249 (F1.35 扉) 戸也 (Page 52 扉) A wooden ~~door with two leaves~~ ``door-leaf`` (Kangxi: 戶扇也, 闔謂之扉, 以木曰扉以葦曰扇); _met._ a rustic house. 【扉】 (pj+j) (_noun-like_) door-leaf
U+7DCB fei2 (common in 緋聞) (F1.35 緋) 絳色也一曰赤練 (Page 52 緋) Red or crimson silk (Kangxi: 帛赤色, 絳色又赤練); _fi p'o_ (fei1 pou4 緋袍), carnation robes. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 終 to 絳.
U+871A (F1.35 蜚) 古飛字聲名顯播也 (Page 52 蜚) ``To fly.`` The Fan Wan defines it, "~~famous.~~ ``prominently spreading,``" ``as a reputation.``

fei2.text: U+871A fai3 (alternative per 《正韻牋》芳未切 for "grasshopper" sense) fai6 (per 《廣韻》扶沸切 for "stinking-insect" and "ominous-animal" senses) (Page 52 蜚 _Fi_ (fei1); here corrected to _Fi_ (fei2)) A stinking bug which eats clothes and grain (Kangxi: ...然則蜚是臭惡之蟲害人衣物...); a fabulous animal ominous of pestilence (Kangxi: 獸名, ...見則天下大疫). The reading _fi_ (fei2) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 敷尾切, encompassing the "grasshopper" and "animal" senses. 《廣韻》府尾切 _fi_ (fei2) is unhelpfully explained 蟲名咸蜚. The grasshopper (Kangxi: 蟲名負蠜也). 【蜚】 (pj+jX, bj+jH) (_noun-like_) stinking-pest

fei1.text: U+88F6 (F1.35 裶) 曳衣也又衣長貌 (Page 52 裶) ~~To lift up the skirt;~~ long, trailing``, as`` dresses (Kangxi: 衣長貌, 曳衣貌); _fi i_ (fei1 ji1 裶衣), ~~to hold up the~~ ``a dragging`` dress. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in headword 裶.
U+970F (F1.35 霏) 雨雪之貌 (Page 52 霏) Rain, sleet, or snow driven by the wind, and filling the air (Kangxi: 雨雪貌, 雰也, 霏甚貌). 【霏】 (phj+j) (_adjective-like_) heavy falling

U+975E (F1.35 非) 不是也 (Page 52 非) Not right, not good (Kangxi: 不是也); not so, opposite (Kangxi: 違也); false, bad, wrong; ~~shameless, low (Kangxi: 下也);~~ to slander (Kangxi: 訾也), to reproach (Kangxi: 責也); _'m chi shi fi_ (m4 zi1 si6 fei1 唔知是非), I don't know whether it is so or not; _kong shi fi_ (gong2 si6 fei1 講是非), to asperse, tittle-tattle; _fi ~~heng~~ ``hing`` yung i_ (fei1 hing1 jung4 ji6 非輕容易) this is not so easy; _fi lai_ (fei1 lai5 非禮), improper, immodest; _hi fi_ (hei2 fei1 豈非), is it not so! i.e. it is true; _fi sheung_ (fei1 soeng4 非常), uncommon, superior; _fi ya_ (fei1 jaa5 非也), not so. Williams' 非輕容易: Corrected _heng_ (heng1) to _hing_ (hing1), and corrected _yung_ (jung1) to _yung_ (jung4). Williams' 非禮: Now to molest. Williams' 非常: Corrected _sheung_ (soeng1) to _sheung_ (soeng4). The 175th radical ("wrong"). 【非】 (pj+j) (_verb-like_) to be not (_adverb-like_) not (_adjective-like_) wrong (_verb-like_) to repugn

U+98DB 蜚 (F1.35 飛) 飛鳥 (Page 52 飛) To fly (Kangxi: 鳥翥, 飛翔); to flit; flying; to go swiftly; lofty; quick; _fi hu_ (fei1 heoi3 飛去) to fly away; _fi niu_ (fei1 niu5 飛鳥), or _fi k'am_ (fei1 kam4 飛禽), birds; _fi kam_ (fei1 gam1 飛金), to put on gold leaf; _fi ~~im~~ ``shim`` tsau pik_ (fei1 sim4 zau2 bik3 飛檐走壁) an expert thief; _fi shu_ (fei1 syu2 飛鼠), a bat; _fi po_ (fei1 bou3 飛報) to haste to tell; _fi t'ang_ (fei1 tang4 飛騰), to rise rapidly in office; _fi t'in pun sz'_ (fei1 tin1 bun2 si6 飛天本事) incomparably clever; _Fi-lim_ (fei1 lim4 飛廉), Boreas, the god of wind (Kangxi: ...世因以飛廉爲風伯之名...). Williams' 飛檐走壁: Changed _im_ (jim4) to common reading _shim_ (sim4). The 183rd radical ("fly"). 【飛】 (pj+j) (_verb-like_) to fly 【飛】 (fei1) To cut off hair. 飛髮 (fei1 faat3): "Get a haircut." 飛短啲 (fei1 dyun2 di1): "Cut it shorter." 飛光佢 (fei1 gwong1 keoi5): "Cut it all off" (i.e. go bald). To skip. 巴士飛站 (baa1 si2 fei1 zaam6): "A bus skipping a stop." A ticket. 買飛 (maai5 fei1): "Buy a ticket." 黃牛飛 (wong4 ngau4 fei1): "Scalped tickets." 【飛起】 (fei1 hei2) Used after 到 to indicate an extreme degree. Similar to "to death" in English after a verb. 熱到飛起 (jit6 dou3 fei1 hei2): "It's so hot I could fly." 鬧到飛起 (naau6 dou3 fei1 hei2): "Scolded so badly I could fly."

U+9A11 匪 (for walking-related senses) (F1.35 騑) 馬行不止 (Page 52 騑) The two outside of a team of four horses (Kangxi: 車有一轅四馬中兩馬夾轅名服馬兩邊名騑馬亦名驂馬); _fi fi_ (fei1 fei1 騑騑), the regular march of horses (Kangxi: 騑騑馬行不止貌). (Page 53 匪 _Fi_ (fei2); here corrected to _Fi_ (fei1) and normalised to 騑) regular march of horses (Kangxi 匪: 同騑騑騑馬行不止貌), beautiful (Kangxi 匪: 行而有文也); 【騑-adjective】 (phj+j) (_adjective-like_) walking (on), trotting (on) 【騑-noun】 (pj+j) (_noun-like_) outside-horse

fei2.text: U+60B1 (F1.39 悱) 口欲言而未能也 (Page 53 悱) Embarrassed, unable to speak out one's ideas (Kangxi: 口欲言而未能之貌). Williams' "Embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed". 【悱】 (phj+jX) (_adjective-like_) eager to express

U+6590 匪 (F1.39 斐) 文貌 (Page 53 斐) Elegant, adorned (Kangxi: 分別文也 etc.), graceful, polished, applied to actions or writings; _fi mi_ (fei2 mei5 斐美), pleasing; _fi in shing cheung_ (fei2 jin4 sing4 zoeng1 斐然成章), ``as splendid as`` finished composition. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 貌. Williams' "adorned": Especially with small markings (Kangxi: 小文之貌). Williams' 斐然成章: Corrected _shing_ (sing1) to _shing_ (sing4). 【斐】 (phj+jX) (_adjective-like_) decorated, splendid

U+68D0 (F1.39 棐) 篤棐輔也 (Page 53 ~~榧~~~~棑~~``棐``) a piece of wood tied to a bow at the grasp to ~~strengthen~~ ``straighten`` it (Kangxi 棐: 輔也, 輔卽弓檠也故从木); to assist; ~~to lean on;~~ _tuk fi_ (duk1 fei2 篤棐), to help ``in earnest``; Fan Wan entry: Fixed incomplete strokes in 輔. Williams does not provide separate entries for 榧 and 棐 (which is misprinted as 棑). Note that 棐 can be used for 榧, but not the other way round. I have split things up accordingly. 【棐】 (pj+jX) (_verb-like_) to assist

U+69A7 棐 (Page 53 榧~~棑~~``棐``) A kind of cabinet wood with lines (Kangxi 榧: 榧似煔而材光文彩如柏古謂文木通作棐), suitable to make furniture (Kangxi 棐: 與榧同木名可爲几); _fi tsz'_ (fei2 zi2 榧子) a long nut resembling the hazel-nut, found in Shantung (山東). Williams does not provide separate entries for 榧 and 棐 (which is misprinted as 棑). Note that 棐 can be used for 榧, but not the other way round. I have split things up accordingly. The nutmeg yew (Kangxi 榧: 木名子可食療白蟲).

U+7BDA 匪 棐 (F1.39 篚) 筐篚箱屬圓曰篚 (Page 53 匪; here normalised to 篚) A ~~square~~ bamboo box``-like container`` (Kangxi 匪: 匪如篋, ~~竹器方曰匪,~~ 器如竹篋今从竹爲筐篚字); (Page 53 篚) A round bamboo basket (Kangxi 篚: 竹器方曰筐圓曰篚, 通作匪, 亦作棐). 【篚】 (pj+jX) (_noun-like_) bamboo-basket

U+7FE1 fai6 (per 《廣韻》扶沸切) fei6 (per 《廣韻》扶沸切) (Page 53 翡 _Fi_ (fei6); here normalised to _Fi_ (fei2)) A kingfisher, with beautiful plumage (Kangxi: 赤羽雀也出鬱林, 鳥似翠而赤); the cock is called _fi_ (fei2 翡), the hen _ts'ui_ (ceoi3 翠) (Kangxi: 翠鳥形如燕赤而雄曰翡靑而雌曰翠); _fi ts'ui yuk_ (fei2 ceoi3 juk6 翡翠玉) ~~chrysoprase~~ ``jadeite``.

U+8AB9 非 fai3 (per 《廣韻》方味切) fei1 (per 《廣韻》甫微切) (F1.39 誹) 誹謗 (Page 53 誹) To backbite, to speak against (Kangxi: 謗也, 𧨚也, 或省作非); _fi p'ong_ (fei2 pong3 誹謗) to slander. The reading _fi_ (fei2) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the readings 妃尾切 and 府尾切, both explained 謗也. 【誹】 (pj+j, pj+jH) (_verb-like_) to slander

fei4.text: U+80A5 fei4-2 (in 阿肥, and rising second in vernacular 肥肥地) (F1.39 肥) 不瘦也潤也 (Page 53 肥) Fat, oily, fleshy (Kangxi: 多肉也), rich (Kangxi: 饒裕也), fertile (Kangxi: 田有肥瘠); _fi chong_ (fei4 zong3 肥壯) robust; _fi tai_ (fei4 daai6 肥大) corpulent; _fi lo_ (fei4 lou2 肥佬) or _a fi_ (aa3 fei4-2 阿肥), a fat man; _fi yun_ (fei4 jeon6 肥潤) fertile; _fi ~~tun tun~~ ``tat tat``_ (fei4 dat1 dat1 肥腯腯) obese, fat as a pig; _fi mi_ (fei4 mei5 肥美), plump, good looking. (Page 714 肥) As a verb. To fatten; fattened; to advantage one; _chat ku fi ki_ (zat1 gu3 fei4 gei2 只顧肥己), he only thinks of benefiting himself. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿸疒央 to 瘦, and corrected ⿰冫閏 to 潤. Williams' 肥腯腯: Normalised _tun_ (deon6) to _tat_ (dat1). A horse (Kangxi: 馬亦稱肥). Fertiliser. 【肥】 (bj+j) (_adjective-like_) fat (_adjective-like_) fertile Cantonese prefers 肥 over 胖, even when referring to fatness in humans. 【肥腯腯】 (fei4 dat1 dat1) Chubby. 【肥嘟嘟】 (fei4 dyut1 dyut1) Synonym of 肥腯腯. 【肥揗揗】 (fei4 tan4 tan4) Fat, in a somewhat clumsy or bumbling way. Fatty, greasy.

U+8153 (F1.39 腓) 足肚也又？也詩小人所腓又病也百卉具腓又庇也牛羊腓字之 (Page 53 腓) Calf of the leg (Kangxi: 脛腨也, 腳腨腸也, 腓䏿也, 脛後肉腓腸也); sick, diseased (Kangxi: 病也, 變也); to avoid, to flee from (Kangxi: 避也); to conceal or cover, as animals do each other when herding (Fan Wan: 庇也). Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing stroke in 詩. The incomprehensible character looks like an incomplete 備; context suggests that this should be 避. Williams' "sick, diseased": Especially worsening disease. 【腓】 (bj+j) (_noun-like_) calf (_verb-like_) to deteriorate (_verb-like_) to protect, to cover

fik1.text: U+20C65 May appear as either ⿰口卉 (+5 assumed here) or ⿰口𠦄 (+6), depending on the font. (Page 714 𠱥) A colloquial word. Seems to be a mispronunciation of _fat_ (fat1); _fa fa fik fik_ (faa1 faa1 fik1 fik1 花花𠱥𠱥) fickle, inconstant, fitful, never finishing anything.

fik6.text: U+639D fak6 (alternative) (Page 53 _Fik_ (fik1) exemplified; here corrected to _Fik_ (fik6) and assigned to 掝) To brush off (as a fly); _fik hoi_ (fik6 hoi1 掝開), brush it off! 【掝】 (fik6, fak6) To swing, to flap, especially quickly. 掝甩 (fik6 lat1): "Flap it off." To flick or to brush away quickly. 掝走佢 (fik6 zau2 keoi5): "Flick it away."

fing1.text: U+6379 May appear as either ⿰扌⿱大卉 (+8 assumed here) or ⿰扌⿱大𠦄 (+9), depending on the font.

fing3.text: U+6379 May appear as either ⿰扌⿱大卉 (+8 assumed here) or ⿰扌⿱大𠦄 (+9), depending on the font.

fing6.text: U+6379 May appear as either ⿰扌⿱大卉 (+8 assumed here) or ⿰扌⿱大𠦄 (+9), depending on the font. (Page 53 捹)

fit1.text: U+227B5 (Page 714 under Fit (fit); here assumed to be _Fit_ (fit1) and assigned to 𢞵) A colloquial word. To whip, to spank, to punish.

fo1.text: U+79D1 (F2.58 科) 科坎科等又科甲 (Page 53 科) ~~A root, the hollow culm of grain;~~ ~~a vacuum, empty~~ ``a hollow in a tree`` (Kangxi: 木中空也); a rule``, a regulation, a clause`` (Kangxi: 條也); practice (in medicine)``, an area or field of study``; a class, series, order, rank, gradation (Kangxi: 等也); classifier of herbs; a literary examination (Kangxi: 取人條格曰科第); _luk fo_ (luk6 fo1 六科), six departments in official establishments; _fo kap_ (fo1 gaap3 科甲) a high literary degree; _hoi fo_ (hoi1 fo1 開科), to open the _kuyan_ (舉人) examination; _fo shau_ (fo1 sau1 科收), to receive taxes; _fo ~~pai~~ ``p'ai``_ (fo1 paai3 科派) compulsory ~~service~~ ``contributions``; _fo muk_ (fo1 muk6 科目) an index, series, rank; _fo lam_ (fo1 laam4-2 科籃), basket carried by candidates into the hall; _ching fo_ (zing3 fo1 正科), the alternate third years when examinations occur; _noi fo_ (noi6 fo1 內科), medical practice; _fo t'au_ (fo1 tau4 科頭), bareheaded, head unarmed (Kangxi: 謂不著兜鍪入敵). Williams' 六科: Corrected _luk_ (luk1) to _luk_ (luk6). Williams' 科派: Corrected _pai_ (baai3) to _p'ai_ (paai3). Williams' 科目: Now a subject or area of study. Williams' 內科: Specifically internal medicine, as opposed to 外科, surgery. A department, a section. Taxation. A pit, a hole (Kangxi: 坎也). To sentence, to punish (Kangxi: 科斷也 etc.). 【科】 (khwa) (_noun-like_) degree, class, rank (_noun-like_) ordinance (_noun-like_) hole, hollow (_verb-like_) to levy

U+7AA0 wo1 (per Fan Wan, common) (F2.59 窠 _Wo_ (wo1)) 鳥在木曰巢在穴曰窠 (Page 30|n73 窠 _Ch'au_ (caau4); here corrected to _Fo_ (fo1)) A nest in a cave (Kangxi: 穴中曰窠樹上曰巢). Erroneously read _wo_ (wo1) in the Fan Wan. (Page 714 窠) A hole (Kangxi: 空也); a nest in the ground or a ~~cliff~~ ``cave``; ~~a nest of clay;~~ a burrow; a roost of pheasants (Kangxi: 雞雉所乳謂之窠); _shu niu t'ung fo_ (syu2 niu5 tung4 fo1 鼠鳥同窠), rats and birds in the same nest, _i. e._ all villains in that place. This is sometimes pronounced _wo_ (wo1). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 窼 to 窠. Williams' "Erroneously": In the sense that it deviates from Middle Chinese 苦禾切.

U+874C 科 (F2.58 蝌) 蝌蚪虫名又字體也 (Page 54 ~~蚪~~``蝌``) The tadpole; _fo tau_ (fo1 dau2 蝌蚪), the tadpole (Kangxi 蝌: 蝌蚪本作科 etc.); also called _lui kung u_ (leoi4 gung1 jyu4-2 雷公魚). Fan Wan entry: Corrected missing/blurred strokes in 體. Williams' 雷公魚: Corrected _lui_ to _lui_.

fo2.text: U+4F19 夥 (for "companion/company" senses) 火 fo2-1 (in interrogative intensifier 家伙) (F2.60 夥) 夥伴 (F2.60 伙) 家用物件曰家伙 (Page 54 火; here normalised to 伙) _tsun fo_ (zeon3 fo2 進伙), to move to a new house; (Page 54 伙) A company of ten in the army (Kangxi 火: 府兵十人爲火 etc.); _ka fo_ (gaa1 fo2 家伙), household furniture; _fo t'au_ (fo2 tau4-2 伙頭), a cook; _fo tsai_ (fo2 zai2 伙仔), a scullion; _fo cheung_ (fo2 zoeng2 伙長), chief mate; _i fo_ (ji6 fo2 二伙) second mate; _ho ka fo_ (hou2 gaa1 fo2 好家伙), well, pleasing, handsome; also used in irony, as detestable, badly, done. (Page 54 夥; here normalised to 伙) A colleague, comrade, accomplice, associate; a company, party, band, society; _fo pun_ (fo2 bun6 伙伴) a partner; _hop fo_ (hap6 fo2 合伙), joint partner; _ki fo_ (gei2 fo2 幾伙), how many? as men; _fo ki_ (fo2 gei3 伙記) (or _kai_ (gai3 計)) a fellow; the word _Fokee_ applied to the Chinese by foreigners. Williams' 進伙: Corrected _tsun_ (zeon1) to _tsun_ (zeon3). Note that the preferred term in Cantonese is 入伙. Williams' "A company of ten in the army": This sense should probably be spelt 火 rather than 伙. Meals, especially communal meals. 【弊家伙】 (bai6 gaa1 fo2) An interjectory expression used in response to something going wrong. 【伙記】 (fo2 gei3) An employee who serves customers, especially a waiter. 【家伙】 (gaa1 fo2, gaa1 fo2-1) A mild intensifier for interrogatives. 做乜家伙 (zou6 mat1 gaa1 fo2): "What the crap are you doing?" 咩家伙啊 (me1 gaa1 fo2-1 a3): "What on earth is this?"
U+5801 fo3 (alternative per 《廣韻》苦卧切 for "dust-rising" sense) Dust rising (Kangxi: 塵起貌).
U+5925 㚌 wo6 (per 《廣韻》胡果切) (F2.60 夥) 夥伴 (Page 54 夥) many, numerous (Kangxi: 多也);

U+706B (F2.60 火) 水火五行之一燒炙物也 (Page 54 火) Fire, flame (Kangxi: 火燬也南方之行炎而上); to burn, to consume (Kangxi: 火化也消化物也 etc.); _fo chuk_ (fo2 zuk1 火燭) a conflagration; _fo fa_ (fo2 faa1 火花), a spark; _fo im_ (fo2 jim6 火焰), a flame; _kau fo_ (gau3 fo2 救火), to put out conflagration; _shat fo_ (sat1 fo2 失火), to catch fire; _t'ai fo shik_ (tai2 fo2 sik1 睇火色) watch how the fire is, see the blaze! _fo ts'eung_ (fo2 coeng1 火鎗), fire-arms; _tse ````fo_ (ze3 fo2 借火), trouble you for a light; _mo fo chung_ (mou5 fo2 zung2 冇火種), 'no fire-seed,' poor; _fo shek fan_, (fo2 sek6 fan2 火石粉) chalk; _fo shun_ (fo2 syun4 火船), a steamer; _fo sik_ (fo2 sik1 火熄) the fire is out; _fo p'iu_ (fo2 piu3 火票) an urgent warrant; _mo ming fo_ (mou4 ming4 fo2 無名火), causeless anger; _fo keng_ (fo2 geng2 火頸), testy; _fo pa_ (fo2 baa2 火把), a torch; _fo lo_ (fo2 lou1 火撈), soot; _fo yan_ (fo2 jan5 火引), a match; _fo hi_ (fo2 hei3 火氣) fiery, feverish ; _ta fo_ (daa2 fo2 打火), to strike a fire; _fo fa_ (fo2 faa3 火化), burned up; _mai shai fo hi_ (mai5 sai2 fo2 hei3 咪使火氣) ~~do n't~~ ``don't`` be so hasty; _fo ku to sz'_ (fo2 geoi1 dou6 si6-2 火居道士) a sect among the Tauists. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 燒. Williams' 火焰: Corrected _im_ (jim1) to _im_ (jim6). Williams' 火船: Dated. Williams' 火熄: Corrected _sik_ (sik6) to _sik_ (sik1). Williams' 無名火: Corrected _mo_ (mou5) to _mo_ (mou4). Williams' 火引: Perhaps better rendered "a kindler". Williams' 火化: Corrected _fa_ (faa2) to _fa_ (faa3). Cremation. Williams' 咪使火氣: Corrected _shai_ (sai5) to _shai_ (sai2). The 86th radical ("fire"). Internal "fire" (or "heat") in Chinese medicine. 【火】 (xwaX) (_noun-like_) fire, (_met._) light 【火】 (fo2) A watt (of power). 幾多火 (gei2 do1 fo2): "How many watts?" 六十火 (luk6 sap6 fo2): "Sixty watts."

U+9846 堁 (for "clod" sense) (F2.60 顆) 小頭物也一顆猶一個一粒也 (Page 54 顆) A little head (Kangxi: 小頭也) ~~or clod~~; ``a clod (Kangxi: 與堁同土塊 etc.);`` classifier of beads, shot, pearls, and round things (Kangxi: 今言物一顆猶一頭也, 凡圜物以顆計又珠琲曰顆); _ki to fo_ (gei2 do1 fo2 幾多顆), how many of them? Williams' "clod": This is a significantly separate sense. It should be separated from "A little head" by a semicolon.

fo3.text: U+8AB2 (F2.61 課) 說課又試也第也計也程也 (Page 54 課) A plan, an example, a task, an exercise; literary pursuits; to essay, to try``, to test, to assess`` (Kangxi: 試也 etc.); to counsel, to deliberate; to exhort; to examine; ~~a poll~~ tax (Kangxi: 稅也); _ko fo ngan_ (gou1 fo3 ngan4 ？課銀), allowance given students; _fo man_ (fo3 man4 課文), to criticise compositions; _ui fo_ (wui6 fo3 會課) to hold a concourse for writing; _ut fo_ (jyut6 fo3 月課) monthly trial for composition; _kung fo_ (gung1 fo3 功課) a stint of work; _yat fo_ (jat6 fo3 日課) a daily task, or lesson. Williams' "to deliberate": This is a dubious rendering of Kangxi 議也 (which is admittedly unhelpful). Williams' 功課: Now "homework". Williams' 日課: Corrected _yat_ (jat1) to _yat_ (jat6). To compute (Kangxi: 計也程也). 【課】 (khwaH) (_verb-like_) to assess (_noun-like_) tax

U+8CA8 (F2.61 貨) 貨財凡物可交易皆曰貨 (Page 54 貨) Goods, merchandise, commodities``, things of value or utility`` (Kangxi: 財也, 貨寶用物, etc.); to trade``, to buy, to sell (Kangxi: 貨賣也)``; to bribe (Kangxi: 賂也); _fo mat_ (fo3 mat6 貨物) goods; _fo ts'oi_ (fo3 coi4 貨財), property, capital; _~~ap~~ ``at`` fo_ (aat3 fo3 押貨) to go with cargo to a ship; _fo t'au_ (fo3 tau4 貨頭), prime goods; _fo lo_ (fo3 lou6 貨賂) to bribe; _fo shik_ (fo3 sik1 貨色) quality of goods. Williams' 押貨: Normalised _ap_ (aap3) to _at_ (aat3). Currency. 【貨】 (xwaH) (_noun-like_) goods, property (_noun-like_) currency

fok3.text: U+6509 (F1.103 攉) 手反覆也搖手曰揮反手曰攉 (Page 55 攉) To ~~recall one with~~ ``flip over`` the hand (Kangxi: 手反覆也); to motion ~~back~~ with the hand (Kangxi: 搖手曰揮反手曰攉); ~~to fan;~~ _hi fok_ (hei3 fok3 戲攉) to make a fool of; _fok shau_ (fok3 sau2 攉手), to shake the hand. Williams' 攉手: More specifically, to shake about the axis of the arm (in the same way that shaking the head is about the axis of the neck).

U+652B gwok3 (per 《廣韻》居縛切) (F1.103 攫) 爪持也撲取也 (Page 54 攫) To grasp with the talons or claws (Kangxi: 扟也, 撲取也, 以腳取之謂之攫以翼擊之謂之搏, 一曰持也); _fok ts'u_ (fok3 ceoi2 攫取), to seize by force, to get anyhow. Fan Wan entry: Corrected incomplete 瓜 to 爪, and fixed missing stroke in 撲. 【攫】 (kjwak) (_verb-like_) to snatch

U+77CD gwok3 (per 《廣韻》居縛切) (F1.103 矍) 矍鑠輕徤貌 (Page 54 矍) The fluttering of a bird when caught (Kangxi: 隹欲逸走也); looking right and left ``in fear`` (Kangxi: 左右驚顧也); to glance the eyes about quickly (Kangxi: 一曰視遽貌, 不專視之容, 目不正也); _fok ~~yeuk~~ ``sheuk``_ (fok3 soek3 矍鑠) active; said of an old man (Kangxi: 矍鑠輕健貌). Williams' 矍鑠: Corrected _yeuk_ (joek3) to _sheuk_ (soek3). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【矍】 (kjwak) (_verb-like_) to glance fearfully
U+7E1B (F1.103 縛) 綁縛 (Page 55 縛) Fan Wan entry: Fixed extra stroke in headword 縛.

U+85FF (F1.103 藿) 藿香草名 (Page 55 藿) Leaves ``or young sprouts`` of pulse (Kangxi: 尗之少也, 藿豆葉), used as food; ~~greens in general;~~ _fok heung_ (fok3 hoeng1 藿香), the _Betonica officinalis_ used in colic. 【藿】 (xwak) (_noun-like_) pulse-leaf, pulse-sprout

U+8EA9 gwok3 (per 《廣韻》居縛切) kwok3 (per 《廣韻》丘縛切) (F1.103 躩) 盤辟貌 (Page 54 躩) A short, quick, stately step, as if ~~going up stairs~~ ``turning on the spot``, deemed respectful to superiors (Kangxi: 足盤辟而爲敬也); _fok po_ (fok3 bou6 躩步) a quick, regular walk (Kangxi: 躩步疾行). Williams' 躩步: Corrected _po_ to _po_. To jump (Kangxi: 躩跳也). 【躩】 (khjwak, kjwak) (_verb-like_) to step turning on the spot, to step quickly
U+9481 gwok3 (per 《廣韻》居縛切) (F1.103 钁) 鋤頭 (Page 55 钁) A large hoe or mattock (Kangxi: 大鉏也 etc.), made of wood, edged with iron.

U+970D 靃 (F1.103 霍) 姓 (Page 55 霍) Speed, celerity, agility (揮霍, 揮霍猝遽也, 揮霍疾貌); a surname (Kangxi: 姓); a range of mountains (Kangxi: 山名 etc.) ~~in Hupeh (湖北)~~, the Atlas of China (Kangxi: 卽天柱山); _fok lun ching_ (fok3 lyun6 zing3 霍亂症) the cholera or bilious colic; _fok in_ (fok3 jin4 霍然), quick, like flying clouds. Williams' "in Hupeh": This is dubious. Kangxi has an extended note discussing whether there are multiple mountains called 霍. Of the candidates, 衡山 is in 湖南, while 天柱山 is in 安徽. To dissipate, to disperse (Kangxi: 霍焉猶渙焉也). 【霍】 (xwak) (_adverb-like_) swiftly

zaa1.text: U+4E4B za1 (shorter syllable) Modern Cantonese dictionaries indicate a reading of zi1 maa3 for 之嘛. I disagree with this reading for three reasons: I have only heard za1 maa3. I believe that 之嘛 (za1 maa3) is a contraction of 啫 (ze1) + 吖嘛 (a1 maa3), in the same way that 𠺢嘛 (ga1 maa3) is shortened from 嘅 (ge3) + 吖嘛 (a1 maa3). I believe that any insistence on the reading of zi1 maa3 is a case of incorrectly letting the spelling dictate the pronunciation. It would be more helpful if za1 maa3 were written 𠾵嘛 instead, but unfortunately the spelling 之嘛 is already well established. 【之嘛】 (za1 maa3) A final particle of limitation, especially used defensively. 講笑之嘛 (gong2 siu3 za1 maa3): "Just kidding!" 等兩日之嘛 (dang2 loeng5 jat6 za1 maa3): "It's just a two-day wait."

zi1.text: U+4E4B (F1.35 之) 於也往也又語助詞 (Page 38|n81 之) A sign of the genitive; when placed between ~~two nouns or verbs~~ ``a noun and a verb``, answers to who, which, that; a``n object`` pronoun, ~~he~~ ``him``, ~~she~~ ``her``, it, them; often used in the abstract, after a noun, or as a relative particle, in apposition; ~~to progress;~~ to go to (Kangxi: 往也); _~~t'eng~~ ``t'ing`` chi chi yan_ (ting3 zi1 zi1 jan4 聽之之人), the person who hears him; _t'in chi ming ming_ (tin1 zi1 ming4 ming6 天之明命) heaven's plain decree; _u chi noi ho_ (jyu4 zi1 noi6 ho4 如之奈何) if so, how then? _mi chi yau ya_ (mei6 zi1 jau5 jaa5 未之有也), there is no such thing; _tseung ho chi_ (zoeng1 ho4 zi1 將何之), where are you going? _ku chi_ (gu2 zi1 古之), the ancients; _t'in ming chi_ (tin1 ming6 zi1 天命之), heaven orders it; _mo chi_ (mou4 zi1 無之), impossible; _hang i pat chi che yau chi_ (hang4 ji4 bat1 zi3 ze2 jau5 zi1 行而不至者有之), to go and not arrive is common; _chu chi che chi wai tai_ (zyu2 zi1 ze2 zi1 wai6 dai3 主之者之謂帝) that which rules is called supreme. Many of the senses given in Kangxi are too unsatisfactory to be mapped to Williams' definitions above. For example, where Kangxi has 語助辭 "speech-assist word" (i.e. particle), the examples given are actually for the objective pronoun sense. Williams' 聽之之人: Corrected _t'eng_ (teng3) to _t'ing_ (ting3). This (Kangxi: 此也). Till, until (Kangxi: 至也). 【之】 (tsyi) (_verb-like_) to go (_preposition-like_) till (_determiner-like_) this, such (_pronoun-like_) him, it, (_met._) a thing (_postposition-like_) of (_speech-assist_) that

zaa1.text: U+54B1 (Page 547 咱) A colloquial word in the north; I, myself (Kangxi: 俗稱自己爲咱); _tsa ka_ (zaa1 gaa1 咱家), I, me; _tsa mun_ (zaa1 mun4 咱們), we. Williams' 咱們: Specifically, the inclusive "we".
U+20FB5 (Page 7 𠾵) A colloquial particle implying a short time; _tang ha cha!_ (dang2 haa5 zaa1 等吓𠾵) wait a moment; _yam ch'a cha_ (jam2 caa4 zaa1 飲茶𠾵), only just to take a cup of tea.

U+63F8 (Page 7 㪥; here corrected to 揸) _cha ye_ (zaa1 je5 揸嘢), to handle. (Page 7 揸) A unauthorized character. To grasp, to grab, to clutch; to hold or take firmly; to work, as a bellows; to use; a handful; _cha fung seung_ (zaa1 fung1 soeng1 揸風箱), to work a bellows; _cha yat tai cha_ (zaa1 jat1 daai6 zaa1 揸一大揸), grab a big handful; _cha kan_ (zaa1 gan2 揸緊), hold it fast; _cha t'an_ (zaa1 taan1 揸攤), to keep a gambling-table; _cha tak wan_ (zaa1 dak1 wan2 揸得穩), I hold it safely; _cha shui chu yuk_ (zaa1 seoi2 zyu1 juk6 揸水豬肉) water-sogged pork; _cha fo t'ung_ (zaa1 fo2 tung4 揸火筒), to be a scullion; _yau cha shau_ (jau5 zaa1 sau2 有揸手) I have security; _mo cha na_ (mou5 zaa1 naa4 冇揸拿), nothing to hold on by; a Budhist priest is so nicknamed; _cha k'un t'au_ (zaa1 kyun4 tau4 揸拳頭), to double up the fist; _cha chu kai tsai_ (zaa1 zyu6 gai3 zai2 揸住髻仔), to grab him by the tail; _cha lan_ (zaa1 laan6 揸爛) to crush in the hand. 【揸】 (zaa1) To hold, to grasp, to control. 揸兜 (zaa1 dau1): "Hold a shallow-bowl" (i.e. be a beggar). 揸拿 (zaa1 naa4): "A grasp of the situation." 揸筷子 (zaa1 faai3 zi2): "Control chopsticks." To drive a vehicle. 揸棍波 (zaa1 gwan3 bo1): "Drive stick." 揸車 (zaa1 ce1): "Drive a car."
U+64BE (F2.74 撾) 擊鼓曰撾 (Page 7 撾) To beat a drum or a bell with a stick (Kangxi: 擊也, 擊鼓也).
U+3AA5 (F2.74 㪥) 以手按物 (Page 7 㪥) To put the hand upon, to feel, to press down (Kangxi: 以指按也), to take (Kangxi: 取也);
U+6A1D (Page 7 樝) A sour red berry as large as a cherry (Kangxi: 似棃而酢), called _shan cha_ (saan1 zaa1 山樝), used for its acid.

U+6E23 (F2.74 渣) 乍平聲渣滓 (Page 7 渣) Name of a river (Kangxi: 水名); dregs, grounds, residuum (Kangxi: 俗以此爲渣滓字); sediment, settlings, refuse after expressing juice; _tsz' kang cha_ (zi2 gang2 zaa1 紫梗渣), shell lac; _mo cha_ (mou5 zaa1 冇渣), there's no refuse left. ~~Often used for the two next (樝, 皻).~~ 【渣】 (zaa1) The residual after moisture is removed; the unswallowable remains after chewing untender meat or stringy vegetables. 渣都冇 (zaa1 dou1 mou5): "Even the scraps are gone." 𦧲渣 (loe1 zaa1): "Spit out the scraps."
U+76BB (Page 7 皻) Pimples on the nose (Kangxi: 鼻上皰), wine blotches (Kangxi: 紅暈似瘡浮起著面鼻者曰酒皻); pimples on the skin; _tsau cha_ (zau2 zaa1 酒皻), wine blossoms.
U+8E45 (F2.74 蹅) 足踏也 (Page 7 蹅) To tread upon; to put the foot on; _cha tap_ (zaa1 daap6 蹅踏) to stamp. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰目杳 to 踏.

zaa2.text: U+9B93 (F2.75 鮓) 魚鮓肉鮓 (Page 7 鮓) A rank condiment of fish or flesh and salted hashed fine, with red rice and oil (Kangxi: 藏魚也 etc.); _la cha_ (laa5 zaa2 揦鮓), rank, dirty, foul. Williams' 揦鮓: Corrected _la_ (laa6) to _la_ (laa5). 【揦鮓】 (laa5 zaa2) Dirty. 𨋢掣揦鮓 (lip1 zai3 laa5 zaa2): "The lift button is dirty." 【鮓】 (zaa2) Low quality or crappy. 咁鮓 (gam3 zaa2): "How is it so crappy?" 鮓嘢 (zaa2 je5): "A crappy thing."

zaa6.text: U+9B93 (Page 7 鮓 _Cha_ (zaa2); here corrected to _Cha_ (zaa6)) _cha u_ (zaa6 jyu4 鮓魚), a sort of Medusa or sea-blubber (Kangxi: 海魚名 etc.);

zaa3.text: U+4E4D 咋 詐 zaa6 (per 《廣韻》鋤駕切) (F2.76 乍) 初也忽也猝也甫然也暫也 (Page 8 乍) At first (Kangxi: 初也), suddenly, hastily (Kangxi: 猝也); for a moment (Kangxi: 暫也); inadvertently, unexpectedly (Kangxi: 忽也); _cha yau cha mo_ (zaa3 jau5 zaa3 mou4 乍有乍無), all at once there is some and then we have none; _cha in_ (zaa3 jin4 乍然), abruptly, at once; _cha kin_ (zaa3 gin3 乍見) happened to see. 【乍】 (dzraeH) (_adverb-like_) suddenly, unexpectedly

U+548B za3 (shorter syllable for vernacular sense) (Page 8 咋) A colloquial particle implying doubt, perhaps; also a final sound, implying it is so; I understand it: _kom yeung cha_ (gam2 joeng6-2 zaa3 噉樣咋) so will do, eh! 【咋】 (za3, zaa3) A final particle of limitation. 三個人咋 (saam1 go3 jan4-2 za3): "Only three people."

zaak3.text: U+548B zaak6 (per 《正韻牋》士革切 for "bite" sense) (Page 8 咋 _Cha_ (zaa3); here corrected to _Chak_ (zaak3)) A loud sound, a rude noise (Kangxi: 大聲). To eat (Kangxi: 啖也), to bite (Kangxi: 嚙也).

zaa3.text: U+643E (F2.76 榨) 壓油之器又糖榨 (F2.76 搾) 以手壓物 (Page 8 搾) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 千 to 手.

U+69A8 搾 醡 (F2.76 榨) 壓油之器又糖榨 (F2.76 搾) 以手壓物 (Page 8 醡) used for the next (榨) (Kangxi 醡: 打油具). (Page 8 榨) An oil-press (Kangxi: 打油具也); a sugar press; to press or extract juices; _lok cha ch'ong_ (lok6 zaa3 cong4 落榨床), to violently extort money; _yau cha_ (jau4 zaa3 油榨) an oil press; _cha liu_ (zaa3 liu4 榨寮), a sugar-press shed. (Page 8 搾) An unauthorized character. To press down, to squeeze between the hand; _lo hong to cha ch'ut yau_ (lou5 hong1 dou1 zaa3 ceot1 jau4 老糠都搾出油), he even squeezes oil from rice chaff. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 千 to 手.
U+70B8 To blow up, to explode, to make explode. To deep-fry.

U+86B1 zaak3 (per 《廣韻》側伯切) (Page 8 蚱) A large ~~locust~~ ``grasshopper``, called _ma cha_ (maa5 zaa3 螞蚱) or _cha mang_ (zaa3 maang5 蚱蜢); _cha ~~shin~~ ``shim``_ (zaa3 sim4 蚱蟬), a small species of cicada. The reading _cha_ (zaa3) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 側駕切, explained 蟬屬. Williams' 蚱蜢: Changed _mang_ (mang5) to common reading _mang_ (maang5). Williams' 蚱蟬: Changed _shin_ (sin4) to common reading _shim_ (sim4).

U+8A50 (F2.76 詐) 好僞也 (Page 8 詐) To deceive, to cheat, to impose upon (Kangxi: 欺也); erroneous, false (Kangxi: 僞也); to feign, to make believe; artful, cunning (Kangxi: 詭譎也); fraudulent; _cha kwai cha ma_ (zaa3 gwai2 zaa3 maa5 詐鬼詐馬), pretending to act like a fool; _ngo cha_ (ngo4 zaa3 訛詐) to extort money falsely; _kan cha_ (gaan1 zaa3 奸詐) to delude; _cha ka i_ (zaa3 gaa2-1 ji3-1 詐假意), false; _cha ngai_ (zaa3 ngai6 詐偽) to humbug, suppositious; _cha fan_ (zaa3 fan3 詐瞓) to sham sleep; _cha ching ki_ (zaa3 zing3 gei3 詐正記) to feign to be good. 【詐】 (tsraeH) (_verb-like_) to deceive, (_met._) cunning 【詐諦】 (zaa3 dai3) To pretend.
U+91A1 (F2.76 醡) 壓酒具 (Page 8 醡) A wine press (Kangxi: 壓酒具也); to press or separate spirits;

zaa4.text: U+55B3 za4 (shorter syllable) 【喳】 (za4, zaa4) An interrogative-ish final particle of limitation, suspicion, disappointment, etc. 三個人喳 (saam1 go3 jan4 za4): "Only three people?"

zaa6.text: U+62C3 【拃】 (zaa6) A handful or a bunch. 拃波子 (zaa6 bo1 zi2): "A handful of marbles." 嗰拃人 (go2 zaa6 jan4): "That bunch of people." Used in 掗拃

zaai1.text: U+658B (F2.25 齊) 竹佳切莊也恭也潔也洗心曰齊 (F2.25 齋) 齋成又燕居之室曰齋 (F2.25 斋) 同上 (Page 10|n53 齋~~？~~``斋``)

U+9F4B 斋 齊 (F2.25 齊) 竹佳切莊也恭也潔也洗心曰齊 (F2.25 齋) 齋成又燕居之室曰齋 (F2.25 斋) 同上 (Page 10|n53 齋~~？~~``斋``) To respect, to reverence (Kangxi 齋: 恭也, 齋敬也); to abstain from (Kangxi 齋: 潔也, 洗心曰齋); pure, reverential, serious (Kangxi 齋: 莊也); a retiring room, a closet (Kangxi 齋: 燕居之室曰齋); _ta chai_ (daa2 zaai1 打齋), to get a soul out of tartarus; _ta chai hok_ (daa2 zaai1 hok6-2 打齋鶴) storks for the soul to ride to heaven; _met._ a pander; _tai sz' chai_ (daai6 si6 zaai1 大士齋), offerings before the purveyor of hungry spirits; _han chai_ (haan4 zaai1 閒齋), a closet; _shu chai_ (syu1 zaai1 書齋), a library; _ts'ing chai_ (cing1 zaai1 清齋), an entire fast; _lo chai_ (lo2 zaai1 攞齋), to beg vegetables; _chai kai_ (zaai1 gaai3 齋戒) to fast from animal food; _chai tsiu_ (zaai1 ziu3 齋醮) to fast at All-souls; _shik chai_ (sik6 zaai1 食齋), to fast on vegetables; _lo hon chai_ (lo4 hon3 zaai1 羅漢齋), all sorts of vegetables; _chai ku_ (zaai1 geoi1 齋居), to live at ease; _chai chong_ (zaai1 zong1 齋莊), respectful, reverential. A colloquial word. A final particle denoting that it was said, that it is so; _mat yan wa chai_ (mat1 jan4 waa6 zaai1 乜人話齋), somebody has said; _ho ts'z' k'u wa chai_ (hou2 ci5 keoi5 waa6 zaai1 好似佢話齋), just as he said. (Page 10|n53 齊) Interchanged with the last (齋) (Kangxi: 與齋同). To respect; decorous; the heart pure, to chasten the desires. Williams' 齋戒: Properly, to abstain from all pleasures (meat, alcohol, sex). Williams' 齋醮: Corrected _tsiu_ (ziu6) to _tsiu_ (ziu3). Williams' 羅漢齋: The name of a dish, commonly called Buddha's Delight. 【齋】 (tsreaj) (_verb-like_) to purify by abstinence 【話齋】 (waa6 zaai1) Emphatic form of "as he said". 好似佢話齋 (hou2 ci5 keoi5 waa6 zaai1): "As he said." 【齋】 (zaai1) Vegetarian food. 初一十五食齋 (co1 jat1 sap6 ng5 sik6 zaai1): "Eating vegetarian on the first and fifteenth of the lunar month." 齋口唔齋心 (zaai1 hau2 m4 zaai1 sam1): "Vegetarian in mouth but not vegetarian at heart" (said of vegetarians that eat imitation meat). Plain, pure, etc. 齋啡 (zaai1 fe1): "Straight coffee." 齋腸 (zaai1 coeng4-2): "Plain rice-noodle rolls." 齋噏 (zaai1 ngap1): "Purely blabbering."

zaai3.text: U+50B5 責 (F2.28 債) 齋去聲債負揭借銀曰債負 (Page 10|n53 債) To bear a burden (Kangxi: 負也); to owe; a debt (Kangxi: 今俗負財曰債); _him chai_ (him3 zaai3 欠債) to owe a debt; _chai chu_ (zaai3 zyu2), a creditor; _chai tsai_ (zaai3 zai2 債主), a debtor; _wan chai_ (waan4 zaai3 還債) to repay; _lo chai_ (lo2 zaai3 攞債) to collect debts; _fong chai_ (fong3 zaai3 放債) to let capital; _~~tsit~~ ``ts'it`` i pin chai tan_ (cit3 ji5 bin3 zaai3 dan2 切耳變債躉), cut off his ears and make him a stool of debt. Williams' 切耳變債躉: Corrected _tsit_ (zit3) to _ts'it_ (cit3). 【債】 (tsreaH) (_noun-like_) debt
U+7C00 (Page 12|n55 簀) Read _chai_ (zaai3); a strainer for spirits (Kangxi: 壓酒具).

zaak3.text: U+7C00 zak1 (per Fan Wan) (F2.32 簀 _Chak_ (zak1)) ？？也 (Page 12|n55 簀 _Chak_ (zak1); here normalised to _Chak_ (zaak3)) Boards of a bed (Kangxi: 牀棧也); a bed mat (Kangxi: 簀謂之笫); to collect (Kangxi: 積聚也); luxuriant (Kangxi: 簀積也言茂盛似如積聚). Fan Wan entry: The first two characters of the explanation are incomprehensible. The first, ⿱𥫗章, does not exist. The second is illegible, but appears like a deformed version of 䓂. 【簀】 (tsreak) (_noun-like_) bed-mat, (_met._) dense-padding

zak1.text: U+7C00 (F2.32 簀) ？？也 (Page 12|n55 簀) Fan Wan entry: The first two characters of the explanation are incomprehensible. The first, ⿱𥫗章, does not exist. The second is illegible, but appears like a deformed version of 䓂.

zaak3.text: U+8CAC (F2.32 責 _Chak_ (zak1)) 責罰責成 (Page 12|n55 責) To reprove, to reprimand, to blame (Kangxi: 責讓也); to punish (Kangxi: 誅責也), to fine; to sustain, to be responsible for, to have charge of (Kangxi: 任也); to ask (Kangxi: 問也), to demand (Kangxi: 求也); _chak fat_ (zaak3 fat6 責罰) to fine; _chak ~~ch'eung~~ ``sheung``_ (zaak3 soeng4 責償), to compel repayment; _chak fat_ (zaak3 fat6 責罰) to punish; _chak man_ (zaak3 man6 責問) to subject to the question; _chak shing_ (zaak3 sing4 責成), to charge with; _chak yam sham chung_ (zaak3 jam6 sam6 zung6 責任甚重) onerous duties devolve on him; Williams' 責償: Changed improper reading _ch'eung_ (coeng4) to _sheung_ (soeng4). Williams' 責罰 (second occurrence): Changed _fat_ (faat6) to modern _fat_ (fat6). 【責】 (tsreak) (_verb-like_) to demand (_verb-like_) to blame, to rebuke (_noun-like_) responsibility

zaai6.text: U+5BE8 砦 (F2.29 寨) 木檽爲之蜜口營壘也 (F2.29 砦) 同上 (Page 10|n53 寨砦) A stockade, a palisade (Kangxi: 山居以木柵, 籬落也); an encampment; a stockaded village; a sheep-pen (Kangxi: 羊栖宿處也); _ts'ak chai_ (caak6 zaai6 賊寨) a robber's hold; _chai chu_ (zaai6 zyu2 寨主), wife of a bandit chief who guards the hold; _ta chai_ (daa2 zaai6 打寨) to frequent brothels; _luk kok chai_ (luk6 gok3 zaai6 六角寨) a stockade defended by chevaux-de-frise; _ying chai_ (jing4 zaai6 營寨) barracks, a camp, a military station. Fan Wan entry: 寨 is printed with bottom component 氺 (rather than 朩 or 木), whilst 蜜 (used for 密) is printed as ⿳冖一虫.

zi6.text: U+5ECC

zaai6.text: U+7725 眦 (Page 617 眥 _Ts'z'_ (ci3); here corrected to _Chai_ (zaai6)) a fierce look; to look at angrily (Kangxi: 恨視也);

zi6.text: U+7725 眦 ci1 (common) zai6 (alternative per 《廣韻》在詣切) (F2.35 眥 _Ts'z'_ (ci3)) 目際也 (F2.35 眦 _Ts'z'_ (ci3)) 同上 (Page 617 眥 _Ts'z'_ (ci3); here corrected to _Tsz'_ (zi6)) The canthus or corner of the eye (Kangxi: 目厓也); a lappel, the place where it folds over. 【眥】 (dzjeH, dzejH) (_noun-like_) canthus

zaai6.text: U+7826 (F2.29 寨) 木檽爲之蜜口營壘也 (F2.29 砦) 同上 (Page 10|n53 寨砦) Fan Wan entry: 蜜 (used for 密) is printed as ⿳冖一虫.

U+8C78 廌 zi6 (alternative per 《廣韻》池爾切 for 廌) (F2.29 豸) 蟲豸有足曰蟲無足曰豸又獬豸 (Page 10|n53 豸) A fabulous ~~feline~~ beast, called _hai chai_ (haai5 zaai6 獬豸) embroidered on censor's robes, indicative of discrimination (Kangxi: 法冠或謂之獬豸冠獬豸神羊能別曲直故以爲冠). Aptly rendered "unicorn", per 《說文》一角 under the seal for 廌. The alternative reading _chi_ (zi6) per 《廣韻》池爾切 for 廌 is suspect, as it does not preserve the internal rhyme in 解豸, "unicorn of understanding". 【豸】 (dreaX) (_noun-like_) unicorn

zi6.text: U+8C78 (F2.29 豸 _Chai_ (zaai6)) 蟲豸有足曰蟲無足曰豸又獬豸 (Page 10|n53 豸) Read _chi_ (zi6); the 153d radical ("badger") of characters of ~~feline~~ ``long-backed`` beasts ``ready to pounce`` (Kangxi: 獸長脊行豸豸然欲有所司殺形); a worm, a hairless grub (Kangxi: 有足謂之蟲無足謂之豸). Williams' "a worm, a hairless grub": Or, legless animals more generally.

zaak3.text: U+5616 zaak6 (per 《廣韻》士革切 for "cry-out" sense) zik1 (per Fan Wan, common) (F1.75 嘖 _Tsik_ (zik1)) 大呼聲 (Page 569 嘖~~賾~~ _Tsik_ (zik1); here normalised to _Chak_ (zaak3)) To bawl, to cry after (Kangxi 嘖: 大呼也, 㗲嘖叫也); to dispute about, noisy quarreling (Kangxi 嘖: 大呼聲, 爭言貌); garrulous, impertinent or meddlesome; ~~to assent to;~~ _~~tsik tsik~~ ``chak chak`` tsan_ (zaak3 zaak3 zaan3 嘖嘖讚) to chuckle admiringly. 【嘖】 (dzreak, tsreak, tsraek) (_verb-like_) to cry out
U+67DE (Page 592 柞) Read _chak_ (zaak3) to root up trees; to fell trees (Kangxi: 除草曰芟除木曰柞); 【柞】 (tsraek) (_verb-like_) to fell away (trees)

zok6.text: U+67DE zok3 (alternative per 《廣韻》則落切) (F1.103 柞) 木名 (Page 592 柞) A ~~spinous~~ tree of firm wood, whose new leaves appear as the old ones fall (Kangxi: 柞堅韌之木新葉將生故葉乃落); ~~a locust?~~

zaak3.text: U+77FA 砸 磧 責 zaak6 (alternative) (Page 12|n55 責; here normalised to 矺) to lay a weight on; to squeeze smooth, to press; _chak chu k'u_ (zaak3 zyu6 keoi5 矺住佢), keep it down; _chak sz'_ (zaak3 sei2 矺死) crushed to death; _chak tsoi_ (zaak3 zoi6 矺儎) to ballast a ship; _chak lan_ (zaak3 laan6 矺爛) crushed in pieces. This character (in the vernacular "weight/crush" senses) is commonly written 砸. In my opinion, 矺 is a better character to use than 砸 because: 矺 is attested in Middle Chinese with both reasonable sound and reasonable meaning (zaak3 per 《廣韻》陟革切, explained 磓也, to stone downward); and 砸 is otherwise read zaap3. This character (in the vernacular "weight/crush" senses) is sometimes written 笮. In my opinion, 笮 is a poor character to use because it is *not* specific to pressure being applied *vertically*. Indeed for 笮, Kangxi has 又《增韻》壓也通作措《史記梁世家》李太后與爭門措指 《註》晉灼曰措字借以爲笮師古曰爲門扇所笮, and in this example I think it more likely for the dowager's finger to have been crushed *horizontally* by the door, rather than vertically. 【矺】 (zaak3, zaak6) To weigh down with a heavy object. 矺住張紙 (zaak6 zyu6 zoeng1 zi2): "Weigh down the piece of paper." To crush (vertically) with a heavy object. 矺扁咗 (zaak3 bin2 zo2): "Squashed flat." Olden attestation for 矺 _chak_ (zaak3) meaning "weight/crush": 《廣韻》陟革切 矺 磓也 《康熙字典》矺 《廣韻》《集韻》𠀤陟格切音舴同磔省詳磔字註 又《廣韻》磓也... Here, 磓 is either to stone downwards or to gather stones (in a heap): 《廣韻》都回切 磓 落也亦作塠 《康熙字典》磓 《廣韻》《集韻》《韻會》《正韻》𠀤都回切音堆聚石也又以石投下也亦作塠...

zaap3.text: U+7838 To crush. To smash, to break. To toss.

zik1.text: U+78E7 cik1 (per 《廣韻》七迹切) (F1.74 磧) 水渚有石者 (Page 569 磧) Rocks under water, half-tide rocks, a stony bottom (Kangxi: 水陼有石者); covered with sand and stones.

zaak3.text: U+7A84 柞 笮 zaak3-2 (rising second in vernacular 窄窄地) (F2.90 窄) 直百切狹也迫也 (Page 12|n55 窄) Narrow, strait (Kangxi: 狹也迫也隘也); compressed; groveling, mean, narrow-minded; _pik chak_ (bik1 zaak3 迫窄) contracted, illiberal; _ts'in chak_ (cin2 zaak3 淺窄) confined, cramped; _sam ch'eung chak_ (sam1 coeng4 zaak3 心腸窄) little-minded; _chak kwo t'au_ (zaak3 gwo3 tau4 窄過頭), too narrow; _ko tsz' chak_ (go3 zi6 zaak3 個字窄) its meanings are few; _chak lo_ (zaak3 lou6 窄路) a lane. (Page 12|n55 笮) Like the last (窄) (Kangxi 笮: 狹也廹也); (Page 592 柞; here normalised to 窄) Read _chak_ (zaak3) narrow, confined (Kangxi 柞: 窄陿也). Fan Wan entry: The supplied dissection 直百切 is incorrect, as it results in the 陽入 reading _chak_ (zaak6), rather than the intended 陰入 reading _chak_ (zaak3).

U+7B2E 措 (for "crush" sense) (Page 12|n55 笮) a quiver (Kangxi: 矢箙); a bamboo ceiling of a roof (Kangxi: 屋上板, 在瓦之下棼之上); to hasten out (Kangxi: 出去疾也); to press``, to crush`` (Kangxi: 壓也); For Cantonese "weight/crush", see 矺 zaak3.

zok6.text: U+7B2E (Page 12|n55 笮 _Chak_ (zaak3); here corrected to _Tsok_ (zok6)) to tattoo or brand, as a punishment (Kangxi: 𠛬名, 黥𠛬也).

zaak3.text: U+8234 (F2.90 舴) 舴船小船 (Page 13|n56 舴) A boat; _chak mang_ (zaak3 maang5 舴艋) a small boat, a punt, a pinnace, a junk's gig (Kangxi: 舴艋小舟). Williams' 舴艋: Changed _mang_ (mang) to common reading _mang_ (maang), and corrected tone __ (去聲) to __ (上聲).

U+8CFE May appear with left component as 𦣝 (+10) or 𦣞 (+11 assumed here), depending on the font. 嘖 zaak6 (per 《廣韻》士革切) (F2.32 賾 _Chak_ (zak1)) 深也雜也 (Page 12|n55 賾 _Chak_ (zak1); here corrected to _Chak_ (zaak3)) Obscure, hard of understanding (Kangxi: 謂幽深難見); the secret springs, the hidden cause; occult, profound. (Page 569 嘖賾 _Tsik_ (zik1); here corrected to _Chak_ (zaak3)) distant, retired, hidden (Kangxi 嘖: 與賾通); Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𧷤 to 賾. 【賾】 (dzreak) (_adjective-like_) abstruse

zaak6.text: U+5B85 zaak6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.89 宅) 家宅居也堂也 (Page 13|n56 宅) A dwelling, a mansion, a residence (Kangxi: 居也, 謂居處也); a grave; to dwell, to fix, to settle (Kangxi: 定也); ~~to conform to;~~ an office, a station (Kangxi: 所居之位亦曰宅); _yeung chak_ (joeng4 zaak6 陽宅) houses, residences; _tai chak i chak_ (daai6 zaak6-2 ji6 zaak6-2 大宅二宅) eldest son and second son's dwellings; _yam chak_ (jam1 zaak6 陰宅) a grave; ancestral halls or shrines; _chak ~~chiu~~ ``shiu``_ (zaak6 siu6 宅兆) to divine for a grave. Williams' 宅兆: Changed _chiu_ (ziu6) to common reading _shiu_ (siu6). 【宅】 (draek) (_noun-like_) dwelling, settlement (_verb-like_) to dwell, to settle

U+6458 tik1 (per 《廣韻》他歷切 for "pick", "point", and "start" senses) zaak3 (per 《廣韻》陟革切 for "take" sense) (F2.89 摘) 以手折花曰擇 (Page 13|n56 摘) To pluck, to cull; to pull, to pick (Kangxi: 拓果樹實也); to lay hold of (Kangxi: 手取也); to move on, to start (Kangxi: 發也動也); to point out (Kangxi: 一曰指近之也); _chak yan_ (zaak6 jan3 摘印) to take away an officer's seal; _chak tu ~~tok~~ ``lok`` lai_ (zaak6 doe2 lok6 lai4 摘朵落嚟), pull a flower down; _yat chak_ (jat1 zaak6 一摘) one picking; _chak kwo_ (zaak6 gwo2 摘果), to pick fruit; _chak teng_ (zaak6 deng2 摘頂), to take away an officer's button. (Page 13|n56 翟) ~~This and the~~ ``The`` last (摘) ~~are~~ ``is`` also read _t'ik_ (tik1) ~~with the same meanings~~. Williams' 摘朵落嚟: Corrected _tok_ (dok6) to _lok_ (lok6). Williams' 摘頂: Corrected _teng_ (deng1) to _teng_ (deng2). 【摘】 (treak) (_verb-like_) to pluck

U+64C7 (F2.89 擇) 揀擇選擇 (Page 13|n56 擇) To select, to pick out, to choose, to prefer (Kangxi: 揀選也); _chak yat_ (zaak6 jat6-2 擇日) to choose a lucky day; _ni kom kan chak_ (nei5 gam3 gaan2 zaak6 你咁揀擇) you are so hard to suit; _yam ni kan chak_ (jam6 nei5 gaan2 zaak6 任你揀擇) just as you like; _chak kau_ (zaak6 gaau1 擇交), to take associates. 【擇】 (draek) (_verb-like_) to select
U+64E2 (F2.89 擢) 拔抽也取用也 (Page 13|n56 擢)

zok6.text: U+64E2 zaak6 (per Fan Wan) (F2.89 擢 _Chak_ (zaak6)) 拔抽也取用也 (Page 13|n56 擢 _Chak_ (zaak6); here normalised to _Chok_ (zok6)) To pull up (Kangxi: 引也); to take out, to select, to raise, to promote (Kangxi: 拔也, 抽也出也); to employ in office; ~~to reject, to extinguish, as good resolutions;~~ _~~chak~~ ``chok`` fat nan sho_ (zok6 faat3 naan4 sou2 擢髮難數), the hairs can not be counted by pulling them out--- his crimes are ~~unmberless~~ ``numberless``.

zaak6.text: U+64F2 (F2.89 擲) 棄擲 (Page 13|n56 擲) To throw down, to throw at (Kangxi: 投也); to throw away, to reject (Kangxi: 拋也掉也); to waste, as one's time; _chak ui_ (zaak6 wui4 擲回), to refuse, to give back; _chak chung_ (zaak6 zung3 擲中) to hit; _chak t'au_ (zaak6 tau4 擲骰) or _chak shik_ (zaak6 sik1 擲色) to throw dice; _chak kau tsai_ (zaak6 gau2 zai2 擲九仔), to throw nines, _i.e._ to gamble; _hi chak_ (hei3 zaak6 棄擲) to discard.

U+6FA4 (F2.89 澤) 恩澤惠澤潤澤雨澤 (Page 13|n56 澤) A marsh, a fen, a pool (Kangxi: 澤水之鐘也, 水草交厝名之爲澤); to fertilize, to soften, to enrich (Kangxi: 澤者言其潤澤萬物以阜民用也); to anoint, to make to shine (Kangxi: 光潤也); to benefit, to favor; humid, slippery, smooth; to show kindness to (Kangxi: 德澤); to wash (Kangxi: 洗濯曰澤), to moisten; to make to grow; fertile; redolent of, as fragrance (Kangxi: 香澤); favor, kindness; _shau chak_ (sau2 zaak6 手澤) to brighten by handling; _yun chak_ (jeon6 zaak6 潤澤) fresh, moist; _wat chak_ (waat6 zaak6 滑澤) glossy; _u chak_ (jyu5 zaak6 雨澤) wet with rain; _yan chak_ (jan1 zaak6 恩澤) to confer favors. Williams' 手澤: Metaphorically, brilliant works by the hand of one now deceased. Undergarments (Kangxi: 褻衣曰澤). 【澤】 (draek) (_noun-like_) pool, marsh (_noun-like_) lustre (_verb-like_) to moisten, to enrich (_verb-like_) to wash (_noun-like_) undergarment
U+7FDF (F2.89 翟) 姓也又音狄 (Page 13|n56 翟) A surname (Kangxi: 亦姓). ~~This~~ (...) ~~also read _t'ik_ (tik1) with the same meanings.~~ Fan Wan entry: Corrected 伙 to 狄, and removed extraneous trailing 翟.
U+274F8 (Page 13|n56 𧓸) A large green caterpillar, called _chak chuk_ (zaak6 zuk6 𧓸蠋) which feeds on bean leaves (Kangxi: 𧓸蠋蟲名).

U+8B2B 讁 zaak3 (alternative(?) per 《廣韻》陟革切) (F2.89 讁) 責也貶也 (F2.89 適) 同上 (Page 13|n56 謫讁) To blame, to reprehend (Kangxi 謫: 責也, 咎也); to ~~disgracc~~ ``disgrace`` officers, to punish (Kangxi 謫: 罰也) by disgracing; to scold, to be angry at (Kangxi 謫: 怒也), to find fault with; flaw, error (Kangxi 謫: 罪也過也); a change of appearance (Kangxi 謫: 變氣也); _chak kan_ (zaak6 gaan1 謫奸), to disgrace an officer. Whilst 《廣韻》 has the headline reading 陟革切 _chak_ (zaak3), it also has the in-entry alternative 又丈厄切 _chak_ (zaak6). It is unclear whether this is intended as a cross-reference or as a genuine alternative for the "blame" sense. 【謫】 (treak) (_verb-like_) to blame (_verb-like_) to punish, to disgrace
U+8B81 (F2.89 讁) 責也貶也 (F2.89 適) 同上 (Page 13|n56 謫讁)

U+8E91 zik6 (per 《廣韻》直炙切) (F2.89 躑) 躑躅蟲名人之奔走追逐也 (F2.89 蹢) 同上 (Page 13|n56 躑) Going back and forth (Kangxi: 躑躅猶踟躕也); embarrassed, undecided (Kangxi: 躑躅行不進也); _chak chuk_ (zaak6 zuk6 躑躅) irresolute. Williams' "embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed".

zaam1.text: U+7C2A 篸 (F2.63 簪) 首飾也又聚也 (Page 553 簪) A hairpin, clasp, or bodkin, used to confine the hair or fasten the coiffure (Kangxi: 首筓也); to insert in the hair, to stick on the head; quick, brisk, ~~to collect~~ speedily (Kangxi: 疾也); _tsam p'ang_ (zaam1 paang5 簪棒), a hairpin; _tsam fa_ (zaam1 faa1 簪花), to stick flowers in the hair; _niu tsam_ (niu5 zaam1 裊簪), a gay headstall; _yuk tsam fa_ (juk6 zaam1 faa1 玉簪花), the tuberose; _kai tsam mai_ (gai1 zaam1 mai5 雞簪米), the fowl pecks the rice---a colloquial phrase. (Page 554 篸 _Ts'am_ (caam2); here corrected to _Tsam_ (zaam1) and normalised to 簪) a hair-pin (Kangxi 篸: 同簪); 【簪】 (tsrim, tsom) (_noun-like_) hairpin (_adjective-like_) swift

zaan2.text: U+5BC1 zaam2 (per 《廣韻》子感切) zip6 (per 《廣韻》疾葉切) (F2.69 寁) 速也 (Page 555 寁) Promptly, quickly (Kangxi: 居之速也); with haste; to hasten, to accelerate. 【寁】 (tsomX, dzjep) (_adverb-like_) hastily

zaam2.text: U+65AC (F2.64 斬) 截也斷也 (Page 15|n58 斬) To sunder, to cut in two (Kangxi: 截也, 裁也); to cut off (Kangxi: 斬...卽斷也); to execute, to decapitate; capital; to root up; to break off, as ~~intercousre~~ ``intercourse``; ~~faded, forgotten, vanished;~~ _cham ~~shui~~ ``ts'ui``_ (zaam2 ceoi1 斬衰), mourning for parents; _cham t'un_ (zaam2 tyun5 斬斷), to cut in two, to break off, as a business; _cham t'au_ (zaam2 tau4-2 斬頭), to decollate; _iu cham_ (jiu1 zaam2 腰斬), to divide a criminal in the middle; _cham teng tsit t'it_ (zaam2 deng1 zit6 tit3 斬釘截鐵) to cut an iron nail---decided, sincere; _kam cham_ (gaam1 zaam2 監斬), to superintend an execution; _cham hoi_ (zaam2 hoi1 斬開), to cut open; _cham fat_ (zaam2 fat6 斬伐) to subjugate; to prune. Williams' 斬衰: Corrected _shui_ (seoi1) to _ts'ui_ (ceoi1). Williams' 腰斬: Now used figuratively for midway cancellation or termination. Williams' 斬伐: Changed _fat_ (faat6) to modern _fat_ (fat6). 【斬】 (tsreamX) (_verb-like_) to chop (off), to sever
U+406A 眨 【䁪】 (zaam2) To blink. 䁪吓眼 (zaam2 haa5 ngaan5): "In the blink of an eye." To flash. 眼燈䁪緊 (ngaan5-2 dang1 zaam2 gan2): "The light's flashing."

zaam3.text: U+8638 May appear as either ⿱艹醮 or ⿱艹⿱醀灬 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.65 蘸) 以物淬水中 (Page 16|n59 蘸) To dip (Kangxi: 以物投水也), as a pencil in ink, or sop a morsel in gravy; used by some for baptize. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿱艹⿰酉隹 (missing 灬) to 蘸, and corrected 禾 to 水. Williams' "pencil": In the (now) dated sense of a brush.

zaam6.text: U+66AB (F2.65 暫) 卽淡切暫時間言不久也 (Page 553 暫) ~~A part of a day;~~ for the time being, briefly, shortly, for a short time, in the interim, meanwhile (Kangxi: 不久也, 須臾也); suddenly (Kangxi: 暫猶卒也); _tsam shi_ (zaam6 si4 暫時), a litte while; _tsam yan_ (zaam6 jan2 暫忍), be patient; _tsam u_ (zaam6 jyu6 暫遇) suddenly met him; _tsam chu_ (zaam6 zyu6 暫住) a temporary lodging; _ho tsam pat ho sheung_ (ho2 zaam6 bat1 ho2 soeng4 可暫不可常), it will do for a temporary arrangement. 【暫】 (dzamH) (_adverb-like_) momentarily (_adverb-like_) suddenly

U+7AD9 zaam3 (per 《廣韻》陟陷切) (F2.71 站 _Chan_ (zaan6)) 站立也又音賺 (Page 16|n59 站) A stage, a journey; _yik cham_ (jik6 zaam6 驛站) a stage; _cham t'au_ (zaam6 tau4 站頭), end of a journey; _yat cham lo_ (jat1 zaam6 lou6 一站路) a stage, usually reckoned to be a league. (Page 19|n62 站 _Chan_ (zaan6); here corrected to _Cham_ (zaam6)) To stand (Kangxi: 久立也); to stand erect; to stop; a stoppage; _~~chan~~ ``cham`` hi loi_ (zaam6 hei2 loi4 站起來), stand up! _~~chan~~ ``cham`` pan_ (zaam6 baan1 站班), to file off in rank, and salute an officer. Fan Wan entry: Note that Fan Wan considers _chan_ (zaan6) to be the canonical reading of 站, with 賺 to be read _cham_ (zaam6). Williams' 驛站: Corrected _yik_ (jik1) to _yik_ (jik6). Stage meaning a relay station for horses.

U+8CFA (F2.65 賺) 中淡切猶眈誤也阻之也生意獲利亦曰賺 (Page 16|n59 賺) To impede, to hinder (Fan Wan: 阻之也); to sell (Kangxi: 賣也), (...); to gain unfairly, to palm off poor goods (Kangxi: 一曰市物失實); _cho cham_ (zo2 zaam6 阻賺) to deceive; to hinder. Fan Wan entry: The supplied dissection 中淡切 is incorrect, as it results in the 陰去 reading _cham_ (zaam3), rather than the intended 陽去 reading _cham_ (zaam6). Normalised 𫉬 to 獲.

zaan6.text: U+8CFA 贃 zaam6 (per 《集韻》直陷切) (F2.65 賺 _Cham_ (zaam6)) 中淡切猶眈誤也阻之也生意獲利亦曰賺 (Page 16|n59 賺 _Cham_ (zaam6); here normalised to _Chan_ (zaan6)) (...) to profit; (Page 20|n63 贃; here normalised to 賺) An unauthorized word. To profit, to make gain in trade; _chan ts'in_ (zaan6 cin4-2 賺錢), to make money; _to chan_ (do1 zaan6 多賺) profitable; _mo tak chan_ (mou5 dak1 zaan6 冇得賺) no profit; _chan tak lo_ (zaan6 dak1 lou1 賺得撈), got my outlay back; _chan wan_ (zaan6 wan6 賺混) nothing but a trouble. Fan Wan entry: The supplied dissection 中淡切 is incorrect, as it results in the 陰去 reading _cham_ (zaam3), rather than the intended 陽去 reading _cham_ (zaam6). Normalised 𫉬 to 獲.

zaan2.text: U+5139 儧 (F2.69 儹) 積儹聚也 (Page 555 儧; here normalised to 儹) To accumulate, to collect together, to hoard up, to store (Kangxi 儧: 聚而計事也).

U+373A 𡣶 zaan3 (per 《廣韻》則旰切) zaan6 (per 《廣韻》徂賛切) Arguably, _tsan_ (zaan2) is a vernacular rising-tone reading of _tsan_ (zaan6) (per 《廣韻》徂賛切), meaning "pretty" (美好皃); see below. 【㜺】 (zaan2) Wonderful, pretty. 【㜺鬼】 (zaan2 gwai2) Intriguing, pretty, neat. Olden attestation for 㜺 _tsan_ (zaan6) meaning "pretty": 《廣韻》徂賛切 㜺 不謹也一曰美好皃
U+661D zaam2 (per 《廣韻》子感切) (F2.69 昝) 姓 (Page 555 昝) Sometimes used for _tsa_ (zaa1) 咱 I, me, in the north. A surname (Kangxi: 姓也).
