zaan6.text: U+68E7 zaan2 (per Kangxi 阻限切 for "small-bridge" sense) zaan6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.71 棧) 閣木爲棚曰棧四川有棧道 (Page 19|n62 棧) ~~A covered loft;~~ palisades; a scaffold (Kangxi: 棚也); a covered, planked road cut out of the side of hills (Kangxi: 閣也, 木棧道也); a hearse (Kangxi: 柩車亦謂之棧), a workshop; a warehouse; a storehouse, a stable (Kangxi: 馬棧亦編木爲之), a pen, a sheepcote; a wooden bridge (Kangxi: 小橋曰棧) or pathway (Kangxi: 編木曰棧); _chan fong_ (zaan6-2 fong4 棧房), a warehouse, a packhouse; _chan tso_ (zaan6-2 zou1 棧租), storage charges; _~~miu~~ ``mau`` chan_ (maau1 zaan6-2 貓棧) a kennel for cats. Williams' 貓棧: Changed _miu_ (miu4) to common reading _mau_ (maau1). An inn or guesthouse. 【棧】 (dzreanX, dzrjenX, dzraenH) (_noun-like_) plank (_adjective-like_) planked, (_met._) hearse (_noun-like_) cote

zaan2.text: U+7416 盞 (F2.69 醆) 爵也夏曰醆殷曰斝周曰爵皆酒器也 (F2.69 琖) 同上 (Page 19|n62 琖) A wine-cup, made ~~deeper than the preceding (盞),~~ of jade, or other valuable stone (Kangxi: 玉爵也, 玉琖小杯). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 牽 (but with top similar to 立) to 斝. 【琖】 (tsreanX) (_noun-like_) jade-cup

U+76DE (F2.69 盞) 酒盞 (Page 19|n62 盞) A shallow cup ~~for oil~~ (Kangxi: 杯也, 酒盞最小杯也); a wine saucer; the classifier of lamps; _tang chan_ (dang1 zaan2 燈盞), a saucer for a night lamp; _yat chan tang_ (jat1 zaan2 dang1 一盞燈), a lamp. 【盞】 (tsreanX) (_noun-like_) cup 【盞】 (zaan2) Classifier for lamps or lights. 盞燈壞咗 (zaan2 dang1 waai6 zo2): "The light's broken." Only, just (for emphasising that a negative outcome is highly likely). 盞嘥氣 (zaan2 saai1 hei3): "Talking to him will just be a waste of breath." 行得慢咪盞遲到 (haang4 dak1 maan6 mai6 zaan2 ci4 dou3): "If we walk slow then we'll only arrive late, won't we?"

U+8DB2 䟎 zaan3 (per 《廣韻》則旰切 for "scatter" sense and 《集韻》則旰切 for "hasten" sense) zaan6 (per 《廣韻》藏旱切 for "scatter" sense) zou1 (per 《集韻》宗蘇切 for "run" sense) (F2.69 趲) 催也促也 (Page 555 䟎; here normalised to 趲) To ~~walk~~ ``run``, to get on (Kangxi 趲: 走也); to flee, to run away, to scatter, to put to flight (Kangxi 趲: 散走也); to hasten, to urge, to quicken, to press (Kangxi: 逼使走也); _tsan po_ (zaan2 bou6 趲步) to walk quicker; _ts'ui tsan_ (ceoi1 zaan2 催趲), to urge on; _yat yat loi tsan_ (jat6 jat6 loi4 zaan2 日日來趲), he comes urging me daily; _tsan k'u hi shan_ (zaan2 keoi5 hei2 san1 趲佢起身), urge him to get out of bed. The reading _tsan_ (zaan2) is not unattested; Kangxi has 《集韻》...又子罕切 for the "run" sense. However, note that 《集韻》 actually has 子䍐切 for the "hasten" sense, explained 逼使赱也. Williams' 日日來趲: Corrected _yat_ (jat1) to _yat_ (jat6). 【走趲】 (zau2 zaan2) Wiggle room or leeway. 冇得走趲 (mou5 dak1 zau2 zaan2): "There's no wiggle room." 有得走趲 (jau5 dak1 zau2 zaan2): "There's wiggle room."

U+9186 (F2.69 醆) 爵也夏曰醆殷曰斝周曰爵皆酒器也 (F2.69 琖) 同上 (Page 19|n62 醆) Muddy, unsettled spirits (Kangxi: 酒濁而微淸也); liquor beginning to clear; a cup, a goblet (Kangxi: 爵也). 【醆】 (tsreanX) (_noun-like_) wine-cup (_noun-like_) slightly-clarified-wine

zaan6.text: U+64B0 譔 (for "write" and "compile" senses) zaan3 (common) (F2.71 撰) 造也脩也述也具也翰林又修撰 (Page 19|n62 撰) ~~To regulate, to correct, to dispose in order;~~ to grasp (Kangxi: 持也); to compose, to record (Kangxi: 撰述也屬辭記事曰撰); a rule, a statute (Kangxi: 則也); a received maxim; a business, an act; to edit, to revise books, to publish; _sau chan_ (sau1 zaan6 修撰) the first Hanlin (翰林) academician; _chan shut_ (zaan6 seot6 撰述) to narrate. (Page 19|n62 譔) Same as the above (撰). _chan man_ (zaan6 man4 譔文), to write essays; _pai chan_ (baai3 zaan6 拜譔) to prepare a eulogistic paper. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 㫦 to 脩. An aspiration, an inclination, a bent. 【撰】 (dzrwaenX, dzrjwenX) (_noun-like_) law of nature (_verb-like_) to take hold (_verb-like_) to prepare, to make (_verb-like_) to compose, to compile

zin1.text: U+6FFA 淺 (Page 574 濺 _Tsin_ (zin3); here corrected to _Tsin_ (zin1)) A swift current (Kangxi: 濺濺水疾流貌); a raceway;

zin3.text: U+6FFA (F1.23 濺) 水激酒也 (Page 574 濺) to spatter up, to dash against; to squirt out, to spurt (Kangxi: 水激也), as blood. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿰冫賤 to 濺. 【濺】 (tsjenH) (_verb-like_) to spatter

zaan3.text: U+7052 濺 濽 (F2.70 灒) 汚洒也 (Page 555 濺灒) To ~~stir up~~ ``spatter`` muddy water (Kangxi 灒: 汙灑也); ~~turbid water;~~ to spatter; to rebound, to recoil; resilient; _tsan nan shan_ (zaan3 nan2 san1 灒㤻身), dirtied and splashed; _tsan hi_ (zaan3 hei2 灒起), to bound back; _tsan lok ti_ (zaan3 lok6 dei6 灒落地) bounded back on the ground; _tsan shap shan_ (zaan3 sap1 san1 灒濕身), spattered against me. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 酒 to 洒.

U+74DA 瓉 zaan6 (per 《廣韻》藏旱切) (F2.70 瓚) 玉瓚祭器也受五升 (Page 555 瓚) A sort of stone ~~sceptre made with a~~ cup, used in pouring out libations (Kangxi: 瓚亦圭也...以進于神也, 一曰祼器, 瓚者器名 etc.); a kind of bottle used in worship. The reading _tsan_ (zaan3) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 則旰切, explained 圭也, which can arguably be interpreted in the "cup" sense rather than the "tablet" sense. Impure jade (Kangxi: 三玉二石也). 【瓚】 (dzanX) (_noun-like_) libation-cup (_noun-like_) impure-jade

U+8B9A 讃 贊 (F2.70 讚) 稱美也 (Page 555 贊) to praise, to commend, in which sense it is interchanged with the next (讚); (Page 555 讚) To praise, to commend, to laud (Kangxi: 稱人之美曰讚); to sing praises to; to record the praises of (Kangxi: 又錄也); to explain (Kangxi: 解也); _tak yan tsan_ (dak1 jan4 zaan3 得人讚) to be praised, praiseworthy; _tsan mi_ (zaan3 mei5 讚美), to praise; _tsan sin_ (zaan3 sin6 讚羨) to commend; _tsung tsan_ (zung6 zaan3 頌讚) to extol, to sing praises to; _tsan tak kwo_ (zaan3 dak1 gwo3 頌得過) laudable. 【讚】 (tsanH) (_verb-like_) to praise

U+8D0A 讚 賛 (F2.70 贊) 助也佐也又贊頌 (Page 555 贊) To visit a superior, to come before a god (Kangxi: 見也); to second (Kangxi: 贊佐也), to assist in bringing forward (Kangxi: 出也助也), to introduce, to bring a present to one; to make clear, to bring to light (Kangxi: 贊明也); ~~to go out;~~ to give evidence; _tsan cho_ (zaan3 zo6 贊助) to aid in, to further; _tsan t'ong_ (zaan3 tong4 贊堂), to still a court, as the crier does; _tsan shing_ (zaan3 sing4 贊成), to assist in bringing about; _tsan kin_ (zaan3 gin3 贊見) to visit. (Page 555 讚) to assist (Kangxi 讚: 佐也); Williams' 贊助: Now has the connotation of *financial* aid, i.e. sponsorship. 【贊】 (tsanH) (_verb-like_) to assist (in), to advance

zaan6.text: U+7DBB (F2.71 綻) 衣縫裂也事不完全曰破綻 (Page 19|n62 綻) A rent seam (Kangxi: 衣縫); to rip, to tear, to rend (Kangxi: 綻猶解也); a hint, inkling; _p'o chan_ (po3 zaan6 破綻) the affair is known, to hint a matter; _ta tak p'i hoi yuk chan_ (daa2 dak1 pei4 hoi1 juk6 zaan6 打得皮開肉綻) beaten till the flesh was laid bare; _chan lit_ (zaan6 lit6 綻裂) rip the seams. To blossom. 綻放 (zaan6 fong3): "To blossom." 【綻】 (dreanH) (_adjective-like_) rent

U+8665 虦 (F2.71 虥) 虎淺毛 (Page 19|n62 虥) A tiger cat (Kangxi: 貓也); _chan ~~mai~~ ``miu``_ (zaan6 miu4 虥苗), a tiger ~~shedding its~~ ``with light-colored`` hair (Kangxi: 虎竊毛謂之虦貓). Williams' 虥苗: Corrected _mai_ (maai4) to _miu_ (miu4). Note that 虥苗 is also spelt 虥貓, as the Middle Chinese reading of 貓 is homophonous with 苗 _miu_ (miu4). In any case, Williams' _mai_ (maai4) is a typo.

U+8B54 cyun1 (alternative per 《廣韻》此縁切) zaan3 (common) (F2.71 譔) 脩也作也作文曰譔文 (Page 19|n62 譔) ~~To exhort by precepts~~ ``To teach focusedly`` (Kangxi: 專敎也); To panegyrise (Kangxi: 善言也). 【譔】 (tshjwen, dzrjwenX, dzrjwenH) (_verb-like_) to panegyrise

U+8F1A (F2.71 輚) 臥車 (Page 19|n62 輚) A tumbril ~~for carrying the wounded~~; ~~a hearse;~~ _chan lo_ (zaan6 lou6 輚輅) a ~~military~~ carriage for sleeping in (Kangxi: 輚輅臥車). Williams' "military carriage": This is a separate sense for 輚 itself (Kangxi: 兵車也).

U+994C (F2.71 饌) 具食 (Page 19|n62 饌) To eat, to drink; to feed persons; to provide for (Kangxi: 具食也, 謂饌陳具設之也); dressed food (Kangxi: 飯食也)~~:~~``;`` _ch'it chan_ (cit3 zaan6 設饌) to set out a dinner; _shing chan_ (sing6 zaan6 盛饌) a banquet; _ts'oi chan_ (coi3 zaan6 菜饌) food; _yau mat ngau chan_ (jau5 mat1 ngaau4 zaan6 有乜餚饌) what delicacy did you have for a relish? 【饌】 (dzrjwenH) (_verb-like_) to consume (_verb-like_) to serve (viands) (_noun-like_) viand

zang1.text: U+5D22 caang4 (per 《廣韻》助庚切) zaang1 (longer syllable) (F2.29 崢) 山峻貌又頭角崢嶸 (Page 21|n64 崢 _Chang_ (zaang1); here normalised to _Chang_ (zang1)) To rise above; conspicuous, eminent, like a lofty mountain (Kangxi: 崝嶸也); _t'au kok chang ~~ying~~ ``wing``_ (tau4 gok3 zang1 wing4 頭角崢嶸) a noble appearance, dignified. Williams' 頭角崢嶸: Corrected _ying_ (jing4) to _wing_ (wing4).

zaang1.text: U+68E6 May appear as either ⿰木争 (+6) or ⿰木爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font.

zang1.text: U+68E6 May appear as either ⿰木争 (+6) or ⿰木爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. caang1 (per 《廣韻》楚耕切) zaang1 (longer syllable) (F2.29 棦) 木節 (Page 20|n63 棦) A bundle of sticks, a number of sticks tied up into a faggot (Kangxi: 木束也).

U+722D zaang1 (longer syllable) (F2.29 爭) 爭競爭闘 (Page 20|n63 爭 _Chang_ (zaang1); here normalised to _Chang_ (zang1) when appropriate) To wrangle, to strive for precedence (Kangxi: 引也, 競也); to contest, to litigate (Kangxi: 訟也); ~~A colloquial word;~~ ~~just, only, nearly, a little;~~ _chang noi_ (zang1 noi6 爭奈) but still, howbeit, nevertheless; _chang tam hi_ (zaang1 daam6 hei3 爭啖氣) unforgiving, choleric; _chang li ho_ (zaang1 lei4 hou4 爭釐毫), to quarrel upon trifles; _chang mat ye_ (zaang1 mat1 je5 爭乜嘢), what are you quarreling about? _chang tik sheung tong_ (zaang1 dik1 soeng5 dong3 爭滴上當) I came near being gulled; _chang tau_ (zang1 dau3 爭鬥) to fight, to fall to blows; _chang tsung_ (zang1 zung6 爭訟) to go to law; _chang kung_ (zaang1 gung1 爭功), to ~~emulate~~ ``claim merit``; _chang tut_ (zang1 dyut6 爭奪) to take by force; _chang chap_ (zang1 zap1 爭執) obstinate; _chang yau han_ (zaang1 jau5 haan6 爭有限) differs a little; _chang tik chuk to k'u_ (zaang1 dik1 zuk1 dou3-2 keoi5 爭滴捉到佢), all but caught him; _chang au_ (zang1 aau3 爭詏) to dispute; _chang tak to_ (zaang1 dak1 do1 爭得多), very different; not enough; _chang kau_ (zaang1 gau3 爭夠) I'm tired of striving, I'll contend no more. Fan Wan places 爭 under the short syllable _chang_ (zang1); Williams places it under the longer _chang_ (zaang1). The longer syllable _chang_ (zaang1) is preferred in the vernacular register, so normalisation to the shorter _chang_ (zang1) is only appropriate in some instances. Williams' "A colloquial word" etc.: Yes it is a vernacular word, but the meaning is to differ or to lack (see below), not "just", "only", etc. Williams' 爭奪: Corrected _tut_ (dyut3) to _tut_ (dyut6). Williams' 爭得多: Corrected _to_ (dou1) to _to_ (do1). 【爭】 (tsreang) (_verb-like_) to strive, to dispute 【爭】 (zaang1) To differ by. 爭好遠 (zaang1 hou2 jyun5): "Off by very far" 爭少少 (zaang1 siu2 siu2): "Off by a little." 爭啲 (zaang1 di1): "Off by a bit." To lack, to be missing, to be short. 爭對筷子 (zaang1 deoi3 faai3 zi2): "Short a pair of chopsticks." 爭一毫 (zaang1 jat1 hou4): "Ten cents short." To owe. 爭住先 (zaang1 zyu6 sin1): "I owe you for now" (i.e. I'll pay you back later). 爭佢錢 (zaang1 keoi5 cin4-2): "I owe him money." 【爭在】 (zaang1 zoi6) To matter. Especially used in contrast to something that does not matter (or in contrast to something that does matter when used in the negative, 唔爭在). 輸就輸硬、爭在輸幾多 (syu1 zau6 syu1 ngaang6, zaang1 zoi6 syu1 gei2 do1): "Yes they've lost for sure, but what matters now is how much they lose by." 唔爭在遲多兩分鐘 (m4 zaang1 zoi6 ci4 do1 loeng5 fan1 zung1): "It won't matter that we're two more minutes late on top of how late we are now." To be lacking in, or to fall short in. 平就平、爭在唔夠飽 (peng4 zau6 peng4, zaang1 zoi6 m4 gau3 baau2): "Yes it's cheap, but it falls short in that it's not adequately filling."

zaang1.text: U+7424 May appear as either ⿰𤣩争 (+6) or ⿰𤣩爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font.

zang1.text: U+7424 May appear as either ⿰𤣩争 (+6) or ⿰𤣩爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. caang1 (per 《廣韻》楚庚切 and 楚耕切) zaang1 (longer syllable) (F2.29 琤) 玉聲 (Page 20|n63 琤) The tinkling sound of gems when they are struck or rung together (Kangxi: 玉聲).

U+7B8F zaang1 (longer syllable) (F2.29 筝) 樂器十二絃 (Page 20|n63 箏) A kind of harpsichord with 12 copper strings (Kangxi: 鼓絃竹身樂也, 箏瑟類本十二絃今則十三); _fung chang_ (fung1 zang1 風箏), glasses hung to jingle in the wind; _la chang_ (laa2 zang1 揦箏), to thrum the guitar; _fong fung chang_ (fong3 fung1 zang1 放風箏), to fly a singing kite.

zaang1.text: U+3B39 May appear as either ⿰月争 (+6) or ⿰⺼爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. 踭 zang1 (per Fan Wan) (F2.29 踭 _Chang_ (zang1)) 足跟筋也 (Page 21|n64 㬹) The tendon Achilles (Kangxi: 足跟筋也); the heel; the elbow; _yat chang ta kwo hu_ (jat1 zaang1 daa2 gwo3 heoi3 一㬹打過去) elbowed him off; _cham chang_ (zam2 zaang1 枕㬹), to lean on the elbow; _t'at chang hai_ (taat3 zaang1 haai4 撻㬹鞋), slipshod; _hai chang_ (haai4 zaang1 鞋㬹), heel of the shoe; _shau chang_ (sau2 zaang1 手㬹), the elbow. 【㬹】 (zaang1) The heel. 腳㬹 (goek3 zaang1): "Heel of the foot." 鞋㬹 (haai4 zaang1): "Heel of a shoe." The elbow. 手㬹 (sau2 zaang1): "The elbow."
U+8E2D May appear as either ⿰𧾷争 (+6) or ⿰𧾷爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.29 踭 _Chang_ (zang1)) 足跟筋也
U+931A May appear as either ⿰釒争 (+6) or ⿰釒爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font.

zang1.text: U+931A May appear as either ⿰釒争 (+6) or ⿰釒爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. caang1 (per 《廣韻》楚耕切) zaang1 (longer syllable) (F2.29 錚) 金聲 (Page 20|n63 錚) A small cymbal, a small sized gong (Kangxi: 鉦也); the ringing sound of metals (Kangxi: 金聲也). The reading _chang_ (zang1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 甾莖切, explained 說文金聲也.

zaang3.text: U+8ACD May appear as either ⿰訁争 (+6) or ⿰訁爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. (F2.29 諍 _Chang_ (zang6)) 諫諍救正之也

zang3.text: U+8ACD May appear as either ⿰訁争 (+6) or ⿰訁爭 (+8 assumed here), depending on the font. 爭 zaang3 (longer syllable) (F2.29 諍 _Chang_ (zang6)) 諫諍救正之也 (Page 20|n63 諍) To remonstrate with; to stop a wrong (Kangxi: 止也, 謂止其失也, 諫諍救正也); _kan chang_ (gaan3 zang3 諫諍) to reprove one's sovereign; to reprehend. (Page 20|n63 爭 _Chang_ (zaang1); _Chang_ (zang3) implied) to reprove, to remonstrate, in which senses it is used for the last (諍) (Kangxi 爭: 本作諍諫諍也止也). Fan Wan entry: 諍 is spuriously placed under the final _ang_ (ang6).

zaap3.text: U+5284 箚 (F2.66 劄) 帋劄又以針刺也又柬疏謂之劄子 (Page 22|n65 劄) To prick, to puncture (Kangxi 箚: 以鍼刺也); to embroider; a document, a paper (Kangxi 箚: 牋劄用以奏事非表非狀者謂之劄子); a particular sort of paper; ~~a diploma;~~ a contract; to write out, as a list of prices; a reply from a higher officer; ~~a remonstrance to the emperor;~~ _chi chap_ (zi2 zaap3 紙劄) stationery; _chap tsz'_ (zaap3 zi2 劄子) ~~a remonstrance~~; _kam chap_ (gaam3 zaap3 監劄) a diploma bought by a _kiensang_ (監生); _chap ka ts'in_ (zaap3 gaa3 cin4 劄價錢), write the prices; _chap lok po_ (zaap3 lok6 bou6-2 劄落簿) put it to account; _chap tan_ (zaap3 daan1 劄單), a contract for a purchase. The word _chop_, used in Canton is derived from this. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 剌 to 刺. Kangxi has 箚 (⿱𥫗㓣) for the headword, but 劄 (⿰答刂) in the explanations.

U+8936 zip3 (per 襵) (Page 22* 褶 _Chip_ (zip3)) Used for the last (襵), but always read _chap_ or _tsap_ (both zaap3). _kw'an chap_ (kwan4 zaap3 裙褶) the plaits on a petticoat; _ta ~~chip~~ ``chap``_ (daa2 zaap3 打褶) to tighten the girdle. For the literary form, see 襵 zip3. Williams' 打褶: Changed _chip_ (zip3) to common reading _chap_ (zaap3).

zaap6.text: U+8936 (Page 22* 褶 _Chip_ (zip3); here corrected to _Tsap_ (zaap6)) a lined riding jacket (Kangxi: 袴褶騎服); _fu ~~chap~~ ``tsap``_ (fu3 zaap6 袴褶) overalls for horseback;
U+6781 (Page 22|n65 极) A colloquial word. A camp chair; _ma chap_ (maa5 zaap6 馬极) a camp-stool; _chap chak fan_ (zaap6 zak1 fan3 极側瞓) to sleep on the side.

U+69E2 (F2.39 槢 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 堅木 (Page 559 槢) Firm, hard wood (Kangxi: 堅木); durable timber; _tsap muk_ (zaap6 muk6 槢木) any kind of hard wood. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰米習 to 槢. A connector-peg (Kangxi: 械楔).

U+7FD2 (F2.39 習 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 學慣熟也狎也練也 (Page 558 習) ~~A continued flight~~ ``Flight by repetition`` (Kangxi: 數飛也); to repeat the same act, to practice, to get accustomed to, to become skilled in; practice, use, custom, habit, exercise, repetition (Kangxi: 習重也); _hok tsap_ (hok6 zaap6 學習) apprenticed to; to learn about; _tsap kwan shing tsz' in_ (zaap6 gwaan3 sing4 zi6 jin4 習慣成自然), practice makes it natural; _tsap shuk_ (zaap6 suk6 習熟) well versed in, habituated to; _tsap lin_ (zaap6 lin6 習練) practice; _tsap hau yam_ (zaap6 hau2 jam1 習口音), to practice speaking; _tsap i pat ch'at_ (zaap6 ji4 bat1 caat3 習而不察) to take without examination, careless practice; _tsap 'm to_ (zaap6 m4 dou3-2 習唔到) cannot learn the use of it; _tsap tsuk ~~shi~~ ``sz'`` in_ (zaap6 zuk6 si2 jin4 習俗使然), I've learned the usages. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 綀 to 練. Williams' "A continued flight": Better rendered "Flight by repetition". In Kangxi 數飛也, note that 數 is to be read in entering tone, _shok_ (sok3). Williams' 習俗使然: Corrected _shi_ (si5) to _sz'_ (si2). An acquaintance, a familiar. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【習】 (zip) (_verb-like_) to practise (_adjective-like_) gentle
U+894D (F2.40 雜 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 參錯也亂也 (F2.40 襍 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 同上 (F2.66 雜) 叅錯也五綵相合也 (F2.66 襍) 同上 (Page 558 雜襍)

U+8972 (F2.39 襲 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 重衣也因也又潛師揜取曰襲 (Page 558 襲) Double garments (Kangxi: 重衣也)~~, lined robes, a court dress~~; a suit; a robe fastened on the left side (Kangxi: 左袵袍也); ~~collected~~ ``to match`` (Kangxi: 合也); repeated (Kangxi: 重也); to make a foray, to invade, to steal into (Kangxi: 掩其不備也); to receive, to inherit (Kangxi: 受也); hereditary, descending in a family; _shai tsap_ (sai3 zaap6 世襲) hereditary; _tsap yam_ (zaap6 jam3 襲蔭) hereditary protection and pension --- for the merit of ancestors; _yat tsap i_ (jat1 zaap6 ji1 一襲衣), a suit of clothes; _shai tsap tsz' tseuk_ (sai3 zaap6 zi2 zoek3 世襲子爵) a hereditary baron. Williams' "Double garments": Seen classically in the verb sense, i.e. to put on a second layer of clothing. Williams' "a court dress": This is a misreading of Kangxi 襲朝服 as a definition, when it is actually an example for the verb sense 服也, to wear. Williams' "collected": This is a misreading of Kangxi 合也. Here, 合 means "match". To wear (Kangxi: 服也). 【襲】 (zip) (_verb-like_) to layer (up), to shroud (_verb-like_) to repeat (_verb-like_) to herit (_verb-like_) to raid

U+9212 zaap6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (Page 22|n65 鈒) A colloquial word. One bladed shears working on a pivot; to slice or pare with shears; _chap hoi_ (zaap6 hoi1 鈒開), cut it open; _pan long chap_ (ban1 long4 zaap6-2 檳榔鈒) betel-nut shears; _chap yeuk ts'oi_ (zaap6 joek6 coi4 鈒藥材), to slice medicine; _chap sui_ (zaap6 seoi3 鈒碎) slice it fine. 【鈒-noun】 (zaap6, zaap6-2) Shears. 【鈒-verb】 (zaap6) To cut or slice, especially with shears.

U+9598 aap3 (per 《廣韻》烏甲切 for "barrier" sense) gap3 (per 《廣韻》古盍切 for "shut" sense) (F2.66 閘) 閘門 (Page 22|n65 閘) A guarded gate, a barrier, a barricade (Kangxi: 開閉門也); dam; a barrier like a turnstile or a stockade; a lock, a flood-gate; to shut a gate (Kangxi: 閉門也); _chap tsap_ (zaap6 zaap6 閘？) a guard-house at a gate; _kwan chap_ (gwaan1 zaap6 關閘) to shut the gate; the Barrier at Macao; _shui chap_ (seoi2 zaap6 水閘) a waste-weir, a mill-race or sluice with gates; _chap fu_ (zaap6 fu1 閘夫) a gatekeeper; _hon chap_ (hon1 zaap6 看閘) to guard the gate; _chap mun_ (zaap6 mun4 閘門), a street gate; _sheung chap_ (soeng5 zaap6 上閘) put in the bars.

U+96B0 (F2.39 隰 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 下溼曰隰 (Page 559 隰) Low, marshy land (Kangxi: 下濕曰隰); a pool, a morass, wet meadows at the base of hills (Kangxi: 阪下濕也); _tsap t'in_ (zaap6 tin4 隰田), low wet fields, marshes. Newly cleared or developed land (Kangxi: 隰謂新發田也). The waterside or riverside (Kangxi: ...水邊也). 【隰】 (zip) (_noun-like_) marshland

U+96C6 (F2.40 集 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 聚集會集 (Page 558 集) To flock together ``on a tree`` (Kangxi: 本作雧羣鳥在木上也); to gather, as clouds; to assemble, to come together, to congregate, to collect (Kangxi: 合也, 聚也會也); to bring together (Kangxi: 同也, 集齊也); ~~to go directly to the mark;~~ to mix properly, to blend; to make a collection, to compile (Kangxi: 雜也); a miscellany, ana; a place where people collect, as a market; ~~often interchanged with the last (雜);~~ _ts'ai tsap_ (cai4 zaap6 齊集) came together, a full concourse; _man tsap_ (man4 zaap6 文集) a miscellany; _yat tsap_ (jat1 zaap6 一集) one division of a book, what is collected under one head; _tsap ui hi_ (zaap6 wui6 hei3 集會戲) a varied bill, as at a theatre; _chung yat hap tsap_ (zung1 jat6 haap6 zaap6 終日狎集) friends here the whole day; _t'in ha wan tsap_ (tin1 haa6 wan4 zaap6 天下雲集) crowded together from all quarters; _tsu tsap_ (zeoi6 zaap6 聚集) to assemble, to collect. Williams' "to go directly to the mark": Perhaps intended for Kangxi 就也成也, but that is better rendered "accomplished" or "completed". 【集】 (dzip) (_verb-like_) to perch (together) (_verb-like_) to collect

U+96DC 什 襍 zaap6-2 (in 打雜, and rising second in vernacular 雜雜地) (F2.40 雜 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 參錯也亂也 (F2.40 襍 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 同上 (F2.66 雜) 叅錯也五綵相合也 (F2.66 襍) 同上 (Page 558 雜襍) A mixture of different colors (Kangxi: 五彩相合也) or ingredients; particolored, streaked; mixed, unassorted, blended, mingled, confused; various, all sorts, miscellaneous, heterogenous, fragmentary (Kangxi: 碎也); to mix, to confuse; ~~to repeat;~~ to revolve (Kangxi: 帀也); to bore through (Kangxi: 穿也); _tsap fo_ (zaap6 fo3 雜貨) miscellaneous goods; _tsap fo p'o_ (zaap6 fo3 pou3 雜貨舖) a variety store; _tsap shik_ (zaap6 sik1 雜色) variegated; _sam tsap_ (sam1 zaap6 心雜) distracted, not keeping long at a purpose; _tsap chung tsai_ (zaap6 zung2 zai2 雜種仔), a bastard; _tsap t'ong kwo_ (zaap6 tong4 gwo2 雜糖果), chowchow sweetmeats; _'ng fong tsap ch'u_ (ng5 fong1 zaap6 cyu5 五方雜處), a place where all sorts of people live; _tsap lun_ (zaap6 lyun6 雜亂) confused, put in disorder; _han tsap yan tang_ (haan4 zaap6 jan4 dang2 閒雜人等) loafers, idlers; _ta tsap_ (daa2 zaap6-2 打雜) man of all work; _shik tsap_ (sik6 zaap6 食雜) to eat anything, not to fast; _lap tsap_ (laap6 zaap6 垃雜) miscellaneous, a medley. Williams' "unassorted": Meaning "unsorted" or "miscellaneous". Williams' 五方雜處: Changed _ch'u_ (cyu2) to common reading _ch'u_ (cyu5). 【雜】 (dzop) (_adjective-like_) mingled 【垃雜】 (laap6 zaap6) Disorganised or cluttered. Commonly spelt 立雜 (see discussion below). 【雜】 (zaap6) Used to describe a place as dodgy, due to the presence of miscellaneous unsavoury characters. 城好雜 (seng4-2 hou2 zaap6): "The city is very dodgy." Offal. 牛雜 (ngau4 zaap6): "Beef assortment of offal." (In Hong Kong noodle shops, the typical parts are tripe (肚 tou5), lung (肺 fai3), intestine (腸 coeng4-2), and spleen (膀 bong2). ) There is an argument that 垃雜 _lap tsap_ (laap6 zaap6) should be spelt 擸𢶍. Indeed 擸𢶍 has an expected reading of _lap tsap_ (lap6 zap6), and is explained "broken" or "cluttered": 《廣韻》盧盍切 擸 折也又擸𢶍破壞也 《廣韻》才盍切 𢶍 擸𢶍和雜 《康熙字典》𢶍 《唐韻》才盍切《集韻》疾盍切𠀤音䪞讀若慙入聲擸𢶍和雜也... However, since 擸𢶍 is also considered the original spelling of 垃圾 laap6 saap3 "rubbish", it would be confusing to use it for laap6 zaap6 also. As a compromise that acknowledges the possible common ancestry, I have opted to spell laap6 zaap6 as 垃雜, even though the common spelling is 立雜.

U+972B (F2.40 霫 _Tsap_ (zap6)) 大雨 (Page 559 霫) A great shower (Kangxi: 雴霫大雨也), (...); an ancient tribe of northerners ~~near Corea or Manchuria~~, spoken of during the T'ang (唐) dynasty (Kangxi: 白霫北狄國). Williams' "near Corea or Manchuria": This is an erroneous merging of two distinct proper-noun (barbarian group) senses: 霫 _sap_ (sap1) as in 奚霫 from the north-east (see 霫 sap1); and 霫 _tsap_ (zaap6) as in 白霫 from the north. The second of these is the one where Kangxi has an example from 《唐書回鶻傳》.

zaat3.text: U+624E (F2.72 扎) 拔也作也 (Page 24|n67 扎) To pluck up (Kangxi: 拔也); To pierce, to jab, to push through. To spread, to extend. Used in 掙扎. 【扎馬】 (zaat3 maa5) To do the horse stance. 【扎醒】 (zaat3 seng2) To wake with a start. 【扎扎跳】 (zaat3 zaat3 tiu3) To jump up and down rapidly.

U+672D (F2.72 札) 柬也又書札 (Page 24|n67 札) A tablet for writing (Kangxi: 牒也, 牒亦木牘也); a letter, a document, writings; untimely death (Kangxi: 夭死爲札); a thin slip of wood (Kangxi: 札木簡之薄小者也); fold of armor (Kangxi: 甲葉也); a paddle (Kangxi: 撥水之櫂曰札形似札也); _ngan chat_ (ngaan6 zaat3 雁札) an epistle; _kan chat_ (gaan2 zaat3 柬札) or _shu chat_ (syu1 zaat3 書札) a letter. 【札】 (tsreat) (_noun-like_) slip (_noun-like_) pestilence

U+7D2E 扎 (F2.72 扎) 拔也作也 (Page 24|n67 扎; here normalised to 紮) to bind, to bind around, to bundle up (Kangxi: 纏束也); a bundle; to make or cut out paper images; _chat yat chat_ (zaat3 jat1 zaat3 紮一紮) bind it into a bundle; _chat keuk_ (zaat3 goek3 紮腳) to bind the feet of girls; _chat kan_ (zaat3 gan2 紮緊), bind it tight; _chi chat_ (zi2 zaat3 紙紮) paper houses, &c., to burn to the dead; also paper strings; _chat mai k'u_ (zaat3 maai4 keoi5 紮埋佢), tie it up in a roll; _yat chat fa_ (jat1 zaat3 faa1 一紮花), a nosegay; _chat tak shang_ (zaat3 dak1 sang1 紮得生), cut out to the life. To station. 紮營 (zaat3 jing4): "Set up camp."
U+8301 (F2.72 茁) 肥壯貌 (Page 24|n67 茁)

zyut3.text: U+8301 zaat3 (alternative per 《廣韻》鄒滑切) (F2.72 茁 _Chat_ (zaat3)) 肥壯貌 (Page 24|n67 茁 _Chat_ (zaat3); here normalised to _Chut_ (zyut3)) Grass or plants starting out of the ground (Kangxi: 草初生貌); fat, hearty, vigorous (Fan Wan: 肥壯貌), ``grown (Kangxi: 茁生長貌),`` said of cattle; _ts'o ~~chat~~ ``chut`` k'i nga_ (cou2 zyut3 kei4 ngaa4 草茁其芽), the grass is sprouting.

zaat3.text: U+8ECB aat3 (per 《廣韻》烏黠切) (Page 24|n67 軋) ~~Creaking~~ ``To crush with the`` roll of a wheel (Kangxi: 車輾也); a creaking, grating, sound; punishment of the ~~rack or~~ wheel (Kangxi: 𠛬名); _seung mo chat_ (soeng1 mo4 zaat3 相磨軋) to harass or impede each other; _chat chat_ (zaat3 zaat3 軋軋) creaking, jingling. Williams' "Creaking": This is a misreading of Kangxi 車載重蹍軋有聲也 as a definition, when it is actually an example. 【軋】 ('eat) (_verb-like_) to crush

zaat6.text: U+66F1 (Page 560 曱 _Ts'at_ (caat6); here corrected to _Tsat_ (zaat6)) Williams erroneously places this under _ts'at_ (caat6), notwithstanding the reading within the entry is correctly _tsat_ (zaat6).

U+7534 曱 zaat6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (Page 560 曱 _Ts'at_ (caat6); here corrected to _Tsat_ (zaat6) and normalised to 甴) A colloquial word. _~~Ka~~ ``Kat`` tsat_ (gaat6 zaat6 曱甴) a cockroach. Williams' 曱甴: Changed _Ka_ (gaa1) to _Kat_ (gaat6). The second character is correctly _tsat_ (zaat6) despite the entry being erroneously placed under _ts'at_ (caat6). 【曱甴】 (gaat6 zaat6, gaat6 zaat6-2) A cockroach. It is disputed whether gaat6 zaat6 is written 曱甴 or 甴曱 (neither character appears in the Middle Chinese rime books). The form 曱甴 is commonly used, but the form 甴曱 is considered standard by HK Education Bureau's Lexical Items and by 《商務新字典》 (ISBN: 9620701402). A blog post by 陳凱文 has investigated this dispute. To paraphrase, his concluding points include that: Based on the readings recorded in 《四聲篇海》, 《篇海類編》, and 《字彙補》, neither 曱甴 nor 甴曱 have an expected reading of exactly gaat6 zaat6, but 甴曱 is the reading with the greater discrepancy. It is possible that the form 甴曱 arose from a mistake by Eitel in parsing 反切 readings when looking up older Chinese sources, with modern dictionary editors following Eitel's form. The HK Education Bureau should not insist on the form 甴曱. I concur with points 1 and 3 above. I disagree with point 2 above, because Eitel's work is based off of Williams', which had already used the form 甴曱 in 1856 (notwithstanding Williams' reading is _ka tsat_ (gaa1 zaat6)). Moreover, I am obliged to point out some inaccuracies in 陳凱文's post: Whilst 陳凱文's text refers to the 1877 edition of Eitel's ''A Chinese Dictionary in the Cantonese Dialect'' (wherein 甴 is found under _Kat_ (gaat6) on Page 228), 陳凱文's screenshot is actually of the *1910* edition of Eitel's dictionary *revised and enlarged by Immanuel Gottlieb Genahr* (wherein 甴 is found under _Kat_ (gaat6) on Page 330). In my copy of 《商務新字典》, whose colophon has 9th print, August 1996 as the latest entry in the list of editions, the native reading for 甴 (on Page 438, under Radical ⽥) is "〔駕壓切低入〕", not just "【駕壓切】" as written by 陳凱文. It is possible that this discrepancy is down to edition (but I note that the year 1992 referenced by 陳凱文 does not appear in the list of editions in the colophon of my edition). In any case, I do not believe that modern dictionary editors have "虛構出一個【駕壓切】" as claimed by 陳凱文. Instead, "〔駕壓切低入〕" in 《商務新字典》 is just a modern (i.e. *not* Middle Chinese) and native (i.e. *not* romanisation) but clumsy way of representing the reading gaat6.

zaau1.text: U+5541 (F2.41 啁) 啁嘐聲 (Page 28|n71 啁) Chirping, bickering of birds (Kangxi: 啁哳鳥鳴也); _chau chau sheng_ (zaau1 zaau1 seng1 啁啁聲), chirping of birds. This reading is used in 啁哳, 啁嘐.

zau1.text: U+5541 Used in 啁噍 (sound of birds) (Kangxi: 啁噍鳥聲). 【啁】 (trjuw) Used in 啁噍. 【啁噍】 (trjuw tsjew) (_onomatopoeia_) twitter

zaau1.text: U+5632 啁 (F2.41 嘲) 以言相譏笑也 (Page 28|n71 嘲) To deride, to jeer at (Kangxi: 謔也); to ridicule; ~~raillery;~~ _pak chau_ (baak6 zaau1 白嘲), a pasquinade; _chau siu_ (zaau1 siu3 嘲笑) to laugh at; _chau ma_ (zaau1 maa6 嘲罵) to jeer and rail at. (Page 28|n71 啁) Interchanged with the last (嘲) (Kangxi 啁: 與嘲謿通). Williams' "raillery": To my knowledge, 嘲 is used for malicious or spiteful ridicule, not for good-natured banter. To recite (especially metrical poetry).

zaau2.text: U+5E1A 箒 zau2 (per 《廣韻》之九切) (F1.53 帚 _Chau_ (zau2)) 帚彗掃也 (Page 25|n68 帚箒 _Chau_ (zau2); here normalised to _Chau_ (zaau2)) A besom (Kangxi 箒: 箕箒也); a broom; to sweep up dirt (Kangxi 帚: 糞也, 掃除糞穢也), for which the first (帚) is most proper; _chap ki chau_ (zap1 gei1 zaau2 執箕帚), term for a wife or concubine. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing character 帚. 【帚】 (tsyuwX) (_noun-like_) besom

zau2.text: U+5E1A (F1.53 帚) 帚彗掃也 (Page 25|n68 帚箒) Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing character 帚.

zaau2.text: U+627E (F2.42 找) 找尋找換 (Page 29|n72 找) To seek; to make up the deficiency, to supply what is wanted (Kangxi: 俗音爪補不足曰找); to barter, to swap, to exchange; _chau ni fui hi_ (zaau2 nei5 fui3 hei3 找你晦氣) he's looking for you to scold you; _chau t'au lo_ (zaau2 tau4 lou6 找頭路) to look for work; _chau sho mi_ (zaau2 sou3 mei5 找數尾), let us settle up that account; _chau ts'am_ (zaau2 cam4 找尋), to look for; _chau un_ (zaau2 wun6 找換) to change money or for money; _chau ts'in p'o_ (zaau2 cin4 pou3 找錢舖) a money-changer's shop. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰扌弋 to 找. 【有找】 (jau5 zaau2) Suffixed after a monetary amount to indicate that the price is strictly lower. (Literally, "and there's change".) 廿蚊有找 (jaa6 man1 jau5 zaau2): "It's twenty bucks and there's change." 【找數】 (zaau2 sou3) To settle a bill or account.

U+6293 爪 (Page 29|n72 爪; here normalised to 抓) to scratch, to claw; to take up with the fingers; _chau lan_ (zaau2 laan6 抓爛) to tear with the claws; _chau shat_ (zaau2 sat6 抓實) to gripe firmly with the claws; _chau wa_ (zaau2 waa2 抓搲), to scratch; (Page 29|n72 抓) Interchanged with the last (爪). To scratch, to titillate (Kangxi: 搔也); to tear with the claws; to cajole. Williams' "gripe": In the (now) archaic sense of "seize" or "grasp". To seize, to grasp.

U+722A 蚤 (F2.42 爪) 手足甲也 (Page 29|n72 爪) Claws, talons, nails (Kangxi: 覆手曰爪, 手足甲也); an agent, a minion; the 87th radical ("claw"); _chau nga_ (zaau2 ngaa4 爪牙), claws and teeth; emissaries, assistants; _mai pan chau_ (maai5 baan6 zaau2 買辦爪), a comprador's market-man. 【爪】 (tsraewX) (_noun-like_) claw, nail 【狗爪邊】 (gau2 zaau2 bin1) The character component 犭. (Literally, "dog's paw beside".) 【狗爪頭】 (gau2 zaau2 tau4) The character component 爫. (Literally, "dog's paw atop".)
U+7B92 (F1.53 箒 _Chau_ (zau2)) 掃箒 (Page 25|n68 帚箒 _Chau_ (zau2); here normalised to _Chau_ (zaau2))

zau2.text: U+7B92 (F1.53 箒) 掃箒 (Page 25|n68 帚箒)

zaau2.text: U+8098 zau2 (per 《廣韻》陟柳切) (F1.53 肘 _Chau_ (zau2)) 臂節也又制肘 (Page 25|n68 肘 _Chau_ (zau2); here normalised to _Chau_ (zaau2)) ~~The fore-arm;~~ the elbow (Kangxi: 臂節也)~~, the wrist~~; to take by ~~the wrist~~ (Kangxi: 爲人捉其肘而留之亦曰肘); a quarter of an animal; _shau chau_ (sau2 zaau2 手肘), the elbow; _chau yat_ (zaau2 jat6 肘腋) ~~arms and sides~~ ``elbows and armpits``, _met._ near relatives; _ts'in chu chau_ (cin4 zyu1 zaau2 前豬肘), a shoulder of pork. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 刖 to 肘. Williams' "the wrist": This is a misreading of Kangxi 《徐曰》寸口手腕動脈處也, which is a very unilluminating attempt to explain 寸手寸口也 from 《說文》. 【肘】 (tsraewX) (_noun-like_) elbow

zau2.text: U+8098 (F1.53 肘) 臂節也又制肘 (Page 25|n68 肘) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 刖 to 肘.

zaau2.text: U+86A4 (Page 584 蚤 _Tso_ (zou2); here corrected to _Chau_ (zaau2)) ~~the rut or trace of a wheel.~~ Williams' "the rut or trace of a wheel": This is a misreading of Kangxi 謂幅入牙中者也, which is the commentary for the example 《周禮冬官考工記》眡其綆欲其蚤之正也. Admittedly, this example is an incredibly difficult passage to parse, and I too would have failed were it not for 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, specifically on the character 箄: ...按考工記注鄭司農云綆讀爲關東言餠之餠謂輪箄也 玄謂輪雖箄爪牙必正也...箄謂偏僻... The passage (眡其綆欲其蚤之正也) therefore reads, "Viewing its deviation, he wisheth that it be clawed correct". The probable meaning is that a crooked wheel should still have spokes going into (or "clawing") the hub perpendicularly. The nails (or to trim them) (Kangxi: 蚤讀爲爪, 謂除手足爪也). To install spokes of a wheel perpendicularly into the hub (Kangxi: 蚤當爲爪謂幅入牙中者也).

zou2.text: U+86A4 (F2.13 早 _Tsu_) 早晨又先也 (F2.13 蚤 _Tsu_) 同上 (Page 584 蚤) A flea (Kangxi: 嚙人跳蟲也); sand fleas, or those produced in sandy ground (Kangxi: 土乾則生蚤);

zaau3.text: U+3DF9 𦻐 zaau6 (per 《廣韻》直敎切) (Page 29|n72 𦻐; here normalised to 㷹) Blazing fire (Kangxi: 火急煎貌); to fry; to boil in fat; _yau chau_ (jau4 zaau3 油㷹) boiled in fat; _chau to ts'ui_ (zaau3 dou3 ceoi3 㷹到脆) fried crisp. The "deep-fry" sense is very dated; 炸 zaa3 is now preferred. Williams' 㷹到脆: Corrected _to_ (dou2) to _to_ (dou3).

U+7B0A zaau2 (alternative per 《廣韻》側絞切 for "strainer" sense) (F2.42 笊) 笊籬 (Page 29|n72 笊) A skimmer made of bamboo or iron wire (Kangxi: 笊篱竹器); nests in caves (Kangxi: 鳥居穴曰笊居樹曰巢); _chau li_ (zaau3 lei4-1 笊籬), a wire ladle.

U+7F69 (F2.42 罩) 嘲去聲取魚器也 (F2.42 䈇) 同上 (Page 29|n72 ~~𦋇~~``罩``) A net, or basket to catch mud fish by covering them (Kangxi: 捕魚器也); to catch or cover over; to shade; a shade, a cover, a protection from wind or dust; _tang chau_ (dang1 zaau3 燈罩) a lamp-globe; _kun chau_ (gun1 zaau3 棺罩) a network pall; _chau u_ (zaau3 jyu4-2 罩魚), to catch fish; _kai chau_ (gai1 zaau3 雞罩) a hen basket; _kiu chau_ (giu6-2 zaau3 轎罩) a rain cover on a sedan. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword 𦋇 to 罩. Williams' 轎罩: Corrected _kiu_ (giu6-5) to _kiu_ (giu6-2). 【罩】 (traewH) (_noun-like_) covering-trap

zaau6.text: U+6389 (Page 29|n72 櫂棹掉 _Chau_ (zaau3); here corrected to _Chau_ (zaau6))
U+6AC2 (F2.42 棹 _Chau_ (zaau3)) 進船之器 (F2.43 櫂 _Chau_ (zaau3)) 同上 (Page 29|n72 櫂棹掉 _Chau_ (zaau3); here corrected to _Chau_ (zaau6))

U+9A5F 騶 zau6 (per 《廣韻》鋤祐切) (F1.55 驟 _Chau_ (zau6)) 馳驟又疾速也 (Page 562 驟 _Tsau_ (zau6); here normalised to _Chau_ (zaau6)) A fleet horse (Kangxi: 馬疾步也); to run (Kangxi: 奔也); quick, fleet; pressing, urgent; frequently, repeatedly (Kangxi: 數也); _~~tsau~~ ``chau`` in i loi_ (zaau6 jin4 ji4 loi4 驟然而來), arrived very quickly; _pat ho ~~tsau~~ ``chau`` tak_ (bat1 ho2 zaau6 dak1 不可驟得) it can not be dune in an instant. 【驟】 (dzrjuwH) (_adjective-like_) speedy

zau6.text: U+9A5F (F1.55 驟 _Chau_ (zau6)) 馳驟又疾速也 (Page 562 驟) Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》鋤祐切.

zai1.text: U+5291 zai6 (per 《廣韻》在詣切 for verb(?) senses) (F1.28 劑) 調劑又劑量 (Page 548 劑) To pare, to cut even, to trim (Kangxi: 劑翦齊也, 齊截也); to equalize, to portion, to adjust (Kangxi: 分劑); to compound, as pills; a dose, a recipe (Kangxi: 藥劑); _yat tsai yeuk_ (jat1 zai1 joek6 一劑藥) a dose of medicine; _t'iu tsai_ (tiu4 zai1 調劑), to arrange amicably; _chat tsai_ (zat1 zai1 質劑), a check, a bond, made so as to cut in twain for evidence (Kangxi: 質劑謂兩書一札而別之也 etc.). It is not clear how to distribute the senses between level and departing tone. Kangxi has "pare even" and "bisected-contract" under the level tone, and the medicine-related(?) senses under the departing tone. 《廣韻》 has 券也 (for the "bisected-contract" sense) under the level tone, and 分劑 (a very ambiguous "portion(?)") under the departing tone. 【劑-departing】 (dzejH) (_verb-like_) to pare (even) 【劑-level】 (tsjwe) (_noun-like_) bisected-contract

U+64E0 zai3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子計切) (F1.28 擠) 排也推也 (Page 9|n52 擠 _Chai_ (zai1)) A colloquial word. To place, to put down, to put by; _chai mai_ (zai1 maai4 擠埋), put it aside; _chai pin ch'u_ (zai1 bin1 cyu3 擠邊處) where shall I put it? _chai pin ch'u to ho_ (zai1 bin1 cyu3 dou1 hou2 擠邊處都好), put ~~in~~ it down anywhere. (Page 548 擠 _Tsai_ (zai1)) To push (Kangxi: 排也一曰推也), to push over, to upset (Kangxi: 擠墜也); to press upon, to crowd; _tsai yung_ (zai1 jung2 擠擁), to crowd and press upon. 【擠】 (tsej, tsejH) (_verb-like_) to push 【擠】 (zai1) To put, or put down. 擠喺度 (zai1 hai2 dou6): "Put it over here." 【擠迫】 (zai1 bik1) Crowded. 人多擠迫 (jan4 do1 zai1 bik1): "Lots of people and crowded."
U+7A67 (F1.28 穧) 刈禾把數也束也 (Page 548 穧)

zai6.text: U+7A67 zai1 (per Fan Wan) zai3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子計切 and 子例切) (F1.28 穧 _Tsai_ (zai1)) 刈禾把數也束也 (Page 548 穧 _Tsai_ (zai1); here normalised to _Tsai_ (zai6)) To reap grain (Kangxi: 穫刈也); a handful of rice (Kangxi: 一曰撮也), as it is cut; to put it together ~~to bind~~ ``in preparation for binding`` into sheaves (Kangxi: 刈禾把數, 穧禾之鋪而未束者). Williams' "as it is cut": It is unclear whether 撮也 "pinch" applies to the grain (after harvesting) or to the crop (during harvest). 【穧】 (dzejH, tsejH, tsjejH) (_verb-like_) to reap

zai1.text: U+8CEB (F1.28 齎) 送也行道所用也 (F1.28 賫) 持送曰賫詔是也 (Page 548 賷賫)
U+8CF7 (F1.28 齎) 送也行道所用也 (F1.28 賫) 持送曰賫詔是也 (Page 548 賷賫)

U+8E8B zai3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子計切) (F1.28 躋) 登也升也 (F1.28 隮) 惡氣上升也 (Page 548 躋隮) To ascend, to go up, as stairs, to mount a steep hill (Kangxi 躋: 登也, 躋升也, 亦作隮); to reach; Williams does not provide separate entries for 躋 and 隮. Kangxi only has them interchangeable for the "ascend" sense. I have split things up accordingly, with duplication where appropriate. 【躋】 (tsej, tsejH) (_verb-like_) to ascend

U+96AE 躋 (for "ascend" sense) zai3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子計切) (F1.28 躋) 登也升也 (F1.28 隮) 惡氣上升也 (Page 548 躋隮) To ascend, to go up, as stairs, to mount a steep hill (Kangxi 隮: 登也); to rise, as vapor (Kangxi 隮: 氣也升也); a rainbow (Kangxi 隮: 隮虹也). Williams does not provide separate entries for 躋 and 隮. Kangxi only has them interchangeable for the "ascend" sense. I have split things up accordingly, with duplication where appropriate. To fall (Kangxi: 隮墜). 【隮-ascend】 (tsej, tsejH) (_verb-like_) to ascend (_noun-like_) rainbow

zai3.text: U+96AE To fall (Kangxi: 隮墜). 【隮-fall】 (tsejH) (_verb-like_) to fall

zai1.text: U+9F4E 賫 賷 zi1 (alternative per 《廣韻》即夷切) (F1.28 齎) 送也行道所用也 (F1.28 賫) 持送曰賫詔是也 (Page 548 賷賫; here normalised to 齎) To take or send a present; to present to, to offer; to dispatch, as documents; to send up to; _ts'an tsai_ (can1 zai1 親齎), to give to personally; _tsai sung_ (zai1 sung3 齎送) to send, as a present. (Page 548 齎) Interchanged with the preceding (賷, 賫) (Kangxi 賷: 齎或作賷). To take in both hands (Kangxi: 持也), to offer (Kangxi: 持遺人也); to give to (Kangxi: 付也, 送也); ~~to prepare~~ necessaries for a journey (Kangxi: 行道所用也); to supply, to ~~leave and~~ store up (Kangxi: 裝也); a sigh (Kangxi: 歎聲); _tsai tsau kun_ (zai1 zau3 gun1 齎奏官), a courier, a bearer of dispatches; _tsz' tsai_ (zi1 zai1 ？齎), to confer on; _fung tsai_ (fung2 zai1 捧齎), to hand up to. Williams' "leave": This is a misreading of Kangxi 遺也. 【齎】 (tsej, tsij) (_verb-like_) to gift (_interjection_) sigh

zi1.text: U+9F4E

zai1.text: U+9F4F (F1.28 虀) 薑蒜爲之又碎也和也 (Page 548 齏) A sort of leek (Kangxi: 𩐌也); to prepare and compound, to blend, to mix (Kangxi: 膾酢也 etc.), as tastes; to make dishes of vegetables as the poor have compounded, mixed; to compare, as opinions; _wo tsai_ (wo4 zai1 和齏), to blend, as when mixing the tastes of spices. Fan Wan entry: Note that the headword 虀 is not the same as Williams' headword 齏.

zai2.text: U+4ED4 zai2-4 (low first in reduplication 仔仔) (F1.31 仔) 皃子也又仔肩 (Page 548 仔) A colloquial word. A child, a son; a diminutive; a little thing, the smaller of two; _tsai nu_ (zai2 neoi5-2 仔女), sons and daughters, children; _kwai tsai_ (gwaai1 zai2 乖仔), a good boy; _tsai tai tsai shai kai_ (zai2 daai6 zai2 sai3 gaai3 仔大仔世界) when a son is old he must take care of himself; _sai man tsai_ (sai3 man1 zai2 細蚊仔), children, lads; _chu tsai_ (zyu1 zai2 豬仔), a pig; _kau tsai_ (gau2 zai2 狗仔), a puppy; _kung tsai_ (gung1 zai2 公仔), images, pictures, playthings; _sz' tsai_ (si6 zai2 事仔), a servant boy; _ts'au tsai ma_ (cau3 zai2 maa5-2 湊仔媽), a nurse. Williams' 豬仔: One sold into slave labour, as in 賣豬仔. 【仔】 (zai2) A kid. 生仔 (saang1 zai2): "Have kids." A son. 生仔 (saang1 zai2): "Have a son." 仔女 (zai2 neoi5-2): "Sons and daughters." 兩仔乸 (loeng5 zai2 naa2): "The two of them, son and mother." 兩仔爺 (loeng5 zai2 je4): "The two of them, son and father." 大個仔 (daai6 go3 zai2): "A big boy now." A guy, lad, etc. 佢條仔 (keoi5 tiu4 zai2): "Her guy" (i.e. boyfriend). 後生仔 (hau6 saang1 zai2): "Young lad." A diminutive suffix. 狗仔 (gau2 zai2): "Puppy." 矮仔 (ai2 zai2): "Shorty." 耳仔 (ji5 zai2): "Ears." 華仔 (waa4 zai2): "Wahsy." 雀仔 (zoek3 zai2): "Birdie."

zi2.text: U+4ED4 zi1 (alternative per 《廣韻》子之切 for "bear" sense) (F2.35 仔) 仔肩任也 (Page 612 仔) To bear, to carry (Kangxi: 克也任也); to undertake, as an officer; _tsz' sai_ (zi2 sai3 仔細) careful; discriminating; _tsz' kin chung yam_ (zi2 gin1 zung6 jam6 仔肩重任) able to sustain important offices. 【仔】 (tsi, tsiX) (_verb-like_) to bear

zai2.text: U+6FDF (Page 549 濟 _Tsai_ (zai3); here corrected to _Tsai_ (zai2)) Name of a river (Kangxi: 水名); _tsai tsai_ (zai2 zai2 濟濟) abundant; a concourse (Kangxi: 濟濟衆盛之貌). Stately (Kangxi: 一曰多威儀也). 【濟-rising】 (tsejX) (_adjective-like_) abundant (_adjective-like_) stately

zai3.text: U+6FDF (F1.32 濟) 周濟又通也 (Page 549 濟) to assist, to succor, to benefit (Kangxi: 益也); to furnish gratuitous aid (Kangxi: 賙救也); to relieve; to cross a stream (Kangxi: 渡也); to make successful, to further, to bring about, to complete (Kangxi: 成也); able, apt, clever (Fan Wan: 通也); _kau tsai_ (gau3 zai3 救濟) to save and relieve; _'m tsai sz'_ (m4 zai3 si6 唔濟事) he will not do, he is inefficient; _tsai mat sham sz'_ (zai3 mat1 sam6 si6 濟乜甚事) what is he good for? _t'ung chau kung tsai_ (tung4 zau1 gung6 zai3 同舟共濟) to cross a river in the same boat; _tsai ki_ (zai3 gei1 濟饑), to feed the hungry; _to tsai t'in ha_ (dou6 zai3 tin1 haa6 道濟天下) to reform the world; _chau tsai_ (zau1 zai3 周濟) to supply another's wants; 【濟-departing】 (tsejH) (_verb-like_) to cross (_verb-like_) to rescue, to relieve (_verb-like_) to aid, to benefit

U+5236 (F1.32 制) 節也禁也 (Page 9|n52 制) To adjust, to cut, to form (Kangxi: 裁也); to govern, to regulate, to rule (Kangxi: 正也御也檢也); to limit, to hinder, to prevent (Kangxi: 禁制也); a law, a rule, a regulation (Kangxi: 成法曰制); _tsoi chai_ (zoi6 zai3 在制) or _shau chai_ (sau2 zai3 守制) to wear mourning for parents; _yau tsit chai_ (jau5 zit3 zai3 有節制) I can do so much; _chai t'oi_ (zai3 toi4 制臺), a governor-general; _kam chai_ (gaam1 zai3 監制) to restrain, to set limits; _chai kan kom to_ (zai3 gan2 gam3 do1 制緊咁多), to limit one; _chai to_ (zai3 dou2 制倒), to tame, to subdue; _tsz' chai_ (zi6 zai3 自制) self-restraint; _chai shu_ (zai3 syu1 制書), an imperial dispatch (Kangxi: 天子之言曰制書); _chai to_ (zai3 dou6 制度) a pattern, laws; _kam chai_ (gam3 zai3 禁制) a prohibition; _chai ti_ (zai3 dei6 制地) the court; _sui chai_ (seoi3 zai3 歲制) a sexagenary; _chai fok_ (zai3 fok3 制縛) a close surveillance. 【制】 (tsyejH) (_verb-like_) to cut out, (_met._) to form (_noun-like_) institution, system (_verb-like_) to control, to regulate 【制】 (zai3) Willing (to do), accepting (of an outcome). 制唔制 (zai3 m4 zai3): "Are you willing or not?" 我唔制 (ngo5 m4 zai3): "I don't accept this!"

U+6662 晣 (F1.32 晣) 小星明也又音若 (Page 9|n52 晣; here normalised to 晢) Brightness of stars (Kangxi 晢: 星光也); _ming sing chai chai_ (ming4 sing1 zai3 zai3 明星晢晢) the twinkling lustre of the stars. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 䀿 to 晣. The alternative reading (又音若) does not make sense; possibly 折 _chit_ (zit3) was intended. 【晢-departing】 (tsyejH) (_adjective-like_) bright

zit3.text: U+6662 Bright (Kangxi: 昭晢明也). 【晢-entering】 (tsyet) (_adjective-like_) bright

zai3.text: U+6663 (F1.32 晣) 小星明也又音若 (Page 9|n52 晣) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 䀿 to 晣. The alternative reading (又音若) does not make sense; possibly 折 _chit_ (zit3) was intended.

U+796D (F1.32 祭) 祀也薦也 (Page 548 祭) To sacrifice, to offer slaughtered victims before the gods or penates, which are also usually cooked (Kangxi: 祭祀也); to bring an oblation, to approach before the gods (Kangxi: 人事至於神也); the time of worship; a sacrifice, an offering; _tsai shing_ (zai3 sing3 祭聖) to worship the sages; _tsai tsz'_ (zai3 zi6 祭祀) to present sacrifices; _tsai man_ (zai3 man4 祭文), a prayer; an elegy hung up during the 49 days of mourning; _pai lo tsai_ (baai2 lou6 zai3 擺路祭) to scatter or lay out offerings along the road --- as at the funerals of worthy men; _tsai yau_ (zai3 jau1 祭幽), to appease ghosts by oblations. 【祭】 (tsjejH) (_noun-like_) sacrifice
U+7A44 (F1.32 穄) 谷也似黍而小 (Page 548 穄) A sort of panicled millet which is not glutinous, and resembles wheat (Kangxi: 𪎭也, 穄似麥而不黏); it grows in Shensi (陝西). 【穄】 (tsjejH) (_noun-like_) panicled-millet

U+88FD 制 (F1.32 製) 造也作也 (Page 9|n52 制; here normalised to 製) to invent, to make; (Page 9|n52 製) Interchanged with the last (制). To invent, to make (Kangxi: 造也); to cut out clothes (Kangxi: 裁也); a fashion, a mode (Kangxi: 式也); to compound, as medicines; a pattern; a fur cloak (Kangxi: 裘也); _mi chai_ (mei5 zai3 美製) well made; _u chai_ (jyu6 zai3 御製) done by his Majesty; _ho chai fat_ (hou2 zai3 faat3 好製法) well compounded; _fat chai ye_ (faat3 zai3 je5 法製嘢), useless things; _chai tso_ (zai3 zou6 製造) to invent; _chai i sheung_ (zai3 ji1 soeng4 製衣裳), to cut out clothes; _hau chai_ (haau2 zai3 巧製) skillfully made. Williams' 製造: Now more "create" than "invent". 【製】 (tsyejH) (_verb-like_) to cut out, to make

U+969B (F1.32 際) 交會也又邊畔也 (Page 548 際) A limit, a border, a ~~region~~ ``perimeter`` (Kangxi: 方也, 邊也畔也), the line of junction of two ~~things~~ ``walls`` (Kangxi: 壁會也); the time or place where another begins (Kangxi: 際接也); to join (Kangxi: 合也, 會也)~~, to begin~~; the time of occurring, now, since; _sz' shang chi tsai_ (si2 sang1 zi1 zai3 死生之際) between life and death; _tsai tsip_ (zai3 zip3 際接) to receive, to blend, to meet; _ho tsai u_ (hou2 zai3 jyu6 好際遇) a good opportunity, a favorable juncture; _ts'z' tsai_ (ci2 zai3 此際) this time; _fung wan tsai ui_ (fung1 wan4 zai3 wui6 風雲際會) the winds and clouds have met; _met._ to receive a favor. 【際】 (tsjejH) (_noun-like_) juncture, connection, (_met._) time

U+973D 濟 zai6 (alternative per 《集韻》才詣切) (F1.32 霽) 雨止而日光也 (Page 549 濟) used for the next (霽) (Kangxi 濟: 與霽通亦止也); (Page 549 霽) The rain stopping (Kangxi: 雨止也), the clouds clearing away and the blue sky appearing (Kangxi: 晴也, 雨晴曰霽); _ts'ing tsai_ (cing4 zai3 晴霽) a fair sky. To cease, to stop (Kangxi: 霽止也). 【霽】 (tsejH) (_verb-like_) to cease (raining)

zai6.text: U+6EEF zai6-2 (rising second in vernacular 滯滯地) (F1.34 滯) 阻滯不通也 (Page 10|n53 滯) Water impeded; condensed, congealed (Kangxi: 凝也); to obstruct, to stop; indigestible, disagreeing, piled up; _shik chai_ (sik6 zai6 食滯) indigestible; _chai chu_ (zai6 zyu6 滯住) to restrain; impeded; an obstruction, stoppage; _ai chai_ (ai3 zai6 翳滯) fullness in the stomach; hindering; slow progress; _fui chai_ (fui3 zai6 晦滯) stupid looking; _pan chai_ (baan2 zai6 板滯) an old fogy; adhering to the old way; _cho chai_ (zo2 zai6 阻滯) hindered, let, detained; _chai hi_ (zai6 hei3 滯氣) a stoppage of secretions. Left-behind (Kangxi: 漏也). 【滯】 (drjejH) (_verb-like_) to congeal (_verb-like_) to stagnate (_adjective-like_) stagnant, (_met._) left-behind 【滯】 (zai6) Stagnated in the stomach, usually from eating too much. 滯住個胃 (zai6 zyu6 go3 wai6): "Feeling stagnant in the stomach." 食滯咗 (sik6 zai6 zo2): "Stuffed myself from eating." 飽飽滯滯 (baau2 baau2 zai6 zai6): "Full and stagnated."

zak1.text: U+4EC4 (F2.32 仄) 平仄傾仄 (Page 12|n55 仄) Inclined, slanting, leaning, oblique (Kangxi: 人處厂下有慮傾側之象); the name of the three deflected tones (上聲, 去聲, 入聲) (Kangxi: 平仄字聲也); _k'ing chak_ (king1 zak1 傾仄) tipped over, upset; _chak ~~sheng~~ ``shing``_ (zak1 sing1 仄聲) the oblique tones; _chak lau_ (zak1 lau6 仄陋) vile, erring. Williams' 仄聲: Corrected _sheng_ (seng1) to _shing_ (sing1). 【仄】 (tsrik) (_adjective-like_) oblique, slanting

U+5074 仄 (F2.32 側 _Ch'ak_ (cak1)) 傍邊曰側 (Page 12|n55 側) Side, the side (Kangxi: 旁也); on the side; to incline (Kangxi: 傾也), to bow down, to bend; lateral; perverted; mean, low (Kangxi: 卑隘也); rebellious, seditious (Kangxi: 叛黨曰反側); _chak shat_ (zak1 sat1 側室) a coucubine; _mo chap chak_ (mou4 zaap6 zak1 毋极側) don't turn it over; _chak pin_ (zak1 bin1 側邊), the side; _chak chak ti_ (zak1 zak1 dei6-2 側側地) a little one sided; _fan chak_ (faan2 zak1 反側) the rear, the back side; a rebellious cabal; _chak i_ (zak1 ji5 側耳), apply the ear; _yat chak_ (jat6 zak1 日側) afternoon; _shi ngo chak_ (si6 ngo5 zak1 侍我側) stand at my side. 【側】 (tsrik) (_noun-like_) side (_adjective-like_) oblique, slanting, (_met._) one-sided (_adjective-like_) lowly

U+5247 (F2.33 則) 法也準也卽也 (Page 550 則) A rule, a precept, a law, a regulation (Kangxi: 法也, 則常也); a principle (Kangxi: 天理不差曰則); a pattern (Kangxi: 則等畫物也), an example; a standard, a measure by which to test an act (Kangxi: 凡制度品式皆曰則); to conform to rule, to imitate, to walk by (Kangxi: 法其可法者曰則); an illative particle, denoting a result, a reason for, a cause for; wherefore, immediately, then; a conditional particle, after that, in that case (Kangxi: 助辭又然後之辭); therefore, next, consequently; _fat tsak_ (faat3 zak1 法則) a rule, a pattern; _chun tsak_ (zeon2 zak1 準則) the pattern, at the time, like the rule, as a carpenter's line is; _ho tsak_ (ho4 zak1 何則) how then? _tsak ko_ (zak1 go3 則個) in the court dialect is like _pa lok_ (baa6 lok3 罷咯) well, then; _e. g._ _shu tsui tsak ko_ (syu3 zeoi6 zak1 go3 恕罪則個) well, then I'll pardon the fault; _yau mat yau tsak_ (jau5 mat6 jau5 zak1 有物有則) both the substance and the immaterial principle; _in tsak_ (jin4 zak1 然則) if so, then; _yat tsak i hi, yat tsak i ku_ (jat1 zak1 ji5 hei2 jat1 zak1 ji5 geoi6 一則以喜一則以懼) to excite both our joy and fear; _tsak yat ya_ (zak1 jat1 jaa5 則一也), then they are alike; _sui tsak kom_ (seoi1 zak1 gam2 雖則敢), then I shall venture. Williams' 恕罪則個: Corrected _tsui_ to _tsui_. 【則】 (tsok) (_noun-like_) statute (_noun-like_) exemplar (_verb-like_) to emulate (_conjunction-like_) then, thus (_speech-assist_)

zik1.text: U+5247 This usage appears to be local to Hong Kong. 【則】 (zik1) An architectural drawing. 則師 (zik1 si1): "Architect." 入則 (jap6 zik1): "Submit the drawings for approval."

zak1.text: U+6603 稷 (F2.32 昃) 日斜也 (Page 12|n55 昃) The sun declining to the west (Kangxi: 日昃昳中也); the afternoon (Kangxi: 日中則昃); ~~to wane, as the moon;~~ _yat chak_ (jat6 zak1 日昃) day is declining. (Page 569 稷 _Tsik_ (zik1); here corrected to _Chak_ (zak1)) _ha ~~tsik~~ ``chak``_ (haa6 zak1 下稷) afternoon (Kangxi 稷: 與昃通, 稷昃也下稷謂晡時). 【昃】 (tsrik) (_adjective-like_) slanting west

zik1.text: U+7A37 (F1.74 稷) 黍稷社稷又舜臣名 (Page 569 稷) Panicled millet _(Milium)_, one of the five grains (Kangxi: 𪗉也五穀之長); quick, rapid (Kangxi: 疾也); the god of ~~agriculture~~ ``grains`` (Kangxi: 神名 etc.); _she tsik_ (se5 zik1 社稷) the Ceres of the Chinese; its altars have no roofs; _hau tsik_ (hau6 zik1 后稷) ~~a god of harvests~~; Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱⿻一火氺 to 黍. Williams' 社稷: Strictly two gods, one of soil (社) and one of grains (稷). Williams' 后稷: Not quite a god, though a legendary figure. Used in the titles of agricultural officials (Kangxi: 農官名曰后稷), after the legendary 后稷. 【稷】 (tsik) (_noun-like_) millet (_adjective-like_) prompt

zak1.text: U+9BFD May appear as either ⿰魚即 (+7 assumed here) or ⿰魚卽 (+9), depending on the font. (F2.33 鯽) 魚名 (Page 551 鯽)

zik1.text: U+9BFD May appear as either ⿰魚即 (+7 assumed here) or ⿰魚卽 (+9), depending on the font. The crucian carp.

zam1.text: U+659F (F2.36 斟) 斟酌 (Page 14|n57 斟) To pour from or into (Kangxi: 斟益也, 言斟酌而益之); to lade out, to lighten; to deliberate, to adjust (Kangxi: 斟取也, 計也); a ladle, a spoon (Kangxi: 勺也); _cham t'o lok_ (zam1 to5 lok3 斟妥咯) all well settled; _t'ung ni cham cham_ (tung4 nei5 zam1 zam1 同你斟斟), let us talk it over; _cham ch'a_ (zam1 caa4 斟茶), to pour out tea; _sai cham_ (sai3 zam1 細斟), to whisper; _cham cheuk_ (zam1 zoek3 斟酌) to consult about. Williams' 斟妥咯: Corrected _lok_ (lok6) to _lok_ (lok3). Williams' 細斟: Corrected _sai_ (sai6) to _sai_ (sai3). 【斟】 (tsyim) (_verb-like_) to pour, to deliberate 【斟茶遞水】 (zam1 caa4 dai6 seoi2) Menial work. (Literally, "pour tea and pass the water".)

U+6939 (Page 14|n57 椹) ~~An ax or bill;~~ ~~a staff.~~ Williams' "An ax or bill": This is a misreading of Kangxi 鐵椹斫木櫍也. Here, 鐵椹 is the anvil or block that is supporting the wood being chopped, not the axe that is chopping the wood.

U+7827 椹 碪 (F2.36 砧) 擣衣石 (F2.36 碪) 與砧同 (Page 14|n57 砧碪) A stone to beat clothes on (Kangxi: 擣衣石也); an anvil (Kangxi 椹: 鐵椹斫木櫍也); a block for lifting by athletes; _muk cham_ (muk6 zam1 木砧), a horse block; _chu pi cham_ (zyu1 bei2 zam1 豬髀砧), a fillet of pork; _cham pan_ (zam1 baan2 砧板), a cutting board, used by butchers. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰衣鳥 to 擣. Williams' 砧板: Now chopping boards in general (not necessarily a butcher's). 【砧板】 (zam1 baan2) A chopping board.
U+78AA (F2.36 砧) 擣衣石 (F2.36 碪) 與砧同 (Page 14|n57 砧碪) Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰衣鳥 to 擣.

U+7BB4 (F2.36 箴) 箴規箴誠有訓敕而救其失者曰箴 (Page 14|n57 箴) a caution, a check (Kangxi: 規戒也); pointed, urgent; to exhort, to rebuke, to urge to reform (Kangxi: 箴者諫誨之辭); _cham kw'ai_ (zam1 kwai1 箴規) admonition, rebukes; _cham ming_ (zam1 ming5 箴銘), cautionary maxims. Williams' 箴銘: Changed _ming_ (ming4) to common reading _ming_ (ming5). 【箴】 (tsyim) (_noun-like_) needle, (_met._) admonition

U+91DD 箴 鍼 (F2.36 針) 所以縫衣服也 (F2.36 鍼) 同上 (Page 14|n57 針鍼) A needle (Kangxi 鍼: 所以縫布帛之錐也, 或作箴亦作針); a cauterizing probe; to prick (Kangxi 鍼: 刺也); to use needles in acupuncture (Kangxi 針: 針灸法); leaves of the pine; _ch'un cham_ (cyun1 zam1 穿針), to thread a needle; _ku ch'ui cham_ (gu2 ceoi4 zam1 鼓錘針), a pin; _cham pi seuk t'it_ (zam1 bei6 soek3 tit3 針鼻削鐵) mean, stingy; _cham chi_ (zam1 zi2 針黹), to embroider; _hon shu cham_ (hon4 syu2 zam1 寒暑針) a thermometer; _ngan cham_ (ngaan5 zam1 眼針), a needle; _man cham_ (man1 zam1 蚊針), a musquito's sting. (Page 14|n57 箴) Used for the last (針). A probe, a surgeon's needle (Kangxi 箴: 古者以石爲箴所以刺病); to probe; . Williams' 鼓錘針: Corrected _ku_ (gu1) to _ku_ (gu2). Williams' 針鼻削鐵: Corrected _seuk_ (soek6) to _seuk_ (soek3) and corrected _t'it_ (tit6) to _t'it_ (tit3).
U+937C (F2.36 針) 所以縫衣服也 (F2.36 鍼) 同上 (Page 14|n57 針鍼)

zam2.text: U+600E (F2.37 怎 _Cham_) 怎麽猶何也語詞也 (Page 15|n58 怎 _Cham_) An interrogative word; what, how? _cham yeung_ (zam2 joeng6 怎樣) in what manner; _cham tik_ (zam2 dik1 怎的) what? _cham shang ta pan_ (zam2 sang1 daa2 baan6 怎生打扮) how would he look? _cham kom pat tai t'au_ (zam2 gam2 bat1 dai1 tau4 怎敢不低頭), how could I decline assent? (Page 551 怎 _Tsam_) What? _tsam mo_ (zam2 mo1 怎麼), what? _tsam kong_ (zam2 gong2 怎講), what does he say? used in the court dialect. ~~This is usually read _sham_ (sam2).~~

U+6795 (F2.37 枕) 臥以薦首者 (Page 14|n57 枕) A pillow (Kangxi: 臥薦首者); ~~a stake;~~ a crossbar in a carriage (Kangxi: 車後橫木也); _cham pin_ (zam2 bin1 枕邊), in bed, in private; _cham pun_ (zam2 bun6 枕伴) a bedfellow; _cham tsik nan on_ (zam2 zik6 naan4 on1 枕席難安), cares disturb his pillow, harassed; _i cham_ (ji5 zam2 耳枕), a support for the ear; _cham t'au_ (zam2 tau4 枕頭), a pillow; _cham kwat_ (zam2 gwat1 枕骨) the occiput; _on cham mo yau_ (on1 zam2 mou4 jau1 安枕無憂), to sleep quietly. Kangxi has the "crossbar" and "bone" senses under departing-tone 之賃切 _cham_ (zam3). I believe that, as nouns, they should appear here, under rising-tone 章荏切 _cham_ (zam2). A certain bone within the head of a fish (Kangxi: 魚腦中骨曰枕 etc.). 【枕-rising】 (tsyimX) (_noun-like_) pillow 【後尾枕】 (hau6 mei5 zam2) The occiput (back of the head/skull). 【枕住】 (zam2 zyu6) Persistently, continually. 枕住咳 (zam2 zyu6 kat1): "Persistently coughing."

zam3.text: U+6795 (Page 14|n57 枕 _Cham_ (zam2); _Cham_ (zam3) optional, here enforced) to pillow on, to lie near to; contiguous, adjacent, lying on; _sz' che seung cham tsik_ (si2 ze2 soeng1 zam3 zik6 死者相枕藉) the dead lay pillowed on each other; _~~huk~~ ``k'uk`` kwang i cham_ (kuk1 gwang1 ji4 zam3 曲肱而枕), pillowed on his arm; As a verb, ~~also~~ read _cham_ (zam3). Williams' 曲肱而枕: Changed _huk_ (huk1) to common reading _k'uk_ (kuk1). 【枕-departing】 (tsyimH) (_verb-like_) to pillow upon
U+6C81 (F2.38 沁) 水名又以物探水爲沁 (Page 551 沁)

zam6.text: U+20E7B 朕 (Page 15|n58 朕; here normalised to 𠹻) A colloquial word. A puff, as of smoke; _yat cham in_ (jat1 zam6 jin1 一𠹻煙), a puff of smoke; _yat cham fo fan_ (jat1 zam6 fo2 fan2 一𠹻火粉), an explosion of incense. 【𠹻】 (zam6) Classifier for smells and odours. 有𠹻味 (jau5 zam6 mei6): "There's a smell."

U+6715 (F2.38 朕) 天子自稱曰朕幽暗之文也又朕兆 (Page 15|n58 朕) Anciently used for I (Kangxi: 我也, 古者貴賤皆自稱朕); now the royal We, Ourself (Kangxi: 秦始皇...定爲至尊之稱 etc.); _cham ~~chiu~~ ``shiu``_ (zam6 siu6 朕兆) subtile, recondite. Williams' 朕兆: Changed _chiu_ (ziu6) to common reading _shiu_ (siu6). Whilst it can mean "subtle", it usually means "omen". An omen. 【朕】 (drimX) (_pronoun-like_) I

zan1.text: U+632F Generous (Kangxi: 仁厚也), honourable (Kangxi: 信厚也). Flourishing (Kangxi: 盛貌). 【振-level】 (tsyin) (_adjective-like_) generous, honourable (_adjective-like_) flourishing

zan3.text: U+632F zan3-2 (rising second in vernacular 振振地) (F1.84 振) 振奮振動振作止也收也藏也 (Page 17|n60 振) To shake, to move (Kangxi: 裂也又動也); to stir up, to agitate (Kangxi: 一曰奮也); to excite, to stimulate; to repair, to restore, to put in order (Kangxi: 整也); to rescue, to save (Kangxi: 舉救也, 拯也); to joggle; to flap, as wings ``in a flock`` (Kangxi: 鳥羣飛貌); to terrify; to uphold, to stop (Kangxi: 止也); to receive, to contain (Kangxi: 收也); _chan tung_ (zan3 dung6 振動) to shake, to set about, up and doing; _chan tsok_ (zan3 zok3 振作) diligent; to encourage; _tai chan ka shing_ (daai6 zan3 gaa1 sing1 大振家聲), made his family famous; _chan mai yat t'un_ (zan3 maai4 jat1 tyun4 振埋一團), cuddled up in a heap, as a shivering child; _chan i_ (zan3 ji1 振衣), to shake the dress. 【振-departing】 (tsyinH) (_verb-like_) to shake, (_met._) to flap (_verb-like_) to uplift, to rouse (_verb-like_) to open up (_verb-like_) to succour (_verb-like_) to organise (_verb-like_) to halt (_verb-like_) to contain, to consolidate (_adjective-like_) flocking (_preposition-like_) since

zan1.text: U+73CD 珎 (F1.79 珍) 寶也重也貴也又珍珠 (F1.79 ？) 同上 (F1.79 珎) 同上 (Page 16|n59 珍) Valuable, precious (Kangxi: 寶也, 貴也), excellent (Kangxi: 美也); important (Kangxi: 重也), rare (Kangxi: 奇也), noble; to prize; a delicacy (Kangxi: 食之美者亦曰珍); a rarity, a treasure; _chan chu_ (zan1 zyu1 珍珠), pearls; _chan po_ (zan1 bou2 珍寶), precious; _chan chung_ (zan1 zung6 珍重) to take care of, as the health; to esteem; _chan mi_ (zan1 mei6 珍味) delicious; _chan chu fan_ (zan1 zyu1 fan2 珍珠粉), pearl sago; _cheung sheung chan_ (zoeng2 soeng6 zan1 掌上珍), a pearl in the hand---a daughter. 【珍】 (trin) (_noun-like_) precious-thing (_adjective-like_) precious
U+771E (F1.79 眞) 實也正也精也純也 (Page 16|n59 眞真)

U+771F 眞 (F1.79 眞) 實也正也精也純也 (Page 16|n59 眞真) True, sincere, unfeigned, genuine, pure (Kangxi 眞: 不虛假也, 實也僞之反也); in fact, truly, indeed, no mistake; a likeness; actual, not secondary (Kangxi 眞: 正也); spiritual, subtle, pure, unmixed (Kangxi 眞: 神也淳也精也); _chan yan_ (zan1 jan4 真人), a sprite, a phantom of a man~~,~~``;`` _chan ko ts'z'_ (zan1 go3 ci5 真個似) in fact, it was like; _se chan_ (se2 zan1 寫真), to draw portraits (Kangxi 眞: 畫像曰寫眞); _chan hai_ (zan1 hai6 真係) it is so; _chan tsong ching fan_ (zan1 zong1 zing3 faan6-2 真贓正犯) this thing proves him a thief; _'m chi chan ka_ (m4 zi1 zan1 gaa2 唔知真假), I don't know whether it is so or not; _chan sam_ (zan1 sam1 真心), true-hearted; _chan fui hi_ (zan1 fui3 hei3 真晦氣) low-spirited; _chan ching_ (zan1 zing3 真正) truly, indeed; _chan kong_ (zan1 gong2 真講), it ts really so. Williams' 真人: Kangxi has 《說文》仙人變形而登天也 under 眞, i.e. "The immortal man changing form and ascending to Heaven". In modern non-Taoist usage this sense is now obsolete, and 真人 now simply means "real person". Williams' 真贓正犯: Corrected _fan_ (fan6) to _fan_ (faan6). 【真】 (tsyin) (_adjective-like_) true 【認真】 (jing6-2 zan1) (_degree_) Really, truly. 認真傻 (jing6-2 zan1 so4): "That's truly dumb." 【嚟真】 (lai4-2 zan1) To do for real (as opposed to a practice, test, or fake run). 今次嚟真㗎喇 (gam1 ci3 lai4-2 zan1 ga3 la3): "This time it's for real!"

zan2.text: U+8CD1 (Page 17|n60 賑) Read _chan_ (zan2); rich, affluent, wealthy (Kangxi: 富也). 【賑-rising】 (tsyinX) (_adjective-like_) rich

zan3.text: U+8CD1 振 (F1.84 賑) 賙濟也 (Page 17|n60 賑) A largess, a bounty; to give, to relieve, to supply (Kangxi: 贍也給也); _chan tsai_ (zan3 zai3 賑濟) to give to the poor; _chan ki_ (zan3 gei1 賑饑), to feed the hungry. Williams' "bounty": In the sense of an act of generosity. *Not* an abundance of wealth, which belongs under the rising-tone reading _chan_ (zan2). 【賑-departing】 (tsyinH) (_verb-like_) to succour

U+4FB2 zan1 (per 《廣韻》職鄰切 for "horsekeeper" sense) (Page 17|n60 侲) A boy (Kangxi: 童子也) ~~of ten or twelve years~~; _chan t'ung_ (zan3 tung4 侲童), boys who play at funerals (Kangxi: 挽歌亦用侲童). Kangxi has both the "boy" sense and the "horsekeeper" sense under departing-tone 之刃切 _chan_ (zan3), followed by the permissive level-tone variation 之人切音眞 _chan_ (zan1) for (presumably) the latter sense. In contrast, 《廣韻》 distinguishes the "horsekeeper" sense under departing-tone 章刃切 _chan_ (zan3) from the "boy ghost-expeller" sense under level-tone 職鄰切 _chan_ (zan1). Williams' "of ten or twelve years": This appears in Kangxi's example, 《後漢禮儀志》...選中黃門子弟十歲以上十二以下, but is not part of the definition. A groom, a horsekeeper (Kangxi: 燕齊閒謂養馬者爲侲).

U+5733 cau4 (per Kangxi 市流切 for "field-drainage" sense) zeon3 (per Fan Wan) (F2.54 圳 _Chun_ (zeon3)) 諄去聲水劣也 (Page 36* 圳 _Chun_ (zeon3); here normalised to _Chan_ (zan3)) A rapid in a stream; _~~chun~~ ``chan`` hau_ (zan3 hau2 圳口), a place where the water runs swiftly. Drainage in a field (Kangxi: 江楚閒田畔水溝謂之圳). Used in 深圳 (Shenzhen).

zeon3.text: U+5733 (F2.54 圳) 諄去聲水劣也 (Page 36* 圳)

zan3.text: U+5E2A zan1 (per 《廣韻》職鄰切) (Page 17|n60 帪) A bag to tie on a horse's head, in which his fodder is placed (Kangxi: 囊也馬兜也, 帪飤馬橐). The reading _chan_ (zan3) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 之刃切, explained 方言飤馬槖.
U+93AD (F1.84 鎭) 鎭守鎭壓鎭靜凡藩鎭山鎭皆取安重鎭壓之義 (Page 17|n60 鎭)

U+93AE 鎭 (F1.84 鎭) 鎭守鎭壓鎭靜凡藩鎭山鎭皆取安重鎭壓之義 (Page 17|n60 鎭; here normalised to 鎮) To press down (Kangxi 鎭: 博壓也, 重也壓也); to repress, to keep in subjection; to pacify, to protect (Kangxi 鎭: 安也); to overrule evil influences, as hills or pagodas do; to guard, to keep quiet; a mart, a trading-place; _chan fuk_ (zan3 fuk6 鎮服) to sway, to be lord paramount; _chan king_ (zan3 ging1 鎮驚), to quell one's fears; _chan t'oi_ (zan3 toi4 鎮臺), a protector; a brigadier; _chan shau pin kwan_ (zan3 sau2 bin1 gwaan1 鎮守邊關), to guard the frontier; _chan at_ (zan3 aat3 鎮壓) to repress disturbance; _tai chan t'au_ (daai6 zan3 tau4 大鎮頭), an entrepot or great trading-mart. Williams' 大鎮頭: Here "entrepot" is Williams' spelling. I know not enough about French to determine if it should instead be spelt "entrepot". 【鎮】 (trinH) (_verb-like_) to suppress, to subdue

U+9707 振 (F1.84 震) 卦名動也起也懼也威也 (Page 17|n60 震) Interchanged with the last (振). To quiver (Kangxi: 震動也); to shiver; the 51st diagram, belongs to thunder (Kangxi: 卦名); ~~to shake, as by~~ ``a crack of`` thunder (Kangxi: 劈歷振物者, 震雷也); to awe, to impress (Kangxi: 威也); thundering, terrible, as a sound; dire, awful; to intimidate (Kangxi: 震懼也); to ~~raise~~ ``start`` (Kangxi: 起也); to alarm; _ti chan_ (dei6 zan3 地震) an earthquake; _chan no_ (zan3 nou6 震怒) incensed; _wai chan_ (wai1 zan3 威震) to exhibit the majesty of China; _chan ~~keng~~ ``king``_ (zan3 ging1 震驚), terrified; _chan tung_ (zan3 dung6 震動) disturbed. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰亻瞿 to 懼. Williams' 震驚: Corrected _keng_ (geng1) to _king_ (ging1). 【震】 (tsyinH) (_noun-like_) thunder-crack (_verb-like_) to shake, to quake 【打冷震】 (daa2 laang5 zan3) To shiver. (_slang_) To have an orgasm.

zan6.text: U+9663 陳 zan6-2 (for vernacular "time", "later", and subjunctive marker senses) (F1.85 陣) 軍師行伍之列 (F1.85 陳) 同上 (Page 17|n60 陣陳) A rank, a file of soldiers, a battalion (Kangxi: 旅也, 列也); an army; to arrange, to place in ranks; a little while, passing, transitory; a battle; to drill; a mode of marshaling; _chan sheung_ (zan6 soeng6 陣上) in the fight; _yat chan chan lang_ (jat1 zan6 zan6 laang5 一陣陣冷), growing colder; _shai nu yan chan_ (sai2 neoi5 jan4-2 zan6 使女人陣) to send a woman to get it; _mai wan chan_ (mai4 wan4 zan6 迷魂陣) besotted in; silly; _pai chan_ (baai2 zan6 擺陣) to ~~post troops~~ ``set up a formation``; _ki t'iu muk chan_ (gei2 tiu4 muk6 zan6 幾條木陣) several wooden sleepers (or supports); _chan shai_ (zan6 sai3 陣勢) the ranks; courageous; _yat chan u_ (jat1 zan6 jyu5 一陣雨), a shower; _yat chan fung u_ (jat1 zan6 fung1 jyu5 一陣風雨), a squall; _yat chan ming, yat chan 'm ming_ (jat1 zan6 ming4 jat1 zan6 m4 ming4 一陣明一陣唔明), now you know, and then you do not know; _pai chan_ (baai6 zan6 敗陣) defeated the troops. Williams' 一陣陣冷: Changed _lang_ (lang5) to common reading _lang_ (laang5). Williams' 使女人陣: Literally, "employ the women's formation". Williams' 陣勢: Figuratively, the situation of the battle. 【陣】 (drinH) (_noun-like_) battle-array, (_met._) battlefield 【一陣-moment】 (jat1 zan6) A moment or short while. 等一陣啊 (dang2 jat1 zan6 a3): "Hold on a bit." 瞓咗一陣 (fan3 zo2 jat1 zan6): "Slept for a bit." 【一陣-later】 (jat1 zan6, jat1 zan6-2) Later, especially a short while later. 一陣先吖 (jat1 zan6 sin1 a1): "I'll see to it later" 一陣出去 (jat1 zan6-2 ceot1 heoi3): "I'll go out later." Marks the subjunctive mood ("what if" for an hypothetical future). 一陣遲到咪弊 (jat1 zan6-2 ci4 dou3 mai6 bai6): "If we arrive late, won't we be stuffed?" 【一陣間】 (jat1 zan6 gaan1) Synonym of 一陣 (both in the "moment" sense and in the "later" and subjunctive marker. 【陣-moment】 (zan6) Short for 一陣 in the "moment" sense. 等陣啊 (dang2 zan6 a3): "Hold on a bit." 瞓咗陣 (fan3 zo2 zan6): "Slept for a bit." 【陣-time】 (zan6, zan6-2) A time, or period of time, or era. 嗰陣 (go2 zan6-2): "At that time." 家陣 (gaa1 zan6-2): "Nowadays." 【陣間】 (zan6 gaan1) Short for 一陣間.

zang1.text: U+51C8 caang1 (per 《廣韻》楚耕切) zaang1 (longer syllable) (Page 20|n63 ~~净~~``凈``) Cold, shivering (Kangxi 凈: 冷貌).

U+589E 曾 (F2.29 增) 益也加也 (Page 556 曾) (...), to add (Kangxi 曾: 與增同); (Page 556 增) To add, to double, to increase (Kangxi: 益也); repeated, doubled (Kangxi: 一曰重也); _ka tsang_ (gaa1 zang1 加增), to augment; _tsang ka_ (zang1 gaa3 增價) to raise the price; _tsang mai_ (zang1 maai4 增埋), to give a higher price, to throw in the difference. Multitudinous (Kangxi: 衆也). 【增】 (tsong) (_verb-like_) to increase (_adjective-like_) multitudinous

U+618E (F2.29 憎) 惡也 (Page 556 憎) To hate, to dislike, to abominate (Kangxi: 惡也); _ho tsang_ (ho2 zang1 可憎), hateful; _tak yan tsang_ (dak1 jan4 zang1 得人憎), to be hated; _hat yan tsang_ (hat1 jan4 zang1 乞人憎), to act so as to be hated; _ngo ho tsang k'u_ (ngo5 hou2 zang1 keoi5 我好憎佢), I perfectly detest him; _tsang ho pat t'ung_ (zang1 hou3 bat1 tung4 憎好不同), capricious, now hating and then loving. 【憎】 (tsong) (_verb-like_) to hate 【乞人憎】 (hat1 jan4 zang1) Despicable. (Literally, "begging others to hate".) 真係乞人憎 (zan1 hai6 hat1 jan4 zang1): "That really is despicable."
U+775C To open the eyes, especially suddenly.

U+7E52 cang4 (per 《廣韻》疾陵切) (F2.29 繒) 魚網 (F2.29 罾) 同上 (Page 556 繒) A general name for common silken fabrics, as pongee, sarsnet, lutestring (Kangxi: 帛也). The reading _tsang_ (zang1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 咨騰切, explained 帛也. Fan Wan erroneously has 繒 explained 魚網 (綱 corrected to 網), and a strange character (like 曾, but with 丅 conjoined to the middle component between 八 above and 曰 below) explained 同上, here normalised to 罾. 【繒】 (dzing) (_noun-like_) silk

U+7F7E (F2.29 繒) 魚網 (F2.29 罾) 同上 (Page 556 罾) A lifting net, suspended by the corners (Kangxi: 罾魚網也形似仰繖蓋四維而舉之); _kwa tsang_ (gwaa3 zang1 掛罾), to let down the net; _ha tsang_ (haa1 zang1 蝦罾), a crab-net made of cloth; _au tsang_ (aau2 zang1 拗罾), to raise the net; _au tsang 'm ki tak t'ong lo shi_ (aau2 zang1 m4 gei3 dak1 tong3 lo4-1 si4 拗罾唔記得熨籮時), he don't like to remember when he dragged for shrimps. Fan Wan erroneously has 繒 explained 魚網 (綱 corrected to 網), and a strange character (like 曾, but with 丅 conjoined to the middle component between 八 above and 曰 below) explained 同上, here normalised to 罾. Williams' 拗罾 etc.: Corrected _au_ (au2) to _au_ (aau2).

zang3.text: U+7511 zang6 (common) (F2.31 甑) 炊飯之器又酒甑 (Page 556 甑) A boiler for distilling (Fan Wan: 酒甑), an alembic, a still made of pewter; a boiler for steaming (Kangxi: 甗也) rice; to steam, to distil; _lok tsang ching_ (lok6 zang3 zing1 落甑蒸), put it in the still and steam it. 【甑】 (tsingH) (_noun-like_) boiler

zang6.text: U+8D08 (F2.32 贈) 送也 (Page 557 贈) To present, to give to, to bestow on (Kangxi: 贈送也); to confer, as by the crown; to add to (Kangxi: 贈增也); _tsang sung_ (zang6 sung3 贈送) to give a present to; _ko tsang_ (gou3 zang6 誥贈) to honor an officer's parents for his merits; _tsang kun hi shik_ (zang6 gun1 hei3 sik1 贈觀氣色) to give a look at one's complexion---a sign of physiognomists; _chit lau tsang hang yan_ (zit3 lau5 zang6 hang4 jan4 折柳贈行人), to give a willow-twig at parting; _toi tsang_ (doi6 zang6 待贈) waiting for promotion, as expectant graduates do, at sixty. 【贈】 (dzongH) (_verb-like_) to gift

zap1.text: U+57F7 (F2.39 執) 持守也拘也捕也 (Page 22|n65 執) To take in the hand, to pick up, to lay hold of, to grasp; to apprehend, to catch (Kangxi: 捕也); to maintain, to retain (Kangxi: 守也持也); to stop up (Kangxi: 塞也); a handfull; _chap yau_ (zap1 jau5 執友), a father's old friend (Kangxi: 父之友曰執友); _shau chap_ (sau1 zap1 收執) to receive and keep as evidence; _chap tsz'_ (zap1 zi6 執字) to set types; _chap ch'au_ (zap1 cau4-2 執籌), to draw lots; _chap chung_ (zap1 zung1 執中), to keep a due medium; _chap au_ (zap1 aau3 執拗) a pertinacious dispute; _ku chap_ (gu3 zap1 固執) obstinate, set in his way; _kok chap yat ngai_ (gok3 zap1 jat1 ngai6 各執一藝) each follows his trade; _po chap_ (bou6 zap1 捕執) to arrest; _chap sz'_ (zap1 si6 執事) to manage business; an officer's retinue; _chap shau_ (zap1 sau2 執手), take by the hand. To manage, to judge (Kangxi: 處也). To carry out, to execute. 【執】 (tsyip) (_verb-like_) to take hold, to hold, (_met._) to carry out (_verb-like_) to seize (_noun-like_) close-held friend 【執】 (zap1) To pick up. 執返佢 (zap1 faan1 keoi5): "Pick it back up." 執垃圾 (zap1 laap6 saap3): "Pick up rubbish." 執狗屎 (zap1 gau2 si2): "Pick up dog shit." To tidy up, or make neater. 執房 (zap1 fong4-2): "Tidy up the room." 執正啲 (zap zeng1 di1): "Make it a bit neater." To pack. 執包袱 (zap1 baau1 fuk6): "Pack my bags". 執行李 (zap1 hang4 lei5): "Pack luggage." Short for 執笠. 【執錯字粒】 (zap1 co3 zi6 nap1) To make a typo. (Literally, "Pick up the wrong character block.") 【執骨】 (zap1 gwat1) To take up the bones from a grave (for a reburial). 【執笠】 (zap1 lap1) To shut up shop, especially after going bankrupt. 執咗笠 (zap1 zo2 lap1): "They've shut up shop." 就快執笠 (zau6 faai3 zap1 lap1): "About to go under." 【執媽】 (zap1 maa1) A midwife (strictly female). 【執碼-verb】 (zap1 maa5) To gather ingredients for a chef to cook. 【執碼-noun】 (zap1 maa5-2) The kitchen assistant who gather ingredients for the chefs to cook. 【執生】 (zap1 saang1) To improvise, or figure something out on the spot. 【執輸】 (zap1 syu1) To miss out. 【執仔】 (zap1 zai2) To midwive. 【執仔婆】 (zap1 zai2 po4-2) A midwife (strictly female).

U+6C41 (F2.39 汁) 液也 (Page 22|n65 汁) Gravy, juice (Kangxi: 液也); thickened liquor; slosh; the juices or essence of a thing got by expressing; sleety rain, wet snow (Kangxi: 雨雪雜下曰汁); _pan long chap_ (ban1 long4 zap1 檳榔汁) betel juice; _ts'oi chap_ (coi3 zap1 菜汁) liquor left after cooking vegetables; _chap 'm tak tam hap_ (zap1 m4 dak1 daam6 haap3 汁唔得啖呷) not had a mouthful of gravy to suck---I've had no benefit from it. Williams' "thickened liquor": From Kangxi's example 汁獻涗於醆酒, with commentary noting that 汁獻 is also spelt 汁莎. 【汁】 (tsyip) (_noun-like_) juice, (_met._) sleet

U+7E36 (F2.39 縶) 羈係也絆馬足曰縶 (F2.39 ？) 同上 (Page 22|n65 縶) To fetter a horse (Kangxi: 縶絆也) or cow ``with rope``; ~~fetters, shackles;~~ to bind, to secure (Kangxi: 相縶也連也); a cord (Kangxi: 縷也). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 紏 to 絆. The variant entry is 馬 with 山 replacing 灬. 【縶】 (trip) (_verb-like_) to tether

U+84FB 𠽃 執 (Page 557 𠽃; here normalised to 蓻) A colloquial word. A heap, a handful, a pile; a group, a knot; _chu mai yat tsap_ (zyu6 maai4 jat1 zap1 住埋一蓻) living in a single community; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` mai yat tsap_ (co5 maai4 jat1 zap1 坐埋一蓻) sitting together in a group; _so mai yat tsap_ (sou3 maai4 jat1 zap1 掃埋一蓻) sweep it up into a pile; _ap tsap_ (ap1 zap1 洽蓻) stingy, craven, slovenly; _yat tsap mai_ (jat1 zap1 mai5 一蓻米), as much rice as the fingers can grasp; _t'au tsap_ (tau1 zap1 偷蓻) to make a little money. Williams' 坐埋一蓻: Corrected _tso_ (zo6 去聲) to _ts'o_ (co5 上聲). 【蓻】 (zap1) A pinch or tuft. 一蓻鹽 (jat1 zap1 jim4): "One pinch of salt." 一蓻頭髮 (jat zap1 tau4 faat3): "One tuft of hair." It is debatable whether this is the same word as 執 _chap_ (zap1). In any case, Williams records the reading as _tsap_ (zap1), and Eitel (1877) prefers the character 蓻, which matches _tsap_ (zap1) in pronunciation: 《廣韻》子入切 蓻 草生多皃 《康熙字典》蓻 《唐韻》子入切音𥠋草生多貌 又《玉篇》茅芽也...

zat1.text: U+53EA (F1.86 只) 專也又語詞 (Page 23|n66 只)

zi2.text: U+53EA 止 (for "only" senses) 祇 (for "only" senses) 祗 (for "only" senses) 衹 (for "only" senses) 軹 (for particle sense) zat1 (per 《五音集韻》之日切) (F1.39 止) 息也定也但也 (F1.39 祗) 敬也但也又音其 (F1.39 只) 樂只君子 (F1.86 只 _Chat_ (zat1)) 專也又語詞 (Page 23|n66 只 _Chat_ (zat1)) Only, merely; but, however, yet; _chat ho kom che_ (zat1 hou2 gam2 ze3 只好噉嗻), this way alone; _chat tak_ (zat1 dak1 只得) no otherwise, only could. Also read _chi_ (zi2), in the same senses; and only _chi_ (zi2), when it is used as a final particle (Kangxi: 語已詞也). (Page 39|n82 止; here normalised to 只) only, but, however; _chi shi_ (zi2 si6 只是) only is; _'m chi kom to_ (m4 zi2 gam3 do1 唔只咁多), not so few; _chi tak kom to_ (zi2 dak1 gam3 do1 只得咁多), only so many; (Page 40|n83 祗; here normalised to 只) only, but merely, however, yet; _chi ts'z'_ (zi2 ci2 只此) only this; _chi yau yat yeung_ (zi2 jau5 jat1 joeng6 只有一樣) only one sort; (Page 152 祇; here normalised to 只 via 祗) Often confounded with and used for _chi_ (zi2) 祗. The sense in the example 樂只君子 given by Fan Wan for 只 is classical speech-assist. Williams' 只好噉啫: Corrected _che_ (ze2) to _che_ (ze3). In classical terms, the reading _chat_ (zat1) for 只 is vulgar. 《五音集韻》 has the reading 之日切, with the note 本之爾切本無質音今讀若質俗所音増. In modern terms, the reading _chat_ (zat1) is obsolete. 【只】 (tsyeX) (_speech-assist_)

zat1.text: U+67B3 (Page 23|n66 枳) A colloquial word. A cork, a plug, a stopple; to cork, to fasten a stopper; _tsau chat_ (zau2 zat1 酒枳) a bottle cork; _chat shat k'u_ (zat1 sat6 keoi5 枳實佢), cork it tightly. 【枳】 (zat1) A cork or plug. To block or stop using a cork or plug.

zi2.text: U+67B3 gei2 (per 《集韻》舉綺切 in 枳椇) (F1.39 枳) 枳棘又枳殼藥名 (Page 40|n83 枳) A hedge thorn, a spinous tree (Kangxi: 木高多刺); a fruit like a pumelo, with a thick skin (Kangxi: 木似橘); hurtful (Kangxi: 枳害也)~~, like thorns~~; _chi kik_ (zi2 gik1 枳棘) hedged up; thorny; _chi ku_ (zi2 geoi2 枳椇) the _Hovenia dulcis_; _chi hok_ (zi2 hok3 枳殼) ~~its seeds~~ ``the skin of the Chinese bitter orange``, used for medicine. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𣗥 to 棘. Williams' "hurtful": In the sense of "harmful". Williams' 枳椇: Corrected _ku_ (geoi6) to _ku_ (geoi2).

zat1.text: U+8CEA (F1.86 質) 樸也又文質 (F1.86 貭) 同質 (Page 23|n66 質) Substance, essence, nature, the material of (Kangxi: 質猶性也); plain, unadorned (Kangxi: 樸也, 質猶本也); sincere, honest (Kangxi: 實也, 質誠也); to cross-examine, to confront (Kangxi: 問也定也); to establish, to substantiate (Kangxi: 質體也); opposite to, to appear in presence of (Kangxi: 質猶對也); firm, as a texture; a disposition, a habit; a target (Kangxi: 的質也); _hi chat_ (hei3 zat1 氣質) the complexion; the elements of; _chat sun_ (zat1 seon3 質訊) to confront the accused and witnesses; _chat p'ok_ (zat1 pok3 質樸) plain; _chat ti_ (zat1 dei6-2 質地) temperament, constitution, talents; _pan chat_ (ban2 zat1 品質) the disposition. 【質-entering】 (tsyit) (_noun-like_) substance (_adjective-like_) simple (_verb-like_) to complete (_verb-like_) to affirm, to verify (_verb-like_) to front (_verb-like_) to interrogate (_noun-like_) target (_adverb-like_) directly 【質】 (zat1) To stuff or pack tightly. 質飽佢 (zat1 baau2 keoi5): "Stuff him full with food." 質到爆 (zat1 dou3 baau3): "Packed till bursting." To query or interrogate in an adversarial manner. 畀律師質 (bei2 leot6 si1 zat1): "Interrogated by a lawyer."

zi3.text: U+8CEA (F1.41 質) 留人爲當以要信也 (Page 9* 質) A pledge, a hostage; to pledge, to guaranty (Kangxi: 以物相贅); to pawn; _on chi_ (on3 zi3 按質) to give a pledge or guaranty; _chi fu_ (zi3 fu3 質庫) a pawnbroker's; _kau chi_ (gaau1 zi3 交質) to exchange hostages. 【質-departing】 (trijH) (_verb-like_) to pledge, to give hostage (_noun-like_) pledge, hostage

zat6.text: U+90C5 zat1 (per 《廣韻》之日切) (F1.41 郅 _Chi_ (zi3)) 世之隆也又極也 (Page 23|n66 郅 _Chat_ (zat1); here normalised to _Chat_ (zat6)) To ascend, to go up (Kangxi: 登也), as a hill; flourishing, as an age; very, exceedingly (Kangxi: 至也); name of a city (Kangxi: 郁郅古縣名); _chat lung_ (zat6 lung4 郅隆), very grand; _yuk chat_ (juk1 zat6 郁郅) an ancient name of Nganhwa hien (安化縣) in Kansuh (甘肅).

zat1.text: U+9455 (Page 23|n66 鑕) An ax, a hatchet (Kangxi: 斧也); an anvil or iron block (Kangxi: 鐵鑕砧, 斧鑕椹也), used by smiths and others.
U+96B2 (F1.86 隲) 陰隲品隲又定也成也陞也生也 (Page 23|n66 ~~？~~``騭``隲)

U+9A2D 隲 (F1.86 隲) 陰隲品隲又定也成也陞也生也 (Page 23|n66 ~~？~~``騭``隲) A stallion (Kangxi: 牡馬也, 牡曰騭); to mount; to ascend (Kangxi: 乗馬登山也); to raise, to promote (Kangxi: 騭陞也); to fix, to determine (Kangxi: 定也); _yam chat_ (jam1 zat1 陰騭) to think upon kindly, a secret benevolence; _tsik yam chat_ (zik1 jam1 zat1 積陰騭) to perform many good deeds. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 降 to 陞. 【騭】 (tsyit) (_verb-like_) to secure (_verb-like_) to raise

zat6.text: U+54A5 (F1.87 咥) 易履虎尾不咥人亨詩又音棄 (Page 24|n67 咥) Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿱𠫓耒 to 棄.

U+59EA zat6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.87 姪) 兄弟之子曰姪 (Page 24|n67 姪) A brother's children (Kangxi: 兄弟之女也, 兄弟之子); _chat tsz'_ (zat6 zi2 姪子) a nephew; _chat nu_ (zat6 neoi5-2 姪女), a ~~neice~~ ``niece``; _ngoi chat_ (ngoi6 zat6-2 外姪) a wife's nephews; _nin chat_ (nin4 zat6-2 年姪) sons of alumni of the same year; _kun chat_ (gyun3 zat6 眷姪) young relatives. 【姪婦】 (zat6 pou5) A brother's son's wife. 【姪仔】 (zat6 zai2) A brother's son.

U+5AC9 疾 (F1.87 嫉) 妬忌也 (Page 559 疾) used for the next (嫉); (Page 559 嫉) Envy, jealousy (Kangxi: 妎也); to envy, to dislike, as a competitor; _tsat in_ (zat6 jin4 嫉賢), to hate the worthy; _tsat to_ (zat6 dou3 嫉妒) jealousy.

U+6303 zat1 (per 《廣韻》陟栗切) (F1.87 挃) 刈禾聲也詩獲之挃挃 (Page 24|n67 挃) ~~To cut~~ ``The sound of cutting`` down grain (Kangxi: 穫禾聲, 挃挃穫也); to beat, to knock with the fist (Kangxi: 撞挃也). 【挃】 (trit) (_onomatopoeia_) slash

U+684E zat1 (per 《廣韻》之日切) (F1.87 桎) 桎梏刑械也又疑也 (Page 24|n67 桎) Fetters, gyves, stocks of wood or iron (Kangxi: 足械也); to manacle; to pierce (Kangxi: 桎刺也); a thing to clog wheels (Kangxi: 桎鎋所以制車者); _chat kuk_ (zat6 guk1 桎梏) fetters; _chat hat_ (zat6 hat6 桎鎋) a linchpin; _met._ a censor of manners, a guide of affairs; _wat wat chat chat_ (wat1 wat1 zat6 zat6 屈屈桎桎) cramped, cribbed, the room is scanty;---a colloquial phrase. 【桎】 (tsyit) (_noun-like_) fetter
