U+75BE (F1.87 疾) 病也患也惡也急也 (Page 559 疾) Sickness, a disease which comes suddenly on one (Kangxi: 病也一曰急也 etc.); ~~calamities,~~ afflictions (Kangxi: 患也); urgent, pressing, prompt, hasty (Kangxi: 速也, 急也); unlucky, infelicitous (Kangxi: 惡也); to hate, to dislike, to envy (Kangxi: 怨也); _tsat peng_ (zat6 beng6 疾病) disease, ailments; _man tsat_ (man6 zat6 問疾) to inquire after a sick person; _tsat mong_ (zat6 mong4 疾忙), testy, hasty, quick; _tsat shi_ (zat6 si6 疾視) to scowl at; _ping mo tsat in_ (bing6 mou4 zat6 jin4 並無疾言), still he never spoke boisterously; _tsat lui_ (zat6 leoi4 疾雷), a crashing clap of thunder; _tsat yan_ (zat6 jan4 疾人), a leper; _tsat ~~tsuk~~ ``ts'uk``_ (zat6 cuk1 疾速) impetuous, in haste; _ts'an tsat chi yan_ (caan4 zat6 zi1 jan4 殘疾之人), thoroughly diseased. Williams' 疾速: Corrected _tsuk_ (zuk1) to _ts'uk_ (cuk1). 【疾】 (dzit) (_noun-like_) sickness, malady (_adverb-like_) hastily (_verb-like_) to detest

U+7A92 zat1 (per 《廣韻》陟栗切) zat6-2 (rising second in vernacular 窒窒地) (F1.87 窒) 窒塞不通也 (Page 23|n66 窒) To close (Kangxi: 塞也), to stop up the mouth of; to hamper~~,~~``;`` the moon in opposition (Kangxi: 月在庚曰窒); _chat shau chat keuk_ (zat6 sau2 zat6 goek3 窒手窒腳) impeded, things not to one's liking; _chat chu k'u_ (zat6 zyu6 keoi5 窒住佢), stop it up tight; _chat ngoi_ (zat6 ngoi6 窒礙) difficulties. 【窒】 (trit, tet) (_verb-like_) to stop up 【窒】 (zat6) To interrupt, especially by making an adversarial counterpoint or challenge. 咪窒住嗮啦 (mai5 zat6 zyu6 saai3 laa1): "Stop interrupting!" 窒到口啞啞 (zat6 dou3 hau2 aa2 aa2): "Challenged till speechless." To chide, especially as an interruption. 窒我錯咗 (zat6 ngo5 co3 zo2): "Chided me for getting it wrong." To be intermittent and non-smooth in action or motion (stuttered speech, audio/video buffering, etc.). 口窒窒 (hau2 zat6 zat6): "Stammering." 影片窒吓窒吓 (jing2 pin3-2 zat6 haa5 zat6 haa5): "The video playback is stop-start."

U+84BA (F1.87 蒺) 蒺藜有三角 (Page 559 蒺) Gorse, furze (Kangxi: 蒺䔧藥草); a thorny shrub (Kangxi: 其刺傷人甚疾而利也), whose seeds are biting and spicy; _tsat lai_ (zat6 lai4 蒺藜), a very spinous plant, the seeds are used in diseases of the eye. 【蒺】 (dzit) Used in 蒺藜. 【蒺藜】 (dzit lej) caltrop
U+86ED zat1 (per 《廣韻》之日切) (F1.87 蛭) 虫名 (Page 24|n67 蛭) A leech (Kangxi: 水蛭 etc.); _shui chat_ (seoi2 zat6 水蛭) a blood-sucker, of which there are several kinds (Kangxi: ...有數種...得齧人腹中有血者...).

U+87C4 zaap6 (per 《廣韻》直立切) zik6 (common) zit3 (common) (F1.87 蟄) 蟄虫藏也又驚蟄節 (Page 24|n67 蟄) Retired into silence (Kangxi: 藏也, 蟄靜也); to burrow; insects burrowing, or becoming torpid in holes (Kangxi: 藏伏靜處也); _king chat_ (ging1 zat6 驚蟄) "excited insects," is the name of the fifth of the 24 terms; March 5th to 20th. To cluster (Kangxi: 和集也). 【蟄】 (drip) (_noun-like_) hibernation (_verb-like_) to cluster

zau1.text: U+4F9C (F1.49 侜) 載也 (Page 25|n68 侜) To conceal, to shade, to hide (Kangxi: 有廱蔽也); ~~something stretched for a shade.~~ Williams' "stretched": This is a misreading of Kangxi 侜張...誑也, to deceive. To deceive. 【侜】 (trjuw) (_verb-like_) to cover up, (_met._) to deceive

U+5468 May appear with the central vertical stroke not poking through (assumed here) or poking through and touching the component 口. 週 (F1.49 周) 周密周濟周備周遍又姓 (Page 25|n68 周) Universal, complete, entire (Kangxi: 備也); plenty, enough; to make a circuit, to environ (Kangxi: 周帀也); a famous dynasty, B. C. 1122--255 (Kangxi: 周代名); secret, fine, subtle; close together, fine (Kangxi: 密也); a curve, a bend (Kangxi: 曲也); to extend everywhere (Kangxi: 徧也); honest, trustworthy (Kangxi: 忠信也); to the end (Kangxi: 終也), entirely; _ho chau to_ (hou2 zau1 dou3 好周到) all things prepared; _chau chi_ (zau1 zi1 周知), ~~let~~ all know; _hu kin chau kung_ (heoi3 gin3 zau1 gung1 去見周公), he's gone to see Duke Chau---asleep; _chau mat_ (zau1 mat6 周密) numerous, close together; _chau pi_ (zau1 bei6 周備) everything ready; _chau wai_ (zau1 wai4 周圍), everywhere, all around; _chau sun_ (zau1 syun4 周旋) to circulate, to treat friends well; _chau ts'un_ (zau1 cyun4 周全), to bring a thing about, to remove ill feeling, to explain; _ho chau chi_ (hou2 zau1 zi3 好周置), it is excellent. (Page 25|n68 週) Often used for the last (周). _chau shi to hai kom_ (zau1 si4 dou1 hai6 gam2 周時都係噉), he is ever the same. Williams' 好周置: Corrected _chi_ (zi1) to _chi_ (zi3). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【周】 (tsyuw) (_noun-like_) cycle, circumference, (_met._) round-part (_verb-like_) to encompass (_adjective-like_) all-encompassing, all-round, (_met._) comprehensive, ultimate, utmost 【周身刀冇張利】 (zau1 san1 dou1 mou5 zoeng1 lei6) Jack of all trades, master of none. (Literally, "Knives all-about the person, but not one sharp".) 【周時】 (zau1 si4) All the time.

U+557E (F1.50 啾) 啾喞小聲也 (Page 560 啾) The wail of infants (Kangxi: 小兒聲也); the hum of insects; _tsau tsik_ (zau1 zik1 啾唧) the cry of small children; _tsau tsau_ (zau1 zau1 啾啾), the chattering of birds (Kangxi: 啾啾鳴聲), squeaking of rats.

U+5DDE (F1.49 州) 九州 (Page 25|n68 州) A district, less than a prefecture; (...), a great division; _chi chau_ (zi1 zau1 知州), the magistrate over a _chau_ (zau 州) district; _chau t'ung_ (zau1 tung4 州同), his deputy; _kau chau_ (gau2 zau1 九州), the empire; the whole world; _tuk keuk chau_ (duk6 goek3 zau1 獨腳州), a district without subdivisions. 【州】 (tsyuw) (_noun-like_) province, district
U+68F7 (Page 560 棷) A kind of wood suitable for fuel (Kangxi: 木薪也); ~~a surname.~~

zau2.text: U+68F7 (Page 560 棷 _Tsau_ (zau1); here corrected to _Chau_ (zau2)) ~~it is used for making~~ watchmen's alarms (Kangxi: 夜戒守有所擊也);

zau1.text: U+6D32 州 (F1.49 洲) 水中可居之也 (Page 25|n68 州) a continent, (...); an island (Kangxi 州: 水中可居曰州); (Page 25|n68 洲) A spot encircled by streams, or in the water, as an islet (Kangxi: 水渚也, 水中可居曰洲); a place where men collect, a village (Kangxi: 聚也人及鳥物所聚息之處也); _ngo u chau_ (ngou4 jyu4 zau1 鰲魚洲), Whampoa I.; _ch'eung chau_ (coeng4 zau1 長洲), Bamboo town; _kau chau_ (gau2 zau1 九洲), the Nine Is. near Macao; _ts'ing chau_ (cing1 zau1 青洲), Green I. off Macao; _sha chau_ (saa1 zau1 沙洲), sands, a sand-bank. In modern usage, 州 is used exclusively in the "administrative-division" sense, and never for the "island/continent" sense, which is always spelt 洲. 【洲】 (tsyuw) (_noun-like_) islet

U+7DC5 zeoi3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子句切) (F1.50 緅) 靑赤色 (Page 560 緅) Silk first dyed red thrice, and then twice dipped in black (Kangxi: 五入爲緅, 染纁者三入而成又再染以黑則爲緅); a dark red-like puce (Kangxi: 帛靑赤色也). 【緅】 (tsuw, tsrjuw, tsjuH) (_adjective-like_) puce

U+821F (F1.49 舟) 船也 (Page 25|n68 舟) A boat, a vessel, a craft of any sort (Kangxi: 船也); to go in a boat; to transport, to carry in a vessel (Kangxi: 載也); the 137th radical ("boat") of characters relating to ships; _p'in chau_ (pin1 zau1 扁舟), a punt; _chau ts'z'_ (zau1 ci3 舟次) on board ship. 【舟】 (tsyuw) (_noun-like_) boat

U+8ACF (F1.50 諏) 咨事曰諏 (Page 560 諏) To consult with (Kangxi: 聚謀也), to take advice, to inquire into matters of government publicly (Kangxi: 問政事也); to choose; _tsau kat_ (zau1 gat1 諏吉) to select a lucky day; _kung tsau k'i ching_ (gung6 zau1 kei4 zing3 共諏其正) to jointly discuss the right of the matter; _tsz' tsau_ (zi1 zau1 咨諏), to take counsel. 【諏】 (tsju) (_verb-like_) to inquire, to consult

U+8B05 cau1 (per 《廣韻》楚鳩切) (Page 560 謅) To whisper (Kangxi: 小言私授謂之謅), to speak into one's ear; to speak in a low tone. The reading zau1 is not unattested, though strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_; 《集韻》 has the reading 甾尤切, explained 小言私授謂之謅. To make up, to fabricate.

U+8B78 侜 (F1.49 譸) 書經譸張爲幻 (Page 25|n68 譸) ~~Hurried, bustling,~~ to impose upon, to deceive (Kangxi: 譸張誑也); _chau cheung wai wan_ (zau1 zoeng1 wai4 waan6 譸張為幻) to delude with false appearances. Williams' 譸張為幻: Corrected _wai_ (wai1) to _wai_ (wai4). 【譸】 (trjuw) (_verb-like_) to deceive

U+8CD9 周 (F1.49 賙) 賑恤也 (Page 25|n68 周; here normalised to 賙) to supply, to provide, to assist; (Page 25|n68 賙) To bestow charity (Kangxi: 給也贍也); _chau tsai_ (zau1 zai3 賙濟) to relieve the indigent, as a society or government; _chau sut_ (zau1 seot1 賙恤) beneficent. 【賙】 (tsyuw) (_verb-like_) to assist

U+8F08 (F1.49 輈) 車轅 (Page 25|n68 輈) A ~~frame in front~~ ``pole or shaft`` of a carriage (Kangxi: 車轅也 etc.) ~~near which the driver sits~~; ~~a sort of dashboard.~~ 【輈】 (trjuw) (_noun-like_) carriage-pole
U+8F16 (F1.49 輖) 匣也 (Page 25|n68 輖) A heavily laden cart, which is low in front (Kangxi: 低也一曰車前重也); a heavy load (Kangxi: 重載也); heavy (Kangxi: 重也).

U+9031 (F1.49 週) 回也也 (Page 25|n68 週) To revolve, to circulate (Kangxi: 迴也); ~~to inform people;~~ a year; _chau nin wong seung_ (zau1 nin4 wong6 soeng3 週年旺相) may the whole year be lucky; _tui chau_ (deoi3 zau1 對週), a return of the year; _yat chau_ (jat1 zau1 一週), a circuit, a year;

U+90F0 鄹 陬 (F1.50 鄹) 孔子故鄕 (Page 560 鄹; here normalised to 郰) The district in Lu (魯) where Confucius was born (Kangxi 郰: 魯下邑孔子之鄕, 又通作鄹, 又通作陬), now in Yenchau fu (兗州府) in Shantung (山東); it is not far from the preceding (鄒). (Page 560 陬; here normalised to 郰) _tsau yap_ (zau1 jap1 郰邑) the residence of Confucius, and interchanged with the preceding (鄹). Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》側鳩切.

U+9112 鄹 (F1.50 鄒) 國名又姓 (Page 560 鄒) A district in the southwest of Shantung (山東) on the Yellow River; an ancient city in Lu (魯) (Kangxi: 魯縣 etc.), where Mencius was born. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》側鳩切. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).
U+9139 (F1.50 鄹) 孔子故鄕 (Page 560 鄹) Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》側鳩切.

U+966C (F1.50 陬) 遐陬 (Page 560 陬) An angle or corner (Kangxi: 阪隅也) ~~of a city wall~~, where it is retired; ~~a bastion;~~ a nook, a corner; to live together (Kangxi: 聚居也); abashed, ashamed (Kangxi: 卑陬愧恧貌); _mang tsau_ (maang6 zau1 孟陬), the first month (Kangxi: 正月爲孟陬); _om tsau_ (am3 zau1 暗陬), a dark corner; _shing tsau_ (sing4 zau1 城陬), the corner of a wall; Williams' 孟陬: Changed _mang_ (mang6) to common reading _mang_ (maang6).

U+9A36 (F1.50 騶) 騶虞仁獸 (Page 560 騶) A fabulous ~~feline~~ beast, called _tsau u_ (zau1 jyu4 騶虞), emblematic of kindness to animals and gentleness (Kangxi: 騶虞義獸至德所感則見馬之屬 etc.); a ~~royal~~ groom or hostler (Kangxi: 廐御也). Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》側鳩切. Williams' "royal groom or hostler": Not necessarily royal, but often so. 【騶】 (tsrjuw) (_noun-like_) horse-keeper

U+9BEB cau2 (per 《集韻》此苟切 for "little (man)" sense) cau4 (per 《廣韻》徂鉤切) zau6 (per 《廣韻》仕垢切) (F1.50 魚名又鯫生小人也) (Page 560 鯫) Minnows, white fish (Kangxi: 白魚也, 一曰小魚) which skip over the water; _met._ a simpleton (Kangxi: 小人貌也); _tsau shang_ (zau1 sang1 鯫生), a foolish fellow. The reading _tsau_ (zau1) is not unattested, at least for the "small-fish" sense; 《集韻》 has the reading 將侯切, explained 小魚也.

zau2.text: U+8D70 zau3 (alternative per 《廣韻》則候切) (F1.53 走) 奔走趨也 (Page 561 走) To go (Kangxi: 走猶去也); to sail; to travel; to run, to hasten, to quicken one's steps (Kangxi: 趨也); to flee, to run off, to clear out; the 156th radical ("run") of characters relating to going; _tsau kwo ki shang_ (zau2 gwo3 gei2 saang2 走過幾省), he has been over several provinces; _tsau 'm lat_ (zau2 m4 lat1 走唔甩) could not get away; _tsau pak ti_ (zau2 baak6 dei6 走白地) all cleared out; _tsau tak man_ (zau2 dak1 maan6 走得慢) she sails slowly; _tsau hu_ (zau2 heoi3 走去) to run off; _tsau shai_ (zau2 sai2 走使), a servant; _tau kai tsau kau_ (dau3 gai1 zau2 gau2 鬥雞走狗), to fight cocks and race dogs; _tsau sz'_ (zau2 si1 走私) to smuggle; _tsau lau_ (zau2 lau6 走漏) to disclose, to let out, as a secret; _tsau shui_ (zau2 seoi2 走水), to voyage; _tsau lok_ (zau2 lok3 走咯) I'm going; _tsau lo_ (zau2 lo1 走囉), clear out! _tsau tak fai ts'ui_ (zau2 dak1 faai3 ceoi3 走得快脆) he runs very fast. Kangxi follows 《廣韻》 in putting the 《釋名》 definition under the departing tone. Williams' 走漏: In the vernacular register, 走漏 means to omit by mistake. 【走】 (tsuwX) (_verb-like_) to run, to run away (_verb-like_) to go, to leave Cantonese retains the classical distinction between 走 "run" and 行 "walk". 【走】 (zau2) To run. 周圍走 (zau1 wai4 zau2): "Running all over the place." 未學行先學走 (mei6 hok6 haang4 sin1 hok6 zau2): "Having not yet learnt to walk, first learning to run." To flee. 走啊 (zau2 aa3): "Run for your lives!" 咪走 (mai5 zau2): "Don't you run off." To leave. 我哋走 (ngo5 dei6 zau2): "We shall leave." 臨走 (lam4 zau2): "Just before leaving." 走啦 (zau2 laa1): "Leave!" To miss (fail to utilise). 走咗班車 (zau2 zo2 baan1 ce1): "Missed the train." 走咗隻東 (zau2 zo2 zek3 dung1): "Missed the east wind tile." To leave out or omit from food, as a preference. 走蔥 (zau2 cung1): "Without spring-onion." 走青 (zau2 ceng1): "Without any green garnishes." 走色 (zau2 sik1): "Without colour" (i.e. without adding beef stock to noodle soup). 菜心全走 (coi3 sam1 cyun4 zau2): "Choy sum all without" (i.e. without adding oyster sauce or brisket juice). 【走雞】 (zau2 gai1) To miss an opportunity (especially a tile in mahjong). 走咗雞 (zau2 zo2 gai1): "I missed it." 【走去】 (zau2 heoi3) To proceed to. 走去偷嘢 (zau2 heoi3 tau1 je5): "He's gone and stolen stuff." 走去吸毒 (zau2 heoi3 kap1 duk6): "He's gone and taken drugs." 【走油】 (zau2 jau4-2) To wok-fry briefly in a moderate amount of oil (so as to give sheen to meat or seafood). 【走甩】 (zau2 lat1) To get away, to successfully escape. 走唔甩 (zau2 m4 lat1): "Couldn't get away." 走甩咗 (zau2 lat1 zo2): "He got away." 【走路】 (zau2 lou6-2) To run away, to flee.

U+9152 (F1.53 酒) 儀狄所造 (Page 561 酒) Spirits (Kangxi: 就也所以就人性之善惡); distilled liquor made from rice (Kangxi: 酒酉也釀之米麴酉澤久而味美也); it is applied also to beer, wine, and other liquors; given to drink; to redden; _shiu tsau_ (siu1 zau2 燒酒), whiskey, samshoo; _tsau tsik_ (zau2 zik6 酒席) a banquet; _wong tsau_ (wong4 zau2 黃酒), sweet spirits; _mo tsau leung_ (mou4 zau2 loeng6 無酒量) I have no ability to drink; _fat tsau fung_ (faat3 zau2 fung1 發酒瘋), irascible from drink; _tsau fung keuk_ (zau2 fung1 goek3 酒瘋腳) gouty; _tsau fong_ (zau2 fong4 酒房), a groggery, a wine room or cellar; _hung tsau_ (hung4 zau2 紅酒), claret; _pak tsau_ (baak6 zau2 白酒), sherry; _~~p'e~~ ``pe`` tsau_ (be1 zau2 啤酒), beer; _shui tsau_ (seoi2 zau2 水酒), weak spirits; _t'in tsau_ (tin1 zau2 天酒), genial dews; _shik tsau_ (sik1 zau2 色酒), mulled spirits; _tsui tsau lo_ (zeoi3 zau2 lou2 醉酒佬), a drunkard; _tsau shan kong_ (zau2 san4 gong3 酒神降) to like the flavor of wine; _tsam tsau_ (zam3 zau2 浸酒), to steep things in spirits; _ko leung tsau_ (gou1 loeng4 zau2 高粱酒), spirits from millet, a pungent sort; _mi tsau_ (mei5 zau2 美酒), good spirits; _tsau pui_ (zau2 bui1 酒杯), a wine-glass. Williams' 啤酒: Corrected _p'e_ (pe2) to _pe_ (be1). 【酒】 (tsjuwX) (_noun-like_) wine

zau3.text: U+50FD zau6 (per 《廣韻》鋤祐切) (Page 560 僽 _Tsau_ (zau1); here corrected to _Tsau_ (zau3)) To scold, to rail at (Kangxi: 僝僽惡言詈也); abusive, scurrilous language; _shan tsau_ (saan4 zau3 僝僽), to say outrageous things, to irritate.
U+546A (F1.55 咒) 符咒又咒咀 (F1.55 呪) 同上 (Page 26|n69 咒呪) Fan Wan entry: Corrected duplicate headword 咒 to 呪.

U+5492 呪 祝 (F1.55 咒) 符咒又咒咀 (F1.55 呪) 同上 (Page 26|n69 咒呪) To imprecate, to rail at, to curse (Kangxi 呪: 呪詛也); to pray or recite spells; an incantation, an imprecation, a charm, a spell; ~~a prayer;~~ _nim chau_ (nim6 zau3 念咒) to recite ~~prayers~~ ``an incantation``; _~~to~~ ``t'o`` chau_ (tou2 zau3 禱咒) to curse; _fu chau_ (fu4 zau3 符咒) to prepare and bless charms; _chau cho_ (zau3 zo3 咒詛) or _chau ma_ (zau3 maa6 咒罵) to curse. Fan Wan entry: Corrected duplicate headword 咒 to 呪. Williams' 禱咒: Changed _to_ (dou2) to common reading _t'o_ (tou2). 【祝-departing】 (tsyuwH) (_verb-like_) to curse

U+54AE (F1.55 咮) 鳥噌曰咮 (Page 26|n69 咮) The bill of birds (Kangxi: 鳥口也, 咮喙也); to peck with the bill~~, the twittering of birds~~. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 味 to 咮. 【咮-departing】 (trjuwH) (_noun-like_) beak

zyu1.text: U+54AE (F1.43 咮) 多言貌 (Page 26|n69 咮) Also read _chu_ (zyu1). Loquacious, chattering (Kangxi: 讋咮多言貌), as birds; ~~_chu u_ (zyu1 jyu4 咮？), talkative~~. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 㹸 to 貌.

zau3.text: U+594F (F1.55 奏) 奏本 (F1.55 𩱛) 與奏同 (Page 561 奏) To report to the throne, to memorialize the emperor, to tell the sovereign, to cause him to hear or know (Kangxi: 人臣言事章疏曰奏); songs or tunes (Kangxi: 樂一更端曰奏); to play music; _tsau pun_ (zau3 bun2 奏本), a memorial; _tsau sheung_ (zau3 soeng5 奏上) to send up a report; _p'in tsau_ (pin3 zau3 片奏) a slip handed up; _tsau lau_ (zau3 lau4 奏留), to report than an officer is detained; _tsau ngok_ (zau3 ngok6 奏樂) to play music, to strike up the band; _tsit tsau_ (zit3 zau3 節奏) to mark the time; _min tsau_ (min6 zau3 面奏) to state to the emperor; _chun tsau_ (zeon2 zau3 准奏) to assent to a memorial. Fan Wan entry: The alternative form 𩱛 (i.e. 糭 but with 鬲 in place of 米) is likely an error. To put forward, to offer (Kangxi: 進也薦也). 【奏】 (tsuwH) (_verb-like_) to put forward (_verb-like_) to perform music (_verb-like_) to report

U+665D (F1.55 晝) 日中曰晝 (Page 26|n69 晝) A day, daytime, daylight (Kangxi: 日之出入與夜爲界); _chau ye pat hit_ (zau3 je6 bat1 hit3 晝夜不歇) going incessantly, never resting; _pak chau_ (baak6 zau3 白晝) broad daylight, openly; _sheung chau_ (soeng6 zau3 上晝) forenoon; _ha chau_ (haa6 zau3 下晝) afternoon. 【晝】 (trjuwH) (_noun-like_) daytime

U+7503 (F1.50 甃 _Ts'au_ (cau1)) 井甓又結砌也 (Page 563 甃 _Ts'au_ (cau1); here corrected to _Chau_ (zau3)) The brick lining of a well (Kangxi: 井甓也); to lay, as bricks (Kangxi: 結砌也); to pile up (Kangxi: 以塼壘井修井之壞謂之甃); to finish off a well. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 并 to 井. To adorn, to decorate (Kangxi: 甃猶飾也). 【甃】 (tsrjuwH) (_noun-like_) brickwork

U+76BA 縐 (F1.55 皺) 面皮蹙也 (Page 561 縐) used for the next (皺); (Page 561 皺) The skin wrinkled (Kangxi: 面皺也) from age; frowning (Kangxi: 眉攢也); _tsau mi t'au_ (zau3 mei4 tau4 皺眉頭), to scowl; _min p'i tsau_ (min6 pei4 zau3 面皮皺) a wrinkled face. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》側救切. 【皺】 (tsrjuwH) (_noun-like_) wrinkle

zuk1.text: U+795D (F1.63 祝) 禱祝又祝壽又祝融火神也 (Page 35* 祝) To pray, to supplicate; to recite prayers; _chuk t'o_ (zuk1 tou2 祝禱), to pray; _chuk man_ (zuk1 man4 祝文), a prayer; _chuk fuk_ (zuk1 fuk1 祝福) to bless; _chuk ko_ (zuk1 gou3 祝告) to implore; _tuk chuk kun_ (duk6 zuk1 gun1 讀祝官), one who reads the prayers; _chuk shau_ (zuk1 sau6 祝壽) to congratulate on one's birthday; _miu chuk_ (miu6 zuk1 廟祝) an acolyth in the temples. A person that makes offerings/sacrifices to tablets/deities (Kangxi: 贊主人饗神者, 祝祭主贊詞者, etc.). To wish (well). To cut off (Kangxi: 斷也). A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

zyu3.text: U+795D To apply medicine topically (Kangxi: 祝讀如注病之注謂附著藥也).

zau3.text: U+7E10 (F1.55 縐) 縮而不光曰縐 (Page 561 縐) Crape (Kangxi: 絺之細者); rumpled, crenulated, crinkled, crisp, frizzled; to corrugate, to wrinkle; _tsau sha_ (zau3 saa1 縐紗), crape; _sin tsau_ (sin3 zau3 線縐) camlet; _tsau mai_ (zau3 maai4 縐埋), puckered, shriveled up, wrinkled; _tsau ch'au_ (zau3 cau4 縐綢), wrinkled silk; _tsau sha t'o_ (zau3 saa1 tou5 縐紗肚), a capacious paunch; _u tsau_ (wu4 zau3 湖縐) Nanking crape; _tsau man_ (zau3 man4 縐紋), marks of wrinkles. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chau_ per 《廣韻》側救切. 【縐】 (tsrjuwH) (_noun-like_) wrinkled-cloth, fine-cloth

zau6.text: U+5191 (F1.55 胄) 后裔也又甲胄 (Page 26|n69 冑) Often confounded with the last (胄). A helmet, a morion (Kangxi: 兜鍪); _kap chau_ (gaap3 zau6 甲冑) helmets and cuirasses. For the visually similar character meaning "posterity", see 胄 zau6. Fan Wan entry: No distinction is made between 冑 (under ⼌) and 胄 (under ⾁). 【冑】 (drjuwH) (_noun-like_) helm

U+5B99 (F1.55 宙) 宇宙古往今來曰宙 (Page 26|n69 宙) ~~To contain, as the earth does;~~ including all ages, from ancient times till now (Kangxi: 往古來今謂之宙). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 行 to 往. 【宙】 (drjuwH) (_noun-like_) time

U+5C31 (F1.56 就) 卽也迎也從也 (Page 561 就) The place to which things tend or culminate at (Kangxi: 就高也从京从尤, 尤異也尤高人所就之處); to go towards, to approach, to draw near; to accompany, accommodated to, to follow (Kangxi: 從也); to complete (Kangxi: 成也), to make a circuit (Kangxi: 帀也), to meet (Kangxi: 迎也); completed, finished (Kangxi: 終也); to come near in point of time; an adverb, then, immediately, just, just now, presently, forthwith; a conjunction, then, if, as if; able to do (Kangxi: 能也)~~, willing~~; _tsau ching loi_ (zau6 zing3 loi4 就正來), he has just come; _tsau ching loi kwo_ (zau6 zing3 loi4 gwo3 就正來過) he's just come and gone; _tsau kom lok_ (zau6 gam2 lok3 就噉咯) let it go, that'll do; _kom tsau ho_ (gam2 zau6 hou2 噉就好), this is right; _tsau ka hu_ (zau6 gaa1 heoi3 就家去) then I'll go; he's about going; _tsau sin_ (zau6 sin1 就先), just a little while ago, just now; _tsau chi hu_ (zau6 zi3 heoi3 就至去) he's just gone; _tsau shau_ (zau6 sau2 就手), came to one's hands, as profits; _tsau ~~loi~~ ``lai``_ (zau6 lai4 就嚟), presently, in a little; coming forthwith; _tung shing sai tsau_ (dung1 sing4 sai1 zau6 東成西就) the east is done and the west finished, everything is correctly done; _tsau hai ko tik_ (zau6 hai6 go3 dik1 就係個滴) it is just that; _tsau ti ts'u ts'oi_ (zau6 dei6 ceoi2 coi4 就地取材), this will serve the purpose; he will do for the occasion; _tsau mai ni_ (zau6 maai4 nei5 就埋你), to bear with, to humor one; _tsau kom yeung le_ (zau6 gam2 joeng6-2 le3 就噉樣唎) well, let it pass; _yan keng tsau ying_ (jan5 geng2 zau6 jing4 引頸就刑), to offer his neck to the axe. Williams' 就正, 就家, 就先: All dated. Williams' 就嚟: Corrected _loi_ (loi4) to _lai_ (lai4). 【就】 (dzjuwH) (_verb-like_) to go unto, to proceed (unto) (_verb-like_) to nigh (_verb-like_) to assume (_verb-like_) to accomplish (_noun-like_) coil 【就】 (zau6) Almost, soon. 就得 (zau6 dak1): "Almost done." 就到 (zau6 dou3): "Almost there." To accommodate, to adjust for another's convenience. 就佢夜放 (zau6 keoi5 je6 fong3): "Adjust the time to accommodate his late clock-off." 唔使就我 (m4 sai2 zau6 ngo5): "No need to adjust for me." 【就快】 (zau6 faai3) Soon. 【就噉】 (zau6 gam2) To leave it at that, to not do any more. 【就嚟】 (zau6 lai4) Soon.
U+5CAB jau4 (alternative per 《集韻》夷周切) (F1.55 岫) 山有石穴曰岫 (Page 562 岫) A den, a cave among the hills (Kangxi: 山穴也); a glen, a ravine, gorge, or defile. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 油 to 岫.

U+7C40 籒 (Page 26|n69 籒; here normalised to 籀) The framer of the seal character, _Chau t'ai sz'_ (zau6 taai3 si2 籀太史) (Kangxi 籒: 史籒周宣王太史名造大篆); _chau man_ (zau6 man4 籀文), the seal character (Kangxi 籒: 籒文者史籒所作也 etc.). Williams' 籀太史: Corrected _t'ai_ (taai6) to _t'ai_ (taai3). To read (Kangxi: 讀書也).

U+7D02 (F1.55 紂) 商王之號 (Page 26|n69 紂) A crupper (Kangxi: 馬緧也); a silk trace; _Chau wong_ (zau6 wong4 紂王), the infamous king who ended the Shang (商) dynasty (Kangxi: 帝辛天下謂之紂). Williams' "ended": Not the best wording. He was the final king of that dynasty; he did not bring that dynasty to an end (unless you count his committing suicide, which would be semantics). 【紂】 (drjuwX) (_noun-like_) crupper

U+80C4 (F1.55 胄) 后裔也又甲胄 (Page 26|n69 胄) Descendants, posterity (Kangxi: 後也); _chau tsz'_ (zau6 zi2 胄子) the eldest son (Kangxi: 長也 etc.); _~~hau~~ ``chau`` yui_ (zau6 jeoi6 胄裔) posterity; _shai chau_ (sai3 zau6 世胄) generations. For the visually similar character meaning "helmet", see 冑 zau6. Fan Wan entry: No distinction is made between 冑 (under ⼌) and 胄 (under ⾁). Williams' 胄裔: Corrected _hau_ (hau3) to _chau_ (zau6). 【胄】 (drjuwH) (_adjective-like_) eldest (_noun-like_) posterity

U+8896 (F1.56 袖) 衫袖 (Page 562 袖) The sleeve (Kangxi: 袂也, 袖由也手所由出入也); to put into the sleeve, to receive in the hand (Kangxi: 亦言受也以受手也); _tsau hau_ (zau6 hau2 袖口), a cuff; _tsau shau_ (zau6 sau2 袖手), to hide the hands in the cuffs; _tsau t'au_ (zau6 tau4 袖頭), official cuffs shaped like a horsehoof; _ling tsau_ (ling5 zau6 領袖) a chief, one born to command; _t'o tsau_ (tou3 zau6 套袖) sleeve-covers; _tsau shau p'ong kun_ (zau6 sau2 pong4 gun1 袖手旁觀), to fold the hands and look on. 【袖】 (zjuwH) (_noun-like_) sleeve

U+914E (F1.55 酎) 新熱之酒 (Page 26|n69 酎) ~~New, ripe spirits;~~ pure strong spirits, thrice distilled (Kangxi: 三重醇酒也); _~~to~~ chau kam_ (~~do6~~ zau6 gam1 ~~？~~酎金), wine money paid the heir apparent---an old usage. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𤍠 to 熱. Williams' "New, ripe spirits": This is a misreading of Kangxi 酒之新熟重者爲酎. Here, 酒之新熟 is merely contextual. Williams' ？酎金: _to_ (do6) is probably a typo for what was intended to be _tso_ (zo6), which is the result of misreading Kangxi's example 《史記平準書》列侯坐酎金. Here, 坐酎金 is not a term; 坐 means "convicted", so that 列侯坐酎金 means "The feudal-lords were convicted in the wine money case". 【酎】 (drjuwH) (_noun-like_) strong-drink
U+9DF2 (Page 562 鷲) A large accipitrine bird; perhaps a harpy eagle, or a whitish headed condor (Kangxi: 鷲悍多力...卽白鵰也).

ze1.text: U+556B May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. 【啫】 (ze1) A final particle of limitation, especially used defensively. 少少啫 (siu2 siu2 ze1): "It's just a little!" 講笑啫 (gong2 siu3 ze1): "Just kidding!" 【啫喱】 (ze1 lei2) Jelly.

ze3.text: U+556B May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (Page 31|n74 啫)

ze1.text: U+55DF (F2.83 嗟) 嘆詞又痛惜也 (F2.83 𭋦) 同上 (Page 563 嗟) To sigh, to lament (Kangxi: 嗟歎也); painful regrets (Kangxi: 一曰痛惜也); an interjection of admiration (Kangxi: 咨也); ah! alas! (Kangxi: 嗟佐也言不足以盡意故發此聲以自佐也) _tse t'an_ (ze1 taan3 嗟嘆) to sigh; _tse loi chi shik_ (ze1 loi4 zi1 sik6 嗟來之食) ~~to groan and beg one's bread~~ ``food given in contempt``. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰亻肯 to 惜. Williams' 嗟來之食: Not to beg for food, but food given in contempt. Per 《禮檀弓》, the phrase 嗟來食 "alas come eat" was the utterance of the wealthy man 黔敖 as he handed out food and drink to the famished. 【嗟】 (tsjae) (_interjection_) alas

ze2.text: U+59D0 ze2-1 (vernacular; high second in reduplication 姐姐) ze2-4 (low endearing suffix after a name; low first in reduplication 姐姐) (F2.84 姐) 姊也 (Page 563 姐) An elder sister (Kangxi: 今俗弟呼女兄曰姐); a miss; formerly applied to one's mother (Kangxi: 蜀人呼母曰姐); proud, saucy (Kangxi: 一曰慢也); _tse mui_ (ze2 mui6-2 姐妹) sisters; _tai tse_ (daai6 ze2-1 大姐), my elder sister; also, a marriageable maidservant; _siu tse_ (siu2 ze2 小姐), a young miss; _ling tse_ (ling6 ze2 令姐), your sister; _tse fu_ (ze2 fu1 姐夫), a sister's husband; _a tse_ (aa3 ze2 阿姐), sister; also a term for a father's concubine. Williams' 姐妹: The preferred term for "sisters" in Cantonese is 姊妹. Williams' 阿姐: This is the meaning when 姐 is read in the original rising tone. 【阿姐】 (aa3 ze2-1) Generic address for an adult woman when the speaker is not a child. (Can be respectful, neutral, or impolite depending on the context.) 【家姐】 (gaa1 ze2-1) Older sister (or older female paternal cousin). 【細姐】 (sai3 ze2) Father's concubine (presumed to be younger than his wife).

ze1.text: U+7F5D (F2.83 罝) 取兔網 (Page 563 罝) A net with which hares are caught (Kangxi: 兔罔也); a net for beasts (Kangxi: 獸罟曰罝); to stop the way, as a net does. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿱罓且 to 罝. 【罝】 (tsjae) (_noun-like_) rabbit-net

U+906E (F2.83 遮) 掩也 (Page 30|n73 遮) To cover, to screen, to vail, to conceal (Kangxi: 蔽也); to shade; to intercept (Kangxi: 斷也, 攔也); a parasol, a shade; _che im_ (ze1 jim2 遮掩), to secrete, to screen; _che k'oi_ (ze1 koi3 遮蓋) to cloak, to cover; _che sau_ (ze1 sau1 遮羞), to conceal the face (as females); _u che_ (jyu5 ze1 雨遮), an umbrella; _'m che tak chu_ (m4 ze1 dak1 zyu6 唔遮得住) it ~~wont~~ ``won't`` cover us, it can not be concealed; _che shik_ (ze1 sik1 遮飾) to disguise, to dupe, throw dust in his eyes; _che lan_ (ze1 laan4 遮攔), to fend off, to ward off; _che yat t'au_ (ze1 jat6 tau4-2 遮日頭), keep the sun off; _che mun_ (ze1 mun4 遮瞞), to hide from. 【遮】 (tsyae) (_verb-like_) to block (_verb-like_) to cover 【遮】 (ze1) An umbrella. 開遮 (hoi1 ze1): "Open an umbrella." 擔遮 (daam1 ze1): "Hold up an umbrella." 收遮 (sau1 ze1): "Close up an umbrella." 縮骨遮 (suk1 gwat1 ze1): "Retractable-frame umbrella" (i.e. a pocket or foldable umbrella).

ze2.text: U+8005 May appear dotless (assumed here) or dotted, depending on the font. (F2.84 者) 語助詞指人指物之稱 (Page 30|n73 者) A ~~relative or~~ demonstrative pronoun (Kangxi: 此也...今俗多用這字); ~~it, this,~~ that, he, who, what; following verbs, it forms a noun, as _hang che_ (hang4 ze2 行者), a walker, he who is walking; after ~~nouns~~ ``adjectives``, indicates a class, as _u che_ (jyu4 ze2 愚者), the foolish; _sz' che_ (si2 ze2 死者), the dead; _mo ts'ing che_ (mou4 cing4 ze2 無情者), people who are without affection; or as put in the abstract, as _sing che_ (sing3 ze2 性者), nature; _shing che_ (sing4 ze2 誠者), truth; placed between two nouns or clauses, it puts them in apposition, as _tak che, pun ya_ (dak1 ze2 bun2 jaa5 德者本也), virtue, that is the root; _yan che ngau shan_ (jan4 ze2 ngaau6 saan1 仁者樂山) the humane man delights in hills; it is often a mere pause to arrest the attention; _ho wai che_ (ho4 wai4 ze2 何為者), what are you doing? _chung ya che t'in ha chi tai pun ya_ (zung1 jaa5 ze2 tin1 haa6 zi1 daai6 bun2 jaa5 中也者天下之大本也), the medium is everywhere the chief point; _sik che_ (sik1 ze2 昔者), formerly; _wak che_ (waak6 ze2 或者), perhaps. Williams' 死者: Corrected _sz'_ (si3) to _sz'_ (si2). Williams' 中也者天下之大本也: Changed _ha_ (haa5) to modern _ha_ (haa6). 【者】 (tsyaeX) (_pronoun-like_) he that, that which, one, -er (_speech-assist_)

U+8D6D May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (F2.84 赭) 赤色 (Page 31|n74 赭) Ochre color (Kangxi: 赤土也); a carnation or reddish brown (Kangxi: 赤也); _che shek_ (ze2 sek6 赭石) ochre; _che i_ (ze2 ji1 赭衣), reddish garments worn by felons (Kangxi: 赭衣罪人服). Williams' "carnation": In the (now) archaic sense of reddish skin colour. 【赭】 (tsyaeX) (_noun-like_) red-ochre

U+9019 ze3 (per Fan Wan) ze5 (common) (F2.84 這 _Che_ (ze3)) 是也此也這裏言此處也這事言此事也 (Page 31|n74 這 _Che_ (ze3); here normalised to _Che_ (ze2)) A demonstrative pronoun, this, that; _che ~~li~~ ``lu``_ (ze2 leoi5 這裡), here; _che yeung_ (ze2 joeng6 這樣) thus, so; _che tang_ (ze2 dang2 這等), such, this sort; _che shi_ (ze2 si4 這時), now. Williams' 這裡: Changed _li_ (lei5) to common reading _lu_ (leoi5). The character 這 is only attested in the Middle Chinese rime books under the reading 魚變切 _in_ (jin6), explained 迎也. Kangxi has 毛晃曰凡稱此箇爲者箇俗多改用這字這乃迎也, which proposes that 這 is a vulgar substitute for 者 when used as a demonstrative "this".

ze3.text: U+9019 (F2.84 這) 是也此也這裏言此處也這事言此事也 (Page 31|n74 這)

U+501F zek3 (alternative per 《廣韻》資昔切) (F2.84 借) 假借借貸又推獎也 (Page 564 借) To assist (Kangxi: 助也); to lend, to borrow (Kangxi: 假也貸也); to ask for, to beg of; to make a pretext of; to pretend, to assume, to suppose; if, for example, supposing, assuming (Kangxi: 設辭); to use for illustration; to praise (Kangxi: 推獎也); _tse loi_ (ze3 loi4 借來), to borrow; _tse hu_ (ze3 heoi3 借去) to lend; _tse ngan_ (ze3 ngan4-2 借銀), to ask for money; _tse tan_ (ze3 daan1 借單), a borrower's note; _tse sham_ (ze3 saam1 借衫), to borrow a jacket; _seung tse_ (soeng1 ze3 相借) to borrow of; _tse u_ (ze3 jyu4 借如), supposing that; _tse shai wai ok_ (ze3 sai3 wai4 ok3 借勢為惡) to avail of power to do wickedly; _tse cheuk ngo sz'_ (ze3 zoek6 ngo5 si6 借着我事) he's got my business, he's stole my thunder; _tse ko kwong_ (ze3 go3 gwong1 借個光), to use another's name, _e. g._ to get a letter of introduction; _tse me tik_ (ze3 me2 dik1 借歪滴) please step aside a little; _tse lo hang_ (ze3 lou6 haang4 借路行), to beg a way through; _tse i_ (ze3 ji3 借意) metaphorically; _tse lai kong_ (ze3 lai4 gong2 借嚟講), said it for illustration, meant nothing by it; _tse k'it_ (ze3 kit3 借揭) to get a loan on interest. Williams' 借着我事: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). Williams' 借路行: Changed _hang_ (hang4) to vernacular reading _hang_ (haang4). 【借】 (tsjaeH, tsjek) (_verb-like_) to borrow, to lend

U+55FB 啫 (Page 31|n74 啫; here normalised to 嗻) A colloquial particle. Just now; a short time, for the nonce, shortly; a form of the subjunctive; _tang ha che_ (dang2 haa5 ze3 等吓嗻) stop a little; _fan lai che_ (faan1 lai4 ze3 返嚟嗻) when I come back; _kom yeung che_ (gam2 joeng6-2 ze3 噉樣嗻) will this do? _ta kwo che_ (daa2 gwo3 ze3 打過嗻) let me whip you; _tang ngo hu tso mat ye che_ (dang2 ngo5 heoi3 zou6 mat1 je5 ze3 等我去做乜嘢嗻) let me do that first.
U+67D8 樜 (F2.84 柘) 桑柘木名 (Page 31|n74 柘) a tree whose leaves are fed to silk worms (Kangxi: 桑屬, 柘葉飼蠶爲絲). 【柘】 (tsyaeH) (_noun-like_) melonberry-tree, melonberry
U+6A1C (F2.84 蔗) 甘蔗 (F2.84 樜) 同上 (Page 31|n74 蔗樜)

U+8517 柘 樜 (F2.84 蔗) 甘蔗 (F2.84 樜) 同上 (Page 31|n74 蔗樜) The sugar cane (Kangxi 蔗: 甘蔗也); _it che_ (jit6 ze3 熱蔗) boiled sugar-cane; _t'it che_ (tit3 ze3 鐵蔗) dark cane; _pak che_ (baak6 ze3 白蔗) white cane; _chuk che_ (zuk1 ze3 竹蔗) or _long che_ (long5 ze3 ？蔗) small cane; _che kai_ (ze3 gai1 蔗雞), suckers of the cane; _che liu_ (ze3 liu4 蔗寮), sugaring sheds; _cha che_ (zaa3 ze3 搾蔗) to express cane juice; _to tam che_ (dou3 daam6 ze3 倒啖蔗) he eats his cane backwards, he has the best still coming. (Page 31|n74 柘) Sometimes used for the above (蔗) (Kangxi 柘: 諸柘一曰甘蔗); Williams' 倒啖蔗: Corrected _to_ (dou2) to _to_ (dou3). 【蔗】 (tsyaeH) (_noun-like_) sugarcane
U+9DD3 (F2.84 鷓) 鷓鴣鳥名 (Page 31|n74 鷓) The partridge (Kangxi: 鳥名鷓鴣越雉也); it also includes francolins and grouse; _che ku_ (ze3 gu1 鷓鴣), the common partridge.

ze6.text: U+69AD (F2.85 榭) 臺榭有屋曰臺無屋曰榭 (Page 564 榭) A terrace having ~~trees around~~ ``a timber structure on`` it (Kangxi: 臺有屋也, 土高曰臺有木曰榭)~~, an arbor~~; a place for storing musical instruments (Kangxi: 榭者所以藏樂器); a high lookout, a keep, a turret; a military school (Kangxi: 講武之屋曰榭); a fencing-room; _ut tse_ (jyut6 ze6 月榭) ~~an open arbor~~. Williams' "having trees", "an arbor": This is a misreading of Kangxi 有木曰榭. Here, 木 means "timber" in the sense of construction, rather than "tree". 【榭】 (zjaeH) (_noun-like_) raised-pavilion
U+707A (F2.85 灺) (Page 564 灺) The snuff of a candle (Kangxi: 燭㶳); the expiring wick of a lamp. Fan Wan entry: No definition is given; only the headword is present.

U+8B1D (F2.85 謝) 辭也絶也又退也彫落也居官致仕曰謝政感德相酧曰謝恩 (Page 564 謝) To decline, to withdraw from, to refuse (Kangxi: 辭去也), to go away (Kangxi: 去也); to resign, to throw up; to stop intercourse with (Kangxi: 絕也); declining, fading (Kangxi: 退也衰也彫落也), as flowers; to express, to inform (Kangxi: 吿也); to thank, to express gratitude (Kangxi: 拜賜曰謝); to acknowledge, to confess (Kangxi: 自以爲過曰謝); to excuse one's self; _to tse_ (do1 ze6 多謝) many thanks; _ling tse_ (ling5 ze6 領謝) to receive with thanks; _tse sz'_ (ze6 si6 謝事) to retire from office; _tse t'ip_ (ze6 tip3-2 謝帖) a reward offered; _pik tse_ (bik1 ze6 璧謝) to decline a present; _fa tse_ (faa1 ze6 花謝) fading flowers; _tse tiu_ (ze6 diu3 謝弔) to decline mourners; _tse po_ (ze6 bou6 謝步) a doctor's fees; _tse hak_ (ze6 haak3 謝客) to excuse one's self to visitors; _tso tse tsau_ (zou6 ze6 zau2 做謝酒), to give one a dinner; _tse tsau_ (ze6 zau2 謝酒), to take wine with; _tse shai_ (ze6 sai3 謝世) to die; _kom tse_ (gam2 ze6 感謝) feeling grateful for; _tap tse_ (daap3 ze6 答謝) to return thanks, to make a present in return; _ch'au tse_ (cau4 ze6 酬謝) to requite in kind. To ask. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【謝】 (zjaeH) (_verb-like_) to decline, to renounce, (_met._) to apologise, to wilt Cantonese prefers 多謝 over 謝謝. Note that 多謝 should only be used to express gratitude for gifts or patronage. To express gratitude for small/menial favours, or for goods/services that the speaker is paying for, 唔該 (TODO) should instead be used.

zek1.text: U+5527 喞 【唧】 (zek1) An final particle of emphasis or emotion. 得未唧 (dak1 mei6 zek1): "Are you ready or not?"

zik1.text: U+5527 喞 (F1.74 喞) 啾喞衆聲又虫鳴 (Page 568 喞; here normalised to 唧) The noise of a crowd (Kangxi 喞: 啾喞也, 啾喞衆聲); the hum of insects; _tsau tsik_ (zau1 zik1 啾唧) a hubbub. (_onomatopoeia_) A clicking (or perhaps chirping) sound (Kangxi 喞: 喞聲也). 【唧】 (zik1) A jack (for raising heavy objects, especially vehicles). 攞個唧嚟 (lo2 go3 zik1 lai4): "Bring me the jack." To raise with a jack. 唧起架車 (zik1 hei2 gaa3 ce1): "Jack up the car." A jack (playing card). 葵扇唧 (kwai4 sin3 zik1): "Jack of spades." 唧夫佬 (zik1 fu1 lou2): "Jack full house."
U+559E (F1.74 喞) 啾喞衆聲又虫鳴 (Page 568 喞)

zek3.text: U+62D3 (F1.78 拓) 拾也又充拓又托推也斥開也拓落不偶也 (Page 14* 摭拓 _Chik_ (zik6); here corrected to _Chik_ (zik3) and normalised to _Chek_ (zek3)) Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of the series 跖蹠拓隻炙 (_chik_) *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 拓 is read 陽入 _chik_ (zik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 直値殖植稙湜埴 (_chik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 拓 under _chek_ (zek3) here.

U+646D 拓 zik3 (uncommon) (F1.78 拓) 拾也又充拓又托推也斥開也拓落不偶也 (Page 14* 摭拓 _Chik_ (zik6); here corrected to _Chik_ (zik3) and normalised to _Chek_ (zek3)) To take up (Kangxi 摭: 拓或从庶, 取也, 猶拾取也); to collect, to gather; _~~chik~~ ``chek`` chap_ (zek3 zap1 摭執) to take or adopt. Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of the series 跖蹠拓隻炙 (_chik_) *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 拓 (and hence 摭) is read 陽入 _chik_ (zik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 直値殖植稙湜埴 (_chik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 摭 under _chek_ (zek3) here. 【摭】 (tsyek) (_verb-like_) to pick up, to pick

U+7099 ze3 (alternative per 《廣韻》之夜切) zik3 (alternative) (F1.78 炙) 燔炙 (Page 14* before 隻) (The three next characters (隻, 蹠跖, 炙) are usually pronounced _chek_ (zek).) (Page 14* 炙 _Chik_ (zik6); _Chek_ (zek6) implied by variational note; here corrected to _Chek_ (zek3)) To roast (Kangxi: 炮肉也, 炕火曰炙); to broil; to dry or toast before the fire; to heat; near (Kangxi: 親近也); to approach, to approximate; to fry in honey, as some physic is; _~~kui~~ ``k'ui`` chek yan hau_ (kui2 zek3 jan4 hau2 膾炙人口), tastes well in people's mouths --- said of fine poetry; _chek ts'o_ (zek3 cou2 炙草), dried liquorice; _chek shau_ (zek3 sau2 炙手), to warm the hand; _fan chek_ (faan4 zek3 燔炙) to roast or broil; _chek kon_ (zek3 gon1 炙乾), dried thoroughly. Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of the series 跖蹠拓隻炙 (_chik_) *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 炙 is read 陽入 _chik_ (zik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 直値殖植稙湜埴 (_chik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 炙 under _chek_ (zek3) here. Williams' 膾炙人口: Corrected _kui_ (gui2) to _k'ui_ (kui2). Williams' "near": Specifically, to come under the (positive) influence of another. 【炙】 (tsyek, tsyaeH) (_verb-like_) to broil

U+7620 zik3 (alternative) zik6 (per 《廣韻》秦昔切) (F1.75 瘠 _Tsik_ (zik3)) 人瘦曰瘠 (Page 568 瘠 _Tsik_ (zik3); here normalised to _Tsek_ (zek3)) Lean, thin, reduced to a bone, emaciated (Kangxi: 瘦也); poor, as land; to make lean; to impoverish; to retrench, to bring down (Kangxi: 瘠謂省約); _~~tsik~~ ``tsek`` t'o_ (zek3 tou2 瘠土), meagre soil. 【瘠】 (dzjek) (_adjective-like_) emaciated, (_met._) lean

zik3.text: U+7620 (F1.75 瘠) 人瘦曰瘠 (Page 568 瘠)

zek3.text: U+810A zik3 (alternative) (F1.74 脊 _Tsik_ (zik3)) 脊骨 (Page 569 before 脊) (This (脊) and the next (席, here corrected to 蓆) are often read _tsek_ (zek).) (Page 569 脊 _Tsik_ (zik3); _Tsek_ (zek3) implied by variational note) The back-bone, the spine (Kangxi: 背呂也, 背脊); the back; convergent, as the ribs; the ridge of a house; a sierra, a ridge (Kangxi: 山脊); ~~a bone;~~ a principle``, a reason`` (Kangxi: 理也); _pui tsek kwat_ (bui3 zek3 gwat1 背脊骨) the spine; _uk tsek_ (uk1 zek3 屋脊) the top of a roof; _mo tsik kwat_ (mou4 zik3 gwat1 無脊骨) no dependance can be put in him; _hi yat t'iu tsek_ (hei2 jat1 tiu4 zek3 起一條脊) raise a ridge; _mai u tsek_ (maai5 jyu4 zek3 買魚脊) buy the fish's back. Williams' "a bone": Only as an alternative form of 㱴 (Kangxi: 與㱴通死骨也), which has a different reading. Williams' 起一條脊: Corrected _t'iu_ (tiu1) to _t'iu_ (tiu4). 【脊】 (tsjek) (_noun-like_) spine, (_met._) grounds

zik3.text: U+810A (F1.74 脊) 脊骨 (Page 569 脊)

zek3.text: U+8DD6 蹠 zik3 (alternative) (F1.78 跖) 盗跖秦之大盗 (F1.78 蹠) 同上又脚掌也又踐履也 (Page 14* before 隻) (The three next characters (隻, 蹠跖, 炙) are usually pronounced _chek_ (zek).) (Page 14* 蹠跖 _Chik_ (zik6); _Chek_ (zek6) implied by variational note; here corrected to _Chek_ (zek3)) the sole of the foot (Kangxi: 足下); metatarsus of birds; _to chek_ (dou6 zek3 盜跖) a ``tyrannical`` leader of thieves~~, a certain Robin Hood~~ in the ~~days of Ts'in (秦)~~ ``Spring and Autumn``. Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of the series 跖蹠拓隻炙 (_chik_) *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 跖 is read 陽入 _chik_ (zik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 直値殖植稙湜埴 (_chik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 跖 under _chek_ (zek3) here. Williams does not provide separate entries for 跖 and 蹠. I have split things up accordingly.

U+8E50 zik3 (alternative) (F1.74 蹐 _Tsik_ (zik3)) 局蹐累足也 (Page 569 蹐 _Tsik_ (zik3); here normalised to _Tsek_ (zek3)) To step carefully; short steps, a mincing walk (Kangxi: 小步也); ~~to walk here and there.~~ 【蹐】 (tsjek) (_verb-like_) to take small steps

zik3.text: U+8E50 (F1.74 蹐) 局蹐累足也 (Page 569 蹐)

zek3.text: U+8E60 zik3 (alternative) (F1.78 跖) 盗跖秦之大盗 (F1.78 蹠) 同上又脚掌也又踐履也 (Page 14* before 隻) (The three next characters (隻, 蹠跖, 炙) are usually pronounced _chek_ (zek).) (Page 14* 蹠~~跖~~ _Chik_ (zik6); _Chek_ (zek6) implied by variational note; here corrected to _Chek_ (zek3)) To tread on (Kangxi: 足履踐也, 蹠蹈也, 蹠踐也); to leap (Kangxi: 楚人謂跳躍曰蹠); Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of the series 跖蹠拓隻炙 (_chik_) *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 蹠 is read 陽入 _chik_ (zik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 直値殖植稙湜埴 (_chik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 蹠 under _chek_ (zek3) here. Williams does not provide separate entries for 跖 and 蹠. I have split things up accordingly. To go unto (Kangxi: 蹠適也).

U+96BB 只 zik3 (alternative) (F1.78 隻) 件也 (Page 14* before 隻) (The three next characters (隻, 蹠跖, 炙) are usually pronounced _chek_ (zek).) (Page 14* 隻 _Chik_ (zik6); _Chek_ (zek6) implied by variational note; here corrected to _Chek_ (zek3)) One, single, not a pair, one of a pair (Kangxi: 鳥一枚也, 奇也, 物單曰隻); a classifier of vessels, boats, animals, birds, and of things in pairs, as legs, arms eyes, cups, saucers, &c.; _chek shan_ (zek3 san1 隻身), myself alone; _chek chek yau mi_ (zek3 zek3 jau5 mei5 隻隻有尾), every one has a tail; _tai chek hat i_ (daai6 zek3 hat1 ji4-1 大隻乞兒), a sturdy beggar; _kiu chek t'eng_ (giu3 zek3 teng5 叫隻艇), call a boat. (Page 713 隻 _Chik_ (zik3); here normalised to _Chek_ (zek3)) This character is constantly contracted to 只 in Canton. Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's placement of the series 跖蹠拓隻炙 (_chik_) *after* 劇屐 (_k'ik_) and 溺 (_nik_), which are 陽入 (see F1.78). Based on this ordering, Williams has inferred that the character 隻 is read 陽入 _chik_ (zik6). However, since Fan Wan has a separate series for 直値殖植稙湜埴 (_chik_), it appears that its authors have attempted to distinguish the vernacular rime _-ek_ from the literary rime _-ik_, notwithstanding all series appear under 第七英影應益. I have corrected Williams by placing 隻 under _chek_ (zek3) here. 【隻】 (tsyek) (_adjective-like_) single (_classifier_)

zik3.text: U+96BB (F1.78 隻) 件也 (Page 713 隻)

zek3.text: U+9DBA 脊 zik3 (alternative) (F1.75 鶺 _Tsik_ (zik3)) 鶺鴒鳥名 (Page 569 鶺 _Tsik_ (zik3); here normalised to _Tsek_ (zek3)) A wagtail, called _~~tsik~~ ``tsek`` ling_ (zek3 ling4 鶺鴒) (Kangxi: 鶺鴒); it has a mottled, black neck; another name for it is _ts'in mo_ (cin4 mou5 錢母), or money mother. 【鶺】 (tsjek) Used in 鶺鴒. 【鶺鴒】 (tsjek leng) (_noun-like_) wagtail

zik3.text: U+9DBA (F1.75 鶺) 鶺鴒鳥名 (Page 569 鶺)

zik6.text: U+5E2D (F1.78 席) 几席薦席草席又越席天子之旗 (F1.78 蓆) 草蓆同上 (Page 570 蓆; here corrected to 席) Mats (Kangxi 席: 藉也, 牀席也, 薦席); a mat spread for eating on; a repast, a meal, a table; pliable, without force of character (Kangxi 席: 席釋也可卷可釋); ~~affection for;~~ to depend on (Kangxi 席: 資也因也); rest, quiet (Kangxi 席: 安也); to spread upon, to lay out, as a mat; _tsau tsik_ (zau2 zik6 酒席) a banquet; _tsik kan t'am k'ap_ (zik6 gaan1 taam4 kap6 席間談及) speaking while at table; _sai tsik_ (sai1 zik6 西席) a teacher; _i tsik_ (ji2 zik6 倚席) to lean on the table; _ch'it tsik_ (cit3 zik6 設席) to spread an entertainment; _mai tsik_ (maai4 zik6 埋席) to ~~sit at table~~ ``draw near and take one's seat``; _tsik ti i tso_ (zik6 dei6 ji4 zo6 席地而坐) to spread a feast and sit down; _kam man t'ung tsik_ (gam1 maan5 tung4 zik6 今晚同席) I dine with you this evening. Williams has mixed up 席 and 蓆. Which is easy to do, as 席 is interchanged with 蓆 for the "mat" sense. Williams' "affection for": Whilst the phrase 席寵 exists, the meaning of 席 therein is still "depend" or "rely". A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【席】 (zjek) (_noun-like_) mat

zek6.text: U+84C6 (F1.78 席 _Tsik_ (zik6)) 几席薦席草席又越席天子之旗 (F1.78 蓆 _Tsik_ (zik6)) 草蓆同上 (Page 570 席 _Tsik_ (zik6); _Tsek_ (zek6) implied by variational note; here corrected to 蓆) Williams has mixed up 席 and 蓆.

zik6.text: U+84C6 席 (for "mat" sense) zek6 (vernacular for "mat" sense) (F1.78 席) 几席薦席草席又越席天子之旗 (F1.78 蓆) 草蓆同上 (Page 569 before 脊) (This (脊) and the next (席, here corrected to 蓆) are often read _tsek_ (zek).) (Page 570 席; here corrected to 蓆) Interchanged with the next (here corrected to 席), meaning a mat (Kangxi 蓆: 與薦席之席通); ~~flowing;~~ wide, large, as garments (Kangxi 蓆: 蓆大也); ample (Kangxi 蓆: 廣多也); _ts'o tsek_ (cou2 zek6 草蓆) grass mats; _t'ang tsek_ (tang4 zek6 藤蓆) rattan mats; _chik tsek_ (zik1 zek6 織蓆) to weave mats; _kai man tsek_ (gaai1 man4 zek6 佳文蓆) fine mats; _fa tsek_ (faa1 zek6 花蓆) colored mats; _tsek pau_ (zek6 baau1 蓆包), a mat bag. Williams has mixed up 席 and 蓆. 【蓆】 (zjek) (_adjective-like_) large

zeng1.text: U+7CBE May appear as either ⿰米靑 or ⿰米青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (Page 576 精 _Tsing_ (zing1); _Tseng_ (zeng1) implied by variational note)

zing1.text: U+7CBE May appear as either ⿰米靑 or ⿰米青 (assumed here), depending on the font. zeng1 (vernacular for "clever" sense) (F1.67 精) 妙也細也純也靈也壯也熟也 (Page 576 before 精) (The three next (精, 井, 淨) are often pronounced _tseng_ (zeng)) (Page 576 精) Cleaned rice (Kangxi: 鑿也); selected (Kangxi: 擇也), ripe, mature (Kangxi: 熟也); fine, subtle, delicate (Kangxi: 細也); the best, the finest; unmixed, devoted to (Kangxi: 專一也), accustomed to, expert at; skillful, as in strategy (Kangxi: 巧也); the pure parts of anything (Kangxi: 凡物之純至者皆曰精), etherial, subtle; the essence of things (Kangxi: 靈也眞氣也); it is prefixed to other adjectives to show a high degree of; the animal spirits; the semen, the germinating principle of nature; an apparition, form taken by spirits; _ho tsing shan_ (hou2 zing1 san4 好精神), in good spirits, smart; _tsing ping_ (zing1 bing1 精兵), flower of the troops, picked men; _lung ma tsing shan_ (lung4 maa5 zing1 san4 龍馬精神), bright as a steed or dragon; _tseng kong tai wa_ (zeng1 gong2 daai6 waa6 精講大話) very clever at lying; _k'u hai tseng_ (keoi5 hai6 zeng1 佢係精) he's smart enough; _tsing yuk_ (zing1 juk6 精肉) lean meat; _tsing hi_ (zing1 hei3 精氣) ether, the essence of; influence, pure, the quintessence; _tsing chi_ (zing1 zi3 精緻) beautiful, handsome; _~~tsing~~ ``tseng`` kwai_ (zeng1 gwaai1 精乖), very clever at; _tsing kwai_ (zing1 gwaai3 精怪) an elf; spirit rappings, prodigies; _wai tsing_ (wai4 zing1 遺精), involunt``a``ry emissions; _ni tseng ngo 'm ngoi_ (nei5 zeng1 ngo5 m4 ngoi4 你精我唔呆), if you are cute, I'm not a fool; _tseng ~~lut~~ ``lat`` pin_ (zeng1 lat1 bin1 精甩辮), smart as a steel-trap; _tseng tsai_ (zeng1 zai2 精仔), a shrewd fellow; _tseng tau tseng_ (zeng1 dau3 zeng1 精鬥精), met his match. Williams' 精講大話: Corrected _kong_ (gong3) to _kong_ (gong2). Williams' 精乖: Changed _tsing_ (zing1) to vernacular reading _tseng_ (zeng1). Williams' 你精我唔呆: Here, "cute" means "clever". Williams' 精甩辮: Corrected _lut_ (leot1) to _lat_ (lat1). 【精】 (tsjeng) (_noun-like_) fine-rice (_adjective-like_) fine (_noun-like_) essence

zeng2.text: U+4E95 (Page 576 井 _Tsing_ (zing2); _Tseng_ (zeng2) implied by variational note)

zing2.text: U+4E95 zeng2 (alternative) (F1.72 井) 穴地出泉曰井 (Page 576 before 精) (The three next (精, 井, 淨) are often pronounced _tseng_ (zeng)) (Page 576 井) A well (Kangxi: 穴地出水曰井); a deep pit; a field laid out regularly (Kangxi: 田九百畝曰井象九區之形); to draw water; arranged, laid out regularly (Kangxi: 井井經畫端整貌); _tseng shui_ (zeng2 seoi2 井水), well water; _shi tseng_ (si5 zeng2 市井), a public market; _shi tseng chi t'o_ (si5 zeng2 zi1 tou4 市井之徒), a worthless loafer; _t'in tseng_ (tin1 zeng2 天井), an open court; _tso tseng kun t'in_ (zo6 zeng2 gun1 tin1 坐井觀天), to look at the sky from a well---inexperienced; _tseng lan_ (zeng2 laan4 井欄), a well-curb; _tseng tseng yau t'iu_ (zeng2 zeng2 jau5 tiu4 井井有條), all in order, do it methodically; _hoi tseng_ (hoi1 zeng2 開井), to dig a well; _yat tseng ti_ (jat1 zeng2 dei6 一井地) a square rood of land; _t'o ku tseng_ (tou4 gu2 zeng2 淘古井), to rinse the old well, _i. e._ to marry a rich widow. Williams' "a field laid out regularly": Not wrong, but misses the definition as nine hundred _mau_ (畝) of land area. Williams' 坐井觀天: More ignorant than inexperienced. Continuously (Kangxi: 往來連屬貌). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【井】 (tsjengX) (_noun-like_) well, (_met._) square (_noun-like_) field-grid (_adverb-like_) (_met._) continuously

zeng3.text: U+6B63 (Page 19* 正 _Ching_ (zing3); _Cheng_ (zeng3) implied by variational note)

zing1.text: U+6B63 (Page 18* 正) The first; _ching ut_ (zing1 jyut6 正月) the first month of the year (Kangxi: 歲之首月也), January. Williams' 正月: Usage to denote Western "January" is dated. Light-facing portions of a chamber (Kangxi: 室之向明處曰正). The centre square of an archery target (Kangxi: 射侯中曰正 etc.). 【正-level】 (tsyeng) (_adjective-like_) central (_noun-like_) centre

zing3.text: U+6B63 zeng3 (vernacular adjective) zeng3-2 (rising second in vernacular 正正地) (F1.72 正) 公也當也不邪也 (Page 19* before 整) (The two next characters (整, 正) are often pronounced _cheng_ (zeng).) (Page 19* 正) Correct (Kangxi: 是也), straight, right, not awry, erect (Kangxi: 方直不曲謂之正), even; not inclined, not deflected; regular, usual``,`` proper (Kangxi: 常也, 物以正爲常); the first, principal (Kangxi: 正長也); just, at the time of; constant, usual; genuine, real``,`` as goods; to put in order; to assume, to enter on, as an office; to adjust, to govern, to execute the laws, to punish (Kangxi: 治其罪亦曰正); _~~ching~~ ``cheng`` 'm ~~ching~~ ``cheng``_ (zeng3 m4 zeng3 正唔正) is it proper? _pat ching king_ (bat1 zing3 ging1 不正經), licentious, immoral; _ching yan kwan tsz'_ (zing3 jan4 gwan1 zi2 正人君子) an upright man; _heung ching_ (hoeng1 zing3 鄉正) village elders; _ching tong_ (zing3 dong3 正當), as it ought to be; _ching chung_ (zing3 zung1 正中), put in the middle; _~~ching~~ ``cheng`` ngam_ (zeng3 ngaam1 正啱), placed right; _cheng cheuk shi_ (zeng3 zoek6 si4 正着時), just in good time; _cheng iu_ (zeng3 jiu3 正要) just as I wanted it; _ching lo kung ming_ (zing3 lou6 gung1 ming4 正路功名), reputation well earned, an honorable fame; _ching king_ (zing3 ging1 正經), moral, virtuous; _ching tsoi ni ch'u_ (zing3 zoi6 ni1 cyu3 正在呢處) he was just here; _~~tso~~ ``ts'o`` cheng_ (co5 zeng3 坐正) to sit properly; _ching kong_ (zing3 gong2 正講), to speak literally; _ching t'ong_ (zing3 tong4 正堂), the main hall, the chief officer; _ching tsung_ (zing3 zung6 正從) principals and seconds; _ching chik_ (zing3 zik6 正直) honest, solid, true; _ching nam min_ (zing3 naam4 min6 正南面) to be emperor; _ching fat_ (zing3 faat3 正法) to inflict capital punishment; _ching sam_ (zing3 sam1 正心), to reform. Williams' 正唔正: Changed _ching_ (zing3) to vernacular reading _cheng_ (zeng3). Williams' 正當: Corrected _tong_ (dong1) to _tong_ (dong3). If 當 is in level tone, then it means "just when" instead of "proper". Williams' 正啱: Changed _ching_ (zing3) to vernacular reading _cheng_ (zeng3). Williams' 正着時: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). Williams' 正經: Also "serious" (as opposed to "playful"). Williams' 坐正: Corrected _tso_ (zo6 去聲) to _ts'o_ (co5 上聲). Williams' 正直: Corrected _chik_ (zik1) to _chik_ (zik6). 【正-departing】 (tsyengH) (_adjective-like_) right, proper (_verb-like_) to right, (_met._) to determine (_adverb-like_) right, exactly (_adjective-like_) principal 【快靚正】 (faai3 leng3 zeng3) Quick, attractive, and quality (said of goods and services). 【平靚正】 (peng4 leng3 zeng3) Cheap, attractive, and quality (said of goods and services). 【四正】 (si3 zeng3) Proper (of appearance, position, etc.). 打呔四正啲 (daa2 taai1 si3 zeng3 di1): "Wearing a tie would look more proper". Neat, tidy. 執得四正 (zap1 dak1 si3 zeng3): "Tidied up neatly." 【正】 (zeng3) Great, excellent, fantastic. 好正喎 (hou2 zeng3 wo3): "Hey, it's really excellent!" 快靚正 (faai3 leng3 zeng3): "Quick, attractive, and good-quality." 平靚正 (peng4 leng3 zeng3): "Cheap, attractive, and good-quality." 【正話】 (zing3 waa6) A moment ago, just now. (Literally, "it was just said".)

zeng6.text: U+6DE8 (Page 576 淨 _Tsing_ (zing6); _Tseng_ (zeng6) implied by variational note)

zing6.text: U+6DE8 凈 瀞 zeng6 (vernacular adjective "clean") (F1.73 淨) 潔淨不汚也 (Page 20|n63 ~~净~~``凈``) Incorrectly but commonly used for 淨 _tsing_ (zing6) clean. (Page 576 before 精) (The three next (精, 井, 淨) are often pronounced _tseng_ (zeng)) (Page 576 淨) Clean, pure, spotless, undefiled (Kangxi: 無垢也潔也); upright, guileless; to wash clean, to cleanse; only; all; a rapids in the R. Han (漢) (Kangxi: 灘名, 漢水東爲淨灘); _kon tseng_ (gon1 zeng6 乾淨) clean, pure; _kit tseng_ (git3 zeng6 潔淨) clean, to purify one's heart; _tsing ~~chung~~ ``ch'ung``_ (zing6 cung5 淨重) the nett weight; _tseng shui fu_ (zeng6 seoi2 fu4), a cleansing charm, used in houses; _yat ko to hu kon tseng_ (jat1 go3 dou1 heoi3 gon1 zeng6 一個都去乾淨) every one has gone. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𠎧 to 潔. Williams' 淨重: Corrected _chung_ (zung6 去聲) to _ch'ung_ (cung5 上聲). 【淨】 (dzjengH) (_adjective-like_) clean (_verb-like_) to cleanse 【淨】 (zing6) Purely, only. 淨麵 (zing6 min6): "Only noodles." 淨魚蛋 (zing6 jyu4 daan6-2): "Only fish-balls" (i.e. without noodles). 淨食餸 (zing6 sik6 sung3): "Only eating dishes" (i.e. without rice). 淨係佢去 (zing6 hai6 keoi5 heoi3): "Only he is going."
U+7A7D (F1.73 阱) 地坑坎也 (F1.73 穽) 同上 (Page 577 穽阱)

zeng6.text: U+912D zing6 (uncommon) (F1.74 鄭) 國名又鄭重 (Page 21* 鄭 _Ch'ing_ (cing6); here corrected to _Ching_ (zing6) and normalised to _Cheng_ (zeng6)) A feudal state (Kangxi: 國名) in the Chau (周) dynasty, now the prefecture of Kaifung (開封) in Honan (河南); it was first placed where the capital of Shensi (陝西) lies; _~~ch'ing~~ ``cheng`` chung_ (zeng6 zung6 鄭重) prudent (Kangxi: 鄭重殷勤之意)~~, inflexible~~. Fan Wan entry: Removed extra character 又 at the end. Williams erroneously puts 鄭 under aspirated _ch'ing_ (cing6). Williams' 鄭重: In modern usage, only "solemn". A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

zing6.text: U+912D (F1.74 鄭) 國名又鄭重 (Page 21* 鄭 _Ch'ing_ (cing6); here corrected to _Ching_ (zing6)) Fan Wan entry: Removed extra character 又 at the end. Williams erroneously puts 鄭 under aspirated _ch'ing_ (cing6).

U+9631 穽 zeng6 (vernacular) (F1.73 阱) 地坑坎也 (F1.73 穽) 同上 (Page 577 穽阱) A pit, a pitfall, a hole to catch beasts in (Kangxi 阱: 坑也, 陷也); ~~to fall into a pit;~~ _ham tsing_ (ham6 zing6 陷阱) ~~to tumble into a hole~~ ``a trap``. Williams' "to fall into a pit": This is a misreading of Kangxi 陷也, which is intended as a noun, not a verb. Williams' 陷阱: Changed _ham_ (haam6) to common reading _ham_ (ham6). 【阱】 (dzjengX) (_noun-like_) pitfall

zeoi1.text: U+6CAE ceoi1 (per 《廣韻》七余切) (F1.44 沮) 水名 (Page 594 沮) The name of an affluent of the Yangtsz' kiang (揚子江) (Kangxi: 水名, 水出漢中房陵東入江) west of ~~K'ingchau~~ ``Kingchau`` fu (荊州府) in Hupeh (湖北); an ancient district now the extreme southwest of Shensi (陝西). The reading _tsu_ (zeoi1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 子余切, explained 水名說文出漢中房陵東入江. Williams considers rising-tone _tsu_ (zeoi2) an acceptable alternative reading for the name sense. This is incorrect.

zeoi2.text: U+6CAE ceoi1 (alternative per 《廣韻》七余切 for "stop" sense) zeoi6 (per 《廣韻》慈吕切) (F1.46 沮) 沮邱 (Page 594 沮) To stop, to prohibit (Kangxi: 止也); ~~to pass over;~~ to divulge``, to leak`` (Kangxi: 沮泄謂泄漏也); to destroy (Kangxi: 壞也); to discomfit (Kangxi: 敗也); to scare (Kangxi: 沮謂恐怖之也); _tsu chi_ (zeoi2 zi2 沮止), to quash, to stop; to intrigue against; _tsu tsok_ (zeoi2 zok6 沮怍) ashamed, awkward, as a rustic; _tsu sit_ (zeoi2 sit3 沮泄) to let out, to blab; to issue, as malaria; The reading _tsu_ (zeoi2) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 再呂切, explained 止之也遏也鬲也抑也. Williams' "to pass over": Kangxi has 過也, but it is unclear whether this means "pass" or "excessive". Williams' "discomfit": In the (now) archaic sense of "defeat". Dejected, despondent. 沮喪 (zeoi2 song3): "Dejected." 【沮-rising】 (dzjoX) (_verb-like_) to stop (_verb-like_) to scare (_verb-like_) to leak

zeoi3.text: U+6CAE (Page 594 沮 _Tsu_ (zeoi2); here corrected to _Tsu_ (zeoi3)) _shap tsu_ (sap1 zeoi3 濕沮), damp, dank. 【沮-departing】 (tsjoH) (_adjective-like_) dank

zeoi1.text: U+75BD ceoi1 (per 《廣韻》七余切) (F1.44 疽) 癰疽 (Page 594 疽) A deep-seated ulcer (Kangxi: 久癰也); a carbuncle or anthrax; _yung tsu_ (jung1 zeoi1 癰疽), an old ulcer; _shun tsu_ (syun5 zeoi1 吮疽), to suck out an ulcer. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿸疒⿰⿱丿邑隹 to 癰. 【疽】 (tshjo) (_noun-like_) ulcer

U+8127 䘒 zoe1 (vernacular) zoe1-4 (low first in reduplication 脧脧) For the visually similar character under the moon radical, see 朘 syun(TODO) In the entry for the moon-radical character, Kangxi carefully notes that the left component is *not* meat (不从肉). In the entry for the meat-radical character, Kangxi erroneously includes senses for the moon-radical character. A boy's genitals (Kangxi: 赤子隂也). 【脧】 (zoe1) The penis. 脧 (zoe1): "Peen." 脧脧 (zoe1-4 zoe1): "Willy."

U+83F9 ze1 (per 《集韻》咨邪切 for "marsh" sense) zeoi3 (per 《集韻》將豫切 for "marsh" sense) (F1.44 菹) 淹菜 (Page 594 菹) Pickled fruits, like melons (Kangxi: 酢菜也 etc.); salted vegetables; ~~a marshy plant or sedge;~~ a marsh (Kangxi: 澤生草曰菹). Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chu_ per 《廣韻》側魚切. Nevertheless, the reading _tsu_ is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 子余切, covering both the "pickle" sense and the "marsh" sense. 【菹】 (tsrjo) (_noun-like_) pickle (_noun-like_) marsh
U+89DC (Page 612 觜) Read _tsui_ (zeoi1); the horns of an owl (Kangxi: 鴟舊頭上角觜也); the curved corners of a roof, or ridgepole; to raise the egret on the head;

zeoi2.text: U+89DC (Page 612 觜 read _Tsui_ (zeoi1); here corrected to _Tsui_ (zeoi2)) ~~Read _tsui_ (zeoi1);~~ ~~to bristle up, to look incensed, to pout~~ the lips ~~in anger~~ (Kangxi: 喙也). Williams' "to bristle up" etc.: This is a misreading of Kangxi's examples (武人厲其觜吻 and 列膆破觜). There is no inherent connotation of anger in 觜, which simply means "beak" or "lips".

zi1.text: U+89DC (F2.34 觜) 宿名 (Page 612 觜) The 21st zodiacal constellation (Kangxi: 星名觜觿西方宿也), consisting of λ and 2π in Orion's head.

zeoi1.text: U+8D84 ceoi1 (per 《廣韻》七余切) (F1.44 趄) 趦趄行不進也 (Page 594 趄) To walk slowly, impeded in going (Kangxi: 趑趄也); _tsz' tsu_ (zi1 zeoi1 趑趄), hobbling, walking step by step.

U+8FFD (F2.55 追) 追述追隨又追也 (Page 33* 追) To pursue (Kangxi: 逐也), to follow after (Kangxi: 隨也); to come up with (Kangxi: 送也), to overtake, to catch (Kangxi: 逮也); to sue; to seek out, to trace back (Kangxi: 凡上遡已往曰追); to reflect on, to look back on, retrospective; ~~to expel;~~ _chui kon_ (zeoi1 gon2 追趕), to chase; _chui to_ (zeoi1 dou3-2 追到) to catch, to reach; _chui fui_ (zeoi1 fui3 追悔) to feel remorse; ~~_chui hu_ (zeoi1 heoi3 追去) to drive out;~~ _chui kau_ (zeoi1 gau3 追究) to investigate thoroughly; _ngan pat chui ui_ (ngan4 bat1 zeoi1 wui4 銀不追回), the money cannot be refunded; _chui k'u fan lai_ (zeoi1 keoi5 faan1 lai4 追佢返嚟), hurry him back here; _chui 'm sheung lok_ (zeoi1 m4 soeng5 lok3 追唔上佢) could not catch him; _chui t'o_ (zeoi1 tou2 追討), to dun; _chui fung_ (zeoi1 fung1 追封), posthumous honors; _chui nim_ (zeoi1 nim6 追念) to recollect. Fan Wan entry: 追也 is self-referential. Williams' "to sue": In the sense of seeking by request. Williams' "to expel": This is a misreading of Kangxi 逐也. 【追】 (trwij) (_verb-like_) to chase (after), (_met._) to reminisce, to send off

U+9310 (F2.55 錐) 鎗劍之屬 (Page 33* 錐) An awl, a borer; a sharp pointed tool (Kangxi: 銳器也); the apex, the point; to bore; trifling; _chui to mut_ (zeoi1 dou1 mut6 錐刀末) a small matter (Kangxi: 錐刀末喩小事); _mo chui_ (mou4 zeoi1 毛錐), a pencil (Kangxi: 毛錐筆也); _chui lung_ (zeoi1 lung4-1 錐窿), to bore a hole; _p'an mo lap chui chi ti_ (pan4 mou4 lap6 zeoi1 zi1 dei6 貧無立錐之地) miserably poor, not a place to stick an awl. Williams' "pencil": In the (now) dated sense of a brush. 【錐】 (tsywij) (_noun-like_) awl
U+96B9 Short-tailed birds in general (Kangxi: 鳥之短尾總名也). The 172nd radical ("short-tailed-bird").
U+96CE ceoi1 (per 《廣韻》七余切) (F1.44 雎) 水鳥詩關關雎鳩 This character is easily confused with 睢 (see 睢 zeoi1). A type of waterbird. 【雎】 (tshjo) Used in 雎鳩. 【雎鳩】 (tshjo kjuw) (_noun-like_) water bird
U+9A05 (F2.55 騅) 馬之蒼黑者 (Page 33* 騅) A black horse with white spots (Kangxi: 蒼白雜毛騅); _u chui ma_ (wu1 zeoi1 maa5 烏騅馬), the charger of Hang Yu (項羽) of Tso (楚). 【騅】 (tsywij) (_noun-like_) piebald

zeoi2.text: U+5480 (F1.46 咀) 咀嚼 (Page 595 咀) To taste (Kangxi: 含味也); to chew, to bite in order to taste (Kangxi: 咀嚼); _tsu tseuk ha_ (zeoi2 zoek3 haa5 咀嚼吓), take a bite of it; _hom ying tsu wa_ (ham4 jing1 zeoi2 waa4 含英咀華), to learn and relish the beauties of a style. It is only in modern Cantonese that 咀 _tsu_ and 嘴 _tsui_ are homophonous (both zeoi2), and that 咀 is an alternative form of 嘴. Williams' 咀嚼吓: Changed _tseuk_ (zoek6) to common reading _tseuk_ (zoek3). Williams' 含英咀華: Corrected _wa_ (waa1) to _wa_ (waa4).

U+5634 咀 觜 (F2.56 嘴) 口唇也 (Page 596 嘴) The lips; a beak, a bill of a bird (Kangxi 𠲿: 鳥喙也); a spout, a nozzle, an aperture, an adit; to wrangle, to chatter, to talk much and impudently, to give lip; _mut ha t'iu tsui_ (mut3 haa5 tiu4 zeoi2 抹吓條嘴), wipe your lips; _ts'an tsui_ (can1 zeoi2 親嘴), to kiss; _tseuk tsui_ (zoek3 zeoi2 雀嘴), a bird's bill; _in t'ung tsui_ (jin1 tung4-1 zeoi2 煙筒嘴), mouthpiece of a pipe; _ho tsui ma_ (hou2 zeoi2 maa5 好嘴馬), or _yau tsui_ (jau4 zeoi2 油嘴), glib at talking, garrulous; _mo tap tsui_ (mou4 daap3 zeoi2 毋答嘴), don't interfere, as in a dispute; _ta tsui pa_ (daa2 zeoi2 baa1 打嘴巴), to beat the lips; _sung siu tsui_ (sung3 siu2 zeoi2 送小嘴), to tell tales, to stir up ill feeling; _pong tsui_ (bong1 zeoi2 幫嘴), to take the part of; _ch'a u tsui_ (caa4 wu4-2 zeoi2 茶壺嘴), a teapot spout; _ho shik tsui t'au_ (hou3 sik6 zeoi2 tau4 好食嘴頭), loving to eat this and that; _ni mo to tsui_ (nei5 mou5 do1 zeoi2 你冇多嘴), don't you talk so much; _tsui kong i_ (zeoi2 gong2 ji6 嘴講易) it's all very easy to talk; _tsui ho ngang_ (zeoi2 hou2 ngaang6 嘴好硬) he ~~wont~~ ``won't`` confess at all. It is only in modern Cantonese that 咀 _tsu_ and 嘴 _tsui_ are homophonous (both zeoi2), and that 咀 is an alternative form of 嘴. Williams' 你冇多嘴: Corrected _to_ (do4) to _to_ (do1). Williams' 嘴講易: Corrected _i_ (ji3) to _i_ (ji6).

zo3.text: U+8A5B zeoi2 (common) (F2.61 詛) 竹過切咒詛 (F2.61 咀) 同上 (Page 26* before 詛) This character (詛) is often called ~~_chu_ (zoe) or~~ _chu_ (zyu). (Page 26* 詛) To curse, to imprecate (Kangxi: 詶也, 呪詛); bad language, curses; _chau cho_ (zau3 zo3 咒詛) to curse. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword self-reference to 上. Williams records _chu_ (zoe) and _chu_ (zyu) as alternative readings without specifying the tone. The former I cannot justify. The latter rhymes with 《集韻》遵遇切 _tsu_, explained 詶也, and also with 咀 _tsu_, but in both cases the pre-merger initial is inconsistent. The common reading zeoi2 is perhaps from identifying 詛咒 with 咀咒, noting that Fan Wan considers 咀 an alternative form of 詛 _cho_ (zo3).

zeoi2.text: U+9F5F zeoi6 (per 《廣韻》牀吕切) (F1.46 齟) 齟齬不相合也 (Page 595 齟) Irregular teeth, which do not interlock easily (Kangxi: 齒不相値曰齟齬); discordant, as opinions, incongruous; to bite, to chew (Kangxi: 齧也, 嚼也). Fan Wan entry: The radical of the headword is printed very poorly.

zeoi3.text: U+6700 冣 (F2.57 最) 殿最前曰最後曰殿 (Page 597 最) To come together, to assemble (Kangxi: 聚也); an adjective denoting the most important, the highest degree of (Kangxi: 極也, 最爲第一); ~~very, extremely, exceedingly~~ ``most``; altogether (Kangxi: 最都凡也); _tsui shin_ (zeoi3 sin6 最善) very ~~good~~ ``best``; _tsui nan_ (zeoi3 naan4 最難), ~~very difficult~~ ``hardest``; _tsui kan iu_ (zeoi3 gan2 jiu3 最緊要) ~~highly~~ ``most`` important; _tsui sin_ (zeoi3 sin1 最先), first, in the first place; _tsui pin tong_ (zeoi3 bin6 dong3 最便當) the most convenient, very handy. 【最】 (tswajH) (_verb-like_) to collect (_determiner-like_) -est, -most

U+7DB4 zeoi6 (per Fan Wan, common) zyut3 (per 《廣韻》陟劣切 for "stitch/connect" senses and common in 點綴) zyut6 (common in 點綴) (F2.24 綴 _Chut_ (zyut3)) 聯也又舞列也又止也禮記綴兆舒疾樂之文也 (F2.57 綴 _Chui_ (zeoi6)) 冠系又聯也 (Page 34* 綴 _Chui_ (zeoi6); here normalised to _Chui_ (zeoi3)) Connected (Kangxi: 連也, 緝也); to carry on (Kangxi: 綴言不絕也); to sew together (Kangxi: 綴猶結也); mixed, variegated (Kangxi: 綴雜彩有文之具); to oversee royal robes (Kangxi: 掌衣服 etc.). (Page 42 綴 _Chut_ (zyut3); here normalised to _Chui_ (zeoi3)) To connect, to join; ~~a band of posture-makers.~~ Williams' "a band of posture-makers": This is perhaps a rendering of Fan Wan 舞列也, or of Kangxi 綴表也所以表行列也, the latter of which is (not particularly helpful) commentary for 《禮樂記》行其綴兆. Undeviating (Kangxi: 不乖離之貌). 【綴】 (trjwet, trjwejH) (_verb-like_) to stitch (together), to connect

zeoi6.text: U+7DB4 (F2.24 綴 _Chut_ (zyut3)) 聯也又舞列也又止也禮記綴兆舒疾樂之文也 (F2.57 綴) 冠系又聯也 (Page 34* 綴)

zyut3.text: U+7DB4 (F2.24 綴) 聯也又舞列也又止也禮記綴兆舒疾樂之文也 (F2.57 綴 _Chui_ (zeoi6)) 冠系又聯也 (Page 42 綴) to stop (Kangxi: 綴猶止也); To bind (Kangxi: 綴猶拘也).

zeoi3.text: U+8DB3 (F1.48 足 _Tsu_ (zeoi6)) 足恭恭之過也 (Page 598 足 read _Tsu_ (zeoi6); here corrected to _Tsu_ (zeoi3)) Read _tsu_ (zeoi3); ~~to act respectfully in order to flatter one~~ ``excessive`` (Kangxi: 過也); ~~careful of one's words;~~ to increase (Kangxi: 添物也, 益也); _tsu kung u yan_ (zeoi3 gung1 jyu1 jan4 足恭於人), ~~to humor and flatter people~~ ``excessively deferential unto others``. 【足-departing】 (tsjuH) (_verb-like_) to plenish, to heap (_adjective-like_) excessive

zuk1.text: U+8DB3 (F1.65 足) 脚也備足足完足 (Page 598 足) The leg (Kangxi: 人之足也); the foot; enough, sufficient (Kangxi: 滿也止也); full, satiated, no deficiency; entirely, in full, wholly; to satisfy, to please; to satisfy the wants of; pure, as silver; actions, conduct; the ~~137th~~ ``157th`` radical ("foot") of characters relating to going; _shap tsuk_ (sap6 zuk1 十足) complete in all respects; _shau tsuk_ (sau2 zuk1 手足) hands and feet, brothers; _tsuk ling_ (zuk1 ling5 足領), enough! no more, I beg of you! _tsz' tsuk_ (zi6 zuk1 自足) self-sufficient, conceited; _tsuk tsuk wan ni pun yat_ (zuk1 zuk1 wan2 nei5 bun3 jat6 足足搵你一日) I've annoyed you half a day; _shat tsuk_ (sat1 zuk1 失足) to slip and fall; to make a blunder, a faux-pas; _tsuk ha_ (zuk1 haa6 足下) your presence, said to a friend; _'m chi tsuk_ (m4 zi1 zuk1 唔知足) discontented, never satisfied; _pat tsuk to_ (bat1 zuk1 dou6 不足道) ~~beyond description, can not be spoken of~~ ``not worth speaking of``; _tsuk sho 'm tsuk_ (zuk1 sou3 m4 zuk1 足數唔足) is the number just right? _fung tsuk_ (fung1 zuk1 豐足) prosperous, to the heart's content; _yan sam mo im tsuk_ (jan4 sam1 mou5 jim3 zuk1 人心冇饜足) the heart is never satisfied; _un tong tsuk tsz'_ (jyun4 dong3 zuk1 zi2 圓當足子) whole dollars alone are reckoned to be full coin. Williams' 足數唔足: This is dated syntax. In modern Cantonese, it would be 足唔足數, with only the adjective hugging the negative.

zeoi3.text: U+9189 zeoi3-2 (rising second in vernacular 醉醉地) (F2.57 醉) 酒醉 (Page 597 醉) Exhilirated with drink (Kangxi: 爲酒所酣曰醉); intoxicated, fuddled, drunken; a debauch; fascinated, enchanted, devoted to, stupefied with; completely engrossed with; _yam tsui_ (jam2 zeoi3 飲醉) drunken; _'m chi tsui_ (m4 zi1 zeoi3 唔知醉) not affected by liquor; _tsui tsau lo_ (zeoi3 zau2 lou2 醉酒佬) a drunkard; _mo tsau sam fan tsui_ (mou4 zau2 saam1 fan1 zeoi3 無酒三分醉) he is ~~half~~ ``three-tenths`` drunk, and has had no wine, _i. e._ he is very stupid, he does not know what he's about; _tsui heung_ (zeoi3 hoeng1 醉鄉), a drunken sot; _tsui u nai_ (zeoi3 jyu4 nai4 醉如泥), drunk as a clod; _tai tsui_ (daai6 zeoi3 大醉) dead drunk. 【醉】 (tswijH) (_adjective-like_) drunk

zeoi6.text: U+589C 隊 (F2.57 墜) 落也 (F2.57 隊) 同上 (Page 33* 墜隊) To fall, to tumble over (Kangxi 墜: 陊也, 落也); to slide; to fall down; to pull on; _chui tai_ (zeoi6 dai1 墜低), pull it down; _lui chui_ (leoi6 zeoi6 累墜) troublesome, requiring constant oversight; _chui lok_ (zeoi6 lok6 墜落) to fall in ruins, to throw over; _chui lok t'oi_ (zeoi6 lok6 toi1 墜落胎), a falling womb, an abortion. Williams' "to pull on": Specifically, to tug downward by hung weight. 【墜】 (drwijH) (_verb-like_) to fall

U+5E8F 叙 (for "order" and "preface" senses) 敍 (for "order" and "preface" senses) 敘 (for "order" and "preface" senses) (F1.48 序) 次也又學名 (Page 595 序) Eastern and western walls ~~or rooms~~ (Kangxi 序: 東西牆謂之序); a college, a school (Kangxi 序: 學也); an asylum for old men; a preface, an introduction, an exordium (Kangxi 敍: 凡書策舉其綱要列卷首爲敍或作序); order, precedence, series (Kangxi 序: 次也); to follow in order; _ts'z' tsu_ (ci3 zeoi6 次序) in order, seriatim, regularly; _tsok yat p'in tsu_ (zok3 jat1 pin1 zeoi6 作一篇序) to write a preface; _tsu ch'i_ (zeoi6 ci2 序齒), to go by ages; _tsu ts'eung_ (zeoi6 coeng4 序庠), a district college; _tsu sz' t'o, tong_ (zeoi6 si6 to5 dong3 序事妥當) everything in its order. (Page 595 敍叙) Similar to the preceding (序); to arrange, to order, to set in their proper places; order, series, rank (Kangxi 敍: 次第也, 敍謂次敍); _t'in tsu_ (tin1 zeoi6 天序) the five social relations. 【序】 (zjoX) (_noun-like_) side-wall (_noun-like_) learning-hall (_noun-like_) order (_noun-like_) preface

U+6558 叙 敍 (F1.48 敘) 會也又陳述也又天敘五倫也 (Page 595 敍叙; here normalised to 敘) to meet, to converse; to discourse or argue upon; _seung tsu_ (soeng1 zeoi6 相敘) or _tsu t'am_ (zeoi6 taam4 敘談), to converse; _i tsu_ (ji5 zeoi6 議敘) to deliberate upon the proper rewards;

U+7E0B (F2.57 縋) 以繩懸物而垂下曰縋 (Page 33* 縋) A rope (Kangxi: 縋繩索也); to let down by a cord (Kangxi: 以繩有所懸也); _chui lok_ (zeoi6 lok6 縋落) to let down, as into a well, or over a wall; _chui un_ (zeoi6 jyun4 縋鉛), lead put in sycee. 【縋】 (drjweH) (_noun-like_) (hung) cord (_verb-like_) to hang cord

U+7F6A 辠 (F1.48 罪) 罰也 (F1.48 辠) 同上 (Page 598 罪辠) A bamboo net for fish (Kangxi 罪: 捕魚竹罔); to become entangled in the net of the law; trespass, crime, sin, fault; a violation of the rules of the land or decorum (Kangxi 辠: 辠犯法也...秦以辠似皇字改爲罪); to criminate, to regard as a criminal; punishment, retribution; _tak tsui u t'in_ (dak1 zeoi6 jyu1 tin1 得罪於天), to sin against Heaven; _tsui kwo_ (zeoi6 gwo3 罪過) a fault, a crime; _~~un~~ tsui yau ho un_ (~~jyun4~~ zeoi6 jau5 ho2 jyun4 ~~？~~罪有可原), the crime can be exculpated; _fat tsui_ (faat3 zeoi6 法罪) to punish crime; _she tsui_ (se3 zeoi6 赦罪) to forgive sins; _kwan tsui_ (gwan1 zeoi6 軍罪) exile to Ili (？？); _shu tsui_ (syu3 zeoi6 恕罪) excuse me; _fan tsui_ (faan6 zeoi6 犯罪) to offend the laws; _shuk tsui_ (suk6 zeoi6 贖罪) to redeem or ransom criminals; _fu king ts'ing tsui_ (fu6 ging1 cing2 zeoi6 負荊請罪) to take a rod and request punishment --- an affected phrase of officers; _fui tsui_ (fui3 zeoi6 悔罪) to repent of sins; _tsui sho ying tak_ (zeoi6 so2 jing1 dak1 罪所應得) a punishment well deserved. Williams' 罪過: Also a polite (interjectional) apology. 【罪】 (dzwojX) (_noun-like_) crime, offence

U+805A May appear as either ⿱取乑 or ⿱取⿸⿹亻人人 (assumed here), depending on the font. 冣 (F1.48 聚) 會也又積聚 (Page 595 聚) To assemble, to congregate (Kangxi: 會也), to gather (Kangxi: 斂也); to meet; to tend to, to concur; to incline towards, popular (Kangxi: 聚謂衆所宜); to collect, to dwell together; a dwelling-place, a village (Kangxi: 居也邑落也, 聚謂村落也); a conjunction, a meeting; a place of meeting; _tsu shau_ (zeoi6 sau2 聚首), a concourse, a gathering of friends; _'m tsu ts'oi_ (m4 zeoi6 coi4 唔聚財) not a lucky place; not a moneymaking man; _tsu po t'ong_ (zeoi6 bou2 tong4 聚寶堂), the shrine of Plutus at the door; _tsu ui_ (zeoi6 wui6 聚會) to call a meeting 【聚】 (dzjuX, dzjuH) (_verb-like_) to gather
U+85C7 (F1.48 藇) 美也 (Page 595 藇) Good, agreeable, pleasant (Kangxi: 美貌), as liquor. 【藇】 (zjoX) (_adjective-like_) pleasant, fine

U+8D05 zeoi3 (per 《廣韻》之芮切) (F2.57 贅) 謂以物質錢也又男附女家謂之贅壻 (Page 34* 贅) To pledge, to pawn (Kangxi: 以物質錢); connected with, hanging to (Kangxi: 贅屬也, 贅猶綴也謂繫綴而屬之), an excrescence (Kangxi: 贅屬也橫生一肉屬著體也), a useless appendage; a blemish; unsuitable; tautology, verbiage (Kangxi: 言煩也); a postscript; _yap chui_ (jap6 zeoi6 入贅) to live in a wife's family; _chui lui_ (zeoi6 leoi6 贅累) reiterated, verbose; _chui in_ (zeoi6 jin4 贅言), to repeat; _chui k'ap_ (zeoi6 kap6 贅及) a postscript. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𢪱 to 物. 【贅】 (tsywejH) (_verb-like_) to pledge (_verb-like_) to annex (_noun-like_) excrescence, (_met._) superfluous
U+8FA0 (F1.48 罪) 罰也 (F1.48 辠) 同上 (Page 598 罪辠)
U+968A (F2.57 墜) 落也 (F2.57 隊) 同上 (Page 33* 墜隊)
U+9C6E (F1.48 鱮) 魴鱮魚名 (Page 595 鱮) The tench or roach; a large, coarse species of carp found in the rivers of Kiangsu (江蘇). The silver carp. 【鱮】 (zjoX) (_noun-like_) tench

zeon1.text: U+554D (F2.52 啍) 啍啍重語貌 (Page 36* 啍)
U+5C0A (F2.51 樽) 酒樽 (F2.51 鐏) 同上 (F2.51 罇) 同上 (F2.51 尊) 同上 (Page 600 樽罇尊) Fan Wan entry: Normalised ⿰𦈢尊 to 罇.

zyun1.text: U+5C0A (F2.19 尊) 尊長崇也重也貴也敬也 (Page 604 尊) Honorable, eminent, respected, noble (Kangxi: 高稱也, 重也貴也 etc.); a term of respect, your; to honor, to respect, to dignify, to venerate (Kangxi: 敬也); _ling tsun_ (ling6 zyun1 令尊) your father; _tsun ka_ (zyun1 gaa3 尊駕) your honor; you, Sir; _tsun sing_ (zyun1 sing3 尊姓) your surname; _tsun fu_ (zyun1 fu2 尊府), your house; _tsun fu yan_ (zyun1 fu1 jan4 尊夫人), your wife; _wai lo pat tsun_ (wai4 lou5 bat1 zyun1 為老不尊), old but without self-respect; _tsun king_ (zyun1 ging3 尊敬) to venerate; _chi tsun_ (zi3 zyun1 至尊), the emperor; _tsun cheung_ (zyun1 zoeng2 尊長), respected, elderly persons; _yat tsun fat kom yeung_ (jat1 zyun1 fat6 gam2 joeng6-2 一尊佛噉樣) stupid as a statue of Budha. Classifier for objects that are heavy or not readily movable.

zeon1.text: U+5C6F May appear with first stroke ㇐ (assumed here) or ㇒, depending on the font. 迍 (for "difficult" sense) (F2.52 屯) 高屯難也屈也 (Page 36* 屯) ~~Budding plants;~~ ``difficulty (Kangxi: 難也);`` difficult to proceed (Kangxi: 屯邅難行不進貌); avaricious (Kangxi: 吝也); pressed down; the 3d of the diagrams. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing bottom-left in 高. Williams' "Budding plants": This is a misreading of Kangxi 象艸木之初生 as a definition, when it is simply the 《說文》 glyph explanation. 【屯-unvoiced】 (trwin) (_adjective-like_) difficult

U+699B (F2.52 榛) 榛檜木名 (Page 601 榛) A fruit like the hazel or filbert, smaller than the chestnut (Kangxi: 果實如小栗); ~~wood, brushwood~~ ``a type of tree`` (Kangxi: 木也); thorny bushes, as in thickset hedges (Kangxi: 一曰蕪也). Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chun_ per 《廣韻》側詵切. Fan Wan entry: Changed 橧 to 檜. 【榛】 (tsrin) (_noun-like_) hazel

U+6A3D 尊 罇 (F2.51 樽) 酒樽 (F2.51 鐏) 同上 (F2.51 罇) 同上 (F2.51 尊) 同上 (Page 600 樽罇尊) A vase for libations; a vase for wine (Kangxi 樽: 酒器也); a bottle, a decanter; a vase, a jar; full, flourishing foliage (Kangxi 樽: ...樽乃林木茂盛之字); to stop (Kangxi 樽: 止也); _yat tsun tsau_ (jat1 zeon1 zau2 一樽酒), a bottle of spirits; _fa tsun_ (faa1 zeon1 花樽), a flower-vase; _hoi tsun_ (hoi1 zeon1 開樽), open the bottle. Fan Wan entry: Normalised ⿰𦈢尊 to 罇. 【樽】 (tswon) (_noun-like_) wine-vase 【樽】 (zeon1) A bottle. 樽豉油 (zeon1 si6 jau4): "The bottle of soy sauce." 樽水 (zeon1 seoi2): "The bottle of water." 樽裝水 (zeon1 zong1 seoi2): "Bottled water." 水樽 (seoi2 zeon1): "Water bottle." 膠樽 (gaau1 zeon1): "Plastic bottle." 酒樽 (zau2 zeon1): "Wine bottle." 玻璃樽 (bo1 lei4-1 zeon1): "Glass bottle." A vase. 花樽 (faa1 zeon1): "A flower vase."

U+6D25 (F2.51 津) 濟渡處又津液 (Page 600 津) A ford (Kangxi: 渡也, 津是渡處), a ferry; a landing-place, a ghaut; to ford, to cross a stream; to imbue, to moisten (Kangxi: 潤也), to soften by soaking; ~~to split wood by the hands;~~ spittle; _tsun leung_ (zeon1 loeng4 津梁), ends of a bridge; _tsun yat_ (zeon1 jat6 津溢) saliva; _tsun hau_ (zeon1 hau2 津口), a ferry; _kwan tsun_ (gwaan1 zeon1 關津), excise stations, guard-houses; _man tsun_ (man6 zeon1 問津), to ask the way; _shang tsun_ (sang1 zeon1 生津), to produce saliva; _t'in tsun_ (tin1 zeon1 天津), the constellation Cygnus; the port of Peking (北京); _tsun tsun yau mi_ (zeon1 zeon1 jau5 mei6 津津有味) I relish it more and more. 【津】 (tsin) (_noun-like_) ford (_verb-like_) to moisten

U+6EB1 (F2.69 溱) 水名 (Page 601 溱) A small stream in Honan (河南) (Kangxi: 水名在河南), one of the headwaters of the R. ~~Hwai~~ ``Hwui`` (Kangxi: 入~~淮~~``匯``); _tsun tsun_ (zeon1 zeon1 溱溱), numerous, abundant (Kangxi: 溱溱衆也, 溱溱盛貌); at ease, loose (Kangxi: 舒也). Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chun_ per 《廣韻》側詵切. 【溱】 (tsrin) (_adjective-like_) multitudinous, abundant
U+7A80 (Page 36* 窀) To inter (Kangxi: 窀穸下棺也); _chun tsik_ (zeon1 zik6 窀穸) the grave; to put away in a vault or grave.
U+7F47 (F2.51 樽) 酒樽 (F2.51 鐏) 同上 (F2.51 罇) 同上 (F2.51 尊) 同上 (Page 600 樽罇尊) Fan Wan entry: Normalised ⿰𦈢尊 to 罇.

U+80AB (F2.52 肫) 懇至貌 (Page 36* 肫) Bones of the face (Kangxi: 面頯也); earnest, sincere (Kangxi: 懇誠貌); _chun chun_ (zeon1 zeon1 肫肫), ~~decided, firm~~ ``earnest, sincere``. Bird gizzard. 【肫】 (tsywin) (_adjective-like_) earnest

U+81FB 溱 (F2.69 臻) 至也 (Page 601 溱) to reach to (Kangxi 溱: 至也與臻同); (Page 601 臻) To arrive or extend to (Kangxi: 至也, 及也); the utmost, the highest degree; many, a multitude (Kangxi: 衆也); to collect, to congregate (Kangxi: 聚也); _pak fuk p'ing tsun_ (baak3 fuk1 ping4 zeon1 百福駢臻), may all happiness come here. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chun_ per 《廣韻》側詵切. 【臻】 (tsrin) (_verb-like_) to arrive

U+84C1 ceon4 (per 《集韻》鋤臻切 for "tree-thicket" sense) (F2.69 蓁) 美盛貌 (Page 601 蓁) Abundant, exuberant herbage (Kangxi: 草盛貌); bushy trees (Kangxi: 與榛通木叢生也); collected, an accumulation of (Kangxi: 積聚之貌); _k'i ip tsun tsun_ (kei4 jip6 zeon1 zeon1 其葉蓁蓁), how abundant is the foliage. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chun_ per 《廣韻》側詵切. 【蓁】 (tsrin) (_adjective-like_) abundant, luxuriant

U+8AC4 啍 zeon3 (per Kangxi 之閏切 for "assist" sense) (F2.52 諄) 詳語貌又懇誠也 (Page 36* 啍) Garrulous (Kangxi 啍: 同諄); _chun chun_ (zeon1 zeon1 啍啍), to reiterate, to say over and over (Kangxi 啍: 啍啍多言者也). (Page 36* 諄) To inculcate, to tell repeatedly (Kangxi: 吿曉之熟也, 誨言重複也); _chun chun in chi_ (zeon1 zeon1 jin4 zi1 諄諄言之), to make plain, to impress on one. Sincere, earnest (Kangxi: 誠懇貌). To assist (Kangxi: 佐也). 【諄】 (tsywin, tsywinH) (_adverb-like_) earnestly, repeatedly (_verb-like_) to assist
U+8FCD (F2.52 迍) 迍邅 (Page 36* 迍) A vain effort to progress (Kangxi: 迍邅也); _chun chin_ (zeon1 zin1 迍邅), making no advance, unsuccessful.

U+9075 zyun1 (uncommon vernacular) (F2.51 遵) 循也率也 (Page 601 遵) To follow (Kangxi: 循也) a superior; to comply with, to obey, to conform to, to act as required, to yield; to induce to follow, to influence to obey (Kangxi: 率也 etc.); obedience, acquiescence; _tsun chi_ (zeon1 zi2 遵旨), to obey the ~~emperior's~~ ``emperor's`` behests; _yam ts'z' yam tsun_ (jam1 ci2 jam1 zeon1 欽此欽遵), ~~to stand in awe and obey~~ ``a phrase that concludes imperial edicts``; _tsun fat_ (zeon1 faat3 遵法) to obey the laws; _tsun i_ (zeon1 ji1 遵依), to cordially receive. Williams' 欽此欽遵: Better rendered "the revered edict is thus, the revered edict is to be obeyed!" 【遵】 (tswin) (_verb-like_) to go along, to adhere
