zeon2.text: U+5118 盡 zeon6 (common) (F2.53 儘) 皆也 (Page 601 儘) ~~Same as _tsun_ (zeon6) 盡 to finish;~~ ~~empty;~~ all (Kangxi 盡: 皆也); the utmost degree (Kangxi 盡: 極也, 盡之也); _tsun ho i_ (zeon2 ho2 ji5 儘可以), it can be done; _tsun kin_ (zeon2 gin3 儘見) seen it through, seen the whole. The common reading zeon6 is reinforced by modern use of 盡快, 盡力, 盡量, etc. instead of 儘快, 儘力, 儘量, etc. Williams' "Same as ...": This is a misreading of Kangxi 同盡, which is only intended for the reading _tsun_ (zeon2). To allow, to let (Kangxi 盡: 任也, 縱令也, 俗作儘). 【盡-unvoiced】 (tsinX) (_adverb-like_) completely

U+51C6 (F2.53 准) 信也實也 (Page 36* 准) To permit, to approve; to allow, to decide; to receive; on, upon, as a day; _chun chong_ (zeon2 zong6 准狀) grant the petition; _chun hang_ (zeon2 hang4 准行), allowed to be done; _chun sun_ (zeon2 seon3 准信) to believe; _chun i shap yat_ (zeon2 ji5 sap6 jat6 准以十日) permission for ten days; _chun shat kom to_ (zeon2 sat6 gam3 do1 准實咁多), worth just that.
U+51D6 (F2.53 凖) 凖則平也擬也 (Page 36* 凖)

U+6E96 准 凖 (F2.53 凖) 凖則平也擬也 (Page 36* 准; here normalised to 準) _chun pi_ (zeon2 bei6 準備) to prepare for; Used for the following (凖). (Page 36* 凖; here normalised to 準) To equalize, to fix, to adjust (Kangxi 準: 謂準擊平正之也); to level, to measure; a rule, a ~~guage~~ ``gauge``, a plumb, a line (Kangxi 準: 平也); even, exact, just (Kangxi 準: 均也); to run true, as a watch; _yau chun t'au_ (jau5 zeon2 tau4 有準頭), he has the right ~~guage~~ ``gauge``, skilled in; _chun chit_ (zeon2 zit3 準折) to barter; _chun shing_ (zeon2 sing4 準繩), a marking-line; _chun tsak_ (zeon2 zak1 準則) a custom, a rule; _chun 'm chun_ (zeon2 m4 zeon2 準唔準), is it exact? To imitate, to emulate (Kangxi: 則也倣也). 【準】 (tsywinX) (_noun-like_) water-level (_adjective-like_) level, even, (_met._) standard

U+71FC 㶳 藎 (for "remainder" sense) zeon6 (per 《廣韻》徐刃切) (F2.53 燼) 火燭之灰也燒後曰燼 (Page 601 燼) The ashes (Kangxi 㶳: 火餘也), embers (Kangxi 㶳: 火餘木), snuff (Kangxi 㶳: 灺也, 燭餘也), or residue after combustion; a quenched brand; the residuum; the relics, what is left, as of a conquered people (Kangxi 燼: 燼遺民); _fa wai fui tsun_ (faa3 wai4 fui1 zeon2 化為灰燼), all turned to ashes; _u tsun_ (jyu4 zeon2 餘燼), the remains, as of embers. (Page 601 藎) Similar to the last (燼); a residue (Kangxi 藎: 藎餘也); 【燼】 (zinH) (_noun-like_) cinder, embers

zeon6.text: U+76E1 尽 (F2.54 盡) 空也竭也終也悉也 (F2.54 尽) 同上 (Page 602 盡尽) An empty vessel (Kangxi: 器中空也); to exhaust, to use all (Kangxi: 竭也); ~~to indulge, as one's grief;~~ a quantity used up, a work brought to an end; made an end of (Kangxi: 止也, 終也), as life; finished, achieved, empty, exhausted; all, entirely, fully, wholly (Kangxi: 悉也); to do to the utmost, with the whole of; a superlative, the uttermost, extreme; _tsun ngo sam_ (zeon6 ngo5 sam1 盡我心), obeying with my whole heart; _tsun ti kom to_ (zeon6 dei6-2 gam3 do1 盡地咁多), there's only so much; _tsun ti tso_ (zeon6 dei6-2 zou6 盡地做) ~~use it all up then~~ ``do to the extent possible``; _yung tsun sam ki_ (jung6 zeon6 sam1 gei1 用盡心機), employing all my abilities; _ka ts'oi san tsun_ (gaa1 coi4 saan3 zeon6 家財散盡) the whole estate is dissipated; _shat ko tsun tsut_ (saat3 go3 zeon6 zyut6 殺個盡絕) slew them all; _tsun ut_ (zeon6 jyut6 盡月) the twelfth month; _tsun ut pi ni_ (zeon6 jyut6 bei2 nei5 盡月畀你), I'll pay you at the ~~at the~~ month's end; _kom tse pat tsun_ (gam2 ze6 bat1 zeon6 感謝不盡) I cannot express all my thanks; _tsun chik_ (zeon6 zik1 盡職) to perform one's official duties fully; _hang tsun_ (haang4 zeon6 行盡) gone through the whole, gone all over; _tsun ni po_ (zeon6 ni1 bou1 盡呢煲), try this one chance; one chance left; _tsun hai shui tsik_ (zeon6 hai6 seoi2 zik1 盡係水漬) all are water-soaked; _tsz' tsun_ (zi6 zeon6 自盡) put an end to himself; _tsun t'au_ (zeon6 tau4 盡頭), gone to the end, at the extreme; _mo k'ung tsun_ (mou4 kung4 zeon6 無窮盡) inexhaustible; _tsun tsit_ (zeon6 zit3 盡節) died a martyr to chastity. Williams' 行盡: Changed _hang_ (hang4) to vernacular reading _hang_ (haang4). 【盡-voiced】 (dzinX) (_verb-like_) to exhaust, to complete 【盡地】 (zeon6 dei6-2) Fully, to the extent possible, all in.

zeon2.text: U+85CE zeon6 (per 《廣韻》徐刃切) (F2.53 藎) 忠愛曰藎臣 (Page 601 藎) a plant whose roots afford a yellow dye (Kangxi: 藎草一名黃草一名盭草可染黃); ~~to advance constantly~~ ``advanced, promoted`` (Kangxi: 藎進也); sincere, as in attachment to (Kangxi: 藎忠愛之篤進進無已也); _tsun shan_ (zeon2 san4 藎臣), or _chung tsun_ (zung1 zeon2 忠藎), a loyal minister. 【藎】 (zinH) (_adjective-like_) promoted, faithful

U+8D10 賮 zeon6 (per 《廣韻》徐刃切) (F2.53 贐) 送行之禮曰贐 (Page 601 贐) Precious things (Kangxi 賮: 財貨也); presents given to friends going on a journey (Kangxi 賮: 送行財幣也); _tsun i_ (zeon2 ji4 贐儀), parting gifts of ceremony. 【贐】 (zinH) (_noun-like_) travelling-gift

zeon3.text: U+4FCA 儁 雋 駿 (F2.53 儁) 知過千人 (F2.53 俊) 才過千人 (Page 602 雋 read _Tsui_ (zeoi3); here corrected to _Tsun_ (zeon3) and normalised to 俊) Also ~~read _tsui_ (zeoi3)~~ a hero (Kangxi 雋: 與儁通). (Page 602 儁; here normalised to 俊) Valiant, brave; superior, excelling others in skill or wisdom (Kangxi 儁: 卓特也); to overcome (Kangxi 儁: 得儁曰克, 勝也); unusual, strange (Kangxi 儁: 異也). (Page 602 俊) Similar to the last (儁) (Kangxi 俊: 與儁...通); superior, remarkable, one of a thousand (Kangxi 俊: 材過千人也 etc.); eminent; _ying tsun_ (jing1 zeon3 英俊) a brave man, a young hero; _kw'an kwai tsun sau_ (kwan4 gwai3 zeon3 sau3 群季俊秀) all distinguished for accomplishments and beauty. (Page 602 駿; here normalised to 俊) excelling (Kangxi 駿: 與俊同英儁也); Fan Wan entry: The bottom-right component of the headword 儁 is printed with the central horizontal stroke wide and joining onto the two vertical strokes on the sides. Williams' "read _tsui_": This is a mistaken interpretation of Kangxi 將遂切音醉 as being applicable to the "hero" sense (與儁通). It is only applicable to the earlier "pestle" sense (本作檇). 【俊】 (zinH) (_noun-like_) eminent-man (_adjective-like_) eminent

U+5101 (F2.53 儁) 知過千人 (Page 602 儁) Fan Wan entry: The bottom-right component of the headword 儁 is printed with the central horizontal stroke wide and joining onto the two vertical strokes on the sides.

U+5CFB 駿 (Page 602 峻) High, lofty, steep, as mountains (Kangxi: 高也, 險也, 大也); impetuous, hasty, stern, severe (Kangxi: 峭也速也嚴急也), as one's temper; _tsun tsai_ (zeon3 zai1 峻劑), a powerful medicine. Fan Wan entry: The headword 峻 is a misprint of 竣. 【峻】 (swinH) (_adjective-like_) lofty

U+6422 May appear as either ⿰扌晉 (assumed here) or ⿰扌晋, depending on the font. 晉 縉 (in 搢紳 spelt 縉紳) 薦 (F2.54 縉) 居官曰縉紳插笏于大帶之間曰縉 (F2.54 搢) 同上 (Page 602 晉晋; here normalised to 搢) to stick (Kangxi 晉: 晉插也), as into a case or about the person; (Page 602 搢) To stick into, to insert``, to tuck`` (Kangxi: 插也); to put in, as into a socket; to shake (Kangxi: 振也). ~~Used for the next (縉).~~ (Page 602 縉; here normalised to 搢) ~~to wrap around;~~ ~~to gird, as with a sash;~~ _tsun shan_ (zeon3 san1 搢紳), the "~~red~~ ``tucking`` sashes" are the gentry or literati; _tsun shan ts'un shu_ (zeon3 san1 cyun4 syu1 搢紳全書), the red book; _tsun shan sin shang_ (zeon3 san1 sin1 saang1 搢紳先生), a literary gentleman. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 千 to 于. Williams' "Used for the next": It is the other way round (縉 is used for 搢). Williams' "to wrap around", "to gird": These are incorrect. Here, 縉 is used for 搢, meaning "insert" or "tuck" (Kangxi 縉: 扱也). Williams' 搢紳先生: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). 【搢】 (tsinH) (_verb-like_) to tuck (in)

U+6649 晋 (F2.54 晉) 進也又國名又卦名 (F2.54 晋) 同上 (Page 602 晉晋) To increase, to flourish, to grow, as the sun makes all things (Kangxi: 進也日出萬物進也, 晉進也, 以今釋古古之晉字卽以進長爲義); to curb in a horse (Kangxi: 晉猶抑也); name of a dynasty, divided into the Western and Eastern Tsin, A.D. 265--419; a feudal state (Kangxi: 國名), now the southern part of Shansi (山西). To rise, to promote. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【晉】 (tsinH) (_verb-like_) to advance
U+6718 (F2.53 朘) 削也竭也 (Page 602 朘)

U+6D5A 濬 (for "deep", "dig", and proper-name senses) (F2.54 浚) 深也 (F2.54 濬) 同上 (Page 602 浚濬) Deep, profound (Kangxi: 深也), as water; to dig a channel, to deepen a water-course; deep, serious, as reverence (Kangxi: 稟浚敬也); abstruse; well read; to use part of, to employ (Kangxi: 抒也, 抒取出之也); a district in Honan (河南); _tsun tseng_ (zeon3 zeng2 浚井), to deepen a well. Some modern Cantonese dictionaries prescribe the reading seon3 for the proper-name sense of 浚, presumably per 《廣韻》私閏切. To me, it does not make sense for *only* the proper-name sense to get this treatment, when 《廣韻》 also has 私閏切 for 濬, explained 深也. 【浚】 (swinH) (_verb-like_) to scoop (_adjective-like_) deep
U+6FEC (F2.54 浚) 深也 (F2.54 濬) 同上 (Page 602 浚濬)
U+7468 May appear as either ⿰𤣩晉 (assumed here) or ⿰𤣩晋, depending on the font. (F2.54 瑨) 美石也 (Page 602 瑨) A pebble or secondary gem (Kangxi: 次玉); a stone which may be considered precious (Kangxi: 美石).
U+756F (F2.54 畯) 田畯治農之官 (Page 602 畯) An officer who superintended the fields (Kangxi: 農夫也一曰典田官, 田畯田大夫也); a land-office; a husbandman, a peasant. 【畯】 (tswinH) (_noun-like_) farm-overseer

U+7743 zyun1 (per 《集韻》遵全切 for name sense) (F2.54 睃) 明也 (Page 602 睃) To see (Kangxi: 視也); a man's name (Kangxi: 人名); ~~_Tsun Tsun cheuk_ (zeon3 zeon3 zoek3 睃晉灼) a hero who lived in the Han (漢) dynasty.~~ Williams' 睃晉灼: This is a misreading of Kangxi's example, 《前漢諸侯王表》魯文王睃晉灼讀. This example should be punctuated as "魯文王睃。晉灼讀。", with 晉灼讀 indicating that the reading _tsun_ (zyun1) is the pronunciation of 睃 according to the scholar 晉灼.

U+7AE3 ceon1 (per 《廣韻》七倫切) (F2.53 竣) 退也完也 (Page 602 竣) To stop work, to finish, to complete a task (Kangxi: 止也); completed, done (Kangxi: 事畢也); to stand still, to wait aside, to retire (Kangxi: 退立也); ~~proud;~~ _ko tsun_ (gou3 zeon3 告竣) to make known the completion; _tsun kung_ (zeon3 gung1 竣工), to finish the work; _sz' tsun_ (si6 zeon3 事竣) the affair is completed. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 峻 to 竣. Williams' "proud": This is a misreading of Kangxi 倨也. Here, 倨 is used for 踞. According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, 倨也 as seen in 《廣韻》 is evidence that the explanation of 竣 should be 居也. 【竣】 (tshwin) (_verb-like_) to stand down, to cease
U+7E09 May appear as either ⿰糹晉 (assumed here) or ⿰糹晋, depending on the font. (F2.54 縉) 居官曰縉紳插笏于大帶之間曰縉 (F2.54 搢) 同上 (Page 602 縉) Carnation colored silk (Kangxi: 帛赤色), a light red (Kangxi: 縉淺赤色也);

U+9032 (F2.53 進) 登也升也向前去也 (Page 601 進) To advance, to enter, to go up or on, to get on (Kangxi: 登也, 升也, 前也); to present, to bring forward, to introduce, to promote, to recommend (Kangxi: 薦也); to make progress in (Kangxi: 效也); to come near (Kangxi: 近也); a promotion, an advance; a division of a hong (行); _tsun kung_ (zeon3 gung3 進貢) to bring in tribute; _ch'o tsun po_ (co1 zeon3 bou6 初進步) the first step ahead --- to be a siuts'ai (秀才); _tsun hok_ (zeon3 hok6 進學) to enter school; _ki tsun sham_ (gei2 zeon3 sam1 幾進深), of how many divistons deep? _tsun t'ui leung nan_ (zeon3 teoi3 loeng5 naan4 進退兩難), hard to advance or retreat, embarrassed; _tsun shan chi kai_ (zeon3 san1 zi1 gai3 進身之計) a contrivance to advance one's self; _ko tsun_ (gou1 zeon3 高進) high attainments, as in degrees; _chung tsun sz'_ (zung3 zeon3 si6 中進士) to reach the third literary degree; _tsun fo tai kat_ (zeon3 fo2 daai3 gat1 進伙帶吉) let prosperity come on the new house --- said when moving into another dwelling; _yan tsun_ (jan5 zeon3 引進) to bring fo``r``ward; _mo cheung tsun_ (mou5 zoeng2 zeon3 冇長進) he makes no headway, he does not improve; _~~tsin~~ ``ts'in`` tsun_ (cin4 zeon3 前進) to make progress. Williams' 前進: Corrected _tsin_ (zin1) to _ts'in_ (cin4), and corrected _tsun_ (zeon6) to _tsun_ (zeon3). 【進】 (tsinH) (_verb-like_) to advance, to put forth

U+942B zyun1 (per 《廣韻》子泉切) (F2.53 鐫) 雕刻也 (Page 601 鐫) To engrave, to carve (Kangxi: 一曰琢石也); to cut, to chissel; to censure or degrade an officer (Kangxi: 謫也 etc.); _san tsun_ (san1 zeon3 新鐫) a new edition, as of blocks; _tsun k'ap_ (zeon3 kap1 鐫級) to reduce an officer in rank; _tsun shek_ (zeon3 sek6 鐫石) to cut in stone, as an epitaph. Fan Wan entry: The bottom-right component of the headword 鐫 is printed with the central horizontal stroke wide and joining onto the two vertical strokes on the sides.
U+96CB (F2.53 雋) 永也味之長也 (Page 602 雋) Fan Wan entry: The bottom component of the headword 雋 is printed with the central horizontal stroke wide and joining onto the two vertical strokes on the sides.

U+9915 (F2.54 餕) 食餘物也 (Page 602 餕) Hot food (Kangxi: 熟食也); to eat the remnants of an offering (Kangxi: 祭畢食神之餘) or a meal (Kangxi: 凡食人之餘及日晚食饌之餘皆云餕); the leavings on the table (Kangxi: 食之餘也). 【餕】 (tswinH) (_noun-like_) leftover

U+99FF (F2.54 駿) 良馬 (Page 602 駿) A steed, a stately, fine horse (Kangxi: 馬之美稱); dignified, majestic; large (Kangxi: 大也); rapid (Kangxi: 速也, 駿者猶迅也, 駿疾也); _tsun fat_ (zeon3 faat3 駿發) quick, as in getting rich. 【駿】 (tswinH) (_noun-like_) steed (_adverb-like_) rapidly (_adjective-like_) grand, lofty

zeon6.text: U+5C3D (F2.54 盡) 空也竭也終也悉也 (F2.54 尽) 同上 (Page 602 盡尽)

zi1.text: U+5179 (F2.34 兹) 此也 (Page 610 茲兹)

U+5431 (Page 38|n81 吱) A colloquial final, like 咯 _lok_ (lok3) expressing certainty; or that an act was immaterial; _kom tsau ho chi_ (gam2 zau6 hou2 zi1 噉就好吱), then he would be well off; _chi chi hai_ (zi1 zi1 hai6 吱吱係) certainly so.

U+54A8 諮 (for "consultation" senses) (F2.34 諮) 詢也 (F2.34 咨) 咨嗟咨謀 (Page 611 咨) To deliberate, to plan, to consult (Kangxi: 謀事曰咨, 咨謀也); to state or ask in writing; a document, a dispatch between equals in rank; an interjection, ah, oh! (Kangxi: 嗟咨𨲠也, 咨嗟也) to sigh; _tsz' man_ (zi1 man4 咨文), an official paper; _ho wai tsz' tse_ (ho4 wai4 zi1 ze1 何為咨嗟), why do you lament so? _i tsz'_ (ji4 zi1 移咨) to send a dispatch. (Page 611 諮) Used for the last (咨) (Kangxi 諮: 與咨同). To deliberate, to consult, to devise a way (Kangxi 諮: 謀也); to ask about (Kangxi 諮: 問也). 【咨】 (tsij) (_verb-like_) to consult (_interjection_) sigh

U+59FF (F2.34 姿) 姿稟形態也 (Page 612 姿) Manner, gait, form, carriage, air (Kangxi: 態也); beautiful, graceful; agreeable parts; natural endowments; _fung tsz'_ (fung1 zi1 丰姿) a fine figure, shapely; _tsz' shik_ (zi1 sik1 姿色) a beauty; _t'in tsz' kwok shik_ (tin1 zi1 gwok3 sik1 天姿國色) a beauty who can overthrow a state, like Yang Kwei-fei (楊貴妃) of the T'ang (唐) dynasty.

U+5B5C 孳 孶 (F2.34 孜) 汲也勤也勉也 (Page 611 孶) unwearied, repeated, diligent, in which it is the same as the next (孜) (Kangxi 孳: 與孜同); (Page 611 孜) Unceasing, unwearied effort, untiring diligence (Kangxi: 一曰力篤愛也勤也); self-denying attachment; _tsz' tsz' wai shin_ (zi1 zi1 wai4 sin6 孜孜為善) ever striving to be good. 【孜】 (tsi) (_adjective-like_) industrious

U+5B73 孶 (F2.34 孳) 生息也亦作孜 (Page 611 孶; here normalised to 孳) To bear, to produce (Kangxi: 孳息也); to suckle, to nourish, said of animals; to grow, to increase (Kangxi 孳: 汲汲生也); ardent, affectionate, loving; strong love, like a mother's; _tsz' sik_ (zi1 sik1 孳息) to bear young; _man mat tsz' shang_ (maan6 mat6 zi1 sang1 萬物孳生), all things gradually multiply. 【孳-level】 (tsi) (_verb-like_) to breed, to multiply

zi6.text: U+5B73 孶 Transformation(?) of the breast (during pregnancy?) (Kangxi: 乳化也). 【孳-departing】 (dziH) (_verb-like_) to mammarise

zi1.text: U+5B76 (F2.34 孳) 生息也亦作孜 (Page 611 孶)

U+5DF5 卮 (F1.36 巵) 酒杯 (Page 38|n81 巵) A goblet (Kangxi: 圜器也); a cup holding four gills (Kangxi: 巵酒器也受四升); ~~a syphon;~~ ~~a vessel for serving rations;~~ _tsau chi_ (zau2 zi1 酒巵), ~~a wine syphon~~. Williams' "a vessel for serving rations": This is a misreading of Kangxi 所以節飮食. Here, 節飮食 is the moderation of consumption for self-cultivation, rather than the rationing of food/drink due to limited resources. 【巵】 (tsye) (_noun-like_) goblet

U+652F (F1.36 支) 持也分也又支离又支干又宗支 (Page 38|n81 支) To branch, to diverge; ~~to hold;~~ to give, to pay; to prop, to succor (Kangxi: 持也, 支拄也); a branch; posterity, descendants; a sept or tribe; to attend to; to bear up, to stand firm; diverging, parting (Kangxi: 一曰分也); the twelve horary characters (Kangxi: 十二支辰名); ~~to measure;~~ the 65th radical ("branch"); _pau chi_ (baau1 zi1 包支), responsible for; _chi kang_ (zi1 gaang1 支更), a watchman; _yat chung chi yung_ (jat6 zung1 zi1 jung6 日中支用) daily outlays and expenses; _chi k'ap_ (zi1 kap1 支給) to give out; _chi kung ngan_ (zi1 gung1 ngan4 支工銀), to pay wages; _chi li_ (zi1 lei4 支離), irrelevant, not to the point, a liar; _chi siu_ (zi1 siu1 支消), to expend, use for expenses; _chi p'ai_ (zi1 paai3 支派) descendants, a tribe; _wang chi_ (waang4 zi1 橫支), a side branch, a concubine's child. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 千 to 干. Williams' 支更: Corrected _kang_ (gang1) to _kang_ (gaang1). To reckon (Kangxi: 支猶計也). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【支】 (tsye) (_noun-like_) branch, limb (_verb-like_) to support (_verb-like_) to pay (for) (_verb-like_) to reckon

U+679D (F1.36 枝) 枝幹 (Page 38|n81 枝) A branch (Kangxi: 木別生條也), a twig; a slender post; to scatter, to branch off (Kangxi: 散也); a tributary of a river (Kangxi: 水別于他水入于大水及海者命曰枝水); a classifier of slender things, as pencils, flowers, pens, &c.; _chi kon_ (zi1 gon3 枝幹) trunk and branches; _yat chi fa_ (jat1 zi1 faa1 一枝花), a flower, a pretty girl; _yat chi kwan_ (jat1 zi1 gwan1 一枝軍), a detachment of troops; _pok chi_ (bok3 zi1 駁枝), to graft; _chi ip_ (zi1 jip6 枝葉) branches and leaves; _sun chi_ (syun1 zi1 酸枝), Chinese ebony; _shan chi_ (saan1 zi1 山枝), wild _sun-chi_ (酸枝) wood, a hard timber. Williams' "pencils": In the (now) dated sense of a brush. 【枝】 (tsye) (_noun-like_) branch (_classifier_)

U+6894 (F1.36 梔) 梔子核可染物 (Page 39|n82 梔) A tree used to dye yellow, a species of Gardenia (Kangxi: 黃木可染); _wong chi_ (wong4 zi1 黃梔), the becho nut, used in dyeing, and as a medicine in fevers. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𣑱 to 染.

U+6DC4 湽 甾 (F2.34 淄) 黑色水名又臨淄齊地也 (Page 611 淄) A river in the northeast of Shantung (山東) (Kangxi: 水名) flowing into the sea; _Tsz' ch'un_ (zi1 cyun1 淄川), a district in Shantung (山東); a dark color (Kangxi: 黑色曰淄). Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》側持切.

U+6ECB May appear as either ⿰氵兹 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰氵茲 (+10), depending on the font. 兹 茲 (F2.34 滋) 潤也益也 (Page 610 滋) ~~Occurs used for the last (茲);~~ sweet, pleasant, rich (Kangxi: 滋味也); humid, soft; to increase, to grow (Kangxi: 蒔也長也益也); to enrich, to moisten, to fertilize; to rise, to overflow; moisture, juices, sap (Kangxi: 液也); numerous, many (Kangxi: 多也蕃也); muddy (Kangxi: 濁也); _tsz' sz'_ (zi1 si6 滋事) to get up a row, to make trouble, to embroil; _tsz' man_ (zi1 maan6 滋蔓) a sprouting tendril; something left, as of a seditious band; _ho tsz' mi_ (hou2 zi1 mei6 好滋味) a fine flavor; _tsz' yun_ (zi1 jeon6 滋潤) to mollify, to increase the juices; _tsz' po_ (zi1 bou2 滋補), to strengthen, as a tonic; _tsz' iu_ (zi1 jiu2 滋擾), to make trouble, to provoke. Williams' "used for the last": This is a misreading of Kangxi 通作兹, (which indicates that 兹 can be written 滋, not the other way round). 【滋】 (tsi) (_verb-like_) to grow (_verb-like_) to breed, to multiply (_adjective-like_) numerous, abundant (_noun-like_) fine-flavour
U+753E (Page 611 甾菑) Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》側持切.

U+77E5 (F1.35 知) 覺也識也明也 (Page 37|n80 知) To know, to perceive, to apprehend (Kangxi: 識也覺也, 喩也); to be acquainted with (Kangxi: 相交曰知); to tell, to inform; a fellow, a friend (Kangxi: 匹也); knowledge, (...); to remember (Kangxi: 猶記憶也); _chi kwo pit koi_ (zi1 gwo3 bit1 goi2 知過必改), reform when you know your wrong; _man chi_ (man4 zi1 聞知), I heard so; _shui chi_ (seoi4 zi1 誰知), unexpected, who would have thought it? _ku chi_ (gu3 zi1 故知), an old friend; _chi ti_ (zi1 di1 知啲), I know a little; _chi ki p'ang yau_ (zi1 gei2 pang4 jau5 知己朋友), an intimate friend; _sin chi_ (sin1 zi1 先知), a prophet; _chi to_ (zi1 dou6-3 知道) I know it; the thing is known; _chi hiu_ (zi1 hiu2 知曉), to understand; _chi kok_ (zi1 gok3 知覺) to notice; _chi to hai kom_ (zi1 dou6-3 hai6 gam2 知道係噉), I know it is so; _im chi sz'_ (jim4 zi1 si6 鹽知事) a salt commissioner; _chi fu_ (zi1 fu2 知府), a prefect; _chi fung_ (zi1 fung1 知風), aware of; _chi tsuk_ (zi1 zuk1 知足) contented; _pat chi tai sai_ (bat1 zi1 dai2 sai3 不知底細) I do not know its history. Williams' 知覺: Now "consciousness". 【知】 (trje) (_verb-like_) to know

U+7957 zi2 (per Fan Wan) (F1.39 祗 _Chi_ (zi2)) 敬也但也又音其 (Page 40|n83 祗 _Chi_ (zi2); here normalised to _Chi_ (zi1)) To respect, to invocate; respect, awe, reverence (Kangxi: 敬也); _chi king_ (zi1 ging3 祗敬) to venerate. 【祗】 (tsyij) (_verb-like_) to reverence

zi2.text: U+7957 (F1.39 祗) 敬也但也又音其 (Page 40|n83 祗) This character is easily confused with 祇 (see 祇 zi2).

zi1.text: U+7DC7 May appear as either ⿰糹甾 (+8 assumed here) or ⿰糹⿱⿻巛一田 (+9), depending on the font. (Page 611 緇) Dark colored silk (Kangxi: 帛黑色, 緇謂之皁), dyed many times (Kangxi: 七入爲緇, 緅又復再染以黑乃成緇矣); the dark color of a sediment (Kangxi: 緇滓也泥緇黑色者曰滓此色然也); black (Kangxi: 緇黑色); _tsz' i_ (zi1 ji1 緇衣), black garments. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》側持切. Fan Wan entry: The headword 緇 is a misprint of 輜. 【緇】 (tsri) (_noun-like_) black-silk (_adjective-like_) black

U+80A2 (F1.36 肢) 四肢 (Page 38|n81 肢) The limbs (Kangxi: 體四胑也); _sz' chi_ (sei3 zi1 四肢), the four limbs; _chi t'ai_ (zi1 tai2 肢體), the whole body; _chi kai_ (zi1 gaai2 肢解), to quarter. 【肢】 (tsye) (_noun-like_) limb
U+80FE (F2.34 胾) 切肉曰胾 (Page 611 胾)

zi3.text: U+80FE zi1 (per Fan Wan) (F2.34 胾 _Tsz'_ (zi1)) 切肉曰胾 (Page 611 胾 _Tsz'_ (zi1); here normalised to _Chi_ (zi3)) To cut up meat into steaks or morsels for eating (Kangxi: 大臠也, 切肉曰胾); slices, steaks, bits of meat. 【胾】 (tsriH) (_noun-like_) chunked-meat

zi1.text: U+8102 (F1.36 脂) 膏也又胭脂 (Page 38|n81 脂) Grease, suet, lard, fat (Kangxi: 戴角者脂無角者膏); hard gums of trees; _met._ honors, glory (Kangxi: 以喩榮祿也); to grease (Kangxi: 用以利物曰脂); fat animals; _in chi_ (jin1 zi1 胭脂), rouge (Kangxi: 燕脂以紅藍花汁凝脂爲之 etc.); _man chi_ (man4 zi1 民脂), the fat of the people, _i. e._ their money; _chi ko_ (zi1 gou1 脂膏), greasy, unctuous; _yeung chi_ (joeng4 zi1 羊脂), mutton tallow; _chi fan_ (zi1 fan2 脂粉), cosmetics. 【脂】 (tsyij) (_noun-like_) grease, fat

U+829D (F1.35 芝) 芝蔴又芝蘭 (Page 38|n81 芝) A sort of boletus or branched mushroom, regarded as felicitous (Kangxi: 神草也) from its durability; _chi ma_ (zi1 maa4 芝麻), sesamum; _chi lan_ (zi1 laan4 芝蘭), a sort of fragrant epidendrum; _chi ngan_ (zi1 ngaan4 芝顏), your fine face; _chi lan chi shat_ (zi1 laan4 zi1 sat1 芝蘭之室) an elegant house, as of the genii; _met._ intimate with good men. 【芝士】 (zi1 si6-2) Cheese.

U+83D1 甾 葘 (F2.34 菑) 田一歲曰菑始反草也 (Page 611 甾菑) ~~Waste, untilled land, overgrown, jungly (Kangxi: 不耕田也);~~ to clear new or waste land; ground under cultivation one year (Kangxi: 田一歲曰葘); to open a road; Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》側持切. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 革 to 草. 【菑-level】 (tsri) (_noun-like_) new-tillage (_verb-like_) to till newly

zi3.text: U+83D1 葘 (Page 611 ~~甾~~菑 _Tsz'_ (zi1); here corrected to _Chi_ and _Tsz'_ (zi3)) to cut, to rend (Kangxi: 剖也裂也); a dead tree still standing (Kangxi: 木立死曰葘). In terms of pre-merger readings: The "dead-tree" sense is _chi_ per Kangxi 側吏切; and The "cut/rend" sense is _tsz'_ per Kangxi 資四切. 【菑-departing】 (tsriH) (_noun-like_) standing-dead-tree

zoi1.text: U+83D1 (F2.43 災) 災禍災害 (F2.43 灾) 同上 (F2.43 烖) 同上 (F2.43 菑) 古灾字又音兹

zi1.text: U+8718 鼅 䵹 (F1.35 蜘) 蜘蛛 (Page 38|n81 蜘) A spider (Kangxi: 蜘蛛); _chi chu_ (zi1 zyu1 蜘蛛), a spider, a general name for all kinds of aranea. 【蜘】 (trje) Used in 蜘蛛. 【蜘蛛】 (trje trju) (_noun-like_) spider
U+8AEE (F2.34 諮) 詢也 (F2.34 咨) 咨嗟咨謀 (Page 611 諮)

U+8CB2 訾 (F2.34 貲) 財也貨也 (Page 611 貲) A fine paid to redeem one's self (Kangxi: 小罰以財自贖也); riches, goods, wealth (Kangxi: 財也貨也); _tsz' ts'in_ (zi1 cin4 貲錢), money; _tsz' ts'oi_ (zi1 coi4 貲財), property; _ka tsz'_ (gaa1 zi1 家貲) family possessions. To estimate, to measure (Kangxi: 與訾同).

U+8CC7 貲 齎 (F2.34 資) 資財資藉 (Page 611 貲) Used for the next (資). (Page 611 資) Property, riches, things of value (Kangxi: 貨也); necessaries, articles wanted; a fee, a douceur, a treat, a present; to take ``or to utilise`` (Kangxi: 資取也); to depend on another; what belongs to one, what is natural, as disposition; what was had at the first; _tsau tsz'_ (zau2 zi1 酒資) or _pan tsz'_ (ban1 zi1 檳資) postage; _heung tsz'_ (hoeng1 zi1 香資) a subscription to a festival or to build a temple; _tsz' pun_ (zi1 bun2 資本), capital; _tsz' fu_ (zi1 fu2 資斧), necessary things, as for a journey; _tsz' kak_ (zi1 gaak3 資格) talents, capabilities, said of officers; _tsz' chat_ (zi1 zat1 資質) natural disposition; a habit; _mo tik tsz' tsik_ (mou4 dik1 zi1 zik6 無滴資藉) without any sources or dependence. ~~Occurs used for the next (漬).~~ Williams' 酒資, 檳資: Very dated. Williams' 資格: Now "eligibility". To accumulate. To supply, to aid. 資敵 (zi1 dik6): "Supply the enemy." 資助 (zi1 zo6): "Subsidise." 【資】 (tsij) (_noun-like_) means, capital, (_met._) capability (_noun-like_) expense (_verb-like_) to utilise (_verb-like_) to depend (upon) (_verb-like_) to accumulate (_verb-like_) to supply
U+8D91 趦 (F2.34 趦) 趦趄行不進也 (Page 612 趑) Unable to advance (Kangxi: 趑趄行不進也, 趑趄難行也), either from fear or disability; to go awry; _tsz' tsu_ (zi1 zeoi1 趑趄), impeded.
U+8DA6 (F2.34 趦) 趦趄行不進也

U+8F1C 輺 (F2.34 輜) 車屬 (Page 611 輜) ~~treasure wagons, or such as carry arms and spoil;~~ large wagons with places for sleeping and carrying stores (Kangxi: 輜車載輜重臥息其中之車也); ~~manure carts;~~ _tsz' chung_ (zi1 zung6 輜重) baggage wagons of an army. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》側持切. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 緇 to 輜. Williams' "manure carts": This is a misreading of Kangxi 厠也. Here, 厠 means "mingled", not "toilet".

zi3.text: U+8F1C 菑 葘 輺 (Page 611 ~~甾~~菑 _Tsz'_ (zi1); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi3)) Occurs used for the next (輜). (Page 611 輜 _Tsz'_ (zi1); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi3)) ~~End of the axle within~~ ``Spokes going into`` the nave (Kangxi: 車輻入牙曰輜, 與葘通, 葘謂輻入轂中者也);

zi1.text: U+9319 鍿 (F2.34 錙) 耕田之器又錙銖 (Page 611 錙) A weight equal to six _chu_ (zyu1) 銖 (Kangxi: 六銖也) or the fourth part of a tael (両), not now in use; _tsz' chu_ (zi1 zyu1 錙銖), a little trifle, a mere penny, a mite. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》側持切. 【錙】 (tsri) (_noun-like_) ounce

U+93A1 May appear as either ⿰釒兹 (+9 assumed here) or ⿰釒茲 (+10), depending on the font. (F2.34 鎡) 鋤頭之屬 (Page 610 鎡) A hoe ~~for removing weeds~~, called _tsz' ki_ (zi1 gei1 鎡基) (Kangxi: 鎡錤鉏也), having a long narrow blade. 【鎡】 (tsi) Used in 鎡基. 【鎡基】 (tsi ki) (_noun-like_) hoe

U+9AED 頿 (F2.34 髭) 鬚也在下曰鬚在上曰髭 (Page 612 髭) The mustaches (Kangxi: 口上須也), which the Chinese wear long; ~~beautiful, good-looking;~~ _tsz' so_ (zi1 sou1 髭鬚), the mustaches. Williams' "beautiful, good-looking": This is misreading Kangxi 《釋名》髭姿也爲姿容之美也 as a standalone definition, when it is intended as an homophony-definition of the "moustache" sense. 【髭】 (tsje) (_noun-like_) moustache

U+9F12 (F2.34 鼒) 小鼎 (Page 612 鼒) A small round tripod (Kangxi: 小鼎也); a round lid``ded`` or cover``ed`` ~~of a~~ tripod (Kangxi: 鼎之圜掩上者). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 𪔇 to 鼒. 【鼒】 (tsi) (_noun-like_) small-tripod-cauldron

zi2.text: U+54AB (F1.39 咫) 咫尺八寸曰咫 (Page 40|n83 咫) An ancient short cubit of 8 _ts'un_ (寸) (Kangxi: 中婦人手長八寸謂之咫周尺也), ~~same as~~ ``similar to`` an English foot; small degree of (Kangxi: 咫言少也); _chi ch'ek chi ti_ (zi2 cek3 zi1 dei6 咫尺之地) a little way, a small interval. Fan Wan entry: Corrected (second) 尺 to 寸. Williams' "same as an English foot": Similar, but not exactly the same. From Williams' entry for 丈 zoeng6, he effectively puts the _ch'ek_ 尺 at 14.1 inches. 80% of this is 11.28 inches, which is somewhat short of an English foot. If we take the _ch'ek_ 尺 to be 14⅝ inches per the later Ordinance No. 8 of 1885 in British Hong Kong, 80% of this is 11.7 inches, which is close enough to an English foot. Short in distance, near (Kangxi: 咫咫尺閒也).
U+5740 阯 (F1.39 址) 基址 (Page 39|n82 址) A foundation (Kangxi: 基也), fundamental; one's own country or origin; _ki chi_ (gei1 zi2 基址), a foundation, a base, a dependance. A site.
U+5741 坻 (Page 11* 坻 _Ch'i_ (ci4); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi2) and normalised to 坁) to stop (Kangxi 坻: 止也).

U+59CA 姉 (F2.35 姊) 姐也女之長者曰姊 (Page 613 姊) An elder sister (Kangxi 姉: 男子謂女子先生爲姉); a school-mistress; _tsz' mui_ (zi2 mui6-2 姊妹) sisters; it sometimes includes all the children, as _pat tsz' mui_ (baat3 zi2 mui6-2 八姊妹) eight of us in all; _tai tsz'_ (daai6 zi2 大姊) an elder sister; _ts'at tsz' mui_ (cat1 zi2 mui6 七姊妹) a sort of rose; a kind of snake. 【姊】 (tsijX) (_noun-like_) elder-sister

U+5B50 (F2.35 子) 子嗣子息子者人之嘉稱故凡有德者稱之曰君子 (Page 612 子) A son (Kangxi: 男子之通稱), anciently it meant a child (Kangxi: 女子亦稱子); a boy, a lad, a person; an heir, posterity, issue (Kangxi: 息也嗣也); interest on money; seeds of plants; a sage, a philosopher, a venerable and worthy man (Kangxi: 子者人之嘉稱故凡成德謂之君子 etc.); Confucius; you, Sir, a term of address~~, somewhat like comrades~~; a degree of nobility, a viscount (Kangxi: 公侯伯子男凡五等); the first of the twelve stems, denoted by the rat; the third watch, from 11 P. M. to 1 A. M.; ~~the people, in distinction from their prince;~~ a spot, as of dirt or on dice; added to many nouns, it indicates that they are smaller than others of the same sort; the 39th radical ("child"); _tsz' sun_ (zi2 syun1 子孫), children, descendants; _t'ai tsz'_ (taai3 zi2 太子) the heir-apparent; _tsz' tseuk_ (zi2 zoek3 子爵) a viscount; _cheung tsz'_ (zoeng2 zi2 長子) the eldest son; _shu tai tsz'_ (syu1 daai1 zi2 書獃子) a stupid scholar; _ch'ik tsz'_ (cik3 zi2 赤子) an infant; _hi tsz'_ (hei3 zi2 戲子) a play-actor; _hau tsz'_ (haau3 zi2 孝子) a son in mourning; a dutiful son; _tsz' mo ts'in_ (zi2 mou5 cin4 子母錢), cash marked with blood of mother and child; _sin tsz'_ (sin1 zi2 先子) my deceased father; _fu tsz'_ (fu1 zi2 夫子) a teacher, a rabbi, a sage; _ting heung tsz'_ (ding1 hoeng1 zi2 丁香子) best cloves; _siu tsz'_ (siu2 zi2 小子) a boy, a pupil; my lad, my child; _noi tsz'_ (noi6 zi2 內子) my wife; _fa tsz'_ (faa1 zi2 花子) a beggar; _lo tsz'_ (lou5 zi2 老子) father, your father; _long tsz'_ (long6 zi2 浪子) a prodigal son; _yeung tsz'_ (joeng5 zi2 養子) a foster-child; _tsz' sik_ (zi2 sik1 子息) children; _kung tsz'_ (gung1 zi2 公子) a rich man's son; _cheung tsz'_ (zoeng2 zi2 長子) the eldest son. Williams' "the people, ...": This is a misreading of Kangxi 子庶民也. This is an example from 《中庸》, where 子 is a verb (to treat as children). It is *not* a zero-copula statement explaining 子 as 庶民. Williams' 子母錢: Corrected _mo_ (mou4) to _mo_ (mou5). Williams' 長子: Appears twice. 【子】 (tsiX) (_noun-like_) child, son, daughter (_noun-like_) master, (_met._) thou (_noun-like_) numerator (_noun-like_) rat-branch Used in 赤子.
U+5E0B (F1.39 紙) 字紙 (F1.39 帋) 帋帛 (Page 39|n82 帋紙) Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰糸氐 to 紙.

U+5FB5 (F1.39 徵) 五音之一 (_music_) The note a perfect fifth (ratio 81:54 = 3:2 per 《史記》 tuning) above 宮 in the olden Chinese pentatonic scale, roughly equivalent to solfege _sol_ (Kangxi: 宮徵也, ...五音配夏). 【徵-rising】 (triX) (_noun-like_) sol

zing1.text: U+5FB5 (F1.68 徵) 証也明也又徵辟 (Page 19* 徵) ~~To act;~~ ~~to set in motion;~~ to witness, to testify; to cite, to summon (Kangxi: 召也); to verify; evidence (Kangxi: 證也明也); to inquire after (Kangxi: 問也); to gather together (Kangxi: 斂也); ~~to aim at;~~ to raise, as troops; to collect, as taxes; fullfilment, proof; _ming ching_ (ming4 zing1 明徵) plain proof; _mo ching_ (mou4 zing1 無徵), no proof; _ching ping_ (zing1 bing1 徵兵), to enlist troops; _kwai ching_ (gwai3 zing1 貴徵), a noble bearing, courteous; to seek a good market; _yau ching_ (jau5 zing1 有徵), proved; _ching chiu_ (zing1 ziu6 徵召) to cite, called upon by government; _ching p'ing_ (zing1 ping3 徵聘) to induce by a present; _ching im_ (zing1 jim6 徵驗) accomplished. To seek (Kangxi: 求也). A sign, an omen. 病徵 (beng6 zing1): "Sign of disease." 徵兆 (zing1 siu6): "Omen". The completion-gift (of the marriage rites) (Kangxi: 成也). 【徵-level】 (tring) (_verb-like_) to call, to summon (_verb-like_) to evidence (_verb-like_) to collect (_verb-like_) to seek (_noun-like_) evidence, sign (_noun-like_) completion-gift

zi2.text: U+6307 (F1.39 指) 手指又指示也 (Page 39|n82 指) A finger (Kangxi: 手指也 etc.), a toe (Kangxi: 將指...足大指也); to point, to refer to; ~~to teach, to command~~ ``to give directions`` (Kangxi: 指麾也); ~~a particular, a mode;~~ to point out (Kangxi: 示也); _chi nam ~~ch'e~~ ``ku``_ (zi2 naam4 geoi1 指南車), the ~~compass~~ ``south-pointing chariot``; _shap chi t'an pang_ (sap6 zi2 taan4 bang1 十指彈崩), to snap the fingers in vexation; _ma chi_ (maa1 zi2 孖指), a double finger; _chi shau wak keuk_ (zi2 sau2 waak6 goek3 指手畫腳) gesticulation; _chi mong_ (zi2 mong6 指望) to expect; _mo chi i_ (mou4 zi2 ji5 無指擬), no chance of, cannot obtain; _chi tung chi sai_ (zi2 dung1 zi2 sai1 指東指西), pointing this way and that, befooling; _chi shi_ (zi2 si6 指示) to point out; _shau chi kung_ (sau2 zi2 gung1 手指公), the thumb; _shik chi_ (sik6 zi2 食指), forefinger; _mo ming chi_ (mou4 ming4 zi2 無名指) ring-finger; _chi tim_ (zi2 dim2 指點), to show how; _chi kap_ (zi2 gaap3 指甲) a finger nail; _chi kap fa_ (zi2 gaap3 faa1 指甲花), the henna _(Lawsonia inermis)_; _chi shat_ (zi2 sat6 指實) certain, sure. Williams' 指南車: Corrected _ch'e_ (ce1) to _ku_ (geoi1). Strictly, this is the south-pointing chariot. The compass is now called 指南針. Williams' 指手畫腳: Figuratively, to make overt criticism, to assume authority and give out orders, etc. To rebuke, to criticise (Kangxi: 斥也). To rely on, to hope for. 【指】 (tsyijX) (_noun-like_) finger (_verb-like_) to point 【尾指】 (mei5-1 zi2) The pinky. 【手指公】 (sau2 zi2 gung1) The thumb. 【手指尾】 (sau2 zi2 mei5-1) The pinky. 【食指】 (sik6 zi2) The index finger. 【指擬】 (zi2 ji5) To rely on. 指擬佢就弊 (zi2 ji5 keoi5 zau6 bai6): "Relying on him we'd be stuffed." To hope for. 唔使指擬買屋 (m4 sai2 zi2 ji5 maai5 uk1): "Needn't bother hoping to buy a house."

U+65E8 恉 (for "intention" senses) 指 (for "intention" and "fine" senses) (F1.39 旨) 意也甘也 (Page 39|n82 旨) Meaning, intention (Kangxi: 意也志也); excellent (Kangxi: 美也); pleasant tasted, delicate; a decretal, orders (Kangxi: 凡天子諭吿臣民曰詔旨下承上曰奉旨); _fung chi_ (fung6 zi2 奉旨), to receive orders; _chi tsau_ (zi2 zau2 旨酒), good liquor; _chi i_ (zi2 ji3 旨意) the import ``of`` the imperial will; _shing chi_ (sing3 zi2 聖旨), H. I. M.'s orders; _ts'ing chi_ (cing2 zi2 請旨), to request commands; _yat cheung chi tai chi_ (jat1 zoeng1 zi1 daai6 zi2 一章之大旨), an important remark, a synopsis. (Page 39|n82 指) used for the last (旨) (Kangxi 指: 與旨恉通意向也, 歸趣也, 美也); 【旨】 (tsyijX) (_adjective-like_) fine-tasting, fine, excellent (_noun-like_) intent

U+6893 (F2.35 梓) 木名 (Page 612 梓) A sort of tree (Kangxi: 楸也), considered to be the best of all trees (Kangxi: 梓爲百木長故呼梓爲木王 etc.) (a sort of cedar?); to cut blocks for books (Kangxi: 俗謂鋟文書於板曰梓); one's village; _tsz' li_ (zi2 lei5 梓里), my village; _song tsz'_ (song1 zi2 桑梓) one's birthplace; _tsz' hang_ (zi2 hang4 梓行), printed by <`....`>; ~~_tsz' t'an_ (zi2 taan4 梓檀), a solid kind of black wood.~~ Williams' 梓檀: Should probably be 紫檀. Woodwork (Kangxi: 治木器曰梓). 【梓】 (tsiX) (_noun-like_) catalpa

U+6B62 (F1.39 止) 息也定也但也 (Page 39|n82 止) To stop (Kangxi: 停也足也); to lodge, to dwell (Kangxi: 居也), to rest (Kangxi: 息也); that which the mind rests in (Kangxi: 心之所安爲止), an object (Kangxi: 止者必至於是而不遷之謂); ~~to detain;~~ interrupted, stopped (Kangxi: 已也); to cease from, to desist, to be still (Kangxi: 靜也, 不妄動也); to remain, to wait; the 77th radical ("stop") of characters mostly relating to rest; _chi chi_ (zi1 zi2 知止) to know when to stop; _chi pat chu_ (zi2 bat1 zyu6 止不住) will not stop, cannot be stopped; _chi no_ (zi2 nou6 止怒) to appease; _chi suk_ (zi2 suk1 止宿) to lodge at; _mo tai chi_ (mou4 dai2 zi2 無底止), nothing to stop at. Williams' "to detain": Either a poor rendering of Kangxi 留也 or a misreading of Kangxi 凡戰而被獲曰止. To keep (Kangxi: 留也). To be captured in battle (Kangxi: 凡戰而被獲曰止). Demeanour, bearing (Kangxi: 容止). Behaviour (Kangxi: 舉止). A mallet used for a certain musical instrument (Kangxi: 所以鼓柷謂之止, 止者其椎名也). Speech-assist (Kangxi: 語辭), specifically a final particle. 【止】 (tsyiX) (_verb-like_) to halt, to cease, to rest, to stay (_noun-like_) deportment (_speech-assist_)
U+6C9A (F1.39 沚) 沼也 (Page 39|n82 沚) An islet (Kangxi: 小渚曰沚); to take up one's lodging on an island (Kangxi: 止息也可以止息其上); _chiu chi_ (ziu2 zi2 沼沚), an islet in a pond. 【沚】 (tsyiX) (_noun-like_) islet

U+6CF2 zai2 (per 《集韻》子禮切) zai3 (common) (F2.35 泲) 水名 (Page 613 泲) Name of a river on the east of China (Kangxi: 泲沇也東流於海); to strain spirits (Kangxi: 莤泲釃酒也); ~~a strainer made of plants.~~ Kangxi has 《玉篇》古文濟字. The common reading zai3 in departing tone is probably a result of using the wrong reading of 濟. The reading of 濟 corresponding to the proper-name sense is rising-tone 子禮切 _tsai_ (zai2).

U+6ED3 zi1 (per Kangxi 又平聲...滓音湽) zoi2 (common) (F2.35 滓) 洒滓渣滓 (Page 613 滓) Sediments, dregs (Kangxi: 澱也); _chap tsz'_ (zap1 zi2 汁滓) grains from spirits; _cha tsz'_ (zaa1 zi2 渣滓) grounds, residuum, dregs; _nai tsz'_ (nai4 zi2 泥滓) muddy settlings. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》阻史切. The common reading zoi2 (after the phonetic 宰) is considered unlearned; I have not yet encountered a dictionary that records it.
U+7947 (Page 152 祇 _K'i_ (kei4); _Chi_ (zi2) implied) This character is easily confused with 祗 (see 祗 zi2).

U+7949 ci2 (per 《廣韻》敕里切) (F1.39 祉) 福祉 (Page 39|n82 祉) Happiness (Kangxi: 福也); _fuk chi_ (fuk1 zi2 福祉), blessed, happy, enduring felicity; _ka chi_ (gaa1 zi2 嘉祉), good fortune. 【祉】 (trhiX) (_noun-like_) blessing, felicity

U+79ED (F2.35 秭) 數也十億曰兆十兆曰京十京曰秭 (Page 613 秭) A stack of corn; ~~to number;~~ ~~the ninth place in notation``,`` a hundred millions;~~ a weight of 640 peculs (Kangxi: 六萬四千斤也). Williams' "to number": This is a misreading of Kangxi 數也. Here, 數 is a noun (departing), not a verb (rising). Williams' "ninth place ...": By "ninth place in notation", Williams intends 10<sup>8</sup>, which is incorrect. Here, Williams has been misled by Fan Wan's progression (億兆京秭), which is missing 垓 before 秭. A large number (Kangxi: 數也). Based on the progression 億兆京垓秭 etc., one of the following: 10<sup>9</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>5</sup> and going up by factors of 10); 10<sup>12</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>8</sup> and going up by factors of 10); 10<sup>24</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>8</sup> and going up by factors of 10<sup>4</sup>); 10<sup>128</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>8</sup> and going up by squares). 【秭】 (tsijX) (_noun-like_) haystack

U+7D19 帋 (F1.39 紙) 字紙 (F1.39 帋) 帋帛 (Page 39|n82 帋紙) Paper (Kangxi: 絮一苫也 etc.); a document; _yat cheung chi_ (jat1 zoeng1 zi2 一張紙), a sheet of paper; _sha chi_ (saa1 zi2 紗紙), wrapping paper; _ts'o chi_ (cou2 zi2 草紙), coarse paper; _chi mui_ (zi2 mui4 紙煤), a paper match; _chi t'ung fa_ (zi2 tung1 faa1 紙蓪花), artificial flowers; _chi p'ai_ (zi2 paai4-2 紙牌), cards; _chi pak_ (zi2 baak6 紙帛) paper houses and clothes burned in sacrifices; _king un chi_ (ging1 jyun6 zi2 ？？紙), cotton paper; _kai p'i chi_ (gai1 pei4 zi2 雞皮紙), brown wrapping paper; _chi ts'in_ (zi2 cin4 紙錢), scolloped pieces of paper used at funerals to buy the road; _fan chi_ (faan4 zi2 幡紙), sized paper; _lang kam tsin chi_ (laang5 gam1 zin1 zi2 冷金箋紙), gold dusted paper; _king sik tsz' chi_ (ging3 sik1 zi6 zi2 敬惜字紙), respect written paper; _yat chi shu_ (jat1 zi2 syu1 一紙書), one letter; _fong chi iu_ (fong3 zi2 jiu4-2 放紙鷂), to fly a kite; _ta shiu chi_ (daa2 siu1 zi2 打燒紙), to torture in prison; _ch'an chi_ (can3 zi2 襯紙), a fly leaf. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰糸氐 to 紙. Williams' 草紙: Also dated for toilet paper. 【紙】 (tsyeX) (_noun-like_) paper 【花紙】 (faa1 zi2) Gift wrapping paper. 【膠紙】 (gaau1 zi2) Sticky tape. 【雞皮紙】 (gai1 pei4 zi2) Kraft paper.

U+7D2B (F2.35 紫) 黑赤間色也 (Page 613 紫) A reddish blue (Kangxi: 帛靑赤色), ~~a clay color,~~ a nankeen tint; ~~a fugacious, undecided hue;~~ _tsz' t'ong shik_ (zi2 tong4 sik1 紫棠色) a dark complexion; _tsz' fan_ (zi2 fan2 紫粉), purple; _tsz' t'an_ (zi2 taan4 紫檀), orange red; _tsz' fan_ (zi2 fan2 紫粉), cudbear; _tsz' li kom shik_ (zi2 lei4 gam2 sik1 紫梨噉色) black and blue, like a bruise; _tsz' piu_ (zi2 biu1 紫摽), a violet; _tsz' fa po_ (zi2 faa1 bou3 紫花布) nankeen cloth; _tsz' shan_ (zi2 san4 紫宸), the court or palace, the rising sun; _tsz' mi_ (zi2 mei4 紫薇), the _Lagerstroemia_, or crape myrtle; a god worshiped for protection against malaria. Williams' "fugacious, undecided hue": This is an attempt at rendering Kangxi 紫閒色... (with 閒 departing). A better rendering would be "mixed hue". Note that the intent of the original Chinese is merely to note that purple is an *instance* of mixed hue (非正色 = not one of 青黃赤白黑). The intent is not to define the character 紫 as meaning "mixed hue" in the general sense. 【紫】 (tsjeX) (_adjective-like_) purple
U+8014 秄 (F2.35 耔) 農人壅禾根也 (Page 612 耔) To ~~weed and hoe~~ ``heap`` earth around the roots of plants, to hill (Kangxi: 壅苗本也). 【耔】 (tsiX) (_verb-like_) to earth roots

U+80CF (F2.35 胏) 乾肉之帶骨者 (Page 613 胏) The remnants of a meal (Kangxi: 食所遺也); dried meat with the bones in it (Kangxi: 脯有骨也); _kon tsz'_ (gon1 zi2 乾胏) dried meat. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》阻史切. 【胏】 (tsriX) (_noun-like_) boneful-meat
U+8A3F (F2.35 訿 _Ts'z'_ (ci2)) 相詆也詩翕翕訿訿

U+8DBE 止 (for "foot" sense) (F1.39 趾) 脚趾 (Page 39|n82 趾) The toes; a foundation of a wall (Kangxi: 趾城足也); the feet (Kangxi: 趾足也); _ku chi ko_ (geoi2 zi2 gou1 舉趾高), to step high, to trip along; _hang chi_ (hang4 zi2 行趾), to walk. 【趾】 (tsyiX) (_noun-like_) foot, toe 【腳趾公】 (goek3 zi2 gung1) The big toe. 【腳趾尾】 (goek3 zi2 mei5-1) The little toe.

U+8EF9 (Page 40|n83 軹) The hole in the hub of a wheel (Kangxi: 車輪小穿), w``h``ere the ~~nave~~ ``axle`` projects; the end of the ~~nave~~ ``axle`` projecting from the hub (Kangxi: 軹指也如指而見于轂頭也, 軹謂轊頭也); diverging, like the forks of a road (Kangxi: 岐也).

U+962F 趾 (in 交阯 spelt 交趾) (F1.39 阯) 交阯夷國名 (Page 39|n82 阯) ~~Used for the last (趾);~~ _Kau chi_ (gaau1 zi2 交阯), Cochinchina (Kangxi: 交阯漢郡名), said to be applied to it because men and women bathed together (Kangxi: 其俗男女同川而浴故曰交阯). Williams' "Used for the last": I have only seen this in the other direction, i.e. using 趾 for 阯.

U+9EF9 (Page 39|n82 黹) To embroider (Kangxi: ...黹謂刺繡也); embroidered, braided; to put on braid or lace; the 204th radical ("embroidery"), relating to embroidery; _cham chi_ (zam1 zi2 針黹), to sew, to do needle work. Needlework (Kangxi: 箴縷所紩衣). 【黹】 (trijX) (_noun-like_) embroidery

zi3.text: U+2BAA6 (Page 40|n83 𫪦) A colloquial particle, implying doubt; _loi mi chi_ (loi4 mei6 zi3 來未𫪦) I doubt if he is here yet.
U+5BD8 May appear as either ⿱宀眞 or ⿱宀真 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.41 置) 立也又安厝也又棄也 (F1.41 寘) 同上 (Page 9* 置寘)

U+5FD7 (F1.41 志) 心之所之也 (Page 40|n83 志) The will, the inclination (Kangxi: 志者心之所之也); a good resolution, a resolve; a sense of right, firm purpose; _tai chi_ (daai6 zi3 大志) high thoughts; _ch'ut tak chi_ (ceot1 dak1 zi3 出得志) not abashed by ridicule, conscious of power; _chi heung_ (zi3 hoeng3 志向) desire, inclination; _chi shing_ (zi3 sing4 志誠), ingenuous, guileless. 【志】 (tsyiH) (_noun-like_) will, mind, (_met._) aims

U+646F (F1.41 摯) 至也又握持也又絆其足也 (Page 9* 摯) To seize with the hand, to grasp (Kangxi: 握持也); to arrive at, to reach to (Kangxi: 一曰至也, 臻也); ~~to extend;~~ to advance (Kangxi: 進也)~~, to enter, to go to the edge~~; to break down (Kangxi: 傷折也), as trees from weight of snow; _ts'ing chi_ (cing4 zi3 情摯) a great liking for; _chi to_ (zi3 dou2 摯倒), to stumble down; _chi hi_ (zi3 hei2 摯起), to pick one up. Extreme, utmost (Kangxi: 極也). Sincere, earnest. 【摯】 (tsyijH) (_verb-like_) to seize, to hold (_verb-like_) to arrive

U+667A 知 (F1.41 智) 心之明哲能辨道理也 (Page 37|n80 知 _Chi_ (zi1); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi3) and normalised to 智) (...), wisdom; (Page 9* 智) Wisdom, understanding (Kangxi: 智知也無所不知也)~~, talent~~; knowledge; prudence; wise, considerate, discreet; able to manage; clever, sharp, shrewd; _chi shik_ (zi3 sik1 智識) good judgment; _chi yung_ (zi3 jung5 智勇), wise and brave; _mo chi_ (mou4 zi3 無智) indiscreet. 【智】 (trjeH) (_noun-like_) knowledge, (_met._) wisdom
U+690D May appear with right component as 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. (Page 13* 植 _Chik_ (zik6); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi3))

zi6.text: U+690D May appear with right component as 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font.

zik6.text: U+690D May appear with right component as 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. zi6 (per 《廣韻》直吏切 for verb "plant" sense and Kangxi 直吏切 for "lean" sense) (F1.78 植) 立也置也栽也種也 (Page 13* 植) To plant (Kangxi: 種也), to set up (Kangxi: 樹立也), to transplant or set out; erect, standing upright (Kangxi: 立也); to lean on, as a staff (Kangxi: 倚也); _tsoi chik_ (zoi1 zik6 栽植) to set out; _chik k'i cheung_ (zik6 kei4 zoeng6 植其杖) ~~put his staff down~~; _u chik_ (wu6 zik6 戶植) door-posts (Kangxi: 植謂戸之維持鏁者也植木爲之); _chik muk_ (zik6 muk6 植木) to transplant trees. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 裁 to 栽. Williams' 植其杖: Legge's rendering, "planted his staff", is better. Kangxi instead interprets this as "leant upon his staff" under the reading _chi_ (zi6). For "put his staff down", this would instead be under the reading _chi_ (zi3) for 置. A plant (Kangxi: 根生之屬曰植). 【植】 (dzyik, driH) (_verb-like_) to plant (_verb-like_) to set up

zi3.text: U+6F2C zi6 (per 《廣韻》疾智切) (Page 611 漬 _Tsz'_ (zi1); here corrected to _Tsz'_ (zi3)) ~~Usually pronounced _tsik_ (zik1).~~ To soak, to steep (Kangxi: 漚也); to dye (Kangxi: 染也); water-soaked, moldy, damaged by soaking; _tsam tsz'_ (zam3 zi3 浸漬) under water; 【漬】 (dzjeH) (_verb-like_) to soak

zik1.text: U+6F2C (Page 611 漬) Usually pronounced _tsik_ (zik1). _shui tsik_ (seoi2 zik1 水漬) damaged by wet, as cargo; _yan tsik_ (jan3 zik1 印漬) discolored, spotted, marks of wetting. When used as a noun, better translated as "stain".

zi3.text: U+7590 (F1.41 疐) 礙不行也 (Page 9* 疐) Hindered, embarrassed, prevented from acting or advancing (Kangxi: 頓也, 礙不行也); ~~to slink away, as a dog does.~~ Fan Wan entry: Corrected 癡 to 礙. Williams' "embarrassed": In the (now) archaic sense of "impeded", "obstructed". To tumble, to stumble (Kangxi: 頓也跲也, 疐仆也). 【疐】 (trijH) (_verb-like_) to stumble
U+75E3 誌 (F1.41 痣) 疤痣 (Page 40|n83 痣) A mole, a hair-mole (Kangxi: 黑子也); black or red spots on the body; _min chi_ (min6 zi3 面痣) spots on the face.

U+7DFB 致 (for "fine/delicate" senses) zi6 (per 《廣韻》直利切) (F1.41 緻) 精緻 (Page 9* 緻) To mend clothes (Kangxi: 補也, 縫補敝衣也); close, fine, as cloth (Kangxi: 密也); soft, delicate; ~~tattered;~~ handsome, elegant; _tsing chi_ (zing1 zi3 精緻) beautiful, fine, delicate; _sai chi_ (sai3 zi3 細緻) careful, particular. Williams' "tattered": This is a misreading of Kangxi 其敝者謂之緻. This is an example from 《揚子方言》 describing the *mending* of tattered clothes as 緻, not the tattered clothes themselves.

U+7F6E May appear with bottom component as the right component of 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. 寘 植 (F1.41 置) 立也又安厝也又棄也 (F1.41 寘) 同上 (Page 9* 置寘) To make firm, to establish (Kangxi: 立也, 設也), to place; to appoint; to arrange (Kangxi: 安置也); to purchase for one's self; ~~to employ;~~ to reject, to dismiss (Kangxi: 赦也, ...與罷同意置之則去之也); to put aside; to determine, to judge, to decide; _ch'u chi_ (cyu2 zi3 處置) to punish, treat as a criminal; _chi pan_ (zi3 baan6 置辦) to buy; _chi ka_ (zi3 gaa1 置家), to take a wife; _on chi_ (on1 zi3 安置) to arrange a place for, as to spend the night; _fai chi_ (fai3 zi3 廢置) to refuse, to remove; _chi shan sz' ngoi_ (zi3 san1 si6 ngoi6 置身事外) it is none of my business; _nim nim pat chi_ (nim6 nim6 bat1 zi3 念念不置) unceasingly thinking of it; _chi shan mo ti_ (zi3 san1 mou4 dei6 置身無地) ashamed, no place to hide myself. (Page 13* 植 _Chik_ (zik6); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi3)) to place, to lay down (Kangxi 植: 通置); A relay station for horses (Kangxi: 置若今之驛也). 【置】 (triH) (_verb-like_) to put, to set 【寘】 (tsyeH) (_verb-like_) to put

U+81F3 (F1.41 至) 到也極也 (Page 40|n83 至) To come (Kangxi: 來也), to go to, to reach (Kangxi: 達也由此達彼也); to arrive, the summit or end; a preposition, to, at, even, till, up to; as to, respecting, in order to; ~~an adverb, much, greatly;~~ most, ~~very,~~ the superlative degree (Kangxi: 極也, 至謂至極也); the solstice (Kangxi: 冬至隂之復也夏至陽之復也); the 133d radical ("arrive"); _chi kik_ (zi3 gik6 至極) at the very extreme; _tsz' ku chi kam_ (zi6 gu2 zi3 gam1 自古至今), from of old till now; _mo sho pat chi_ (mou4 so2 bat1 zi3 無所不至) he goes every where, he is very wild; _chi kwan kan iu_ (zi3 gwaan1 gan2 jiu3 至關緊要) of the highest moment; _tung chi_ (dung1 zi3 冬至) the winter solstice; _chi u_ (zi3 jyu1 至於), with respect to; _chi shiu_ (zi3 siu2 至少), the least of; _hak chi_ (hak1 zi3 刻至) he will come soon; _chi to_ (zi3 dou3 至到) to arrive; _chi ho_ (zi3 hou2 至好), the best; _chi cheuk_ (zi3 zoek6 至着) everything proper; _chi shing_ (zi3 sing4 至誠), wholly sincere; _chi kan_ (zi3 gan2 至緊), urgent; _kat chi_ (gat1 zi3 訖至) a little while, presently. Williams' 至到: In the vernacular register, "until". Williams' 至着: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). Perfect, good (Kangxi: 善也). Great (Kangxi: 大也). 【至】 (tsyijH) (_verb-like_) to arrive, to reach (_preposition-like_) unto (_adverb-like_) most (_adjective-like_) utmost (_noun-like_) solstice Used in 至於. 【至於】 (tsyijH 'jo) (_preposition-like_) as to 【至】 (zi3) Short for 先至 噉至得咖嘛 (gam2 zi3 dak1 ga1 maa3): "Only like so will it work."

U+81F4 May appear as either ⿰至夊 (+3 assumed here) or ⿰至攵 (+4), depending on the font. (F1.41 致) 使也又到也極也推也 (Page 40|n83 致) ~~Interchanged with the last (至).~~ To go, to cause to go; to convey to (Kangxi: 送詣也); leading to, tending; to accompany; to visit; to communicate (Kangxi: 傳致也), to intimate to; to resign, to give over to (Kangxi: 納也); to induce, bring on (Kangxi: 招致也); to jeopard, to hazard (Kangxi: 委也); to regulate, to control, to order (Kangxi: 制也); an aim, object, or end; a tendency; to investigate fully (Kangxi: 致猶㴱審也); used before a verb, implies what is caused to be done; that, in order to; to put forth; ~~the extreme;~~ _chi shai k'u loi_ (zi3 sai2 keoi5 loi4 致使佢來), make him come; _chi i_ (zi3 ji3 致意) to intimate to, to inform in any way; _chi sz'_ (zi3 si6 致仕) to throw up an office; _chi ming_ (zi3 ming6 致命) delivered his orders. Williams' "Interchanged with the last": Kangxi has the sense 至也, but this should not be regarded as an interchanging. Williams' "tending": In the sense of contributing toward an outcome. Williams' 致使佢來: Corrected _shai_ (sai3) to _shai_ (sai2). Williams' 致命: Now "fatal". To arrive (Kangxi: 至也). To do fully, to the utmost (Kangxi: 極也). 【致】 (trijH) (_verb-like_) to convey (_verb-like_) to hand over, (_met._) to retire (_verb-like_) to bring about (_verb-like_) to arrive (_verb-like_) to utmost, to do utmostly

U+89F6 zi1 (alternative per 《廣韻》章移切) (F1.41 觶) 酒器實曰觴虛曰觶 (Page 9* 觶) A goblet or cup (Kangxi: 鄕飮酒角也, 酒觴也) holding three _shing_ (sing1 升) or gills (Kangxi: 三升曰觶觶適也); a cup; ~~to fine one so many cups.~~ To punish with drink in a drinking game (Kangxi: 罰爵也). 【觶】 (tsyeH, tsye) (_noun-like_) drinking-vessel

U+8A8C 志 識 (for "remember" and "record" senses) (F1.41 誌) 記也 (F1.41 識) 同上 (Page 40|n83 志) to record (Kangxi 志: 又記也與誌同或作識); history, annals; statistical or topographical works; (Page 40|n83 誌識) To remember (Kangxi 識: 與誌同記也); to record (Kangxi 誌: 記誌也); to write, to inscribe; ~~used for the last (志);~~ _mo chi_ (mou6 zi3 墓誌) an epitaph, a eulogy; _sam kwok chi_ (saam1 gwok3 zi3 三國誌) annals of the Three States; _chi shu_ (zi3 syu1 誌書), history, records; _chi u ts'z'_ (zi3 jyu1 ci2 誌於此) mind is fixed on this. 【誌】 (tsyiH) (_verb-like_) to remember, to record
U+8B58 (F1.41 誌) 記也 (F1.41 識) 同上 (Page 40|n83 誌識)

U+8D04 摯 質 (F1.41 贄) 初相見之禮物或玉帛或禽鳥 (Page 9* 摯) to present to; used for the next (贄) (Kangxi: 執物以爲相見之禮也與贄質通); (Page 9* 贄) A present to make way for one, given at an audience or first interview, or when entering school; to present gifts; _chi i_ (zi3 ji4 贄儀), presents; _chi kin_ (zi3 gin3 贄見) to visit with a present. 【贄】 (tsyijH) (_noun-like_) first-meeting-gift
U+8E93 疐 (F1.41 躓) 顚蹶 (Page 9* 躓) To stumble (Kangxi: 跲也); to trip and fall down; _tin chi_ (din1 zi3 顛躓) fell headlong. 【躓】 (trijH) (_verb-like_) to stumble

U+8F0A (F1.41 輊) 車之覆而前也 (Page 9* 輊) A carriage with a front lower than the back, or turning down, from the lading (Kangxi: 低也, 車前重也). 【輊】 (trijH) (_noun-like_) low-fronted (chariot), front-heavy (chariot)

U+9DD9 摯 (F1.41 鷙) 惡鳥 (Page 9* ~~？~~``鷙``) Birds of prey, accipitrine birds (Kangxi: 擊殺鳥也, 猛鳥也); violent, ruthless, hawklike (Kangxi: 又凡鳥之勇獸之猛者皆曰鷙); ~~to seize by violence.~~ Williams' "to seize by violence": This is a misreading of Kangxi 鷙執也. This describes the "bird-of-prey" sense as "a bird that seizeth". It does not define a verb. 【鷙】 (tsyijH) (_noun-like_) bird-of-prey (_verb-like_) to prey (upon)

zi6.text: U+4F3A si1 (per 《廣韻》息兹切) si3 (per 《廣韻》相吏切) si6 (alternative) (F2.35 伺) 窺竊也候也 (Page 614 伺) To wait upon, to wait for; to spy, to narrowly examine (Kangxi: 偵𠋫也又察也); _tsz' hau_ (zi6 hau6 伺候) in readiness, waiting.

U+4FDF 竢 (F2.35 俟) 待也候也 (Page 614 俟竢) To wait, to stay (Kangxi: 待也); to wait upon; to expect, to look for, to hope; to prepare for; until, expecting; _tsz' hau_ (zi6 hau6 俟候) waiting for; _lap tsz' ui yam_ (lap6 zi6 wui4 jam1 立俟回音), wait for an answer; _tsz' ha ut_ (zi6 haa6 jyut6 俟下月) till next month. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》牀史切. Used in 俟俟 (walking slowly in a large group) (Kangxi: 俟俟衆多徐行貌). 【俟】 (zriX) (_verb-like_) to await (_adjective-like_) ambling together
U+5024 (F1.34 値 _Chai_ (zai6)) 遇也當也又音直作價值 (Page 10* 値)

zik6.text: U+5024 (F1.78 値) 適也遇也又音滯 (Page 13* 値)

zi6.text: U+503C 値 (F1.34 値 _Chai_ (zai6)) 遇也當也又音直作價值 (Page 10* 値; here normalised to 值) To manage, to take hold of (Kangxi 値: 又持也); (Page 13* 値 _Chik_ (zik6); here corrected to _Chi_ (zi6) and normalised to 值) (...), to hold in the hand; 【值-hold】 (driH) (_verb-like_) to hold

zik6.text: U+503C 値 直 zi6 (per Kangxi 直吏切 for "meet/happen" sense and as alternative for "worth" sense) (F1.78 値) 適也遇也又音滯 (Page 10* 値 _Chi_ (zi6); here normalised to 值 _Chik_ (zik6)) to occur, to meet with, to happen (Kangxi 値: 又遇也); _shik ~~chi~~ ``chik``_ (sik1 zik6 適值) it so happened. (Page 13* 直) price of; used for the ~~three~~ next (値~~, 殖, 植~~); (Page 13* 値; here normalised to 值) Price, value, worth of (Kangxi 値: 物價曰値或作直); just; to meet; happened, chanced, occurred; a turn, (...); to manage, to attend to; _chik 'm chik_ (zik6 m4 zik6 值唔值) is this cheap or not? _'m chik yat ko ts'in_ (m4 zik6 jat1 go3 cin4 唔值一個錢), not worth a cash; _'m chik ts'in_ (m4 zik6 cin4-2 唔值錢), not worth much; _shik chik_ (sik1 zik6 適值) it happened; _chik yat_ (zik6 jat6 值日) ruling days, are days when certain officers serve in rotation; _chik sz'_ (zik6 si6 值事) do it in turn; _'m ho chik u_ (m4 hou2 zik6 jyu6 唔好值遇) an unlucky event. In modern usage, the reading _chi_ (zi6) for the "meet/happen" sense (and as an alternative for the "worth" sense) is obsolete. 【值-worth】 (drik, driH) (_verb-like_) to withstand, to face (_verb-like_) to stand for, to be worth (_noun-like_) worth

zi6.text: U+55E3 (F2.35 嗣) 子息也繼也 (Page 614 嗣) To connect, as a family; ~~to adopt;~~ to inherit, to succeed to (Kangxi: 嗣續也繼也); children, posterity, heirs, descendants; an ~~adopted~~ heir; to practice, to learn fully (Kangxi: 嗣習也); till; hereafter, then; _hau tsz'_ (hau6 zi6 後嗣) descendants; _kai tsz'_ (gai3 zi6 繼嗣) an ~~adopted~~ heir; _tsut tsz'_ (zyut6 zi6 絕嗣) posterity cut off; _mo tsz' t'an_ (mou4 zi6 taan4 無嗣壇), an orbate temple; _tsz' wai_ (zi6 wai6 嗣位) to succeed to the throne; _tsz' yan pit ku_ (zi6 jan1 bit6 gu3 嗣因別故) from other causes following it. Williams' 繼嗣: Not necessarily adopted. 【嗣】 (ziH) (_verb-like_) to succeed, to inherit (_verb-like_) to continue (_noun-like_) successor

U+5B57 (F2.35 字) 文字女子嫁人亦曰字又撫也愛也慈也乳也生也 (Page 613 字) A character (Kangxi: 文字 etc.), a letter; lettered, marked, written on; a writing, a letter, an order; a word, a symbol of thought; to bear, to nourish, to love, to suckle (Kangxi: 乳也又愛也); the style or title (Kangxi: 名字, 男子二十冠而字) ~~taken at marriage~~ (Kangxi: 女子許嫁筓而字); ~~a name,~~ a designation; to betroth a daughter (Kangxi: 女許嫁曰字); _se tsz'_ (se2 zi6 寫字) to write; _tsz' mo tsz' t'au_ (zi6 mou5 zi6 tau4 字母字頭), finals and initials, as used by the Chinese to express a third sound; _tsz' mo_ (zi6 mou5 字母), letters of an alphabet; _mo tsz' mak_ (mou5 zi6 mak6 冇字墨) unlearned, unskilled in writing; _man tsz'_ (man4 zi6 文字) composition, writing; _yat cheung tsz'_ (jat1 zoeng1 zi6 一張字) a note, an order; _mo tsz' un_ (mou5 zi6 jyun4 冇字緣), no mind for learning; _fa ma tsz'_ (faa1 maa5 zi6 花碼字) contracted forms of the ten digits; _tsz' ho_ (zi6 hou6 字號) name of a shop; a "chop," as of tea; _chuk tsz' shat_ (zuk1 zi6 sat1 捉字蝨) to seek flaws in a writing; _tsz' ngan_ (zi6 ngaan5 字眼), a set phrase, an expression; _pat tsz'_ (bat1 zi6 不字) not betrothed; _ut tsz'_ (wut6 zi6 活字) or _tsz' nap_ (zi6 nap1 字粒) a type; _tsun tsz'_ (zyun1 zi6 尊字) what is your respected style? _pit tsz'_ (bit6 zi6 別字) the familiar style, taken on entering life; _yan tsz' kak_ (jan3 zi6 gaak3-2 印字格) a copy-slip; _tsz' kak_ (zi6 gaak3 字格) black lines; _tsz' tsik_ (zi6 zik1 字跡) a mark, traces of writing. Williams' "taken at marriage": Per 《禮記》, men are styled at twenty, whilst women are styled at betrothal (whence 待字 "awaiting style-name" for a daughter unbetrothed). 【字】 (dziH) (_verb-like_) to bear child, to breed (_verb-like_) to nurture, to love (_noun-like_) character (_noun-like_) style (_verb-like_) (_met._) to betroth 【字】 (zi6) A point within an hour, corresponding to one of the markings 1 through 11 on an analogue clock. 五點十個字 (ng5 dim2 sap6 go3 zi6): "Five fifty" (shortenable to 五點十). 七點三個字 (cat1 dim2 saam1 go3 zi6): "Seven fifteen" (shortenable to 七點三). 踏四個字 (daap6 sei3 go3 zi6): "Twenty past" (shortenable to 踏四). 踏四個半字 (daap6 sei3 go3 bun3 zi6): "Twenty-two and a half minutes past" (shortenable to 踏四個半). A duration of five minutes. 遲四個字 (ci4 sei3 go3 zi6): "Late by twenty minutes." 爭兩個字六點 (zaang1 loeng5 go3 zi6 luk6 dim2): "Ten minutes short of six o'clock."

U+5BFA 䦙 zi6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F2.36 寺) 僧舍也又官名如大理寺之類又寺人閹宦也 (F2.36 䦙) 亦作寺閹宦也 (Page 614 寺) An official residence, chambers about a court; a court, an office, a hall (Kangxi: 廷也有法度者也); an eunuch (Kangxi: 宦寺); a monastery, a Budhist temple (Kangxi: ...後名浮屠所居皆曰寺); Budhists; _tsz' mun_ (zi6 mun4 寺門), Budhists; _yap tsz'_ (jap6 zi6-2 入寺) to become a priest; _tai li tsz'_ (daai6 lei5 zi6-2 大理寺) the Court of Appeal at Peking (北京); _tsz' shing_ (zi6 sing4 寺丞), chief clerk in a court; _yau tsz'_ (jau4 zi6 遊寺) to be changed from one office to another; to saunter in a monastery. ~~Used for the next (䦙).~~ (Page 614 䦙; here normalised to 寺) A court, a hall where officers hold sittings (Kangxi 䦙: 廷也有法度者也本作寺); an eunuch (Kangxi: 宦寺); a chamberlain, an officer in waiting; _tsz' yan_ (zi6 jan4 寺人), an eunuch. 【寺】 (ziH) (_noun-like_) court, (_met._) eunuch (_noun-like_) temple Used in 寺人. 【寺人】 (ziH nyin) (_noun-like_) eunuch
U+5DF3 (F2.36 巳) 時辰也 (Page 614 巳) The sixth of the 12 branches, denoted by a snake (Kangxi: ...故巳爲蛇象形), and corresponding to 9--11 A. M. 【巳】 (ziX) (_noun-like_) snake-branch

U+5F58 zai6 (per 《廣韻》直例切) (F1.43 彘) 豬母也 (Page 10* 彘) A sow, a pig (Kangxi: 豕也, 豬也); _yan chi_ (jan4 zi6 人彘) ~~a corpse cut to pieces,~~ alludes to a ~~speech of a princess~~ ``designation made by an empress`` of the Han (漢) dynasty. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿱彑⿲土矢比 to 彘. Williams' 人彘: Neither "a corpse cut to pieces" nor "of a princess", but a designation made by Empress Lu (呂后) after horrifically maiming her rival Lady Ts'ik (戚夫人), as alleged in 《呂太后本紀》. The weight of this phrase cannot be fully comprehended until one reads that text, which describes extreme cruelty such as I will not quote here. For them that wish to spare themselves from that text, the result is that 人彘 "human swine" is used to refer to victims of horrific cruelty. 【彘】 (drjejH) (_noun-like_) swine

U+6D98 (F2.35 涘) 水涯又音哀 (Page 614 涘) The banks of a river (Kangxi: 水厓也), the shores or mouth of a stream; _shui tsz'_ (seoi2 zi6 水涘) the bank. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》牀史切. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 竢 to 涘. 【涘】 (zriX) (_noun-like_) riverbank, bank
U+7278 (F2.36 牸) 牝牛 (Page 614 牸) A cow (Kangxi: 牝牛); the female of domesticated animals (Kangxi: 牝馬亦曰牸, 牸雌也); _tsz' ma_ (zi6 maa5 牸馬), a mare.

U+75D4 (F1.43 痔) 痔瘡 (Page 10* 痔) The piles (Kangxi: 後病也, 隱瘡也); ~~to gnaw, as insects do;~~ _chi ch'ong_ (zi6 cong1 痔瘡), piles; _noi chi_ (noi6 zi6 內痔) bloody piles; _ngoi chi_ (ngoi6 zi6 外痔) blind piles; _ch'un ch'eung chi_ (cyun1 coeng4 zi6 穿腸痔) fistula in ano. Williams' "to gnaw, as insects do": This is a misreading of Kangxi 痔食也蟲食之也, which is an (erroneous) explanation from 《釋名》 that haemorrhoids are (or caused by) some parasite feeding on the person. 【痔】 (driX) (_noun-like_) piles

U+7940 (F2.36 祀) 祭祀 (Page 614 祀) To sacrifice to gods or ~~devils~~ ``ancestral spirits``, to offer sacrifices (Kangxi: 祭也); sacrificial; a sacrifice; a year (Kangxi: 年也); like, as if (Kangxi: 祀者似也); _mo tsz'_ (mou4 zi6 無祀) no descendants; _un tsz'_ (jyun4 zi6 元祀) first year of a reign; _tsz' shan_ (zi6 san4 祀神), to sacrifice to the gods; _tsz' tso_ (zi6 zou2 祀祖), to worship ancestors. 【祀】 (ziX) (_verb-like_) to sacrifice (_noun-like_) annus

U+7A1A 稺 穉 (F1.43 稚) 幼小之稱 (F1.43 穉) 同上 (Page 10* 穉稚) Small, delicate (Kangxi 稺: 凡人物幼小皆曰稺); young, tender; young grain, or that which ripens late (Kangxi 稺: 幼禾也, 晚禾); _t'ung chi_ (tung4 zi6 童稚) a youngster; _chi hi_ (zi6 hei3 稚氣) childish, immature; _chi tsz'_ (zi6 zi2 稚子) a boy. 【稚】 (drijH) (_noun-like_) immature-grain (_adjective-like_) immature (_verb-like_) to sow late
U+7A49 (F1.43 稚) 幼小之稱 (F1.43 穉) 同上 (Page 10* 穉稚)
U+7AE2 (Page 614 俟竢) Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chi_ per 《廣韻》牀史切. Fan Wan entry: The headword 竢 is a misprint of 涘.

U+7B25 si3 (per 《廣韻》相吏切) (F2.36 笥) 竹筒 (Page 614 笥) A hamper, a basket (Kangxi: 飯及衣之器也); _shu tsz'_ (syu1 zi6 書笥) a portable book and writing-case; _fuk tsz'_ (fuk1 zi6 腹笥) learned, well read. 【笥】 (siH) (_noun-like_) hamper

U+801C 㭒 ci5 (not recast to departing) (F2.36 耜) 耒耜耕田所以起土者 (Page 614 耜) A ploughshare (Kangxi: 臿也, 耜者耒之金也); a colter, the part of a plough which enters the earth; a plough. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 𮋦 to 耜. 【耜】 (ziX) (_noun-like_) ploughshare, plough

U+81EA (F2.35 自) 自己獨也從來也由也 (Page 613 自) A preposition, from, commencing at (Kangxi: 由也, 從也); a pronoun, self, I (Kangxi: 己也); my own, myself, personally; to use (Kangxi: 用也); the 132d radical ("self"); _tsz' ki_ (zi6 gei2 自己), myself, my own; _ni tsz' ki_ (nei5 zi6 gei2 你自己), you yourself; _tsz' wai_ (zi6 wai4 自為), I did it; _tsz' O chi sheng_ (zi6 ou3 zi3 seng4-2 自澳至城), from Macao to Canton; _tsz' in_ (zi6 jin4 自然), natural, spontaneous, self-existent; certainly, surely; _tsz' tsz' in in ui hiu_ (zi6 zi6 jin4 jin4 wui5 hiu2 自自然然會曉), it will soon become natural and easy to understand; _tsz' in t'ung_ (zi6 jin4 tung4 自然銅), native copper; _pat ts'ing tsz' loi_ (bat1 cing2 zi6 loi4 不請自來), he came uninvited on his own lookout, _i. e._ the responsibility is his own, as a patient undergoing an operation; _tsz' 'ng_ (zi6 ng6 自誤) my fault; _tsz' shi_ (zi6 si6 自是) self-confident, self-righteous; _tsz' tso_ (zi6 zou6 自造) our own manufacture; _tsz' tsz' tsoi tsoi_ (zi6 zi6 zoi6 zoi6 自自在在) in high spirits; _tsz' hau_ (zi6 hau6 自後) henceforth; _tsz' yau chu i_ (zi6 jau5 zyu2 ji3 自有主意) I will manage it; _tsz' ts'am tun kin_ (zi6 cam4 dyun2 gin3 自尋短見) self destruction, suicide; _tsz' tsok tsz' shau_ (zi6 zok3 zi6 sau6 自作自受) he gets what he gave; _tsz' shik tsz'_ (zi6 sik6 zi6 自食自) I find my own food; _tsz' loi ko shi_ (zi6 loi4 go2 si4 自來嗰時), ever since that time; _tsz' chi t'ai kwo_ (zi6 zi1 taai3 gwo3 自之太過) presumptuous. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 巳 to 己. Williams' 自來嗰時: Changed _ko_ (go3) to modern _ko_ (go2). Of itself, naturally (Kangxi: 自然無勉强也). 【自】 (dzijH) (_pronoun-like_) self (_adjective-like_) own (_preposition-like_) since, from (_adverb-like_) naturally (_conjunction-like_) if (_conjunction-like_) though
U+4999 (F2.36 䦙) 亦作寺閹宦也 (Page 614 䦙)

U+96C9 (F1.43 雉) 野雞 (Page 10* 雉) A pheasant (Kangxi: 雉有十四種 etc.), including also francolins; an embrasure on a wall; ~~to rule;~~ ~~to put in order;~~ _chi mi_ (zi6 mei5 雉尾), pheasant's tail feathers, used by actors; _chi tip_ (zi6 dip6 雉堞) parapet walls; _chi kai_ (zi6 gai1 雉雞), the Tartar pheasant; _chi mun_ (zi6 mun4 雉門), the south door of a seraglio. Williams' "to rule", "to put in order": These are attempts at rendering Kangxi 雉理也. The text of 《揚子方言》 is 紕繹督雉理也...凡物曰督之絲曰繹之. The common meaning of the characters presented in this passage is beyond me, but my guess would be _to elicit_. A unit of wall area (Kangxi: 方丈曰堵三堵曰雉). 【雉】 (drijX) (_noun-like_) pheasant (_noun-like_) perch

U+98DF 飤 飼 (F2.35 食) 以食餵物曰食又飯也 (Page 614 飤飼) Interchanged with the next (食). (Page 614 食) To feed, to set food before (Kangxi: 以食與人也); to bring up (Kangxi: 食謂乳養之也, 食養生也); food (Kangxi: 食飯屬也), provision (Kangxi: 糧也); _ngau yeung yan sho tsz'_ (ngau4 joeng4 jan4 so2 zi6 牛羊人所食) men rear sheep and oxen. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 偎 to 餵. 【食-departing】 (ziH) (_verb-like_) to feed
U+98E4 (Page 614 飤飼)
U+98FC 飤 (F2.35 飼) 以食食人也 (Page 614 飤飼) To feed, to nourish (Kangxi: 以食食人也); to set food before; to rear, as animals; feed, provisions, eatables (Kangxi 飤: 糧也, 食也與飼同).

zik1.text: U+52E3 (F1.74 勣) 功也勞也 (Page 569 勣) Merit, praiseworthy acts (Kangxi: 功也); meritorious deeds, conduct worthy of reward.

U+5373 卽 ze1 (lazy in 即係) (F1.74 卽) 就也山也則也 (Page 568 卽; here normalised to 即) ~~Eating, about~~ ``Nearing`` to eat (Kangxi 卽: 卽猶就也就食也); an adverb, now, immediately, soon, presently, when, then, forthwith; now (Kangxi 卽: 今也); near (Kangxi 卽: 卽尼也, 尼近也); if, perhaps (Kangxi 卽: 卽猶若也); ~~this,~~ that is, or, alias; the snuff of a candle (Kangxi 卽: 燭炬之燼曰卽); _tsik tsik_ (zik1 zik1 即即) an abundance, full of; _tsik hak_ (zik1 hak1 即刻) instantly; _tsik yat_ (zik1 jat6 即日) the same day; _tsik hai_ (zik1 hai6 即係) just that, it is so; _tsik kam_ (zik1 gam1 即今), just now; _tsik ~~shi~~ ``sz'`` kan_ (zik1 si2 gaan1 即使間), supposing it to be; _nin man tsoi tsik_ (nin4 maan5 zoi6 zik1 年晚在即) the end of the year is near; _tsik tsik fan ~~loi~~ ``lai``_ (zik1 zik1 faan1 lai4 即即返嚟), come back immediately; _tsik shi tang kan_ (zik1 si4 dang2 gan2 即時等緊), it is wanted very soon; _tsik tsik in_ (zik1 zik1 jin6 即即現) money on the nail. Williams' 即使間: Corrected _shi_ (si4) to _sz'_ (si2). Williams' 即即返嚟: Corrected _loi_ (loi4) to _lai_ (lai4). To ascend, to assume a position. (_copula_) To be (precisely). 【即】 (tsik) (_verb-like_) to approach (_verb-like_) to take place (_adverb-like_) forthwith (_adverb-like_) that is, which is (_verb-like_) to be (_conjunction-like_) though
U+537D (F1.74 卽) 就也山也則也 (Page 568 卽)

U+5832 May appear as either ⿱即土 (+7 assumed here) or ⿱卽土 (+9), depending on the font. zat1 (per 《廣韻》資悉切 for "fired-brick" and "ember" senses) zik6 (per 《廣韻》秦力切 for "abhor" senses) (F1.75 堲) 疾也書經堲讒說 (Page 568 堲) To make bricks (Kangxi: 火熟曰堲); ~~to make an arched grave;~~ the snuff of a candle, embers (Kangxi: 火之餘燼曰堲); to dislike, to have a horror of, to snuff at (Kangxi: 堲疾也). The reading _tsik_ (zik1) is not unattested for the "fired-brick" sense; 《集韻》 has the reading 節力切, explained 燒土周棺也. Kangxi has the "abhor" sense under 子力切 _tsik_ (zik1). However, 《廣韻》 explains this reading as 風堲 (which I do not comprehend), with the "abhor" sense instead given under 秦力切 _tsik_ (zik6). Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰糸⿱免丿 to 讒. Williams' "to make an arched grave": This is a poor rendering of Kangxi 堲者冶土爲？而四周于棺之坎也. The idea here is that fired-brick is used to encase about the four sides of a coffin (四周于棺). 【堲-abhor】 (dzik) (_verb-like_) to abhor 【堲-fired-brick】 (tsit) (_noun-like_) fired-brick
U+5E58 zaak3 (per 《廣韻》側革切) (F1.75 幘) 巾幘 (Page 569 幘) A turban or cloth wound around the head (Kangxi: 髮有巾曰幘 etc.); a kerchief used instead of a cap.

U+755F cik1 (per 《廣韻》初力切) (F1.75 畟) 田器之尖利者 (Page 569 ~~？~~``畟``) ~~A~~ sharp ~~agricultural instrument~~ like a mattoc (Kangxi: 治稼畟畟進也, 畟畟耜也, 言嚴利); ~~to attend to husbandry.~~ The reading _tsik_ (zik1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 節力切, explained 畟畟耜利也. Whilst 《廣韻》 also has the reading 子力切, it is without explanation (it simply cross-references _ch'ik_ (cik1) as 又初力切). Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿳由儿夂 to 畟, and corrected 犬 to 尖. 【畟】 (tsrhik, tsik) (_adjective-like_) sharp

U+7A4D zi3 (per 《廣韻》子智切 for "store" sense) (F1.74 積) 聚也蓄也累也 (Page 569 積) To gather, to hoard up, to accumulate (Kangxi: 聚也), to store (Kangxi: 聚也儲蓄也); to increase, to add to, to pile upon (Kangxi: 累也); increasingly; _fat tsik_ (faat3 zik1 發積) to accomplish, to succeed in; _shui tsik mai_ (seoi2 zik1 maai4 水積埋), water standing, as in puddles; _tui tsik_ (deoi1 zik1 堆積) to heap up, as rubbish; _tsik fuk_ (zik1 fuk1 積福) much happiness derived from good works; _tsik nin_ (zik1 nin4 積年), during many years, for a long time; _tsik ch'uk_ (zik1 cuk1 積蓄) to hoard or lay by. A product (result of multiplication), area, or volume. 【積】 (tsjek, tsjeH) (_verb-like_) to accumulate (_noun-like_) product, area, volume

U+7E3E 勣 (for "achievement" and "merit" senses) (F1.74 績) 襞績紡績又功績 (Page 569 勣) Interchanged with the next (績) (Kangxi 勣: 通作績). (Page 569 績) To twist hempen threads (Kangxi: 緝也) on the knee; ~~to split hempen fibres;~~ merit by laudable works (Kangxi: 績功也); ~~an affair, a business;~~ to complete, to bring to a conclusion (Kangxi: 事也成也); ~~to join, to piece;~~ _tsik ma_ (zik1 maa4 績麻), to twist hemp; _kung tsik_ (gung1 zik1 功績) works of ~~public~~ merit. Williams' "public merit": Public is 公, not 功. An achievement (Kangxi: 績業也). To continue (Kangxi: 績繼也). 【績】 (tsek) (_verb-like_) to twist (thread) (_noun-like_) achievement

U+8077 (F1.76 職) 官職品秩也職任職掌 (Page 13* 職) To govern, to rule (Kangxi: 執掌也); to attend to; ~~to record;~~ ~~to act officially;~~ having the direction of (Kangxi: 職主也); official duty (Kangxi: 職事也); title, office; tribute, offerings or presents of other states (Kangxi: 貢也); _chik shau_ (zik1 sau2 職守), to direct; _chik yam_ (zik1 jam6 職任) in office, the duties of an office; _ka chik_ (gaa1 zik1 加職) to confer a title; _pi chik_ (bei1 zik1 卑職) my office; _chik un_ (zik1 jyun4 職員), honorary title; _kun chik_ (gun1 zik1 官職) official title; _chik cheung_ (zik1 zoeng2 職掌), to superintend; _kak chik_ (gaak3 zik1 革職) to deprive of a title; _lok chik_ (lok6 zik1 落職) to deprive of office; _shau chik_ (sau6 zik1 受職) to hold an office; _chik chik_ (zik1 zik1 職職) many (Kangxi: 職職多也); _chik fan_ (zik1 fan6 職分) a title, official duties. Williams' "to record": This is a misreading of Kangxi 記微也, "knower of minutiae". Here, 記 means "know", not "record". Williams' 職員: In modern usage, "employee". Exclusively (Kangxi: 專也). 【職】 (tsyik) (_noun-like_) office, duty (_verb-like_) to officiate (_adverb-like_) exclusively

U+8DE1 蹟 迹 (F1.74 蹟) 足蹟 (F1.74 跡) 形跡踪跡 (F1.74 迹) 同上 (Page 569 迹跡蹟) A trace, a foot-mark, a foot-step (Kangxi 迹: 步處也, 足跡也); vestiges, effects, consequences, signs (Kangxi 迹: 凡有形可見者皆曰迹); the results of previous conduct (Kangxi 迹: 凡功業可見者曰迹); to trace out, to follow up, to search for the effects of (Kangxi 迹: 循實而考之亦曰迹); _keuk tsik_ (goek3 zik1 腳跡) a foot-print; _shan tsik_ (san4 zik1 神跡) evidences of divine power, miracles; _ying tsik ho i_ (jing2 zik1 ho2 ji4 影跡可疑), a suspicious appearance; _mo ying tsik_ (mou4 jing2 zik1 無影跡) no clue of him; _shing tsik_ (sing3 zik1 勝跡) renowned, magnificent, as a view; _ping mo tsung tsik_ (bing6 mou4 zung1 zik1 並無蹤跡) I can find out nothing about him; _yan tsik_ (jan3 zik1 印跡) a stain; _han tsik_ (han4 zik1 痕跡) a streak, as of dirt; bad feelings left in the mind. A vestige, a remnant (Kangxi 迹: 凡前人所遺留者曰迹). To follow, to adhere to (Kangxi 迹: 凡有所遵循亦曰迹). 【跡】 (tsjek) (_noun-like_) footprint, footstep, trace, (_met._) accomplishment

U+8E16 cik1 (per 《集韻》七迹切 for "with demeanour/bearing" sense) zik6 (per 《廣韻》秦昔切 and 《集韻》祥亦切 for "stride/tread over" sense) (Page 569 踖) To stride (Kangxi: 長脛行也); to straddle or step over ``or step on`` a thing (Kangxi: 踖猶躐也, 踐也); to walk reverently, to step in a formal manner, to measure one's steps (Kangxi: 一曰踧踖也, 踧踖敬貌, 踧踖恭敬也). Williams' "step over": Or, to step *on*. 躐也 is ambiguous. With (reverent?) demeanour or bearing (Kangxi: ...有容也, ...有容儀也). 【踖-step】 (dzjek) (_verb-like_) to step on, to step over 【踖-demeanour】 (tshjek) Used in 踖踖. 【踖踖】 (tshjek tshjek) (_adverb-like_) with reverent demeanour 【踖-reverential】 (tsjek) Used in 踧踖. 【踧踖】 (tsjuwk tsjek) (_adjective-like_) moving reverently, reverential
U+8E5F (F1.74 蹟) 足蹟 (F1.74 跡) 形跡踪跡 (F1.74 迹) 同上 (Page 569 迹跡蹟)
U+8FF9 (F1.74 蹟) 足蹟 (F1.74 跡) 形跡踪跡 (F1.74 迹) 同上 (Page 569 迹跡蹟)

U+965F (F1.76 陟) 我也進也 (Page 13* 陟) To ascend, to enter on a higher office (Kangxi: 陟陞也, 登也); promoted, advanced (Kangxi: 進也); ~~to mount, to go up;~~ to advance, to proceed to; _chik lam_ (zik1 lam4 陟臨), to behold from on high, as God does; _chik kong_ (zik1 gong3 陟降) promotion and degradation. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿰阝歩 to 陟. The explanation 我也 is erroneous. 【陟】 (trik) (_verb-like_) to ascend

zik6.text: U+57F4 May appear with right component as 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.78 埴) 土之黏膩者 (Page 14* 埴) Adhesive clay (Kangxi: 黏土也); _t'un chik_ (tyun4 zik6 摶埴) to mold in clay; _chak chik_ (zaak6 zik6 擿埴) to feel one's way with a stick (Kangxi: 瞽者以杖擿地而後行謂之擿埴). 【埴】 (dzyik) (_noun-like_) clay

U+5915 (F1.78 夕) 朝夕 (Page 570 夕) Evening, dusk (Kangxi: 晨之對暮也); late; last day of, as a year or month; out of the perpendicular (Kangxi: 斜也); the 36th radical ("evening"); _chiu tsik_ (ziu1 zik6 朝夕) morning and evening; _ch'u tsik_ (ceoi4 zik6 除夕) last day of the year; _kam tsik_ (gam1 zik6 今夕) this evening. An evening meeting (Kangxi: 朝見曰朝夕見曰夕). 【夕】 (zjek) (_noun-like_) evening

U+5BC2 (F1.78 寂) 寂寞幽靜也 (Page 570 寂) Still, silent, as an uninhabited house (Kangxi: 無人聲也); quiet, unmoved, like a recluse (Kangxi: 靜也安也); solitary, desolate, lonesome; _tsik tsing_ (zik6 zing6 寂靜) a dead silence; _un tsik_ (jyun4 zik6 圓寂) completely silent, said of a priest's death. Williams' "recluse": In the (now) archaic sense of a place of seclusion. 【寂】 (dzek) (_adjective-like_) still, silent

U+6B96 May appear with right component as 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.78 殖) 貨殖生殖 (Page 13* 殖) To grow, to increase, to flourish (Kangxi: 生也, 蕃也長也)~~,~~``;`` to enrich, to amass~~;~~``,`` as money (Kangxi: 興生財利曰殖); to plant (Kangxi: 種也); ~~to raise the price of;~~ gain; ~~price, value;~~ rotten ~~bones~~ (Kangxi: 脂膏久殖也); _shang chik_ (sang1 zik6 生殖) to grow, to increase; _chik fo_ (zik6 fo3 殖貨) to ~~raise the price~~ ``accumulate commodities``; _cheung chik man mat_ (zoeng2 zik6 maan6 mat6 長殖萬物) nature flourishes. To cultivate (Kangxi: 封殖也). To establish (Kangxi: 立也). Level and upright (Kangxi: 殖殖平正也). 【殖】 (dzyik) (_verb-like_) to grow (_verb-like_) to cultivate (_verb-like_) to set up (_adjective-like_) level
U+6C50 (F1.78 汐) 潮汐朝曰潮夕曰汐 (Page 570 汐) The evening tide, high tide at sunset (Kangxi: 海濤早曰潮夕曰汐); the name of a stream (Kangxi: 水名).

U+6E5C sat6 (common) (F1.78 湜) 水淸見底也 (Page 14* 湜) Pure, limpid water, through which the bottom can be seen (Kangxi: 水淸底見也); pure-minded; to maintain firmly (Kangxi: 一曰持正貌). Fan Wan entry: Corrected 㡳 to 底. 【湜】 (dzyik) (_adjective-like_) clear

U+76F4 May appear as the right component of 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. zik6-2 (rising second in vernacular 直直地) (F1.78 直) 正直不偏開也伸也常也但也 (Page 13* 直) To look ``straight`` ahead (Kangxi: 正見也); straight, direct (Kangxi: 正也, 不曲也); straightforward, upright, true; just, exactly; to stretch off (Kangxi: 伸也), to proceed, to go direct; to make straight, to straighten; only, merely, but (Kangxi: 猶但也); purposely (Kangxi: 猶故也); stiff and straight; _ching chik_ (zing3 zik6 正直) upright; _chik in_ (zik6 jin4 直言), to speak the truth; _kang chik_ (gang2 zik6 梗直) inflexibly right; _chik ch'ing hu_ (zik6 cing4 heoi3 直程去) go directly there; _yat chik hu_ (jat1 zik6 heoi3 一直去) go on straight; _chik tai shang_ (zik6 dai6 saang2 直隸省), the superintending province (_i. e._ Chihli (直隸)); _chik tai chau_ (zik6 dai6 zau1 直隸州), an inferior department; _mok sun chik chung chik_ (mok6 seon3 zik6 zung1 zik6 莫信直中直) do not believe everything called true; _chik loi ni ch'u_ (zik6 loi4 ni1 cyu3 直來呢處) came straight here; _shan chik keuk_ (san1 zik6 goek3 伸直腳) legs stretched out, _i. e._ dead; _chik sz'_ (zik6 si6 直士) a headman in a neighborhood. Smooth (Kangxi: 順也). Suitable (Kangxi: 猶宜也). To right a wrong (Kangxi: 理枉曰直). Speech-assist, specifically an initial particle (Kangxi: 語發聲). 【直】 (drik) (_adjective-like_) straight (_adjective-like_) straightforward, direct (_adverb-like_) just 【直程】 (zik6 cing4) Directly. 直程返去 (zik6 cing4 faan1 heoi3): "Directly go back." Simply, truly, clearly, especially used exaggeratively. 直程冇得頂 (zik6 cing4 mou5 dak1 ding2): "Straight up can't be beat." 直程係啦 (zik6 cing4 hai6 laa1): "It clearly is!" Used to imply decisiveness or extremeness of an action. 我直程報警㗎 (ngo5 zik6 cing4 bou3 ging2 gaa3): "I won't hesitate to just call the police!" 直程走咗 (zik6 cing4 zau2 zo2): "He simply left." 【直頭】 (zik6 tau4) Synonym of 直程.

U+7A19 May appear with right component as 値 or 值 (assumed here), depending on the font. zik1 (per 《廣韻》竹力切) (F1.78 稙) 早種曰稙 (Page 14* 稙) Grain planted early, the first crop (Kangxi: 早種禾也); to sow in good time (Kangxi: 先種曰稙後種曰穉); the first wife (Kangxi: 靑徐人謂長婦曰稙長). 【稙】 (trik) (_verb-like_) to sow early

U+7A78 (F1.78 穸) 窀穸墓穴幽堂也穸夜也言穴中厚暗如長夜也 (Page 570 穸) ~~Dark, obscure, as a winter night;~~ a vault; ~~a niche or recess in~~ a sepulchre (Kangxi: 窀穸也); _chun tsik_ (zeon1 zik6 窀穸) the tomb, the grave. Williams' "Dark, obscure, as a winter night": This is taking Kangxi 《註》窀厚也穸夜也...一曰...長夜謂之穸 as a definition for 穸, when it is simply an attempt in commentary to explain the glyphs 窀穸.

U+7C4D 藉 (F1.78 籍) 書籍典籍又册籍也 (Page 570 ~~？~~``籍``) A book for records (Kangxi: 書籍)~~, a tablet~~; a list, a register of the people (Kangxi: 戸籍); the place for enroling, one's native place; _shu tsik_ (syu1 zik6 書籍) books; _ui tsik_ (wui4 zik6 回籍) to return to one's home; _un tsik_ (jyun4 zik6 原籍) place where one's family originated; _lok tsik_ (lok6 zik6 落籍) to be registered; _sheung tsik_ (soeng1 zik6 雙籍) enrolled in two places; _mo tsik yau man_ (mou4 zik6 jau4 man4 無籍遊民), vagrants, gypsey-like, without origin. Williams' 雙籍: Now 雙重國籍 for "dual nationality". Land tax (Kangxi: 租籍). (The emperor) personally tilling the field (Kangxi: 籍田 etc.). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【籍】 (dzjek) (_noun-like_) register, book (_adjective-like_) personally-tilled

U+85C9 籍 ze3 (common for "mat" and "borrow" senses) ze6 (per 《廣韻》慈夜切 for "mat" and "borrow" senses and per Kangxi 慈夜切 for "generous" sense) (F1.78 藉) 賴也借也 (Page 570 ~~？~~``籍``; here normalised to 藉) _long tsik_ (long4 zik6 狼藉) notorious, infamous; (Page 570 藉) A mat on which things can be presented (Kangxi: 祭藉也); to borrow; ~~to assist, to help;~~ to use for a while; to borrow from, to avail of, to make a means; by; in all which senses it is often read _tse_ (ze3); in confusion (Kangxi: 狼藉離披雜亂貌)~~, disturbed~~; liberal, generous (Kangxi: 寬博有餘也); to lead, as by a ~~string~~ ``rope`` (Kangxi: 藉繩也); _tsik fuk_ (zik6 fuk1 藉福) by your kindness, I am well; _tsik hau_ (zik6 hau2 藉口), to make a pretext, to gammon; _tsik shan pi yau_ (zik6 san4 bei3 jau6 藉神庇佑) he relied on Divine help; _tse tsik_ (ze6 zik6 謝藉) to rely on for assistance; _tsik shai_ (zik6 sai3 藉勢) to use the authority of others. Williams' "to assist, to help": This is a misreading of Kangxi 助者藉也. This is an example from Mencius, where 藉也 "borrowing/dependence" is used to explain the specific instance of 助 in the earlier 殷人七十而助. It does *not* define 藉 as "assist/help". Tribute (Kangxi: 藉謂貢獻). 【藉-departing】 (dzjaeH) (_noun-like_) straw-mat (_verb-like_) to depend (upon) (_verb-like_) to borrow, to lend 【藉-entering】 (dzjek) (_noun-like_) tribute

zim3.text: U+4F54 (F2.50 占) 霸占擅據也 (Page 16* 占佔)
