zim1.text: U+5360 詹 zem1 (for vernacular "jam" sense) (F2.47 占) 騐也測也候也 (Page 15* 占) To divine, to cast lots; divination, casting lots (Kangxi: 視兆問也); to observe, to wait for a verification (Kangxi: 𠋫也, 視也); to look towards (Kangxi: 占亦瞻也); _chim pat ling_ (zim1 bat1 ling4 占不靈), a false prediction; _chim sun ha_ (zim1 syun3 haa5 占算吓), cast a lot about it; _chim kwa_ (zim1 gwaa3 占卦) to prognosticate; _chim im_ (zim1 jim6 占驗) a verification of lots; _chim hau_ (zim1 hau6 占候) to foretell seasons; _chim ~~chiu~~ ``shiu``_ (zim1 siu6 占兆) to see signs, to foretell by signs. Williams' 占兆: Changed _chiu_ (ziu6) to common reading _shiu_ (siu6). 【占-level】 (tsyem) (_verb-like_) to prognosticate 【占】 (zem1, zim1) Jam (fruit preserve). 果占 (gwo2 zem1): "Fruit jam." 油占多 (jau4 zim1 do1): "Butter jam toast."

zim3.text: U+5360 佔 (F2.50 占) 霸占擅據也 (Page 16* 占佔) To usurp, to seize, to take forcibly (Kangxi: 固有也, 擅據也著位也); to assume, to arrogate, to take the liberty; to possess (Kangxi: 有也); _pa chim_ (baa3 zim3 霸占) to take possession of; _chim sin hu_ (zim3 sin1 heoi3 占先去) to assume precedence; _chim siu shin_ (zim3 siu2 sin6 占小善) ~~to praise one's self;~~ _chim p'in i_ (zim3 pin4 ji4-2 占便宜), to take what one likes, to assume more for one's self; _chim shan fan_ (zim3 san1 fan6 占身份) to regard one's place, to exercise one's rights. The second character (佔) is commonly but incorrectly used in this sense. Fan Wan entry: Corrected illegible character to 據. Williams' "to praise one's self": This is a misreading of 占小善 from Kangxi's example 《韓愈進學解》占小善者率以錄, which should be rendered "they that possess even a little quality are all employed". Williams' "incorrectly used": The form 佔 is "incorrect" to the extent that, in classical usage, it is an alternative form of 覘 rather than departing-tone 占. See 覘 zim1. 【占-departing】 (tsyemH) (_verb-like_) to occupy (_verb-like_) to possess

zim1.text: U+5C16 (F2.48 尖) 小也 (Page 571 尖) Sharp, pointed, acute, tapering to a peak or point (Kangxi: 末銳也小也); wedge-like, pyramidal, conical, or needle-shaped; clever, ingenious; _ting tsim_ (ding2 zim1 頂尖), ready at, smart; _yuk tsim_ (juk6 zim1 玉尖), a lady's fingers (Kangxi: 玉尖十尖指也); _tsim li_ (zim1 lei6 尖利) sharp, quick-witted; _ta tsim_ (daa2 zim1 打尖), to wedge in, to work one's self in; _pan tsim hai_ (baan1 zim1 haai4 班尖鞋), to dress in women's shoes, to act the female parts. Harsh or ear-piercing (of sound). 【打尖】 (daa2 zim1) To push in. 【尖隊】 (zim1 deoi6-2) To push in line.

U+6CBE 霑 (F2.47 沾) 漬也濡也又沾恩 (Page 15* 沾) To moisten, to tinge (Kangxi: 漬也濡也); to receive benefits; to participate in; to be a recipient of; obliged, benefited; imbued with; infected, affected; _chim kwong_ (zim1 gwong1 沾光), got it through your favor; _fu lok kwan chim_ (fu2 lok6 gwan1 zim1 苦樂均沾), sorrow and joy equally bestowed; _chim yan chak_ (zim1 jan1 zaak6 沾恩澤) received benefits; _chim peng_ (zim1 beng6 沾病) to catch a disease; _chim im shai ts'ing_ (zim1 jim5 sai3 cing4 沾染世情), to be corrupted by bad influences; _ni chim cheuk yau tik 'm ho p'i hi_ (nei5 zim1 zoek6 jau5 dik1 m4 hou2 pei4 hei3 你沾着有滴唔好脾氣) you have contracted a bad temper. (Page 15* 霑) Interchanged with the last (沾). Williams' 沾恩澤: Corrected _chak_ (zaak3) to _chak_ (zaak6). Williams' 你沾着有滴唔好脾氣: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). 【沾】 (trjem) (_verb-like_) to moisten, to soak

U+77BB 詹 (F2.47 瞻) 瞻仰 (Page 16* 瞻) To look up to, to reverence, to regard with respect (Kangxi: 仰視曰瞻); _chim yeung_ (zim1 joeng5 瞻仰), to look up to; to have an audience; _yeung chim shan tau_ (joeng5 zim1 saan1 dau2 仰瞻山斗), I look up to you, said to a sage or magnate; _chim mong_ (zim1 mong6 瞻望) to long for, to anticipate; _chim pi yat ut_ (zim1 bei2 jat6 jyut6 瞻彼日月) I look at the sun and moon, _i. e._ on my conscience; _chim shi_ (zim1 si6 瞻視) to expect. To look forward. 【瞻】 (tsyem) (_verb-like_) to look (at) (_verb-like_) to look up (to)

U+7C98 黏 nim4 (per 《廣韻》女廉切) (F2.47 粘) ？也 (F2.47 秥) 秥米 (Page 15* 粘) (Page 16* 黏; here normalised to 粘) Used for the preceding (粘); rice; _chim mai_ (zim1 mai5 粘米), white rice, of which there are many kinds, as _sut chim_ (syut3 zim1 雪粘), _ai miu chim_ (ai2 miu4 zim1 矮苖粘), _chim tsai_ (zim1 zai2 粘仔), &c. Fan Wan entry: The first character of the explanation for 粘 is incomprehensible, but appears like ⿰苗月.

U+8998 佔 占 沾 cim1 (per 《廣韻》丑廉切) cim3 (per 《廣韻》丑豔切) (F2.47 覘) 闚視也 (Page 15* 覘) To peep, to spy at, to glance at (Kangxi: 窺也); _chim hau_ (zim1 hau6 覘候) to wait for; _chim kin_ (zim1 gin3 覘見) to take a look at. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰古見 to 覘. 【覘】 (trhjem, trhjemH) (_verb-like_) to spy (upon)

U+8A79 (F2.47 詹) 姓亦竹名 (F2.47 𦧕) 同上 (Page 16* 詹) ~~Tattling~~, wordy (Kangxi: 多言也); to reach (Kangxi: 至也); ~~at, extreme, such a time;~~ to examine and direct; _chim chim_ (zim1 zim1 詹詹), loquacious; _kan chim_ (gan2 zim1 謹詹), carefully ~~provide~~ ``select``. Probably cognate with 譫 zim1. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿸厃⿳八土口 to 𦧕. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【詹】 (tsyem) (_adjective-like_) verbose (_verb-like_) to arrive

U+8B6B (F2.47 譫) 多言也 (Page 16* 譫) Wordy, loquacious (Kangxi: 多言也); _peng chim_ (beng6 zim1 病譫), out of his head, incoherent; _chim u_ (zim1 jyu5 譫語), wandering talk. Probably cognate with 詹 zim1. Delirium (Kangxi: 病也).

U+9711 (F2.47 霑) 霑足雨之多也 (Page 15* 霑) A drizzling, dripping, pattering, rain (Kangxi: 雨𩃵也); to wet through (Kangxi: 霑濕也又濡也漬也); soaked, saturated; _chim tsuk_ (zim1 zuk1 霑足) wet to the skin; _chim i_ (zim1 ji1 霑衣), wet clothes. 【霑】 (trjem) (_verb-like_) to moisten, to soak
U+9ECF (F2.47 粘) ？也 (F2.47 秥) 秥米 (Page 16* 黏) Fan Wan entry: The first character of the explanation for 粘 is incomprehensible, but appears like ⿰苗月.

zim6.text: U+9B59 zaam2 (per Kangxi 側減切) (Page 728 魙) A prince of demons (Kangxi: 鬼名), at the sight of whom ogres and vampires flee; used as a charm (Kangxi: 辟邪也符尾也).

zin1.text: U+5103 sin4 (per 《廣韻》市連切) taan4 (per 《廣韻》徒干切) (F1.20 儃) 態也又儃回不進也 (Page 16* 儃) Manner, air, figure (Kangxi: 態也); look; _chin ui_ (zin1 wui4 儃佪), undecided whether to go on, irresolute (Kangxi: 儃佪不進貌). Williams' 儃佪: Corrected _ui_ (wui1) to _ui_ (wui4). 【儃-level】 (dzyen) (_adjective-like_) hesitating

U+65C3 (F1.20 旃) 通白之器又解曰之 (Page 16* 旃) A silken banner (Kangxi: 通帛爲旃); a ``flag with a`` crooked flag-staff (Kangxi: 旗曲柄也); ~~to signalize one to come.~~ Contraction of 之焉. 【旃】 (tsyen) (_noun-like_) banner Contraction of 之焉 (`tsyi hjen`).
U+6BE1 (F1.20 氊) 以毛爲物曰氊 (F1.20 毡) 同上 (Page 16* 毡氊)

U+6C08 旃 毡 氊 (F1.20 氊) 以毛爲物曰氊 (F1.20 毡) 同上 (Page 16* 毡氊; here normalised to 氈) Hair or wool worked into a felt (Kangxi 氈: 撚毛也); coarse woolens, as blankets, carpets, rugs; _ti chin_ (dei6 zin1 地氈), a carpet; _chin mo_ (zin1 mou6-2 氈帽) a felt hat; _pak chin_ (baak6 zin1 白氈), a blanket; _hai chin_ (haai4 zin1 鞋氈), felt for soles; _ch'euk chin_ (coek3 zin1 桌氈), a table-cover; _chin pau_ (zin1 baau1 氈包), a carpet-bag; _chin chi_ (zin1 zi2 氈紙), coarse rough paper. 【氈】 (tsyen) (_noun-like_) felt
U+6C0A (F1.20 氊) 以毛爲物曰氊 (F1.20 毡) 同上 (Page 16* 毡氊)
U+6E54 (F1.20 湔) 湔洗 (Page 573 湔) To sprinkle with the hand, to bespatter (Kangxi: 手瀚也灑也傍沾也); name of a river in Sz'chuen (四川) (Kangxi: 水名). To wash.

U+714E (F1.20 煎) 熬煎 (Page 572 煎) To fry, to roast``, to decoct`` in a pan (Kangxi: 熬也, 火去汁也, 熟煑也, 火乾也, etc.); to vex, to harass; _tsin ko_ (zin1 gou1 煎膏), to express lard; _yung yau tsin_ (jung6 jau4 zin1 用油煎), use fat in frying it; _man fo tsin u_ (maan6 fo2 zin1 jyu4-2 慢火煎魚), a slow fire tries fish; _i. e._ small bets will erelong drain the purse; _tsin nung_ (zin1 nung1 煎燶), to fry brown; _ngo tsin_ (ngou4 zin1 熬煎), to simmer, to decoct~~; vexed, irritated~~. Williams' 煎燶: Corrected _nung_ (nung4) to _nung_ (nung1). Williams' 熬煎: Corrected _ngo_ (ngou6) to _ngo_ (ngou4). To shallow-fry. To smelt. 【煎】 (tsjen) (_verb-like_) to decoct

zin3.text: U+714E (Page 572 煎) Read _tsin_ (zin3); to decoct, to boil, to digest with heat; to simmer; _tsin chap_ (zin3 zap1 煎汁) to fry in its own juices, as fruit; _tsin t'ong_ (zin3 tong1 煎湯), to boil slowly; _tsin yeuk_ (zin3 joek6 煎藥) to decoct medicines. Classically, the "decoct" sense is in the level tone. The (vernacular?) reading in the departing tone is perhaps to distinguish it from the "shallow-fry" sense. Used in 甲煎 (a fragrant concoction) (Kangxi: 甲煎香名).

zin1.text: U+76BD (Page 17* 皽) ~~To tear off, as a scab or placard;~~ to flay; to rip up; _chin hi_ (zin1 hei2 皽起), rip it up; _chin lat_ (zin1 lat1 皽甩) rip it off.

zin2.text: U+76BD ziu1 (alternative per 《廣韻》止遥切) (Page 17* 皽 _Chin_ (zin1); here corrected to _Chin_ (zin2)) thin scurf skin, a scab (Kangxi: 離也, 皮寬也, 皮肉之上魄膜也); 【皽】 (trjenX, tsyenX) (_noun-like_) skin-crust

zin1.text: U+7B8B 牋 (F1.20 箋) 簡牒 (Page 573 箋) Tablets or slips on which to make memoranda (Kangxi: 表識書也); a letter, a writing (Kangxi: 書也); note paper with water-line or stamped pictures; writings handed to the monarch; _tsin chi_ (zin1 zi2 箋紙), fancy notepaper; _ts'at hong tsin_ (cat1 hong4 zin1 七行箋), seven lined billet paper; _lap tsin_ (laap6 zin1 蠟箋), glazed billet paper. A commentary at a lower level than a 傳 or 註, used self-deprecatingly (Kangxi: 聖人制作曰經賢者著述曰傳...謙敬不敢言註但表識其不明者耳). 【箋】 (tsen) (_noun-like_) lesser-note

U+7C5B (F1.20 籛) 彭祖姓 (Page 573 籛) A man's name, called _Tsin Hang_ (zin1 hang1 籛鏗), or Tsien Hing, who lived in the Shang (商) and Chau (周) dynasties, to the age of 767, ~~and then vanished~~ ``yet waned not`` (Kangxi: 彭祖姓籛名鏗...歷夏殷周年七百六十七歲而不衰). Williams' "man's name": Specifically, a surname (Kangxi: 姓也).

U+7FB6 羴 膻 sin1 (per 《廣韻》式連切) (F1.20 羶) 腥羶羊脂也 (Page 17* 羶) The rank oder of goats (Kangxi: 羊臭也, 羊脂也羊氣也); frowzy; ~~sheep's fat, mutton tallow;~~ _ch'au chin_ (cau3 zin1 臭羶), fetid, rank; _seng chin_ (seng1 zin1 腥羶), frowzy. Williams' "sheep's fat, mutton tallow": Here, Williams has been misled by Kangxi 羊脂也, which (I think) is intended not just as "sheep's fat", but should be interpolated to "the smell of sheep's fat". 【羶】 (syen) (_noun-like_) rank-odour
U+81BB

U+9085 zin6 (per 《廣韻》持碾切 for "turn" sense and 除善切 for "shift/move" sense) (F1.20 邅) 迍邅 (Page 17* 邅) Unable to advance (Kangxi: 迍邅難行不進貌); _chun chin_ (zeon1 zin1 迍邅), making no progress, unlucky in life, unfortunate. Kangxi erroneously has 𧾍上聲 and 𧾍去聲 the wrong way round. To turn (Kangxi: 轉也逐也). To shift, to move (Kangxi: 移也). 【邅-departing】 (drjenH) (_verb-like_) to turn 【邅-level】 (trjen) (_adjective-like_) hindered
U+986B sin1 (per 《集韻》尸連切) (F1.20 顫) 四肢奏掉曰顫 (Page 17* 顫) Discerning of smells (Kangxi: 謂審於氣臭也). 【顫-level】 (syen) (_noun-like_) discernment of smells

zin3.text: U+986B zin1 (per Fan Wan) (F1.20 顫 _Chin_ (zin1)) 四肢奏掉曰顫 (Page 17* 顫 _Chin_ (zin1); here normalised to _Chin_ (zin3)) ~~The head awry (Kangxi: 頭不正也);~~ shivering with cold; the limbs cold and trembling (Kangxi: 四支寒動也); fearful, frightened; _sz' chi chin tung_ (si3 zi1 zin3 dung6 四肢顫動) trembling all over; _hon chin_ (hon4 zin3 寒顫), shivering. Williams' "The head awry": According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, 頭不正也 should instead be 頭不定也 "the head not steady". In other words, shaking or trembling. 【顫-departing】 (tsyenH) (_verb-like_) to tremble

zin1.text: U+9958 zin2 (alternative per 《廣韻》旨善切) (F1.20 饘) 粥也 (F1.20 飦) 同上 (Page 17* 饘) Congee or gruel made from rice, thoroughly boiled (Kangxi: 糜也); _chin chuk_ (zin1 zuk1 饘粥) congee. 【饘】 (tsyen, tsyenX) (_noun-like_) porridge

U+9C63 (F1.20 鱣) 魚名 (Page 17* 鱣) ~~A sea-monster;~~ a large sturgeon, or perhaps a grampus (Kangxi: 大魚似鱏 etc.); _chin k'ing_ (zin1 king4 鱣鯨), whales and grampuses, applied to an unscrupulous, grasping man. 【鱣】 (trjen) (_noun-like_) great-sturgeon
U+9E07 (F1.20 鸇) 食雀之鳥 (Page 17* 鸇) A falcon, goshawk, or kite (Kangxi: 鷙鳥); a yellow bird which pursues pigeons (Kangxi: 鸇似鷂黃色...擊鳩鴿燕雀食之); a sparrow-hawk. 【鸇】 (tsyen) (_noun-like_) hawk

zin2.text: U+526A 翦 (F1.22 剪) 裁也又較剪 (F1.22 翦) 齊也截以殺也 (Page 573 翦剪) to cut off smooth, to clip even (Kangxi 翦: 翦齊也); to shear; shears, scissors; _yat pa tsin to_ (jat1 baa2 zin2 dou1 一把剪刀), a pair of scissors; _tai kau tsin_ (daai6 gaau3 zin2 大鉸剪), shears; _chap tsin_ (zaap6 zin2 鈒剪), betel-nut shears; _ts'oi tsin_ (coi4 zin2 裁剪), to cut out garments; to manage, to plan; Fan Wan entry: Corrected 栽 to 裁 and ⿰糸殳 to 殺. Williams does not provide separate entries for 剪 and 翦. In modern usage, the "scissors" and "cut" senses is spelt 剪, whilst the other (classical) senses are spelt 翦. I have split things up accordingly, with duplication where appropriate. Williams' 鈒剪: Corrected _chap_ (zaap3) to _chap_ (zaap6). 【鉸剪】 (gaau3 zin2) Scissors. Also spelt 較剪 (per Fan Wan). 把鉸剪鈍 (baa2 gaau3 zin2 deon6): "The pair of scissors is blunt."

U+5C55 (F1.22 展) 舒展 (Page 17* 展) To open out, to unroll (Kangxi: 開也)~~, and inspect~~; to exhibit (Kangxi: 展陳也), to disentangle; to stretch out at one's ease (Kangxi: 舒也), to expand; ``to inspect,`` to judge of, to look into (Kangxi: 審也視也, 展省閱也); true, sincere (Kangxi: 一曰誠也); cheerful, pleasant; ~~gratified;~~ _yuk chin_ (juk6 zin2 玉展), may you open this; _chin hon_ (zin2 hon3 展看) to open and look at; _chin hoi_ (zin2 hoi1 展開), to spread out, to open (as a letter); _chin mi_ (zin2 mei4 展眉), a pleasant face; _chin un_ (zin2 wun6 展緩) to defer, to put off to get time; _chin han_ (zin2 haan6 展限) to extend the time. To record (Kangxi: 展猶錄也). To implement, to bring about. 【展】 (trjenX) (_verb-like_) to unfurl, to exhibit (_verb-like_) to examine (_adverb-like_) verily
U+6229 (F1.22 戩) 盡也 (Page 573 戩) to finish, to exhaust; entirely (Kangxi: 盡也); _pat tsin_ (bat1 zin2 不戩), unlimited. A blessing. 【戩】 (tsjenX) (_adjective-like_) complete
U+78BE (F1.22 碾) 轢也又音研 (Page 17* 碾)

U+7FE6 剪 戩 (F1.22 剪) 裁也又較剪 (F1.22 翦) 齊也截以殺也 (Page 573 翦剪) Even, regular, as feathers grow (Kangxi 翦: 羽生也); to cut off smooth, to clip even (Kangxi 翦: 翦齊也, 齊斷也); to kill, to destroy (Kangxi 翦: 殺也, 翦斷滅之言也); to reduce, to clip off (Kangxi 翦: 翦削也); light, as a dye (Kangxi 翦: 翦淺也); _tsin tsin_ (zin2 zin2 翦翦), subtle at argument; _tsin ti k'au wo_ (zin2 dei6 kau4 wo4 翦地求和), to give up territory and beg for peace. (Page 573 戩; here normalised to 翦) To destroy, to kill, to exterminate (Kangxi 戩: 與翦通, 滅也); to clip, to shear; Fan Wan entry: Corrected 栽 to 裁 and ⿰糸殳 to 殺. Williams does not provide separate entries for 剪 and 翦. In modern usage, the "scissors" and "cut" senses is spelt 剪, whilst the other (classical) senses are spelt 翦. I have split things up accordingly, with duplication where appropriate. Utterly (Kangxi: 翦盡也). Pale (Kangxi: 翦淺也). 【翦】 (tsjenX) (_verb-like_) to clip, to cut, (_met._) to annihilate (_adverb-like_) utterly (_adjective-like_) pale

U+8AD3 cin5 (per 《廣韻》慈演切) zin1 (alternative per 《集韻》將先切) zin6 (per 《廣韻》才線切) (F1.20 諓 _Tsin_ (zin1)) 巧言也 (Page 573 諓) To flatter (Kangxi: 諂也); pleasant, kind words (Kangxi: 善言也); sly insinuations (Kangxi: 巧讒貌); _tsin tsin_ (zin2 zin2 諓諓), shallow, artful talk (Kangxi: 巧言也, 淺薄貌). The reading _tsin_ (zin2) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 子淺切, explained 諓諓淺薄皃一曰巧言也一曰善也. 【諓】 (dzjenX, dzjenH) (_adjective-like_) artful
U+8B2D (F1.22 謭) 謭劣粗也陋也 (Page 573 謭)

U+8B7E 謭 (F1.22 謭) 謭劣粗也陋也 (Page 573 謭; here normalised to 譾) Shallow-pated, superficial, not profound; unskilled, shallow, not deep (Kangxi: 淺也); _ts'oi tsin_ (coi4 zin2 材譾), mediocre, of small capacity; _tsin lut_ (zin2 lyut6 譾劣) mean, insignificant. Williams' 譾劣: Changed _lut_ (lyut3) to _lut_ (lyut6).

U+8F3E 展 (F1.22 輾) 轉之半曰輾 (Page 17* 輾) To roll over half way (Kangxi: 輾者轉之半也); to turn over (Kangxi: 轉也); _chin chun fan ~~ch'ak~~ ``chak``_ (zin2 zyun2 faan2 zak1 輾轉反側) to turn back and forth, to think on. Williams' 輾轉反側: Corrected _ch'ak_ (cak1) to _chak_ (zak1). 【輾】 (trjenX) (_verb-like_) to roll (over), (_met._) to toss

zin3.text: U+6230 (F1.25 戰) 閩戰又恐懼貌 (Page 17* 戰) To fight (Kangxi: 鬭也); ~~to be alarmed,~~ struck with dread (Kangxi: 懼也); fearful~~, anxious~~; a battle, an engagement; war, hostilities; military, pertaining to war; _hut chin_ (hyut3 zin3 血戰) a bloody fight; _chin shing_ (zin3 sing3 戰勝) a victory; _ta chin_ (daa2 zin3 打戰) terrified; _chin tau_ (zin3 dau3 戰鬥) or _kau chin_ (gaau1 zin3 交戰) to join battle; _chin ~~ch'an~~ ``chan``_ (zin3 zan6 戰陳), troops drawn up for battle; _chin pai_ (zin3 baai6 戰敗) to be discomfited; _chin chin king king_ (zin3 zin3 ging1 ging1 戰戰兢兢), frightened, afraid of; _chin shun_ (zin3 syun4 戰船), a war-junk, a ship of war; _chin shu_ (zin3 syu1 戰書), a challenge to fight. Fan Wan entry: 閩 is probably a typo for 鬪 or similar. Williams' 戰陳: Corrected _ch'an_ (can4) to _chan_ (zan6). Williams' 戰敗: Corrected _pai_ (baai3) to _pai_ (baai6). 【戰】 (tsyenH) (_verb-like_) to battle (_verb-like_) to tremble

U+6D0A 瀳 zin6 (per 《廣韻》在甸切) (F1.23 洊) 重復也 (Page 573 洊) ~~Water flowing on;~~ a stream bursting forth again; repeated (Kangxi 洊: 通薦); _tsin lik_ (zin3 lik6 洊歷) successively. Water arriving (Kangxi 瀳: 水至也).
U+726E (F1.23 牮) 屋斜用牮 (Page 574 牮) A prop to shore up a leaning house (Kangxi: 屋斜用牮); a sluice or weir of earth or stone to lead water (Kangxi: 以土石遮水亦曰牮). Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰余刂 to 斜.

U+7BAD (F1.23 箭) 弓箭 (Page 573 箭) An arrow (Kangxi: 矢也); an archer; a bowshot; to dart out very quickly; swift, like an arrow; a slender sort of bamboo (Kangxi: 竹之小者曰箭, 箭篠也); ~~bristling;~~ _she tsin_ (se6 zin3 射箭) to shoot an arrow; _kung tsin_ (gung1 zin3 弓箭) bow and arrow; _lau tsin_ (lau6 zin3 漏箭) the gauge in a clepsydra; _fo tsin_ (fo2 zin3 火箭) a fire arrow; _heung tsin_ (hoeng2 zin3 響箭) a whirring arrow; _ho tsin_ (hou2 zin3 好箭) a good shot; _ma tsin_ (maa5 zin3 馬箭) mounted archers; _om tsin sheung yan_ (am3 zin3 soeng1 jan4 暗箭傷人), to injure one with a secret arrow---slander; _fong lang tsin_ (fong3 laang5 zin3 放冷箭) to shoot at privily; _yat tsin chi ti_ (jat1 zin3 zi1 dei6 一箭之地) a bowshot, or 120 _ch'ek_ (cek3 尺). Williams' 火箭: Now "rocket". Williams' 放冷箭: Changed _lang_ (lang5) to common reading _lang_ (laang5). 【箭】 (tsjenH) (_noun-like_) arrow (_noun-like_) small-bamboo 【箭嘴】 (zin3 zeoi2) An arrow. 紅箭嘴 (hung4 zin3 zeoi2): "Red arrow." 綠箭嘴 (luk6 zin3 zeoi2): "Green arrow."

U+8350 洊 薦 zin6 (per 《廣韻》在甸切) (F1.23 薦) 舉薦祭亦曰薦 (F1.23 荐) 同上 (Page 573 荐) also to continue; ~~some;~~ to succeed, to recur repeatedly (Kangxi: 重也, 仍也), as a famine; repeatedly, again. Nomadically (Kangxi: 荐草也言逐水草而居徙無常處). 【荐】 (dzenH) (_noun-like_) grass, (_met._) nomadically (_adverb-like_) repeatedly

U+85A6 墊 (for "padding" senses) 荐 din3 (for 墊 "padding") din6 (per Williams for 墊 "padding") zin3-2 (vernacular rising-tone for "padding" senses) (F1.23 薦) 舉薦祭亦曰薦 (F1.23 荐) 同上 (Page 519 墊 _Tin_ (din6); here normalised to 薦 _Tsin_ (zin3)) To lay down, to place on; to place under, to wedge; _i ~~tin~~ ``tsin``_ (ji2 zin3 椅薦) a chair cushion; _t'ang ~~tin~~ ``tsin``_ (tang4 zin3-2 藤薦) rattan table mats; _k'ong ~~tin~~ ``tsin``_ (kong3 zin3 炕薦) a long sofa cushion; _~~tin~~ ``tsin`` yuk_ (zin3 juk6-2 薦褥) a mattress; (Page 573 薦) Grass eaten by animals (Kangxi: 獸之所食草也); pasturage; ~~to lead animals to good pastures;~~ to introduce, to recommend; to lay out (Kangxi: 進也) food (Kangxi: 未食未飮曰薦 etc.); to adore, to worship without a victim (Kangxi: 無牲而祭曰薦); to wedge, to wedge in; _tsin t'oi keuk_ (zin3 toi4-2 goek3 薦檯腳) to wedge the table; _tsin tso_ (zin3 zou2 薦祖), to worship ancestors; _ku tsin_ (geoi2 zin3 舉薦) to recommend one; _se tsin shu_ (se2 zin3 syu1 寫薦書), to write an introductory letter; _yau tsin t'au_, (jau5 zin3 tau4 有薦頭) there is some opening; _fu tsin_ (fu6 zin3 附薦) to get a soul out of purgatory by priests; _tsin ts'oi_ (zin3 coi3 薦菜) take a little greens---with your wine; said to a guest; _tsin hi_ (zin3 hei2 薦起) raise it up. (Page 573 荐) Interchanged with the last (薦); A straw mat (Kangxi 荐: 荐薦席也). 【薦】 (tsenH) (_noun-like_) grass-fodder (_noun-like_) padding (_verb-like_) to present, to recommend 【薦-noun】 (zin3, zin3-2) A pad, cushion, or mat. 杯薦 (bui1 zin3): "A cup-coaster." 鞋薦 (haai4 zin3-2): "Shoe padding". 【薦-verb】 (zin3) To pad or cushion underneath. 薦底 (zin3 dai2): "Padding out the bottom" (i.e. coming last, especially in a higher division or more prestigious environment). 薦鐺底 (zin3 caang1 dai2): "To cushion under the bottom of the pot." 冇嘢薦住 (mou5 je5 zin3 zyu6): "Nothing cushioning it underneath." Olden attestation for 薦 meaning "mat/cushion": 《說文》葬：藏也从死在茻中一其中所以薦之... 《說文》枕：臥所薦首者... 《廣韻》作甸切 薦 薦席又薦進也... Strictly speaking, the original word for "mat/cushion" is 荐 _tsin_ (zin6), with 薦 _tsin_ (zin3) being a later spelling based on phonetic nearness. In modern usage, the spelling 墊 is common for _tsin_ (zin3), from appropriation of formal 墊 _tin_ (din3). However, note that 墊 _tin_ (din3) meaning "padding" is not attested in the Middle Chinese rime books.

U+991E cin5 (per 《廣韻》慈演切) zin6 (per 《廣韻》才線切) (F1.23 餞) 以酒食送行曰餞 (Page 574 餞) To present food to one, to give a farewell dinner to (Kangxi: 送去也, 酒食送也); ~~to send food to;~~ parting present of ~~money or~~ food (Kangxi: 餞者進飮食之名也); comfits; _tsin hang_ (zin3 hang4 餞行), to give a dinner to one traveling; _tsin pit_ (zin3 bit6 餞別) a leave-taking dinner; _mat tsin t'ong kwo_ (mat6 zin3 tong4 gwo2 蜜餞糖果), comfits and sweetmeats; _tsin lai_ (zin3 lai5 餞禮), presents to a traveler. To send off by accompanying (Kangxi: 凡送皆曰餞). 【餞】 (dzjenX, dzjenH) (_verb-like_) to departure-feast, to send off

zin6.text: U+8CE4 (F1.25 賤) 卑下也 (Page 574 賤) Worthless, vile, mean, low, ignoble, base (Kangxi: 卑下也不貴也); cheap, low-priced (Kangxi: 賈少也); to disesteem, to depreciate, to lower (Kangxi: 輕賤); used for what belongs to one's self; _p'an tsin_ (pan4 zin6 貧賤) poor and mean; _tsin noi_ (zin6 noi6 賤內) my wife; _tsin sing Wai_ (zin6 sing3 wai6 賤姓衛) my surname is Wai (衛); _tsin seung_ (zin6 soeng3 賤相) a poor physiognomy, a thief's face; _tsin kwat t'au_ (zin6 gwat1 tau4 賤骨頭), a miserable stick; _tsin yan_ (zin6 jan4 賤人), you poor baggage! said of women; _ka ts'in ho tsin_ (gaa3 cin4 hou2 zin6 價錢好賤) the price is very low; _tsin kak_ (zin6 gaak3 賤格) a miserable lot, of poor talents; _tsin mat tau k'ung yan_ (zin6 mat6 dau3 kung4 jan4 賤物鬥窮人), the poor may meddle with cheap things; _tsok tsin_ (zok3 zin6 作賤) he disgraced himself. Williams' 賤人: Now (and in classical illeism) said of both sexes. 【賤】 (dzjenH) (_adjective-like_) cheap, lowly (_verb-like_) to hold cheap 【賤格】 (zin6 gaak3) Vile, base, despicable.

zing1.text: U+5075 cing1 (per 《廣韻》丑貞切) (F1.68 偵) 伺探也 (Page 18* 偵) A spy (vulgo, _ye pat shau_ (je6 bat1 sau1 夜不收)), a scout; to reconnoitre, to inquire (Kangxi: 𠋫也探伺也); _yau ching_ (jau4 zing1 遊偵) or _ching t'am_ (zing1 taam3 偵探) to spy. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 何 to 伺. Williams' 夜不收: Corrected _shau_ (sau4) to _shau_ (sau1).

U+5F81 正 (F1.68 征) 往也取也伐也又正也 (Page 18* 征) To go (Kangxi: 行也)``, to expedition``; to take (Kangxi: 征取也); to collect taxes; to go out to chastise, to reduce refractory states (Kangxi: 征伐也, 征者上伐下也), to subjugate, to conquer; ~~to spy;~~ _ching fat_ (zing1 fat6 征伐) to reduce to subjection; _ching t'o_ (zing1 tou2 征討), to ~~collect tribute~~ ``punish`` by force; _ching fu_ (zing1 fu1 征夫), warriors; _ching shau_ (zing1 sau1 征收), to assess taxes; _ching chu_ (zing1 zyu1 征誅), to exterminate; _ch'ut ching_ (ceot1 zing1 出征), to go abroad on an expedition; _ching shui leung_ (zing1 seoi3 loeng4 征稅糧), to levy taxes; _ching tsiu_ (zing1 ziu2 征勦), to exterminate rebels. Williams' 征伐: Changed _fat_ (faat6) to modern _fat_ (fat6). 【征】 (tsyeng) (_verb-like_) to (make) expedition (_verb-like_) to correct punitively (_verb-like_) to take (_verb-like_) to levy

U+65CC sing1 (common) (F1.67 旌) 旌旗又旌表 (Page 575 旌) A banner, a standard having plumes, which when waved encourage the troops (Kangxi: 析羽注旄首所以進士卒); to make signals; insignia, signals; ~~to discriminate,~~ to make manifest, to prove, to show forth (Kangxi: 旌旗所以表識貴賤故傳以旌爲識, 旌章也, 旌表也, 旌明也); _tsing k'i_ (zing1 kei4 旌旗), flags; _ming tsing_ (ming4 zing1 明旌), red banners hung by a corpse; _tsing piu_ (zing1 biu2 旌表), testimonials of merit granted by the sovereign, and often placed over the door. Williams' "to discriminate": This is a misreading of Kangxi 《書畢命》旌別淑慝《傳》言當識別頑民之善惡《疏》旌旗所以表識貴賤故傳以旌爲識. Here, 旌 means "signify". It is 別 which means "discriminate". 【旌】 (tsjeng) (_noun-like_) pennon (_verb-like_) to signify

U+6676 (F1.67 晶) 日之光也又水晶 (Page 576 晶) Lustre, brightness (Kangxi: 精光也); clear, pure, light; crystal; crystaline; _shui tsing_ (seoi2 zing1 水晶), quartz crystal; _ch'a tsing_ (caa4 zing1 茶晶), tea-stone, cairngorm stone; _fat tsing_ (faat3 zing1 髮晶), moss agate; _mak tsing_ (mak6 zing1 墨晶), smoky quartz; _hung tsing_ (hung4 zing1 紅晶), rose quartz; _t'in hi tsing_ (tin1 hei3 zing1 天氣晶), clear weather; _shui tsing kung_ (seoi2 zing1 gung1 水晶宮), Neptune's palace.

U+6968 (F1.68 楨) 楨榦 (Page 18* 楨) ~~A kind of fir;~~ _nu ching_ (neoi5 zing1 女楨), a coniferae growing on Taishan (太山) in Shantung (山東) (Kangxi: 太山之上多楨木, 女楨也); _nu ching tsz'_ (neoi5 zing1 zi2 女楨子) the seeds of this tree; _ching kon_ (zing1 gon3 楨榦) ``posts and`` planks with which adobie walls are made (Kangxi: 楨榦築牆所立兩木也); ~~vulgo, _kap ts'eung pan_ (gaap3 coeng4 baan2 夾牆板)~~. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰龺余 to 榦. Williams' 楨榦: Here, 楨 refers to *posts*, not planks. It is 榦 that refers to planks (vulgo 夾牆板). Kangxi has 《孔傳》題曰楨旁曰榦楨當牆兩端者也榦在牆兩邊者也. Hardwood (Kangxi: 剛木也). A support, a pillar. 【楨】 (trjeng) (_noun-like_) support-post

U+70DD 蒸 (F1.68 烝) 衆也 (F1.68 蒸) 衆也進也又薰蒸又冬祭曰蒸 (Page 18* 烝) a multitude, all (Kangxi: 衆也); ~~to enter,~~ to advance (Kangxi: 進也); clouds of dust (Kangxi: 塵也, 人衆所以生塵埃); ~~liberal~~ ``stately``, princely (Kangxi: 君也); a winter sacrifice (Kangxi: 冬祭曰烝, 進品物也); to commit ~~incest with superiors in rank~~ ``adultery with an elder`` (Kangxi: 上淫曰烝); ~~to lay down;~~ _t'in shang ching man_ (tin1 sang1 zing1 man4 天生烝民) heaven produced all men; _ching sheung_ (zing1 soeng4 烝嘗), sacrifice ~~in winter~~; property left by a person; _ching tsun_ (zing1 zeon3 烝進) to intrude one's self; (Page 18* 蒸) Used for the last (烝) (Kangxi 蒸: 通烝). all, numerous (Kangxi 蒸: 衆也); Williams' 烝嘗: Not just winter sacrifice, but sacrifice in general (烝 for winter, and 嘗 for autumn). Air convecting upward from a fire (Kangxi: 火气上行也). Speech-assist, specifically an initial particle (Kangxi: 烝發語辭). 【烝】 (tsying) (_verb-like_) to convect upward, to steam (_verb-like_) to adulter upward (_verb-like_) to put forth, to advance (_adjective-like_) multitudinous (_adjective-like_) stately (_noun-like_) winter-sacrifice (_speech-assist_)

U+7665 (F1.68 癥) 腹内結疾 (Page 19* 癥) Colic arising from obstruction (Kangxi: 腹結病也); obstinate constipation; _ching ~~ha~~ ``ka``_ (zing1 gaa2 癥瘕), obstruction of the bowels with griping. Williams' 癥瘕: Corrected _ha_ (haa1) to _ka_ (gaa2).

U+775B May appear as either ⿰目靑 or ⿰目青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.67 睛) 眼睛 (Page 576 睛) The ball of the eye; the pupil; _ngan hak tsing_ (ngaan5 hak1 zing1 眼黑睛), the iris; _pak tsing_ (baak6 zing1 白睛), the white of the eye; _fat tsing kwong_ (faat3 zing1 gwong1 發睛光), night blindness; _muk pat chun tsing_ (muk6 bat1 zyun2 zing1 目不轉睛), eyes fixed. Kangxi 目珠子也, is to me, a poor definition. It is not clear whether 珠子 is the whole eyeball, from the iris inward, or just the pupil.
U+798E (F1.68 禎) 祥也又善也 (Page 18* 禎) A favourable omen (Kangxi: 祥也); felicitous, lucky; _ching ts'eung_ (zing1 coeng4 禎祥), a happy sign. 【禎】 (trjeng) (_noun-like_) good-omen

U+84B8 烝 (F1.68 烝) 衆也 (F1.68 蒸) 衆也進也又薰蒸又冬祭曰蒸 (Page 18* 烝; here normalised to 蒸) A vapor made by heat, steam; vapor, mist; to steam, to cook by steaming, to distil; _ching shuk ha_ (zing1 suk6 haa5 蒸熟吓), steam it thoroughly; _ching fan_ (zing1 faan6 蒸飯) to steam rice; _ching tsau_ (zing1 zau2 蒸酒), to distil spirit; _ching lung_ (zing1 lung4 蒸籠), a steaming basket; _ching ngo_ (zing1 ngo4-2 蒸鵝) a ~~boiled~~ ``steamed`` goose. (Page 18* 蒸) Twigs of hemp for burning (Kangxi: 折麻中榦也); small firewood (Kangxi: 麤曰薪細曰蒸); ~~hemp torches;~~ vapor, steam; to rise, as vapor or steam; _ching ching yat sheung_ (zing1 zing1 jat6 soeng5 蒸蒸日上), daily rising better and higher. Williams' "to rise, as vapor or steam": Kangxi has this sense under departing-tone _ching_ (zing3) (see 蒸 zing3). 【蒸】 (tsying) (_verb-like_) to steam (_noun-like_) twig

zing3.text: U+84B8 (Page 18* 蒸) Read _ching_ (zing3). The hot reflection of the sun; the sun striking on one; _it t'au ching chu_ (jit6 tau4-2 zing3 zyu6 熱頭蒸住) the sun strikes on me. Vapour or air rising (Kangxi: 氣之上達也).

zing1.text: U+8C9E 偵 (F1.68 貞) 正也潔也淸白也又易元亨利貞 (Page 18* 貞) To divine by shells (Kangxi: 卜問也); chaste, virtuous; pure, correct, moral (Kangxi: 貞正也); uncorrupted; well principled; _ching kit_ (zing1 git3 貞潔) chaste; _ping ching_ (bing1 zing1 冰貞), a pure woman; _ching tsit_ (zing1 zit3 貞節) a chaste widow; _ching lit_ (zing1 lit6 貞烈) inflexibly upright or virtuous. The lower trigram of a hexagram (Kangxi: 內卦曰貞外卦曰悔). Resolved, resolute (Kangxi: 貞定也精定不動惑也). 【貞】 (trjeng) (_verb-like_) to divine (_noun-like_) inner-trigram (_adjective-like_) correct, proper, (_met._) determined

U+9266 (F1.68 鉦) 鐃也 (Page 19* 鉦) ~~Cymbals set in a frame~~ ``Bronze bells with a vertical stick through the middle``, used to signalize a halt in armies (Kangxi: 鐃類也 etc., 鉦以靜之鼓以動之); a cymbal used by priests, a brass tamborine; _kwa t'ung ching_ (gwaa3 tung4 zing1 掛銅鉦), to hang up the cymbal. 【鉦】 (tsyeng) (_noun-like_) halting-bell

zing2.text: U+6574 𢿫 (F1.72 整) 修造也齊也葺也理也 (Page 19* before 整) (The two next characters (整, 正) are often pronounced _cheng_ (zeng).) (Page 19* 整) To even off, to place evenly (Kangxi 𢿫: 齊也); to repair, to mend, to put in order, to make; to arrange; to adjust; to adorn; to make new, to trim up; _ching shik shui_ (zing2 sik1 seoi2 整色水) or _ching ku tso kwai_ (zing2 gu2 zou6 gwaai3 整古做怪) to sham, as poverty; to dissemble, to simulate; _ching k'u kwai ma_ (zing2 keoi5 gwai2 maa5 整佢鬼馬), set a catch for him, as in play; _ching ts'ai_ (zing2 cai4 整齊), to put things in order, to settle; _ching piu_ (zing2 biu1 整錶), to mend a watch; _ching t'in_ (zing2 tin4 整田), to prepare fields for planting; _ching ho_ (zing2 hou2 整好), or _ching fan_ (zing2 faan1 整返), to repair; _~~cheng~~ ``ching`` k'iu sau lo_ (zing2 kiu4 sau1 lou6 整橋修路) to mend bridges and repair roads; _ching chi yan_ (zing2 zi6 jan4 整治人), to correct a man; _ching wai_ (zing2 waai6 整壞), to spoil, to injure. Williams considers _cheng_ (zeng2) a possible reading variation for 整. However, I have never heard 整 read _cheng_ (zeng2). Williams' 整色水: More commonly 整色整水. Williams' 整橋修路: Changed _cheng_ (zeng2) to _ching_ (zing2), and corrected _k'iu_ (kiu1) to _k'iu_ (kiu4). Williams' 整壞: Corrected _wai_ (waai4) to _wai_ (waai6). Whole, entire. Whole (of a number). 【整】 (tsyengX) (_verb-like_) to arrange, to straighten 【整】 (zing2) To make, to create. 整緊蛋糕 (zing2 gan2 daan6 gou1): "Making a cake." 整嘢食 (zing2 je5 sik6): "Make something to eat." To cause to become, to render. 整熱啲 (zing2 jit6 di1): "Make it hotter." 整損手 (zing2 syun2 sau2): "Abrased my hand." 整冧咗 (zing2 lam3 zo2): "Made it fall over". 整爛咗 (zing2 laan6 zo2): "I broke it" (physically). 整壞咗 (zing2 waai6 zo2): "I broke it" (functionally). 【整蠱】 (zing2 gu2) To play a trick or prank on. 【整色整水】 (zing2 sik1 zing2 seoi2) To do the superficial (as cover for lack of substance), to window-dress.

zing3.text: U+653F 正 (F1.72 政) 國政所以止人之不正也又典法曰政 (Page 19* 政) To rule, to govern (Kangxi: 正也 etc.); a standard, a regulator; a treatise, a guide on a subject; government, politics; laws, regulations; _ching sz'_ (zing3 si6 政事) governmental affairs; _ka ching_ (gaa1 zing3 家政) family regulations; _shin ching_ (sin6 zing3 善政) good rule; _ts'ung ching_ (cung4 zing3 從政) in official employ; _nung ching_ (nung4 zing3 農政) farming encyclopedia; a supervisor of agriculture; _ts'at ching_ (cat1 zing3 七政) the sun, moon, and five planets; _ching ling_ (zing3 ling6 政令) official orders. Williams' 家政: Now "home economics". Williams' 從政: Or, "pursuing politics". 【政】 (tsyengH) (_noun-like_) governance

U+75C7 (Page 20* 症) An unauthorized character. Disease, ailments, disorder; _peng ching_ (beng6 zing3 病症) sickness; _kap ching_ (gap1 zing3 急症) ~~a dangerous~~ ``acute`` sickness; _tsap ching_ (zaap6 zing3 雜症) unusual malady.
U+8A3C (F1.72 証) 騐也考也 (F1.72 證) 騐也質也明也 (Page 20* 証) To remonstrate with, to admonish, to expostulate (Kangxi: 諫也). 【証】 (tsyengH) (_verb-like_) to remonstrate (with)

U+8B49 証 (F1.72 証) 騐也考也 (F1.72 證) 騐也質也明也 (Page 20* 證) To prove, to confront, to testify (Kangxi: 告也); to substantiate (Kangxi: 驗也, 𠋫也質也); to prove by authorities; evidence; _ching yan_ (zing3 jan4 證人), a witness; _ching kin_ (zing3 gin3 證見) an eyewitness; _kon ching_ (gon1 zing3 干證) a witness; _ching im_ (zing3 jim6 證驗) to verify; _ching ku_ (zing3 geoi3 證據) evidence. (Page 20* 証) Interchanged with the above (證). Williams' 證據: Corrected _ku_ (geoi6) to _ku_ (geoi3). 【證】 (tsyingH) (_verb-like_) to attest, to prove

zing6.text: U+9756 May appear as either ⿰立靑 or ⿰立青 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.73 靖) 安也又謀也理也審也 (Page 577 靖) To become ~~clean and~~ quiet; small, fine (Kangxi: 一曰細貌); to plan (Kangxi: 謀也), to think carefully on (Kangxi: 思也); to tranquillize, to restore peace, to clear (Kangxi: 和也, 安也), as the sea from pirates; to regulate, to settle disturbances (Kangxi: 理也, 靖治也); _ti fong on tsing_ (dei6 fong1 on1 zing6 地方安靖) the land is now quiet; _tsing yik_ (zing6 jik6 靖逆) to put down the rebels. Williams' "clean": Here, Williams has been misled by Kangxi 立淨也, which is a typo for 立竫也 "standing quiet". Respectful, observant, of few words (Kangxi: 恭己鮮言曰靖). 【靖】 (dzjengX) (_verb-like_) to tranquilise, to (be) quiet (_adjective-like_) tranquil, quiet, (_met._) observant

U+975A May appear as either ⿰靑見 or ⿰青見 (assumed here), depending on the font. (F1.73 靚) 女容徐靚粉白黛黑謂之靚粧 (Page 577 靚) To call, to invite (Kangxi: 召也); ~~to allure;~~ to ornament, to bedizen (Kangxi: 裝飾也); _tsing shik_ (zing6 sik1 靚飾) dark eyebrows and clear skin.

U+975C May appear as either ⿰靑爭 or ⿰青爭 (assumed here), depending on the font. 靚 zing6-2 (rising second in vernacular 靜靜地, 靜靜雞) (F1.73 靜) 寂也定也 (Page 577 靜) Still (Kangxi: 動之對也), silent, ~~impassible,~~ imperturbable; at rest (Kangxi: 息也), quiet (Kangxi: 靜安也); peaceable, mild (Kangxi: 和也); pure (Kangxi: 澄也, 潔淸...), like a pool; no bustle; to judge (Kangxi: 審也); to ponder, to meditate on; to desist (Kangxi: 息也); _sam shui tsing_ (sam1 seoi2 zing6 心水靜) contented, patient; _tsing to liu pat tak_ (zing6 dou3 liu5 bat1 dak1 靜到了不得) very still; _tsing yeung_ (zing6 joeng5 靜養), to cultivate quiet, to nurse one's self at home; _tsing tsing ti_ (zing6 zing6 dei6 靜靜地) keep quiet; _tsing siu siu_ (zing6 siu1 siu1 靜蕭蕭), solitary; no business, no bustle; _p'ing tsing_ (ping4 zing6 平靜) at peace, as a country; _ye tsing_ (je6 zing6 夜靜) the stilly night. (Page 577 靚; here normalised to 靜) still; To plan, to plot (Kangxi: 靜謀也). Chaste (Kangxi: 靜貞靜也). 【靜】 (dzjengX) (_verb-like_) to (be) quiet (_adjective-like_) quiet, still (_adjective-like_) pure 【靜雞雞】 (zing6 gai1 gai1) Synonym of 靜靜雞. 【靜靜雞】 (zing6 zing6-2 gai1) Quietly, stealthily.

zip3.text: U+56C1 jip6 (alternative per 《廣韻》而涉切) (Page 22* 囁) An unbridled tongue (Kangxi: 口無節也); ~~vilifying,~~ grumbling, ``privately`` scolding (Kangxi: 私罵也)~~, slandering~~; _chip ~~su~~ ``u``_ (zip3 jyu4 囁嚅), chattering, querulous (Kangxi: 囁嚅多言也). Williams' 囁嚅: Corrected _su_ (seoi1) to _u_ (jyu4). The mouth moving (Kangxi: 口動也).
U+6174 (F2.51 褶) 衣也 (F2.51 襵) 同上 (F2.51 慴) 同上 (Page 22* 慴) Fan Wan erroneously has 慴 as an alternative form of 褶.

U+63A5 (F2.50 接) 相續也運也受也待也 (Page 579 接) To receive in the hand (Kangxi: 持也受也承也); to receive, to connect, to join (Kangxi: 接續也連也), to succeed; combined with, united to (Kangxi: 合也); to take in, as jobs; to meet, to associate with (Kangxi: 交也, 會也); to take, as reflected light; quick (Kangxi: 捷也); _ying tsip_ (jing4 zip3 迎接) to greet, to go and meet, as a visitor; _tsip p'ui_ (zip3 pui4 接陪), to conduct a visitor to a seat; _tsip tsuk_ (zip3 zuk6 接續) to succeed to, to continue; _mo tak tsip tsuk_ (mou4 dak1 zip3 zuk6 無得接續) nothing comes after it; _tsip yan_ (zip3 jan3 接印) to receive the seals; _tsip fung_ (zip3 fung1 接風), to welcome a friend back; _shing tsip nai shui_ (sing4 zip3 nai4 seoi2 承接泥水), orders received for masonwork; _tsip lam ni_ (zip3 laam5 nei5 接？你), I'll divide the present with you; _tsip ka ma_ (zip3 gaa3 maa5 接駕馬), paranymphs sent for the bride; _kwai tsip_ (gwai6 zip3 跪接) to kneel and receive, as a subaltern does; _ngo tak tsip_ (ngo5 dak1 zip3 我得接) I have got it; _tsip kun ~~t'eng~~ ``t'ing``_ (zip3 gun1 ting4 接官亭), a porch for receiving officers on landing. Williams' 接官亭: Corrected _t'eng_ (teng4) to _t'ing_ (ting4). To catch. To pick up. 【接】 (tsjep) (_verb-like_) to connect, to contact (_verb-like_) to succeed (_verb-like_) to receive (_adjective-like_) (_met._) near (_adverb-like_) quickly

U+647A (F2.51 摺) 摺叠 (Page 22* 摺) To fold, to double up (Kangxi: 摺疊也); (...), to destroy (Kangxi: 敗也), (...); to pile up; to bend, as the knee; a fold, a doubling over; _yat chip chip_ (jat1 zip3 zip3 一摺摺) folds, as of a fan; _chip mai kan p'o_ (zip3 maai4 gaan1 pou3 摺埋間舖) to fold (or close) a shop's business; _p``'a``u chip tsz``'``_ (paau2 zip3 zi2 跑摺子), a fleet courier; _chip tsz'_ (zip3 zi2 摺子) a document or report for the emperor; _chip ~~t'ip~~ ``tip``_ (zip3 dip6 摺疊) to heap up or on; _chip mai_ (zip3 maai4 摺埋), to shut up, as a book; _chip chi_ (zip3 zi2 摺紙), to fold paper; _chip kok_ (zip3 gok3 摺角) to turn down dog's ears; _shau chip_ (sau2 zip3 手摺) a folded paper for memoranda; _chip kam ngan_ (zip3 gam1 ngan4 摺金銀), to fold paper to burn as clothes. Williams' 跑摺子: Inferred unprinted characters _'a_ in _p'au_, and fixed missing apostrophe at the end of _tsz'_. Williams' 摺疊: Corrected _t'ip_ (tip3) to _tip_ (dip6). A folded booklet. 【摺埋】 (zip3 maai4) To fold, to shut up (of a business).
U+696B (Page 578 楫) An oar, a paddle, or whatever is used to propel a boat (Kangxi: 舟櫂也, 行舟具); to row; to avail of for one's purposes. 【楫】 (tsjep) (_noun-like_) oar

U+6D79 (F2.50 浹) 浹洽融？通徹也 (Page 578 before 浹) (This character (浹) is also read _hap_ (haap)~~ and _hip_ (hip)~~.) (Page 578 浹) ~~Interchanged with _hip_ (hip) 挾 to assist.~~ Water flowing through (Kangxi: 徹也); moistened, imbibed, damp (Kangxi: 謂潤澤浹洽相霑徹也); to instil, to penetrate; a complete turn of, a circuit (Kangxi: 周浹猶周匝也); _tsip yat_ (zip3 jat6 浹日) a decade (Kangxi: 浹日謂從甲至癸也); _tsip shan_ (zip3 san4 浹辰) twelve days or hours, a circuit of the 12 branches; _tsip hap_ (zip3 hap1 浹洽) fully imbued with, to interpenetrate; to treat cordially, as friends. Fan Wan entry: The incomprehensible character looks like 液 minus 亻. Williams records _hap_ (haap) and _hip_ (hip) as alternative readings without specifying the tone. The former is consistent with 《集韻》轄夾切 _hap_ (haap6) for 浹 used in 浹渫. The latter is dubious. Note that the similar character 𠗉 has three readings: 胡頰切 _hip_ (hip6), explained 𠗉𠗨冰凍. 子協切 _tsip_ (zip3), explained 𠗉𠗨 with cross reference 又音狎. 胡甲切 _hap_ (haap6), explained 𠗉𠗨冰凍相著. Williams' 浹日: Here, "decade" means ten days (甲 through 癸). Williams' 浹洽: Changed _hap_ (haap6) to common reading _hap_ (hap1). 【浹】 (tsep) (_verb-like_) to soak through (_noun-like_) cycle
U+776B (F2.50 睫) 目睫目旁毛也 (Page 579 睫) Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword (with right component like 偼) to 睫.

zit6.text: U+776B zip3 (per 《廣韻》即葉切) zit3 (common) (F2.50 睫 _Tsip_ (zip3)) 目睫目旁毛也 (Page 579 睫 _Tsip_ (zip3); here normalised to _Tsit_ (zit6)) The eye-lashes (Kangxi: 目旁毛也); _met._ the eye; _sheung ~~tsip~~ ``tsit``_ (soeng6 zit6 上睫) the upper eye-lashes. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword (with right component like 偼) to 睫.

zip3.text: U+8975 褶 (F2.51 褶) 衣也 (F2.51 襵) 同上 (F2.51 慴) 同上 (Page 22* 襵) To fold garments (Kangxi: 詘也); to plait, to double; plaits, frounces, gathers (Kangxi: 謂衣襞積), like those of a Chinese lady's petticoat; plaited. For the vernacular form, see 褶 zaap3.

U+8F12 zit3 (common) (F2.51 輒) 急然也專也卽也就也 (Page 22* 輒) The sides of a ~~a~~ carriage (Kangxi: 車兩輢也), where arms are stuck; unceremoniously, suddenly, abruptly (Kangxi: 忽然也); directly, without permission (Kangxi: 專輒也); _chip in_ (zip3 jin4 輒然), forthwith. Williams' 輒然: Also "unmoving". The feet unable to move past (or cross over) one another (Kangxi: 足疾也, 輒者何兩足不能相過也 etc.). Immediately. Always, invariably, consistently. 【輒】 (trjep) (_adverb-like_) immediately (_adverb-like_) consistently

zit3.text: U+6298 sit6 (per 《廣韻》常列切 for "broken" sense) (F1.25 折) 損也耗也 (Page 22* 折) To sunder, to snap in two, to break off (Kangxi: 拗折也); to fold, to bend (Kangxi: 屈也); to oppress, to repress (Kangxi: 挫也); to reprehend (Kangxi: 直指人過失也); to decide, to discriminate (Kangxi: 斷之也); to deduct; to stop (Kangxi: 止也), to injure; to lose one's heir (Kangxi: 父喪子曰折); an act of a play; to exchange, to lose in trade; to make amends for, to set over against; to abase, to lower; ~~unmarried~~ ``to die early, especially before marriage`` (Kangxi: 短折不祿也, 未婚曰折); _chit tsit ha kau_ (zit3 zit3 haa6 gaau1 折節下交), to condescend to men of low estate; _chit yuk_ (zit3 juk6 折獄) to ~~have a jail delivery~~ ``make judgement in a litigation``; _mui p'at chit leung i shek_ (mui5 pat1 zit3 loeng4 ji6 sek6 每匹折量二石) each piece was reckoned as two stone of corn; _'m chit tak_ (m4 zit3 dak1 唔折得) cannot cheapen it; can't break it; _ki shing chit_ (gei2 sing4 zit3 幾成折) what discount will you make? _chit shik_ (zit3 sik6 折食) a composition in money for supplying viands; _chit kwai_ (zit3 gwai3 折桂) to pluck the cassia twig --- to be a graduate; _chit ho kam tik_ (zit3 hou3 gaam2 dik1 折耗減滴) to discount a trifle; a little broken; _ta chit_ (daa2 zit3 打折) broken off; _chit mo_ (zit3 mo4 折磨), greatly afflicted; _tseung kung chit tsui_ (zoeng1 gung1 zit3 zeoi6 將功折罪) to atone for error by meritorious conduct; _chit sun_ (zit3 syun2 折損), to break; _chit ka_ (zit3 gaa3 折價) to lower the price. Kangxi has the adjectival (or intransitive?) "broken" sense under the reading 食列切 _shit_ (sit6). Williams' 幾成折: Corrected _shing_ (sing1) to _shing_ (sing4). To turn, to change direction (Kangxi: 曲也). To destroy (Kangxi: 毀也). A burial frame (Kangxi: 葬具也 etc.). A setback, a difficulty. To admire, to be convinced of. 【折-voiced】 (dzyet) (_adjective-like_) broken 【折-unvoiced】 (tsyet) (_verb-like_) to break (off), (_met._) to decide, to die early (_verb-like_) to bend (_noun-like_) burial-frame 【折】 (zit3) A discount. 冇得折 (mou5 dak1 zit3): "No discount." 折咗都係貴 (zit3 zo2 dou1 hai6 gwai3): "Even discounted it's still expensive." 九折 (gau2 zit3): "Nine-tenths discount" (i.e. 10% off). 七五折 (cat1 ng5 zit3): "Seven-tenths-five discount" (i.e. 25% off).

zit1.text: U+64F3 May appear as either ⿰扌⿱𥫗即 (+13 assumed here) or ⿰扌⿱𥫗卽 (+15), depending on the font. 㘉 折 瀄 (for "squirt" senses) (Page 22* 折; here normalised to 擳) A colloquial word. To tickle; to splash up, to spatter at; to squirt at, as from a hose; _chit nan shan_ (zit1 nan2 san1 擳㤻身), splash me all over. 【擳】 (zit1) To tickle. 怕擳 (paa3 zit1): "Ticklish." To squeeze, to pinch. 擳暗瘡 (zit1 am3 cong1): "Pop a pimple." 擳牙膏 (zit1 ngaa4 gou1): "Squeeze toothpaste" (used figuratively for slow progress in small amounts). A small amount. 擳都唔笑 (zit1 dou1 m4 siu3): "Not a pinch of laughter." To squirt, to spray. 擳到周圍都係 (zit1 dou3 zau1 wai4 dou1 hai6): "Squirted it all over the place."

zit3.text: U+54F2 喆 嚞 悊 (F1.25 哲) 明哲 (F1.25 喆) 同上 (Page 22* 哲喆) Wise, perspicacious, sage (Kangxi: 哲智也, 哲知也); to know intuitively; versed in; to be fully aware of; _ming chit_ (ming4 zit3 明哲) to know thoroughly; _pat chit_ (bat1 zit3 不哲) dull-headed. 【哲】 (trjet) (_adjective-like_) wise
U+5586 (F1.25 哲) 明哲 (F1.25 喆) 同上 (Page 22* 哲喆)

U+5A55 zip3 (per 《廣韻》即葉切 for "woman-officer" sense) zip6 (per 《集韻》疾葉切 for "beautiful" sense) (Page 728 婕) Handsome, elegant (Kangxi: 美貌); a female officer, called _tsit u_ (zit3 jyu4 婕妤), a sort of palatial housekeeper (Kangxi: 婕妤婦官).

zit6.text: U+6377 zip6 (per 《廣韻》疾葉切) zit3 (common in certain compounds, e.g. 快捷, 捷克) (F1.27 捷) 勝也敏疾也報勝曰捷報 (F2.50 捷 _Tsip_ (zip3)) 勝也 (Page 580 捷) ~~To obtain a victory;~~ successful, victorious (Kangxi: 尅也); a victory (Kangxi: 勝也); to complete (Kangxi: 成也); to announce, as a victory or promotion (Kangxi: 報勝曰捷); hastily, prompt, quick (Kangxi: 佽也疾也急也), as a herald; ~~joyful news;~~ _kap tsit_ (gap1 zit6 急捷) nimble, fleet; _tsit king_ (zit6 ging3 捷徑) to take a short cut, a compendious way; _tsit po_ (zit6 bou3 捷報) to hasten with good tidings; _lin tsit_ (lin4 zit6 連捷) to attain degrees at successive examinations; _fai tsit_ (faai3 zit6 快捷) quick. This is also read _tsip_ (zip6). Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword (with right component like 偼) to 捷 for both readings, and fixed missing strokes in 勝 for the second reading. Williams' "also read ...": Corrected _tsip_ (zip3) to _tsip_ (zip6). That obtained through warfare (Kangxi: 獵也軍獲得也).

zit3.text: U+6ADB May appear as either ⿰木⿱𥫗即 (+13 assumed here) or ⿰木⿱𥫗卽 (+15), depending on the font. (F1.26 櫛) 櫛風沐雨 (Page 579 櫛) A comb of any kind (Kangxi: 梳枇之總名也); to comb, to clean (Kangxi: 理髮也); _kan tsit_ (gan1 zit3 巾櫛) towels and combs. Strictly, the pre-merger reading should be _chit_ per 《廣韻》阻瑟切. To remove, to weed out (Kangxi: 剔除也). 【櫛】 (tsrit) (_noun-like_) comb
U+6D59 (F1.25 浙) 浙江 (Page 23* ~~淅~~``浙``) A river (Kangxi: 江名) in Chehkiang (浙江), called _Chit shui_ (zit3 seoi2 浙水), from which the province is named.
U+7664 May appear as either ⿸疒⿱𥫗即 (+13 assumed here) or ⿸疒⿱𥫗卽 (+15), depending on the font. (F1.26 癤) 小瘡 (Page 580 癤) A small boil, a pimple, a sore (Kangxi: 癰也, 瘍類與癰疽別瘍之小者爲癤).

U+7BC0 May appear as either ⿱𥫗即 (+7 assumed here) or ⿱𥫗卽 (+9), depending on the font. (F1.26 節) 制也儉也符節時節 (F1.26 节) 同上 (Page 579 節) The joint of bamboos (Kangxi: 竹節也); a joint, a knot, a node; an article, a verse, a section of a chapter; a limit, a term; a holiday, a festival; a time, a regular interval (Kangxi: 時節); an emergency, the period or manner of doing a thing; economy, temperance, moderation (Kangxi: 止也檢也制也 etc.); chastity, virtue, in widows; to limit a thing, to maintain, to restrain one's passions; ~~to mark~~ ``rhythm or beat``, as ``in`` music; a classifier of joints, limbs, affairs; _tsit yam shik_ (zit3 jam2 sik6 節飲食) temperate in eating; _fan leung tsit_ (fan1 loeng5 zit3 分兩節) do it two ways, pay it at two times; _mo tsit chai_ (mou4 zit3 zai3 無節制) no limits to his outlay, careless; _fu tsit_ (fu4 zit3 符節) tally, passport, credentials (Kangxi: 符節所以示信); _tsit i_ (zit3 ji6 節義) chaste, in not remarrying; _shat tsit_ (sat1 zit3 失節) lost her virtue; _tsit ling_ (zit3 ling6 節令) the 24 solar terms; _tso tsit_ (zou6 zit3 做節) to keep holiday; _pai tsit_ (baai3 zit3 拜節) holiday visits; _hi tsit_ (hei3 zit3 ？節) anger, feeling; _yat tsit sz'_ (jat1 zit3 si6 一節事) an affair; _mai kwan tsit_ (maai5 gwaan1 zit3 買關節) to get degrees by bribery, to bribe; _tsit tsau_ (zit3 zau3 節奏) ~~to mark the time in music~~ ``musical beat``; _to shang chi tsit_ (do1 sang1 zi1 zit3 多生枝節) full of cares and business; _na shi tsit_ (naa5 si4 zit3 那時節) on that occasion; _t'in chung tsit_ (tin1 zung1 zit3 天中節) dragon-boat festival; _shau tsit cheung_ (sau1 zit3 zoeng3 收節賬) to collect bills at four terms; _tsit tsit ko_ (zit3 zit3 gou1 節節高), gradually rising in rank or wealth; _tsit kim_ (zit3 gim6 節儉) frugal, within the limits. Williams' 買關節: Dated. Williams' 節儉: Corrected _kim_ (gim3) to _kim_ (gim6). 【節】 (tset) (_noun-like_) joint, node, juncture, (_met._) division (_noun-like_) moral restraint, (_met._) regulation, chastity (_verb-like_) to restrain, (_met._) to regulate (_noun-like_) token

U+8707 (F1.25 蜇) 海蜇水母足 (Page 23* 蜇) A sting (Kangxi: 蟲螫也); any part of insects which inflicts a poisonous wound; _chit p'i_ (zit3 pei4 蜇皮), the dried skin of a blubber fish, called _shui mo_ (seoi2 mou5 水母), when alive. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 毋 to 母. Williams' 水母: "Blubber" in the sense of jellyfish (海蜇).

zit6.text: U+622A (F1.27 截) 斷也止也 (Page 580 截) To cut in pieces, to cut up; to cut off (Kangxi: 斷也), to amputate; to intercept, to cut off; to obstruct; to discriminate, to make nice distinctions; a portion, a half; _lan tsit_ (laan4 zit6 攔截) or _tsit chu k'u_ (zit6 zyu6 keoi5 截住佢), to stop, to arrest one's progress; _fan leung tsit_ (fan1 loeng5 zit6 分兩截) cut it in two; _tsit i_ (zit6 ji1 截衣), a robe whose body and skirt are of two colors; _tsit ~~tun~~ ``t'un``_ (zit6 tyun5 截斷) to cut apart. Williams' "to intercept": E.g. 截的士, "stop a taxi". Williams' "a portion": More generally, a classifier for segments. Williams' 攔截: Corrected _tsit_ (zit3) to _tsit_ (zit6). Williams' 截斷: Corrected _tun_ (dyun6 去聲) to _t'un_ (tyun5 上聲).

ziu1.text: U+564D (F2.3 噍) 無噍類言絶也 (Page 580 噍) ~~A husky, suppressed voice;~~ the chattering of birds (Kangxi: 啁噍聲也, 鳥聲); harsh, dissonant cry (Kangxi: 噍𠴫也). Urgent (Kangxi: 噍子遙反謂急也). 【噍】 (tsjew) Used in 啁噍.

ziu6.text: U+564D 嚼 zeu6 (vernacular) (Page 24* 齸 _Chiu_; here corrected to 噍 _Tsiu_) ~~A colloquial word.~~ To chew the cud, to ruminate; to bite hard on, to craunch; to chew, as tobacco. (Page 581 噍) To bite, to chew, to gnaw (Kangxi: 齧也, 嚼也); _ngau tsiu mau tan_ (ngau4 ziu6 maau5 daan1 牛噍牡丹), the ox eats the peony, (not knowing it from grass); _tsiu lan_ (ziu6 laan6 噍爛) to chew fine. Williams' 牛噍牡丹: Changed _mau_ (mau5) to common reading _mau_ (maau5). 【噍】 (zeu6, ziu6) To chew. 噍爛啲 (zeu6 laan6 di1): "Chew it up finer." 噍香口膠 (ziu6 hoeng1 hau2 gaau1): "Chew chewing gum." 牛噍牡丹 (ngau4 ziu6 maau5 daan1): "As an ox chewing the peony." Olden attestation for 噍 _tsiu_ (ziu6) meaning "chew": 《廣韻》才\笑\切 噍 嚼也

ziu1.text: U+62DB (F2.2 招) 手呼也以言曰召以手曰招 (Page 24* 招) To beckon, to motion to with the hand, to hail (Kangxi: 手呼也 etc.); ~~to let people know;~~ to invite, to induce (Kangxi: 來之也); to raise, as troops; to confess; self-crimination; to excite, to entangle; a handbill, a sign-board; a wave of the hand, a signal; _chiu fu_ (ziu1 fu1 招呼), to call and beckon; _chiu 'm chiu_ (ziu1 m4 ziu1 招唔招), do you confess? _chiu t'ip_ (ziu1 tip3 招貼) a handbill, a placard; _chiu p'ai_ (ziu1 paai4 招牌), a sign-board; _chiu long yap she_ (ziu1 long4 jap6 se3 招郎入舍) to bring a son-in-law to one's house; _chiu sho_ (ziu1 sou3 招數) to own to the account, to become responsible for; _chiu ye hoi sz'_ (ziu1 je5 hoi6 si6 招惹害事) to involve one's business; _chiu ping_ (ziu1 bing1 招兵), to enlist soldiers; _chiu ying_ (ziu1 jing6 招認) or _chiu tsui_ (ziu1 zeoi6 招罪) to confess guilt; _chiu ka_ (ziu1 gaa3 招架) to ward off; _chiu ts'oi t'ung tsz'_ (ziu1 coi4 tung4 zi2 招財童子) the god of wealth, worshiped at shop doors. To seek (Kangxi: 求也). To recruit. To tie, to restrain (Kangxi: 𦊰也, 招羈其足也). An archery target. (_martial arts_) A move, a strike, a technique (or instance thereof). 【招績】 (ziu1 zik1) Conceited, arrogant.

U+6912 (F2.3 椒) 胡椒皇后稱椒房以椒塗壁取其温柔又姓 (Page 580 椒) Pepper (Kangxi: 莍也 etc.); peppery, hot; _u tsiu_ (wu4 ziu1 胡椒), black pepper; _fa tsiu_ (faa1 ziu1 花椒), cayenne or red pepper; _tsiu fong_ (ziu1 fong4 椒房), the "pepper room," is a term for a queen (Kangxi: 皇后以椒塗壁稱椒房取其溫也); _tsiu mut_ (ziu1 mut6 椒末) ground pepper; _ch'un tsiu_ (cyun1 ziu1 川椒), Sz'chuen (四川) pepper. A mountaintop (Kangxi: 山頂亦曰椒).

U+7126 zeu1 (vernacular for "crust" and "scab" senses) (F2.3 焦) 火燒黑也又勞心曰焦 (Page 580 焦) Scorched, burned (Kangxi: 火所傷也); blackened, charred (Kangxi: 火燒黑也); smell of burning (Kangxi: 火之臭味也); vexed, harassed, anxious (Kangxi: 凡苦焦者皆屬焉); _tsiu pa kon_ (ziu1 baa1 gon1 焦疤乾), dry, at the last extremity, starving; _fan tsiu_ (faan6 ziu1 飯焦), crust left after boiling rice; _tsiu t'au lan ngak_ (ziu1 tau4 laan6 ngaak6 焦頭爛額) head burned and scorched, as by powder; _sam tsiu_ (sam1 ziu1 心焦), in great distress. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【焦】 (zeu1, ziu1) The crust of scorched rice. 飯焦 (faan6 ziu1): "Rice crust." A scab on the skin. 起咗焦 (hei2 zo2 ziu1): "Formed a scab." 塊焦好痕 (faai3 zeu1 hou2 han4): "The scab's really itchy."

U+71CB (Page 581 燋 _Tsiu_ (ziu3); here corrected to _Tsiu_ (ziu1)) to char ~~wood~~, to sear, to scorch (Kangxi: 傷火); _tsiu muk_ (ziu1 muk6 燋木) to char wood to bend it. That used to ignite torches (Kangxi: 所以然持火也).

ziu3.text: U+71CB (Page 581 燋) ~~Properly used for the last (醮), in the sense of burning the moxa;~~ _kau sam tsiu_ (gau3 saam1 ziu3 灸三燋) to apply the moxa thrice; A torch used in plastromancy (Kangxi: 灼龜炬也).

ziu1.text: U+7901 (Page 581 礁) An unauthorized character; half tide rocks, rocky islets, rocks in the stream; stones for fording; _~~ch'uk~~ ``chuk`` tsiu_ (zuk1 ziu1 觸礁), to run on a rock. Williams' 觸礁: Changed _ch'uk_ (cuk1) (incorrectly printed _ch'uk_) to common reading _chuk_ (zuk1).

U+81B2 焦 (F2.3 膲) 二膲五臟之上系管爲三膲又從頭至心心至臍臍至足爲上下三膲 (Page 580 焦) Used for the next (膲) (Kangxi 焦: 三焦者...別作膲). (Page 580 膲) The parts of the body between the heart and groin, three imaginary organs which regulate the secretions (Kangxi: 人之三膲, 三膲無形之府通作焦 etc.). Fan Wan entry: Normalised malformed 臟 to 臟. Williams' "three imaginary organs": The "triple burner" (三膲) of Chinese medicine.

U+8549 (F2.3 蕉) 芭蕉花名 (Page 580 蕉) The banana or plantain (Kangxi: 芭蕉); _heung nga tsiu_ (hoeng1 ngaa4 ziu1 香芽蕉), green plantains; _tsiu tsau_ (ziu1 zau2 蕉酒), spirits with plantains steeped in it; _ku ch'ui tsiu_ (gu2 ceoi4 ziu1 鼓錘蕉), large plantains; _mi yan tsiu_ (mei5 jan4 ziu1 美人蕉), thin skinned plantains. Raw hemp, which has not yet been treated by soaking.

U+940E ciu4 (alternative per 《廣韻》昨焦切) (F2.3 鐎) 鐎斗卽刀斗其器可受二升晝炊夜擊 (Page 581 鐎) A ~~soldier's~~ brass kettle or skillet, about the size of a peck (斗) measure (Kangxi: 鐎斗也, 溫器三足而有柄, etc.); a brass pan. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 書 to 晝.

U+9DE6 (F2.3 鷦) 鷦鷯鳥名 (Page 581 鷦) A wren (Kangxi: 鷦𪃦桃蟲也一名鷦鷯); _tsiu liu_ (ziu1 liu4 鷦鷯), a wren, tarin, tody, or some such small bird, which nestles on high grass; _hok tak kom tsiu liu_ (hok6 dak1 gam3 ziu1 liu4 學得咁鷦鷯), playful, skittish, inattentive.

ziu2.text: U+6CBC (F2.5 沼) 池沼圓曰池方曰沼 (Page 24* 沼) A pool or tank (Kangxi: 池也一說圓曰池曲曰沼), used for a fishpond; _lin chiu_ (lin4 ziu2 蓮沼), a water-lily pond; _ch'i chiu_ (ci4 ziu2 池沼), ponds.

ziu3.text: U+7167 炤 (F2.6 照) 明也燭也 (F2.6 炤) 同上 (Page 24* 照炤) Light; to illuminate, to brighten, to shine on (Kangxi: 明所燭也); to patronize, to regard, to care for, to oversee, to assist; conformably to, as, according to, like, same as; _chiu keng_ (ziu3 geng3 照鏡) to look in a glass; _chiu shan keng_ (ziu3 san1 geng3 照身鏡) a pier-glass; _chiu pik_ (ziu3 bik1 照壁) a wall opposite offices or temples; _ko chiu_ (gou1 ziu3 高照) a high lantern used to lead processions; _fan chiu_ (faan2 ziu3 反照) reflected light; reillumined; to shine again; _chiu ku_ (ziu3 gu3 照顧) to assist, to patronize, to buy of; _chiu yeung tso_ (ziu3 joeng6-2 zou6 照樣做) make it like the pattern; _chiu sz' hang sz'_ (ziu3 si6 hang4 si6 照事行事) do what you've begun; _ta chiu min_ (daa2 ziu3 min6 打照面) catch a glimpse of; _chiu i k'u_ (ziu3 ji1 keoi5 照依佢), do as he does; _chiu chu k'u_ (ziu3 zyu6 keoi5 照住佢), light him with a lantern; _chiu kin ni ke sam kon_ (ziu3 gin3 nei5 ge3 sam1 gon1 照見你嘅心肝), I can see through your design. To reflect. To inform. (_adverb_) Still (used to describe an action being done despite reasons against it). 【照計】 (ziu3 gai3) Used to emphasise an expectation associated with normal or unexceptional circumstances.

U+8A54 (F2.6 詔) 告也上命也又敎導之也 (Page 24* 詔) To promulge, to announce, to proclaim (Kangxi: 吿也); to teach, to exhort, to instruct ~~the empire~~ (Kangxi: 導也 etc.); a proclamation; ~~to encourage;~~ _yan chiu_ (jan1 ziu3 恩詔) a gracious command, as a pardon; _chiu ming_ (ziu3 ming6 詔命) a royal mandate; _chiu ko_ (ziu3 gou3 詔告) to proclaim; _chiu tui_ (ziu3 deoi3 詔對) to be called into the presence. Williams' "to encourage": This is a misreading of Kangxi 謂贊勉. Here, 贊勉 is to guide, not to encourage. An order from above, especially from the emperor (上命也秦漢以下天子獨稱之). Written proclamation (Kangxi: 播吿之書也).

U+91AE (F2.7 醮) 祭名酌酒而無酧酢曰醮又醮席又建醮 (Page 581 醮) To sacrifice to ancestors and spirits (Kangxi: 醮祭也); a sacrifice, a requiem (Kangxi: 凡僧道設壇祈禱曰醮); to give a cup of wine to a son at his wedding (Kangxi: 父親醮子 etc.); finished, used up (Kangxi: 盡也); to ~~complete~~ ``exhaust``; ~~to burn moxa;~~ _ta tsiu_ (daa2 ziu3 打醮) or _kin tsiu_ (gin3 ziu3 建醮) to celebrate the all-souls' festival; _wan t'in tsiu_ (waan4 tin1 ziu3 還天醮) a thanksgiving service for heaven's mercy; _tsoi tsiu_ (zoi3 ziu3 再醮) to remarry, said mostly of widows; _ta fo sing tsiu_ (daa2 fo2 sing1 ziu3 打火星醮) the autumnal festival to the God of Fire. Williams' "wine to a son at his wedding": Also at the capping ceremony where a twenty-year-old son is given his style-name. See below. The giving of a cup of wine from father to son at a capping or wedding cermony (Kangxi: 冠娶禮祭), where the son does not return a cup to the father (Kangxi: 酌而無酬酢曰醮). To marry (of a woman), especially to remarry.

ziu6.text: U+5146 (F2.7 兆) 億兆十億曰兆又宅兆墓域也又吉兆兆者以求吉爲主故以召言吉又京兆猶言京師也 (Page 24* 兆)

U+53EC (F2.7 召) 呼也 (Page 24* 召) To call, to summon (Kangxi: 𧦝也, 以手曰招以言曰召), to invoke, to cite; to order one to be present; _chiu ting_ (ziu6 ding2 召頂), to issue proposals to take a stock in trade; _chiu kin_ (ziu6 gin3 召見) to call to court; _chiu pat to_ (ziu6 bat1 dou3 召不到) not to appear when summoned.
U+56BC May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font.

zoek3.text: U+56BC May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font. zoek6 (per 《廣韻》在爵切) (F1.94 嚼 _Tseuk_ (zoek6)) 咀嚼咬嚼 (Page 565 嚼 _Tseuk_ (zoek6); here normalised to _Tseuk_ (zoek3)) To chew, to bite, ~~to ruminate,~~ to craunch, to mumble, to munch, to masticate (Kangxi: 齧也); a mouthful, a bite; _ngau tseuk_ (ngaau5 zoek3 咬嚼) to chew; _tseuk lan_ (zoek3 laan6 嚼爛) chewed it to pieces; _hom ch'a tseuk fan_ (ham4 caa4 zoek3 faan6 含茶嚼飯) to mumble for a child; _ngau man tseuk tsz'_ (ngaau5 man4 zoek3 zi6 咬文嚼字) to bite and chew phrases---pedantry. Williams' "to ruminate": Not incorrect per 《說文》噍或从爵, but 噍 is now read _tsiu_ (ziu6) per 《廣韻》才笑切.

zoek6.text: U+56BC May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font. (F1.94 嚼) 咀嚼咬嚼 (Page 565 嚼)

ziu6.text: U+65D0 (F2.7 旐) 旂旐 (Page 24* 旐)

U+8D99 (F2.7 趙) 趨也久也利也又姓 (Page 24* 趙) To hasten to (Kangxi: 趨趙也); to announce to a suzerain (Kangxi: 趙朝也本小邑朝事于大國也); a long time; ~~sharp, acute;~~ a few; a feudal state (Kangxi: 國名) in the south of Shansi (山西) and Chihli (直隸), its capital was the present Chauching (趙城) in Shansi (山西). Williams' "a long time", "a few": Per 久也 and 少也 from 《廣韻》. I am not aware of any texts that use these senses. Williams' "sharp": This is perhaps a rendering of Fan Wan 利也, which is likely erroneous. Williams' "acute": This is a misreading of 刺也, which belongs under the reading _t'iu_ (tiu5) per 《集韻》徒了切. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

zo2.text: U+4FCE 爼 (F2.60 爼) 爼豆祭器 (Page 25* 爼; here normalised to 俎) A basin or bowl like a tripod, used in sacrifice (Kangxi: 祭享之器); _cho tau_ (zo2 dau6 俎豆) a basin used in worship. A small table (Kangxi: 俎几也). A chopping board (Kangxi: 刀俎, 俎椹版也).

U+5497 唨 阻 (Page 25* 阻; here normalised to 咗) A colloquial word. A sign of ~~the past tense~~ ``completion``, placed after verbs``,`` like _hiu_ (hiu1 嘵); _~~ning~~ ``ling`` cho lai_ (ling1 zo2 lai4 拎咗嚟), brought it; _hu cho_ (heoi3 zo2 去咗), gone. Williams' 拎咗嚟: Changed _ning_ (ning1) to _ling_ (ling1). 【咗】 (zo2) Marker of the perfective aspect (whole completion of an action). 走咗 (zau2 zo2): "He's gone." 死咗 (sei2 zo2): "He's died." 笨咗添 (ban6 zo2 tim1): "Oh, I've been foolish!" 瞓着咗 (fan3 zoek6 zo2): "Fallen asleep." 畢咗業 (bat1 zo2 jip6): "I've graduated." 食咗飯未 (sik6 zo2 faan6 mei6): "Have you eaten dinner yet?" Used with certain imperatives. 飲咗佢吖 (jam2 zo2 keoi5 a1): "Drink it up." 剷咗條片啦 (caan2 zo2 tiu4 pin3-2 laa1): "Delete that video!" 噉炒咗佢囉 (gam2 caau2 zo2 keoi5 lo1): "So just fire him!"
U+5CA8 (F2.60 岨) 險岨 (Page 26* 岨)

U+5DE6 (F2.61 左) 左右又姓 (Page 582 左) The left (Kangxi: 左右之對), the left hand; second; an assistant; deflected (Kangxi: 僻左不正也); depraved, bad (Kangxi: 手足便右以左爲僻故凡幽猥皆曰僻左); to degrade, to reduce in rank (Kangxi: 漢時依古法朝廷之列以右爲尊故謂降秩爲左遷); _tso im_ (zo2 jim6 左驗) to verify, to prove, as a coadjutor can (Kangxi: 左證左也); _tso pin_ (zo2 bin6 左便) on the left; _shai kwan tso shau_ (sai2 gwaan3 zo2 sau2 使慣左手), left-handed; _tso sing_ (zo2 sing3 左性) crotchetty, set in his way; _chun wan tso pin_ (zyun3 waan1 zo2 bin6 轉彎左便) turn to the left hand; _seung tso_ (soeng1 zo2 相左), a mutual mistake; not to suit, disagreeing; _min tso tso_ (min6 zo2 zo2 面左左), to dislike; _tso sz' yau seung_ (zo2 si1 jau6 soeng2 左思右想), thinking of this way and that; _tso shau kau yau shau_ (zo2 sau2 gaau1 jau6 sau2 左手交右手), to exchange from hand to hand, _i. e._ not to give credit; _lit u tso_ (lit6 jyu1 zo2 列於左), written as follows. Williams' 轉彎左便: Corrected _chun_ (zyun2) to _chun_ (zyun3). Now simply 轉左. Williams' 列於左: Perhaps this should be 列如左. The idea here is that Chinese is (classically) written right to left, so text that comes afterward will be on the left. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).
U+723C (F2.60 爼) 爼豆祭器 (Page 25* 爼)

U+963B 岨 (for "mountain-pass" sense) (F2.60 阻) 阻滯阻隔險阻 (F2.60 岨) 險岨 (Page 25* 阻) To hinder, to impede (Kangxi: 隔也), to stop, to make delay; grieved (Kangxi: 憂也); ~~dangerous;~~ an impediment; a defile; to suspect, to doubt (Kangxi: 疑也); _him cho_ (him2 zo2 險阻), an obstruction in the way; _cho chai_ (zo2 zai6 阻滯) prevented from being ready; _cho kak_ (zo2 gaak3 阻隔) to prevent going; _cho nan_ (zo2 naan4 阻難), difficulties; _cho chu_ (zo2 zyu6 阻住) to hinder, blocked up; _cho tong_ (zo2 dong2 阻擋), to stand in the way of; _cho ch'i_ (zo2 ci4 阻遲), to delay; _cho 'ng sz' kon_ (zo2 ng6 si6 gon3 阻誤事幹) to retard or interrupt business. (Page 26* 岨) Used instead of the last (阻). Cliffs; a defile; _him cho_ (him2 zo2 險岨), a dangerous pass. A difficulty (Kangxi: 阻艱難也). To rely upon (Kangxi: 阻恃也). To reject.

zo3.text: U+4F50 左 zo2 (common in 佐敦) (F2.61 佐) 輔佐 (Page 582 佐) To assist, to second; an assistant, a deputy, a second (Kangxi: 輔也貳也), a substitute; _tso ling_ (zo3 ling5 佐領), a major-general, a resident among the Mongols; _wong tso chi ts'oi_ (wong4 zo3 zi1 coi4 王佐之才) talents of the prime minister; _tso tsap kun_ (zo3 zaap6 gun1 佐雜官), low officers assisting in a yamun (衙門). To encourage (to drink or to eat).

zo6.text: U+52A9 (F2.62 助) 輔助益也 (Page 26* 助) To assist, to help (Kangxi: 左也); strengthening, beneficial (Kangxi: 益也); to succor; _seung cho_ (soeng1 zo6 相助) to help each other; _cho shing ko kin sz'_ (zo6 sing4 go2 gin6 si6 助成嗰件事) help finish that business; _cho in_ (zo6 jin4 助言), a helping word; _cho ni yat pi chi lik_ (zo6 nei5 jat1 bei3 zi1 lik6 助你一臂之力) lend you a helping hand; _ts'ui yan lok cho_ (ceoi4 jan4 lok6 zo6 隨人樂助) each one helping willingly. Williams' 助成嗰件事: Changed _ko_ (go3) to modern _ko_ (go2).

U+5EA7 坐 zo6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun for "base" sense) (F2.62 座) 位也 (Page 582 坐; here normalised to 座) a seat, a place; (Page 583 座) A seat (Kangxi: 牀座也, 坐具), a throne; a shrine; a classifier of houses, towers, hills, cities, pagodas, movable pavilions, &c.; _hoi kwong shing tso_ (hoi1 gwong1 sing1 zo6 開光陞座) to vivify and enshrine an idol; _yat tso t'ap_ (jat1 zo6 taap3 一座塔) one division of a hong (行); _hi tak sam tso uk_ (hei2 dak1 saam1 zo6 uk1 起得三座屋) can build three houses there; _kung tso i_ (gung1 zo6 ji2 公座椅), an easy chair; _pat tso_ (baat3 zo6 八座) eight chair-bearers. Williams' 起得三座屋: Corrected _hi_ (hei3) to _hi_ (hei2). A stand or base. Short for 星座 (constellation).

zoek3.text: U+52FA coek3 (common) soek3 (common) (F1.94 勺) 挹取也 (Page 32|n75 勺) A ladle or spoon (Kangxi: 所以挹取也); a spoonful; _~~tau~~ ``lau`` cheuk_ (lau6 zoek3 漏勺) a cullender; _yat cheuk shui chi to_ (jat1 zoek3 seoi2 zi1 do1 一勺水之多), much as a spoonful of water (Kangxi: 一勺水之多言少也). Williams' 漏勺: Corrected _tau_ (dau6) to _lau_ (lau6).
U+5981 (F1.94 妁) 媒妁 (Page 32|n75 妁) A go-between (Kangxi: 媒也); to consult about a marriage; _mui cheuk_ (mui4 zoek3 媒妁) a match-maker.
U+65AB (F1.94 斫) 斬截也 (Page 32|n75 斫) To cut with a sword or knife (Kangxi: 刀斫); to amputate; to chop, to hew (Kangxi: 擊也); _cheuk hoi_ (zoek3 hoi1 斫開), to cut open.

U+721D May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font. ziu3 (alternative per 《廣韻》子肖切) zoek6 (alternative per 《廣韻》在爵切) (F1.94 爝 _Tseuk_ (zoek6)) 炬火也 (Page 565 爝 _Tseuk_ (zoek6); here normalised to _Tseuk_ (zoek3)) A flambeau, a torch, a link; a lighted match burned at night on an alarm. Per 《說文》苣火祓也, a torch specifically for warding off evil. Both the dark-entering reading 即略切 _tseuk_ (zoek3) and the light-entering reading 在爵切 _tseuk_ (zoek6) are attested in 《廣韻》, explained 炬火 "torch fire", along with departing 子肖切 _tsiu_ (ziu3), which is simply explained 火 "fire".

zoek6.text: U+721D May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font. (F1.94 爝) 炬火也 (Page 565 爝)

zoek3.text: U+7235 May appear with bottom-left component as the bottom of 飠 (+13 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+14), depending on the font. (F1.94 爵) 鳥名又酒爵又爵祿 (Page 565 爵) A cyathus with three legs, a cup for libations (Kangxi: 禮器也 etc.), a metallic wine cup; a bamboo wine pottle (Kangxi: 竹器所以酌酒也); a degree of nobility, rank, station (Kangxi: 爵位也, 封也 etc.); nobility; _tseuk pui_ (zoek3 bui1 爵杯), a wine cup; _'ng tseuk_ (ng5 zoek3 五爵) the five orders of nobility; _tseuk wai_ (zoek3 wai6 爵位) of noble rank; _t'in tseuk_ (tin1 zoek3 天爵) heaven's nobility --- is a love of virtue; _tseuk luk_ (zoek3 luk6 爵祿) income of a nobleman.

U+76AD May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font. ziu3 (per 《廣韻》子肖切) zoek6 (per 《廣韻》在爵切) (F1.94 皭 _Tseuk_ (zoek6)) 白色又？淨也 (Page 565 皭 _Tseuk_ (zoek6); here normalised to _Tseuk_ (zoek3)) A white color (Kangxi: 白色), pure, unsullied, clean (Kangxi: 淨貌); nice, clean looking. The reading _tseuk_ (zoek3) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the reading 即約切, explained 白色. Fan Wan entry: The unidentified character is ⿰正朿, possibly a badly typed 疎 (same as 疏).

zoek6.text: U+76AD May appear with bottom-left of right component as the bottom of 飠 (+17 assumed here) or 𩙿 (+18), depending on the font. (F1.94 皭) 白色又？淨也 (Page 565 皭) Fan Wan entry: The unidentified character is ⿰正朿, possibly a badly typed 疎 (same as 疏).

zoek3.text: U+7740 著 (F1.94 着) 被服也又置也 (Page 32|n75 着著) to cover over, to put on, as clothes (Kangxi 著: 被服也); _ho cheuk_ (hou2 zoek3 好着) sets well, as a garment; _cheuk i fuk_ (zoek3 ji1 fuk6 着衣服) to dress; This character is often distinguished into _cheuk_ (zoek3) & _cheuk_ (zoek6). Williams (following Fan Wan) does not provide separate entries for 陰入 _cheuk_ (zoek3) ("wear" sense) and 陽入 _cheuk_ (zoek6) (other senses). I have split things up accordingly. 【着-wear】 (zoek3) To wear, to put on. 着褲 (zoek3 fu3): "Wearing pants." 唔着衫 (m4 zoek3 saam1): "Not wearing clothes." Olden attestation for 著 (alternative form 着) _cheuk_ (zoek3) meaning "wear": 《廣韻》張略切 著 服衣於身又直略張豫二切 《康熙字典》著 ...又《類篇》陟略切音芍被服也...

zoek6.text: U+7740 著 (F1.94 着 _Cheuk_ (zoek3)) 被服也又置也 (Page 32|n75 着著 _Cheuk_ (zoek3); here corrected to _Cheuk_ (zoek6)) Right, correct; according to; to cause, to order, to send; a particle placed after verbs, showing a transitive and present action, and is like _chu_ (zyu6 住) in colloquial; before verbs, let, make, permit; _'m cheuk_ (m4 zoek6 唔着) unsuitable; _cheuk kwo t'au_ (zoek6 gwo3 tau4 着過頭), just right; _sheung cheuk_ (soeng6 zoek6 上着) best for use; _fan 'm cheuk_ (fan3 m4 zoek6 瞓唔着) I cannot sleep; _cheuk shat ta_ (zoek6 sat6 daa2 着實打), gave him a flogging; _u ka cheuk mong_ (jyu6 gaa1 zoek6 mong4 愈加着忙), he was still more vexed; _cheuk sho_ (zoek6 sou3 着數) cheap, good for the price; _cheuk yung_ (zoek6 jung6 着用) useful; _cheuk shing 'm cheuk tau_ (zoek6 sing1 m4 zoek6 dau2 着升唔着斗), it will do for a gill if not for a peck, serve for this if not for that; _cheuk i_ (zoek6 ji3 着意) to suit one; _cheuk lik_ (zoek6 lik6 着力) to exert one's self; _yau cheuk lok_ (jau5 zoek6 lok6 有着落) all right, settled satisfactorily; _shiu cheuk_ (siu1 zoek6 燒着) it is on fire; _cheuk shat_ (zoek6 sat6 着實) entirely right; _ts'am cheuk_ (cam4 zoek6 尋着) or _wan cheuk_ (wan2 zoek6 搵着) found it; _cheuk 'm cheuk_ (zoek6 m4 zoek6 着唔着) is it right or not? _tim u tak cheuk_ (dim2 jyu6 dak1 zoek6 點遇得着) how shall I get to him? _mo cheuk lok_ (mou4 zoek6 lok6 無着落) I ~~dont~~ ``don't`` know what to do; _pui cheuk yan tso_ (bui3 zoek6 jan4 zou6 背着人做) hidden, sub rosa, underhand. This character is often distinguished into _cheuk_ (zoek3) & _cheuk_ (zoek6). Williams (following Fan Wan) does not provide separate entries for 陰入 _cheuk_ (zoek3) ("wear" sense) and 陽入 _cheuk_ (zoek6) (other senses). I have split things up accordingly. To put (Kangxi 著: 著置也). To attach (Kangxi 著: 附也). To contact, to touch (Kangxi 著: 著地無足). A plan, a stratagem. To catch fire, to ignite. 【着-on】 (zoek6) To turn on. 着燈 (zoek6 dang1): "Turn on the light." 部機開着咗 (bou6 gei1 hoi1 zoek6 zo2): "The machine is turned on."

zoek3.text: U+799A (F1.94 禚) 齊地 (Page 32|n75 禚) A city in the feudal state of ~~Tsi~~ ``Ts'i`` (齊) (Kangxi: 齊地名), now a place in Shantung (山東) province.
U+8457 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (Page 32|n75 着著)

zoek6.text: U+8457 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (Page 32|n75 着著 _Cheuk_ (zoek3); here corrected to _Cheuk_ (zoek6))

zyu3.text: U+8457 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. 箸 (F1.47 著) 明也又著述 (Page 31* 著) To manifest (Kangxi: 明也); to set forth; clear; to narrate, to write; ~~to fix, to settle;~~ ~~a porch;~~ _chu tsok_ (zyu3 zok3 著作) to compose; _chu shut_ (zyu3 seot6 著述) to write an account of. To mark (Kangxi: 著標著也).

zoek3.text: U+914C 勺 (F1.94 酌) 注也斟也益也挹取也沽也又叅酌審擇量度也 (Page 32|n75 勺; here normalised to 酌) to bale or pour out; to adopt, to follow; (Page 32|n75 酌) To pour out liquor, to fill a cup (Kangxi: 盛酒行觴也, 酌猶斟也); a cup, a glass; _met._ wine, liquor (Kangxi: 淸酌酒也); a dinner, a feast; to avail of, to choose the good and act upon it (Kangxi: 取善而行曰酌, 酌猶取也); to deliberate (Kangxi: 參酌); to imitate or adopt; _~~sham~~ ``ts'am`` cheuk_ (caam1 zoek3 參酌) or _cheuk i_ (zoek3 ji5 酌議), to deliberate; _pok cheuk_ (bok6 zoek3 薄酌) a slight repast; _cheuk leung t'o tong_ (zoek3 loeng4 to5 dong3 酌量妥當) it is all settled right; _keung cheuk_ (goeng1 zoek3 薑酌) a feast on a birth; _sheung cheuk_ (soeng6 zoek3 上酌) a sumptuous feast; _chung cheuk_ (zung1 zoek3 中酌) the servants' course; _~~tsui~~ ``tsiu`` cheuk_ (ziu3 zoek3 醮酌) a wedding entertainment to a son; _~~miu~~ ``mui`` cheuk_ (mui4 zoek3 梅酌) a return feast given by a bridegroom. Williams' 參酌: Corrected _sham_ (sam1) to _ts'am_ (caam1). Williams' 醮酌: Corrected _tsui_ (zeoi3) to _tsiu_ (ziu3). Williams' 梅酌: Corrected _miu_ (miu4) to _mui_ (mui4).

U+96C0 爵 zoek3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.94 雀) 孔雀又小鳥亦曰雀 (Page 565 雀) A sparrow (Kangxi: 依人小鳥也); a term for small birds (Fan Wan: 小鳥亦曰雀), like the finch or lark; ~~a kind of cap;~~ a kind of wheat (Kangxi: 蘥雀麥, 卽燕麥也); _tseuk lap_ (zoek3 lap6 雀立) to skip, to hop, as a sparrow (Kangxi: 雀立踊也); _hung tseuk_ (hung2 zoek3-2 孔雀) a peacock; _ma tseuk_ (ma4 zoek3-2 麻雀) a sparrow; _shan ma tseuk_ (saan1 ma4 zoek3-2 山麻雀) a lark; _tseuk kok_ (zoek3 gok3 雀角) litigation; _wo fa tseuk_ (wo4 faa1 zoek3-2 禾花雀) rice-birds, ortolans; _mui fa tseuk_ (mui4 faa1 zoek3-2 梅花雀) the avedevat _(Fringilla amanduva)_; _shi shan tseuk_ (si4 san4 zoek3-2 時辰雀) a canary-bird; _tseuk shit fa_ (zoek3 sit3 faa1 雀舌花), the _Gardenia radicans_. (Page 565 爵) occurs for the preceding (雀); Williams' "a kind of cap": This is a misreading of Kangxi 雀韋弁. The subcommentary makes it clear that 雀 is an adjective describing the *colour* of the cap (Kangxi: 雀言如雀頭色也). Williams' 雀舌花: Changed _shit_ (sit6) to common reading _shit_ (sit3). 【麻雀】 (maa4 zoek3, maa4 zoek3-2) Mahjong. 打麻雀 (daa2 maa4 zoek3-2): "Play mahjong." 麻雀館 (maa4 zoek3 gun2): "Mahjong parlour." A mahjong tile. 跌咗隻麻雀 (dit3 zo2 zek3 maa4 zoek3-2): "Dropped a mahjong-tile." 【雀局】 (zoek3 guk6) Mahjong games (bundled with dinner at a restaurant). Erroneously spelt 鵲局.

zoeng1.text: U+5ADC (F1.89 嫜) 姑嫜 (Page 33|n76 嫜) A husband's father (Kangxi: 夫之父母也); _ku cheung_ (gu1 zoeng1 姑嫜), a husband's parents; _hing cheung_ (hing1 zoeng1 兄嫜), a husband's ``older`` brother (Kangxi: 夫之兄曰兄嫜).

U+5F35 zoeng3 (per Kangxi 知亮切 for "boast" sense) (F1.89 張) 開也設山？也又宿名 (Page 33|n76 張) To draw a bow (Kangxi: 施弓弦也); to extend, to stretch, to open (Kangxi: 開也); to draw up, as a list; to increase; to boast (Kangxi: 自侈大也); to state, to proclaim, to publish; a classifier of things spread out, as a table, chair, bed, paper, &c.; _cheung kwa_ (zoeng1 gwaa3 張掛) to hang out, as festoons; _hoi cheung_ (hoi1 zoeng1 開張), to open quick or strongly; _san hoi cheung_ (san1 hoi1 zoeng1 新開張), to open a new shop; _cheung tai_ (zoeng1 daai6 張大) to make much of, boasting; _p'o cheung wa lai_ (pou1 zoeng1 waa4 lai6 鋪張華麗) to make a great show; _kwai cheung_ (gwaai1 zoeng1 乖張), crossgrained; _cheung yeung_ (zoeng1 joeng4 張揚) to make known; _cheung hoi hau_ (zoeng1 hoi1 hau2 張開口), open your mouth; _chu cheung_ (zyu2 zoeng1 主張), to direct, to lord over; _yat cheung mun lim_ (jat1 zoeng1 mun4 lim4-2 一張門簾), a door-curtain. Fan Wan entry: The unidentified character is ⿵𡗜弓, where 𡗜 is ⿻大丷. Williams' 主張: Dated; 主張 now means to assert or hold a position or opinion. Great (Kangxi: 大也). To spread (Kangxi: 施也). To set out, to arrange (Kangxi: 設也). To string (a string instrument) (Kangxi: 施絃曰張). To spread a net to catch animals (Kangxi: 羅取鳥獸曰張). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). A position, an opinion. To look. 【張】 (zoeng1) (_mahjong_) Classifier for (revealed) tiles. Also spelt 章. 九張 (gau2 zoeng1): "Nine tiles revealed" (three melds). 十二張 (sap6 ji6 zoeng1): "Twelve tiles revealed" (four melds).

U+5F70 (F1.89 彰) 著也明也 (Page 33|n76 彰) Luminous (Kangxi: 明也, 著也); beautiful; elegant; to manifest (Kangxi: 著明之也); variegated plumage or pelage (Kangxi: 毛髮貌謂鳥獸羽毛之文); _cheung ming_ (zoeng1 ming4 彰明), clearly exhibited. Adorned (Kangxi: 文彰也, 文章飾也).
U+615E (F1.89 慞) 惶恐 (Page 33|n76 慞) Alarm; apprehensive; _cheung wong_ (zoeng1 wong4 慞惶), terrified, fearful (Kangxi: 慞惶懼也); a horrified look.

U+6A1F (F1.89 樟) 木名 (Page 33|n76 樟) The camphor _(Laurus camphora)_ (Kangxi: 豫樟木也 etc.); _cheung muk_ (zoeng1 muk6 樟⽊) camphor wood; _cheung no_ (zoeng1 nou5 樟腦), gum camphor; _heung cheung_ (hoeng1 zoeng1 香樟), a camphor odor.
U+6F33 (F1.89 漳) 水名 (Page 34|n77 漳) Name of a river (Kangxi: 水名); the prefecture of Changchau (漳州) (Kangxi: 州名) in Fuhkien (福建) near Amoy (廈門).

U+6F3F 䊢 (F1.89 漿) 水漿酒漿 (F1.89 䊢) 米䊢 (Page 566 漿䊢) Thick, half fluid, pasty (Kangxi 漿: 酢漿也一曰水米汁相將也); starch, syrup, pus, or matter; to starch; broth boiled down thick, congee; _nai tseung_ (nai4 zoeng1 泥漿), mire; _tsau tseung_ (zau2 zoeng1 酒漿), a banquet; _tseung i fuk_ (zoeng1 ji1 fuk6 漿衣服) to starch clothes; _tseung u_ (zoeng1 wu4 漿糊), paste; _mo tseung_ (mo6 zoeng1 磨漿), to prepare starch. The second form (䊢) is unauthorized. Williams' 磨漿: Corrected _mo_ (mou6) to _mo_ (mo6).

U+7350 麞 (F1.89 獐) 鹿屬無角 (F1.89 麞) 獸名 (Page 34|n77 麞; here normalised to 獐) A kind of chevrotain or gazelle, hornless, and of elegant shape (Kangxi 麞: 與獐同, 麋屬, 麞如小鹿而美); _ngan cheung_ (ngan4 zoeng1 銀獐), the white gazelle---appears in good reigns (Kangxi 麞: 符瑞志有銀麞白色王者𠛬罰中理則出).
U+748B (F1.89 璋) 玉名 (Page 34|n77 璋) A kind of plaything made of jade (Kangxi: 剡上爲圭半圭爲璋); _lung cheung_ (lung6 zoeng1 弄璋), to bear a son.

U+7AE0 (F1.89 章) 文章采章章明表章 (Page 33|n76 章) A piece of music (Kangxi: 樂竟爲一章); a chapter, a section (Kangxi: 篇章); a composition; a statement; rules, laws, regulations (Kangxi: 條也程也); clear (Kangxi: 明也), beautiful (Kangxi: 文章也), manifest; a display; ~~a grove;~~ a metonic cycle (Kangxi: ...十九年爲一章...); _ts'oi cheung_ (coi2 zoeng1 彩章), variegated; _hin cheung_ (hin3 zoeng1 憲章), magistrate's orders; _cheung ku_ (zoeng1 geoi3 章句) sections and paragraphs; _sho cheung_ (sou3 zoeng1 數章), many papers or statements; _cheung ch'ing_ (zoeng1 cing4 章程), regulations, rule of conduct; _wong cheung_ (wong4 zoeng1 王章), laws of the land; _t'o cheung_ (tou4 zoeng1 圖章) or _yan cheung_ (jan3 zoeng1 印章), a seal (Kangxi: 印章). Colourful adornment (Kangxi: 采也). A completed matter or composition (Kangxi: 成事成文曰章). Great forest trees (Kangxi: 大林木曰章). Order. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).
U+42A2 (F1.89 漿) 水漿酒漿 (F1.89 䊢) 米䊢 (Page 566 漿䊢)

U+8523 (Page 566 蔣 _Tseung_ (zoeng2); here corrected to _Tseung_ (zoeng1)) _ku tseung_ (gu1 zoeng1 菰蔣), an aquatic grain (Kangxi: 苽蔣); wild rice; _tseung mau_ (zoeng1 maau4 蔣茅), stubble, straw. Williams' 菰蔣: Specifically, _Zizania latifolia_ (or Manchurian wild rice). Williams' 蔣茅: Corrected _mau_ (mau4) to _mau_ (maau4).

zoeng2.text: U+8523 (F1.92 蔣) 地名周公子封于蔣後遂爲姓 (Page 566 蔣) A surname (Kangxi: 姓);

zoeng1.text: U+87BF (F1.89 螿) 寒螿虫名 (Page 566 螿) Small species of cicada or grasshopper, whose elytra are green (Kangxi: 寒螿蟬屬 etc.).

U+9123 (F1.89 鄣) 地名 (Page 34|n77 鄣) A feudal state, now part of T``'``ai-ngan fu (泰安府) in Shantung (山東); an ancient city in Ku (莒), also in the modern Shantung (山東). The two senses in Kangxi are 《說文》紀邑也 and 莒邑. It is not clear to me whether these are states or cities (or one each).
U+9E9E (F1.89 獐) 鹿屬無角 (F1.89 麞) 獸名 (Page 34|n77 麞)

zoeng2.text: U+596C (F1.92 奬) 褒奬勸也勵也稱舉也 (Page 566 奬)

U+638C (F1.91 掌) 手掌又主也掌握掌管又鞅掌失容也 (Page 34|n77 掌) The palm of the hand (Kangxi: 手中也, 手心也謂指本也); the paw or sole of animals; a webbed foot; to grasp; to rule, to control (Kangxi: 職掌主也); a control, a jurisdiction; _shau cheung_ (sau2 zoeng2 手掌), the palm; _ta yat pa cheung_ (daa2 jat1 baa1 zoeng2 打一巴掌), a slap of the hand; _cheung kun kung yan_ (zoeng2 gun2 gung1 jan4 掌管工人), to oversee workmen; _hop cheung_ (hap6 zoeng2 合掌), to clasp the hands, as in prayer; _cheung ak ping k'un_ (zoeng2 ak1 bing1 kyun4 掌握兵權), to command the forces; _cheung kau_ (zoeng2 gaau3 掌教) to teach graduates; _hon shau cheung_ (hon3 sau2 zoeng2 看手掌), to examine the hand, palmistry; _p'ak cheung_ (paak3 zoeng2 拍掌), to clap the hands; _cheung kwai_ (zoeng2 gwai6-2 掌櫃) a bookkeeper, a salesman; _i u fan cheung_ (ji6 jyu4 faan2 zoeng2 易如反掌), as easy as to turn the hand over. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𬋩 to 管. Used in 鞅掌 (uncomposed, bustling) (Kangxi: 鞅掌失容也, 言促遽也, 言事煩不暇爲容儀也). To slap.
U+69F3 (F1.92 槳) 船槳 (Page 566 槳) An oar (Kangxi: 楫屬 etc.); _chau tseung_ (zaau6 zoeng2 櫂槳), to row; _tseung shun_ (zoeng2 syun4 槳船), a revenue cutter, because it has many oars.

U+734E 奬 (F1.92 奬) 褒奬勸也勵也稱舉也 (Page 566 奬; here normalised to 獎) To exhort, to animate, to excite, to encourage (Kangxi 奬: 勸勉也); to laud, to praise, to commend (Kangxi 奬: 褒美也); ~~to vindicate,~~ to give efficacy to (Kangxi 奬: 助也); ~~to set on, as dogs;~~ _tseung hun_ (zoeng2 hyun3 獎勸) to exhort; _tseung sheung_ (zoeng2 soeng2 獎賞) to hold out rewards to; a reward; _ho yung kwo tseung_ (ho4 jung6 gwo3 zoeng2 何用過獎), why so much overpraise? Williams' "to set on, as dogs": This is a misreading of Kangxi 嗾犬厲之也. This is the 《說文》 explanation of the hound radical, and should be rendered "to sound at an hound to encourage it". A reward, a prize.

zoeng6.text: U+4ED7 杖 (for "rely" sense) zoeng6-3 (for "battle" sense, except in 陣仗) (F1.93 仗) 兵器刀劍總名又儀衞曰仗又倚仗憑依也 (Page 35|n78 仗) ~~A sharp weapon~~ ``Weaponry`` (Kangxi: 劒戟總名); to fight; to rely on, to lean on, to look up to (Kangxi: 憑倚也); _ta shing cheung_ (daa2 sing3 zoeng6-3 打勝仗) to get the victory; _tsip cheung_ (zip3 zoeng6-3 接仗) in battle; _ta cheung_ (daa2 zoeng6-3 打仗) to fight, at war; _tak cheung_ (dak1 zoeng6 得仗) to have a friend; _yeung cheung_ (joeng5 zoeng6 仰仗) to look to for aid; _cheung wai_ (zoeng6 wai6 仗衛) imperial guard; _ping cheung_ (bing1 zoeng6 兵仗) arms; _i cheung_ (ji2 zoeng6 倚仗) to trust to. Battle. To hold, to wield. 【大陣仗】 (daai6 zan6 zoeng6) A big scene, or to make a big fuss. (Literally, "great battle-array".) 好大陣仗 (hou2 daai6 zan6 zoeng6): "A very big fuss."
