zoeng3.text: U+5E33 張 (F1.92 帳) 帷幕也 (Page 34|n77 帳) A curtain, a screen (Kangxi: 帳張也張施於牀上也, 帷也張也幬也); a tent; ~~a house;~~ ~~to calculate;~~ to spread; ~~a reason, a plan;~~ _po cheung_ (bou3 zoeng3 布帳) an awning; _cheung lim_ (zoeng3 lim4-2 帳簾), a hanging door-screen; _ch'it cheung_ (cit3 zoeng3 設帳) to pitch one's tent, to become a teacher; _man cheung_ (man1 zoeng3 蚊帳) a bed-curtain; _wan cheung_ (wan6 zoeng3 混帳) unreasonable, incoherent; _p'ing cheung_ (ping4 zoeng3 屏帳) a movable screen of silk; _shau cheung_ (sau6 zoeng3 壽帳) a scroll given to old people.

U+6F32 (F1.92 漲) 大水貌又水泛溢 (F1.92 涱) 同上 (Page 35|n78 漲) An inundation (Kangxi: 水大貌); to overflow (Kangxi: 溢也); a sheet of water; the southern sea (Kangxi: 南海名); _shui cheung_ (seoi2 zoeng3 水漲) water is rising, overflowing.

U+7634 (F1.93 瘴) 瘴癘熱病 (Page 35|n78 瘴) Malaria (Kangxi: 瘴癘也, 熱病); pestilential vapors; pestiferous air; _in cheung_ (jin1 zoeng3 煙瘴) unwholesome climate; _lam cheung_ (laam4 zoeng3 嵐瘴) noxious vapor; _hi cheung_ (hei3 zoeng3 氣瘴) deleterious malaria. Fan Wan entry: Normalised 𤍠 to 熱.

U+8139 張 漲 zoeng3-2 (rising second in vernacular 脹脹地) (F1.92 脹) 腫脹 (Page 35|n78 脹) A swelled belly (Kangxi: 腹滿也); a swelling; dropsical; _shui cheung_ (seoi2 zoeng3 水脹) dropsy of the belly, ascites; _cheung mun_ (zoeng3 mun5 脹滿), belly hard or puffy; _pau cheung_ (baau2 zoeng3 飽脹) a bellyful, a feeling of fullness; _cheung mun hung t'ong_ (zoeng3 mun5 hung4 tong4 脹滿紅堂), swelling with rage, incensed.

U+8CEC 帳 (F1.92 賬) 賬目 (Page 34|n77 帳) an account, for which the next (賬) is used (Kangxi 帳: 計簿也); (Page 34|n77 賬) An unauthorized character. A debt, a claim, an account, a charge; to reckon, to sum up; to calculate~~.~~``;`` <del> A colloquial word. </del> ~~A time, an occasion;~~ ~~_lai kwo yat cheung_ (lai4 gwo3 jat1 zoeng3 嚟過一賬) I have been here once;~~ _'m sun cheung_ (m4 syun3 zoeng3 唔算賬) do not put it to my account, it's none of my business; _ngan cheung_ (ngan6 zoeng3 韌賬) a tough account, a refused bill; _cheung muk_ (zoeng3 muk6 賬目) accounts; _shau cheung_ (sau1 zoeng3 收賬) to receive money on account; _cheung tan_ (zoeng3 daan1 賬單), a bill; _chui cheung_ (zeoi1 zoeng3 追賬) to dun; _cheung fong_ (zoeng3 fong4 賬房), a counting-room; _hau cheung_ (hau6 zoeng3 候賬) waiting for a settlement; _sun cheung_ (syun3 zoeng3 算賬) to reckon accounts; _cheung po_ (zoeng3 bou6-2 賬簿), an account book; _tui cheung_ (deoi3 zoeng3 對賬) to balance accounts by offsetting``;`` _ts'ing cheung_ (cing1 zoeng3 清賬) or _wan cheung_ (waan4 zoeng3 還賬) to pay off an account; _him cheung_ (him3 zoeng3 欠賬) to owe. <ins> A colloquial word. A time, an occasion; _lai kwo yat cheung_ (lai4 gwo3 jat1 zoeng3 嚟過一賬) I have been here once. </ins> "A colloquial word" applies only to the "time/occasion" sense. I have fixed this by moving that sense to the bottom.

U+91AC (F1.93 醬) 醢也肉醬謂之醢又豉醬 (Page 566 醬) A sort of sauce, pickle or condiment (Kangxi: 醢也, 醢肉醬也又豉醬又菜茹亦謂之醬), made of salt and sugar; relishes, seasoning, like oilmen's stores; salted preparations; _kon tseung_ (gon1 zoeng3 乾醬) dried seasoning; _tseung un_ (zoeng3 jyun4-2 醬園), a shop where condiments are sold; _tseung liu_ (zoeng3 liu6-2 醬料) materials for preserves; _lui tseung_ (leoi4 zoeng3 擂醬) to mix sauces; _'ng mi tseung_ (ng5 mei6 zoeng3 五味醬) a compounded sauce; _min shi tseung_ (min6 si6-2 zoeng3 麪豉醬) a sort of bean relish.

U+969C (F1.92 障) 隔也蔽也塞也 (Page 35|n78 障) To separate (Kangxi: 隔也); to divide (Kangxi: 界也); to shut up, to include; to raise an embankment; something which prevents progress; an intervening distance; a barricade (Kangxi: 障衞也), a trench, a fence; a protection~~, as a carpet~~; a dike, a terminus; _pan cheung_ (baan2 zoeng3 板障) a board partition; _cheung sak_ (zoeng3 sak1 障塞) a hindrance or stoppage; _po cheung_ (bou2 zoeng3 保障) a defense or embankment; to defend a barrier; _cheung pai_ (zoeng3 bai3 障蔽) to close against ingress. A screen (Kangxi: 步障也). Used in 故障 (malfunction, failure).

zoeng6.text: U+4E08 zoeng6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun in 姑丈, 姨丈) (F1.93 丈) 十尺曰丈又大丈夫 (Page 35|n78 丈) Ten _ch'ek_ (尺) (Kangxi: 十尺曰丈) or 141 English inches; to measure; an elder (Kangxi: 長老之稱); _cheung leung_ (zoeng6 loeng4 丈量), to measure land; _cheung fu_ (zoeng6 fu1 丈夫), my husband; _lo cheung_ (lou5 zoeng6 老丈) or _cheung yan kung_ (zoeng6 jan4 gung1 丈人公), a wife's father; _tai cheung fu_ (daai6 zoeng6 fu1 大丈夫), a talented man; _fong cheung_ (fong1 zoeng6 方丈) the abbot of a monastery. Williams' 141 English inches: The Ts'ing Dynasty _ch'ek_ (尺) was only standardised to 14⅝ inches by the British in Hong Kong in Ordinance No. 8 of 1885. See also commentary at 咫 zi2. Williams' "abbot of a monastery": Originally the *residence* of the abbot (Kangxi: 釋氏所居曰方丈).

U+50CF May appear as either ⿰亻象 (assumed here) or ⿰亻𧰼, depending on the font. (F1.93 像) 形像又倣摸也 (Page 566 像) like, similar (Kangxi: 形象也肖似也); a figure, image, likeness; a statue, an idol; to resemble, to imitate (Kangxi: 摹倣也); _ngau tseung_ (ngau5 zoeng6 偶像) a statue; _shan tseung_ (san4 zoeng6 神像) an idol; _tseung shang_ (zoeng6 sang1 像生) like life; _mo tseung_ (mou4 zoeng6 模像) to limn a copy; _yan tseung_ (jan4 zoeng6 人像) a portrait; _seung tseung_ (soeng2 zoeng6 想像) an idea, an illustration; _'m tseung yeung_ (m4 zoeng6 joeng6-2 唔像樣) not according to rule.

U+5320 (F1.93 匠) 工匠 (Page 567 匠) A workman, a mechanic, an artisan of any kind (Kangxi: 工匠); _ta ngan tseung_ (daa2 ngan4-2 zoeng6 打銀匠) a silversmith; _muk tseung_ (muk6 zoeng6 木匠) a joiner, a carpenter; _nai shui tseung_ (nai4 seoi2 zoeng6 泥水匠) a bricklayer; _kung tseung_ (gung1 zoeng6 工匠) a mechanic. Originally, a carpenter (Kangxi: 木工也).

U+6756 zoeng6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.93 杖) 梃也 (Page 35|n78 杖) A cane, a staff (Kangxi: 所以扶行也); a cudgel, a club (Kangxi: 木梃也); to lean on (Kangxi: 憑倚也); to hold (Kangxi: 持也); to beat; aged, an old man; to mourn; _kwai cheung_ (gwaai2 zoeng6-2 拐杖) a staff for old folks; _ch'i cheung_ (ci1 zoeng6 笞杖) to bamboo; _mun cheung_ (mun5 zoeng6 滿杖) to beat 8O strokes; _cheung u heung_ (zoeng6 jyu1 hoeng1 杖於鄉), a village sexagenarian; _~~sik~~ ``sek`` cheung_ (sek3 zoeng6 錫杖) a crosier; _cheung ki fu_ (zoeng6 gei1 fu1 杖期夫), mourners of one year; _hang cheung_ (hang4 zoeng6 行杖) bambooing, beating; _chap cheung_ (zap1 zoeng6 執杖) to carry a staff at a funeral. Williams' 錫杖: Changed _sik_ (sik1) to common reading _sek_ (sek3).

U+6A61 May appear as either ⿰木象 (assumed here) or ⿰木𧰼, depending on the font. Acorn, the fruit of the oak (Kangxi: 栩實也). Rubber (from the rubber tree), used in compounds. 【橡筋】 (zoeng6 gan1) A rubber band. 畀橡筋紮住 (bei2 zoeng6 gan1 zaat3 zyu6): "Bound by rubber band." Jellyfish (as food).

U+8C61 像 (for "resemble", "imitate", and "image" senses) 𧰼 (F1.93 象) 獸名又似也 (Page 566 象) An elephant (Kangxi: 長鼻牙南越大獸 etc.); a figure, a form, an image; the shape things take (Kangxi: 象形也); superstitious auguries or fancy; imagination, resemblance; a law (Kangxi: 法也); to imitate; ~~pantomime;~~ _tseung nga_ (zoeng6 ngaa4 象牙) ivory; _tseung pat_ (zoeng6 bat6 象拔) a proboscis; _tseung p'i_ (zoeng6 pei4 象皮), elephant's skin, a medicine; a name for india rubber; _tseung kom tai chek keuk_ (zoeng6 gam3 daai6 zek3 goek3 象咁大隻腳) my leg is as heavy as an elephant's --- I'm ashamed to go back; _tai pan tseung_ (daai6 ban6 zoeng6 大笨象) stupid, dull. (Page 566 像) Interchanged with the preceding (象); Williams' 象皮: The "rubber" sense is now spelt 橡皮. 【大笨象】 (daai6 ban6 zoeng6) An elephant.

zoi1.text: U+54C9 (F2.43 哉) 語助詞又始也 (Page 588 哉) An interjection (Kangxi: 言之閒也) of praise or surprise; an interrogative particle, intimating doubt or conviction, according to the scope (Kangxi: 疑辭也); a superlative; a particle placed after important words to draw attention (Kangxi: 語助), but the regimen alters its meaning much; to begin (Kangxi: 哉始也); ~~to burst forth, as plants in spring;~~ _k'i ku ho tsoi_ (kei4 gu3 ho4 zoi1 其故何哉), on what account, pray? _ho yau u ngo tsoi_ (ho4 jau5 jyu1 ngo5 zoi1 何有於我哉), what is this to me? _hi ho sik tsoi_ (hei2 ho2 sik1 zoi1 豈可惜哉), would you pity him! _yan un ~~u~~ ``fu`` tsoi_ (jan4 jyun5 fu4 zoi1 仁遠乎哉), has humanity so far gone! _on pat pi tsoi_ (on1 bat1 bei1 zoi1 安不悲哉), how could I forbear pity? _sik tsoi_ (sik1 zoi1 惜哉), sad indeed! _tai tsoi man_ (daai6 zoi1 man6 大哉問) ah! truly an important question; _tai tsoi Hung tsz'_ (daai6 zoi1 hung2 zi2 大哉孔子) Confucius, oh, how great! _u fu oi tsoi_ (wu1 fu1 oi1 zoi1 嗚呼哀哉), alas, sad indeed! _ye tsoi Yau ya_ (je5 zoi1 jau4 jaa5 野哉由也) what a clown is Yau! _ku chi tsoi, ku chi tsoi_ (gu1 zi1 zoi1, gu1 zi1 zoi1 沽之哉沽之哉), sell it! sell it! Williams' "to burst forth, as plants in spring": This is a misreading of Kangxi 說文云才草木之初也以聲近借爲哉始之哉, which says that the "begin" sense of 哉 was sometimes spelt 才 due to the phonetic nearness and the (now considered incorrect) explanation of 才 as a sprouting plant in 《說文》. Williams' 仁遠乎哉: Changed _u_ (wu4) to common reading _fu_ (fu4). Williams' 野哉由也: Corrected _ya_ (jaa6) to _ya_ (jaa5).

U+683D (F2.43 栽) 種植也 (Page 588 栽) To plant seed (Kangxi: 種也); to set out saplings, to put suckers in the ground (Kangxi: 草木之殖曰栽); to hill, to heap up earth at the roots; a sapling (Kangxi: 穉曰栽長曰樹); to assist; _fa tsoi_ (faa1 zoi1 花栽), flower suckers; _tsoi p'ui_ (zoi1 pui4 栽培), to put earth to, as to roots; _ho i tsoi p'ui_ (ho2 ji5 zoi1 pui4 可以栽培), he is worthy of your care. To tumble, to fall.

zoi6.text: U+683D Long panelling used to construct walls (Kangxi: 築牆長板).

zoi1.text: U+707D 灾 烖 甾 菑 (F2.43 災) 災禍災害 (F2.43 灾) 同上 (F2.43 烖) 同上 (F2.43 菑) 古灾字又音兹 (Page 588 災灾烖) Natural calamities (Kangxi 災: 天火也), as floods or drought; divine judgments, plagues; misfortunes, miseries, dangers (Kangxi 災: 害也); adverse, calamitous, unfortunate; _tsoi hoi_ (zoi1 hoi6 災害) injurious, afflictive; _tsoi wo_ (zoi1 wo6 災禍) miserable; afflictions; _kau tsoi_ (gau3 zoi1 救災), to relieve sufferings; _chiu tsoi_ (ziu1 zoi1 招災), to bring evil on one's self; _t'in tsoi wang wo_ (tin1 zoi1 waang4 wo6 天災橫禍) divine and unforeseen judgments; _sung fo tsoi_ (sung3 fo2 zoi1 送火災), to dismiss all risk of fire, as is done at a _ta tsiu_ (daa2 ziu3 打醮); _shui tsoi_ (seoi2 zoi1 水災), a flood; _t'in tsoi lau hang_ (tin1 zoi1 lau4 hang4 天災流行), an epidemic; _tsoi yeung kok ti t'au_ (zoi1 joeng1 gok3 dei6 tau4 災殃各地頭), each place has its own calamity. Fan Wan entry: Added (hypothesised) homophone 兹 for 菑. Williams' 打醮: Corrected _ta_ to _ta_.
U+707E (F2.43 災) 災禍災害 (F2.43 灾) 同上 (F2.43 烖) 同上 (F2.43 菑) 古灾字又音兹 (Page 588 災灾烖)
U+70D6 (F2.43 災) 災禍災害 (F2.43 灾) 同上 (F2.43 烖) 同上 (F2.43 菑) 古灾字又音兹 (Page 588 災灾烖)

zoi2.text: U+5BB0 (F2.44 宰) 主宰烹宰又邑宰家宰 (Page 588 宰) To govern, to rule, to superintend (Kangxi: 治也, 主也, 制也, 爲事物主也); ~~to fashion to one's liking;~~ a ruler, a headman, a governor (Kangxi: 官稱); to slaughter animals, to butcher, to skin and dress animals (Kangxi: 屠也烹也); ~~to cook;~~ _tsoi seung_ (zoi2 soeng3 宰相) a prime-minister; _chu tsoi_ (zyu2 zoi2 主宰), the ruler of all, the chief governor; the mind; _ka tsoi_ (gaa1 zoi2 家宰), a steward (Kangxi: 家臣之長曰宰, 諸宰家宰也); _kam chi t'o tsoi_ (gam3 zi2 tou4 zoi2 禁止屠宰), prohibited the butchering of animals, as in time of drought; _un tsoi_ (jyun6 zoi2 縣宰), a district magist``r``ate; _tsoi ngau_ (zoi2 ngau4 宰牛), to kill an ox. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 宰父 (a compound surname) (Kangxi: 宰父複姓).

U+8F09 (F2.44 載) 年也 (Page 589 載) A year (Kangxi: 年也); to commence, as after a full revolution (Kangxi: 載始也取物終更始之義); _pun tsoi_ (bun3 zoi2 半載), half a year; _ts'in tsoi ha_ (cin1 zoi2 haa6 千載下) handed down a thousand years; _ki tsoi_ (gei2 zoi2 幾載), several years. (Page 589 載 _Tsoi_ (zoi3); here corrected to _Tsoi_ (zoi2)) an incalculably high number; Williams' "incalculably high number": Based on the progression 億兆京垓秭壤溝澗正載, one of the following: 10<sup>14</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>5</sup> and going up by factors of 10); 10<sup>17</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>8</sup> and going up by factors of 10); 10<sup>44</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>8</sup> and going up by factors of 10<sup>4</sup>); 10<sup>4096</sup> (starting from 億 = 10<sup>8</sup> and going up by squares). It is an intuitive feeling that has led me to put this sense under rising-tone _tsoi_ (zoi2) rather than departing-tone _tsoi_ (zoi3), noting I have not seen solid evidence to back up my decision. I am happy to be proven wrong, and for Williams to be vindicated.

zoi3.text: U+8F09 zoi6 (per 《廣韻》昨代切 for "transport" sense) (F2.45 載) 裝載又載籍 (Page 589 載) To contain, to hold, as a carriage or ship (Kangxi: 乗也); to fill in, to load on, to bear, to sustain (Kangxi: 承也勝也); to act, to do (Kangxi: 行也), to ~~fullfil~~ ``fulfil`` (Kangxi: 成也), to effect; to record (Kangxi: 記載也); to adorn, to beautify (Kangxi: 飾也); to load in and transport (Kangxi: 舟車運物也); written, recorded; filled, loaded (Kangxi: 滿也); an auxiliary word, then, thereupon (Kangxi: 則也助語辭); _chong tsoi_ (zong1 zoi3 裝載) to store, to load, as a carriage; _tsoi mun_ (zoi3 mun5 載滿), loaded full; _tsoi lok ho shu_ (zoi3 lok6 ho4 syu1 載落何書), in what book is it recorded? _'m kau tsoi_ (m4 gau3 zoi3 唔夠載) not enough to load it; _mo ye tsoi chu_ (mou5 je5 zoi3 zyu6 冇嘢載住) I've nothing to put it in; _pi ko t'o tsoi_ (bei2 go3 tou5 zoi3 畀個肚載) he lays it up in his mind, he'll remember it; _nang tsoi ki tam_ (nang4 zoi3 gei2 daam3 能載幾擔) she carries several peculs. Williams' 冇嘢載住: Corrected _chu_ (zyu3) to _chu_ (zyu6). Deeds, works (Kangxi: 事也).

U+518D (F2.46 再 _Tsoi_ (zoi6)) 又也乃也 (Page 589 再) Again; doubled, repeated (Kangxi: 兩也, 重也); a second time (Kangxi: 一舉而二也); then, further, continued, longer (Kangxi: 仍也); to repeat; _ts'ing tsoi kong_ (cing2 zoi3 gong2 請再講), please say it again; _tsoi iu t'im_ (zoi3 jiu3 tim1 再要添), I shall want some more; _ming yat tsoi loi_ (ming4 jat6 zoi3 loi4 明日再來), come back to-morrow; _tsoi kwo ki yat_ (zoi3 gwo3 gei2 jat6 再過幾日) again some days elapsed; _tsoi shang_ (zoi3 sang1 再生) another life; _tsoi sam tsoi sz'_ (zoi3 saam1 zoi3 si3 再三再四) again and again I ordered him; _tsoi 'm ku to_ (zoi3 m4 gu2 dou3-2 再唔估到) I should not have guessed it; _tsoi hai kom 'm tak_ (zoi3 hai6 gam2 m4 dak1 再係噉唔得) it will not do for you to do so again; _tsoi che_ (zoi3 ze2 再者), further; _tsoi tuk kwo_ (zoi3 duk6 gwo3 再讀過) read it again; _tsoi pat loi_ (zoi3 bat1 loi4 再不來), I shall not return.

zoi6.text: U+510E (Page 589 儎) A colloquial word. The cargo, the loading of a boat; the tonnage or capacity of a vessel; _ki shap tsoi_ (gei2 sap6 zoi6 幾十儎) several scores of cargoes; _shun mun tsoi_ (syun4 mun5 zoi6 船滿儎) the ship is loaded; _chak tsoi shek_ (zaak3 zoi6 sek6 矺儎石) stone ballast; _tsoi keuk_ (zoi6 goek3 儎腳) freight money; _chong man pat tsoi_ (zong1 maan6 baat3 zoi6 裝萬八儎) she carries 18,000 catties.

U+5728 (F2.46 在) 居也存也 (Page 589 在) To be in, to pertain to, to be at; to reside, to remain, to live, to dwell at (Kangxi 𡉈: 居也); to belong to, to consist in; involved in, depending on; to be preserved or continue; existing, being, alive (Kangxi 𡉈: 存也); is in, in, at; to examine (Kangxi 𡉈: 察也); a place, a home; _'m tsoi uk_ (m4 zoi6 uk1 唔在屋) not at home; _'m tsoi ch'u_ (m4 zoi6 cyu3 唔在處) not in, not here; _in tsoi_ (jin6 zoi6 現在) at this moment, now; _wan tsoi_ (waan4 zoi6 還在) still here; _tsoi hok t'ong_ (zoi6 hok6 tong4-2 在學堂), at school; _tsoi wai ki noi ~~ni~~ ``ne``_ (zoi6 wai6 gei2 noi6 ne1 在位幾耐呢), how long has he reigned? _fu mo tsoi_ (fu6 mou5 zoi6 父母在) parents are alive; _tsoi ha tai_ (zoi6 haa6 dai1 在下低), is down stairs; _'m tsoi_ (m4 zoi6 唔在) he's not in; dead; no occasion, no need, --- a polite phrase; _tsoi ka to_ (zoi6 gaa1 do1 在家多), remains much at home; _ni tsoi sam_ (nei5 zoi6 sam1 你在心), remember it; _'m tsz' tsoi_ (m4 zi6 zoi6 唔自在) not feeling well; _tsoi ngo tsau kom yeung_ (zoi6 ngo5 zau6 gam2 joeng6-2 在我就噉樣) I've made up my mind; _nin man tsoi tsik_ (nin4 maan5 zoi6 zik1 年晚在即) new year's eve is at hand; _sho tsoi pit yau_ (so2 zoi6 bit1 jau5 所在必有), it is everywhere, they are in all places; _ts'o tsoi ngo_ (co3 zoi6 ngo5 錯在我), it is my fault; _sz' tsoi tso man_ (sei2 zoi6 zou2 maan5 死在早晚), his ailment is imminent; he does things blunderingly; _pat tsoi ~~u~~ ``fu``_ (bat1 zoi6 fu4 不在乎), does not consist in; _tsoi pin ch'u_ (zoi6 bin1 cyu3 在邊處) where is it? _tsoi ni_ (zoi6 nei5 在你), it's your affair. Williams' 在位幾耐呢: Changed _ni_ (ni1) to modern _ne_ (ne1). Williams' 在下低: Corrected _tai_ (dai2) to _tai_ (dai1). Williams' 不在乎: Changed _u_ (wu4) to common reading _fu_ (fu4). Whereabouts (Kangxi: 所在也).

zok3.text: U+4F5C (F1.102 作) 起也造也爲也 (Page 592 作) To act, to do, to make (Kangxi: 爲也); to begin (Kangxi: 始也), to discover, to invent (Kangxi: 造也); to arise, to appear (Kangxi: 興起也); to rouse, to animate (Kangxi: 振也), as to a reform; work, workmanship; _tsok wai_ (zok3 wai4 作為) acts, conduct, doings; _tsok fuk_ (zok3 fuk1 作福) to supplicate blessings; _peng fat tsok_ (beng6 faat3 zok3 病發作) my ailment has come again; _ha tsok_ (haa6 zok3 下作) mean acts; _tsok ka_ (zok3 gaa1 作家), a writer, an essayist; _ho tsok ka_ (hou2 zok3 gaa1 好作家), fine, delicate work; well set out; _sai tsok_ (sai3 zok3 細作) a spy; _tsok man cheung_ (zok3 man4 zoeng1 作文章), to compose essays; _tsok fan_ (zok3 faan2 作反), to turn rebel; _tsok hai kom_ (zok3 hai6 gam2 作係噉), well, let it be this way; _tsok sz' lai lok_ (zok3 sei2 lai4 lok3 作死嚟咯) you wish to die, I think, said in anger to a careless man; _kung tsok_ (gung1 zok3 工作) workmanship. Williams' 工作: Now "work". 【老作】 (lou5 zok3) To make up, to fabricate.

zok6.text: U+6628 zok3 (common) (F1.103 昨) 昔日也 (Page 592 昨) Yesterday (Kangxi: 累日也, 昨日隔一宵也); time past; recently; _tsok yat_ (zok6 jat6 昨日) yesterday; _tsok man_ (zok6 maan5 昨晚), last evening, last night; _tsok man_ (zok6 man4 昨聞), I recently heard it.
U+600D (F1.103 怍) 愧怍 (Page 592 怍) Ashamed, disconcerted (Kangxi: 慚也, 心作動也), out of countenance (Kangxi: 顏色變也, 色不和曰怍); to blush, to redden; blushing, shamefaced.

U+6FEF (F1.104 濯) 洗濯又肥澤貌 (Page 26* 濯) To rinse, to wash (Kangxi: 𤃬也, 濯滌也, 濯漑也); fat (Kangxi: 濯濯肥也), sleek, bright (Kangxi: 光明也); ~~to drink (Kangxi: 飮也);~~ _chok chok_ (zok6 zok6 濯濯) sleek, as fat animals; _sha chok_ (saa2 zok6 灑濯) to sprinkle (_i. e._ cleanse) the heart (Kangxi: 洗心亦曰洒濯). Williams' "to drink": Kangxi 將飮之而跪之曰賜濯 is erroneous. Here, 賜濯 should be 賜灌 from the Classic of Rites. Without vegetation (of a hill) (Kangxi: 山無草木之貌).

U+947F zok3 (per 《廣韻》則落切 for "bright/clear" sense) zok6-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.103 鑿) 鏨鑿又精也實也 (Page 592 鑿) A chissel, a punch (Kangxi: 鏨也, 鑿所以穿木也); a cold chissel; to cut, to bore into, to chissel (Kangxi: 穿木也); to dig, as a well; to open out, as a road (Kangxi: 開也); to brand, to mark (Kangxi: 黥𠛬), as a thief; _shek tsok_ (sek6 zok6-2 石鑿) a stone chissel; _tsok shek_ (zok6 sek6 鑿石) to cut stone; _tsun tsok_ (zyun3 zok6 鑽鑿) to bore a bole, to punch the face; _kun ts'oi tsok_ (gun1 coi4 zok6-2 棺材鑿) you coffin chissel! you impertinent fellow! _ngo mi tsok_ (ngo4 mei4 zok6-2 蛾眉鑿) a half-moon gouge; _in chi tsok tsok ho ~~t'eng~~ ``t'ing``_ (jin4 zi1 zok6 zok6 ho2 ting3 言之鑿鑿可聽) he will believe if he has repeated proofs. Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿱⿰凿殳金 to 鑿, and corrected ⿱⿰車⿱𠂉力金 to 鏨. Williams' 言之鑿鑿可聽: Corrected _t'eng_ (teng3) to _t'ing_ (ting3). To employ far-fetched or outlandish reasoning (Kangxi: 恣意不求合義理謂之鑿). Bright, clear (Kangxi: 鮮明貌). Definite, reliable, irrefutable.

U+9DDF zuk6 (per Kangxi 叶昨木切 and Fan Wan) (F1.66 鷟 _Tsuk_ (zuk6)) 鸑鷟鳥名 (Page 599 鷟 _Tsuk_ (zuk6); here normalised to _Chok_ (zok6)) A phoenix of a carnation color (Kangxi: 鸑鷟也, 紫鳳曰鷟); a large species of duck near river banks (Kangxi: 江中有鸑鷟似鳧而大此別一種); an albatross.

zuk6.text: U+9DDF (F1.66 鷟) 鸑鷟鳥名 (Page 599 鷟)

zong1.text: U+599D 粧 裝 (F1.95 粧) 粧飾又嫁粧 (F1.95 妝) 同上 (Page 27* 粧妝) To adorn (Kangxi: 飾也) the head; ornamented, dressed; to dress; to rouge, to adorn; to feign, to appear in false guise; _sho chong_ (so1 zong1 梳妝), to dress the hair; _chong pan_ (zong1 baan6 妝扮) the dress, the style; _chong pan hi lai_ (zong1 baan6 hei2 lai4 妝扮起嚟), her toilet is made; _im chong_ (jim6 zong1 豔妝), elegantly dressed; _chong shik_ (zong1 sik1 妝飾) dressed out, elegant, adorned; _chong lim_ (zong1 lim4 妝奩), or _ka chong_ (gaa3 zong1 嫁妝), a bride's trousseau, or dowry; _sho chong p'un_ (so1 zong1 pun4 梳妝盆), a paper toilette burned to Diana in the 7th moon; _so chong_ (sou3 zong1 素妝), plainly dressed; _chong hong_ (zong1 hong1 妝腔), proud, distant; _yung chong_ (jung4 zong1 濃妝), overdressed, bedizened. (Page 27* 裝) Used for the preceding (妝);
U+5E84 (F1.95 莊) 端嚴也敬也威也又齊莊 (F1.95 庄) 田庄 (Page 27* 庄)

U+6A01 (F1.95 樁) 松樁 (Page 27* ~~？~~``樁``) ~~A club;~~ a post, a stake driven deep into the ground (Kangxi: 橛杙也); _ta chong_ (daa2 zong1 打樁), to drive piles; _ts'ung chong_ (cung4 zong1 松樁), fir piles.
U+7242 (F1.96 牂) 母羊也 (Page 593 牂) A ~~ram or~~ ewe (Kangxi: 牡羒牝牂); full, plenty (Kangxi: 盛也); _tsong tsong_ (zong1 zong1 牂牂), flourishing, as foliage (Kangxi: 牂牂然盛貌).
U+7CA7 (F1.95 粧) 粧飾又嫁粧 (F1.95 妝) 同上 (Page 27* 粧妝)

U+838A 庄 (F1.95 莊) 端嚴也敬也威也又齊莊 (F1.95 庄) 田庄 (Page 27* 莊) Plants growing rank or luxuriantly (Kangxi: 草盛貌, 艸芽之壯也); sedate, serious, grave, stern (Kangxi: 嚴也); correct in conduct; adorned (Kangxi: 盛飾也); a farmhouse (Kangxi: 田舍也); a ``major`` road (Kangxi: 莊六軌之道); _chong im_ (zong1 jim4 莊嚴), severely stern; _chong king_ (zong1 ging3 莊敬) serious, solemn; _hong chong_ (hong1 zong1 康莊), a level road. (Page 27* 庄) Used for the above (莊); a barn, a grange, a cottage, a workshed; a farmhouse; a dead house or public lararium; a division of a township, a sort of parish; _shan chong_ (saan1 zong1 山莊), a ~~watchman's shed~~ ``villa or manor``; _chong hak_ (zong1 haak3 莊客) field laborers; _ki chong_ (gei3 zong1 寄莊), to store in the deadhouse; _ch'a chong_ (caa4 zong1 茶莊), a tea depot in the hills; _ts'un chong_ (cyun1 zong1 村莊), a village; _t'in chong_ (tin4 zong1 田莊), a farmstead; _chong u_ (zong1 wu6 莊戶) a farmer; _chong ting_ (zong1 ding1 莊丁), laborers on a farm. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). A villa. The dealer or banker in gambling games.

U+88DD (F1.95 裝) 裝束整飾也又嫁裝又行裝 (Page 27* 裝) to bind, to tie on, to dress (Kangxi: 裝束也); style, dress, fashion; to put into, to store, to load or stow in (Kangxi: 裹也); to pack; to catch in, as rain; to receive, to contain; to send to, as a present (Kangxi: 齎也); _hang chong_ (hang4 zong1 行裝), dress for travel; baggage; _han chong_ (haan4 zong1 閒裝), dishabille, a common dress; _mat ye chong t'au_ (mat1 je5 zong1 tau4 乜嘢裝頭), what style, what sort? _chong lim_ (zong1 lim5 裝殮), to enshroud; _chong piu_ (zong1 biu2 裝裱), to put up, as pictures; _chong hau_ (zong1 hau2 裝口), a foreign mercantile house; _chong kiu_ (zong1 giu6-2 裝轎), tip up the sedan; _mo t'in chong_ (mou4 tin1 zong1 無天裝), no place or heaven for you; _pan cheuk t'ong chong_ (baan6 zoek6 tong4 zong1 扮着唐裝) to disguise one's self as a Chinese; _chong ching_ (zong1 zing2 裝整), to put in order, to adorn; _chong tsoi_ (zong1 zoi3 裝載) to pack, to contain; _chong ts'iu_ (zong1 ciu3 裝？) to pack duties to be forwarded; _chong t'au mai min_ (zong1 tau4 maai6 min6 裝頭賣面) to place the best goods on top to sell by; _Ming chong_ (ming4 zong1 明裝), style of the Ming (明) dynasty; _chong mo tsok yeung_ (zong1 mou4 zok3 joeng6 裝模作樣) to take airs on one; _chong ch'uk ho hon_ (zong1 cuk1 hou2 hon3 裝束好看) well dressed. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 朿 to 束. Williams' 裝轎: Corrected _kiu_ (giu6-5) to _kiu_ (giu6-2). Williams' 扮着唐裝: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). To contain within oneself, to conceal (Kangxi: 藏也). Used to denote book-binding. 平裝 (ping4 zong1): "Paperback." 精裝 (zing1 zong1): "Hardback." 線裝 (sin3 zong1): "Thread-bound." Used to denote packaging. 罐裝 (gun3 zong1): "Canned." To pretend, to fake. 【裝飯】 (zong1 faan6) To fill a bowl with rice. 【裝香】 (zong1 hoeng1) To insert incense into a censer.
U+8CCD (F1.95 贓) 非理而得財物曰贓官之貪者曰贓官 (F1.96 賍) 同上 (Page 593 贓賍) Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing final stroke in 財.

U+8D13 臧 賍 贜 (F1.95 贓) 非理而得財物曰贓官之貪者曰贓官 (F1.96 賍) 同上 (Page 593 贓賍) Booty, plunder, loot, stolen goods; to receive bribes (Kangxi: 納賄曰贓); ~~to suborn, to bribe;~~ ~~to hide, to secrete~~ ``that which is stored`` (Kangxi: 藏也); _tsong kun_ (zong1 gun1 贓官), an officer who takes bribes; _hi tsong_ (hei2 zong1 起贓), to seize the booty; _tsong mat_ (zong1 mat6 贓物) spoil, things secreted; _t'am tsong_ (taam1 zong1 貪贓), sordid, covetous of bribes; _tso tsong_ (zo6 zong1 坐贓), to take bribes; bribery. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing final stroke in 財.

zong3.text: U+585F (F1.100 塟) 殯塟理也瘞也 (F1.100 葬) 同上 (Page 593 葬塟) Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿸疒圶 to 瘞.

U+58EF (F1.100 壯) 强壯壯大壯盛 (Page 28* 壯) Robust, strong, bold, lusty, hardy, healthy (Kangxi: 彊也); full grown (Kangxi: 大也), manhood; age of thirty (Kangxi: 三十曰壯); full, flourishing (Kangxi: 盛也); a cauterization (Kangxi: 醫用艾灸一灼謂之壯); _chong tai_ (zong3 daai6 壯大) lusty, large; _chong wong_ (zong3 wong6 壯旺) fat, strong, as an animal; _chong shing_ (zong3 sing6 壯盛) vigorous, prosperous, healthy; _chong yung_ (zong3 jung5 壯勇), the ablebodied, the braves; _chong ting_ (zong3 ding1 壯丁), full grown; _chong sz'_ (zong3 si6 壯士) one accomplished in manly sports; _shiu chong_ (siu3 zong3 少壯) young and hearty; _shiu sam chong_ (siu1 saam1 zong3 燒三壯) blistered it thrice; _chong chu k'u tam_ (zong3 zyu6 keoi5 daam2 壯住佢膽), to inspirit him, get up his pluck; _chong kin_ (zong3 gin6 壯健) robust; _chong hi_ (zong3 hei3 壯氣) obstinate, courageous. To enlarge. The eighth lunar month (Kangxi: 八月爲壯).

U+846C 塟 (F1.100 塟) 殯塟理也瘞也 (F1.100 葬) 同上 (Page 593 葬塟) To bury, to inter, to put under the ground (Kangxi: 葬者藏也); _tsong mai_ (zong3 maai4 葬埋), to bury; _sung tsong_ (sung3 zong3 送葬) to carry to the grave; _koi tsong_ (goi2 zong3 改葬) or _ts'in tsong_ (cin1 zong3 遷葬) to change bodies to another grave; _hau tsong_ (hau5 zong3 厚葬) a deep grave; to bury in rich dresses; _fo tsong_ (fo2 zong3 火葬) to burn the dead, cremation; _ko tsong_ (gou2 zong3 稿葬) buried in a mat; _pa k'u kam shang tsong_ (baa2 keoi5 gaam3 saang1 zong3 把佢監生葬) to be buried alive. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿸疒圶 to 瘞. Williams' 厚葬: Changed _hau_ (hau6) to common reading _hau_ (hau5). Williams' 把佢監生葬: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1).

zong6.text: U+649E cong4 (alternative per 《廣韻》宅江切) zong6-2 (in 撞邪, 撞鬼) (F1.100 撞) 衝突也又忽至曰撞 (Page 28* 撞) To beat, to pound; to run or knock against, to dash upon or against (Kangxi: 突也又擊也); to tap on, as a hoop; to strike accidentally or abruptly (Kangxi: 卂擣也); _chong cheuk_ (zong6 zoek6 撞着) to meet suddenly, to run against; _chong p'in_ (zong6 pin3 撞騙) to swindle, to peculate; _chong ngam_ (zong6 ngaam1 撞啱), luckily met; _chong ngak_ (zong6 ngaak6 撞額) face to face; _chong kwai_ (zong6 gwai2 撞鬼), to meet a devil (a foreigner); _chong hau kwa_ (zong6 hau2 gwaa3 撞口卦) to hear an ominous word; _chong 'm kin_ (zong6 m4 gin3 撞唔見) I didn't meet him; _chong pan_ (zong6 baan2 撞板), to strike castanets, _i. e._ discordant, a disappointment; _pak chong_ (baak6 zong6 白撞) to go into on a pretence, a thief; _pak chong u_ (baak6 zong6 jyu5 白撞雨), a sudden shower. Williams' 撞着: Corrected _cheuk_ (zoek3) to _cheuk_ (zoek6). 【白撞】 (baak6 zong6) To steal by opportunistic trespass. One who steals through opportunistic trespass. 【白撞雨】 (baak6 zong6 jyu5) A sun shower. 【撞板】 (zong6 baan2) To meet with failure, to encounter a difficulty that halts an attempt at something. 【撞彩】 (zong6 coi2) To obtain, succeed, win, etc. by luck or guessing. 【撞邪】 (zong6-2 ce4) To be affected by malign supernatural forces. 【撞鬼】 (zong6-2 gwai2) To be affected by a devil or ghost. To have seen a ghost.

U+72C0 zong6-2 (in 告狀) (F1.100 狀) 體也形容也象也牒也 (Page 28* 狀) Appearance, form (Kangxi: 形也), fashion; ~~to appear;~~ to declare in writing; to accuse, to state; an accusation, a complaint; _t'ai chong_ (tai2 zong6 體狀) appearance; _chong mau fi sheung_ (zong6 maau6 fei1 soeng4 狀貌非常), a beautiful face; _mo chong_ (mou5 zong6 冇狀) in dishabille, unprepared; _chong un_ (zong6 jyun4 狀元), first of the Hanlin (翰林); _ko chong_ (gou3 zong6-2 告狀) to accuse one before the court; _chong ts'z'_ (zong6 ci4 狀詞) an accusation; _kung chong_ (gung1 zong6 供狀) a plea or excuse in defence; _chong sz'_ (zong6 si1 狀師) or _chong kwan_ (zong6 gwan3 狀棍) notaries, pettifoggers. To describe (Kangxi: 形容之也陳也). Merit (recorded in writing) (Kangxi: 言...功狀). A situation.

U+81DF 臧 (F1.101 臟) 臟腑五臟 (Page 593 臟) The viscera (Kangxi: 五臟也 etc.); the entrails; _'ng tsong_ (ng5 zong6 五臟) the five viscera---heart, lungs, ~~stomach~~ ``spleen``, liver, and kidneys; _chu tsong tuk_ (zyu1 zong6 duk1 豬臟篤) the caecum of hogs; _'ng tsong luk fu_ (ng5 zong6 luk6 fu2 五臟六腑), all the inwards; _mo tsun tsong_ (mou4 zeon6 zong6 無盡臟) a great eater, gluttonous.

zou1.text: U+79DF (F2.9 租 _Tsu_) 田賦也納谷納屋錢地稅俱曰租又聚也詩子所蓄租 (Page 584 租) Rent in kind; rental, rent; tribute, taxes (Kangxi: 凡稅皆曰租); to rent; _tso ngan_ (zou1 ngan4 租銀), the rent; _shau tso_ (sau1 zou1 收租), to collect rents; _kot tso_ (got3 zou1 割租), to deduct from the rent; _tso yam_ (zou1 jam6 租賃) to lease; _tso uk chu_ (zou1 uk1 zyu6 租屋住) to live in a hired honse; _sheung k'i tso_ (soeng6 kei4 zou1 上期租), to prepay the rent; _tam tso_ (daam1 zou1 擔租), security for the rent; _hi tso_ (hei2 zou1 起租), to raise the rent. Land tax levied on farmers (Kangxi: 田賦也). To accumulate (Kangxi: 積也). Grain-stalks (Kangxi: 田中禾稭也).

U+7CDF (F2.9 糟 _Tsu_) 酒糟又糟粕 (Page 584 糟) The sediment~~;~~ ``of`` grains left after distilling spirits (Kangxi: 酒滓也, 泲曰淸不泲曰糟); dregs; _tso hong_ (zou1 hong1 糟糠), dregs and husks; _tsau tso_ (zau2 zou1 酒糟), distiller's grains; _tso u_ (zou1 jyu4-2 糟魚), fish laid in grains of glutinous rice; _tso hong chi ts'ai_ (zou1 hong1 zi1 cai1 糟糠之妻), a wife who has passed through poverty with one. To pickle in wine. To rot, to decompose. 【糟質】 (zou1 zat1) To treat harshly, to abuse, etc.

U+906D (F2.9 遭 _Tsu_) 遭逢遭遇 (Page 584 遭) To encounter, to meet (Kangxi: 遇也); to suffer, to endure, to occur, to happen; to cruize, to go about (Kangxi: 巡也); to make a revolution (Kangxi: 遭猶匝也); an occasion, a time; _tso ts'ak kip_ (zou1 caak6 gip3 遭賊劫) to be robbed; _tso kip_ (zou1 gip3 遭劫) time of a great affliction; _tso t'at mat kin_ (zou1 taat3 mat6 gin6-2 遭塌物件) to waste and misuse things; _tso t'at yan_ (zou1 taat3 jan4 遭塌人), to abuse, to treat harshly; _pak tsau yat tso_ (baak6 zau2 jat1 zou1 白走一遭), went once for nothing; _tso wo_ (zou1 wo6 遭禍) to suffer calamity; _'m tso t'at k'u_ (m4 zou1 taat3 keoi5 唔遭塌佢), be patient with him, don't berate him. To meander (Kangxi: 邐行也).

zou2.text: U+65E9 蚤 (F2.13 早 _Tsu_) 早晨又先也 (F2.13 蚤 _Tsu_) 同上 (Page 584 早) The morning (Kangxi: 晨也); early, soon (Kangxi: 先也); betimes, beforehand, early, previously; just commenced, unskilled; then, presently; _tso shan_ (zou2 san4 早晨), good morning; _tso yat fan lai_ (zou2 jat6 faan1 lai4 早日返嚟), come back soon; _kam tso_ (gam1 zou2 今早), this morning; _tai tso wa ni_ (daai6 zou2 waa6 nei5 大早話你), I told you in good time; _tso wo_ (zou2 wo4 早禾), the first crop; _chiu t'au tso_ (ziu1 tau4 zou2 朝頭早), early dawn; _tso fan_ (zou2 faan6 早飯) breakfast; _ch'an tso_ (can3 zou2 趁早) start early, and not be hurried; _tso t'oi_ (zou2 toi4 早臺), the morning play; _wan tso_ (waan4 zou2 還早), still early; _tso tso_ (zou2 zou2 早早) very early. (Page 584 蚤) Used for the preceding (早). 【一早】 (jat1 zou2) Very early on, ages ago. 一早話咗 (jat1 zou2 waa6 zo2): "Said so ages ago." 【晨早】 (san4 zou2) Very early on, ages ago. 晨早去咗 (san4 zou2 heoi3 zo2): "I went ages ago." 【早晨】 (zou2 san4) Good morning. 早晨啊 (zou2 san4 a3): "Good morning!"

U+68D7 (F2.13 棗 _Tsu_) 果名 (Page 584 棗) The fruit of the Cannarium or jujube tree (Kangxi: 果名 etc.); _mat tso_ (mat6 zou2 蜜棗), dried dates, the fruit of this tree; _hung tso_ (hung4 zou2 紅棗), red jujube plums; the _tso_ (zou2 棗) and _lut_ (leot6 栗) are emblematic of female virtue (Kangxi: 以早自謹飭爲義棗早也栗肅也).
U+74AA (F2.13 璪 _Tsu_) 玉飾也 (Page 585 璪) Pendents of precious stones or pearls hung like beads around the edge of a coronet (Kangxi: 雜采絲貫玉爲冕飾).

U+7956 (F2.13 祖 _Tsu_) 祖宗 (Page 584 祖) A grandfather (Kangxi: 父之父也); a progenitor (Kangxi: 又先祖始祖通謂之祖); ancestors; the founder of a family; an ancestral temple (Kangxi: 始廟也); the beginning, the origin, the first (Kangxi: 始也上也本也)~~, as of a family~~; to do like, to imitate a founder (Kangxi: 法也); accustomed to (Kangxi: 習也); to worship wayside gods (Kangxi: 祭道神曰祖); _sin tso_ (sin1 zou2 先祖) or _tso fu_ (zou2 fu6 祖父) a grandfather; _tsung tso_ (zung1 zou2 宗祖), my ancestors; _tsang tso_ (zang1 zou2 曾祖), a great-grandfather; _ko tso_ (gou1 zou2 高祖), a great-great-grandfather; the first emperor of a dynasty; _tso miu_ (zou2 miu6-2 祖廟) a temple of Shangti (上帝); an ancestral temple; _~~ni~~ ling tso_ (ling6 zou2 令祖), your grandfather; _ka tso_ (gaa1 zou2 家祖), my live grandfather. To give a farewell feast. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

U+7D44 (F2.13 組 _Tsu_) 綬也織也 (Page 584 組) A band, a fillet, a string, a tape, for tying (Kangxi: 綬屬其小者以爲冕纓) ~~the dress or hair~~; ~~a fringe or tuft of silk;~~ _kai tso kwai t'in_ (gaai2 zou2 gwai1 tin4 解組歸田), to untie the cord and go home --- to resign office. Williams' "the dress or hair": These are not technically wrong, per Kangxi's examples from 《儀禮士喪禮》 pertaining to tying: 著組繫《註》組繫可爲結也 (in the context of tying an eye-covering/veil) 鬠用組《註》用組組束髮也 (for tying the hair) However, the *main* definition of 組 should not be specific to these applications. The metaphorical sense of "official" comes from the use of such ribbon to tie up an official seal (hence Williams' 解組歸田). Woven ribbon (Kangxi: 組織組也). Thin, wide ribbon (Kangxi: 組紃俱爲絛薄闊爲組似繩者爲紃). An official (or his seal). A group, an organisation. To group, to organise.

U+85FB (F2.13 藻 _Tsu_) 水草 (Page 585 藻) A sort of aquatic grass (a Vallisneria?) (Kangxi: 水草也) which ducks delight in (Kangxi: 鳧得水藻言喜悅也); to joy in, delighted with; _met._ fine composition, because this grass is marked; _fu tso chi sz'_ (fu4 zou2 zi1 si6 鳧藻之士) officers ~~mutually~~ pleased; _hoi tso_ (hoi2 zou2 海藻), conferva or algae.

zou3.text: U+7076 竈 (F2.17 竈 _Tsu_) 炊竈也 (F2.17 灶 _Tsu_) 同上 (Page 585 竈灶) A furnace; a place for cooking, a kitchen-range (Kangxi 竈: 炊竈也); a bunch of grass for fuel; _tso kwat lung_ (zou3 gwat1 lung4-1 灶骨窿), the hole for the fuel; _ki tso fo_ (gei2 zou3 fo2 幾灶火), fired up several times, as in cooking with twigs or grass; _ta tso_ (daa2 zou3 打灶) to build a cooking-place; _to tso_ (dou2 zou3 倒灶) upset the furnace, _i. e._ failed, bankrupt; _hang shun tso_ (hang4 syun4 zou3 行船灶) portable furnaces, cabooses; _tso shan_ (zou3 san4 灶神) or _tso kwan_ (zou3 gwan1 灶君), God of the Furnace.
U+7AC8 (F2.17 竈 _Tsu_) 炊竈也 (F2.17 灶 _Tsu_) 同上 (Page 585 竈灶)

zou6.text: U+505A (F2.18 做 _Tsu_) 作事曰做 (Page 585 做) To do, to make, to act; to be, to perform the duties of; ~~much used for the next (造);~~ _tso tak_ (zou6 dak1 做得) very well, it will do; yes; I can do it, I will do it; _tso kun_ (zou6 gun1 做官), to be an officer; _tso muk_ (zou6 muk6 做⽊) is a carpenter; _tso tsau_ (zou6 zau2 做酒), to prepare a feast; _tso hi yan_ (zou6 hei2 jan4 做起人), a prosperous man; _tso shang i_ (zou6 saang1 ji3 做生意) is in business, he trades; _ui tso yan_ (wui5 zou6 jan4 會做人), he understands the world, popular; _'m tso tak_ (m4 zou6 dak1 唔做得) it will not do, isn't the thing; _ni tso tak 'm tso tak_ (nei5 zou6 dak1 m4 zou6 dak1 你做得唔做得) can you undertake this, can you do it? _ni tso mat ye_ (nei5 zou6 mat1 je5 你做乜嘢) what are you doing? _tso tak 'm ham 'm ~~tam~~ ``t'am``_ (zou6 dak1 m4 haam4 m4 taam5 做得唔鹹唔淡) it's neither salt nor fresh---unsatisfactory; _tso mat_ (zou6 mat1 做乜) wherefore, why? Williams' "much used for the next": I have not seen 做 used for 造. The only sense that is liable to confusion is the "make" sense of 做, but this is rarely used in Cantonese, which typically only uses the "do/perform/be" senses of 做. Williams' 做生意: Changed _shang_ (sang1) to vernacular reading _shang_ (saang1). Williams' 你做得唔做得: This is dated syntax. In modern Cantonese, it would be 你做唔做得, with only the verb hugging the negative. Williams' 做得唔鹹唔淡: Corrected _tam_ (daam6 去聲) to _t'am_ (taam5 上聲). 【做】 (zou6) To work, to be employed. 做嘢未 (zou6 je5 mei6): "Do you work yet?" 做廚 (zou6 cyu4-2): "Work as a chef." 冇嘢做 (mou5 je5 zou6): "Out of work." To be airing or broadcasting. 做緊新聞 (zou6 gan2 san1 man4-2): "The news is on." 做完 (zou6 jyun4): "The show has finished airing." (_mahjong_) To try for a hand, especially a hand of a numbered suit. 做牌 (zou6 paai4-2): "Going for a numbered-suit hand." 做大佢 (zou6 daai6 keoi5): "Make it big" (i.e. don't settle for a mediocre hand). 做索子 (zou6 sok3 zi2): "Going for rods." 做十三幺 (zou6 sap6 saam1 jiu1): "Going for Thirteen Littles." 【做低】 (zou6 dai1) To kill, to eliminate lethally. 做低咗男主角 (zou6 dai1 zo2 naam4 zyu2 gok3): "He took out the male main character." 【做乜】 (zou6 mat1) Synonym of 做乜嘢. 【做乜嘢】 (zou6 mat1 je5) Interrogative phrase pertaining to action. 做乜嘢啊 (zou6 mat1 je5 a3): "What are you doing?" Interrogative phrase pertaining to something wrong. 做乜嘢啊 (zou6 mat1 je5 a3): "What's wrong?" 你做乜嘢啊 (nei5 zou6 mat1 je5 a3): "What's with you?" Why. 做乜嘢遲到啊 (zou6 mat1 je5 ci4 dou3 a3); "Why are you late?" 【做咩】 (zou6 me1) Short for 做乜嘢. 做咩啊 (zou6 me1 a3): "What are you doing?" 做咩啊 (zou6 me1 a3): "What's wrong?" 做咩喊啊 (zou6 me1 haam3 a3): "Why are you crying?"

U+7682 皁 (for all senses except "soap") 草 (for "acorn" sense) zau2 (per Kangxi 叶子苟切 for "unhardened-seed" sense) (F2.18 皂 _Tsu_) 黑色又皂隸 (Page 585 皁皂) A black color (Kangxi 皁: 色黑也 etc.); underlings, lictors, low officers who execute commands (Kangxi 皁: 皁隸臣也, 賤人也); a stud of twelve horses (Kangxi 皁: 馬十二匹爲皁); a manger or pen (Kangxi 皁: 櫪也, 馬閑, 食牛馬器以木作如槽); _tso pan_ (zou6 baan1 皂班), torturers; _tso tai_ (zou6 dai6 皂隸) lictors, understrappers; _fi tso_ (fei4 zou6 肥皂) a sort of fragrant soap; _tso fan_ (zou6 faan4 皂礬), copperas. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰⿱匕示隶 to 隸. Used in 皂斗 (acorn) (Kangxi 皁: 草斗櫟實也). Seed that is yet to harden (Kangxi 皁: 穀實未堅曰皁). Soap.

U+795A (F2.18 祚 _Tsu_) 福也又後裔 (Page 585 祚) Happiness``,`` ~~derived from~~ wealth (Kangxi: 祿也)``,`` or office; dignities (Kangxi: 位也), felicity (Kangxi: 福也); a year (Kangxi: 歲也); _fuk tso_ (fuk1 zou6 福祚) good fortune; _kwok tso_ (gwok3 zou6 國祚) long duration, as of a dynasty; _po nin tso_ (bou2 nin4 zou6 保年祚) to recreate in one's old age. Williams' "wealth": Or, "salary".

U+80D9 (F2.18 胙 _Tsu_) 祭肉 (Page 585 胙) Roasted flesh offered in sacrifices (Kangxi: 祭福肉也 etc.); to set up an altar to Ceres (Kangxi: 建置社稷曰胙); to recompense, to reward (Kangxi: 報也有德之人必有美報); blessings, happiness (Kangxi: 福也); to bless; a seat, a place (Kangxi: 位也); _tso yuk_ (zou6 juk6 胙肉) sacrificial flesh; _kak tso_ (gaak3 zou6 革胙) to reject the oblation of an undutiful son.

U+963C (F2.18 阼 _Tsu_) 阼階東階也 (Page 585 阼) The steps on the east to ascend to the hall (Kangxi: 主階也, 阼階東階...); steps; landing where the host receives his guest at the top of the stairs (Kangxi: ...所以答酢賔客).

zuk1.text: U+56D1 (F1.63 囑) 囑括又吩咐也 (Page 35* 囑) To order, to bid, to enjoin (Kangxi: 付囑也, 託也); _chuk fu_ (zuk1 fu3 囑咐) to desire, to charge; _chuk shu_ (zuk1 syu1 囑書), a will; _chuk t'ok_ (zuk1 tok3 囑託) to commission; _mai chuk_ (maai5 zuk1 買囑) to bribe, to get evidence in one's favor. Williams' 囑咐: Changed _fu_ (fu6) to common reading _fu_ (fu3).

U+6349 zuk3 (uncommon) (F1.94 捉 _Cheuk_ (zoek3)) 捕也搦也 (Page 35* 捉) To seize, to grasp, to gripe and hold (Kangxi: 搤也一曰握也); to catch (Kangxi: 捕也); _chuk na_ (zuk1 naa4 捉拿), to pursue, for the purpose of seizing; _chuk wok_ (zuk1 wok6 捉獲) to arrest a criminal; _chuk yung shan_ (zuk1 jung6 san4 捉用神), to divine; _chuk lo shu_ (zuk1 lou5 syu2 捉老鼠), to catch rats; _chuk to_ (zuk1 dou1 捉刀), 'to grasp the knife,' to countenance or help one, to inform the government. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword ⿰木足 to 捉, and corrected 榒 to 搦. 【捉】 (zuk1) To play a board game. 捉棋 (zuk1 kei4-2): "Play chess." 捉圍棋 (zuk1 wai4 kei4-2): "Play surrounding-chess" (i.e. go). 捉一鋪 (zuk1 jat1 pou1): "Play one game of chess." 唔識捉 (m4 sik1 zuk1): "I don't know how to play." 【捉伊人】 (zuk1 ji1 jan4-1) Hide and seek. 【捉字蝨】 (zuk1 zi6 sat1) To nit-pick choice of words (or writing more generally).

U+67F7 cuk1 (alternative per 《廣韻》昌六切) (F1.63 柷) 柷圉樂器也 (Page 35* 柷) A sort of tub with a stick in the centre (Kangxi: 柷狀如桼桶方二尺四寸深一尺八寸中有椎柄連厎挏之令左右擊); _chuk u_ (zuk1 jyu5 柷敔), instruments to direct a band (Kangxi: 郭璞云樂之初擊柷以作之樂之末戛敔以止之).

U+71ED (F1.63 燭) 火燭 (Page 35* 燭) A candle; to illumine (Kangxi: 照也); _lap chuk_ (laap6 zuk1 蠟燭) a wax candle, a candle; _fo chuk_ (fo2 zuk1 火燭) a house on fire; _yau chuk_ (jau4 zuk1 油燭) painted candles; _hon chuk_ (hon3 zuk1 看燭) show candles; _mui yat fo chuk ki to_ (mui5 jat6 fo2 zuk1 gei2 do1 每日火燭幾多), what are your daily expenses? _chuk fa_ (zuk1 faa1 燭花), spreading wick, an omen; _chuk t'oi_ (zuk1 toi4 燭臺), a candlestick; _mo mi pat chuk_ (mou4 mei4 bat1 zuk1 無微不燭) lighted everywhere, to assist one's poor relations. A torch (Kangxi: 庭燎火燭也, 燈燭). 【火燭】 (fo2 zuk1) Fire (as an accident, danger, or disaster). 【火燭車】 (fo2 zuk1 ce1) A fire truck.
U+77DA (F1.63 矚) 視之甚也 (Page 35* 矚) To gaze, to look earnestly (Kangxi: 視也, 視之甚也); to fix the eyes on.

U+7AF9 (F1.63 竹) 竹木 (Page 34* 竹) The bamboo (Kangxi: 冬生靑艸 etc.); _chuk k'eung_ (zuk1 koeng2 竹蔃), whangees, bamboo roots; _chuk po_ (zuk1 bou3 竹布) linen; _chuk sun_ (zuk1 seon2 竹筍), bamboo sprouts; _chuk tsit yeung_ (zuk1 zit3 joeng6 竹節樣) ridged; _chuk wong_ (zuk1 wong4 竹黃), tabasheer; _chuk shat_ (zuk1 sat6 竹實) seeds of the bamboo; _chuk p'au_ (zuk1 paau3 竹炮) a kind of large fireworks. Bamboo musical instruments (Kangxi: 八音之一). Bamboo writing slips (Kangxi: 竹簡). The 118th radical ("bamboo"). 【竹花頭】 (zuk1 faa1 tau4-2) The character component 𥫗. (Literally, "bamboo flower atop".)
U+7AFA (F1.63 竺) 天竺國 (Page 35* 竺) A country; _t'in chuk kwok_ (tin1 zuk1 gwok3 天竺國) India, or the land where Budha was born (Kangxi: 天竺國名). A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

U+7B51 (F1.64 筑) 擊筑 (Page 35* 筑) A sort of bamboo dulcimer with 13 cords (Kangxi: 筑似箏而十三絃)~~, used in worship at the municipal temple~~. 13 is the number of strings per 《廣韻》. Other sources have 5 or 21. Short for 貴陽.

U+7BC9 (F1.63 築) 擣也築室築城 (Page 34* 築) To make adobie walls; to rear, to pile up earth; to build, to erect; _chuk ts'eung_ (zuk1 coeng4 築牆), to build a ~~wal!~~ ``wall``; _chuk ma t'au_ (zuk1 maa5 tau4 築碼頭), to lay a jetty; _chuk ch'eung po_ (zuk1 coeng4 bou2 築場圃), to lay out a kitchen-garden; _chuk t'in ki_ (zuk1 tin4 gei1 築田基), to raise dikes in or between fields. A pestle for pounding earth. To pound with a pestle (Kangxi: 擣也). A residence, a building.

U+7CA5 鬻 (F1.63 粥) 糜粥 (Page 35* 粥) Congee (Kangxi: 糜也); gruel; _po chuk_ (bou1 zuk1 煲粥) to boil congee; _chu yuk chuk_ (zyu1 juk6 zuk1 豬肉粥) a rice porridge with bits of pork in it; _chuk yam_ (zuk1 jam2 粥飲), gruel of congee. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 縻 to 糜. A meek or feeble appearance (Kangxi: 粥粥卑謙貌, 粥粥柔弱專愚之貌).

zuk6.text: U+4FD7 (F1.66 俗) 風俗 (Page 599 俗) What common people desire (Kangxi: 俗欲也俗人所欲也); inelegant, uneducated, common, vulgar, unpolished, rude (Kangxi: 不雅曰俗); the groveling, common, low, business of life; the laity; _tsuk wa_ (zuk6 waa6-2 俗話) common talk, colloquial, in distinction from the book style; _fung tsuk_ (fung1 zuk6 風俗) manners, customs of a place (Kangxi: 習也上所化曰風下所習曰俗); _shai tsuk_ (sai3 zuk6 世俗) manners of the age; _tsuk tsz' ngan_ (zuk6 zi6 ngaan5 俗字眼), a vulgar expression; _wan tsuk_ (waan4 zuk6 還俗) to again become a layman; _ch'an tsuk_ (can4 zuk6 塵俗) the busy world; _tsuk ka_ (zuk6 gaa1 俗家), the laity; _t'ut tsuk_ (tyut3 zuk6 脫俗) to eschew vulgarity; _tsuk yan_ (zuk6 jan4 俗人), a vulgar person.

U+65CF (F1.66 族) 宗族 (Page 599 族) A barb of a dart (Kangxi: 矢鋒也); to collect, to draw together, as a banner does (Kangxi: 一曰从㫃㫃所以標衆矢之所集又聚也); a clan, a tribe; a family or kindred (Kangxi: 族者屬也) of the same surname; degrees of kin; a class, a kind (Kangxi: 族類也); _tsung tsuk_ (zung1 zuk6 宗族) of the same clan; _tsuk p'o_ (zuk6 pou2 族譜), a genealogical register of the clan; _ch'ut tsuk_ (ceot1 zuk6 出族) to disown, to turn out of the clan; _tsuk lo_ (zuk6 lou5 族老), a senior in a clan; _sam tsuk_ (saam1 zuk6 三族) father, self, and son (Kangxi: 三族謂父子孫); father, mother, and wife's kindred; _shui tsuk_ (seoi2 zuk6 水族) aquatic animals. Collective familial punishment (Kangxi: 一人有罪𠛬及父母妻子). To exterminate.
U+67DA (F1.66 柚) 杼柚布機之受經者

U+6FC1 zuk6-2 (rising second in vernacular 濁濁地) (F1.66 濁) 混濁不淸也 (Page 35* 濁) Muddy, turbid, thick, foul (Kangxi: 水不淸也); impure, vicious; profligate; _chuk ts'ing_ (zuk6 cing1 濁清), corrupt --- pure, foul --- clear; _chuk yau_ (zuk6 jau4 濁油), thick oil; _chuk lau_ (zuk6 lau4 濁流), the rabble; _chuk shui_ (zuk6 seoi2 濁水), turbid water. (_phonetics_) Voiced. 【濁】 (zuk6) To choke. 飲水濁嚫 (jam2 seoi2 zuk6 can1): "Choked while drinking water."

U+7E8C May appear as either ⿰糹賣 (assumed here) or ⿰糹𧶠, depending on the font. (F1.66 續) 繼續連續 (Page 599 續) To continue, to join on, to succeed (Kangxi: 繼也); to keep up; tied together, ligatured (Kangxi: 連也); following, continuous, successively; carrying on what another has begun; _tsip tsuk_ (zip3 zuk6 接續) to join on, as a short cord; to take up and carry on; _tsuk ts'u_ (zuk6 ceoi2 續娶) or _tsuk in_ (zuk6 jin4 續弦), to join the guitar string---to ~~take a second wife~~ ``remarry after becoming a widower``; _luk tsuk yau lai_ (luk6 zuk6 jau5 lai4 陸續有嚟), coming one after another; _ch'un tsuk_ (cyun4 zuk6 傳續) to continue a family; _tsuk chu mi tso_ (zuk6 zyu6 mei5 zou6 續住？做) to work by spells. Williams' 續娶: Changed _ts'u_ (ceoi3) to common reading _ts'u_ (ceoi2). To renew, to extend. To reprise.
U+84EB (F1.66 蓫) 追逐驅逐斥逐 (Page 35* 蓫) Weeds which are hard to root out, such as the dock or thistles (Kangxi: 蓫惡菜也, 牛蘈也陸註今人謂之羊蹄). Fan Wan entry: Normalised headword ⿺辶⿱艹豕 to 蓫.

U+85DA May appear as either ⿱艹賣 (assumed here) or ⿱艹𧶠, depending on the font. (F1.66 藚) 草名 (Page 599 藚) A marshy, grassy``,`` and climbing plant, with leaves like purslane, called also cow's lips (Kangxi: 水舄也, 藚牛脣, 今澤瀉也). Specifically, the water plantain.

U+8E85 (F1.66 躅) 蹢躅行貌 (Page 35* 躅) To walk unsteady; _chak chuk_ (zaak6 zuk6 躑躅) irresolute, to walk unevenly (Kangxi: 蹢躅). A footprint, a trace, a result (Kangxi: 迹也). To stamp with the foot (Kangxi: 躑躅以足擊地).

U+8EF8 柚 (for "spindle/spool" sense) (F1.66 軸) 車軸又盡也 (F1.66 柚) 杼柚布機之受經者 (Page 35* 軸) ~~The end of~~ an axle ~~or nave~~ (Kangxi: 持輪 etc.); a pivot, centre of motion; the roller of a map; a drawing, a picture; _tsai chuk_ (zai3 zuk6 祭軸) a eulogistic inscription for a dead man suspended near the coffin before burial; _wa sam chuk_ (waa6-2 saam1 zuk6 畫三軸) three drawings. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing 灬 in 盡. A spindle or spool (Kangxi: 織具, 杼持緯者軸受經者). A roll, a scroll (Kangxi: 卷軸). An ailment of immobilisation (Kangxi: 病也, 盤桓不行之意).

U+9010 (F1.66 逐) 追逐驅逐斥逐 (Page 35* 逐) To push or drive out (Kangxi: 驅也); to order away; to expel (Kangxi: 斥也放也); to take up in order; _chuk ch'ut_ (zuk6 ceot1 逐出) to drive out; _chuk ma_ (zuk6 maa6 逐罵) to drive out with harsh language; _chuk shiu tsun po_ (zuk6 siu2 zeon3 bou6 逐少進步) to advance gradually, to learn step by step; _chuk yat chuk i_ (zuk6 jat1 zuk6 ji6 逐一逐二) to take up one by one; _chuk chuk_ (zuk6 zuk6 逐逐) in earnest (Kangxi: 逐逐篤實也); _kon chuk_ (gon2 zuk6 趕逐) to drive out, to eject. To chase, to pursue (Kangxi: 追也). To contend, to strive (Kangxi: 競也).
U+93C3 zuk1 (per 《廣韻》作木切) (F1.66 鏃) 箭嘴 (Page 599 鏃) The barb of a dart or arrow, the iron head of a dart (Kangxi: 箭鏃也); sharp ~~pointed~~ (Kangxi: 利也).

zung1.text: U+4E2D 鍾 (in vernacular 中意 spelt 鍾意) (F1.57 中) 中正不偏也 (Page 38* 中) The middle, the centre~~:~~``;`` half (Kangxi: 半也), in the middle of; within, in (Kangxi: 內也); _chung kan_ (zung1 gaan1 中間), between, inside; _'m chung yung_ (m4 zung1 jung6 唔中用) useless, inefficient; _noi chung yau_ (noi6 zung1 jau5 內中有), there are some (or such); _yan chung_ (jan4 zung1 人中), the suture on the lip; _chung ching_ (zung1 zing3 中正) indifferent, in the centre; _chung i_ (zung1 ji3 中意) to like, agreeable; _chung chi_ (zung1 zi2 中止), half done, incomplete; _chung nin_ (zung1 nin4 中年) middle-aged, _i.e._ forty; _chung tang yan ts'oi_ (zung1 dang2 jan4 coi4 中等人才), mediocre, of common abilities; _Chung kwok_ (zung1 gwok3 中國) China; _sam chung_ (sam1 zung1 心中), in my mind; _chung t'o_ (zung1 tou4 中途), halfway. Williams' 唔中用: In the literary register, 中 would be departing _chung_ (zung3). Williams' 人中: That is, the philtrum. Williams' 中意: In the literary register, 中 would be departing _chung_ (zung3). The Kangxi entry is rather poor, launching straight into a series of unannotated examples, starting with 《書大禹謨》允執厥中, without ever explaining the main sense, "centre". 【中意】 (zung1 ji3) To like. Also spelt 鍾意. 中意睇戲 (zung1 ji3 tai2 hei3): "I like watching movies." 唔中意佢 (m4 zung1 ji3 keoi5): "I don't like him." 【中文】 (zung1 man4-2) The Chinese language, as realised through spoken Cantonese. 識講中文 (sik1 gong2 zung1 man4-2): "Know how to speak Cantonese." When I was younger, I was amongst the camp that believed 鍾意 to be the "correct" spelling of zung1 ji3, based on terms like 鍾情. However, I no longer think this is as clear cut, since 鍾 in 鍾情 derives from the sense 聚也 "gather/concentrate". I now find it more plausible for zung1 ji3 to be a vernacular misreading of literary 中意 zung3 ji3.

zung3.text: U+4E2D 衷 (for "appropriate amount" sense) (F1.62 中) 合式曰中 (Page 40* 中) To hit the centre (Kangxi: 矢至的曰中); struck by (Kangxi: 著其中曰中); to attain, to accomplish; exact (Kangxi: 合也); _chung ngo sam shui_ (zung3 ngo5 sam1 seoi2 中我心水), exactly as I wish; _ku chung_ (gu2 zung3 估中) to guess right; _chung tsau_ (zung3 zau2 中酒), to get drunk; _chung pat tak_ (zung3 bat1 dak1 中不得) can not reach, unattainable; _chung chong un_ (zung3 zong6 jyun4 中狀元), to reach the head of the Hanlin (翰林); _chung kai_ (zung3 gai3 中計) I'm found out, I'm entrapped; _chung ku yan_ (zung3 geoi2 jan4 中舉人), to become a kujin (舉人); _chung ok_ (zung3 ok3 中惡) it makes me vomit; _chung fung_ (zung3 fung1 中風) taken in a fit, as of apoplexy. Williams' 中惡: Also to die suddenly. Important (Kangxi: 要也).

zung1.text: U+5B97 (F1.57 宗) 宗族祖宗又主也本也 (Page 606 宗) An ancestral temple (Kangxi: 尊祖廟也); an ancestral tablet (Kangxi: 宗者何宗有尊也爲先祖主也宗人之所尊也); what is honored (Kangxi: 謂所尊祭者 etc.); to honor, to reverence; that which men (or things) recur or resort to (Kangxi: 人物所歸往亦曰宗); to revert to; the natural centre, an origin, the source (Kangxi: 宗者本也); a clan, those of the same surname (Kangxi: 同姓曰宗); a sort, a matter, a manner; _tsung miu_ (zung1 miu6 宗廟) an ancestral temple; _t'ung tsung_ (tung4 zung1 同宗) clansmen; _t'in tsung_ (tin1 zung1 天宗), heavenly bodies (Kangxi: 天宗三日月星); _ti tsung_ (dei6 zung1 地宗), rivers, seas``,`` and mountains (Kangxi: 地宗三河海岱); _tso tsung_ (zou2 zung1 祖宗) ancestors; _tsung yan fu_ (zung1 jan4 fu2 宗人府), the Board of the Imperial Kindred; _tsung shat_ (zung1 sat1 宗室) the Imperial Clan; _tsung ts'an_ (zung1 can1 宗親), all the kindred; _tsung tsz'_ (zung1 zi2 宗子) the lineal descendant in the eldest son; _yat tsung mi sz'_ (jat1 zung1 mei5 si6 一宗美事) a noble act; _k'u tsung pan sing_ (keoi5 zung1 ban2 sing3 佢宗品性) his whole disposition; _tai tsung fo_ (daai6 zung1 fo3 大宗貨) a great lot of goods; _ko tsung sz'_ (go2 zung1 si6 嗰宗事) that single affair; _ni tsung_ (ni1 zung1 呢宗), this thing. Williams' 嗰宗事: Changed _ko_ (go3) to modern _ko_ (go2). An ancestor. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 宗政 (a compound surname).

U+5FE0 (F1.57 忠) 忠臣又盡己之謂忠 (Page 39* 忠) Faithful, loyal (Kangxi: 不貳也), patriotic; ~~attached to~~ ``devoted``, sincere (Kangxi: 內盡其心而不欺也); upright (Kangxi: 直也); generous (Kangxi: 竭誠也), unselfish (Kangxi: 無私也); _chung shan_ (zung1 san4 忠臣), a loyal minister; _chung sam kang kang_ (zung1 sam1 gang2 gang2 忠心耿耿), faithful and honest; _chung lit_ (zung1 lit6 忠烈) faithful to death; _chung hau_ (zung1 hau5 忠厚) honest. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 巳 to 己. Williams' 忠厚: Changed _hau_ (hau6) to common reading _hau_ (hau5).

U+68D5 椶 㯶 (F1.57 棕) 木名 (F1.57 㯶) 同上 (Page 606 棕椶) A kind of gomuti palm, the _Chamoerops_ (Kangxi 椶: 栟櫚也 etc.), whose bracts furnish coir for ropes, mats, &c.; the coir from the _Borassus gomutus_; a dark brown color, umber; _tsung seung_ (zung1 soeng1 棕箱), a coir trunk; _tsung so_ (zung1 sou3-2 棕掃) a coir broom; _tsung chuk_ (zung1 zuk1 棕竹) a blackish bamboo, whose roots furnish canes; _tsung tin_ (zung1 din6-2 棕墊), a coir mat.
U+6936 (F1.57 棕) 木名 (F1.57 㯶) 同上 (Page 606 棕椶)

U+7D42 (F1.57 終) 末也盡也 (Page 39* 終) The end, the termination, a finis (Kangxi: 極也窮也, 一曰盡也); death; to end, to terminate; to the last; to end one's days; _fun ho pat chung_ (fun1 hou2 bat1 zung1 歡好不終) not again to be on good terms; _chung su_ (zung1 seoi1 終須), must have it, need it; _chung shan_ (zung1 san1 終身), all one's life; _lam chung_ (lam4 zung1 臨終), near death; _chung yat_ (zung1 jat6 終日) the whole day; _shin chung_ (sin6 zung1 善終) a happy death; _chung sing nan koi_ (zung1 sing3 naan4 goi2 終性難改), hard to change one's nature; _chung pat koi kak_ (zung1 bat1 goi2 gaak3 終不改格) never reformed; _ts'ung yat i chung_ (cung4 jat1 ji4 zung1 從一而終), firm to one husband. Williams' "death": In classical usage, the death of a superior man as opposed to a little man (Kangxi: 君子曰終小人曰死). Williams' "to the last": That is, the whole. Williams' 終不改格: Or 終不改革. A surname (Kangxi: 姓).

U+7D9C zung3 (per 《廣韻》子宋切) (F1.57 綜) 理也又治也 (Page 606 綜) to hold the reins of authority (Fan Wan: 理也又治也). Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing dot in 治. Williams' "to hold the reins of authority": That is, to manage or to administer. To gather, to synthesise (Kangxi: 綜謂總聚).

zung3.text: U+7D9C (F1.62 綜) 織機綜線 (Page 606 綜 _Tsung_ (zung1); here corrected to _Tsung_ (zung3)) The harness of a loom (Kangxi: 織縷也 etc.); to work the slaie, and arrange the patterns in weaving; ~~to grasp threads;~~ Williams' "to grasp threads": This is a poor rendering of Kangxi 持絲交, which is intended as a noun, "that which graspeth threads to cross".

zung1.text: U+7E31 從 (F1.57 縱) 縱橫 (Page 606 縱) Perpendicularly, downward (Kangxi: 縱橫也, 或作從); a meridian line (Kangxi: 東西曰衡南北曰從); _tsung wang_ (zung1 waang4 縱橫), crosswise, up and across; versatile, pettish, cross-grained. 【從-vertical】 (tsjowng) (_adjective-like_) vertical, (_met._) meridian

zung2.text: U+7E31 Used in 縱縱 (hurriedly, hastily) (Kangxi: 讀如總領之總急遽趨事貌).

zung3.text: U+7E31 從 (F1.62 縱) 縱肆又故縱 (Page 607 縱) Remiss, careless; ~~extravagant, as in speech;~~ to allow, to wrongfully permit, to connive at; to let things take their course; to indulge in (Kangxi: 緩也, 恣也放也); to let go, as a mouse; although, allowing, perhaps; to shoot an arrow (Kangxi: 發矢曰縱); _tsung yung_ (zung3 jung4 縱容), not to check, unfettered, unrestrained; _ku tsung_ (gu3 zung3 故縱) purposely conniving at; _tsung in kom yeung_ (zung3 jin4 gam2 joeng6-2 縱然噉樣) although it is so; _tsung kwan_ (zung3 gwaan3 縱慣) overindulgence, as by parents; _tsung tai k'u ko tam_ (zung3 daai6 keoi5 go3 daam2 縱大佢個膽), not to restrain. Williams' "extravagant, as in speech": This is a misreading of Kangxi 縱言汎說事. Here, 汎說事 means "generally speaking of matters." Williams' "conniving at": In the (now) archaic sense of turning a blind eye. Williams' "to shoot an arrow": That is, to let it loose. To leap up.

zung1.text: U+8202 sung1 (per 《廣韻》書容切) (F1.57 舂) 擣米也 (Page 39* 舂) To beat with a pestle (Kangxi: 擣粟也); to pound firmly; _chung mai_ (zung1 mai5 舂米), to hull rice; _chung wan kai_ (zung1 wan1 gai1 舂瘟雞), you act like a hen in a fit; _chung ts'eung_ (zung1 coeng4 舂牆), to beat mud walls; _chung ch'a tsai mut_ (zung1 caa4 zai2 mut6 舂茶仔末) to triturate tea to powder for a detergent; _chung fui sha_ (zung1 fui1 saa1 舂灰沙), to beat chunam. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 舂 to 春.

U+87BD (F1.57 螽) 螽斯虫名一生九十九子 (Page 39* 螽) A longheaded grasshopper; _chung sz'_ (zung1 si1 螽斯) a green locust (a _Truxalis_) (Kangxi: 螽斯蜙蝑也, 蜙蝑長而靑 etc.); _chung sz' in hing_ (zung1 si1 jin2 hing3 螽斯衍慶) may your children be numerous.

U+8C75 (F1.58 豵) 一歲豕也 (Page 607 豵) A pig six months or a year old (Kangxi: 生六月豚一曰一歲豵尚叢聚也); ~~a pig,~~ a shote; ~~the last of a litter, a litter;~~ ~~_met._ a large family.~~ Williams' "a large family": This is incorrect. Kangxi has 《爾雅釋獸》豕生三豵《註》豬生子常多故別其少者之名, which gives the sense of "sow birthing three", with the commentary saying, "Pig-birthed offspring are commonly many: therefore we distinguish the name of them that have few thereof." Based on this, having three piglets is considered few, not many. A sow that has given birth to three (Kangxi: 豕生三豵).
U+8E2A (F1.57 踪) 踪跡 (F1.57 蹤) 同上 (Page 606 踪蹤) Fan Wan entry: Corrected 土 to 上.

U+8E64 縱 踪 (F1.57 踪) 踪跡 (F1.57 蹤) 同上 (Page 606 踪蹤) A vestige, a trace, a footstep (Kangxi 蹤: 跡也, 從也人形從之也); to follow in the track of another; _tsung ying_ (zung1 jing2 蹤影), a shadow; _tsung tsik_ (zung1 zik1 蹤跡) a trace; _chui tsung_ (zeoi1 zung1 追蹤), to follow out a clue, on the scent; _mo tsung_ (mou4 zung1 無蹤), no trace of him. (Page 606 縱; here normalised to 蹤) a vestige, a step (Kangxi 縱: 與蹤通); Fan Wan entry: Corrected 土 to 上.

U+937E (F1.57 鍾) 茶鍾酒鍾又聚也又姓 (Page 39* 鍾) ~~A measure equal to 4 _tau_ (dau 斗);~~ to collect``, to concentrate`` (Kangxi: 聚也); ~~to bestow, to confer~~ ``that bestowed or conferred from Heaven`` (Kangxi: 天所賦予亦曰鍾); ~~to repeat;~~ ~~heavy;~~ ~~gifted with, endowed;~~ _sho chung oi_ (so2 zung1 oi3 所鍾愛) whom I love best; _chung ling_ (zung1 ling4 鍾？), the luck of a spot for a grave; Williams' "A measure equal to 4 _tau_": This is a misreading of Kangxi 六斛四斗曰鍾, which puts the measure at 64 _tau_, not 4 _tau_. Williams' "to repeat", "heavy": These are renderings of Kangxi 鍾重也. It is unclear whether 重 here is level-tone _ch'ung_ (cung4) "repeated" or rising-tone _ch'ung_ (cung5) "heavy", though the latter is slightly more likely due to what follows (染色欲其深重). A vessel for storing alcohol (Kangxi: 酒器也 etc.). A measure (Kangxi: 量名) of either 64 pecks (Kangxi: 六斛四斗曰鍾), 80 pecks (Kangxi: 八斛也), or 100 pecks (Kangxi: 鍾十斛). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 鍾離 (a compound surname) (Kangxi: 鍾離複姓).

U+9418 鍾 (for "bell" sense) (F1.57 鐘) 鐘鼓樂器也 (Page 39* 鐘) A bell (Kangxi: 樂鐘也); _chong chung_ (zong6 zung1 撞鐘) to strike a bell; _ngo chung_ (ngou4 zung1 摮鐘) or _iu chung_ (jiu4 zung1 搖鐘), ring the bell; _shi shan chung_ (si4 san4 zung1 時辰鐘), a clock; _chung piu p'o_ (zung1 biu1 pou3 鐘錶舖) a watchmaker's shop. Williams' 時辰鐘: Now rarely heard. A clock. 【鐘】 (zung1) An hour. 半個鐘 (bun3 go3 zung1): "Half an hour." 一個鐘 (jat1 go3 zung1): "One hour." 個半鐘 (go3 bun3 zung1): "An hour and a half." 三個鐘 (saam1 go3 zung1): "Three hours." Time. 過咗鐘 (gwo3 zo2 zung1): "It's past time." 夠鐘食晏 (gau3 zung1 sik6 aan3): "It's time to eat lunch." 揼波鐘 (dam1 bo1 zung1): "Stalling for time" (originally in ball games, but now more generally).
U+9A23 Kangxi erroneously has 全騣馬冠也 instead of 金騣馬冠也, and fails to distinguish "horse-helmet" as a separate sense to the "mane" sense. A mane (Kangxi: 馬鬣也). A horse-helmet (Kangxi: 金騣馬冠也).

U+9B03 騣 (for "mane" sense) cong4 (per 《廣韻》士江切 for "high-topknot" sense) cung4 (per 《廣韻》藏宗切 for "high-topknot" sense) (F1.57 鬃) 後鬃 (Page 607 鬃) A mane (Kangxi: 馬鬃也或作騣); the ridge of bristles on the neck of hogs; a high ~~headdress~~ ``topknot`` (Kangxi: 高髻也); the back lappet of a Chinese lady's headdress, which extends over the neck; _chu tsung mo_ (zyu1 zung1 mou4 豬鬃毛), hog's bristles; _ma tsung_ (maa5 zung1 馬鬃), a horse's mane; _man tsung_ (man5-2 zung1 抆鬃), to put up the lappet with gum; _ka tsung_ (gaa2 zung1 假鬃), a false lappet, or lady's periwig; _sho tsung_ (so1 zung1 梳鬃), to comb back the lappet.

U+9B37 (F1.58 鬷) 衆也又釜屬 (Page 607 鬷) A kind of boiler (Kangxi: 釜屬也); ~~a run, a hank, as of hempen threads;~~ ~~to count;~~ name of a place (Kangxi: 地名). Williams' "hempen threads": This is a rendering of Kangxi 鬷謂麻縷 (which is part of the subcommentary, but not particularly helpful). Williams' "to count": This is a misreading of Kangxi 鬷數也. To gather (Kangxi: 總也).

zung2.text: U+5C30 瘇 zung6 (per 《廣韻》時宂切) (F1.61 尰) 足腫 The reading _chung_ (zung2) is not unattested; 《正韻牋》 has the reading 知隴切 for ⿺尣重, explained 足腫亦作瘇. Swelling of the legs (Kangxi: 脛氣腫).

U+7A2E (F1.61 種) 種蒔 (Page 39* 種) A seed, a kernel (Kangxi: 穀種也); a sort, a class, a kind (Kangxi: 種類); that which sprouts; _chung chung sz' ts'ing_ (zung2 zung2 si6 cing4 種種事情), every kind of affair; _tsap chung_ (zaap6 zung2 雜種), mixed, illegitimate; _wai chung_ (wai4 zung2 遺種), to leave issue; _chung lui_ (zung2 leoi6 種類) a sort, a race; _ta chung_ (daa2 zung2 打種), to beget; _ch'un chung_ (cyun4 zung2 傳種), to propagate or raise elsewhere. Guts, determination.

zung3.text: U+7A2E (F1.62 種) 栽植 (F1.62 众) 同上 (Page 39* 種) To plant, to sow (Kangxi: 布也), to grow; to cultivate, to raise; _chung tau_ (zung3 dau6-2 種痘) to vaccinate; _chung chik_ (zung3 zik6 種植) to plant trees; _chung ts'oi_ (zung3 coi3 種菜) to raise greens; _chung fuk_ (zung3 fuk1 種福) to bequeath happiness.

zung2.text: U+7CBD (F1.61 粽) 角黍也 (F1.61 糭) 同上 (Page 607 粽糉)

zung3.text: U+7CBD 糉 糭 zung3-2 (vernacular rising-tone noun) (F1.61 粽 _Tsung_ (zung2)) 角黍也 (F1.61 糭 _Tsung_ (zung2)) 同上 (Page 607 粽糉 _Tsung_ (zung2)) Dumplings made by boiling glutinous rice first soaked in weak lye, and then wrapped in leaves (Kangxi 糉: 蘆葉裹米角黍也 etc.), with a piece of sapan wood in the middle to color it; they are eaten on the fifth of the fifth month; _ka tsung_ (gaa2 zung3-2 假粽), the dumplings not wrapped in leaves; _ham yuk tsung_ (haam4 juk6 zung3-2 鹹肉粽), salt-meat dumplings; _kau tam tsung_ (gau2 daam1 zung3-2 狗擔粽), a dog eating dumplings --- a fool not relishing an allusion. The original departing reading zung3 is almost never heard, having been supplanted by vernacular rising zung3-2. Note that Fan Wan considers the rising reading to be canonical.

zung2.text: U+7CC9 (F1.61 粽) 角黍也 (F1.61 糭) 同上 (Page 607 粽糉)

U+7E3D May appear as either ⿰糹⿱囪心 (assumed here) or ⿰糹⿱囱心, depending on the font. 搃 総 (F1.61 總) 統也括也合也 (F1.61 搃) 同上 (Page 607 總搃) To collect and tie up (Kangxi 總: 聚束也); to unite the whole (Kangxi 總: 合也); to combine or comprehend in one; all, altogether, the whole (Kangxi 總: 皆也); still, nevertheless; a supervisor, one who controls all; a general of generic term; generally; _tsung kung_ (zung2 gung6 總共) or _lung tsung_ (lung5 zung2 攏總), all, the whole; _tsung pat nang ying yat tsz'_ (zung2 bat1 nang4 jing3 jat6 zi2 總不能應日子) he never once came on the promised day; _a tsung_ (aa3 zung2 阿總) or _tsung t'au_ (zung2 tau4-2 總頭), boss, a head workman; _tsung kok_ (zung2 gok3 總角) tufts of hair on young children (Kangxi 總: 總聚其髮以爲兩角); _yat tsung ki to ts'in_ (jat1 zung2 gei2 do1 cin4 一總幾多錢), how many cash for the whole? _tsung iu hu_ (zung2 jiu3 heoi3 總要去) still I must go; _tsung 'm hang_ (zung2 m4 hang2 總唔肯), after all he refused; _tsung lai mai_ (zung2 laai1 maai4 總拉埋), lump or bring them all together; _tsung 'm t'ung_ (zung2 m4 tung4 總唔同) not all the same; _tsung ch'am yam_ (zung2 cam4 jam1 總沉音), not the least answer; _tsung tuk_ (zung2 duk1 總督) a governor-general; _tsung ping_ (zung2 bing1 總兵), a major-general; _ts'in tsung_ (cin1 zung2 千總), a chiliarch, a colonel; _tsung kun_ (zung2 gun2 總管), a general manager or clerk; _tsung ch'e ke ngan_ (zung2 ce2 ge3 ngan4 總扯嘅銀), an average price. Williams' 攏總: Corrected _lung_ (lung2) to _lung_ (lung5). To lead (Kangxi: 總將領也). To tie, to knot (Kangxi: 總結也).

U+816B (F1.61 腫) 脹也 (F1.61 瘇) 同上 (Page 39* 腫~~瘇~~) To swell up, to tumefy (Kangxi 腫: 膚肉浮滿也, 脹也); a swelling; swelled; inflated~~, boastful~~; _chung cheung_ (zung2 zoeng3 腫脹) to swell; _wong chung_ (wong4 zung2 黃腫), puffed, dropsical; _fau chung_ (fau4 zung2 浮腫), a dropsical swelling; _chung sheung_ (zung2 soeng1 腫傷), a bruise or wound. Williams (following Fan Wan) erroneously has 瘇 as an alternative form of 腫 (rather than of 尰, "leg-swelling"). An ulcer or boil (Kangxi: 癰也, 疾也).

U+8E35 dung2 (common) (F1.61 踵) 足跟又繼也躡也 (Page 39* 踵) The heel (Kangxi: 足後); to follow one, to imitate (Kangxi: 因也), to go after one (Kangxi: 追也); to visit (Kangxi: 踵至也); ~~to tread hard;~~ ~~to connect;~~ _tsip chung_ (zip3 zung2 接踵), uninterruptedly; _chung mun_ (zung2 mun4 踵門), to enter the door; _chung kwai kwok_ (zung2 gwai3 gwok3 踵貴國) to come to your country; _chung fu_ (zung2 fu2 貴府), to visit your house. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰⿱口土聶 to 躡. Williams' 接踵: Corrected _tsip_ (zip6) to _tsip_ (zip3). To continue, to succeed (Kangxi: 繼也). Repeatedly, frequently (Kangxi: 頻也).

zung3.text: U+773E 衆 (F1.62 衆) 多也 (F1.62 众) 同上 (Page 40* 衆; here normalised to 眾) A multitude (Kangxi: 多也), a concourse of at least three (Kangxi: 國語三人爲眾數成於三也); ~~a quorum, a majority;~~ a sign of the plural; ~~they;~~ much people, many, all; _chung to_ (zung3 do1 眾多), a great many; _chung muk sho kin_ (zung3 muk6 so2 gin3 眾目所見) every body saw it; _ho chung ti fong_ (hou2 zung3 dei6 fong1 好眾地方), a thickly settled place; _tai chung_ (daai6 zung3 大眾) ~~all of you; the company~~ ``the masses``; _chung yan_ (zung3 jan4 眾人), all men, mankind; _yat chung ~~sang~~ ``tsang``_ (jat1 zung3 zang1 一眾僧), a company of priests; _yat chung_ (jat1 zung3 一眾) one bonze; _chung yeuk_ (zung3 joek3 眾約) all the neighborhoods; _chung i_ (zung3 ji5 眾議), public opinion; _chung wai_ (zung3 wai6-2 眾位) all the gentlemen. Williams' 一眾僧: Changed _sang_ (sang1) to common reading _tsang_ (zang1). The masses, the multitude (Kangxi: 寡者爲人上者也眾者爲人下者也).

zung6.text: U+4EF2 (F1.63 仲) 伯仲 (Page 40* 仲) The second of three (Kangxi: 仲中也位在中也); the second born of brothers; inferior; the second month of a season; _pak chung_ (baak3 zung6 伯仲) two brothers; _chung ts'au_ (zung6 cau1 仲秋), the eighth month; _chung fu_ (zung6 fu6 仲父) a father's younger brother (Kangxi: 父之弟曰仲父); an old title, like chief advisor. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 仲孫 (a compound surname) (Kangxi: 仲孫複姓).

U+8A1F 誦 (for "openly" sense) (F1.63 訟) 官訟爭辨也又爭財曰訟 (Page 607 訟) To contend before rulers; to demand justice (Kangxi: 上書爲人雪冤曰訟); litigation, pleadings, contention, wrangling (Kangxi: 爭也, 爭辯也), ---it mostly refers to legal strifes; law cases; _tsung ts'z'_ (zung6 ci4 訟辭) an indictment; _chang tsung_ (zang1 zung6 爭訟) disputes; _~~t'eng~~ ``t'ing`` tsung_ (ting3 zung6 聽訟) to hear a case; _nau tsung_ (nau2 zung6 扭訟) to grapple a man and carry the dispute into court; _sik tsung_ (sik1 zung6 息訟) to settle a dispute, to quash a case; _kun tsung_ (gun1 zung6 官訟) cases in court; _tsz' tsung_ (zi6 zung6 自訟) self reproach; _tsung kwan_ (zung6 gwan3 訟棍) pettifoggers. Williams' 爭訟: Normalised _chang_ (zaang1) to _chang_ (zang1). Williams' 聽訟: Corrected _t'eng_ (teng3) to _t'ing_ (ting3). To reproach, to reprove (Kangxi: 責也). Openly (Kangxi: 公也).

U+8AA6 頌 (F1.63 誦) 誦讀 (Page 608 誦) To hum, to recite over, to read in a humming tone (Kangxi: 諷也, 臨文爲誦, 讀誦也, etc.), as priests do; a murmuring, recitative way of reading; ~~disputing,~~ reviling (Kangxi: 怨謗亦曰誦); _pui tsung_ (bui6 zung6 背誦) to say memoriter; _tsung king_ (zung6 ging1 誦經), to recite the liturgy; _tsung tuk_ (zung6 duk6 誦讀) to read in a singing way, as children do in school. Williams' 背誦: Corrected _pui_ (bui3) to _pui_ (bui6). Williams' 誦經: Especially Buddhist scriptures. To discourse, to speak of (Kangxi: 論也言也 etc.).

U+980C 訟 (for "laud" sense) 誦 (for "laud" sense) (F1.63 頌) 稱頌 (Page 608 頌) to praise publicly, to make known the virtues of, to eulogize (Kangxi: 稱述也 etc.); _tsung tsan_ (zung6 zaan3 頌讚) to laud and praise. A type of ode in the Classic of Poetry.

zyu1.text: U+4F8F 朱 (F1.43 侏) 侏儒矮人 (Page 30* 侏) A pigmy (Kangxi: 侏儒容貌短小也); a short pillar in a roof like a king-post (Kangxi: 梁上短柱亦曰侏儒); _chu u_ (zyu1 jyu4 侏儒), a dwarf (Kangxi: 侏儒短人也).

U+6731 (F1.43 朱) 紅色又姓 (Page 29* 朱) A coniferae with red heartwood (Kangxi: 赤心木松柏之屬); vermilion; a cinnabar red (Kangxi: 朱赤深纁也); _kung kit chu ch'an_ (gung6 git3 zyu1 can4 共結朱陳), to make a contract of marriage; _chu pat_ (zyu1 bat1 朱筆) a vermilion pencil; _chu mun_ (zyu1 mun4 朱門), gentry, officials; _chu pat tim t'au_ (zyu1 bat1 dim2 tau4 朱筆點頭), to dot off with a red point. Williams' "pencil": In the (now) dated sense of a brush. A surname (Kangxi: 姓). 【朱古力】 (zyu1 gu2-1 lik6-1) Chocolate.

U+682A (F1.43 株) 樹株 (Page 30* 株) The trunk or bole of a tree (Kangxi: 木根也徐曰在土曰根在土上曰株); a classifier of trees (Kangxi: 木身也); degraded, low down (Kangxi: 謂最處底下也); _song shu ts'at chu_ (song1 syu6 cat1 zyu1 桑樹七株), seven mulberry trees; _chu shau yat u_ (zyu1 sau2 jat1 jyu4 株守一隅), confined in a corner, kept back in obscurity.
U+6D19 syu4 (per 《廣韻》市朱切) (F1.43 洙) 洙泗 (Page 30* 洙) A small stream in Shantung (山東) flowing north from Tai shan (泰山) into the R. Sz' (泗) (Kangxi: 水名, 洙水出泰山郡蓋縣臨樂子山西北入泗).

U+7026 May appear with 者 dotless (+15 assumed here) or dotted (+16), depending on the font. 潴 豬 (F1.44 潴) 潴水水所聚 (Page 29* 瀦) A pool where water settles (Kangxi: 水所停也); _chu shui_ (zyu1 seoi2 瀦水), an affluent of the Grand Canal in Shantung (山東). Williams' 瀦水: Probably a reference to 瀦水河, now 洙水河. In the generic sense, 瀦水 just means "gathered waters". To gather.
U+732A May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (F1.43 猪) 豕也 (Page 29* 猪)

U+73E0 䏭 (for "cheek" sense) (F1.43 珠) 珍珠 (F1.43 䏭) 面䏭 (Page 30* 珠) A pearl (Kangxi: 蚌之隂精); a bead; a necklace; beadlike; pearly; fine, excellent; _chu un yuk yun_ (zyu1 jyun4 juk6 jeon6 珠圓玉潤) pearly, fat cheeks; handsome; _nim chu_ (nim6 zyu1 念珠) or _so chu_ (sou3 zyu1 素珠), a rosary; _yeuk chu_ (joek6 zyu1 藥珠), seed pearls; _ngan chu_ (ngaan5 zyu1 眼珠), the iris; _ch'iu chu_ (ciu4 zyu1 朝珠), court beads; _yat ch'un chu_ (jat1 cyun3 zyu1 一串珠), one string of pearls or beads; _ye ming chu_ (je6 ming4 zyu1 夜明珠), a carbuncle; _chu po_ (zyu1 bou2 珠寶), pearls. (Page 30* 䏭; here normalised to 珠) An unauthorized character. The cheeks; _min chu tan_ (min6 zyu1 dan1 面珠墩), the cheeks. Williams' 眼珠: Or, "eyeball". 【面珠】 (min6 zyu1) The cheek. 【面珠墩】 (min6 zyu1 dan1) Synonym of 面珠.

U+7843 朱 (F1.43 硃) 銀硃硃砂 (Page 30* 硃) Cinnabar (Kangxi: 丹砂也); _chu sha_ (zyu1 saa1 硃砂), cinnabar ore; _ngan chu fan_ (ngan4 zyu1 fan2 銀硃粉), vermilion; _chu mak_ (zyu1 mak6 硃墨) red ink; _chu p'ai_ (zyu1 pai1 硃批), the emperor's rescript; _chu pat_ (zyu1 bat1 硃筆) an official autograph in red ink; _chu kun_ (zyu1 gyun2 硃卷), essays of successful _kujin_ (舉人) or _tsinsz'_ (進士).

U+7969 zyu3 (per 《廣韻》之戍切) (Page 30* 祩) To curse, to imprecate misfortunes upon one (Kangxi: 咒祩, 詛也). The level-tone reading _chu_ (zyu1) is not unattested; 《集韻》 has the readings 鍾輸切 (explained 博雅詛也) and 追輸切 (explained 詛也). Moreover, the 《廣韻》 entry for 秼 is likely a typo for 祩; it has the reading 章俱切, and is explained 詛也又音注.
U+43ED (F1.43 䏭) 面䏭 (Page 30* 䏭)
U+8331 syu4 (per 《廣韻》市朱切) (F1.43 茱) 茱茰 (Page 30* 茱) A warm, peppery medicine (Kangxi: 茱萸藥名); _chu u_ (zyu1 jyu4 茱萸), hellebore; _shan chu u_ (saan1 zyu1 jyu4 山茱萸), the cornel?

U+86DB 鼄 (F1.43 蛛) 蜘蛛 (Page 30* 蛛) The spider (Kangxi: 蜘蛛也); _chu sz'_ (zyu1 si1 蛛絲) the spider's silk or web; _chi chu lok shan yau hi sz'_ (zi1 zyu1 lok6 san1 jau5 hei2 si6 蜘蛛落身有喜事) it is luck to have a spider fall on you. Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing character 蛛. 【蛛】 (trju) Used in 蜘蛛.

U+8A85 (F1.43 誅) 征誅 (Page 30* 誅) ~~To make judicial inquiry;~~ to punish (Kangxi: 罰也), to destroy, to kill (Kangxi: 討也, 殺也, 誅者戮其人); to exterminate (Kangxi: 罪及餘人曰誅); to reprove (Kangxi: 責也 etc.); ~~to involve in;~~ _t'in chu_ (tin1 zyu1 天誅), divine (or imperial) punishment; _ching chu_ (zing1 zyu1 征誅), to punish refractory states; _chu mit_ (zyu1 mit6 誅滅) to extirpate; _chu fat_ (zyu1 fat6 誅罰) to punish. Williams' 誅罰: Changed _fat_ (faat6) to modern _fat_ (fat6). To rid (Kangxi: 翦除也). To seek.

U+8AF8 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (F1.43 諸) 衆也 (Page 29* 諸) To discriminate~~, to discuss~~ (Kangxi: 辯也, 別異之辭); not one alone, all, several, many, every (Kangxi: 非一也皆言也, 凡衆也); in, respecting; a pronoun at the end of a sentence, or a word implying doubt (contraction of 之乎) (Kangxi: 有諸疑辭); a sign of the dative, upon, to, from, at (contraction of 之於) (Kangxi: 諸猶於也); it is often introduced to make rhythm (Kangxi: 語助辭); _shu chu shan_ (syu1 zyu1 san1 書諸紳), wrote it on his girdle; _chu shang_ (zyu1 sang1 諸生), siutsai (秀才) graduates; _yan yau she chu_ (jan4 jau5 se2 zyu1 人有舍諸), will men reject him? _chu u ts'z' lui_ (zyu1 jyu4 ci2 leoi6-2 諸如此類) all are like this; _chu fan_ (zyu1 faan4 諸凡), all; _chu sz' to lo_ (zyu1 si6 do1 lou4 諸事多勞), I give you much trouble; _chu hau_ (zyu1 hau4 諸侯), feudal prince; _chu pun mat kin_ (zyu1 bun1 mat6 gin6-2 諸般物件) all sorts of things. Williams' "to discuss": This is a misreading of Kangxi 辯也. Here, 辯 is used for 辨. Synonym of 之 (third-person object pronoun) (Kangxi: 諸之也). A surname (Kangxi: 姓). Used in 諸葛 (a compound surname) (Kangxi: 複姓). 【諸事】 (zyu1 si6) Nosy or meddling. 咪咁諸事啦 (mai5 gam3 zyu1 si6 laa1): "Don't be so nosy!"

U+8C6C May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. 猪 (F1.43 猪) 豕也 (Page 29* 猪; here normalised to 豬) A hog (Kangxi: 豕而三毛叢居者 etc.); _chu na_ (zyu1 naa2 豬乸), a sow; _chu kung_ (zyu1 gung1 豬公), a boar; _shan chu_ (saan1 zyu1 山豬), a wild boar; _ts'o chu fa_ (cou4 zyu1 faa1 槽豬花), to raise pigs; _chu yuk_ (zyu1 juk6 豬肉) pork; _chu yau_ (zyu1 jau4 豬油), lard, pork-fat; _chu p'o sham_ (zyu1 po4 sam1 豬婆參), a kind of warty beche-de-mer; _chu kom ngoi_ (zyu1 gam3 ngoi4 豬咁呆), stupid as a pig; _chu luk_ (zyu1 luk6 豬樚) or _chu tau_ (zyu1 dau3 豬竇) a pig-sty; _chu keuk tung_ (zyu1 goek3 dung3 豬腳凍) pig's feet jelly; _chu mi lung_ (zyu1 mei5 lung4 豬尾龍), a spare-rib; _chu p'ai kwat_ (zyu1 paai4 gwat1 豬排骨) pork-chops.

U+90BE (F1.43 邾) 國名 (Page 30* 邾) A feudal state in the south of Shantung (山東), now the district of Tsau (鄒) (Kangxi: 魯附庸國 etc., 邾今魯國鄒縣也); _siu chu_ (siu2 zyu1 小邾) was a principality lying south of it.

U+9296 syu4 (per 《廣韻》市朱切) (F1.44 銖) 錙銖 (Page 30* 銖) ~~A small ancient silver coin,~~ ``A unit of weight`` estimated as equal to 100 grains of millet (Kangxi: 一曰十黍爲絫十絫爲銖); 24 made a tael (両) (Kangxi: 二十四銖爲兩); ~~farthings, coppers;~~ trifles; blunt, dull (Kangxi: 鈍也). I have not seen any texts define 銖 as being an actual coin rather than merely as a unit of weight.

zyu2.text: U+4E36 (Page 30* 丶) The flame of a lamp (Kangxi: 鐙中火丶也); a stop``,`` a point (Kangxi: 有所絕止丶而識之也); the third radical ("dot").

U+4E3B 丶 (F1.46 主) 君也宰也又賓主 (Page 30* 丶) Occurs used for the next (主) (Kangxi 丶: 借爲主宰字). (Page 30* 主) A rule, a lord, a master (Kangxi: 君也); a host (Kangxi: 賔之對也); the chief, the head; to govern, to rule (Kangxi: 宰也守也宗也); to make one the head; certainly, undoubtedly, as a lord's behests; _pit chu ying fuk_ (bit1 zyu2 jing3 fuk1 必主應福) will doubtless bring luck; _chu tsoi ni ka_ (zyu2 zoi6 nei5 gaa1 主在你家), I will make you my lord in your house, _i.e._ I am going to your house; _chu kung_ (zyu2 gung1 主公), your Highness, my Lord our prince; _ip chu_ (jip6 zyu2 業主), landlord; _kung chu_ (gung1 zyu2 公主), a princess (Kangxi: 天子女曰公主); _sheung chu_ (soeng5 zyu2 上主), to put up the tablet; _shui tsok chu_ (seoi4 zok3 zyu2 誰作主), who is head? _yan chu_ (jan4 zyu2 人主) the sovereign; _chu sheung_ (zyu2 soeng6 主上) H. I. M.; _shi chu_ (si1 zyu2 施主), a donor, a term used by priests; _chu mo_ (zyu2 mou5 主母), a mistress of a house; _chan chu_ (zan1 zyu2 真主), the true God, used by Moslems; _shan chu_ (san4 zyu2 神主), the ancestral tablet (Kangxi: 神主宗廟立以棲神用栗木爲之); _ts'oi chu lo_ (coi4 zyu2 lou2 財主佬), a rich man; _chu i_ (zyu2 ji3 主意) the will, decision; _chu chi_ (zyu2 zi6 主治) to govern; _chu ku_ (zyu2 gu3 主顧) a patron; _chu ku_ (zyu2 gu3 主固) solid, firm; _chu hau_ (zyu2 haau2 主考), literary examiner; _chu ~~ts'oi~~ ``tsoi``_ (zyu2 zoi2 主宰), to direct, to control; the head of; _chu ch'i_ (zyu2 ci4 主持), an abbot; to manage; _chu fan_ (zyu2 fan1 主婚), manager of a marriage; _yau t'au chu_ (jau5 tau4 zyu2 有頭主), she has a head, she is married. Williams' 主宰: Corrected _ts'oi_ (coi2) to _tsoi_ (zoi2). An owner. Main, principal. A person or party involved. To signify. To occupy (Kangxi: 主猶坐也). Short for 公主.

U+62C4 柱 (Page 33* 柱 _Ch'u_ (cyu5); here corrected to _Chu_ (zyu2) and normalised to 拄) to support, to uphold; (Page 33* 拄 _Ch'u_ (cyu5); here corrected to _Chu_ (zyu2)) ~~Used for the last (柱).~~ ~~A prop, a post~~ to shore up (Kangxi: 牚也支也); ~~to pierce;~~ to stick, to point at (Kangxi: 刺也距也一曰從旁指也). Williams' "Used for the last": It is the other way round (柱 is used for 拄). Williams' "to pierce": This is a misreading of Kangxi 刺也, which is a *metaphorical* pierce (i.e. to satirise).

U+6E1A May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (F1.46 渚) 小洲也 (Page 31* 渚) An affluent ~~of the R. Hwai (淮) near Hu chau (許州) in Honan (河南)~~ (Kangxi: 水名, 水出常山中丘逢山東入湡); an islet (Kangxi: 小洲曰渚); a place awash in a river.

U+716E May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. 煑 䰞 (F1.46 煮) 烹飪也 (Page 31* 煮) To boil in water, to steep, to decoct; to cook (Kangxi 煑: 亨也), to dress food; boiled; _chu fan_ (zyu2 faan6 煮飯) to boil rice; _chu shuk_ (zyu2 suk6 煮熟) to cook thoroughly; _chu ming_ (zyu2 ming5 煮茗), to make tea, to give an entertainment. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 飳 to 飪.
U+7FE5 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. (F1.46 翥) 飛舉也 (Page 31* 翥)

zyu3.text: U+7FE5 May appear with 者 dotless (+8 assumed here) or dotted (+9), depending on the font. zyu2 (per 《正韻牋》腫庾切逸字) (F1.46 翥 _Chu_ (zyu2)) 飛舉也 (Page 31* 翥 _Chu_ (zyu2); here normalised to _Chu_ (zyu3)) To fly up (Kangxi: 飛舉也), to fly away; to soar (Kangxi: 翥舉也, 謂軒翥也), as a phoenix does.

zyu2.text: U+9E88 (F1.46 麈) 鹿之大者尾能主風 (Page 31* 麈) A large ruminant, (a yak?) with a bushy tail (Kangxi: 麋屬, 麈似鹿而大其尾辟麈); _chu mi_ (zyu2 mei5 麈尾), a chowry used by fairies.

zyu3.text: U+6CE8 澍 (F1.47 注) 意之所主又灌注厝注 (Page 31* 注) ~~Water disparting in streamlets,~~ to lead water in channels (Kangxi: 引也); to soak; to collect (Kangxi: 聚也); to strike (Kangxi: 擊也); to fix the mind on (Kangxi: 意所嚮曰注); _chu i_ (zyu3 ji3 注意) attend to closely; attentive; _chu nim_ (zyu3 nim6 注念) to remember, to ponder; _chu muk_ (zyu3 muk6 注目) to gaze at. To pour in, to inject (Kangxi: 灌也, 水流射也). A stake, a wager.
U+6F8D 霔 syu6 (alternative per 《廣韻》常句切) Timely rain (Kangxi: 時雨澍生萬物).

U+70B7 zyu2 (alternative per 《廣韻》之庾切) (F1.47 炷) 炷香 (Page 31* 炷) A wick (Kangxi: 燈炷也, 火炷燼所著者); a stick of incense; to light; _yat chu heung_ (jat1 zyu3 hoeng1 一炷香), a set of three incense sticks; _chu heung_ (zyu3 hoeng1 炷香), to light the incense stick.

zyu6.text: U+7BB8 筯 (F1.49 筯) 快子 (F1.49 箸) 同上 (Page 31* 筯箸) Chopsticks (Kangxi: 匙箸飯具); _nga chu yat tui_ (ngaa4 zyu6 jat1 deoi3 牙箸一對) a pair of ivory chopsticks; _pi chu_ (bei2 zyu6 匕箸) spoons and chopsticks; _pat hom ha chu_ (bat1 ham1 haa6 zyu6 不堪下箸) it is not worth nipping up, uneatable. 【箸】 (zyu6) Classifier for a chopstickful of long or strand-like food. 夾箸麪 (gaap3 zyu6 min6): "Take a chopstickful of noodles." 留返箸菜 (lau4 faan1 zyu6 coi3): "Save a chopstickful of vegetables for later."

zyu3.text: U+86C0 (F1.47 蛀) 虫名 (Page 31* 蛀) An insect _(Ptinus)_ which eats books~~; moths in~~``,`` clothes, wood, &c. (Kangxi: 木蠹蟲亦名蛀蟲); to eat, as insects do; _chu lan_ (zyu3 laan6 蛀爛) spoiled by the worms; _chu t'ung_ (zyu3 tung1 蛀通), all eaten through; _ch'ung chu_ (cung4 zyu3 蟲蛀) worm-eaten.

U+8A3B 注 (F1.47 註) 訓釋也記也 (Page 31* 注) to record (Kangxi 注: 記也 etc.); to comment (Kangxi 注: 凡以傳釋經曰注通作註); (Page 31* 註) Used with the last ~~two~~ (~~著,~~ 注). To define, to explain, to illustrate (Kangxi: 訓釋也, 註者著也言爲之解說使其義著明也); to record, to write about (Kangxi: 識也, 記物曰註); a commentary, note, definition (Kangxi: 疏也解也); _chu shik_ (zyu3 sik1 註釋) to explain; _lok chu kai_ (lok6 zyu3 gaai2 落註解), it needs a commentary; _chu i_ (zyu3 ji6 註義) or _chu kai_ (zyu3 gaai2 註解), an explanation; _po chu_ (bou2 zyu3 補註) to add to the comments. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰訁㔾 to 記. Williams' "Used with the last two": I have not seen 著 used for 註 (or vice versa). Kangxi's reference to 《毛詩序疏》註者著也 is an explanation, not an interchanging.

U+9444 (F1.47 鑄) 鎔鑄 (Page 31* 鑄) To melt, to fuse metals (Kangxi: 銷金成器也, 鎔鑄也); to cast; _chu ts'in_ (zyu3 cin4 鑄錢), to cast cash; _chu p'au_ (zyu3 paau3 鑄炮) to make cannon. Fan Wan entry: Corrected 鋊 to 鎔. To form, to cultivate.
U+9714 (F1.47 霔) 時雨也 (Page 31* 霔) A seasonable shower (Kangxi: 霔霖, 霖霔, 本作澍時雨澍生萬物); _chu lam_ (zyu3 lam4 霔霖), an opportune rain.
