U+99D0 (F1.47 駐) 馬止也 (Page 31* 駐) To stop a horse (Kangxi: 馬立也, 馬止也); to rest, to tarry, to sojourn; a stopping-place; _chu tsuk_ (zyu3 zuk1 駐足) to lodge, to put up at; _chu chap_ (zyu3 zaap3 駐劄) to board at; stationed at, appointed to a post; _chu ma_ (zyu3 maa5 駐馬), to tarry a while at. To halt, to stay.

zyu6.text: U+4F4F (F1.49 住) 居住 (Page 31* 住) To desist from, to cease, to stop (Kangxi: 止也); to detain; to dwell, to live in (Kangxi: 居也); to endure; to withstand; ~~to erect (Kangxi: 立也);~~ after another verb, it is ~~often merely~~ a sign ~~of past time, or~~ that the action ~~has rested~~ ``is ongoing``; _cha chu_ (zaa1 zyu6 揸住) ~~held~~ ``holding`` it; _hit chu_ (hit3 zyu6 歇住) wait a little; _shau chu lo hau_ (sau2 zyu6 lou6 hau2 守住路口), guard``ing`` the passage; _chu po_ (zyu6 bou6 住步) to stop walking; _chu shau_ (zyu6 sau2 住手), hold up work; _ni chu pin ch'u_ (nei5 zyu6 bin1 cyu3 你住邊處) where do you live? _yan pat chu_ (jan2 bat1 zyu6 忍不住) I can not bear it. (Page 613 自 _Tsz'_ (zi6); here corrected to 住 _Chu_ (zyu6)) _mai ~~tsz'~~ ``chu``_ (mai5 zyu6 咪住) stop, hold up! _'m ho hu ~~tsz'~~ ``chu``_ (m4 hou2 heoi3 zyu6 唔好去住) don't go away, wait here a while; 【住】 (zyu6) Marker of the continuous aspect (ongoingness of an action). 着住長褲 (zoek3 zyu6 coeng4 fu3): "Wearing long pants." 食住先 (sik6 zyu6 sin1): "Let's be eating first before the others arrive." 睇住佢 (tai2 zyu6 keoi5): "Watch over him." 我揸住 (ngo5 zaa1 zyu6): "I'm holding onto it." Yet (for negative imperatives and indicatives). 咪行住 (mai5 haang4 zyu6): "Don't walk off yet!" 未得住 (mei6 dak1 zyu6): "Not ready yet."
U+7B6F (F1.49 筯) 快子 (F1.49 箸) 同上 (Page 31* 筯箸)

zyun1.text: U+5C08 剸 耑 (F2.19 專) 一也獨也擅也 (F2.19 耑) 同上 (Page 37* 專耑) One, single, alone (Kangxi 專: 獨也), undivided; bent on, to turn to one thing (Kangxi 專: 壹也誠也, 純篤也); to take upon one, to presume, to assume (Kangxi 專: 擅也自是也); self willed; _chun sam_ (zyun1 sam1 專心), undivided attention; _chun yat_ (zyun1 jat6 專日) daily; _tsz' chun_ (zi6 zyun1 自專), by one's self, to assume; _chun tang lai_ (zyun1 dang1 lai4 專登嚟), came for that purpose; _chun loi_ (zyun1 loi4 專來), only came here; _chun yat_ (zyun1 jat1 專一) to apply to one thing; _chun fong_ (zyun1 fong4 專房), to engross a husband's company. Williams' 專房: Corrected _fong_ (fong1) to _fong_ (fong4). Specifically, a husband that one is sharing with other wives and concubines. Autocratic. Specially, exclusively. 【專登】 (zyun1 dang1) Specially. 專登買㗎 (zyun1 dang1 maai5 gaa3): "I bought it especially!" Deliberately, on purpose. 專登輸畀佢 (zyun1 dang1 syu1 bei2 keoi5): "Lost to him on purpose."
U+750E (F2.19 磚) 土之有模而出者或坭磚或火磚整牆用 (F2.19 甎) 同上 (Page 37* 磚甎)

U+78DA 塼 甎 (F2.19 磚) 土之有模而出者或坭磚或火磚整牆用 (F2.19 甎) 同上 (Page 37* 磚甎) A brick, a tile (Kangxi 甎: 㼾甎, 甎瓦, 燒墼也, 甓也); to cover with brick; _ts'ing chun_ (cing1 zyun1 青磚), a burnt blue brick; _nai chun_ (nai4 zyun1 泥磚), adobie bricks; _kai chun_ (gaai1 zyun1 階磚), red tiles; _tai nai chun_ (daai6 nai4 zyun1 大泥磚), a great dolt, a villain; _chun ti_ (zyun1 dei6 磚地) to tile a floor. 【階磚】 (gaai1 zyun1) Ceramic floor tiles. Diamonds (card suit). 階磚三 (gaai1 zyun1 saam1): "Three of diamonds."
U+8011 (F2.19 專) 一也獨也擅也 (F2.19 耑) 同上 (Page 37* 專耑)

zyun3.text: U+947D 攢 (for verb senses read zyun1) 鑚 zyun1 (per 《廣韻》借官切 for verb senses) (F2.23 鑽) 尊去聲以鉄爲之所以穿物孔 (Page 604 鑽鑚) An awl, a gimlet, an ~~augur~~ ``auger``, a borer; an instrument for perforating (Kangxi: 所以穿也); ~~point of a weapon;~~ to bore, to pierce, as a sting (Kangxi: 刺也); to worm one's self into, to search into secrets (Kangxi: 凡深入者借用鑽); _tsun ngan_ (zyun3 ngaan5 鑽眼), to bore a hole; _t'it tsun_ (tit3 zyun3 鐵鑽) an awl; _kam kong tsun_ (gam1 gong1 zyun3 金剛鑽) ~~a~~ diamond or corundum ~~borer~~ (Kangxi: 金剛鑽生水底 etc.); _tsun ling_ (zyun3 ling6 鑽？) to seek into, to act wilily; _shing tsun_ (sing1 zyun3 ？鑽) a centre-bit; _tsun lung tsun la_ (zyun3 lung4-1 zyun3 laa3 鑽窿鑽罅) to pry into every nook and corner, to search thoroughly; _chi ma tsun_ (zi1 maa4 zyun3 芝麻鑽) a flaxseed awl,---a skinflint; _man tsz' tsun_ (man4-1 zi2 zyun3 蚊子鑽) a hardhearted villain. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰⿱丿口斤 to 所. Whilst 《廣韻》 has the headline reading 借官切 _tsun_ (zyun1) for 鑚 in the verb sense (explained 刺也), it also has the in-entry alternative 又借玩切 _tsun_ (zyun3). It is unclear whether this is intended as a cross-reference to 子筭切 _tsun_ (zyun3) (explained 錐鑚) as a separate noun sense or as a genuine alternative for the verb sense. Kangxi has: Level-tone 借官切 _tsun_ (zyun1) covering *both* the noun and the verb senses; then Departing-tone 子算切 _tsun_ (zyun3) as 義同 "synonymous"; then Some commentary noting that an insistence on distinguishing the verb/noun senses into level/departing tone is 泥 "clinging unto an outdated tradition": 《六書故》去聲借貫切穿器也用之穿物曰鑽平聲 《正字通》按以鑿鑿木皆讀昨不聞鑿一音鑿物又一音鑽亦如之必分平去二聲泥 The commentary gives as reasoning the homophony of the noun and verb senses of 鑿 _tsok_ (zok6) "chisel". Interestingly, in the vernacular register of Cantonese, one has the option of changing the tone to rising (zok6-2) to explicitly indicate the noun sense of "chisel". Pointed weaponry (Kangxi: 鑽謂矛矢及鏃也). Diamond.

zyun1.text: U+9C44 (F2.19 鱄) 魚名魚美也又姓也 (Page 37* 鱄) A large fish (Kangxi: 魚之大者名爲鱄), a pike? found in the Lake Tungt``'``ing (洞庭) (Kangxi: 魚之美者洞庭之鱄).

zyun2.text: U+5642 譐 cyun5 (per Fan Wan) zeon2 (per Williams) (F2.22 噂 _Ts'un_ (cyun5)) 存上聲噂沓聚語也 (Page 605 噂 _Ts'un_ (cyun5); here normalised to _Tsun_ (zyun2)) Many persons conversing (Kangxi: 聚語也) ~~amicably~~; to talk agreeably to one; unitedly, as a chorus; _~~ts'un~~ ``tsun`` tap_ (zyun2 daap6 噂沓) to flatter and then traduce; _~~ts'un~~ ``tsun`` tap mai sai_ (zyun2 daap6 maai4 saai3 噂沓埋嗮) lay them all aside; --- a colloquial phrase, in which the word is also pronounced _tsun_ (zeon2).

U+56C0 zyun3 (per 《廣韻》知戀切) (F2.22 囀) 鶯聲 (Page 37* 囀) The song of a nightingale (Fan Wan: 鶯聲); a warbling note (Kangxi: 鳥鳴, 鳥吟); delicate modulations (Kangxi: 聲轉); _ang chun_ (ang1 zyun2 鶯囀), the nightingale's song.

U+7E82 繤 纉 纘 (F2.22 纂) 編緝也 (Page 604 纂~~藄~~) A kind of carnation band or cord (Kangxi: 似組而赤); ~~to connect;~~ to collect together (Kangxi: 一曰集也); to compile a work, to edit, to abridge; a resume, a compend, a digest, a collection, a compilation; _tsun sau_ (zyun2 sau1 纂修), to arrange literary materials; _tsun tsap_ (zyun2 zaap6 纂集) a collectanea; _tsun ts'ap_ (zyun2 cap1 纂緝) to prepare and publish. Fan Wan entry: Corrected ⿰糸⿱巳耳 to 緝. Williams erroneously has 藄 for an alternative form instead of 繤.

U+7E98 繤 纂 纉 (F2.22 纘) 繼也集也 (Page 604 纘) ~~To tie things together;~~ to continue, to carry on (Kangxi: 繼也); to take up where others left off; to imitate, as virtues. Fan Wan entry: The character 繼 is printed poorly.

U+8F49 (F2.22 轉) 轉動轉運旋轉 (Page 37* 轉) To turn, to revolve (Kangxi: 動也旋也); to transmit, to forward; to transfer to; to transport (Kangxi: 轉運也); to carry; to circulate (Kangxi: 轉者輾之周); ~~to comprehend;~~ _chun k'i_ (zyun2 kei4 轉旗), turned his flag, left his party; _chun ngan kan_ (zyun2 ngaan5 gaan1 轉眼間), in a twinkling; _chun wan_ (zyun2 wan6 轉運) to transport goods; turned over a new leaf, bettered; _chun sik kan_ (zyun2 sik1 gaan1 轉息間), in a breath; _chun chip_ (zyun2 zip3 轉摺) changeable; _chun chi_ (zyun2 zi3 轉致) to send a message; _chun p'ai u yan_ (zyun2 pai1 jyu1 jan4 轉批於人), to sub-let to others. I have yet to see a satisfactory account of how the senses of 轉 ought to be split between rising-tone _chun_ (zyun2) and departing-tone _chun_ (zyun3). The classical explanations are not particularly helpful, and often contradict each other. For example: 《廣韻》 has: Rising: 動也 (to move) and 運也 (to convey). Departing: 流轉 (?). 《正韻牋》 has: Rising: 輾轉 (to toss and turn), 反側也 (to overturn), 又旋也 (to rotate), 自運也 (to self-move), etc. Departing: 運也 (to move), 又遷轉 (to shift/alter), etc. Kangxi has: 凡物自轉則上聲, "Whenever an object self-turneth, it is rising tone". 以力轉物則去聲, "To turn an object with force, is departing tone". To use Kangxi's classification, one must bear in mind that Classical Chinese scholars would not have known that torque is the rate of change of angular momentum. Thus, 自轉 should be interpreted as _beginning to turn of its own accord_, whilst 以力轉物 should be interpreted as _continuing to turn due to a supplied force to counteract friction/drag_. According to 段玉裁's annotations of 《說文》, the separation into rising and departing is the result of "shallow men" giving themselves trouble (淺人分別上去異義無事自擾). In light of the above, I have followed in the footsteps of said shallow men and split things up between _chun_ (zyun2) and _chun_ (zyun3). The supplementary entries given here are a poor attempt to exemplify the split based on personal experience. Williams' 轉批於人: Corrected _u_ (jyu4) to _u_ (jyu1). To turn, in certain cases (e.g. 旋轉, 好轉, 扭轉, 逆轉, 轉運, 轉眼). To change or to transfer, when used as a verb before another verb (e.g. 轉乘, 轉嫁, 轉換, 轉播, 轉發, 轉變, 轉讓, 轉賣).

zyun3.text: U+8F49 (F2.22 轉 _Chun_ (zyun2)) 轉動轉運旋轉 (Page 37* 轉 _Chun_ (zyun2); here corrected to _Chun_ (zyun3)) _seung kau chun k'u_ (soeng2 gaau2 zyun3 keoi5 想搞轉佢), I will try to bring him round; _chun sai pun ti wa_ (zyun3 saai3 bun2 dei6 waa6-2 轉嗮本地話) ~~to~~ translate``d into`` the local patois; _fan chun t'au_ (faan1 zyun3 tau4 返轉頭), turn around; _chun wan_ (zyun3 waan1 轉彎), to turn a corner; _chun fan t'au_ (zyun3 faan1 tau4 轉返頭), to look behind; See 轉 zyun2 for commentary on the split between rising-tone _chun_ (zyun2) and departing-tone _chun_ (zyun3). To rotate, in certain cases (e.g. 轉圈, 轉動). Rotating (e.g. 轉盤). Around, when used after a verb (e.g. 反轉, 擰轉, 調轉). To turn, in the sense of direction (e.g. 轉左, 轉右, 轉彎). To change or to switch, when used as a verb before a noun (e.g. 轉工, 轉手, 轉機, 轉膊, 轉臺, 轉車).

zyut3.text: U+525F (F2.24 剟) 取也 (Page 42 剟) To clip, to pare (Kangxi: 刊也), to cut off (Kangxi: 割也 etc.), to stab (Kangxi: 刺也); ~~to reject;~~ to engrave; _chut ting_ (zyut3 ding6 剟訂) to cut blocks and publish.
U+20B74 掇 䂐 Short (Kangxi: 吳人呼短物也).

U+555C 啐 (for vernacular "suck" sense) 嘬 (for vernacular "suck" sense) 諁 (for "talkative" sense) cyut3 (per 《廣韻》昌恱切 for "taste/sip" sense) zyut3-1 (for vernacular "kiss" sense) (F2.24 啜) 餔啜飮也嘗也 (Page 42 啜~~歠~~) To sob (Kangxi 啜: 泣貌), to talk incessantly (Kangxi 啜: 言多不止); to taste (Kangxi 啜: 嘗也, 啜茹也, 啜者拾食), (...); to kiss; _chut ha_ (zyut3 haa5 啜吓), to sip; _chut min chu_ (zyut1 min6 zyu1 啜面珠), to kiss the cheeks. (Page 609 啐 _Tsut_; here normalised to 啜) to suck, (...); _tsut kon tseng_ (zyut3 gon1 zeng6 啜乾淨) sucked it dry. Williams does not provide separate entries for 啜 (taste/sip) and 歠 (drink). Kangxi proscribes interchanging them in its entry for 歠. I have split things up accordingly. Williams' 啜吓: Corrected _ha_ (haa2) to _ha_ (haa5). To sip. 【啜-suck】 (zyut3) To suck. 啜手指公 (zyut3 sau2 zi2 gung1): "Suck the thumb." To be stuck due to the friction caused by suction, tightness, or clinging. 衫袖啜住 (saam1 zau6 zyut3 zyu6): "The shirt-sleeve is stuck." 【啜-kiss】 (zyut3, zyut3-1) To kiss. 啜我一啖 (zyut3-1 ngo5 jat1 daam6): "Kiss me once."

U+62D9 zyut6 (common) (F2.24 拙) 不巧也蠢拙 (Page 42 拙) Stupid, unskillful, unhandy, unapt (Kangxi: 不巧也); _ch'un chut_ (ceon2 zyut3 蠢拙) stupid, inexpert; _chut king_ (zyut3 ging1 拙荊), a 'dull thorn,' i. e. my wife; _chut pat_ (zyut3 bat1 拙筆) ~~an~~ ``my`` unskillful penman``ship``; _chut tsz'_ (zyut3 zi2 拙子) my son. Williams' 拙筆: Corrected _pat_ (bat6) to _pat_ (bat1).
U+6387 (F2.24 掇) 採也拾也 (Page 42 掇) To gather, to take with the hand (Kangxi: 拾取也), to pluck (Kangxi: 採也), to seize.
U+68B2 棁 棳 (F2.24 梲) 梁上短柱 (Page 42 梲) A joist to support a plate or girder (Kangxi: 同棳梁上楹也, 梲侏儒柱也梁上短柱); a sort of king-post. Fan Wan entry: Corrected headword 梚 to 梲, and fixed missing stroke in 短.

U+6B60 啜 cyut3 (per 《廣韻》昌恱切) (F2.24 啜) 餔啜飮也嘗也 (Page 42 啜歠) (...), to drink (Kangxi 歠: 飮也); _lau chut_ (lau4 zyut3 流歠) to swill down (Kangxi 歠: 流歠大歠嫌欲疾也) ~~with a noise~~; Williams does not provide separate entries for 啜 (taste/sip) and 歠 (drink). Kangxi proscribes interchanging them in its entry for 歠. I have split things up accordingly. To drink in great gulps (Kangxi 歠: 大飮也).
U+8F1F 綴 (for "stop" sense) zyut6 (common) (F2.24 輟) 作輟止也 (Page 42 輟) To stop (Kangxi: 已也, 止也, 歇也); a carriage repaired (Kangxi: 車小缺復合者); _chut kung_ (zyut3 gung1 輟工), to stop work, to rest.

zyut6.text: U+7D55 絶 (F2.25 絶) 斷絶滅絶又最也 (Page 609 絕) To interrupt a connection with, to cut off a thread (Kangxi: 斷絲); to interrupt; to sunder, to break off (Kangxi: 斷也), to terminate; to exterminate, to utterly destroy (Kangxi: 滅也); to renounce, to abjure; to cross water (Kangxi: 絕度也, 絕過也); to overpass; ~~shooting athwart, like a meteor;~~ ~~a superlative~~ ``absolutely``, very, extremely, entirely, most; a stanzas of four lines; _shat tsut_ (saat3 zyut6 殺絕) to kill every one; _miu tsut_ (miu6 zyut6 妙絕) superior to all, finest; _tsut shik_ (zyut6 sik1 絕色) exceedingly beautiful; _tsut u_ (zyut6 wu6 絕戶) or _tsut hau_ (zyut6 hau6 絕後) leaving no heir, posterity cut off; _tsut mo ho ch'u_ (zyut6 mou4 hou2 cyu3 絕無好處) no good luck at all; ~~by no means a good place;~~ _tun tsut_ (dyun6 zyut6 斷絕) to stop, as a ration; _tsut lo_ (zyut6 lou6 絕路) the road is stopped; ~~resources are~~ all cut off; _kon to tsut lung ~~ling~~ ``leng``_ (gon2 dou3 zyut6 lung4 leng5 趕到絕龍嶺), I am driven to the edge, reduced to the last extremity; _'ng tsut_ (ng5 zyut6 五絕) a stanzas in pentameters; _ma pat tsut hau_ (maa6 bat1 zyut6 hau2 罵不絕口), he never ceases to rail; _t'in mo tsut yan chi lo_ (tin1 mou4 zyut6 jan4 zi1 lou6 天無絕人之路) heaven never intends to cut off ~~men's resources~~ ``man``; _tsut mai_ (zyut6 maai6 絕賣) a final sale, as land never to be redeemed``.`` Fan Wan entry: Fixed missing strokes in 斷 and 滅. Williams' 趕到絕龍嶺: Changed _ling_ (ling5) to vernacular reading _leng_ (leng5). Williams' 天無絕人之路: Better rendered "Heaven lays no path that cuts off man". Desperate, dire, extreme. Remote, isolated. To refuse.
